DOODLYSuper Admin User Manual · Volume I
00Document Control

About this manual

This manual is written in plain English so a brand-new employee — with no prior knowledge of DOODLY — can sit down and operate the platform confidently.

6
User roles covered
120+
Screens documented
50+
Admin modules
9
Master workflows

Who should read this

AudienceHow to use this manual
Super AdminYour complete reference. You have access to every module; read the permissions matrix (§4) to understand what you are delegating to others.
Administrators & ManagersRead the modules your role can access. Each screen lists Who can access at the top.
AccountantFocus on Finance (Expenses, Wallet, GST, Payroll) and the Reports & Revenue chapters.
Delivery ExecutiveRead the Delivery Executive chapter — your daily route, deliveries, bottle & cash collection.
New employee / trainerStart with §1 (Platform Overview) and §3 (Master Workflows), then the module for your job. Use this as an SOP during onboarding.

How to read a screen page

Every screen in this manual is documented in the same fixed structure, so once you learn to read one page you can read them all:

  1. Screen name & purpose — what the screen is for, in one line.
  2. Who can access — the roles allowed to open it.
  3. Navigation path — the exact clicks to reach it, shown like Admin → Commerce → Orders.
  4. Wireframe sketch — a labelled layout of the screen.
  5. Field table — every field: purpose, accepted values, mandatory/optional, validation, backend behaviour, example.
  6. Button table — every button: what it does, the backend process, validation and the permission required.
  7. Notes, Tips, Warnings & Best Practices — the callout boxes explained below.

Callout legend

ℹ️
Note

Extra context or a detail worth knowing. Nothing will break if you skip it.

💡
Tip

A faster or smarter way to do the task.

✅
Best Practice

The recommended way of working, based on how DOODLY is meant to run.

⚠️
Warning

Do this carefully — it affects customers, money, or live data.

⛔
Caution — irreversible

This action cannot be undone. Read twice before you confirm.

DOODLY1 · Platform Overview
01Foundations

Platform overview & architecture

DOODLY is a farm-to-doorstep A2 buffalo-milk business. The platform is one system with several front doors — a public storefront, a customer account area, an admin back-office, and a delivery-executive app — all sharing one database.

The four surfaces

Everything a person can see belongs to one of four surfaces. Which surface you land on is decided by your role when you sign in.

SurfaceWho uses itWhat it doesEntry point
Storefront (public)AnyoneBrowse products, read the brand story, sign up, place the first order./
Customer AccountSigned-in customersManage subscriptions, deliveries, wallet, invoices, bottles, referrals, rewards./account/dashboard
Admin Back-officeSuper Admin, Admin, Manager, AccountantRun commerce, operations, supply, finance, growth, content, HR & system./admin/dashboard
Delivery ExecutiveDelivery executivesSee today's route, mark deliveries, collect bottles & cash./delivery/dashboard

How the pieces fit together

Figure 1.1 — One API and one database serve all four surfaces; external services handle money, maps and messaging.
ℹ️
Note

Because every surface shares one database, a change an admin makes — a new price, a new serviceable pincode, a published blog post — appears on the storefront instantly, with no code change and no waiting.

What the platform can do — at a glance

Commerce
Orders, subscriptions, payments, B2B
Catalogue
Products, pricing, inventory, bottles
Operations
Delivery, auto-assign, routes, SLA
Supply
Farmers, procurement, quality
Finance
Expenses, wallet, GST, payroll
Growth
Coupons, offers, referrals, loyalty
Content
Blogs, CMS, help centre
System & HR
Roles, audit, employees, payroll
DOODLY2 · Roles & Access
02Foundations

Roles & access model

DOODLY uses role-based access control (RBAC). Your role decides which modules you see and what you can do inside them. The Super Admin sits at the top and can grant, restrict or override access for anyone.

The six roles

RoleSeesTypical responsibilities
Super AdminEverythingFull control. The only role that manages Roles, Permissions, GST, and can reverse sensitive money actions.
AdminMost modulesDay-to-day running of commerce, operations, catalogue and content.
ManagerOperations & teamDeliveries, drivers, routes, procurement and staff supervision.
AccountantFinanceExpenses, wallet, GST, payroll, and financial reports.
Delivery ExecutiveDelivery app onlyTheir assigned route, deliveries, bottle & cash collection, attendance.
CustomerAccount area onlyTheir own orders, subscription, wallet, deliveries and rewards.
✅
Best Practice — least privilege

Give each employee the lowest role that lets them do their job. You can always grant a single extra permission to one person (a per-user override) instead of promoting them to a higher role.

Two layers of access

  1. Role defaults — every role starts with a sensible set of permissions defined centrally in code. This is the safety net.
  2. Per-user overrides — the Super Admin can add or remove one specific permission for one specific person, without changing the whole role. Managed in Admin → System → Roles & Permissions.
⚠️
Warning

A per-user override always wins over the role default. If someone can see something they shouldn't (or can't see something they should), check their overrides first before changing the role.

DOODLY3 · Master Workflows
03Foundations

Master workflows

These are the journeys that flow across many modules at once. Understanding them first makes every individual screen easier to place.

3.1 — The order & delivery lifecycle

Figure 3.1 — Order lifecycle. Green = money step. The dashed loop is the reusable-glass return.

3.2 — Referral & reward flow

Figure 3.2 — The one-time ₹100 referral reward is delayed until the friend reaches an eligible plan, which blocks fraud.

3.3 — Delivery auto-assignment

Figure 3.3 — Orders + available drivers → engine balances load (≈45 bottles/executive) → routes → delivery.
💡
Tip

The assignment strategy (Equal, Capacity, Area or Manual) is a single setting. Full detail lives in the Auto Assignment and Delivery Settings module chapters.

3.4 — Supply chain: farm to bottle

Figure 3.4 — A failed quality test decrements raw-milk inventory and blocks the batch; only passed milk moves to bottling.

Other master workflows fully diagrammed in this manual: AutoPay renewal & retry, Customer signup & onboarding, Coupon + wallet at checkout, Bottle deposit & refund, Payroll run.

CapabilitySuper AdminAdminManagerAccountantDelivery

ℹ️
Note

This is a summary. The live, authoritative matrix — with every module and every action — is on the Admin → System → Permissions screen, and changes there apply instantly.

DOODLY5 · Reading a Screen Page
05Foundations

How every screen is documented

Every module chapter uses the same anatomy. Here it is, annotated once, so the rest of the manual reads fast.

Figure 5.1 — The fixed anatomy of every screen page in this manual.
DOODLY6.1 · Admin Dashboard
06Reference Module · Worked in full

Admin Dashboard

The first screen you see after signing in as staff. It is a live control tower — the health of the whole business on one page.

Dashboard

Live operations & revenue overview, refreshed from the production database.

Purpose See today's revenue, orders, deliveries and alerts at a glance and drill into any of them.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager Accountant (widgets vary by role) Path Sign in → Admin → Dashboard
Figure 6.1 — Dashboard layout: KPI strip, 14-day revenue chart, and a colour-coded "needs attention" panel.

What each area shows

AreaMeaningClick to…
KPI stripRevenue today, orders today, active subscriptions, on-time delivery %.Open the matching module (e.g. Revenue, Orders).
Revenue chartDaily revenue for the last 14 days, from real transactions.Open Growth → Revenue for the full ledger.
Needs attentionThe most urgent items — failed payments, overdue bottles, QC flags.Jump straight to the affected record.
Quick filtersSwitch the whole page between Today / This week / This month.Recalculate every widget for the chosen period.

Buttons & controls

ControlWhat it doesPermission
Period filter (Today / Week / Month)Re-queries every widget for the selected range. Read-only — changes nothing in the data.Any staff role
RefreshPulls the latest figures from the database without reloading the page.Any staff role
ExportDownloads the current dashboard figures as a spreadsheet (CSV/Excel).Admin+
Alert item (click)Opens the specific failed payment / overdue bottle / flagged batch so you can act on it.Depends on target module
💡
Tip

Start every shift on the Dashboard and clear the Needs attention panel first — it surfaces the few things that actually need a human today.

✅
Best Practice

The KPIs are role-aware. If a widget looks empty for a colleague, it usually means their role doesn't include that data — not that the business has zero. Confirm in the permissions matrix before worrying.

DOODLY6.2 · Customers
06Reference Module · Worked in full

Customers

The master list of everyone who has an account. From here you find a customer, read their history, and act on their behalf when they call support.

Customers — list

Search, filter and open any customer record.

Purpose Find and manage customer accounts.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager (view) Path Admin → Commerce → Customers
Figure 6.2 — Customers list: search, status tabs, a sortable table, row actions, and pagination.

The table explained

ElementDetail
ColumnsName, Phone, Email, Plan, Status, Joined, Actions. Click a column header to sort.
Filters (tabs)All · Active · Paused · Trial · Churned — narrows the list to that segment.
SearchMatches name, phone or email as you type.
Pagination20 rows per page; page controls at the bottom.
ExportDownloads the current (filtered) list as CSV/Excel.
Row actionsView opens the full record; Edit opens editable fields.

Fields — Add / Edit customer

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Full nameWho the customer isText, 1–80 charsYesTrimmed; shown on invoices & delivery labelsD. Lakshmi
PhonePrimary contact & delivery SMS/WhatsApp7–15 digits, optional +YesMust be unique — one account per phone (anti-fraud)+91 93919 41220
EmailLogin & invoice deliveryValid emailYesUnique; lower-cased; used as the login IDd.lakshmi@…
StatusAccount stateActive · Paused · Trial · ChurnedAutoUsually derived from subscription activityActive
AddressWhere milk is deliveredLine, area, pincodeOpt.Pincode checked against serviceable areasBenz Circle, 520010

Buttons — customer record

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
SaveStores the edited details.Validates uniqueness, writes the record, logs an audit entry.Admin+
Add customerCreates a new account.Same as customer signup, but staff-initiated.Admin+
Reset passwordSends the customer a reset link.Emails a single-use link; does not reveal the password.Admin+
Credit walletAdds balance (e.g. goodwill/refund).Idempotent wallet credit + audit; visible to the customer instantly.Admin+
SuspendBlocks the account from ordering.Sets status; the customer can't check out until reinstated.Admin+
⚠️
Warning

Credit wallet and Reset password both affect the customer immediately. Confirm you have the right account open — the search box shows masked phone numbers to avoid mix-ups.

⛔
Caution — irreversible

Deleting a customer is a soft delete (hidden, not erased) so history and invoices stay intact. Only the Super Admin can permanently remove personal data, and only on a verified request.

Module chapter

Customer Module

13 screen groups documented from the live application.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Customer Dashboard
§Customer Module

Customer Dashboard

The signed-in customer's home screen — a single, live "everything about your milk today" view that summarises the subscription, next delivery, wallet, reward points, referrals, orders, invoices, bottles, the loyalty tier card, the Monthly Puzzle Challenge and a preview of recent orders, every tile deep-linking to its full page.

ℹ️
Who this screen belongs to

This is a customer-facing page inside the "My Account" surface (URL /account/dashboard.html), not an admin console. It appears in this manual so Super Admins, Managers and Support staff understand exactly what a customer sees, how it is fed with data, and how to explain it or reproduce a complaint. Every number is computed live from the signed-in customer's own records — a staff member cannot edit anything here; they observe it (for example while helping over the phone or via impersonation).

Dashboard — overview page

The full "My milk today" landing screen after a customer signs in.

Purpose Give a customer a one-glance status of their subscription, next delivery, money, rewards and bottles, with a fast route to every detail page.
Access Customer (own account only) · viewable by Super Admin+ through impersonation for support Path My Account → Overview → Dashboard
My Account — Dashboard · greeting · sidebar · top bar (notifications, profile menu)
Page head (greeting)"Good morning/afternoon/evening, <First name> 👋" + subtitle "Here's everything about your milk today." Greeting word is chosen by the time of day and personalised once real data loads.
Live KPI grid11 clickable stat cards (Active Subscription, Upcoming Delivery, Wallet, Reward Points, Referral Earnings, Referral Code, Total Orders, Total Deliveries, Pending Payments, Latest Invoice, Bottles Pending).
Quick actions row4 shortcut tiles: Pause plan, Extra milk, Return bottles, Track delivery.
Pure Rewards cardLoyalty tier card — tier name, available points, progress bar to next tier. Hidden if the customer is signed out or the programme is off.
Puzzle Challenge cardMonthly slide-puzzle promo — month, title, phase, prize, player stats and the customer's own attempt/rank. Hidden when no challenge is live.
Current subscription cardPlan, status, bottle, per-delivery price and renewal date, with Pause / Resume and Add extra milk buttons — or an honest "No active subscription" empty state.
Recent orders tableCompact order history (Order, Date, Items, Amount, Status). No toolbar, no pager — a preview that links to My Orders.
Figure 1 — Labelled layout of the Customer Dashboard (top to bottom render order from the page recipe).
ℹ️
Block recipe (for reference)

The page is assembled from these blocks, in this order: page head → customerKpis (page "dashboard") → quickActions → loyaltyCard → puzzleCard → customerSub → table (dataset "orders", toolbar off, pager off). Each block renders an empty mount that is then filled with live data by its module (Customer, Loyalty, Puzzle) after the account data is fetched.

Region A — Greeting (page head)

The recipe seeds the heading as "Good morning 👋" with the subtitle "Here's everything about your milk today." As soon as the signed-in customer's data is loaded, the greeting is rewritten to the correct time of day and personalised with the customer's first name.

Time of day (device clock)Greeting shown
Before 12:00 (noon)Good morning, <First name> 👋
12:00 to 16:59Good afternoon, <First name> 👋
17:00 onwardsGood evening, <First name> 👋
ℹ️
Note

The first name comes from the customer profile returned by the backend. If the data fetch fails, the customer's real name is still used (never the demo "Ananya" account), so the greeting stays correct even offline.

Region B — Live KPI grid (11 cards)

Every card is computed live from the customer's own records. Each card shows a big value, a small caption underneath, an icon, and a chevron indicating it is clickable; tapping the card opens the matching detail page. There are no editable controls here.

#Card (label)Value shownCaption underneathOpens
1Active SubscriptionPlan name (e.g. "Daily Fresh")Status · bottle variant (e.g. "Active · 500 ml")/account/subscription.html
2Upcoming DeliveryNext delivery date + "before 7 AM", or "No delivery scheduled"Track it live/account/tracking.html
3Wallet BalanceRupee balance (₹)Cashback & credits/account/wallet.html
4Reward PointsPoints total<Tier> tier (Silver / Gold / Platinum)/account/rewards.html
5Referral EarningsTotal ₹ earned from referralsN friends joined/account/referrals.html
6Referral CodeThe customer's own codeTap to share & earn/account/referrals.html
7Total OrdersCount of all ordersN active/account/orders.html
8Total DeliveriesCount of deliveredN skipped/account/deliveries.html
9Pending PaymentsSum ₹ of unpaid invoicesN unpaid invoice(s)/account/invoices.html
10Latest InvoiceNewest invoice number, or "—"₹ amount · status (or "No invoices")/account/invoices.html
11Bottles PendingBottles awaiting collection₹ deposit held/account/bottles.html
💡
Tip — how the numbers are derived

The core figures (name, wallet balance, loyalty points, bottles pending, next delivery, active subscription and the order/delivery/invoice counts) come from one backend call, GET /api/account/summary. Referral earnings, active-order counts, skipped-delivery counts and the "latest invoice" line are computed on the device from the customer's own hydrated order, delivery, invoice and referral records. Tier is derived from points (Silver below 500, Gold 500–1499, Platinum 1500+).

Region C — Quick actions

Four large shortcut tiles for the most common self-service tasks. Each is a plain link — no confirmation, no data change on this screen; they simply navigate.

TileSub-textWhat it doesOpens
Pause planGoing away?Start a vacation / pause request/account/vacation.html
Extra milkGuests over?Add a one-off extra quantity to an upcoming delivery/account/extra-milk.html
Return bottlesManage emptiesView and request collection of empty bottles/account/bottles.html
Track deliveryLive nowOpen live delivery tracking/account/tracking.html

Region D — DOODLY Pure Rewards card

A compact loyalty tier card. It is fetched from GET /api/account/rewards and rendered only when the customer is signed in and the rewards programme is enabled; otherwise it renders nothing (the region simply collapses).

ElementShows
Eyebrow + tier iconTier medal (🥉 Fresh / 🥈 Silver / 🥇 Gold / 💎 Platinum) and "DOODLY Pure Rewards"
Tier nameCurrent tier (e.g. "Gold"), or "Fresh Member" if none
PointsAvailable points
Progress barFill toward the next tier (full when top tier reached)
Footer line"<lifetime earned> / <next-tier minimum> · <points away> to <Next tier>", or "Top tier unlocked — enjoy the perks! 🎉"
Call to action"View rewards →" — the whole card links to /account/rewards.html

Region E — Monthly Puzzle Challenge card

Promotes the running slide-puzzle competition. Fetched from GET /api/puzzles; the card is hidden unless the feature is enabled and a challenge exists for the current month.

ElementShows
Band eyebrow"🏆 Monthly Puzzle Challenge — Month X of N"
Title & subThe month's puzzle title + status line + "Prize: FREE 7-Day Fresh Start Subscription"
Status line (phase)Upcoming ("Unlocks <date>") · Live ("Live now — closes <date>") · Judging ("Closed — winner announced <date>") · Announced ("Winner announced")
Stat tilesPlayers, Completed, (Your moves & Your rank if you completed), Winner announced date
Your lineYour attempt status — completed with moves/time/rank, in progress, or "You haven't played this month yet."
Win bannerIf you placed 1st after announcement: "🏆 You won this month! Your FREE 7-Day Fresh Start subscription has been added to your account."
Past winnersUp to the 3 most recent winners (name, month, moves)
Button"Play now" / "Continue puzzle" / "View leaderboard" (when live) or "View challenge" — opens /puzzle.html

Region F — Current subscription card

A summary of the active plan with quick management buttons. If there is no active subscription it shows an honest empty state instead of a fabricated plan.

StateWhat appears
Active subscriptionDefinition list: Plan, Status, Bottle (variant), Per delivery (₹ per delivery, shown only when known from the live subscription), Renews (renewal date, shown only when known). Buttons: "Pause / vacation" (or "Resume plan" when paused) and "Add extra milk". A "Manage →" link opens the full Subscription page.
No active subscriptionEmpty state: "No active subscription — Subscribe today to enjoy fresh farm-to-home dairy deliveries." with a "Browse subscription plans" button to /subscriptions.html.
ℹ️
Note — the "Per delivery" and "Renews" rows are conservative

Those two rows are only drawn when the live subscription supplies a real per-delivery price and end date. If a figure isn't available, the row is omitted rather than guessed — so a customer is never shown an invented price or renewal date. The exact figures always live on the Subscription page.

Region G — Recent orders (preview table)

A compact, read-only order-history preview. It uses the shared "orders" table definition but with the toolbar and pager switched off, so it is a short list rather than the full searchable table found on the My Orders page.

AttributeDetail
ColumnsOrder (order number) · Date · Items · Amount (₹) · Status (coloured badge — Active / Completed / Refunded / Cancelled)
Toolbar / searchOff on this screen (no search, filter, saved views or export here)
PaginationOff on this screen (preview list)
SortingNewest first (as returned by the backend)
Row actionsNone on the dashboard preview — for full order detail, filters and export the customer uses My Orders
Empty state"No orders yet — When you place your first DOODLY order it'll appear here with its status and invoice." with "Browse products" and "Start a subscription" buttons
Data sourceGET /api/orders?limit=100 (mapped to Order / Date / Items / Amount / Status rows)
ℹ️
Related pages (reachable from this screen)

Subscription, Delivery Tracking, Wallet, Rewards, Referrals, My Orders, Deliveries, Invoices, Bottle Tracking, Vacation/Pause, Extra Milk and the Puzzle game are all opened from the tiles and cards above. Each of those has its own section in this manual.

How the dashboard loads its data

Session check, data fetch, and what happens when the backend is slow or unreachable.

Purpose Explain the load sequence so support staff can tell a real outage from an empty account.
Access Customer session required Path My Account → Overview → Dashboard
Workflow — dashboard data hydration
Customer opens Dashboard Session identity read Reset to real user + empty data (clears any demo seed) Fetch account summary + orders/deliveries/invoices/bottles/referrals/notifications concurrently Fill KPI cards, subscription card & orders table Loyalty & Puzzle cards fetch their own data and fill in Greeting personalised
Figure 2 — Load sequence. All the account requests fire together, so total wait is roughly the slowest one, not the sum.
SituationWhat the customer sees
Signed-in customer, backend healthyAll cards populate with live figures; greeting shows real name + time of day.
Section fetch fails (e.g. referrals) but profile loadsThat one section stays empty/zero; the rest of the dashboard is normal — no error is forced.
Session expired / revoked (401)The dashboard drops the stale session and redirects to /login/customer.html?expired=1 so the customer signs in again. Demo data is never shown.
Backend unreachable / timeout while signed inHonest empty states everywhere plus a connectivity banner; the page never falls back to the sample "Ananya" account.
Not signed in (public/demo)Access is guarded — the customer is prompted to log in or create an account before the account pages open.
✅
Best Practice

When a customer says "my dashboard is empty," first confirm whether it is a new account with no orders yet (genuine empty states, no banner) or a connectivity banner (backend unreachable). The two look different: empty states carry friendly "get started" buttons, while the connectivity banner is a fixed pill telling the customer we're offline. Only the second one is an incident.

⚠️
Warning — this screen is read-only for staff

Nothing on the dashboard can be edited from here. Changes to a subscription, wallet, rewards or bottles happen on their own pages (and, for staff, in the Admin modules). If a KPI looks wrong, correct the underlying record in the right module — the dashboard will recompute the next time it loads.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common questions Support hears about the customer dashboard.

Purpose Fast answers for the help desk.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager Accountant Path My Account → Overview → Dashboard
SymptomLikely causeFix
All KPI cards show "—", zero or "No active plan"New account with no orders yet, OR backend was unreachable at loadCheck for the connectivity banner. If none, it's simply an empty account — ask the customer to place a first order/subscription. If the banner is present, treat as an outage and retry.
Greeting still says the seeded name / wrong nameProfile name not returned yet, or profile not updatedHave the customer refresh; if the name is wrong, correct it on the Profile page — the greeting reads the profile name.
Pure Rewards card is missing entirelyCustomer signed out, or the rewards programme is disabledConfirm the customer is signed in; check the loyalty programme is enabled in the admin Loyalty configuration.
Puzzle card is missingNo challenge configured for the current month, or the feature is offConfirm a challenge is scheduled/active in the admin Puzzle module — the card only shows when one is live.
"Per delivery" / "Renews" rows are absent from the subscription cardThose figures weren't supplied by the live subscriptionExpected behaviour — the rows are hidden rather than guessed. The exact price and renewal date are always on the Subscription page.
Kicked to the login page with "?expired=1"Session expired or was revoked (e.g. after a password reset)Ask the customer to sign in again; the dashboard will reload with fresh data.
Wallet / points on the dashboard differ from the Wallet / Rewards pageDashboard uses the cached summary from the last loadRefresh the dashboard; both pages read the same backend, so a reload reconciles them.

Is the customer dashboard the same as the admin dashboard?

No. This is the customer's own "My Account" home. The admin operations dashboard is a separate screen in the Admin surface with business-wide KPIs and charts.

Can a staff member change anything from this screen?

No. The dashboard is a read-only summary. All edits happen on the dedicated pages or in the Admin modules; the dashboard recomputes from those records on the next load.

Why does "Upcoming Delivery" always say "before 7 AM"?

DOODLY runs a single early-morning delivery run, so the promise is a cut-off ("before 7 AM"), not a time slot. The card shows the next scheduled delivery's date with that promise, or "No delivery scheduled" when there is none.

Why can the same customer see different tiles than another customer?

Cards are data-driven. The Pure Rewards and Puzzle cards only appear when their programmes are enabled and relevant; the subscription card switches to an empty state without an active plan; and captions like "N unpaid invoices" reflect that customer's real records.

DOODLYCustomer Module · My Orders
§Customer Module

My Orders

Every order the signed-in customer has ever placed with DOODLY — searchable, filterable and drillable — plus a live panel to rate delivered orders and earn loyalty points.

ℹ️
Where this screen lives

My Orders is a customer self-service page, not an admin screen. Each customer sees only their own orders — the backend scopes every query to the signed-in userId, so one customer can never see another's orders here. It is documented in this manual so a Super Admin knows exactly what the customer is looking at during a support call. Route /account/orders.html, manifest key account/orders. The page is assembled from three blocks: a page head, a table (dataset orders), and a reviewsPanel. To see or manage everyone's orders, a Super Admin uses Admin → Commerce → Orders instead.

⚠️
Two data modes — this matters for support

When nobody is signed in with a real account, the page renders the sample "exploration" dataset (mock orders) so the site stays browsable. The moment a genuine customer is signed in, a startup routine (hydrateAccount) wipes the demo seed and refills the table from the customer's real orders via GET /api/orders?limit=100. If a customer reports "these aren't my orders", first confirm they are actually logged in — a logged-out browser only ever shows demo data.

My Orders — order history table

The full list of the customer's orders with search, filters, sort, export and pagination.

Purpose Give the customer one place to see every order they have placed — its number, date, items, amount paid and current status — and to open any order for full detail and self-service actions (cancel, repeat, report an issue, rate).
Access Customer (own account only — the list is built from orders scoped to the signed-in user). A Super Admin or Admin can view the same page while signed in as a customer, but cannot see another customer's orders here — platform-wide orders live under Admin → Commerce → Orders. Path My Account → Orders & plans → My Orders
Header — My Orders · action + New subscription
Page headTitle "My Orders", sub-line "Every order you've placed with DOODLY.", and a primary button "New subscription" linking to /subscriptions.html.
Table toolbarInstant search box, Status filter, date-range picker, sort, saved views, favourites, export (CSV / Excel / PDF), refresh and clear/reset.
Filter chipsRemovable chips showing every active filter (e.g. "Status: Active", "This Month"); a "Clear all" clears them at once.
Order history tableFive columns — Order, Date, Items, Amount, Status — one row per order. Each row is clickable to open the order detail modal.
Status badgeColour-coded pill per row: Active (green), Completed (grey), Refunded (red), Cancelled (red).
Pagination barPage-size selector (10 / 25 / 50 / 100, default 25), page numbers and total count.
Empty stateWhen there are no orders: a box icon, "No orders yet", helper text, and buttons "Browse products" and "Start a subscription".
Reviews panelBelow the table — the "Rate your deliveries" panel (documented as Screen 3). Hidden when there is nothing to rate.
Figure — labelled layout of the My Orders page for a signed-in customer.

Table columns

The orders table has a fixed set of columns. Values are formatted on the client from the raw order records loaded at page start.

ColumnWhat it showsBackend sourceExample
OrderThe human-readable order number (falls back to the raw id if no number exists).number = DOO-<last 6 of id>DOO-4F9A2C
DateThe date the order was placed, formatted "DD Mon YYYY".createdAt08 Jul 2026
ItemsA short summary of up to three line items ("qty× product variant"). Falls back to "Trial Pack" for sample orders or "Subscription delivery" for subscription orders.itemsSummary2× A2 Buffalo Milk 500ml
AmountThe total the customer paid, in rupees (bold).totalPaise ÷ 100₹120
StatusA colour-coded status pill (see the status table below).derived from cancelled, paymentStatus, fulfilmentActive

What each Status means

The status pill is computed on the client in a fixed priority order — the first rule that matches wins. There are exactly four possible pills:

PriorityStatus pillColourConditionMeaning to the customer
1CancelledRedthe order has a cancelledAt timestampThe order was cancelled (by the customer or by the business).
2RefundedRedpaymentStatus = REFUNDEDThe payment was refunded (a cancelled paid order is refunded to the DOODLY Wallet).
3CompletedGreyfulfilment = DELIVEREDThe order has been delivered.
4ActiveGreennone of the aboveThe order is placed and still moving through the pipeline (processing, confirmed, preparing, out for delivery, etc.).

Table specification — the unified DataTable engine

Like every DOODLY table, My Orders is upgraded by the shared DataTable engine (DOODLY_TABLE). It reuses the raw order rows already in memory, so search / sort / filter are instant and need no extra server calls.

CapabilityBehaviour on this screen
ColumnsOrder, Date, Items, Amount, Status (five, fixed).
SearchOne instant search box; matches across every visible cell (order number, item summary, amount, status text).
FiltersAn auto-detected Status facet (the manifest lists All / Active / Completed / Refunded). Only the statuses that actually occur in the customer's data appear as choices; each shows a count.
Date rangeFilters on the order Date column. Presets: All time, Today, Yesterday, This Week, Last Week, This Month, Last Month, Last 30 Days, Last 90 Days, This Quarter, Last Quarter, This Year, Last Year, plus a Custom Range.
SortingClick a column header to sort; multi-column sort supported. Amount sorts numerically; Date sorts chronologically.
PaginationPage sizes 10 / 25 / 50 / 100 (default 25) with page numbers and a total row count.
Saved views & favouritesThe customer can save a filter combination and star favourite rows; both are stored per-user in the browser (localStorage). The active search / filter / sort / page-size also persists across reloads.
ExportsCSV, Excel and PDF of the current (filtered) view. Export honours the RBAC "export" permission; on the customer account surface there is no module gate, so a signed-in customer may export their own order list.
Row actionsNo inline buttons in a row. Clicking anywhere on a row (except a link or button) opens the order detail modal — see Screen 2.
ℹ️
How the data is loaded

On page start, hydrateAccount fetches GET /api/orders?limit=100. The backend caps limit at 50, so up to the 50 most recent orders (newest first) are loaded. All search, filtering and paging then happen in the browser over that set. The backend also supports server-side paymentStatus, type, date from/to, free-text q, cancelled and sort parameters, but the customer page relies on the client DataTable for those controls.

✅
Best Practice

When a customer calls about a specific order, ask them to read out the Order number (DOO-XXXXXX). It is the fastest key — it matches their row here, the order id in the backend, and the invoice. Then have them click that row to open the detail modal, which carries the timeline and self-service actions.

Order detail modal

Opens when the customer clicks an order row — full breakdown, timeline and self-service actions.

Purpose Let the customer see the complete detail of one order (status, line items, money breakdown, event timeline, invoice number) and act on it — cancel, repeat, report an issue, or rate the delivery — all against the live backend.
Access Customer (own order only — every action is authorised against the signed-in userId). The modal only wires up for a genuine signed-in customer; in demo mode the rows are not clickable. Path My Account → Orders & plans → My Orders → (click a row)
Modal — Order DOO-XXXXXX · close ✕
Header"Order <number>" title with a close (✕) button.
Summary rowsStatus (+ payment status), Placed (date & time), Delivery (date · slot, when present), Invoice number (when present).
Items listEach line item as "qty× product variant" with its line total, then Subtotal, Discount, Bottle deposit and a bold Total.
TimelineA dotted vertical list of order events (title, optional note, date & time), oldest to newest — or "No events yet."
Action barButtons: Cancel order (only when still cancellable), Report issue, Rate, and a primary Repeat order.
Figure — labelled layout of the order detail modal (from GET /api/orders/[id]).

Fields shown in the modal

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
StatusThe fulfilment stage of the order plus the payment status.Processing, Confirmed, Preparing, Quality checked, Packed, Out for delivery, Arriving soon, Delivered, Cancelled · payment PENDING / PAID / FAILED / REFUNDEDYesRead-only. fulfilment derived from the latest order event or the linked delivery; payment from paymentStatus.Out for delivery · payment PAID
PlacedWhen the order was created.date & timeYesRead-only. createdAt.08 Jul, 6:40 AM
DeliveryThe scheduled delivery date and slot, when the order has a linked delivery.date · slotOpt.Read-only. Shown only when a delivery date is present.09 Jul · Before 7 AM
InvoiceThe invoice number, when one has been generated.textOpt.Read-only. invoice.number.DOODLY/2026/00142
ItemsEvery line item with its line total.listYesRead-only. From items[] (qty, product name, variant, line total).2× A2 Buffalo Milk 500ml — ₹120
Money breakdownSubtotal, Discount, Bottle deposit (refundable) and a bold Total.currencyYesRead-only. Discount and deposit rows appear only when non-zero.Subtotal ₹120 · Total ₹120
TimelineThe chronological event history of the order.listOpt.Read-only. From OrderEvent records; "No events yet." when empty.Order placed · Out for delivery
ℹ️
The backend returns more than the modal shows

GET /api/orders/[id] also returns the delivery driver's name and phone, the delivery address, the payment method, wallet transactions tied to the order, and any bottle-ledger entries for the delivery. The current customer modal displays the subset above; the extra data is available for support tooling and richer views.

Buttons and actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Cancel orderAsks "Cancel this order? Paid amounts are refunded to your DOODLY Wallet." then cancels it. Shown only when the order is still cancellable (not already cancelled and not yet Out for delivery / Arriving / Delivered).POST /api/orders/[id] {action:"cancel"} → marks cancelledAt, sets any linked delivery to SKIPPED, logs a "Order cancelled" event. If the order was PAID, its total is credited back to the wallet as a refund transaction and the order becomes REFUNDED. Audited as order.cancel. Page reloads.Customer
Report issuePrompts "Describe the issue with this order" and records the customer's note (up to 500 characters).POST /api/orders/[id] {action:"report", issue} → adds an "Issue reported" note event on the order. Modal closes with a thank-you toast.Customer
RatePrompts "Rate this delivery (1–5 stars)" and records a quick star rating.POST /api/orders/[id] {action:"rate", rating} → adds a "Delivery rated" note event (★ out of 5). This is a lightweight rating on the order timeline — distinct from the points-earning verified review in Screen 3.Customer
Repeat orderRe-creates the same order as a fresh one-time order (primary action, always shown).POST /api/orders/[id] {action:"reorder"} → duplicates the line items and amounts into a new PENDING one-time order, logs "Order placed / Reordered from <number>". Audited as order.reorder. Page reloads so the new order appears in the list.Customer
Workflow — Cancel & refund a paid order
Click Cancel orderConfirm promptServer checks it is not already on its way / deliveredOrder marked cancelled · delivery skippedPaid total credited to Wallet (order → Refunded)Page reloads · status shows Refunded/Cancelled
Figure — what happens when a customer cancels a paid order.
⚠️
Cancellation is time-boxed

An order can no longer be cancelled once it is Out for delivery, Arriving soon or Delivered (or the linked delivery has moved to On the way / Reached / Delivered). The server enforces this and returns "This order is already on its way or delivered and can't be cancelled. Please contact support." — the Cancel button is also hidden in those states. There is no self-service hard delete of an order; cancelling is the terminal customer action.

💡
Tip — refunds land in the Wallet, not the card

Cancelling a paid order refunds the full total to the customer's DOODLY Wallet (a wallet credit), not back to the original card. If a customer expects a card refund, that has to be handled by support/admin — set the right expectation on the call.

Rate your deliveries — verified reviews panel

A panel below the table where the customer reviews delivered orders and earns loyalty points.

Purpose Turn a completed order into a verified product review. The customer rates delivered orders (1–5 stars, optional title and comment); the review goes through admin moderation and, once verified, earns DOODLY Pure Rewards points — once per delivered order.
Access Customer (own orders only). The server verifies order ownership, that the order is PAID, and that it was actually delivered before it accepts a review. Path My Account → Orders & plans → My Orders → Rate your deliveries
Panel — Rate your deliveries · tag ⭐ +N points per review
Panel header"Rate your deliveries" with a points-per-review tag when the loyalty programme is enabled.
Reviewable order rowFor each delivered, not-yet-reviewed order: the order number, its item label and placed date.
Star pickerFive clickable stars (1–5). The Submit button stays disabled until a rating is chosen.
Title fieldOptional short title, up to 120 characters.
Comment fieldOptional longer comment, up to 1000 characters.
Submit reviewSends the review; a toast confirms and shows any points awarded.
Your reviews listUp to 8 of the customer's past reviews: stars, target, comment, a status badge and the date, each with an Edit button.
All-reviewed stateWhen every delivered order is reviewed: "All your delivered orders are reviewed — thank you!"
Figure — labelled layout of the "Rate your deliveries" panel (GET /api/account/reviews).

Review form fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Rating (stars)The 1–5 star score for the delivered order.integer 1–5YesSubmit is disabled until a star is picked; server enforces an integer 1–5.5
TitleAn optional short headline for the review.text ≤ 120 charsOpt.Trimmed; max 120 characters (client and server).Rich and fresh
CommentAn optional longer note about the order.text ≤ 1000 charsOpt.Trimmed; max 1000 characters on the form.Delivered before 7 AM, sealed well.

Review status badges (Your reviews)

BadgeColourMeaning
PENDINGAmberSubmitted and waiting for admin moderation — not yet public.
APPROVEDGreenVerified by an admin — eligible to show publicly.
REJECTEDRedReviewed by an admin and not published.
HIDDENGreyApproved earlier but currently hidden from the public site.

Buttons and actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
★ (star pick)Sets the rating 1–5 and enables Submit.Client-side only.Customer
Submit reviewSubmits the rating, title and comment for that order.POST /api/account/reviews {orderId, rating, title, comment} → server verifies the order is the customer's own, PAID and delivered; enforces one review per product; saves it as PENDING (moderation) and awards loyalty points once per order (idempotent). A 1–2★ review also alerts admins/support to follow up. Toast confirms points.Customer
EditTurns a past review row into an editor with the star picker, title and comment pre-filled.Client-side (opens the editor).Customer
Save changesSaves the edited review.PATCH /api/account/reviews {reviewId, rating, title, comment} → updates the review, resets its status to PENDING (edits go back through moderation), un-features it and recomputes the product's public rating.Customer
CancelDiscards the in-progress edit and re-renders the panel.Client-side only.Customer
Workflow — Verified review → points
Pick 1–5 starsAdd optional title/commentSubmit reviewServer verifies own · paid · deliveredSaved as PENDING (moderation)Points awarded once per orderAdmin approves → shows publicly
Figure — how a delivered order becomes a public, points-earning review.
ℹ️
"Rate" in the modal vs. this panel

The Rate button in the order detail modal (Screen 2) just adds a quick "Delivery rated" note to the order timeline. This Rate your deliveries panel is the real, verified-review flow: it is server-verified, moderated, feeds the public product rating and earns loyalty points. When a customer asks "why didn't my rating give me points?", they most likely used the modal Rate button — point them here.

ℹ️
One review per product

A customer can review each product only once. Re-buying the same product does not open a new review slot — they edit the existing review instead (which sends it back through moderation). The panel stays completely hidden when there is nothing reviewable and no past reviews.

✅
Best Practice

When encouraging reviews on a support call, remind the customer that points are awarded once per delivered order and only after the review passes moderation. Editing an approved review resets it to PENDING, so it temporarily drops off the public product rating until re-approved — set that expectation before they edit.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common My Orders questions a Super Admin will field.

SymptomLikely causeFix
"These orders aren't mine / they look fake."The customer is not signed in, so the page shows the demo exploration dataset.Have them log in at the customer login. Once signed in, hydrateAccount replaces the demo data with their real orders.
Order list is empty with a warning banner.The customer is signed in but the backend was unreachable or slow to respond (genuine offline/timeout).Retry / reload. If it persists, check backend availability — the page intentionally shows an honest empty state rather than demo data.
An old order is missing from the list.Only the 50 most recent orders are loaded (the request asks for 100 but the server caps at 50).Look the order up by number under Admin → Commerce → Orders, which is not capped for staff.
Clicking a row does nothing.Row-click detail only wires up for a real signed-in customer; the row also ignores clicks that land on a link or button.Confirm the customer is signed in and clicking the row body (not a link).
"I can't cancel my order."The order is already Out for delivery, Arriving or Delivered, so the Cancel button is hidden and the server rejects it.Handle it through support — self-service cancellation is closed once an order is on its way.
"My review isn't showing on the product page."New and edited reviews are PENDING until an admin approves them.Approve it under Admin → Reviews. Only approved, verified reviews appear publicly.
"I rated my delivery but got no points."They used the modal Rate button (a timeline note), not the verified review panel.Direct them to Rate your deliveries below the table; only that flow earns points.

Can a customer delete an order from this page?

No. There is no delete. The only terminal customer action is Cancel order, and only while the order is still cancellable. A cancelled paid order is refunded to the wallet and shown as Refunded/Cancelled.

Where does a refund go when an order is cancelled?

To the customer's DOODLY Wallet as a credit (a refund transaction), and the order's payment status becomes REFUNDED. It is not returned to the original card automatically.

What is the difference between "Repeat order" and starting a new subscription?

"Repeat order" duplicates that exact order as a one-time PENDING order for immediate re-purchase. The header's "New subscription" button instead sends the customer to /subscriptions.html to set up a recurring plan.

DOODLYCustomer Module · My Subscription & History
§Customer Module

My Subscription & History

The customer's self-service home for their recurring milk plan — see the live plan, pause or resume it, skip a day, switch auto-renew on or off, manage AutoPay, and read a timeline of every change and renewal.

ℹ️
Where this lives

These are customer-facing account pages served from the storefront (/account/subscription.html and /account/subscription-history.html). As a Super Admin you will use them when helping a customer over the phone, or after switching into a customer view. To manage anyone's subscription from the back office, use Admin → Commerce → Subscriptions (/admin/subscriptions.html) — a separate module. Every page here loads the signed-in customer's own data only; the panels stay blank (honest empty states) until a real account is signed in.

My Subscription — plan overview & controls

The customer's live recurring plan with one-tap lifecycle actions.

Purpose Show the active plan (bottle, schedule, price, renewal) and let the customer pause, resume, skip the next delivery, toggle auto-renew, or cancel — all against their own subscription record.
Access Customer (signed-in, own account) · Super Admin when viewing as the customer Path Account → Orders & plans → My Subscription
Header — My Subscription · "Manage your plan, bottle size and delivery." · Pause button (top-right → Pause / Vacation)
Your live planStatus chip (Active / Paused until… / Cancelled) + plan detail list + action buttons. Injected right under the header for a signed-in customer.
AutoPay panelRight column — live Razorpay mandate: next renewal, amount, retry attempts, pause / resume / retry / cancel.
Manage — quick tilesFour shortcuts: Pause / Vacation · Extra milk · Change plan · Cancel.
Auto-renew noticeReminder strip: "Your subscription auto-renews. You can pause, skip a day, or cancel anytime before the renewal date."
Figure — labelled layout of the My Subscription screen (signed-in customer)
ℹ️
Two rendering paths

For a real signed-in customer the page fetches GET /api/account/subscription and draws a live "Your live plan" panel plus the real AutoPay mandate. For a guest / demo preview (not signed in) it instead shows a local sample "Active subscription" card so the layout can be explored. This manual documents the live behaviour, which is what a Super Admin sees when assisting a customer.

The "Your live plan" panel — fields shown

Read straight from the customer's subscription record. Every row is display-only; the buttons below the list drive the changes.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Status chipCurrent state of the planActive · Paused (with "until" date) · the raw status for other statesYesFrom status (SubStatus: ACTIVE, PAUSED, VACATION, CANCELLED, COMPLETED); "Paused" is shown for VACATION with pausedUntil● Active
PlanThe subscription plan nameText (from the Plan catalogue)Yesplan.name via the linked PlanDaily 30-day
BottleWhat is delivered each timequantity × variant label + productYesFirst subscription item: qty × items[0].label items[0].product2× 500 ml A2 Buffalo Milk
Next deliveryWhen the next bottle arrives + slotDate · time-slot textOpt.nextDeliveryAt + deliverySlot (default 06:00-08:00); "—" if none scheduled13 Jul 2026 · 06:00-08:00
Per deliveryAmount charged for one deliveryRupee amountYesComputed perDeliveryPaise = Σ(item qty × variant dailyPaise), formatted to ₹₹110
EndsPlan end / expiry dateDate or "—"Opt.endDate (set when cancelled or plan term ends)12 Aug 2026
Auto-renewWhether the plan renews automaticallyOn / OffYesautoRenew booleanOn
Skipped daysHow many upcoming deliveries are skippedCount (row hidden when zero)Opt.Length of the skipDates array1

Buttons on the "Your live plan" panel

Which buttons appear depends on the current status. Every action calls POST /api/account/subscription with the subscription id and an action, then re-renders the panel with the fresh record.

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Pause (only when Active)Asks "Pause for how many days?" (1–90). Deliveries resume automatically on the return date.action:"pause" with until = now + N days → status VACATION, sets pausedFrom = now and pausedUntil = untilCustomer
Skip next delivery (only when Active)Confirms, then skips just the next scheduled delivery — "Skipped days are never charged."action:"skip" → pushes nextDeliveryAt (or supplied date) onto the skipDates array; errors if there is no upcoming deliveryCustomer
Resume deliveries (only when Paused / Vacation)Ends the pause and restarts deliveries.action:"resume" → status ACTIVE, clears pausedFrom and pausedUntilCustomer
Turn auto-renew on / offToggles automatic renewal. Label flips to match current state.action:"autopay_on" or "autopay_off" → sets autoRenew true / falseCustomer
Cancel plan (hidden once Cancelled)Confirms "Cancel your subscription? You can start a new plan anytime.", then cancels.action:"cancel" → status CANCELLED, stamps endDate = now, sets autoRenew = falseCustomer
⛔
Caution — Cancel plan is a one-way action

"Cancel plan" sets the subscription to CANCELLED and turns auto-renew off. There is no "un-cancel" — the customer must start a fresh subscription from /subscriptions.html. When in doubt, use Pause instead: it stops (and un-charges) deliveries but keeps the plan alive.

The action request (POST /api/account/subscription)

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
idWhich subscription to act onSubscription idYesNon-empty string; must belong to the signed-in user or a 404 is returnedclx8k2…
actionWhat to dopause · resume · cancel · skip · autopay_on · autopay_offYesEnum-validated (Zod); anything else is rejectedpause
untilVacation end date (pause only)ISO date-timeOpt.Stored as pausedUntil; null if omitted2026-07-20T00:00:00Z
dateWhich delivery to skip (skip only)ISO date-timeOpt.Defaults to nextDeliveryAt when omitted2026-07-13T00:00:00Z
ℹ️
Every change is audited

The backend writes an audit record on each action — subscription.pause, subscription.resume, subscription.cancel, subscription.skip, subscription.autopay_on/off — tagged with the customer's user id and role. These feed the enterprise Audit Trail, so a Super Admin can always see who changed a plan and when.

Header & "Manage" quick tiles

Button / tileWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Pause (header, top-right)Opens the Pause / Vacation page to schedule a pause window.Navigation → /account/vacation.html (the pause form posts action:"pause" with the chosen resume date)Customer
Pause / Vacation (tile)Same as above — going-away pause with auto-resume.→ /account/vacation.htmlCustomer
Extra milk (tile)Add a one-off extra bottle to tomorrow's delivery (guests over).→ /account/extra-milk.htmlCustomer
Change plan (tile)Pick a different bottle / plan.→ /subscriptions.htmlCustomer
Cancel (tile)Shortcut back to this page, where the live "Cancel plan" button performs the real cancellation.→ /account/subscription.html (the actual cancel runs through action:"cancel")Customer
💡
Tip

The Cancel tile only navigates back to this page — it does not cancel anything by itself. The cancellation only happens when the customer clicks the red Cancel plan button on the live plan panel and confirms. Point customers to that button if they say "I clicked Cancel but nothing happened."

AutoPay settings panel

The live Razorpay recurring mandate attached to the subscription.

Purpose Show and control hands-free renewals: next charge date, amount, retry attempts, and the ability to pause, resume, retry, change method, view history, or cancel the mandate.
Access Customer (own AutoPay) · Super Admin when assisting Path Account → My Subscription → AutoPay (right column)
Panel — AutoPay · status badge
Mandate rowsPlan · Next renewal · Renewal amount · Retry attempts.
Failure alertShown when the last renewal failed (RETRY / SUSPENDED) with a "retry it now" prompt.
Action buttonsPause · Resume · Retry payment · Change method · View history · Cancel.
Payment historyCollapsible list of past renewal charges with success / failed dots.
Figure — AutoPay mandate panel on the My Subscription screen

This panel is fed by GET /api/subscriptions/autopay and is fully documented in the AutoPay (Subscription Billing) section — here is the customer-side summary as it appears on this page.

Row / controlWhat it shows or doesBackend processPermission
PlanPlan / plan-slug the mandate renewsRead from the mandate (plan / planSlug)Customer
Next renewalDate of the next automatic chargenextRenewalAtCustomer
Renewal amountRupee amount that will be chargedamountPaise → ₹Customer
Retry attemptsHow many times a failed charge has been retriedattemptsCustomer
Pause AutoPay (Active)Stops automatic charging temporarilyPATCH …/autopay {action:"pause"}Customer
Resume AutoPay (Inactive / Suspended)Restarts automatic chargingPATCH …/autopay {action:"resume"}Customer
Retry payment (Retry / Suspended)Re-attempts the failed renewal charge nowPATCH …/autopay {action:"retry"}Customer
Change payment methodExplains that the card/UPI can't be swapped silently — cancel and re-enable at next checkout so the bank re-authorises the mandateShows an inline note only (no API call)Customer
View payment historyExpands the list of past renewal charges (amount, status, date)Reads the mandate's renewals[]Customer
Cancel AutoPayConfirms, then cancels the mandate — subscription stops renewing automaticallyDELETE …/autopay?id=<gatewaySubId>Customer
ℹ️
When AutoPay isn't set up

If the subscription has no mandate the panel shows a friendly "AutoPay isn't set up for this subscription" state with a Start / renew a subscription button. AutoPay can only be enabled at checkout (the bank must authorise the e-mandate), so there is no "enable" button on this page.

Subscription History

A read-only timeline of every plan change and renewal.

Purpose Give the customer (and support staff) a chronological record — started, paused, resumed, cancelled, renewed, cashback, refunds — built from their own subscription data. Never fabricated.
Access Customer (own history) · Super Admin when assisting Path Account → Orders & plans → Subscription History
Header — Subscription History · "A record of every plan change and renewal."
Timeline panelNewest-first list of lifecycle events, each with a coloured dot (done / active / paused / warning) and a date.
Empty state"No subscription history yet" — shown until the customer has ever subscribed.
Figure — the Subscription History timeline
Workflow — how the timeline is built
GET /api/account/subscription-historyDerive lifecycle from each subscription (started / active / paused / cancelled)Add explicit events (RENEWED · RESUMED · RESCHEDULED · CASHBACK · REFUND)Sort newest-firstRender dots & dates — or keep empty state
Figure — data flow for Subscription History

Timeline event types

EventMeaningDot / styleSource
Started — {plan}The subscription began; shows the per-delivery priceDone (check)Derived from startDate
Active — {plan}Currently running; shows the next delivery dateActive (highlight)Derived from ACTIVE status
Paused — {plan}Plan was paused (Vacation Mode)NeutralDerived from VACATION + pausedFrom
Cancelled — {plan}Plan was cancelledWarning (amber/red)Derived from CANCELLED + endDate
Renewed / Resumed / RescheduledExplicit lifecycle eventsDoneSubscriptionEvent rows (RENEWED, RESUMED, RESCHEDULED)
Cashback / RefundMoney movement tied to the planWarning for refundSubscriptionEvent rows (CASHBACK, REFUND)
ℹ️
Honest empty state

If the customer has never subscribed, the page keeps its seeded "No subscription history yet" message rather than inventing records. The client also has a fallback: if the dedicated history endpoint is unavailable it rebuilds a basic timeline from the plain subscription list, so the page never breaks.

✅
Best Practice

When a customer wants to stop deliveries for a trip, always steer them to Pause (with a resume date) rather than Cancel. Pause keeps their plan, pricing and AutoPay intact and auto-resumes on the chosen date; Cancel is permanent and forces a fresh signup. For a single day off, use Skip next delivery — it removes just that day and never charges for it.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"You don't have a subscription yet" on the pageThe signed-in account has no subscription recordStart one at /subscriptions.html; the live plan panel appears once a subscription exists.
Pause button won't accept the daysValue entered is outside 1–90Enter a whole number between 1 and 90 days.
"No upcoming delivery to skip"No nextDeliveryAt is scheduled (paused, ended, or none yet)Resume the plan or wait for the next scheduled delivery before skipping.
AutoPay panel shows "Not set up"No recurring mandate on this subscriptionEnable AutoPay at the next checkout — it can't be turned on from this page (bank must authorise the e-mandate).
Can't change the AutoPay card hereRazorpay mandates can't be swapped silentlyCancel AutoPay, then re-enable it at the next checkout so the bank re-authorises the new card/UPI.
Subscription History is blankCustomer has never subscribed (honest empty state)This is expected; events appear after the first subscription and each change.

Frequently asked questions

Q. What is the difference between "Cancel plan" and "Turn auto-renew off"?

A. "Turn auto-renew off" keeps the plan running for its remaining term but stops it renewing at the end. "Cancel plan" stops the subscription immediately (status CANCELLED, end date set today) and also turns auto-renew off — it cannot be undone.

Q. Will the customer be charged for a skipped or paused day?

A. No. Skipped days are added to the skipDates list and are never charged, and a paused (Vacation) plan makes no deliveries and no charges until it resumes.

Q. Can a Super Admin change a customer's plan from here?

A. Only by acting as that customer (their own account). To manage subscriptions across all customers from the back office, use Admin → Commerce → Subscriptions instead.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Deliveries & Live Tracking
§Customer Module

Deliveries & Live Tracking

The customer's own record of every milk delivery — upcoming, delivered and skipped — plus a live, minute-by-minute tracking screen that follows the bottle from the farm to the doorstep.

ℹ️
Who sees this

These are two customer-facing account screens (/account/deliveries.html and /account/tracking.html). Every request is scoped strictly to the signed-in customer — the backend filters on subscription.userId OR order.userId, so one customer can never see another customer's deliveries. A Super Admin does not open these pages for a customer directly; the same delivery records are managed operationally under Admin → Operations → Delivery Mgmt. This section documents exactly what the customer experiences so support staff can guide them accurately.

Deliveries

A searchable, filterable ledger of the customer's upcoming and past deliveries.

Purpose Give the customer a complete, self-service history of every delivery tied to their subscriptions and one-off orders, with a quality summary and one tap to live tracking.
Access Customer (own account only) · Super Admin reviews the same data via Delivery Mgmt Path My Account → Deliveries → Deliveries
Header — Deliveries · "Upcoming and past deliveries." · Track live button
Page headTitle, sub-line and a primary "Track live" button (pin icon) that opens the Delivery Tracking screen
Your delivery qualitySmall stat strip — Deliveries · On-time · Late · Avg time · Apologies — for the last 30 days
Deliveries tableOrder · Date · Slot · Products · Executive · Status — with search, filters, sort, export and pagination
ToolbarSearch box, Filters panel (auto-detected on Status), Export ▾ (CSV / Excel / PDF)
Empty stateTruck icon, "No deliveries yet", with "Browse products" and "Subscribe now" buttons
Figure 1 — Labelled layout of the Deliveries screen (page head, quality strip, data table).

The "Your delivery quality" strip

Directly under the page head, the lateCustomerStats block mounts a compact card titled "Your delivery quality — last 30 days". For a real signed-in customer it is computed live from their own hydrated deliveries (_rawDeliveries) measured against DOODLY's 7:00 AM delivery promise; it never shows the demo sample once a real customer is logged in.

StatWhat it meansHow it is calculated
DeliveriesTotal deliveries on record for the customerCount of all rows in _rawDeliveries
On-timeDelivered at or before 7:00 AMDelivered rows whose deliveredAt time is ≤ 07:00 (green tone)
LateDelivered after the 7:00 AM promiseDelivered rows with deliveredAt after 07:00 (red tone when > 0)
Avg timeAverage clock time deliveries actually arrivedMean of all delivered deliveredAt times, shown as HH:MM
ApologiesApology messages auto-sent for late arrivalsEqual to the Late count — DOODLY sends one apology per late delivery
ℹ️
Friendly note under the strip

The card ends with a sentence that adapts to the numbers: if there are no deliveries yet it reads "Your delivery quality will appear here once your deliveries begin."; if there were late deliveries it names how many apology messages were sent; if everything was on time it reads "Every delivery arrived before our 7:00 AM promise. 🥛".

The Deliveries table

The main block is the unified DataTable engine bound to the deliveries dataset. Each row is one delivery. The data is fetched from GET /api/deliveries (newest-first, up to 180 rows) and hydrated into the table by hydrateAccount().

ColumnWhat it showsSource field / mappingExample
OrderThe order or plan the delivery belongs toorderRef (e.g. DOO-3F9A21) → else plan name → else "#" + last 6 of the delivery idDOO-3F9A21
DateThe delivery date, in plain wordsRelative label from date: Today / Tomorrow / Yesterday, otherwise "DD Mon"Tomorrow
SlotWhen it was (or will be) deliveredDelivered time from deliveredAt; else the slot; else "—"6:40 AM
ProductsWhat was delivereditemsSummary (order items or subscription items) → else plan name → else "—"A2 Buffalo Milk 1000 ml
ExecutiveThe delivery executive who handled itdriver.name → else "—" when unassignedRamesh K.
StatusColour-coded delivery stateBadge derived from the delivery status (see status table below)Scheduled

Status badges

The raw backend DeliveryStatus is translated into a friendly, colour-coded badge (the DEL_STATUS map). Support staff should know every mapping:

Badge (colour)Meaning to the customerBackend status(es)
Scheduled (amber)Booked, not yet startedSCHEDULED (and any unmapped state)
Assigned (blue)A delivery executive has been allocatedASSIGNED
Preparing (blue)Accepted and packed at the hubACCEPTED, PACKED
Out for delivery (amber)On the way to the doorstepOUT_FOR_DELIVERY, ON_THE_WAY, REACHED
Delivered (green)Completed successfullyDELIVERED
Skipped (grey)Skipped for that day (e.g. a pause)SKIPPED
Failed (red)Could not be completedFAILED

Table controls (the DataTable engine)

The Deliveries table is powered by the shared DataTable engine, so it carries the same rich controls as every other DOODLY list.

ControlBehaviour
SearchInstant free-text search across every visible column (placeholder "Search deliveries…").
FiltersFacet filters are auto-detected from enum-like columns — in practice the Status column, so the customer can tick Scheduled / Delivered / Skipped / Out for delivery, etc. (The screen's configured filter hint is All · Scheduled · Delivered · Skipped.) Active filters show as removable chips.
SortClick any column header to sort; multi-column sort is supported.
Date rangeA date-range preset picker plus a custom From/To range (auto-enabled because the Date column is a date field).
Saved filters & favoritesThe customer's search/filter/sort/page-size view is remembered per user in local storage; saved filters and favorites are available.
ExportExport ▾ menu offers CSV (.csv), Excel (.xls) and PDF. (Export is permission-gated for admin tables; on the customer's own deliveries it is available.)
PaginationPaged list, default 25 rows per page, with a pager below the table.

Buttons on this screen

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Track live (page head)Opens the Delivery Tracking screen for the customer's active deliveryNavigates to /account/tracking.html (no write)Customer
Export ▾ (CSV / Excel / PDF)Downloads the current (filtered) delivery listClient-side export of the loaded rowsCustomer
Browse products (empty state)Sends a customer with no deliveries to the catalogueNavigates to /products.htmlCustomer
Subscribe now (empty state)Starts a recurring milk subscriptionNavigates to /subscriptions.htmlCustomer
ℹ️
Where the data comes from

GET /api/deliveries joins each Delivery to its subscription/order, and returns: id, date, status, slot, sequence, deliveredAt, bottlesOut, bottlesIn, bottleCount, customerRemark, driver name, order reference, plan name, an items summary and the formatted delivery address. The Deliveries table only surfaces six of these; the rest feed the Tracking and Calendar screens.

✅
Best Practice

When a customer disputes a delivery, ask them to open Deliveries, filter Status to the state in question (e.g. Skipped or Failed) and read out the Order reference (DOO-…). That reference maps one-to-one to the row a Super Admin opens under Delivery Mgmt, so both sides are looking at the exact same delivery.

Delivery Tracking

A live, stage-by-stage view of today's delivery with the executive, an ETA and a moving map.

Purpose Let the customer watch a single delivery progress in real time — from "Scheduled" through "Out for delivery" to "Delivered" — and, while it is en route, see the executive's live position on a map.
Access Customer (own active delivery only) Path My Account → Deliveries → Delivery Tracking
Header — Delivery Tracking · headline updates live with the delivery state
Live headlineSentence that changes with status — "on the way", "arrived", "Delivered … ✓", or a miss notice
Progress timelineFour stages: Scheduled → Packed at the farm → Out for delivery → Delivered (or "Not delivered")
Driver & proof panelExecutive avatar + name, Delivery ID, Status, ETA / Delivered-at time, and bottle counts
Live mapDestination pin plus the executive's moving GPS marker while the delivery is en route
Empty state"Nothing to track yet" until the customer's first delivery begins
Figure 2 — Labelled layout of the Delivery Tracking screen (two columns: timeline + driver/map panel).

Which delivery is shown

The tracking screen (wireTracking()) picks today's delivery from the customer's real rows; if there is none for today it falls back to the newest delivery on record. It then computes a stage index from the delivery status:

Stage indexTimeline node reachedBackend status(es)
0ScheduledSCHEDULED / ASSIGNED (anything not below)
1Packed at the farmPACKED, ACCEPTED
2Out for deliveryOUT_FOR_DELIVERY, ON_THE_WAY, REACHED
3DeliveredDELIVERED

If the status is FAILED or SKIPPED, the final node flips to "Not delivered" (a warning state) — "Skipped for this day" for a skip, or "We missed you — we'll make it right" for a failure.

The live headline

The sentence under the page title reflects the exact state:

SituationHeadline shown
Missed / skipped"We couldn't complete your delivery <day> — our team has been notified."
Delivered"Delivered <day> at <time> ✓"
Executive has arrived (REACHED)"Your delivery executive has arrived 🏠"
On the way"Your bottle is on the way — arriving before 7 AM."
Scheduled"Your next delivery is scheduled <for today / day>, before 7 AM."

The Driver & proof panel

The right-hand panel is rebuilt from the real delivery record:

FieldWhat it showsSource
ExecutiveAvatar (initials) + name, labelled "Your delivery executive"driver.name → "Assigning…" if none yet
Delivery IDShort reference for the delivery"D-" + last 6 of the delivery id (upper-case)
StatusSkipped / Missed / Delivered / On the way / ScheduledDerived from the delivery status + stage
ETA / Delivered at"Delivered at <time>" once done, otherwise "Before 7 AM"deliveredAt time, or the 7 AM promise
Bottles"<n> out · <n> collected" (only when bottle data exists)bottlesOut / bottlesIn

The live map

The map card upgrades from a placeholder to a real map whenever a location is available. It calls GET /api/account/tracking immediately and then re-polls every 20 seconds until the delivery is finished or the customer leaves the page. The endpoint returns:

  • dest — the delivery address coordinates. If the saved address has no pin, the backend geocodes it once (keyless) and caches the result on the Address, so a destination marker appears even without a Google Maps key.
  • driver — the executive's live GPS (lat/lng/lastSeenAt), but only while the delivery is en route (status OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, ON_THE_WAY or REACHED). Otherwise the driver position is never exposed.
  • active, deliveryId, status, enRoute flags that drive the map state.

The map shows a "…is on the way" label with an "updated Ns/Nm ago" freshness stamp derived from lastSeenAt. Polling stops automatically once the delivery is delivered or no longer active (the drawn map stays on screen).

Workflow — a delivery's journey on the Tracking screen
ScheduledPacked at the farmOut for delivery (map goes live)Reached / ArrivedDelivered ✓ (polling stops)
Figure 3 — The stage flow the timeline and headline walk through; the executive's GPS marker only appears from "Out for delivery" onward.
⚠️
Privacy — driver location is gated

The executive's live GPS is returned only for a delivery that is actively out for delivery to that specific customer. It is never shown for scheduled, packed, delivered, skipped or failed deliveries. Do not promise a customer "live driver tracking" before their bottle is on the way.

ℹ️
Related: the Live Order banner

DOODLY also has a rich live-order banner (order-confirmed → preparing → quality check → packed → out for delivery → delivered, with a moving van animation). That banner is mounted on the account dashboard home, not on the Deliveries page. From the Deliveries page the customer reaches live tracking via the Track live button.

✅
Best Practice

If a customer says the map is blank, first confirm their delivery status is actually Out for delivery / On the way / Reached. Before that stage there is intentionally no driver marker — only the destination pin (once the address has coordinates) will show.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common questions support staff field about Deliveries and Live Tracking.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Deliveries table is empty with "No deliveries yet"Customer has never placed an order or started a subscription (no Delivery rows)Guide them to Browse products / Subscribe now; deliveries appear once the first one is scheduled.
Deliveries page shows nothing and a "backend unreachable" bannerAccount data failed to hydrate (offline / cold-start timeout) — the app shows empty, never demo dataRefresh; if it persists check the backend is reachable. The list repopulates once /api/deliveries succeeds.
Executive column shows "—"No delivery executive has been assigned yetNormal before assignment; the name fills in once Auto Assignment / Delivery Mgmt allocates a driver.
Tracking map stays a placeholderDelivery is not en route yet, or the address has no coordinates and geocoding hasn't cached a pinWait until status is Out for delivery; the destination pin appears once the address is geocoded (automatic, keyless).
Tracking shows "Nothing to track yet"Customer has no delivery rows at allSame as the empty Deliveries table — deliveries begin after the first order/subscription.
"Your delivery quality" shows the wrong figuresViewing while not fully signed in (sample) vs. signed-in (real)Ensure the customer is logged in; the strip then uses their own records against the 7:00 AM promise.

Why does a delivery show a time in the Slot column but others show "—"?

The Slot column prefers the actual deliveredAt time once a delivery is completed. Before delivery it shows the scheduled slot; if neither exists it shows "—".

What is the difference between "Skipped" and "Failed"?

Skipped (grey) means the delivery was intentionally not made for that day — for example during a subscription pause. Failed (red) means an attempted delivery could not be completed; the tracking screen tells the customer the team has been notified.

Can a customer track someone else's delivery with a delivery ID?

No. Every delivery and tracking request is filtered to the authenticated user's own subscriptions and orders on the server, so a shared or guessed ID returns nothing for another account.

⛔
Caution — these are read-only customer views

The Deliveries and Tracking screens do not let the customer edit, cancel or reassign a delivery. Any operational change (reassigning an executive, marking delivered, resolving a failed delivery) must be done by staff under Admin → Operations → Delivery Mgmt. Never tell a customer they can change a delivery from these pages.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Delivery Calendar
§Customer Module

Delivery Calendar

A month-at-a-glance grid that shows the signed-in customer every delivery, skip and pause on their own account, painted directly from their real delivery records.

ℹ️
Where this screen lives

The Delivery Calendar is a customer self-service page, not an admin screen. Each customer sees only their own deliveries. It is documented here so a Super Admin understands exactly what the customer is looking at when they call in about a "missing dot" or a "wrong month". Route /account/calendar.html, manifest key account/calendar, block type calendar.

Delivery Calendar — month grid

One printed month showing delivered / missed / upcoming deliveries as coloured day cells.

Purpose Give the customer a single visual view of their delivery history and next scheduled drop, so they can spot at a glance which mornings were delivered, which were skipped or failed, and when milk is coming next.
Access Customer (own account only — the grid is built from deliveries scoped to the signed-in user). A Super Admin or Admin can view the same page while signed in as a customer, or reproduce it during support by impersonating / exploring the account. Path My Account → Deliveries → Calendar
Delivery Calendar — June 2026 · month navigation ‹ Month ›
Page headTitle "Delivery Calendar" and the sub-line "See your deliveries, skips and pauses at a glance."
Month headerCurrent month + year (e.g. "June 2026") on the left; segmented control ‹ / Month / › on the right.
Day-of-week rowSeven single-letter headers: S M T W T F S.
Calendar gridBlank leading cells to align weekday 1, then one numbered cell per real day of the month, colour-coded by delivery status.
Delivered cellGreen cell with a small dot for every DELIVERED day.
Missed / skipped cellAmber cell for FAILED or SKIPPED deliveries.
Next-delivery cellOutlined green box marking the next scheduled delivery day.
Today markerToday's date carries a dark forest outline so the customer can orient instantly.
LegendThree keys: ● Delivered, ● Missed / skipped, ◻ Next delivery.
Figure — labelled layout of the live Delivery Calendar for a signed-in customer.
ℹ️
Two render modes — this matters for support

The page ships a static demo grid (a fixed "June 2026" with sample dots and a paused day) that renders for anyone exploring the site who is not signed in with a real account. The moment a genuine customer is signed in, a live routine (wireCalendar) replaces that whole panel with the customer's real month built from their delivery rows. If a customer says "the calendar shows the wrong month / fake dots", first confirm they are actually logged in — a logged-out browser only ever sees the demo grid.

What every day cell means

The live grid maps each delivery's status onto one day cell. Days with no delivery record stay blank (plain white cells). These are the only states the code produces:

Delivery statusCell appearanceMeaning to the customerBackend source
DELIVEREDGreen cell with a filled dotMilk was delivered that morning.status = DELIVERED
FAILED or SKIPPEDAmber cell (no dot)The delivery could not be completed, or was skipped/paused for that day.status = FAILED / SKIPPED
SCHEDULED, ASSIGNED, ACCEPTED, PACKED, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, ON_THE_WAY, REACHEDGreen cell (no dot)A delivery record exists for that day and is in progress or upcoming.any other live status
Next scheduled deliveryGreen cell with an inset outline boxThe very next delivery due today or later — highlighted so the customer knows when milk arrives next.summary.nextDelivery.date
TodayDark forest outline around the cell"You are here" marker for the current date, layered on top of any status colour.device clock (browser date)
No recordPlain cell, number onlyNothing scheduled or delivered that day.—
⚠️
The "Next delivery" outline only shows when today's cell is not already outlined
