Today's forest outline takes priority over the green next-delivery box on the same cell. So when the next delivery is today, the customer sees the "today" outline rather than the "next delivery" box — this is expected, not a bug.

Buttons and controls

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
‹ (Previous month)Steps the visible month back by one and repaints the grid.None — client-side only. Re-uses the delivery rows already loaded; no new request is sent.Customer
Month (centre)Active-state label indicating the calendar is in month view. It is not a menu — there is no week or day view.None (display only).Customer
› (Next month)Steps the visible month forward by one and repaints the grid.None — client-side only, same loaded data set.Customer
💡
Tip — the calendar has no filters, search, sort, export or pagination

Unlike the admin data tables in this manual, the Delivery Calendar is a pure visual grid. There is nothing to search, no columns to sort, and no export button. Navigation is limited to the ‹ and › month arrows. If a customer needs a line-by-line list they can export, point them to My Account → Deliveries (the Deliveries table) or their Invoices, not the calendar.

Data behind the calendar

The live grid is assembled from two backend calls that fire once when any account page loads (the account hydrator), then cached on the page. The calendar itself sends no requests when you flip months.

DataEndpointFields usedScope / security
Delivery history (each coloured cell)GET /api/deliveriesdate, status (also available: slot, deliveredAt, bottlesOut, bottlesIn, bottleCount, driver.name, orderRef, planName, itemsSummary, address)Strictly scoped to the signed-in user via subscription.userId OR order.userId — one customer can never see another's deliveries. Returns newest-first, up to 180 rows.
Next scheduled delivery (outline box)GET /api/account/summarynextDelivery.date, nextDelivery.statusThe first open-status delivery dated today or later, ordered soonest-first. Open statuses: SCHEDULED, ASSIGNED, ACCEPTED, PACKED, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, ON_THE_WAY, REACHED.
ℹ️
180-row window

/api/deliveries returns at most the 180 most recent deliveries. For a daily subscriber that is roughly the last six months. Flip far enough back and older months will simply show blank cells — the data has scrolled off the window, it was not deleted.

Workflow — how a delivery becomes a coloured cell
Ops schedules / drivers deliver → Delivery rows get a status Customer opens any account page Hydrator loads /api/deliveries + /api/account/summary Rows cached on the page (raw dates & statuses) Calendar groups rows by day and paints the current month Customer taps ‹ / › to walk months (no reload)
Figure — from a real Delivery record to a coloured day on the customer's calendar.
✅
Best practice — fix the data, not the calendar

The calendar never invents anything — every dot is a real Delivery row. If a customer's calendar looks wrong (a delivered day showing amber, a missing green dot, or no "next delivery" box), the correct fix is in the operational data: update the delivery's status in Delivery Management or reschedule the subscription. Once the underlying Delivery record is corrected, the customer's calendar reflects it on their next page load. Do not chase it as a display bug.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Calendar shows a fixed "June 2026" with sample dots and a paused day 16.The browser is not signed in with a real customer account, so the static demo grid is showing.Have the customer log in at /login/customer.html. The live grid replaces the demo automatically.
All cells are blank — no dots at all for a real customer.No delivery records yet, or the backend fetch timed out / the session expired.Confirm the customer has an active subscription or a paid order that generated deliveries. Check for the offline banner; a session that expired after a password reset bounces to re-login.
A delivered morning shows amber instead of green.The Delivery row is marked FAILED or SKIPPED, not DELIVERED.Correct the delivery status in Delivery Management; the cell turns green on the customer's next load.
No outlined "next delivery" box anywhere.There is no open-status delivery dated today or later (subscription paused, ended, or all future days already delivered/failed).Verify the subscription is ACTIVE with upcoming deliveries scheduled; the summary endpoint only surfaces a future open delivery.
Very old months are empty even though deliveries happened.Those rows fell outside the 180 most-recent-deliveries window.Expected behaviour. Use the Deliveries list or Invoices for older history.

FAQ

Can the customer change a delivery from the calendar (skip, pause, reschedule)?

No. The calendar is read-only. The only interactive controls are the ‹ and › month arrows. Skips, pauses and reschedules are done from My Subscription, and the results then show up here as amber (skipped) or moved dots.

Why does today's cell look different from the "next delivery" cell?

They are two separate markers. "Today" is a dark outline that always tracks the current date. "Next delivery" is a green outline box on the soonest upcoming delivery. When both fall on the same day, the "today" marker wins.

Does flipping to another month reload data or cost a request?

No. All the customer's delivery rows are loaded once when the account page opens. The ‹ / › arrows only re-draw the grid from that cached data, so month navigation is instant and offline-safe.

Can a customer see another customer's deliveries here?

Never. The /api/deliveries query is locked to the signed-in user's own subscriptions and orders, so the calendar can only ever contain that customer's own deliveries.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Wallet
§Customer Module

Wallet

The customer's own money hub inside DOODLY — live balance, Trial Pack cashback, referral and bottle-deposit refunds, a secure "Add Money" flow, and a searchable record of every rupee that has ever moved in or out.

ℹ️
Where this screen lives

This is a customer-facing page. A signed-in customer opens it from the account sidebar under Bottles & money → Wallet. A Super Admin never edits money from here — staff use the separate Admin → System → Wallet Management screen (same DOODLY_WALLET engine, admin view) to credit, debit, refund, freeze or reverse. This manual page documents what the customer sees so support staff can walk a caller through it and read the same numbers.

DOODLY Wallet (customer dashboard)

Balance hero, quick actions, savings metrics, benefit cards, activity charts and full transaction history — all on one page.

Purpose Show the customer their real wallet balance and every transaction, and let them top the wallet up or carry it to checkout.
Access Customer (own wallet only) · viewable by Super Admin for support Path Account → Bottles & money → Wallet
DOODLY Wallet — balance ₹ hero · ＋ Add Money · Use at checkout
Balance heroBig animated ₹ balance, "Secure" lock chip (or red "Frozen" badge), "Available at checkout · ₹X cashback pending", buttons ＋ Add Money and Use at checkout
Quick actions rowAdd Money · Use Wallet · History · Cashback · Bottle Refunds · Referrals · Help
Savings metrics7 tiles: Available balance, Pending credits, Lifetime cashback, Referral rewards, Trial Pack cashback, Bottle deposit refunds, Total wallet savings
Benefit cardsTrial Pack Benefit status · Bottle Deposit Refunds · Referral Rewards
Insights charts"Monthly wallet activity" (6-month Credited vs Used bars) and "Where your credits came from" (source breakdown bars)
Transaction historySearch box + source filter + paged table (Date & time, Source, Amount, Before, After, Reference)
Figure — labelled layout of the customer Wallet dashboard (top to bottom).

What the page loads

For a real signed-in customer the panel first paints from the cached balance already in the account summary, then calls GET /api/wallet and re-renders with the authoritative ledger. Demo/guest visitors see a local sample wallet; a genuine signed-in customer never sees demo data — an empty or failed fetch shows a real (possibly zero) balance, never a seeded name.

Backend field (/api/wallet)Shown asMeaning
balancePaiseHero balanceCurrent spendable wallet balance (stored in paise on User.walletPaise; shown in ₹).
transactions[]Transaction history rowsUp to the last 100 ledger entries, newest first — id, type, kind, amount, balance-after, reference, description, date.
summary.cashbackEarnedPaiseLifetime / Trial Pack cashback tileSum of all cashback credits.
summary.referralRewardsPaiseReferral rewards tileSum of all referral credits.
summary.promoCreditsPaiseFeeds the credit-source breakdownSum of all promo credits.
summary.usedPaise"Used" in the activity chartSum of all debits (money spent from the wallet).

Savings metric tiles

The seven tiles are computed from the transaction list on the client (money reversed by staff is excluded from every total):

TileWhat it showsHow it is calculated
Available balanceMoney the customer can spend nowEquals the hero balance.
Pending creditsCashback waiting to be claimedConfigured cashback amount if the Trial Pack was bought, the promo is active, and cashback is not yet credited — otherwise ₹0. Tile turns amber with "Upgrade to claim".
Lifetime cashbackAll cashback ever earnedSum of cashback credits.
Referral rewardsEarned from inviting friendsSum of referral credits.
Trial Pack cashbackCashback from the ₹200 Trial Pack ruleSame as lifetime cashback (the cashback kind).
Bottle deposit refundsGlass-bottle deposits returned to walletSum of refund credits whose note mentions "deposit"/"bottle".
Total wallet savingsEverything the wallet has saved themcashback + referral + promo + refunds.

Benefit cards

CardRows shownCall to action
Trial Pack BenefitTrial Pack purchased (✓/✕) · Eligible for wallet credit (✓ / "Already claimed" / ✕) · Wallet credit status badge (Credited / Pending / Not started / Promotion unavailable) · Credited-on dateIf eligible: "Upgrade to a 30-day plan & get ₹200 back →" (to /subscriptions.html). If not yet bought and promo is live: "Start with the Trial Pack →".
Bottle Deposit RefundsRefunded to wallet (₹) · link "Track your bottles"Opens Account → Bottle Tracking.
Referral RewardsEarned so far (₹) · "Invite & earn"Opens Account → Referrals.
ℹ️
The Trial Pack cashback rule (why "Pending" shows)

A customer who completed the paid ₹200 Trial Pack and then subscribes to an eligible plan (by default the 30-Day or 90-Day plan) is credited a one-time ₹200 to their wallet. The backend enforces this once per customer — TrialCashback.userId is unique, so even two payments at the same moment can only credit once. The credit fires automatically after the eligible subscription is paid; a refunded Trial Pack disqualifies it. Amount, eligible plans and on/off are all admin-configurable (no code change).

Insights charts

  • Monthly wallet activity — a grouped bar chart of the last 6 months with a "Credited" bar and a "Used" bar per month (legend above). Reversed transactions are ignored. Bars animate up on load (skipped if the visitor prefers reduced motion).
  • Where your credits came from — horizontal bars for Recharge, Cashback, Referral, Promo and Refunds; each shows the amount and its share of total credits. Shows a friendly empty line until the customer has any credits.

Transaction history table

AspectBehaviour (from the real panel)
ColumnsDate & time · Source · Amount (+green credit / −grey debit) · Before (balance before) · After (balance after) · Reference (WTX-XXXXXX)
Source labelsWallet Recharge, Trial Pack Cashback, Referral Reward, Promo Credit, Bottle Deposit Refund, Refund, Order Payment, Manual Credit, Manual Debit, Reversal (each row also shows the raw description; reversed rows are struck through and tagged "· reversed").
SearchFree-text box matches description, reference, source label and amount.
Filter"All sources" dropdown with the 10 source labels above.
SortingFixed newest-first (backend orders by created date descending).
Pagination8 rows per page with ‹ Prev / Next › and a "Page X of Y" indicator; a live "N transactions" count badge in the header.
Row actionsNone for the customer — the history is read-only. (Reversal/credit/debit live only in the admin screen.)
ExportNot available to the customer. CSV export exists only in Admin → Wallet Management.

Buttons on the dashboard

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
＋ Add Money (hero) / Add Money (quick action)Opens the secure "Add Money" flow (Screen 2)None until payment — see recharge flowCustomer
Use at checkout / Use WalletNavigates to /subscriptions.html to spend the balance on an orderBalance applied at checkout via POST /api/wallet/apply (server caps to min of balance and order total)Customer
HistorySmooth-scrolls to the transaction tableClient onlyCustomer
CashbackJumps to history pre-filtered to "Trial Pack Cashback"Client filterCustomer
Bottle RefundsOpens Account → Bottle TrackingClient navigationCustomer
ReferralsOpens Account → ReferralsClient navigationCustomer
HelpOpens the Help CenterClient navigationCustomer
Search / source filter / ‹ Prev / Next ›Filter and page the transaction listClient only (list already loaded)Customer

Add Money (Wallet recharge)

A guided pop-up: choose an amount, pick a method, pay on the Razorpay secure screen, and watch the balance update the instant payment succeeds.

Purpose Let the customer top up their wallet safely, with the credit created only after the payment is signature-verified on the server.
Access Customer (must be signed in) Path Account → Wallet → ＋ Add Money
💰 Add money to wallet — modal dialog · closes on ✕ / Esc / backdrop
Step 1 — AmountCurrent balance line · preset amount chips · custom amount input (₹) · "Secured by DOODLY Pay" note with min/max · Proceed to pay button
Step 2 — Method"Adding ₹X" summary · UPI / Card / Net Banking toggles · Razorpay note · Back and Pay securely buttons
ProcessingSpinner + "Contacting the gateway… / Verifying your payment…" · "Please don't close this window"
SuccessCoins animation · ✓ badge · "₹X added!" · new balance · reference id · Done
Failure! badge · reason message · Cancel and Retry payment buttons
Figure — the five states of the Add Money dialog.

Fields (Step 1 — Amount)

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Preset amount chipsOne-tap common top-up amountsThe admin-configured preset list (default ₹100, ₹250, ₹500, ₹1,000, ₹2,000, ₹5,000)Opt.Selecting a chip fills the custom field; still checked against min/max₹500
Custom amount (₹)Any amount the customer wants to addWhole number, min to maxYesClient checks rechargeMin–rechargeMax; the server re-validates in /recharge/order against getRechargeConfig() before creating the gateway order750
Payment method (Step 2)Which rail to pay byUPI · Card · Net BankingYesPassed as a label; actual method is chosen on the Razorpay screenUPI
⚠️
Two different limit numbers — read carefully

The storefront config defaults to min ₹100 and max ₹1,00,000 with presets ₹100–₹5,000. The backend recharge config (appSetting "wallet.recharge") defaults to minPaise 10000 (₹100), maxPaise 1,00,00,000 (₹1,00,000) and preset paise values. The server number is the one that actually gates a real payment — if the two ever disagree, the customer sees a clear "Minimum/Maximum recharge is ₹X" error from the server. Keep them aligned when you change limits.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Proceed to pay ₹XMoves from amount to the method step (disabled until a valid amount is entered)Re-checks min/max client-sideCustomer
BackReturns to the amount stepClient onlyCustomer
Pay ₹X securelyStarts the real gateway paymentPOST /api/wallet/recharge/order creates a Razorpay order tagged with the customer id → Razorpay Checkout pop-up opensCustomer
Razorpay Checkout (external)Customer pays via UPI/card/net-bankingRazorpay returns razorpay_order_id, razorpay_payment_id, razorpay_signatureCustomer
(auto) Verify & creditConfirms the payment and credits the walletPOST /api/wallet/recharge/confirm verifies the signature, re-fetches the order (server-trusted amount + owner tag), then credits idempotently using the payment id as the unique referenceCustomer
DoneCloses the dialog and refreshes the panel with the new balancePanel re-reads the ledgerCustomer
Retry paymentReturns to the method step to try againRestarts the flowCustomer
Cancel / ✕ / EscCloses the dialog with no chargeNoneCustomer
Workflow — secure wallet recharge
Enter amount Pick method Server creates Razorpay order (validates min/max) Pay on Razorpay screen Server verifies signature + order owner Wallet credited (idempotent by payment id) Balance updates instantly
Figure — money never lands in the wallet until the server has verified the payment.
⛔
Caution — the client can never mint balance

In production, credits happen only through the signature-verified /recharge/confirm path. The plain POST /api/wallet/recharge route refuses unverified credits when the gateway is configured (returns use_gateway). A repeated confirm with the same payment id returns the original credit instead of adding money twice. On localhost a simulated payment path exists for demos only.

ℹ️
Recharge blocked states

If an admin has switched recharge off, the dialog shows "Wallet recharge is currently unavailable." If the specific wallet is frozen, it shows "Your wallet is temporarily frozen. Please contact support…" and no payment can start. Freezing/unfreezing is done from Admin → Wallet Management.

💡
Tip — what the customer gets notified about

A fresh top-up, a Trial Pack cashback, a referral reward and a bottle-deposit refund each fire an in-app notification, and (where the customer has opted in) a WhatsApp message — the recharge and cashback also send a branded email. Idempotent replays never re-notify, so a caller who refreshes will not get duplicate messages.

✅
Best Practice

When a customer says "I paid but my balance didn't change", ask for the Razorpay payment id shown on the success screen (or their bank statement). Search that id in Admin → Wallet Management: because every credit uses the payment id as its unique reference, you can instantly tell whether the credit landed, and if the gateway charged but confirmation failed, the amount is auto-refunded by the gateway — never manually credit a second time without confirming the first did not post.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Balance shows ₹0 for a real customer/api/wallet failed or the customer is not truly signed in (no token)Confirm they are logged in; reload. A signed-in customer is shown a real (possibly zero) balance, never demo data — a persistent zero means no credits yet.
"Add Money" says wallet is frozenThe wallet was frozen by an adminUnfreeze from Admin → Wallet Management → Freeze/Unfreeze after resolving the reason.
"Wallet recharge is currently unavailable"Recharge switched off in admin settingsEnable "Allow customers to add money" in Admin → Wallet Management → Settings.
"Minimum/Maximum recharge is ₹X" on payingAmount outside the server recharge limitsEnter an amount within range, or adjust minPaise/maxPaise in Settings.
Paid on Razorpay but no creditSignature/confirm step failed after chargeSearch the payment id in Wallet Management; the gateway auto-refunds an unconfirmed charge. Do not double-credit.
Trial Pack cashback stuck on "Pending"Eligible plan not yet paid, or Trial Pack refundedConfirm a PAID Trial Pack (SAMPLE order) exists and the customer paid an eligible (30/90-day) plan; credit fires automatically once both are true.
Payments not configured (503)Razorpay keys missing in the environmentSet the Razorpay keys in the deployment env; recharge order creation needs them.

FAQ

Q. Can a customer spend more than their balance at checkout?

No. When the wallet is applied to an order the server takes the smaller of the wallet balance and the order total, and the debit is idempotent per order, so a retry can never over-spend.

Q. Why does the same cashback amount appear under both "Lifetime cashback" and "Trial Pack cashback"?

Both tiles read the cashback credit total. Trial Pack cashback is the only kind of cashback DOODLY issues today, so the two figures match by design.

Q. Does wallet money expire?

Not currently. The config has an "Expiry (days)" setting and the reports expose an "expired credits" field, but the expiry policy is future-ready and left blank (never expires) by default.

Q. Can support add money to a customer's wallet directly?

Yes, but not from this customer page. Staff use Admin → Wallet Management to post a Manual Credit, Manual Debit, or Bottle Deposit Refund — each is audited and appears in the customer's history with the matching source label.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Referrals
§Customer Module

Referrals

The customer's "refer a friend" screen — it shows their personal DOODLY code and share link, tracks the friends they invited, and pays a one-time wallet reward when a friend subscribes to a qualifying plan.

My Referral Program

The signed-in customer's referral dashboard at /account/referrals.html.

Purpose Give every customer a shareable referral code and link, let them share it in one tap, and show — in real time — which friends joined, which converted, and how much wallet money they earned.
Access Customer (their own account only). The reward policy and moderation are controlled by Super Admin / Admin in the Growth → Referrals admin console. Path My Account → Rewards → Referrals
Referrals — Refer a friend, earn ₹100 · code · share · history
Referral code cardLarge "Your referral code" (e.g. DOODLY8F4XK), read-only share-link box with Copy link, and share row: Share (native), Copy code, WhatsApp, SMS, Email.
Policy line"Earn ₹100 when a friend subscribes to a 30-day or longer plan. Terms apply." — values come live from the reward policy.
Stat tiles (×4)Friends joined · Successful · Pending · Wallet earned (green). All derived from the backend.
"Friends you referred" tableColumns: Friend · Joined · Status. Colour-coded status badge per friend; honest empty state when there are no referrals yet.
Figure — labelled layout of the customer Referrals screen (#referralPanelMount, built by account.js → GET /api/account/referrals).
ℹ️
Note — this screen is read-only for the customer

The customer does not fill in a form here. Everything on this page is fetched from the server: the code, the share link, the four totals, and the friends list. The only editable control is the share-link box, which is read-only text kept there so the customer can select and copy it.

Data shown on this screen

Returned by GET /api/account/referrals under referral. All figures are server-derived; nothing is stored in the browser.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Your referral codeThe customer's unique, shareable codeDOODLY + 5–6 unambiguous charsYesensureReferralCode() — reads User.referralCode; a legacy cuid code is silently upgraded to the DOODLY format. Character set excludes 0/O/1/I.DOODLY8F4XK
Share linkPre-built registration URL that carries the codeFull https URL (read-only)YesstorefrontBase() + /signup.html?ref=CODE. Always the .html path — the extensionless /signup 404s.https://doodly.in/signup.html?ref=DOODLY8F4XK
Friends joinedHow many people signed up with this codeWhole number ≥ 0YesCount of User.referredById = me.4
SuccessfulFriends whose reward was actually creditedWhole number ≥ 0YesCount of ReferralReward rows with status CREDITED for this referrer.2
PendingFriends who joined but haven't earned a reward yetWhole number ≥ 0YesFriends whose status is not "Reward Credited" and not "Reward Cancelled".2
Wallet earnedTotal referral money paid into this customer's walletCurrency (₹)YesSum of WalletTxn where type = CREDIT and kind = referral.₹200
Friend — NameWho was referredText (falls back to "A friend")YesUser.name of each referred customer.Meera S.
Friend — JoinedWhen they signed upDateYesUser.createdAt, newest first (max 100).24 Jun 2026
Friend — StatusWhere the friend is in the reward journeySee status legend belowYesDerived from the reward ledger + the friend's qualifying subscription / paid orders.Reward Credited

Friend status legend

BadgeColourWhat it meansHow the backend decides
RegisteredGreyFriend signed up with the code but has not made a qualifying purchase.Referred user exists, no reward and no qualifying subscription/paid order.
First Purchase PendingAmberFriend has paid for something, but not yet an eligible 30-day+ subscription.Friend has a PAID order but no qualifying subscription.
Qualifying Subscription PurchasedBlueFriend bought an eligible plan; the reward is about to be / being processed.Friend has an ACTIVE/COMPLETED subscription on a plan with days ≥ policy minimum.
Reward CreditedGreenThe ₹100 reward has been paid into this customer's wallet.ReferralReward.status = CREDITED.
Reward CancelledRedThe reward was voided or reversed by an admin (fraud / error).ReferralReward.status = VOID.

Share actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Copy linkCopies the full share URL to the clipboard and shows a "Link copied ✓" toast.Client-side navigator.clipboard; no server call.Customer
Copy codeCopies just the DOODLY code and shows a "Code copied ✓" toast.Client-side clipboard; no server call.Customer
ShareOpens the device's native share sheet (mobile / supported browsers). Hidden automatically where the OS share API is unavailable.navigator.share() with the code + link; no server call.Customer
WhatsAppOpens WhatsApp with a pre-written invite message containing the code and link.wa.me deep link in a new tab; no server call.Customer
SMSOpens the phone's messaging app pre-filled with the same invite text.sms: link; no server call.Customer
EmailOpens the mail client with subject "Try DOODLY fresh milk" and the invite body pre-filled.mailto: link; no server call.Customer

"Friends you referred" — table specification

PropertyBehaviour
ColumnsFriend · Joined · Status (three columns).
SortingFixed — newest joiner first (server orders by sign-up date, descending).
Search / FiltersNone on the customer screen. (Search, status filters and exports exist only in the admin console.)
PaginationNone; the server returns up to the 100 most recent friends.
Row actionsNone — the list is informational for the customer.
Empty state"No referrals yet — share your code to start earning."
⚠️
Warning — a Trial Pack does not earn the reward

The reward is only released when the referred friend pays for a subscription whose plan length is at least the policy minimum (30 days by default). Trial Packs, single pours, 7-day plans and any shorter or free plan do not qualify. If the friend starts on a Trial Pack, their status stays "First Purchase Pending" until they upgrade to an eligible plan.

ℹ️
Note — one reward per friend, ever

Each referred friend can trigger the reward only once. Renewals or additional purchases by the same friend never pay a second time — the server keeps a single ReferralReward row per referred customer as the idempotency guard.

Workflow — from sharing a code to a wallet credit
Customer shares code / link Friend opens /signup.html?ref=CODE Code validated live (/api/referral/validate) Friend registers → linked as referredById Referrer notified "A friend joined 🎉" Friend pays for a 30-day+ subscription ₹100 auto-credited to referrer's wallet (once)
Figure — end-to-end referral lifecycle. The credit is idempotent and eligibility-gated; a failed, refunded or ineligible order never pays out.
ℹ️
Note — how the code is applied (registration side)

The share link carries the code as ?ref=CODE. On the sign-up page the code is checked in real time by GET /api/referral/validate, which returns whether it is valid plus the referrer's first name (only the first name, for privacy). At registration the server re-validates it — see the field below — and links the new account to the referrer via User.referredById.

Referral-code field at sign-up

This is the one place a code is entered. It belongs to the registration form (POST /api/auth/register) but is the entry point of this module's workflow.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Referral codeLink the new customer to the friend who invited themDOODLY-format or legacy code, up to 40 chars; blank = skipOpt.Trimmed and upper-cased. If present: program must be enabled, the code must resolve to a real customer, and it must not be the sign-up person's own email/phone (self-referral blocked). An invalid code blocks registration with a clear message; validity is checked against the database, not by length.DOODLY8F4XK
⚠️
Warning — self-referrals are rejected

If the referral code belongs to the same person signing up (matching email or phone), registration fails with "You can't use your own referral code." The admin console also blocks duplicate-account and circular referrals and logs them as fraud alerts.

Reward policy (what the customer screen reflects)

Set by the Super Admin; the customer screen only displays these values.

Purpose Define the reward amount, the minimum plan length that qualifies, and whether the whole program is on — the single source of truth read by both the customer page and the automatic credit logic.
Access Super Admin to change; Admin may view/manage per RBAC (payments/billing rights). Path Admin → Growth → Referrals → Settings
SettingPurposeDefaultBackend
Referral program enabledMaster on/off switch. When off, codes stop working and no rewards are paid.OnReferralConfig.enabled
Reward amount (₹)Wallet credit paid per successful referral.₹100 (10000 paise)ReferralConfig.rewardAmountPaise
Eligible from plan duration (days)Minimum subscription length a friend must buy to qualify.30 daysReferralConfig.minPlanDays
ℹ️
Note — one shared policy

These three values drive the customer page's headline ("Earn ₹100 … 30-day or longer plan"), the stat tiles, and the automatic credit. Changing them in the admin Settings tab (saved via POST /api/admin/referrals, action config) instantly changes what every customer sees.

✅
Best Practice

Coach customers to share via the WhatsApp or Share buttons rather than typing the code by hand — the pre-built link already carries ?ref=CODE, so the friend's code is filled in automatically at sign-up and can't be mistyped. When helping a customer over support, confirm a "Pending" friend simply hasn't bought a qualifying plan yet; the reward will credit itself the moment they do — no manual action needed.

💡
Tip

"Wallet earned" on this screen and the wallet's referral credits always agree, because both are driven by the same WalletTxn rows (kind = referral). If a customer disputes their earnings, cross-check the Wallet page — the numbers are the same ledger.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"Couldn't load your referrals" warning on the page.Customer is signed out, or the backend is unreachable (offline).Confirm the customer is logged in and online; the screen requires an authenticated session and reloads on refresh.
A friend joined but shows "Registered" and no reward.The friend hasn't bought a qualifying 30-day+ subscription yet (or only bought a Trial Pack).Expected. The reward credits automatically once they purchase an eligible plan. Nothing to do manually.
Friend's status is "First Purchase Pending" indefinitely.They paid for a non-qualifying item (trial / short plan).Encourage the friend to upgrade to a 30-day or longer subscription; the reward releases on that purchase.
Reward shows "Reward Cancelled" (red).An admin voided or reversed the reward (fraud, duplicate account, or error).Check the Fraud Alerts / referral record in the admin console for the reason before re-issuing.
Friend couldn't register — "That referral code isn't valid."Code mistyped, program disabled, or self-referral (own email/phone).Re-share the link (auto-fills the code); confirm the program is enabled in Settings; a customer cannot use their own code.
Reward never paid even though the friend bought a plan.The program was disabled, or the reward was already voided for that friend, or the order was refunded/failed.Verify the program is enabled and the friend's subscription is ACTIVE/COMPLETED; ineligible, failed or refunded orders never pay out.

Is the referral reward paid every time my friend renews?

No. It is a one-time credit per referred friend. Once their first eligible subscription pays out the ₹100, further renewals or purchases never pay again.

Can a customer change or regenerate their code?

Not from this screen — the code is fixed and shown as-is. An admin can regenerate a customer's code from the referral console if needed, which invalidates the old link.

Where does the reward money go?

Into the referrer's DOODLY wallet as a CREDIT of kind "referral". It appears both here under "Wallet earned" and on the Wallet page, and can be spent at checkout like any other wallet balance.

Why does the friends list only show first names / partial contact?

Privacy. On the customer screen only the friend's name and join date are shown. Full contact details, referrer codes and lifecycle actions live in the admin referral console, which is role-restricted.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Rewards (Pure Rewards)
§Customer Module

Rewards (Pure Rewards)

The customer's DOODLY Pure Rewards page — their loyalty tier, points balance, live tier progress, expiring-points warnings, a one-tap "redeem points to wallet" tool, the full list of ways to earn, every tier's benefits, and a complete points history — all driven by the server-authoritative loyalty ledger.

ℹ️
Where this screen lives — and where staff configure it

This is a customer-facing page. A signed-in customer opens it from the account sidebar under Rewards → Rewards (URL /account/rewards.html). Everything on it is display + the redeem action — the points ledger, tiers, earning rates and expiry are all decided on the server. A Super Admin never edits reward rates from here; staff use the separate Admin loyalty module (the #loyaltyAdminMount screen backed by /api/admin/loyalty/config, /members and /adjust) to change earning rates, tiers, redemption ratio, expiry and to grant or deduct a member's points. This manual page documents what the customer sees so support staff can walk a caller through it and read exactly the same numbers.

ℹ️
How the page is assembled (from the route manifest)

The account/rewards route stacks three blocks in order: a heading ("DOODLY Pure Rewards — The more you stay fresh, the more you're rewarded."), the loyaltyProgram block (mount #loyaltyMount, built by loyalty.js), and a puzzleCard block (the Monthly Puzzle Challenge card, mount #puzzleCardMount). This section documents the loyaltyProgram — the main rewards experience — plus the compact rewards card that appears on the account dashboard.

DOODLY Pure Rewards (full programme page)

Tier hero, points KPIs, redeem-to-wallet tool, "earn more" grid, tier ladder, points history and the FAQ / terms — all on one page.

Purpose Show the customer their tier, points and rewards, and let them turn points into DOODLY Wallet credit.
Access Customer (own rewards only) · viewable by Super Admin for support Path Account → Rewards → Rewards
DOODLY Pure Rewards — tier hero · redeem to wallet · earn more · history
Tier heroAnimated farm scene (🐃🥛🍃), tier badge + name (e.g. "Fresh Member"), big available-points number, progress bar, and "X / Y points · N points to <next tier>" (or "Top tier unlocked 🎉")
Campaign bannerOnly when a bonus campaign is live: "✨ Bonus campaign live — earn X% extra points until <date>"
KPI row4 tiles: Available points · Redeemable value (₹) · Lifetime earned · Lifetime redeemed
Expiring noticeOnly when points expire within 30 days: "⏳ N points expiring within 30 days — next M on <date> (D days)"
Redeem panelPreset chips (100 / 500 / 1000 / 2000 / all), a points input with live "= ₹X", and a "Redeem to wallet" button — or a "Keep earning!" state below the minimum
Earn more grid13 ways to earn, each with its emoji and current points value from config
Tiers & benefitsThe tier ladder (Fresh / Silver / Gold / Platinum) with each tier's minimum points and benefit list; current tier shows a "You" chip
Rewards historyTable of the last 40 points events (Activity · Points ± · When)
FAQ & termsTwo collapsible panels: Frequently asked questions and Rewards terms & conditions
Figure — labelled layout of the customer Rewards page (top to bottom).

What the page loads

On open, a signed-in customer first sees a skeleton, then the page calls GET /api/account/rewards and renders from the returned rewards summary. The summary is cached for 20 seconds client-side and force-refreshed after any redeem. Guests and demo visitors never see another customer's data — the page self-gates (see states below).

Backend field (/api/account/rewards)Shown asMeaning
enabledWhole page on/offMaster switch (LoyaltyConfig.enabled). When off, the page shows a friendly "taking a short break" panel instead.
points.availableHero "available points" + Available-points KPISpendable points balance (cached on User.loyaltyPoints).
points.lifetimeEarnedLifetime-earned KPI + progress numeratorAll points ever earned — this is what drives the tier (redeeming never lowers it).
points.lifetimeRedeemedLifetime-redeemed KPIAll points ever converted to wallet credit.
tier {key, name, min, benefits}Hero tier badge + nameThe member's current tier, derived from lifetime earned.
nextTier {name, min, pointsAway, progressPct}Progress bar + "N points to <tier>"The next tier up and how far away it is; null at the top tier.
allTiers[]Tiers & benefits ladderEvery configured tier with its threshold and benefit list.
redemption.pointsPerRupee"X points = ₹1" + input stepConversion ratio (default 10 points = ₹1).
redemption.minRedeemPoints"min N points"Smallest redemption allowed (default 100).
redemption.redeemablePaiseRedeemable-value KPI (₹)What the available points are worth right now, rounded down to whole rupees.
expiring.within30Days / nextExpiryAt / nextExpiryPointsExpiring noticePoints due to expire within 30 days, and the date/size of the very next expiry.
earnRules"Earn more points" gridThe 13 current earning values (see the Earn table).
campaign {multiplier, endsAt}Campaign bannerPresent only when a bonus multiplier > 1 is live and not past its end date.
history[]Rewards history tableThe last 40 ledger rows, newest first (type, kind, points, description, dates).

Page states (what the customer sees when)

StateWhat showsTrigger
Guest / not signed in"Join DOODLY Pure Rewards" panel with a 🥇 badge and a Sign in button → /login/customer.htmlNo real signed-in customer id (guest or demo static- user).
LoadingSkeleton lines in a panelWhile the summary is being fetched.
Programme off"Rewards are taking a short break" panel — "Your points are safe and will be here when it returns."enabled = false in config.
Fetch failedAmber notice: "Couldn't load your rewards right now — please try again shortly."The API call errored or returned nothing.
NormalThe full programme (hero → history → FAQ)Signed-in customer, programme on, summary loaded.

Tier hero & progress

The hero shows the tier badge and name, the big available-points number, and a progress bar filled to nextTier.progressPct. Below it: "lifetimeEarned / nextTier.min points" and "pointsAway points to <next tier>". At the top tier the bar is full and it reads "You've reached <tier> — Top tier unlocked 🎉". The tier icon set is 🥉 Fresh · 🥈 Silver · 🥇 Gold · 💎 Platinum. Tier follows lifetime earned, so spending points never demotes a member.

ℹ️
Bonus campaign banner

When an admin sets a campaignMultiplier above 1 with a future campaignEndsAt, a banner appears: "✨ Bonus campaign live — earn X% extra points until <date>", where X = (multiplier − 1) × 100 (a 1.05 multiplier shows "5% extra"). The multiplier only boosts spend/activity earns that opt in (order points, subscribe and renewal), not fixed one-off bonuses.

KPI row

TileShowsSource
Available pointsPoints the customer can redeem nowpoints.available
Redeemable valueThose points in ₹ (whole rupees)redemption.redeemablePaise = ⌊available ÷ ratio⌋ × ₹1
Lifetime earnedAll points ever earnedpoints.lifetimeEarned
Lifetime redeemedAll points ever spentpoints.lifetimeRedeemed

Expiring-points notice

Shown only when expiring.within30Days > 0: "⏳ N points expiring within 30 days — next M on <date> (D days). Redeem them for wallet credit below." Points are lots that each expire 12 months after they were earned; the server's expiry cron zeroes past-due lots and sends 30-day and 7-day reminders (in-app + email, and WhatsApp where opted in).

Redeem points (points → wallet credit)

The one action on the page: turn points into DOODLY Wallet credit at the configured ratio, subject to a minimum and whole-rupee rule.

Purpose Let the customer convert points into spendable wallet credit, validated end-to-end on the server.
Access Customer (own points only) Path Account → Rewards → Rewards → Redeem points
Redeem points — "10 points = ₹1 · min 100 points"
Preset chipsQuick amounts 100 / 500 / 1000 / 2000 pts (only those ≥ minimum and ≤ your balance), plus an "all" chip for your whole redeemable balance — each shows "= ₹X"
Amount fieldNumber input (min = minimum, max = available, step = ratio) with a live "= ₹X" value beside it
Redeem button"Redeem to wallet" — posts the redemption; inline message shows success or the exact error
Below-minimum stateIf the balance is under the minimum: a "Keep earning!" card — "You need at least N points to redeem. You have M."
Figure — the redeem panel and its below-minimum fallback.

Fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Preset chipOne-tap points amount100, 500, 1000, 2000, or "all" — only chips that are ≥ minimum and ≤ your balance appearOpt.Fills the amount field; still re-checked against the rules below500 pts = ₹50
Points to redeemHow many points to convertWhole number from minRedeemPoints (100) up to your available balance, in steps of the ratio (10)YesClient checks: ≥ minimum, ≤ available, and a multiple of the ratio. Server (redeemPoints) re-enforces all three plus the programme on/off switch.750
ℹ️
The maths (default config)

The ratio is 10 points = ₹1 and the minimum is 100 points (both admin-tunable). Credit = (points ÷ ratio) × ₹1, so 750 points → ₹75. Points must convert to a whole rupee — the input steps by the ratio and the server rejects any amount that isn't an exact multiple. The credit lands in the customer's DOODLY Wallet (a WalletTxn of kind "loyalty"), so it can then be spent on any order, renewal or top-up.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Preset chip (e.g. "500 pts = ₹50")Fills the amount field with that value and highlights the chipClient onlyCustomer
Redeem to walletSubmits the redemption; button shows "Redeeming…" while it worksPOST /api/account/rewards/redeem with { points } → server validates, consumes points-lots oldest-expiry-first (FIFO), and credits the wallet — all in one serializable transactionCustomer
(on success)Toast "₹X added to your wallet 🎉", refreshes the wallet, and re-renders the card + this page with the new balancesCache invalidated; wallet panel refreshedCustomer
Workflow — redeem points to wallet
Pick a preset or type points Live "= ₹X" preview Redeem to wallet Server checks on/off · min · whole-rupee · balance FIFO consume oldest-expiry lots Wallet credited (WalletTxn "loyalty") Points + wallet balances update instantly
Figure — redemption is entirely server-authoritative; the client only sends the points amount.

Redemption errors (server messages)

Reason codeMessage the customer seesCause
disabled"The rewards programme is currently unavailable."Programme switched off between page load and redeem.
invalid_amount"Enter a valid number of points to redeem."Zero or non-positive amount.
below_min"That's below the minimum redemption."Fewer than minRedeemPoints.
not_whole_rupee"Points must convert to a whole rupee amount."Amount isn't a multiple of the ratio.
insufficient"You don't have enough points for that."More than the available balance.
error"Couldn't process the redemption. Please try again."Unexpected server error.
✅
Best Practice — idempotent, so a retry never double-spends

Every redemption is recorded as a unique ledger row and mirrored to a single wallet credit. If a caller taps "Redeem" twice or their connection drops after the credit posted, the server returns the original credit instead of spending points again. When helping a customer who is unsure whether a redemption went through, check their Wallet history for a "Loyalty redemption" credit before re-attempting — don't advise a second redeem.

Earn more · Tiers & benefits · Rewards history

The reference sections below the redeem tool — how to earn, what each tier gives, and every points event.

Purpose Explain how points are earned and spent, and give the customer a full, auditable record of their points.
Access Customer (own data) · viewable by Super Admin for support Path Account → Rewards → Rewards

Earn more points (13 ways)

Each row reads its live value from config; the values below are the defaults. All earns are awarded automatically on the server after the qualifying action succeeds — the customer never claims them manually — and each is idempotent (fires at most once per event).

Way to earnDefault pointsConfig fieldWhen it fires
🥛 Every ₹100 spent10 pts / ₹100pointsPerHundredOn a paid order, computed on product spend (order total minus refundable deposit and coupon discount). Campaign multiplier applies.
📅 Subscribe (30-day)+300earnSubscribe30Subscribing to a 30-day (or longer, under 90) plan.
🗓️ Subscribe (90-day)+1,000earnSubscribe90Subscribing to a 90-day (or longer) plan.
🔁 Every renewal+150earnRenewalEach subscription renewal cycle. Campaign multiplier applies.
♻️ Bottle returned+15 / bottleearnBottleReturnPer glass bottle returned in good condition.
🔥 12 deliveries in a row+200earnStreak1212 consecutive deliveries without a pause.
🤝 Refer a friend+500earnReferralCredited to the referrer after the referred friend completes a qualifying 30-day+ subscription payment.
🎂 Birthday+250earnBirthdayAnnual birthday reward (needs a date of birth on file).
🥳 DOODLY anniversary+1,000earnAnniversaryEach anniversary of joining DOODLY.
🧩 Play the monthly puzzle+50earnPuzzlePlayParticipating in the monthly puzzle.
🏆 Win the monthly puzzle+2,500earnPuzzleWinWinning the monthly puzzle (the free 7-day plan prize is handled separately by the puzzle engine).
⭐ Verified review+30earnReviewOnce per delivered order, for a verified review left on the My Orders "Rate your deliveries" panel.
✅ Complete your profile+25earnProfileOne-time, when name + email + phone are all filled in.

There is also a one-time Welcome bonus of +50 (earnRegistration) granted at account creation; it appears in history as "Welcome bonus" though it isn't listed in the earn grid.

Tiers & benefits (default ladder)

Tier is derived from lifetime points earned; redeeming points never demotes a member. The current tier shows a "You" chip. Thresholds and benefit copy are all admin-tunable JSON.

TierFrom (lifetime points)Benefits
🥉 Fresh Member0+Access to Rewards · Birthday Bonus · Referral Program
🥈 Silver Member1,000+2% Additional Wallet Cashback (selected campaigns) · Early Access to Offers · Priority Customer Support
🥇 Gold Member3,000+5% Extra Reward Points during campaigns · Free Surprise Dairy Gift (selected campaigns) · Faster Support
💎 Platinum Member6,000+Exclusive Products · Priority Delivery · Special Festival Gifts · VIP Customer Support · Exclusive Events & Promotions
💡
Tip — tier upgrades notify automatically

The instant an earn pushes a member's lifetime total across a tier threshold, the server sends a congratulations notification ("You're now a <tier>! 🎉") in-app and by email, plus a WhatsApp tier_upgrade template where the customer has opted in. Staff don't need to do anything to celebrate an upgrade.

Rewards history table

AspectBehaviour (from the real panel)
ColumnsActivity (friendly label + optional description) · Points (with sign) · When (date)
Sign & colourEARN shows "+N" in green; REDEEM / EXPIRE / ADJUST show "−N" (redeem/adjust amber, expiry muted grey)
Activity labelsWelcome bonus · Profile completed · Order points · Subscription bonus · Referral bonus · Bottle return · Renewal bonus · Delivery streak bonus · Birthday reward · Anniversary bonus · Puzzle played · Puzzle winner · Review reward · Redeemed to wallet · Points expired · Adjustment
RowsThe last 40 ledger events, newest first (server orders by created date descending)
Sorting / filters / searchNone — the history is a fixed newest-first read-only list
Pagination / exportNot available to the customer (capped at 40 rows)
Empty state"No points activity yet — your rewards build up with every delivery, referral and bottle return."

FAQ & terms (collapsible)

Two expandable panels sit at the foot of the page. The FAQ answers: how to join (every registered customer is auto-enrolled), how to redeem (ratio + minimum → wallet credit), when points expire (12 months, with 30- and 7-day reminders), what earns points, and whether points can be transferred or cashed out (no — wallet credit only). The Terms & conditions panel lists seven rules, including that points are earned only after an eligible action completes successfully, bottle-return points need bottles in good condition, referral rewards credit after the friend's qualifying subscription payment, points can't be cashed out or transferred, rates may change (never affecting points already earned), and that fraud/duplicate/self-referral accounts can have points reversed and be suspended.

Rewards card (account dashboard)

A compact tier card on the account dashboard that summarises rewards and links to the full page.

Purpose Give the customer an at-a-glance tier + points view on their dashboard and a one-tap route into the full Rewards page.
Access Customer (own rewards only) Path Account → Overview → Dashboard
DOODLY Pure Rewards card — tier + points · View rewards →
Top row"🥉 DOODLY Pure Rewards" eyebrow + tier name on the left; available points number on the right
Progress barFilled to the same next-tier progress percentage as the hero
Foot"lifetimeEarned / nextTier.min · N to <next tier>" (or "Top tier unlocked — enjoy the perks! 🎉") and a "View rewards →" link
Figure — the compact rewards card on the dashboard (mount #loyaltyCardMount).

The card reads the same /api/account/rewards summary. It renders nothing for guests or when the programme is off, so the dashboard stays clean. The whole card is a link to /account/rewards.html. After a redeem on the full page, this card is re-rendered so its numbers stay in sync.

ℹ️
Also on this route — the Monthly Puzzle card

Below the loyalty programme, the Rewards page also mounts the Monthly Puzzle Challenge card (#puzzleCardMount, built by puzzle.js). It is a separate feature — playing earns +50 and winning earns +2,500 points into this same rewards ledger — and is documented in its own Puzzle Challenge section.

💡
Tip — what the customer gets notified about

Members are messaged (in-app, and email/WhatsApp where they've opted in) when they earn a welcome, profile, subscription, referral, streak, birthday, anniversary or puzzle-win bonus, when they cross a tier threshold, and 30 and 7 days before points expire. Because every earn is idempotent, a webhook retry or page refresh never sends a duplicate.

⚠️
Warning — points are not cash

Points can't be exchanged for cash, sold, or transferred between accounts — they are redeemable only as DOODLY Wallet credit. If an account is permanently deleted, all unused points expire. DOODLY may reverse points and suspend accounts involved in fraud, duplicate accounts, self-referrals or automated abuse. Never promise a customer a cash payout of points.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"Join DOODLY Pure Rewards" shows instead of the programmeNot truly signed in (guest or demo user)Have the customer sign in via /login/customer.html; a real signed-in customer is auto-enrolled and sees their tier.
"Rewards are taking a short break"Programme switched off (enabled = false)Re-enable it in the Admin loyalty module → Programme settings toggle. Points are preserved while off.
"Couldn't load your rewards right now"/api/account/rewards failedReload; if it persists, check the backend/API is up. The summary is a live server read.
"Keep earning!" — can't redeemBalance below the minimum (default 100)The customer needs at least the minimum points; every delivery, referral and bottle return gets them closer.
"Points must convert to a whole rupee amount"Amount isn't a multiple of the ratio (10)Enter points in steps of the ratio (e.g. 100, 110, 120…). The input's step already enforces this.
"You don't have enough points for that"Amount exceeds the available balanceRedeem up to the available balance shown in the KPI, or use the "all" preset chip.
Redeemed points but wallet unchangedConfusion between points and wallet, or a display cacheCheck Wallet history for a "Loyalty redemption" credit; the credit is created in the same transaction, so if points dropped the wallet was credited. Reload the wallet page.
Points expected but not earnedUnderlying action didn't complete (cancelled order, failed payment, refunded Trial Pack, damaged bottle)Confirm the qualifying action succeeded; earns fire only after the eligible action completes. If it did complete, an admin can grant points via the Adjust tool with an audited reason.

FAQ

Q. How does a customer join Pure Rewards?

There is nothing to sign up for — every registered DOODLY customer is automatically enrolled and starts at Fresh Member. Signing in is all that's needed to see the page.

Q. Do points expire?

Yes — each batch of points is valid for 12 months from the date it was earned (config expiryDays, default 365). The server reminds the customer 30 and 7 days before any points expire, and a cron job zeroes past-due points. Redeeming always consumes the points closest to expiry first, so nothing is wasted.

Q. Can support add or remove a customer's points?

Yes, but not from this customer page. Staff with loyalty-edit permission use the Admin loyalty module's Adjust tool (POST /api/admin/loyalty/adjust): a positive number grants points, a negative number deducts them (oldest-expiry-first), and a reason is required and recorded for the audit. The change appears in the customer's history as "Adjustment".

Q. Why doesn't the tier drop when a customer redeems points?

Tier is based on lifetime earned, not the current balance, so spending points never demotes a member — only the available-points number goes down.

Q. Where does the redeemed money go?

Into the customer's DOODLY Wallet as a "loyalty" credit. From there it can be spent on any order, renewal or top-up. Money stays in one place — the redemption never creates a separate cash-out.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Invoices
§Customer Module

Invoices

The customer's own record of every billed order — a searchable, filterable list of GST tax invoices that each open into a full printable document and download as a receipt.

ℹ️
Where this lives

This is a Customer-surface page reached at /account/invoices.html. A logged-in customer sees their own invoices; a Super Admin or Admin viewing the customer account (for support) sees the same screen. The page is built from two real pieces of code: the invoices data table (registered in blocks.js, driven by the shared DataTable engine in datatable.js) and the full tax-invoice document that opens on View (built by invoice.js, DOODLY_INVOICE.mountB2C).

Invoices — list

Every tax invoice for the account, with search, filters and downloads.

Purpose Give the customer one place to review, verify, open and download every GST invoice raised against their orders and subscription billing.
Access Customer (own account) · Super Admin Admin (viewing a customer for support) Path Account → Bottles & money → Invoices

The screen is intentionally simple: a page header ("Invoices — Download GST invoices for every payment.") sits above a single data table. The table shows one row per invoice. Every column, filter, sort control, the search box, pagination and the export menu come from the unified DataTable engine, so this page behaves exactly like the other list pages in the app.

Invoices — Download GST invoices for every payment
ToolbarSearch box · Filters button · Date-range · Save view / favorite · Export ▾ (CSV / Excel / PDF)
Filter chipsRemovable chips for the active search, status filter, date range and sort
Data tableColumns: Invoice · Date · Amount · Tax · Status · Actions (View / Download)
PagerPage size (25 default) and page navigation at the foot of the table
Empty stateReceipt icon + "No invoices yet" + "Browse products" button when the account has none
Figure — labelled layout of the Invoices list screen

Table columns

The invoices table is defined once in blocks.js (the TABLES.invoices registry entry). Each row is drawn from a single invoice record.

ColumnPurposeSource fieldHow it rendersExample
InvoiceThe unique invoice number — the customer's reference for support and accounting.idShown as a highlighted ID chip (cell-id).INV-2026-0428
DateThe invoice / billing date.datePlain text; this field also powers the date-range filter.26 Jun 2026
AmountThe total billed on the invoice.amountFormatted as Indian Rupees, rounded, bold (inr()).₹3,588
TaxTells the customer how GST is treated on this invoice.gstFree text label as stored on the record.incl. GST
StatusWhether the invoice is settled.statusA coloured status badge — the label and colour come from the record (e.g. green "Paid").Paid
(Actions)Open or download the invoice. The header for this column is intentionally blank.—A View link and a Download icon button (see the Button table).👁 View · ⬇
ℹ️
Where the invoice records come from

The table reads its rows from DOODLY_DATA.invoices. Each record carries exactly these fields: id, date, amount (a number), gst (a label such as "incl. GST") and status (a colour + label pair, e.g. ["green","Paid"]). Per the page's own copy, invoices are generated automatically after each paid order; the customer never creates one by hand on this screen. The customer Dashboard reuses these same records for its "Latest Invoice" and "Pending Payments" cards (an unpaid invoice is any record whose status label is not "Paid").

Table controls (DataTable engine)

The list is powered by the shared DataTable engine. The controls below are what the customer actually gets on this table.

ControlBehaviour on this table
SearchInstant, type-ahead box (placeholder "Search invoices…"). Matches across all visible cell text — invoice number, date, amount, tax label and status. Debounced (~180 ms) and resets to page 1 on each keystroke.
FiltersA Filters button opens a side panel with auto-detected facet groups. For invoices the engine detects a Status facet and lists a checkbox for each distinct status found in the data (for example "Paid", and "Pending"/"Overdue" once such invoices exist). The Status group only appears when the account has two or more distinct statuses; with a single status there is nothing to filter by.
Date rangeBecause the Date column holds real dates, the engine offers date-range presets plus a custom from/to range so the customer can narrow to a month, quarter or year.
SortClick any column header to sort; click again to reverse; multiple columns can be stacked. A ▲/▼ indicator marks the sorted column. Sorting is by the visible text of the cell.
Filter chipsEvery active search term, status filter, date range and sort shows as a removable chip above the table; clicking the ✕ on a chip clears just that condition.
Saved views & favoritesThe customer's current search / filters / sort / page-size is remembered per user and per table (in local storage) so the view is restored on the next visit.
ExportAn Export ▾ menu offers CSV (.csv), Excel (.xls) and PDF of the currently filtered rows. On this customer page export is available (no admin module permission gates it).
PaginationRows are paged, 25 per page by default, with a pager at the foot of the table.
Empty stateWhen the account genuinely has no invoices, a designed empty state appears — a receipt icon, "No invoices yet", the line "Invoices are generated automatically after each paid order — you'll be able to view and download them here", and a Browse products button. When a filter simply matches nothing, a lighter "No results found — try adjusting your search or filters" message with a Clear all button is shown instead.
⚠️
The Status filter is data-driven, not a fixed toggle

The Status facet is generated from whatever statuses are actually present. Do not promise a customer an "All / Paid" switch: if every invoice on the account is Paid, the engine has nothing to filter and the Status group is hidden. The full list (the "All" case) is simply the table with no filter applied.

Row actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
View (eye icon) Opens the full tax-invoice document for that invoice on a dedicated page. Navigates to /invoice.html?id=<invoice-id>. That page mounts the premium B2C invoice document via DOODLY_INVOICE.mountB2C (see Screen 2). Customer+
Download (download icon) Instantly downloads a receipt file for that one invoice, without opening it. A single click handler (js-invoice-dl, wired once in layout.js) builds a CSV named invoice-<id>.csv containing: Document ("DOODLY — Tax Invoice"), Invoice number, Date, Seller (legal company name), GSTIN, Registered address, Amount (₹) and GST — the seller/GSTIN/address are pulled from the brand's company profile. Customer+
Workflow — from paid order to a downloadable invoice
Customer's order is paidInvoice generated automaticallyAppears as a row in this listCustomer searches / filters / sortsView → full document · or · Download → CSV receipt
Figure — how an invoice reaches, and leaves, the Invoices list
✅
Best Practice

When helping a customer find one invoice, use the Date range plus the Search box together (e.g. type the amount or the last four digits of the invoice number) rather than scrolling. Then use the row Download button for a quick receipt, and View only when the customer needs the full itemised, printable document.

Invoice document

The full, printable tax invoice that opens when a customer clicks View.

Purpose Present a complete, GST-compliant tax invoice — itemised products, delivery summary, payment details and totals — that can be printed, saved as PDF, emailed or shared on WhatsApp.
Access Customer (own invoice) — plus a Super-Admin-only settings control on the document Path Account → Invoices → View (opens /invoice.html?id=…)

Clicking View opens the premium customer invoice built by invoice.js. It is an animated, print-ready document (count-up totals, section reveals, a paid-in-full stamp; animations switch off automatically for printing and for users who prefer reduced motion).

Invoice document — TAX INVOICE · action bar
Action barPrint · Download PDF · Email · WhatsApp · (Super Admin) Settings ⚙
HeaderBrand + tagline · Invoice no · Date · Billing period · Status badge
PartiesBilled to (name, customer ID, mobile, address) · Subscription (plan, delivery slot) · pay QR
Line itemsProduct · Qty · Unit · Unit price · GST · Amount
Delivery summaryTotal · Delivered · Skipped · Paused · Bonus chips
PaymentStatus · Method · Transaction ID · Paid on
SummarySubtotal · Discount · GST · Wallet used · Referral used · Delivery · Grand total · Outstanding / Paid in full
FooterThank-you · support contacts · terms · authorised signatory
Figure — labelled layout of the full tax-invoice document

What the document shows

SectionPurposeFields shownNotes / backend
HeaderIdentify the document.Brand name, tagline, "TAX INVOICE", Invoice number, Date, Billing period, Status badge.Brand name / tagline come from the Invoice Settings (see below).
Billed toWho the invoice is for.Customer name, Customer ID, mobile, delivery address.—
SubscriptionWhich plan was billed.Plan name, Delivery slot.Plan label is read from the saved subscription.
Pay QRScan-to-pay / verify.A UPI QR encoding the payee and amount.Shown only when "Show QR code" is enabled in settings.
Line itemsItemise what was billed.Product, Qty, Unit, Unit price, GST (% and value, or "Nil"), Amount.GST percent per product is resolved from DOODLY_GST (e.g. milk is GST-nil, ghee 12%, curd 5%).
Delivery summaryShow the month's fulfilment.Total, Delivered, Skipped, Paused, Bonus counts.Animated count-up chips.
PaymentProve settlement.Status, Method, Transaction ID, Paid on date.—
SummaryShow how the total was reached.Subtotal, Discount, GST, Wallet used, Referral reward used, Delivery charges, Grand total, and either an Outstanding balance or a "Paid in full" stamp.GST line hides if "Show GST" is turned off in settings.
FooterTrust & contact.Thank-you message, support phone / WhatsApp / website, terms, authorised signatory line.All four texts are editable in Invoice Settings.

Document action bar

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
PrintOpens the browser print dialog for a clean, animation-free copy.Calls window.print().Customer+
Download PDFSaves the invoice as a PDF.Uses the same print path (window.print() → "Save as PDF").Customer+
EmailStarts an email with the invoice number, amount and a link to view it online.Opens a pre-filled mailto: draft in the user's mail app.Customer+
WhatsAppShares a message with the invoice number, amount and view link.Opens a wa.me share link in a new tab.Customer+
Settings ⚙Opens the Invoice Settings modal to change how every invoice looks and reads.Renders the config modal; saving audits invoice.config. Only rendered for Super Admin.Super Admin
⛔
Caution — the premium document is template-only on the live site

On production, the full B2C invoice document deliberately shows a clean empty state — "No invoice to display · Your tax invoice will appear here once your billing has been generated" — because these premium documents are not yet wired to live billing and must never display fabricated figures. The rich itemised document above renders with data only when demo mode is allowed. The customer's Invoices list (Screen 1) and its per-row Download CSV are the reliable path today; treat the on-screen document as a template until live billing is connected.

Invoice Settings

Super-Admin control over how every invoice looks and reads.

Purpose Set the branding, numbering format and legal text applied to every generated invoice — one place, applied everywhere.
Access Super Admin only Path Invoice document → Settings ⚙

The gear button in the document action bar opens a modal. Every value is saved to the shared invoice configuration and applied to all invoices immediately; saving is written to the audit trail.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Brand nameName printed on the invoice header.Short textOpt.Falls back to the brand default when blank.DOODLY
TaglineSub-line under the brand name.Short textOpt.—Fresh A2 buffalo milk, delivered daily
Invoice prefixPrefix used when numbering invoices.Short textOpt.—DOODLY
Number formatTemplate that shapes the invoice number.Pattern with tokensOpt.Should contain a running-number token like {#####}.DOODLY/INV/{YYYY}/{#####}
Footer messageThank-you line at the foot of the invoice.Multi-line textOpt.—Thank you for choosing DOODLY…
Terms & conditionsLegal / payment terms printed on the invoice.Multi-line textOpt.—Payment due within the agreed credit period…
Signature lineAuthorised-signatory text above the signature line.Short textOpt.—For DOODLY Foods Pvt Ltd
Watermark textOptional diagonal watermark across the page.Short text (blank = none)Opt.Blank hides the watermark.PAID / DRAFT
Show GSTWhether the GST line appears in the summary.On / Off (checkbox)Opt.Off removes the GST row.On
Show QR codeWhether the scan-to-pay QR is shown.On / Off (checkbox)Opt.Off hides the QR block.On
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save settingsApplies all changes and re-renders the invoice with the new look.Persists the config, writes an invoice.config audit entry ("updated invoice settings"), then re-mounts the document.Super Admin
✕ / backdropCloses the modal without saving.Dismisses the dialog.Super Admin
💡
Tip

Set the Number format and Terms & conditions once, at go-live, and leave them. Changing the number format later can make new invoice numbers look inconsistent next to older ones. Use the Watermark field ("DRAFT") only while testing, and clear it before real invoices go out.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common questions when helping a customer with invoices.

SymptomLikely causeFix
The list shows "No invoices yet" for a paying customer.No invoice records have been generated for the account yet (invoices are raised automatically after a paid order), or you are on a fresh session with no data loaded.Confirm the customer has at least one paid order. Invoices appear only after payment settles; a pending order will not have an invoice.
The Status filter group is missing from the Filters panel.All invoices on the account share one status (e.g. every one is "Paid"), so there is nothing to filter by.This is expected. The full list already is the "All" view. The Status group returns as soon as a differently-statused invoice exists.
Clicking View shows an empty "No invoice to display" page.On the live site the premium document is template-only until live billing is wired, so it renders a safe empty state instead of fabricated figures.Use the row-level Download (CSV receipt) meanwhile. The full document renders once live billing is connected (or in demo mode).
The downloaded file is a .csv, not a formatted PDF.The row Download button produces a CSV receipt by design; the formatted PDF comes from inside the document via Download PDF.For a formatted invoice, click View then Download PDF (or Print). For a quick data receipt, the row CSV is correct.
The GST line or QR is missing from the document."Show GST" or "Show QR code" is turned off in Invoice Settings.A Super Admin can re-enable them from the Settings ⚙ modal on the document.

Is this the same as Business Invoices?

No. This customer page shows personal (B2C) tax invoices for a household account. Commercial partners have a separate Business Partner Statement under Admin → Commerce → Business Invoices, with credit limits, outstanding trackers and supply analytics.

Can a customer edit or delete an invoice here?

No. The list is read-only for the customer — they can only search, filter, sort, open (View) and download. Invoices are generated by the system after payment; only a Super Admin can change how they are branded and numbered, via Invoice Settings.

Where do the seller name, GSTIN and address on the downloaded receipt come from?

From the brand's company profile (legal name, GSTIN, registered address). They are stamped onto the CSV automatically at download time, so every receipt carries the correct seller details.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Bottle Tracking
§Customer Module

Bottle Tracking

A read-only, per-customer ledger of every reusable glass bottle DOODLY has issued, collected back, or lost — plus the refundable deposit held against them.

ℹ️
Where this screen sits

Bottle Tracking is a customer self-service page at /account/bottles.html. It shows the signed-in customer only their own ledger. A Super Admin normally reviews this screen while helping a customer over support, or reconciles the same numbers from the operations side under Admin → Catalogue → Bottle Inventory (the warehouse fleet) and the driver's Bottle Collection task list. This page never edits the ledger — entries are written automatically by delivery, checkout and refund flows (see the Workflow below).

Bottle Tracking page

Deposit & empties dashboard plus the full bottle ledger for one customer.

Purpose Give the customer a transparent, tamper-proof record of every bottle sent out and returned, the number still pending collection, and the deposit money held against un-returned glass.
Access Customer (owns the data — the API resolves the ledger from the logged-in user id) · Super Admin Admin may view the same data while assisting a customer Path My Account → Bottles & money → Bottle Tracking
Route /account/bottles.html Data source GET /api/bottles (authenticated) → hydrated into DOODLY_DATA.bottleLedger Manifest key account/bottles
Bottle Tracking — Header · "Request collection" button
Page headerTitle "Bottle Tracking", subtitle "Every glass bottle issued, returned and pending", and a primary "Request collection" button that opens the Bottle Return page.
KPI strip (4 cards)Total issued · Returned · Pending (awaiting collection) · Deposit held (refundable). Each card is a clickable tile that deep-links onward.
Warning noticeAmber banner reminding the customer to leave empties out for the next delivery.
Bottle ledger tableChronological list — Date · Type · Qty · Note · Balance. No search/filter toolbar; paginated 25 rows/page. Shows a friendly empty state if the customer has no bottle activity yet.
Figure — labelled layout of the Bottle Tracking screen (four stacked regions).

The four KPI cards

The KPI strip is rendered live by DOODLY_CUSTOMER (the customerKpis block with page:"bottles"). Every card is a link — clicking it navigates to the destination in the last column.

CardWhat it showsHow it is calculated (real code)Sub-labelClicks through to
Total issuedHow many "Issued" entries appear in the ledger.Count of ledger rows whose type is Issued (from the up-to-120 most-recent rows the API returns).—/account/bottles.html
ReturnedHow many "Returned" entries appear in the ledger.Count of ledger rows whose type is Returned.—/account/bottles.html
PendingEmpties still out with the customer, awaiting pickup.From the account summary: Σ ISSUED − Σ RETURNED − Σ LOST, floored at 0 (never negative)."awaiting collection"/bottle-return.html
Deposit heldRefundable money held against un-returned glass, in ₹.depositPaise ÷ 100, where depositPaise = Σ DEPOSIT_CHARGED from the ledger."refundable"/account/wallet.html
ℹ️
Two different counting methods — on purpose

Total issued and Returned count the number of ledger entries of that kind, while Pending uses the sum of bottle quantities (ISSUED − RETURNED − LOST). In everyday use each delivery issues one bottle (qty 1), so the numbers line up. If you ever see "Total issued" and "Pending" disagree in a way maths can't explain, check for a ledger row with qty > 1 or a LOST event — those are the two cases where the entry-count and the quantity-sum diverge.

Buttons & actions

Button / controlWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Request collection (header, primary)Opens the public Bottle Return page explaining the closed glass loop and how to hand back empties.Plain navigation to /bottle-return.html. It does not itself write to the ledger — the actual RETURNED entry is created later when a delivery executive collects the empties.Customer
Pending cardDeep-links to the Bottle Return page so the customer can arrange pickup.Navigation only.Customer
Deposit held cardDeep-links to the Wallet, where a returned bottle's deposit refund lands.Navigation only. A refund shows up as a DEPOSIT_REFUNDED ledger row plus a matching wallet credit.Customer
Column header (Date / Type / Qty / Note / Balance)Click to sort the ledger by that column; click again to reverse; the sort survives page reloads.Handled entirely in the browser by the DataTable engine — no server call.Customer

Bottle ledger — table specification

The table is declared as tbl("bottleLedger", {toolbar:false}), so it runs on the shared DataTable engine but with the toolbar deliberately switched off (this is a simple chronological statement, not a data-mining grid).

AspectBehaviour on this screen
ColumnsDate · Type · Qty · Note · Balance (5 columns).
SearchHidden — the search box is part of the toolbar, which is off.
Facet filtersHidden — no filter chips or filter panel on this table.
Date rangeHidden (toolbar off).
SortingAvailable by clicking any column header (multi-column, with ▲/▼ indicators); the chosen sort persists per user and table.
PaginationOn — 25 rows per page by default, newest first.
ExportHidden (CSV/Excel/PDF export lives in the toolbar, which is off).
Row actionsNone — rows are informational only.
Empty stateBottle icon with "No bottle activity yet", the line "Every bottle we deliver and collect is tracked here, along with your refundable deposit", and a "Start a subscription" call-to-action.

Reading a ledger row — event types

Each ledger row carries one event. The screen renders it as a coloured Type badge and formats the Qty column differently for bottle-movement events versus money events. The Balance column is a running count of bottles still out (recomputed in the browser, floored at 0).

Event (backend)Type badgeQty column showsEffect on BalanceMeaning
ISSUEDgreen "Issued"+ qty (e.g. +1)increasesA full bottle was delivered to the customer.
RETURNEDblue "Returned"− qty (e.g. −1)decreasesAn empty was collected back by the delivery executive.
LOSTred "Lost"− qtydecreasesA bottle was written off as broken or not returned.
DEPOSIT_CHARGEDamber "Deposit"₹ amount (from amountPaise)no changeA refundable glass-bottle deposit was charged.
DEPOSIT_REFUNDEDgreen "Refund"₹ amountno changeA deposit was refunded (e.g. after the bottle came back).
ColumnWhat it contains
DateRelative date of the entry (from createdAt).
TypeThe coloured event badge (see table above).
QtyFor bottle events: signed bottle count (+1 / −1). For deposit events: the ₹ amount.
NoteThe entry's note if present (e.g. "Collected by delivery executive", "Refundable glass-bottle deposit"); otherwise "Delivery <date>" when the row is linked to a delivery.
BalanceRunning total of bottles still out after this entry, never shown below 0.

How a ledger entry is born

The customer never writes to this ledger — it is append-only and populated automatically by operational events. Understanding those triggers is how a Super Admin explains any number on this page.

Workflow — bottle lifecycle that feeds the ledger
Deposit charged at first order → DEPOSIT_CHARGEDDriver marks delivery done with bottle out → ISSUED (+1)Empties left out → "Request collection"Executive collects empty → RETURNED (−1)Broken / not returned → LOST (−1)Deposit returned → DEPOSIT_REFUNDED + wallet credit
Figure — the events that generate ledger rows, written by delivery, checkout and refund flows (not by this screen).
Ledger rowWhich flow writes it (real endpoint)
ISSUEDDriver completes a delivery with bottles out → POST /api/driver/deliveries/[id].
RETURNEDDriver records empties collected → /api/driver/deliveries/[id] or the delivery stop update /api/delivery/stop/[id] (note "Collected by delivery executive").
DEPOSIT_CHARGEDRefundable deposit taken (e.g. at checkout / first issue).
DEPOSIT_REFUNDEDWallet refund service writes the refund row plus a matching wallet CREDIT (kind "refund").
LOSTAn operations write-off for a broken or unreturned bottle.
✅
Best Practice

When a customer disputes their pending-bottle count, don't adjust anything from this screen — it is read-only by design. Instead trace the underlying delivery: an over-count almost always means an ISSUED row exists without its matching RETURNED row because a driver forgot to tick "collected empty" at the stop. Fix it at the source (the driver's delivery record), and this ledger and every KPI recompute themselves.

💡
Tip — the deposit is separate from the bottle count
