DEPOSIT_CHARGED / DEPOSIT_REFUNDED events move money (₹), not the bottle balance. That is why the "Deposit held" card links to the Wallet, while the "Pending" card links to Bottle Return. A refund shows here as a green "Refund" row and simultaneously as a credit in the customer's wallet.

⚠️
Warning — the API returns the 120 most recent rows

GET /api/bottles returns at most the 120 latest ledger entries for the table. The Pending and Deposit held figures are computed server-side from all-time grouped totals, so they stay correct even beyond 120 rows — but "Total issued" and "Returned" cards (which count visible rows) could under-count for an extremely long-tenured customer. Trust the Pending and Deposit numbers as the authoritative balances.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"Pending" stays high after the customer returned bottles.The delivery executive did not tick "collected empty", so no RETURNED row was written.Update the delivery stop / driver record to log the collection; the ledger and Pending KPI refresh automatically.
Ledger table shows the empty state for a paying customer.No bottle events yet (brand-new customer) or the API returned no rows because the session isn't authenticated.Confirm the customer has had at least one delivery; verify they are logged in (the endpoint needs a valid user id).
"Deposit held" is ₹0 but a deposit was expected.No DEPOSIT_CHARGED row exists for this customer (deposit not levied on their plan).Check the order/checkout that should have charged the deposit; deposit policy may not apply to that plan.
Balance column looks off by one.A ledger row has qty > 1, or a LOST event reduced the balance.Read the Qty column row-by-row — Balance is the running ISSUED − RETURNED − LOST and is always floored at 0.

Frequently asked questions

Q. Can a Super Admin edit or delete a bottle ledger entry from this page?

No. The ledger is append-only and this screen is strictly read-only. Corrections are made by writing a new, opposite event (for example a RETURNED to offset an over-issued bottle) through the delivery/operations flow — never by altering history.

Q. Why does "Pending" link to Bottle Return but "Deposit held" links to the Wallet?

Because they represent two different things: Pending is a bottle count the customer clears by handing empties back (Bottle Return), while Deposit held is money that is refunded into the Wallet once the glass is returned.

Q. Where does the "Request collection" button actually take the customer?

To the public /bottle-return.html page that explains the reusable-glass loop. Pickup itself happens on the next delivery — the button starts the conversation; the driver's collection creates the RETURNED ledger row.

Q. Is this the same as the admin "Bottle Inventory" screen?

No. This customer page is the per-customer deposit-and-empties ledger. Admin → Catalogue → Bottle Inventory tracks the physical fleet of glass bottles by capacity and lifecycle stage across the whole business. They reconcile against each other but are separate views.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Addresses
§Customer Module

Addresses

Where the customer pins the exact GPS doorstep DOODLY delivers to, and manages every saved delivery address — add, edit, set default and delete — with live serviceability enforcement.

ℹ️
Note — two runtimes behind one page

The page /account/addresses.html renders the manifest block addressManager. What the visitor actually sees depends on whether they are truly signed in:

• Signed-in customer (production path): account.js → DOODLY_ACCOUNT.wireAddresses() takes over and drives the real database through /api/addresses. This is Screen A and Screen B below.
• Demo / not-signed-in preview: maps.js → DOODLY_MAPS.mountAddressManager() mounts a local card grid stored only in the browser (localStorage "doodly-addresses"). This is Screen C. The layout code guards this with if (am && !realCust), so demo cards never overwrite a real customer's saved list.

Screen A — Saved Addresses (live list)

The customer's real address book, loaded from the database.

Purpose Show every address the signed-in customer has saved, mark the default, and offer Add / Edit / Set default / Delete / open in Maps.
Access Customer (own addresses only — requireUserId + ownership check) · visible to Super Admin for support Path Account → Account → Addresses
Your saved addresses — panel · + Add address
Panel headerTitle "Your saved addresses" + primary "+ Add address" button
Address card gridAuto-fill grid of cards (min 280px each), one per saved address
Card headerLabel (Home / Office …) + green "Default" pill on the default card
Card contact lineContact name · phone (bold), when present
Card bodyOne-line summary: house/building/floor · street/area · city, state, pincode
Landmark & note"Landmark: …" muted line + italic delivery note in quotes
Card actionsMap · Edit · Set default (hidden on default) · Delete (red)
Empty statePin icon + "No addresses yet" + prompt to add one
Figure A — Saved-addresses panel with the card grid and per-card actions.

List behaviour — the list is a responsive card grid (not a sortable data table). Its behaviour is fixed by the API and the renderer:

AspectBehaviour (from the code)
SourceGET /api/addresses — returns only the signed-in user's rows (where: { userId }).
OrderDefault address first, then alphabetical by label (orderBy: [{ isDefault: "desc" }, { label: "asc" }]).
Card summaryComposed by addrSummary(): [houseNo, buildingName, floor] · [street, area] · [city, state, pincode], blanks skipped.
Default badgeGreen "Default" pill shown when isDefault = true.
Filters / search / paginationNone — the full list renders at once (a household normally has a handful of addresses).
ExportNone. (Each card has a "Map" link that opens the pin in Google Maps in a new tab.)
Empty state"No addresses yet — Add your delivery address so we know exactly where to bring your fresh milk."
Load failureAmber notice: "Couldn't load addresses: <reason / offline>".

Card & panel buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
+ Add addressOpens the Add-address modal (Screen B) with a blank form.None until Save.Customer
MapOpens the pinned location in Google Maps directions in a new browser tab.Client only — builds a google.com/maps/dir URL from the saved lat/lng (falls back to a text search when no pin).Customer
EditOpens the modal pre-filled with this address for changes.PATCH /api/addresses/{id} on save.Customer
Set defaultMakes this address the default (hidden on the current default).PATCH /api/addresses/{id} with { isDefault: true }; clears the flag on all other rows in one transaction.Customer
DeleteRemoves the address after a browser "Delete this address?" confirm.DELETE /api/addresses/{id}. Blocked with a toast if it is linked to a subscription.Customer
⚠️
Warning — deleting a linked address

If an address is attached to an active subscription, the delete fails and the customer sees a toast such as "Couldn't delete (it may be linked to a subscription)". Reassign or cancel the subscription's address first, then delete.

Screen B — Add / Edit Address

The full doorstep form: pin the map, then confirm the last-mile details.

Purpose Capture a precise, serviceable delivery address — exact GPS pin plus structured house / building / floor / landmark details — so the delivery executive reaches the doorstep first time.
Access Customer (creates/edits own rows only) Path Account → Addresses → Add address / Edit
Add address / Edit address — modal dialog
Pin your exact doorstepDraggable map (Google when a key is set, else built-in SVG map) + search box + "use my location"
Serviceability lineLive status: 📍 Detecting… / 🟢 We deliver here / 🔴 Not serviceable
Delivery contactContact name, Mobile number, Alternate number
Where should we deliver?House/Flat, Building name, Floor, Street, Area, Landmark, City, State, Pincode
More details (optional)Block / Tower, Wing, Society gate number, Door colour
Label this addressChips: Home · Office · Apartment · Friend & Family · Other (+ custom)
Delivery instructionsPreset chips + free-text note (250-char counter)
Footer"Make this my default" checkbox · Cancel · Save address / Save changes
Figure B — Add / Edit modal: map pin at the top drives the serviceability gate and auto-fills the address fields.
💡
Tip — the pin does the typing

Dragging the pin (or picking a search suggestion, or tapping "use my location") calls GET /api/geo/reverse and auto-fills pincode, area, city and state, plus house number / street / landmark when the geocoder returns them. The customer can still edit any field — a value they type by hand wins over the auto-fill (the map keeps only the GPS pin).

Field reference — every field on the form, in order. "Req." marks fields the form blocks Save on.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Map pin (GPS)Exact doorstep location for delivery & live tracking.Draggable pin / search / GPS locateAutoSets hidden lat/lng sent with the save. If no pin is dropped the backend geocodes the address (OSM) as a fallback.16.5062, 80.6480
Contact nameWho the executive asks for / calls.Text, ≤ 80 charsYesPre-filled from the profile; trimmed. Stored as contactName.Ravi Kumar
Mobile numberPrimary delivery contact number.Digits / +, 7–15 longYesClient regex /^[+]?[0-9\s-]{7,15}$/; same rule on the server. Stored as contactPhone.+91 98765 43210
Alternate contact numberBackup number to call.Digits / +, 7–15 longOpt.Same phone regex when filled. Stored as altPhone.+91 90000 11122
House / Flat numberUnit at the address.Text, ≤ 60 charsYesFeeds line1. Backend requires a composed line1 ≥ 3 chars.Flat 302 · H.No 12-45
Apartment / Building / House nameBuilding the executive looks for.Text, ≤ 120 charsYesStored as buildingName; part of line1 and the OSM geocode query.Sri Sai Residency
FloorWhich floor to climb to.DropdownYesOne of: Independent House, Ground Floor, 1st–10th Floor, Above 10th Floor. Stored as floor.2nd Floor
Street / RoadStreet name.Text, ≤ 120 charsYesAuto-filled from the pin; part of line2.MG Road
Area / LocalityNeighbourhood.Text, ≤ 80 charsYesAuto-filled from the pincode / pin unless typed. Part of line2.Benz Circle
LandmarkNearest recognisable point.Text, ≤ 120 charsYesShown on the card and woven into line2 as "(near …)".Opposite SBI Bank
CityCity of delivery.Text, ≤ 60 charsYesDefaults to "Vijayawada"; auto-set from the serviceable pincode.Vijayawada
StateState of delivery.Text, ≤ 60 charsYesDefaults to "Andhra Pradesh"; auto-set from the pincode.Andhra Pradesh
PincodeThe serviceability key.6 digits, numericYesClient /^[1-9][0-9]{5}$/; server normalises ("520 010" → "520010") and requires a match in the live serviceable list. Auto-filled from the pin.520010
Block / TowerExtra last-mile detail.Text, ≤ 60 charsOpt.Stored as block.Block B
WingExtra last-mile detail.Text, ≤ 40 charsOpt.Stored as wing.A Wing
Society gate numberWhich gate to enter by.Text, ≤ 40 charsOpt.Stored as gateNumber.Gate 2
Door colourVisual cue for the executive.Text, ≤ 40 charsOpt.Stored as doorColor.Brown
LabelNames this address.Chip or custom text ≤ 30Opt.Home / Office / Apartment / Friend & Family / Other; "Other" reveals a custom-label box. Defaults to "Home".Home
Delivery instructionsNote for the executive.Free text, ≤ 250 charsOpt.Live char counter; preset chips append text. Stored as deliveryNote.Ring the bell once. Beware of pets.
Make this my default addressSets this as the default delivery address.CheckboxOpt.First address saved is always default; ticking this clears the flag on all other rows server-side.☑

Button reference

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Use my location (⊕ in map)Centres the pin on the device's GPS position.Browser navigator.geolocation; then reverse-geocode. Toast on denial: "Couldn't get your location — drag the pin instead."Customer
Search suggestionRecentres the pin on the picked place.GET /api/geo/search?q= (≥ 3 chars, debounced) → then reverse-geocode the chosen point.Customer
CancelCloses the modal without saving.None.Customer
Save address / Save changesValidates the form, then creates or updates the address.POST /api/addresses (new) or PATCH /api/addresses/{id} (edit). Server re-checks serviceability, composes line1/line2, sets the delivery zone, geocodes if no pin, and writes an audit record.Customer
⚠️
Warning — Save is gated on serviceability

The Save button stays greyed out until the pinned pincode is confirmed serviceable (the internal svOK flag). While the check is in flight it reads "📍 Detecting address…" then "Checking delivery availability…" and Save is disabled. If the location is out of area it turns red — "🔴 Sorry! DOODLY currently does not deliver to this location." — and the box shakes if Save is forced. The backend enforces the same rule again, so a stale UI can never save an out-of-area address.

ℹ️
Note — duplicate guard (new addresses only)

When adding a new address whose house number + pincode already exist in the list, the form asks "You already have an address at this house number and pincode. Save another one anyway?" before continuing. Edits skip this check.

Workflow — Add / edit an address
Open Add / EditPin doorstep on mapReverse-geocode auto-fills fieldsServiceability check 🟢/🔴Confirm contact + house detailsSaveServer re-validates + geocode fallbackAudit + list refresh
Figure — End-to-end address save. Serviceability is checked in the browser and re-checked on the server before the row is written.
✅
Best Practice

Coach customers (and support agents doing it on their behalf) to drag the pin onto the actual doorstep, not the building centroid, and to fill Landmark, Floor and Door colour. The exact GPS drives live delivery tracking and the executive's route, so a precise pin plus a good landmark is what makes a first-time, on-time delivery.

Screen C — Demo address cards (preview only)

The offline / not-signed-in variant rendered by the maps widget.

Purpose Let a visitor explore the address experience before signing in. Data lives only in the browser (localStorage) and never reaches the database.
Access Customer (demo / signed-out) — suppressed for a real signed-in customer Path Account → Account → Addresses
Addresses (demo) — card grid + Add address tile
Address cardMini-map thumbnail + label + Default pill + Serviceable/Not-serviceable badge
Card bodyline1, line2 + area, city, state, pincode
Card linksSet default (non-default only) · Edit · Delete
Add tileDashed "+ Add address" card that opens the simple modal
ModalMap picker + serviceability line + label chips (Home/Office/Apartment/Other) + House/Flat + Landmark + read-only resolved address
Figure C — Demo card grid with per-address mini-maps; simpler than the live form and stored only in the browser.
⚠️
Warning — demo data is not real

Screen C seeds one sample "Home" address and keeps everything in localStorage "doodly-addresses". It is only shown when the visitor is not a real signed-in customer. Nothing here is written to /api/addresses, and it has fewer fields than the live form (no contact, floor, block, wing, gate, door colour or delivery note). Support staff must confirm a customer is signed in before treating what they see as their real address book.

Serviceability & geo services

The shared checks and endpoints behind the map, the pincode and Save.

Purpose Explain how DOODLY decides whether it delivers to a location, and where the data comes from.
Access Customer (public read) · configured by Admin+ via Serviceable Areas

The pincode is the single primary key for serviceability. Every check (map, typed pincode, and both save endpoints) resolves against the same live ServiceablePincode table (rows that are enabled and not soft-deleted), so an admin adding, disabling or removing a pincode in Admin → Serviceable Areas takes effect immediately with no redeploy.

EndpointUsed byReturns
GET /api/geo/reverse?lat=&lng=Pin drag / search / "use my location".Structured address (house/street/area/city/state/pincode) + serviceability (charge / slot / eta when serviceable).
GET /api/geo/search?q=The map search box (India-scoped, ≥ 3 chars).Up to 6 place suggestions; best-effort, empty on failure.
GET /api/geo/serviceable?pincode=Typed-pincode re-check (validateLive).{ valid, serviceable, area, city, state, charge, slot, eta }.
GET /api/addressesScreen A list load.The user's own addresses, default first.
POST /api/addressesSave a new address.Created row (201); re-asserts serviceability + audits.
PATCH /api/addresses/{id}Edit / set default.Updated row; location edits re-assert serviceability.
DELETE /api/addresses/{id}Delete an address.{ deleted: true }; ownership enforced.
ℹ️
Note — works with or without a Google key

When a Google Maps key is configured the picker upgrades to the real interactive Google map with Places autocomplete; otherwise it uses DOODLY's built-in dependency-free SVG map and keyless OpenStreetMap (Nominatim) geocoding. The workflow, fields and serviceability gate are identical either way, so the page always works.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common issues and quick answers.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Save button is greyed out.Serviceability not yet confirmed (svOK false) — still checking, or out of area.Wait for the 🟢 line, or move the pin to a serviceable location.
"🔴 Sorry! DOODLY currently does not deliver to this location."The pincode isn't in the serviceable list, or an admin disabled it.Pick a nearby serviceable pincode; or ask an admin to add the pincode in Serviceable Areas.
"Please enter the house/flat number and building name."Composed line1 was shorter than 3 characters.Fill both House / Flat number and Building name.
"Couldn't delete (it may be linked to a subscription)."The address is referenced by an active subscription.Reassign or cancel the subscription's address, then delete.
Map shows a plain drawn map, not Google.No Google Maps key, or the key was rejected (billing / referrer).Expected fallback — it still works. Set a valid key in the environment to get the Google map.
"Couldn't get your location — drag the pin instead."The browser denied or lacks geolocation.Drag the pin manually, or allow location access and retry.
"Couldn't load addresses: offline".The backend was unreachable when the list loaded.Check the connection / that the customer is signed in, then reload.

Does the customer have to pin a location on the map?

No — the pin is the best path because it captures exact GPS and auto-fills the pincode, but the customer can type the address by hand. If no pin is dropped, the server geocodes the typed address (via OpenStreetMap) so live delivery tracking still has a destination.

Can a customer save an address outside the delivery area?

No. Both the form and the backend require the pincode to match an enabled row in the ServiceablePincode table. An out-of-area address is blocked in the browser and rejected again on the server.

How is the default address chosen?

The first address a customer saves is automatically the default. After that, ticking "Make this my default address" (or the card's "Set default") promotes one address and clears the default flag on all others in a single transaction.

Is there a limit on how many addresses I can save?

The code sets no fixed cap — customers can keep Home, Office, a friend's place and more. Each is validated and serviceable-checked independently.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Support
§Customer Module

Support

The customer's self-service help desk — raise a support ticket, watch its status change as staff work it, and reach DOODLY by WhatsApp, phone or help articles.

ℹ️
Where this lives

This is a Customer-facing screen inside the customer account area at Admin → Customer Module → Support (/account/support.html). Every ticket a customer raises here lands in the staff desk at Admin → System → Support Tickets (/admin/support.html). A Super Admin uses this page to understand exactly what a customer sees and does; the staff-side triage, assignment and reply tools are documented in the Support Tickets module.

Support home

Raise a ticket, review your open tickets, and jump to live channels — all on one page.

Purpose Let a signed-in customer log a written support request and track its progress without calling in, while still offering WhatsApp, phone and help-article shortcuts.
Access Customer (must be signed in) — a Super Admin can view the same page while impersonating or reviewing a customer account. Path Admin → Customer Module → Support
Support — Raise a ticket or browse help
Page headerTitle "Support" and sub-line "Raise a ticket or browse help."
"Raise a ticket" formTwo-column card: Subject, Category (dropdown), Message (full-width). Green "Submit ticket" button.
"Your tickets" panelLive table of this customer's past tickets — Ticket no., Subject, Category, Status badge, Raised date. Empty state when none.
Contact cards (×3)Live chat (WhatsApp), Call us (phone), Help articles (Help Center).
Figure — labelled layout of the Support home screen (top to bottom: header, ticket form, live ticket list, contact cards)
ℹ️
The list you see is live, not the demo table

The page recipe includes a placeholder "tickets" data table, but on load the account script hides it and injects the real "Your tickets" panel above it, fed by the backend. So a customer never sees stale sample rows — only their own tickets.

Form — "Raise a ticket"

Three fields, laid out in two columns (the Message box spans the full width). The form submits to the backend as a new ticket; identity (name, email, phone) is taken from the signed-in account and is never typed here.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Subject (subject) A short one-line summary of the problem so staff can scan the queue quickly. Free text. Yes Browser + script require it; the client blocks anything shorter than 3 characters. Backend (Zod) enforces trimmed length 3–200; shorter is rejected with "Please add a short subject". Milk not delivered this morning
Category (category) Buckets the ticket so the desk can route and report on it. Dropdown, one of: Delivery issue, Billing & payments, Bottle return, Subscription, Product quality, Other. Opt. Optional; backend trims and caps at 40 characters, stored as-is. If left blank it shows as "—" in the list. Delivery issue
Message (message) The full description of the issue — what happened, when, and any order/bottle details. Free text (multi-line textarea, full width). Yes Client requires non-empty; backend requires trimmed length 1–20,000 ("Please describe your issue"). This text becomes the first customer message on the ticket thread. My 7 AM delivery didn't arrive and the app still shows "out for delivery".
ℹ️
Field names are auto-derived

The form builder turns each label into a lowercase machine name (Subject → subject, Category → category, Message → message). The submit handler reads those exact names, so renaming a label in the recipe would also rename the field the backend receives.

Buttons & actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Submit ticket Validates the form, then raises a new support ticket and refreshes the "Your tickets" list. On success a toast reads "Ticket TK-#### raised ✓" and the form clears; on failure it shows the error message. POST /api/account/support with { subject, category, message }. The call is intercepted at capture phase so the generic demo form handler never runs — a real record is written every time. Customer
Start chat (card) Opens WhatsApp chat with the DOODLY support line for the fastest reply. Link to https://wa.me/919117799143 — no backend write. Customer
Call now (card) Dials the support phone number from a mobile device. Link to tel:+919117799143. The displayed number comes from the brand config (+91 91177 99143). Customer
Browse (card) Opens the public Help Center for self-serve answers on delivery, bottles and billing. Link to /help.html — no backend write. Customer
💡
Tip — support hours & channels

The brand config publishes support hours of Mon–Sat, 8 AM – 8 PM, a support phone of +91 91177 99143, a WhatsApp line, and the support email doodlyoffl@gmail.com. A ticket can be raised any time; the WhatsApp and phone cards are best used inside those hours.

Table — "Your tickets"

Below the form, the page loads the customer's own tickets (newest first) into a live panel. It only ever shows tickets belonging to the signed-in account — the request is filtered server-side by the authenticated user, so no other customer's tickets can appear.

AspectBehaviour (from the code)
ColumnsTicket (number, e.g. TK-2042, bold) · Subject · Category (or "—") · Status (coloured badge) · Raised (created date).
Status badge coloursGreen for Resolved / Closed; blue for In Progress / Assigned; amber for everything else (Open, Waiting for Customer). The badge text is the human status label.
SortingFixed — newest ticket first (server orders by creation date, descending).
Filters / SearchNone on this customer view — the built-in demo table's toolbar and pager are switched off in the recipe, and the live panel is a plain read-only list.
PaginationNone shown; the request fetches up to 100 of the customer's tickets in one page.
Row actionsNone — the customer view is read-only. Replies, status changes and assignment happen staff-side in Support Tickets.
Empty state"No support tickets yet — Raise a ticket above and we'll get back to you."
Error stateIf the list can't load, a warning reads "Couldn't load your tickets: <reason>" (e.g. offline).
ℹ️
What the customer can and cannot see

The backend deliberately returns only safe fields to the customer: ticket number, subject, category, status & status label, priority & priority label, and the created / updated / resolved dates. Internal details — the assignee, SLA due date, internal notes, resolution text and tags — are never sent to the browser.

Ticket lifecycle

Workflow — a customer ticket from raise to close
Customer submits form Ticket created — Open, TK-#### Staff assign → Assigned Staff work → In Progress Waiting for Customer (if info needed) Resolved Closed
Figure — the six ticket statuses. The customer only ever performs the first step; the status badge on this page updates as staff move the ticket forward.

When the form is submitted, the system:

  1. Generates the next ticket number in the form TK-<n> (the counter starts around TK-2042 and steps forward, skipping any number already used).
  2. Creates the ticket with status Open and default priority Medium (the customer form does not set priority).
  3. Stamps a target response time behind the scenes based on priority — 4 h Urgent, 8 h High, 24 h Medium, 48 h Low. This SLA is staff-only and is not shown to the customer.
  4. Attaches the customer's message as the first entry on the ticket thread, plus a system note recording that the ticket was created.
  5. Fills the customer's name, email and phone from their signed-in account — these are trusted from the session, never from the form.
✅
Best Practice

Coach customers (and support staff onboarding on this page) to pick the most specific Category and write a clear one-line Subject. Good categorisation drives the desk's routing and reporting; a vague "Other / help" ticket is slower to triage. For anything time-critical (missed delivery, payment failure), the Start chat WhatsApp card gets a faster human response than a ticket.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"Submit ticket" does nothing / shows "Please add a subject and describe your issue."Subject is under 3 characters or the Message box is empty.Enter a real subject (3+ characters) and a description, then submit again.
Ticket list shows "Couldn't load your tickets".The device is offline or the backend session isn't authenticated.Check the connection and confirm the customer is signed in; reload the page.
New ticket doesn't appear after submitting.The submit failed (a toast error shows the reason), so no ticket was written.Read the toast message, correct the input (e.g. shorten an over-long subject to ≤200 chars), and resubmit.
Customer says a ticket "disappeared".Staff merged, closed, or soft-deleted it on the desk side.Look it up by number in Admin → System → Support Tickets; deleted tickets can be restored there.
Old sample tickets briefly flash on screen.The account script hasn't finished swapping the demo table for the live panel yet.This clears on its own once the live list loads; if it persists, hard-refresh to reload the account script.

Q · Can a customer edit or reply to a ticket from this page?

A. Not from here. This screen is for raising a ticket and tracking its status. All replies, status changes and resolution notes are handled by staff in the Support Tickets desk; the customer sees the outcome as an updated status badge.

Q · Where does the ticket number come from, and is it unique?

A. The system assigns a sequential TK-#### number at creation and checks for collisions before saving, so each ticket has its own unique number that both the customer and staff use to refer to it.

Q · Does raising a ticket set a priority?

A. Every customer ticket starts at Medium priority. Support staff can raise or lower priority (Low / Medium / High / Urgent) on the desk side; the customer sees the current priority label but cannot set it.

Q · What's the difference between "Live chat" here and the on-screen assistant?

A. The Live chat card opens WhatsApp to reach a human on the DOODLY support line. That is separate from any in-app assistant widget; use the card when the customer wants a real person quickly, and a ticket when they want a written, tracked record of the issue.

DOODLYCustomer Module · Profile, Notifications & Settings
§Customer Module

Profile, Notifications & Settings

The signed-in customer's self-service account hub — edit personal details, read and clear the in-app inbox, and control language, notification opt-ins and password security.

ℹ️
Note — who sees these screens

These three screens live under the storefront Account menu, not the Admin console. Every one of them is strictly own-data only: the backend scopes every read and write to the verified signed-in user id, so a customer (or any staff member who is also a signed-in account holder) can only ever see and change their own record. A Super Admin previewing these pages sees their own profile — never another customer's. To edit a different customer's details as an operator, use Admin → Commerce → Customers instead.

My Profile

Edit your name, phone and email; the record that drives every invoice, delivery and reward.

Purpose Let the customer keep their own personal details current. The saved name/email flow onto invoices and notifications; completing all three fields unlocks a one-time loyalty bonus.
Access Customer self-service · any signed-in account (own record only) Path Account → Profile
Header — Profile · "Your personal details."
Full nameText input, pre-filled from the live account
PhoneTel input, pre-filled with the account phone
EmailEmail input, pre-filled with the account email
AreaText input (display only — not persisted here)
Save profilePrimary button — PATCHes the profile
ResetGhost button — clears edits back to loaded values
Figure — two-column profile form (name · phone / email · area) with Save and Reset.

The form renders from the manifest as a two-column card. On page load the client calls GET /api/account/profile and fills the first three inputs from the live record. When you press Save profile, a submit handler collects the inputs and sends PATCH /api/account/profile.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Full nameThe name shown on invoices, greetings and delivery slipsFree text, up to 80 charactersYesServer: trimmed, min 1, max 80. Saved to User.nameVivek Devineni
PhoneContact number for delivery and OTP-style updatesDigits, spaces, dashes and an optional leading +, 7–15 charsOpt.Server regex ^[+]?[0-9\s-]{7,15}$; a blank value is sent as "unchanged". Must be unique — a clash returns 409 Conflict "already in use by another account."+91 91177 99143
EmailWhere invoices, receipts and account emails are sentA valid email addressOpt.Server lower-cases and validates; a blank value is sent as "unchanged". Must be unique — a clash returns 409 Conflict.you@example.com
AreaLocality hint shown in the form onlyFree textOpt.Not saved by this form. Only inputs 1–3 (name, phone, email) are read on submit. Manage the real delivery locality under Account → Addresses.Benz Circle, Vijayawada
⚠️
Warning — the "Area" box is cosmetic here

The renderer only reads the first three inputs on submit, so anything typed into Area is ignored and never stored. Serviceable delivery locality, house number, landmark and GPS pin all belong to the Addresses screen. Don't rely on the profile Area for delivery routing.

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save profileSends the edited name/phone/emailPATCH /api/account/profile → validates + uniqueness check → updates User → writes audit account.profile.update → mirrors name/email into the local session → toast "Profile saved ✓". If name + email + phone are all present it also awards the one-time profile-complete loyalty bonus (idempotent).Customer (self)
ResetReverts the form fields to the values last loaded, discarding unsaved editsClient-side form reset only — no requestCustomer (self)
Workflow — saving a profile edit
Page loads → GET profileFields pre-fillCustomer edits name / phone / emailSave profile → PATCHValidate + uniquenessUser updated + auditToast + loyalty bonus if complete
Figure — end-to-end profile save, from pre-fill to the profile-complete reward.
✅
Best Practice

Encourage customers to fill all three of name, email and phone. Beyond keeping invoices and delivery contact correct, a complete profile triggers a one-time DOODLY Pure Rewards bonus, and a verified email/phone is what makes password-reset and notifications actually reach them.

Notifications

The in-app inbox — delivery updates, reminders and rewards, with read and delete controls.

Purpose Show the customer their latest account notifications and let them mark items read, delete them, or clear everything at once.
Access Customer self-service · own inbox only Path Account → Notifications
Header — Notifications · "Delivery updates, reminders and rewards." · Mark all as read
Feed listLatest 50 items, newest first, each with an icon
Unread markerHighlighted row until it is read
Title + subtitleThe notification message and its detail line
Row actions✓ Mark as read (unread only) · ✕ Delete
Empty state"You're all caught up — No notifications right now."
Figure — scrolling notification feed with per-row read/delete and a bulk "Mark all as read".

On load the feed is hydrated from GET /api/account/notifications, which returns the 50 most recent notifications (newest first) plus the unread count. Each row shows an icon, a bold title and a subtitle. The client then attaches the action controls to every row, and adds a Mark all as read button to the page header when at least one item is unread.

ElementDetail
SourceGET /api/account/notifications — Notification rows scoped to the signed-in user id
OrderNewest first (createdAt desc)
LimitLatest 50 items per load
UnreadRows with no readAt render with an "unread" highlight; the endpoint also returns the total unread count
Columns shownIcon · Title (message) · Subtitle (detail line)
Row actions✓ Mark as read (shown only while unread) · ✕ Delete
Bulk actionMark all as read (page header — appears only when something is unread)
Empty stateAn honest "You're all caught up / No notifications right now." panel
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
✓ Mark as readClears the unread highlight on one row and removes its ✓ controlPOST /api/account/notifications { action:"read", id } → stamps readAt on that row (scoped to the user id) → toast "Marked as read"Customer (self)
✕ DeleteFades out and removes one notificationPOST /api/account/notifications { action:"delete", id } → deletes that row (scoped to the user id)Customer (self)
Mark all as readClears every unread highlight and removes the ✓ controls at oncePOST /api/account/notifications { action:"readAll" } → stamps readAt on all of the user's unread rows → toast "All caught up ✓"Customer (self)
ℹ️
Note — read vs delete

Mark as read keeps the item but stops it counting as unread. Delete permanently removes it from the inbox. Both operations only ever touch rows that belong to the signed-in user, so there is no way to affect another customer's notifications.

Settings

Four tabs — General, Notifications, Security and Billing — for preferences and account control.

Purpose Central place for the customer to set their language, choose which channels DOODLY may contact them on, change their password, sign out everywhere, and jump to billing pages.
Access Customer self-service · own preferences only Path Account → Settings
Header — Settings · "Preferences, notifications and account." · tabbed
Tab barGeneral · Notifications · Security · Billing
General panelLanguage selector + Save settings
Notifications panelFive On/Off channel toggles + Save
Security panelChange password + sign out of all devices
Billing panelQuick tiles to Subscription / Wallet / Invoices
Figure — the four-tab Settings screen; General and Notifications persist, Security acts, Billing links out.

Tab 1 — General

A single-field form for the customer's interface language. It pre-fills from GET /api/account/settings and saves via PATCH /api/account/settings.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
LanguagePreferred interface / communication languageEnglish · తెలుగు (coming soon, disabled) · हिन्दी (coming soon, disabled)YesMapped to a code and validated as one of en / te / hi; upserted onto CustomerPreference.language (default en)English
ℹ️
Note

Only English is selectable today — Telugu and Hindi appear as disabled "coming soon" options. The subtitle on the panel says exactly that.

Tab 2 — Notifications

Five On/Off dropdowns control which channels DOODLY may use. Each pre-fills from the customer's saved preferences and saves back with PATCH /api/account/settings as booleans. These opt-ins are honoured by the transactional messaging layer, so turning a channel Off genuinely stops those messages.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendDefault
Email updatesTransactional emails (orders, deliveries, receipts)On / OffOpt.Boolean → emailOptInOn
SMS updatesText-message alertsOn / OffOpt.Boolean → smsOptInOff
WhatsApp updatesTransactional WhatsApp messagesOn / OffOpt.Boolean → whatsappOptInOn
Push notificationsBrowser / app push alertsOn / OffOpt.Boolean → pushOptInOn
Promotions & offersMarketing messages and dealsOn / OffOpt.Boolean → marketingOptInOn
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save settings (General)Persists the chosen languagePATCH /api/account/settings { language } → upserts CustomerPreference → audit account.settings.update → toast "Settings saved ✓"Customer (self)
Save notificationsPersists all five channel opt-insPATCH /api/account/settings { emailOptIn, smsOptIn, whatsappOptIn, pushOptIn, marketingOptIn } → upserts CustomerPreference → audit → toast "Notification preferences saved ✓"Customer (self)

Tab 3 — Security

Two real actions: change the account password, and sign out of every device. A reset-by-email link is offered for customers who have forgotten their current password.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Current passwordProves it is really you before changing the passwordYour existing passwordYesClient requires it; server re-verifies it against the stored bcrypt hash (when a password is set). Wrong value → "Your current password is incorrect."••••••••
New passwordThe password you want to switch toStrong passwordYesClient: at least 8 characters. Server (authoritative): 8–72 chars and must include an uppercase letter, a lowercase letter, a number and a special character.Milk@2026
Confirm new passwordGuards against typos in the new passwordMust equal New passwordYesClient: must match New password ("New passwords don't match.")Milk@2026
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Update passwordValidates the three fields, then changes the password and signs you out to re-authenticatePOST /api/account/password { currentPassword, newPassword } → rate-limited (8 / minute) → verifies current → writes new bcrypt hash, clears forcePwReset, increments tokenVersion (revokes every session) → audit account.password.change → after ~1.4 s the client clears its token and redirects to the customer login.Customer (self)
Sign out of all devicesEnds every logged-in session, including this oneConfirm prompt → POST /api/logout → increments tokenVersion so all bearer tokens are instantly rejected → audit auth.logout → local token cleared and redirect to loginCustomer (self)
Reset it via email →Link for a forgotten current passwordNavigates to /forgot-password.html (email-based reset flow)Customer (self)
Workflow — changing your password
Enter current + new + confirmClient checks (≥8, match)Update password → POSTServer verifies current + strengthHash saved, tokenVersion bumpedAll sessions revokedRedirect to login → sign in again
Figure — a password change deliberately signs the customer out everywhere and forces a fresh sign-in.
⛔
Caution — both Security actions log you out everywhere

Updating the password and "Sign out of all devices" each bump the account's tokenVersion, which instantly invalidates every active session — including the one you are using. This is intentional (it locks out anyone else who was signed in), but it means you must sign in again straight afterwards. Warn customers before they click.

Tab 4 — Billing

An informational panel with three quick-link tiles. It stores nothing; it simply routes the customer to the relevant billing pages.

TileGoes to
Subscription/account/subscription.html
Wallet/account/wallet.html
Invoices/account/invoices.html
✅
Best Practice

Set notification preferences with intent: keep at least one transactional channel (Email or WhatsApp) On so delivery and payment alerts still arrive, and use Promotions & offers as the single switch for marketing. If a customer complains about "too many messages," check this tab first — turning off Promotions usually solves it without silencing the essential delivery updates.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"That email / phone is already in use by another account."Another DOODLY account already holds that email or phone number (uniqueness is enforced)Use a different value, or locate and merge the duplicate account under Admin → Commerce → Customers.
Profile "Area" edit doesn't stickThe Area box on Profile is display-only and never savedSet the delivery locality on Account → Addresses instead.
"New password must be at least 8 characters" / "New passwords don't match"Client-side checks failed before sendingEnter a new password of 8+ characters and type it identically in Confirm.
Password rejected even though it is 8+ charactersServer strength rules are stricter than the client hintInclude an uppercase letter, a lowercase letter, a number and a special character (max 72 chars).
"Your current password is incorrect."The current password entered doesn't match the stored hashRe-enter it carefully, or use "Reset it via email →" to reset without knowing it.
Signed out immediately after changing the passwordExpected — the change revokes all sessionsSign in again with the new password; this is by design.
"Please try again" on password change after several attemptsRate limit hit (8 changes per minute per account)Wait about a minute and retry.
Notifications list is emptyNo notifications exist for this account yetNone needed — the "You're all caught up" state is correct.
Fields don't pre-fill / saves fail silentlyThe page is in demo mode (not truly signed in) or the token expiredConfirm a real sign-in (a valid token in the session); sign in again if needed.

Frequently asked questions

Can a Super Admin edit another customer's profile from these screens?

No. Every request here is scoped to the signed-in user's own id, so these pages only ever show and change your own account. To edit another customer's details as an operator, use the Customers module in the Admin console.

Why does changing my password log me out of this device too?

Changing the password (and "Sign out of all devices") increments the account's token version, which invalidates every existing session for security. You'll be redirected to sign in again with the new password.

If I turn off WhatsApp or Email here, will I still get delivery alerts?

Those alerts respect your opt-ins, so turning a channel Off stops its messages. Keep at least one transactional channel (Email or WhatsApp) On so you still receive delivery and payment notifications; use the Promotions & offers toggle to silence marketing only.

What does completing my profile unlock?

Once your name, email and phone are all filled in and saved, DOODLY awards a one-time Pure Rewards "profile complete" loyalty bonus. It is idempotent, so it is granted only the first time all three are present.

Module chapter

Admin · Commerce

7 screen groups documented from the live application.

DOODLYAdmin · Commerce · Orders
§Admin · Commerce

Orders

A single, searchable list of every order placed across the whole DOODLY platform — who ordered, what they ordered, how much they paid, whether the money cleared and where the order is in fulfilment.

ℹ️
Where this screen lives and what it is

This is the staff-facing Orders list at route /admin/orders.html (manifest key admin/orders). It is assembled from just two blocks: a page head (title "Orders", sub-line "Every order across the platform.", with no action buttons) and a single table bound to the adminOrders dataset. Unlike the customer's My Account → Orders page — which shows only that one customer's orders — this screen shows all customers' orders in one place, pulled live from the DOODLY database via GET /api/admin/orders.

⚠️
This screen is read-only by design

The Orders list is a monitoring and lookup screen. There is no header button, no per-row "Manage" button and no inline edit on this page — you cannot create, edit, cancel, refund or delete an order from here. Those lifecycle actions happen elsewhere: a customer cancels/repeats from their own My Account → Orders; refunds are handled under Admin → Commerce → Payments; fulfilment moves under Admin → Operations → Delivery Mgmt; recurring plans under Admin → Commerce → Subscriptions. Use this screen to find and read orders, then jump to the right screen to act.

ℹ️
Live vs. demo data — read the banner

When the page loads it calls the backend and shows a status banner just above the table. A green "● Live — N order(s) from the DOODLY database (…)" banner means you are looking at real orders. If the backend can't be reached you'll see "⚠ Backend offline — couldn't load live data."; if your role lacks permission you'll see "⚠ No permission to view orders (403)." Any other error is shown verbatim as "⚠ <message>". Before you trust a count on this screen, confirm the green Live banner is showing.

Orders — platform-wide order list

The full table of all orders with instant search, facet filters, sort, export and pagination.

Purpose Give staff one authoritative view of every order on the platform — its number, the customer, what type of order it is, the amount, the payment result and the current fulfilment status — so they can look up an order during a support call, spot failed payments or on-hold orders, and export a filtered slice for reporting.
Access Super Admin (full) · Admin (full) · Support (manage). The backend requires the orders : view permission; roles without it get a 403 and an empty table. No other role can open this screen. Path Admin → Commerce → Orders
Header — Orders · "Every order across the platform." (no action buttons)
Page headTitle "Orders" and the sub-line "Every order across the platform." There are no buttons in the header — this is a view-only screen.
Live bannerA one-line status strip above the table: green "● Live — N order(s)…", or an amber/red warning if the backend is offline or the role lacks permission.
Table toolbarInstant search box, a Filters button (Payment and Status facets), a Saved views dropdown, a favourite (★) toggle, an Export ▾ menu and a Refresh button.
Filter chipsRemovable chips for every active search term / facet / sort, plus "Clear all" and "Reset".
Orders tableSix columns — Order, Customer, Items, Amount, Payment, Status — one row per order, newest first. Rows are read-only (no row buttons).
Payment & Status pillsTwo colour-coded badge columns: the payment result (Paid / Pending / Failed / Refunded) and the fulfilment status.
Pagination barRows-per-page selector (10 / 25 / 50 / 100, default 25), page numbers, a "1–25 of N" count and a Go-to-page box.
Figure — labelled layout of the Admin Orders screen.

Table columns

The adminOrders table has a fixed six-column layout. Each cell is formatted on the client from the raw order record returned by the backend.

ColumnWhat it showsBackend sourceExample
OrderThe short, human-readable order number — the letters "DOO-" followed by the last six characters of the order id, upper-cased."DOO-" + id.slice(-6).toUpperCase()DOO-4F9A2C
CustomerThe name of the customer who placed the order; a dash when no name is on file.user.name (else "—")Priya Nair
ItemsThe order type, title-cased — this column labels what kind of order it is, not the individual products.type → Subscription / One time / Extra / SampleSubscription
AmountThe order total in rupees (bold), formatted with Indian digit grouping.totalPaise ÷ 100, via ₹ toLocaleString("en-IN")₹1,200
PaymentA colour-coded pill showing whether the money cleared (see the Payment badges table).derived from status (PaymentStatus)Paid (green)
StatusA colour-coded pill showing the fulfilment stage — the live delivery status when the order has a delivery, otherwise a fallback derived from the payment status (see the Status badges table).delivery.status (else fallback)Out for delivery (amber)
ℹ️
"Items" is the order type, not the product list

Despite the column header, the Items cell on this admin screen shows the order type (Subscription, One time, Extra, Sample), not the line-item products. The backend does return richer detail (customer email & phone, the discount, the delivery date, the invoice number) that this table does not render — see "What the backend returns but the table hides" below. To read the actual products and money breakdown of a specific order, open it from the customer's My Orders view or the invoice.

Payment badges (Payment column)

The Payment pill maps the order's payment status one-to-one:

PillColourPayment statusMeaning
PaidGreenPAIDPayment captured successfully — the money has cleared.
PendingAmberPENDINGOrder created but payment not yet confirmed (awaiting the gateway/webhook, or a manual/COD flow).
FailedRedFAILEDThe payment attempt failed — no money captured.
RefundedGreyREFUNDEDA previously paid order was refunded (e.g. after a cancellation, credited to the customer's wallet).

Status badges (Status column)

The Status pill is computed in two layers. If the order has a linked delivery, the pill shows that live delivery status (title-cased, amber). If it does not, the pill falls back to a fulfilment label derived from the payment status.

WhenPill shownColourMeaning
Order has a deliveryDelivery status — one of: Scheduled, Assigned, Accepted, Packed, Out for delivery, On the way, Reached, Delivered, Failed, SkippedAmberThe real position of the delivery in the operations pipeline; this always wins over the payment-derived fallback.
No delivery · payment PAIDActiveGreenPaid and moving, but no delivery record is linked yet.
No delivery · payment PENDINGProcessingAmberOrder placed, payment not yet confirmed and no delivery scheduled.
No delivery · payment FAILEDOn holdRedPayment failed and nothing is scheduled — the order is stuck.
No delivery · payment REFUNDEDRefundedGreyMoney was returned; the order is closed out.
💡
Tip — the two pill columns answer different questions

Read Payment to answer "did we get the money?" and Status to answer "where is the order?". A healthy fresh order is often Paid + Out for delivery; a problem order to chase is Failed + On hold. Filter on those two facets together to build a work queue.

Order types (the "Items" column values)

TypeShown asWhat it is
SUBSCRIPTIONSubscriptionA delivery generated from a recurring milk plan (the default order type).
ONE_TIMEOne timeA single, non-recurring purchase.
EXTRAExtraAn add-on / extra order on top of a subscription (e.g. a top-up).
SAMPLESampleA trial / sample-pack order.

Table specification — the unified DataTable engine

Like every DOODLY list, the Orders table is upgraded by the shared DataTable engine (DOODLY_TABLE). It works over the order rows already loaded in the browser, so search / filter / sort are instant and need no extra server round-trips.

CapabilityBehaviour on this screen
ColumnsOrder, Customer, Items, Amount, Payment, Status (six, fixed).
SearchOne instant search box ("Search orders…"). Matches across every visible cell — so you can find by order number, customer name, type, amount or status text. Debounced (~180 ms).
Filters (facets)Auto-detected from the data: a Payment facet and a Status facet, each listing only the values that actually occur, with a count. Tick one or more values to narrow; a badge on the Filters button shows how many facet values are active. (Sub-titles list All / Active / Processing / On hold as the expected states.)
Date rangeNot available on this screen. The rows carry no date column, so the DataTable shows no date-range picker here. To filter orders by date, use the backend's from/to parameters or export and filter in a spreadsheet.
SortingClick any column header to sort (▲/▼). Hold Shift and click more headers for multi-column sort. Amount sorts numerically; text columns sort alphabetically.
PaginationRows-per-page 10 / 25 / 50 / 100 (default 25), first/prev/next/last controls, numbered pages, a "from–to of total" count and a Go-to-page box.
Saved views & favouritesSave a search+filter+sort combination as a named view, and star this screen as a favourite. Both are stored per-user in the browser (localStorage). The active view (search / filters / sort / page-size) also persists across reloads.
Filter chipsEvery active search term, facet value and sort shows as a removable chip; "Clear all" and "Reset" wipe them in one click.
ExportsExport the current (filtered, sorted) view as CSV, Excel (.xls) or PDF. The button is disabled for roles without the orders : export permission. Every export is written to the audit trail.
RefreshThe ↻ button re-applies the current view. (It re-renders from data already in memory; to re-pull from the backend, reload the page.)
Row actionsNone. Rows are not clickable and carry no buttons — this is a read-only list.

How the list loads

When the Orders route mounts, the front end calls GET /api/admin/orders?pageSize=200, maps each order into a table row, replaces the adminOrders dataset and re-mounts the DataTable, then shows the Live banner with the row count and the API base URL.

⚠️
Only the 100 most recent orders load

The front end asks for pageSize=200, but the backend caps the result at the 100 most recent orders (ordered newest-first) and ignores the pageSize value. So the table shows at most 100 rows and the on-screen search/filter only ever look within that window. If an order isn't in the list, it is most likely older than the newest 100 — narrow the query at the source (see the next note) rather than assuming it doesn't exist.

ℹ️
The backend supports server-side filters this screen doesn't use

GET /api/admin/orders accepts status (PENDING / PAID / FAILED / REFUNDED), type (SUBSCRIPTION / ONE_TIME / EXTRA / SAMPLE) and q (matches customer name, email or phone). The Orders page does not send these — it fetches the recent 100 and filters in the browser. For power lookups (e.g. every FAILED order, or all orders for one phone number) an admin can hit the endpoint with those query parameters directly.

What the backend returns but the table hides

The API selects more per order than the six visible columns. These fields are fetched and available (for tooling, exports or a future detail view) even though they are not shown as columns here:

Returned fieldMeaningShown in table?
createdAtWhen the order was placed (used for the newest-first sort).No
discountPaiseThe plan discount applied to the order.No
user.email, user.phoneCustomer contact details (searchable via the backend q param).No
delivery.dateThe scheduled delivery date (only delivery.status is shown).No
invoice.numberThe generated invoice number for the order.No

Roles & access

Access to Orders is governed by the DOODLY permission matrix. The screen loads only for roles granted the orders module, and the backend independently enforces orders : view on every request.

RoleLevel on OrdersCan do here
Super AdminFull (all actions)View the list and export.
AdminFullView the list and export.
SupportManage (view / create / edit / export)View the list and export.
Manager, Accountant, Delivery Executive, Customer & other operational rolesNoneNo access — the screen 403s and shows an empty table with the "No permission" banner.
ℹ️
Permission vocabulary vs. what this screen exposes

The orders module defines a rich set of actions in the permission matrix — view, create, edit, delete, export, approve, print, cancel, restore, download. That vocabulary controls order-related actions across the platform (for example a customer cancelling their own order, or export gating here). This particular Orders screen only ever exercises view (to load the list) and export (the Export button). It never creates, edits, cancels or deletes an order, regardless of how much access your role has.

Workflow — using the Orders screen to triage
Open Admin → Commerce → OrdersConfirm green "Live" bannerSearch by number / customer, or filter Payment + StatusRead the row (type, amount, payment, fulfilment)Export the filtered view if neededJump to Payments / Deliveries / Subscriptions to act
Figure — the read-then-act pattern this screen is built for.
✅
Best Practice

Build a reusable Saved view for the two problem states you care about — e.g. filter Payment = Failed for a payments follow-up queue, or Status = On hold for stuck orders — and star the screen as a favourite. Because the active view also persists across reloads, your triage filter is one click away every shift. When you need everything (not just the recent 100) or a date range, export or query the API with status/type/q parameters rather than scrolling.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common Orders-screen questions a Super Admin will field.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Table is empty with "⚠ No permission to view orders (403)."The signed-in role lacks the orders : view permission.Grant the role the Orders module (view or higher) under Admin → System → Roles & Permissions, or sign in as Super Admin / Admin / Support.
Table is empty with "⚠ Backend offline — couldn't load live data."The backend API was unreachable when the page loaded.Check backend availability and reload. The page shows an honest empty state rather than stale demo rows.
A recent order isn't in the list.Only the newest 100 orders are loaded; older orders fall outside that window.Query the API with ?q= (name/email/phone) or ?status=/?type= to fetch it directly, or find it via the customer's own Orders page or the invoice.
No date filter appears in the toolbar.By design — the order rows carry no date column, so the DataTable hides the date-range control on this screen.Use the backend from/to parameters, or export to CSV/Excel and filter by date there.
The Export button is greyed out.The role does not have the orders : export permission.Grant export on the Orders module, or ask a Super Admin/Admin to run the export.
Clicking a row does nothing.Expected — this is a read-only list with no row detail or actions.To act on an order, open the relevant screen (Payments for refunds, Deliveries for fulfilment, the customer's My Orders for cancel/repeat).
The "Items" column just says "Subscription" / "One time".That column shows the order type, not the product list.Open the order's invoice or the customer's My Orders detail to see the actual products.

Can I cancel, refund or delete an order from this screen?

No. Orders is view-only. There are no create/edit/cancel/delete controls here. Cancellations happen from the customer's own My Orders page (within the allowed window); refunds are handled under Admin → Commerce → Payments. There is no hard-delete of orders anywhere in the product.

Why does the Status column sometimes show a delivery stage and sometimes "Active"/"Processing"?

When an order has a linked delivery, the Status pill shows that live delivery stage (e.g. Packed, Out for delivery, Delivered). When it has no delivery yet, the pill falls back to a label derived from the payment status: Paid → Active, Pending → Processing, Failed → On hold, Refunded → Refunded.

Does this screen include B2B orders and subscriptions?

This is the orders feed (subscription-generated, one-time, extra and sample orders). Recurring plans have their own screen at Admin → Commerce → Subscriptions, and business orders live under Admin → Commerce → B2B Orders. All three reuse the same DataTable engine, so search/filter/export behave identically.

How current is the data — do I need to refresh?

The list is fetched once when the page opens (the newest 100 orders). The ↻ Refresh button re-applies your current search/filter over the data already in memory; to pull fresh orders from the database, reload the page.

DOODLYAdmin · Commerce · Subscriptions
§Admin · Commerce

Subscriptions

The back-office register of every recurring milk plan on DOODLY — a live, searchable, exportable list of active and lapsed subscriptions, backed by a full management engine that lets an Admin create a plan for a customer and pause, resume, skip, edit, cancel, refund or re-mandate it.

ℹ️
What this module is

A subscription is a customer's standing order for milk — a chosen bottle (variant), a quantity, a delivery slot and a plan length (for example a 30-day or 90-day plan). The engine delivers automatically each morning, honours a strict 8 PM cut-off for changes, and respects skip and pause rules. This screen is where the office watches and manages all of them. It is a separate module from Subscription Billing (which handles the AutoPay charges) and from the customer's own My Subscription self-service page.

⚠️
Strictly Admin & Super Admin

Every subscription endpoint is guarded by requireSubsAdmin. Only the Admin and Super Admin roles may view or manage subscriptions — never a customer, Delivery Executive, Accountant, Manager, or even Support. Any other role receives a hard 403 Forbidden from the server; the on-screen filtering is only for convenience, the real security boundary is in the backend guard.

Subscriptions register — list & KPIs

The live table of every subscription with four headline numbers.

Purpose Give the office a single, always-current view of the subscriber base — how many are active, paused, new and churning — and a filterable, exportable table to find any one plan.
Access Super Admin · Admin only Path Admin → Commerce → Subscriptions (/admin/subscriptions.html)
Header — Subscriptions · "Active and lapsed subscriptions."
KPI stripFour cards: Active · Paused · New (wk) · Churn. Static demo numbers on first paint, overwritten live from the stats endpoint.
Live bannerGreen "● Live — N subscription(s) from the DOODLY database" once the backend responds; red warning if offline or 403.
ToolbarSearch box · Filters button (facets + date range) · Export ▾ (CSV / Excel / PDF) — from the unified DataTable engine.
Subscriptions tableColumns: Order · Customer · Items · Amount · Payment · Status. One row per subscription, mapped from the live record.
Filter chips + pagerRemovable active-filter chips above the table; page-size selector and page controls below.
Figure — labelled layout of the Subscriptions register
ℹ️
How the page comes alive

On load the page paints the recipe (header, four demo KPI cards, a table shell). Then wireSubscriptionsBackend() calls GET /api/admin/subscriptions?pageSize=200, maps each record into a table row, re-mounts the table and shows the green live banner. A second call to GET /api/admin/subscriptions/stats overwrites the four KPI numbers. If the backend is unreachable you get an amber/red banner and the demo numbers stay — so the layout is never blank.

The four KPI cards

Each card shows a big number and a label. The label text is fixed by the page; the number is replaced live by the stats endpoint (matched by the label text).

CardWhat it countsLive sourceHow it is computedExample
ActiveCurrently running subscriptionsstats.activeCount of subscriptions whose status is ACTIVE1,284
PausedTemporarily halted plansstats.pausedCount of PAUSED plus VACATION status96
New (wk)Recently started plansstats.newThisMonthCount of subscriptions created since the 1st of the current month (label says "wk", value is month-to-date)38
ChurnShare of subscribers who cancelledderivedcancelled ÷ total × 100, one decimal, e.g. 2.1%; shows 0% when there are no subscriptions2.1%
💡
Tip — the stats endpoint returns more than four numbers

Behind the four visible cards, /stats also returns total, cancelled, expired (completed + lapsed active/paused past their end date), autopayOn (active plans on AutoPay), renewalsDue7d (active plans ending in the next 7 days), mrrPaise (monthly recurring revenue = Σ daily price × 30 across active plans), trialCashback (credited count and amount) and newThisMonth. These power reporting and can be surfaced without new backend work.

The subscriptions table — columns

The table reuses the shared "Orders" layout (dataset adminOrders), so its six columns are labelled for orders but each row is a subscription. The mapping below is exactly what mapApiSub() puts in each cell.

ColumnShowsSource fieldNotes
OrderShort subscription IDshortIdLast 8 characters of the record id, upper-cased (falls back to last 6 if no shortId)
CustomerSubscriber namecustomer.name"—" if the linked customer has no name
ItemsWhat is delivered each dropitems[]Joined as "qty× product variant", e.g. "2× A2 Buffalo Milk 500 ml"; falls back to the plan name
AmountPlan total (₹)planTotalPaisePer-delivery price × plan days, less the plan discount; shown in rupees
PaymentRenewal method badgeautoRenewGreen AutoPay when auto-renew is on, grey Manual when off
StatusLifecycle badgestatus / expiredActive · Paused · Vacation · Cancelled · Completed; a red Expired badge overrides when the plan is past its end date but not cancelled

Status badge colours

BadgeColourMeaning
ActiveGreenRunning normally, delivering on schedule
PausedAmberTemporarily halted (customer or office paused it)
VacationAmberA dated pause window (holiday hold)
ExpiredRedPast the end date but never cancelled — needs a renewal decision
CancelledGreyClosed by cancellation; will not renew
CompletedBlueRan its full term and finished cleanly

Table controls (search · filter · sort · export · pagination)

The list is driven by the unified DataTable engine, so it carries the same tools as every other admin table.

ControlBehaviour
SearchFree-text box ("Search orders…") filters the loaded rows across all columns as you type.
FiltersA slide-out panel with facet checkboxes (built from the loaded data), a date-range preset/custom picker, and a "Clear all filters" button. The recipe seeds status chips All · Active · Paused · On hold.
SortingClick a column header to sort; multiple sort keys are supported and shown as removable chips.
Filter chipsEach active search / facet / sort appears as a removable chip above the table; click the × to drop just that one.
ExportExport ▾ menu offers CSV (.csv), Excel (.xls) and PDF of the current view. The button is disabled for roles without export permission.
PaginationPage-size selector and page controls at the foot. The page pulls up to 200 subscriptions in one call, then paginates them client-side.
Saved views & favouritesYour search/filter/sort/date/page-size state is remembered per user and table, so the view you leave is the view you return to.
ℹ️
Server-side filters also exist

The GET /api/admin/subscriptions endpoint itself accepts rich query parameters — status (with PAUSED folding in VACATION and a special EXPIRED bucket), autopay (on/off), plan, product, zone, a from/to start-date range, a text query q (id, name, email, phone), plus sort, dir, page and pageSize — and returns filter facets (plans, products, zones) for building a filter UI. The current storefront page loads the first 200 unfiltered and lets the client DataTable do the filtering; the same endpoint powers server-side filtering when needed.

Create a subscription (on a customer's behalf)

Start a standing milk order for a customer from the office.

Purpose Let an Admin set up a recurring plan for a customer — for example when signing up a walk-in or phone order — choosing the customer, plan, delivery address, bottles, start date and renewal mode.
Access Super Admin · Admin only Path Admin → Commerce → Subscriptions → Create · POST /api/admin/subscriptions
ℹ️
Backed by the options endpoint

The create form is fed by GET /api/admin/subscriptions/options, which returns three data shapes depending on the query: with no query it returns active plans (id, name, days, discount) and subscribable products with their subscription variants (label, ml, daily price); with ?q= it searches customers by name / email / phone (max 10); with ?userId= it returns that customer's saved addresses (default first). This drives the customer picker → address picker → plan → bottle flow.

Form — New subscription · Customer → Address → Plan → Bottles → Schedule
CustomerType-ahead search (name / email / phone) → pick a customer. Sets userId.
Delivery addressDropdown of that customer's saved addresses (default first). Sets addressId.
PlanActive plans with length + discount. Sets planId; drives the plan total.
Bottles (items)One or more subscription variants each with a quantity (1–20). Sets items[].
ScheduleOptional start date + delivery slot; AutoPay toggle (on by default).
Figure — the create-subscription form and the fields it submits

Create — field reference

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Customer (userId)Who the plan belongs toA customer id from the searchYesMust exist — 404 "Customer not found." otherwisecus_9f2a…
Plan (planId)Plan length & discountAn active plan idYesMust exist — 404 "Plan not found."; sets the end date = start + plan.days30-day
Address (addressId)Where milk is deliveredOne of the customer's saved addressesYesMust belong to the same customer — else 400 "Address does not belong to the customer."addr_home
Bottles (items[])What is delivered each dropArray of { variantId, qty }YesAt least one; each variant must exist; qty is an integer 1–20 — else 400 "Invalid subscription items."2× 500 ml
Start date (startDate)First delivery dayISO date-timeOpt.Defaults to the earliest date honouring the 8 PM cut-off (tomorrow, or the day after if placed after 8 PM); the real first delivery skips non-deliverable days2026-07-14
Delivery slot (deliverySlot)Morning time windowSlot textOpt.Defaults to 06:00-08:0006:00-08:00
Auto-renew (autoRenew)Renew automatically at term endOn / Off (boolean)Opt.Defaults to on (true)On
Workflow — create a subscription
Search customerPick addressChoose planAdd bottles + qtySet start / slot / AutoPaySubmitACTIVE + timeline event
Figure — from search to a live plan; a "CREATED" event is written to the timeline and a 201 is returned
✅
Best practice — respect the 8 PM cut-off

Any change that lands after 8 PM takes effect a day later than you might expect. When a customer wants milk "from tomorrow", place the order before 8 PM; after the cut-off the earliest first delivery is the day after tomorrow. The system enforces this automatically, so trust the computed start date rather than back-dating.

Subscription detail & lifecycle actions

Open one plan to see its full history and run every management action.

Purpose Give the office the complete picture of one subscription — customer, address, bottles, price, 14-day schedule preview, recent deliveries, wallet, AutoPay mandate, trial cashback and an audit timeline — plus the buttons to pause, resume, skip, edit, cancel, refund, toggle AutoPay and add notes.
Access Super Admin · Admin only Path Admin → Commerce → Subscriptions → (row) · GET / PATCH /api/admin/subscriptions/[id]
Detail — Subscription #ID · status chip · action buttons
Customer & addressName, email, phone, wallet balance; delivery address with zone, executive, pin location and delivery note.
Plan & bottlesPlan name/length/discount, each item (product, variant, ml, daily price), per-delivery price, plan total and amount saved.
Schedule previewNext 14 days, each marked deliver / not with a reason (scheduled · skipped · paused · before start · ended).
DeliveriesLast 12 deliveries (date, status, bottles out/in) + counts: total, delivered, skipped, failed.
MoneyWallet balance + last 8 wallet transactions; AutoPay mandate (status, amount, next renewal, attempts); trial cashback status.
TimelineUp to 50 audit events (created, edited, paused, resumed, skipped, cancelled, refund, AutoPay on/off, notes) with who and when.
Figure — the subscription detail record returned by GET /api/admin/subscriptions/[id]

The 14-day schedule preview — reason codes

ReasonMeaning
scheduledA milk delivery is due that day
skippedThe customer / office skipped that specific date
pausedThe plan is paused, or the day falls inside a dated pause window
before startThe day is before the plan's start date
endedThe day is after the plan's end date

Lifecycle action buttons

All actions post to PATCH /api/admin/subscriptions/[id] with an action. Each one writes a timeline event and an audit-log entry, and every change respects the same delivery rules as the customer's own controls.

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
EditChange plan, address, delivery slot, start date, bottles, auto-renew or notesaction:"update" → only changed fields are applied; a plan change re-computes the end date; item changes replace the item rows in a transaction; logs an "UPDATED" event with a field-by-field diff. Blocked on cancelled/completed plans.Admin+ Super Admin
PauseTemporarily stop deliveriesaction:"pause" with optional until date and reason → status → PAUSED, stamps pausedFrom/pausedUntil; logs "PAUSED". Blocked on closed plans.Admin+ Super Admin
ResumeRestart a paused planaction:"resume" → status → ACTIVE, clears the pause window and recomputes the next deliverable date (skip-aware, honouring the cut-off); logs "RESUMED".Admin+ Super Admin
SkipSkip one upcoming deliveryaction:"skip" with optional date (defaults to the next delivery) → adds the date to skipDates and moves nextDeliveryAt forward; logs "SKIPPED". Blocked on closed plans.Admin+ Super Admin
AutoPay on/offSwitch automatic renewalaction:"autopay" with on → sets autoRenew; if a Razorpay mandate exists it is set ACTIVE (on) or SUSPENDED (off); logs "AUTOPAY_ON" / "AUTOPAY_OFF".Admin+ Super Admin
CancelClose the subscription for goodaction:"cancel" with optional reason and refundPaise → status → CANCELLED, end date = now, auto-renew off; an optional refund credits the customer's wallet (up to ₹10,000) and logs a "REFUND" event. Errors if already cancelled.Admin+ Super Admin
Add noteAttach an internal commentaction:"note" with text (1–500 chars) → appends a date-stamped line to the plan's notes and logs a "NOTE" event.Admin+ Super Admin
Workflow — subscription lifecycle
ACTIVEPause → PAUSEDResume → ACTIVESkip a dayTerm ends → COMPLETED / ExpiredCancel → CANCELLED (+ optional wallet refund)
Figure — the states a subscription moves through and the actions that drive them
⛔
Caution — cancellation and refunds

Cancelling sets the end date to now, turns off auto-renew and cannot be undone from this screen — a cancelled plan cannot be edited or re-paused; you would create a new subscription instead. If you enter a refund amount it is credited to the customer's wallet immediately (capped at ₹10,000) and appears in their wallet ledger. Double-check the amount before you confirm — a wallet credit is real money out of the business.

✅
Best practice — always give a reason

Pause, skip and cancel all accept a reason, and edits record a diff. Because every action lands in the timeline and the audit log with your role and the time, always type a short reason ("customer travelling 10–20 Jul", "wrong bottle size", "duplicate plan"). Six months later that one line is the difference between a clear history and a mystery.

Subscription reports & export

Break the subscriber base down by status, plan and zone for a chosen window.

Purpose Produce the numbers behind the KPIs — counts by status, plan and zone, AutoPay split, trial-cashback totals, monthly recurring revenue and renewals due — plus a flat row-per-subscription export for spreadsheets.
Access Super Admin · Admin only Path GET /api/admin/subscriptions/reports (optional ?from= & ?to=)
Report blockWhat it contains
By statusCount of subscriptions in each status (Active, Paused, Vacation, Cancelled, Completed)
By planPer plan: how many subscriptions and the active monthly recurring revenue they contribute
By zoneCount of subscriptions per delivery zone ("Unzoned" when no zone is set)
AutoPay splitHow many plans are on AutoPay (on) vs manual (off)
Trial cashbackCredited trial-cashback count, the eligible active count (30/90-day plans) and total amount
RevenueActive MRR, active count and the average per-delivery value across active plans
Renewals dueActive plans ending within the next 7 days, with customer and plan total
Rows (export)One line per subscription: short id, customer, phone, plan, status, start/end date, slot, AutoPay yes/no and per-delivery rupees — ready for CSV
💡
Tip — window the report by start date

Pass from and to (ISO dates) to limit the report to subscriptions that started in that window — useful for "how many new plans did the July campaign bring in, and to which zones?" Leave them off for the all-time picture. For a quick export of what is currently on screen, the table's own Export ▾ (CSV / Excel / PDF) is faster.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common questions when managing subscriptions.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner "No permission to view subscriptions (403)"You are signed in as a role other than Admin / Super Admin (e.g. Support, Manager, Accountant)Subscriptions are Admin-only by design. Sign in with an Admin or Super Admin account.
Banner "Backend offline — couldn't load live data"; KPIs show demo numbersThe backend API is unreachable, or the API base URL is wrongCheck the backend is running and reachable at the shown base; the demo numbers are placeholders, not real counts.
A plan shows a red "Expired" badge but is not CancelledThe plan is past its end date and was never cancelled or renewedOpen it and decide: renew (edit the plan / start date) or cancel it. Expired is a prompt, not a final state.
"Cannot edit / pause a closed subscription"The plan is CANCELLED or COMPLETED — closed states are lockedCreate a fresh subscription for the customer instead of reopening the closed one.
"Address does not belong to the customer."The chosen address is saved under a different customerPick an address from that customer's own saved list (the options endpoint returns only theirs).
