A change didn't take effect todayIt was made after the 8 PM cut-offChanges after 8 PM land a day later by design — expected behaviour, not a bug.

Is "Subscriptions" the same as "Subscription Billing"?

No. This module manages the plans — who subscribes, to what, and the pause/skip/cancel lifecycle. Admin → Commerce → Subscription Billing is a separate module that handles the money — the Razorpay AutoPay mandates and recurring charges. The AutoPay toggle here flips renewal on/off and suspends the mandate; the billing module is where charge attempts and failures are worked.

Why does the table say "Order" when these are subscriptions?

The screen reuses the shared Orders table layout to save code, so the header labels read "Order / Customer / Items / Amount / Payment / Status". Every row is a real subscription — the "Order" cell is the short subscription id and the "Payment" cell shows AutoPay or Manual, not a payment record.

What does the "New (wk)" card actually measure?

The label reads "New (wk)", but the live number is new this month — the count of subscriptions created since the 1st of the current month. Read it as a month-to-date "new subscribers" figure.

Can I refund a customer when I cancel?

Yes. The cancel action accepts an optional refund amount (up to ₹10,000). It is credited straight to the customer's DOODLY wallet (not their card), recorded in their wallet ledger and logged as a "REFUND" event on the subscription timeline. Leave it blank to cancel without any refund.

DOODLYAdmin · Commerce · Payments
§Admin · Commerce

Payments

The single, canonical money ledger for DOODLY — every rupee collected through a Razorpay order, a subscription renewal, an Auto-Pay charge, a cash-on-doorstep hand-off or a manually recorded payment lands here, where staff can search it, refund it, reconcile it, send a receipt and export it for accounting.

ℹ️
Two surfaces, one ledger

The Payments item under Admin → Commerce is served by two front-ends that both read the same database tables through the same APIs. (1) The storefront admin page (/admin/payments.html) shows four live KPIs and a read-only ledger table with the shared DataTable toolbar. (2) The full Payments board in the Next.js admin app adds five working tabs — Dashboard, Payments, Record Payment, Reports, Gateways — with refunds, receipts, reconciliation and CSV exports. This manual documents the complete capability set. Money is always stored as integer paise (₹1 = 100 paise) and rendered in ₹; every change appends an immutable PaymentEvent so the history can never be quietly edited.

ℹ️
How records get into the ledger

The ledger is a superset of every payment source. Paid order Payment rows and paid SubscriptionBilling rows are synced in automatically (both a one-time back-fill and forward on every new event), so the ledger is never missing a gateway payment. Staff add MANUAL rows for cash or off-gateway settlements. Each row carries a source of ORDER, SUBSCRIPTION, AUTOPAY, MANUAL or B2B, and a unique code like PAY-000042. Sync is idempotent — re-running it updates the existing row rather than creating a duplicate.

Payments Dashboard

Live money KPIs across the whole ledger — collections today and this month, lifetime revenue, and a count of every payment by status.

Purpose Give staff a one-glance financial pulse: how much came in today, how much this month, how many payments are still pending or failed, and how much revenue arrived through wallet and Auto-Pay.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — anyone with payments:view Path Admin → Commerce → Payments → Dashboard
Payments — Dashboard · KPI grid (reads GET /api/admin/payments/stats)
Collections KPIsTotal payments · Today's collections · Monthly collections · Total revenue
Status countsSuccessful · Pending · Failed · Refunded (each a live count)
Channel KPIsWallet payments · Auto-Pay collections · Outstanding (pending + failed value)
Figure — the eleven KPI tiles of the full Payments Dashboard tab.

The storefront /admin/payments.html page shows a trimmed four-tile version of the same data: Captured today, Failed, Refunding and Disputed. "Refunding" maps to the count of refunded payments; "Disputed" is a fixed 0 placeholder (chargeback disputes are not yet tracked). The KPI numbers are re-filled from the backend the moment the page loads.

Every KPI and where the number comes from

KPIPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Total paymentsSize of the whole ledgerCountRead-onlycount() of all PaymentRecord rows.1,284
Today's collectionsMoney actually received today₹ amountRead-onlySum of netPaise for SUCCESS rows created since midnight.₹1.7L
Monthly collectionsMoney received this calendar month₹ amountRead-onlySum of netPaise for SUCCESS rows since the 1st.₹24.6L
Total revenueLifetime collected₹ amountRead-onlySum of netPaise for all SUCCESS rows.₹1.9Cr
SuccessfulPayments that capturedCountRead-onlyGrouped count where status = SUCCESS.1,201
PendingAwaiting captureCountRead-onlyGrouped count where status = PENDING.14
FailedGateway declinedCountRead-onlyGrouped count where status = FAILED.2
RefundedFully or partly returnedCountRead-onlyCount of REFUNDED + PARTIALLY_REFUNDED.7
Wallet paymentsValue paid from DOODLY wallets₹ amountRead-onlySum of walletUsedPaise across all rows.₹58,400
Auto-Pay collectionsMoney from recurring mandates₹ amountRead-onlySum of netPaise for SUCCESS rows with source AUTOPAY.₹3.1L
OutstandingUncollected exposure₹ amountRead-onlySum of netPaise for PENDING + FAILED rows.₹6,200

Payments Ledger (list)

The searchable, filterable, exportable table of every payment — with row selection for bulk reconcile / receipts / export.

Purpose Find any payment fast (by code, transaction, order, subscription, invoice, customer name or phone), narrow the ledger with filters, and act on one or many rows at once.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — payments:view to read, payments:edit for bulk actions Path Admin → Commerce → Payments → Payments
Payments — Ledger · search · filters · sort · bulk bar · paged table
Filter rowSearch box · Status · Method · Gateway · Source · "Wallet used" checkbox · ₹ min / ₹ max · From / To dates · Sort
Bulk action barAppears when rows are ticked: "N selected" · Reconcile · Send receipts · Export CSV · clear
Ledger tableSelect ✓ · Payment · Customer · Source · Method / gateway · Amount · Wallet · Net · Status · Invoice · View
FooterTotal count · "page collected ₹X" · Prev / Page n of m / Next paging
Figure — the Payments ledger with its filter row, bulk bar and paged table.

Table columns

ColumnShowsBackend field
(checkbox)Row select for bulk actions; header ticks/unticks the whole page—
PaymentThe PAY-000000 code, then the transaction / order reference, then the datecode, transactionId/orderId, createdAt
CustomerCustomer name and phonecustomer.name, customer.phone
Sourceorder / subscription / autopay / manual / b2b, with a "✓" when reconciledsource, reconciled
Method / gatewayUPI / Card / Net Banking / Wallet / Cash over the gateway namemethod, gateway
AmountGross amount chargedamountPaise
WalletHow much of it was paid from the wallet ("—" if none)walletUsedPaise
NetNet received through the gateway/cash, with any refunded amount in red belownetPaise, refundedPaise
StatusColoured pill: Captured / Pending / Failed / Refunded / Part refundstatus
InvoiceLinked invoice number, if anyinvoiceNumber
ViewOpens / closes the full detail & actions panel for that row—

The storefront table (/admin/payments.html) is the same data through the shared DataTable engine with a slimmer column set — Payment ID · Customer · Method · Amount · Status · Date — and a status filter chip row of All · Captured · Failed · Refunding. That engine adds free-text search, column sort, a date-range picker, saved views, favourites, CSV export and pagination on top. A green banner ("● Live — N payment(s) from the DOODLY database") confirms live data; a 403 shows "Your role doesn't have permission to view payments".

Filters, search & sort (full Payments tab)

ControlPurposeAccepted valuesBackend param
SearchMatch across many keys at onceFree textq — matches code, transactionId, orderId, subscriptionId, invoiceNumber, customer name, phone or userId
StatusFilter by outcomeSuccess / Pending / Failed / Refunded / Partially refundedstatus
MethodFilter by payment methodUPI / CARD / NETBANKING / WALLET / CASH (options come from live facets)method
GatewayFilter by processorrazorpay / wallet / cash / manual (live facets)gateway
SourceFilter by where the payment originatedORDER / SUBSCRIPTION / AUTOPAY / MANUAL / B2Bsource
Wallet usedOnly rows where some wallet balance was appliedCheckboxwalletUsed=1
₹ min / ₹ maxAmount band (on gross)Rupees (converted to paise)amountMin, amountMax
From / ToDate range on when the payment was createdDatesfrom, to
SortOrder the listLatest / Oldest / Highest ₹ / Lowest ₹ / Customer A–Zsort + dir

Table behaviour

AspectBehaviour
SearchOne box, server-side, case-insensitive across code / txn / order / subscription / invoice / customer / phone.
FiltersStatus, method, gateway, source, wallet-used, amount band and date range — all combine (AND).
SortingLatest (default), Oldest, Highest ₹, Lowest ₹, Customer A–Z.
Pagination15 rows per page in the full board (200 pulled at a time on the storefront page); footer shows total count, value collected on the page, and Prev / Next.
Row actions"View" expands the in-line detail & actions panel (Screen 3).
Bulk actionsTick rows → Reconcile · Send receipts · Export CSV (max 500 rows per call).
ExportsCSV of the selected rows (or the whole page if none selected): Payment ID, Transaction ID, Order ID, Subscription ID, Customer, Date, Method, Gateway, Amount, Wallet, GST, Discount, Net, Status, Invoice, Collected By.

Bulk actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
ReconcileMarks the selected payments as reconciled (matched to a bank/settlement)POST /api/admin/payments/bulk action reconcile → sets reconciled=true, appends a RECONCILE event per rowSuper AdminAdminAccountant
Send receiptsE-mails a payment receipt to each selected customerbulk action receipts → queues an EMAIL notification + RECEIPT event per rowSuper AdminAdminAccountant
Export CSVDownloads the selected rows as a spreadsheetBuilds the CSV in the browser; also logs a bulk export NOTE event on the selected rowsSuper AdminAdminAccountant

Payment Detail & Actions

The full picture of a single payment — breakdown, gateway response, wallet activity, refund history, retry attempts, timeline — plus the refund / retry / receipt / reconcile / note controls.

Purpose Investigate one payment end-to-end and take the right action on it: refund a customer, retry a failed charge, resend a receipt, mark it reconciled or add an internal note.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — view with payments:view; refund needs the payments:refund special; retry / reconcile / note / receipt need payments:edit Path Admin → Commerce → Payments → Payments → View
Payment PAY-000042 · three-column detail panel
Customer & linksName, e-mail, phone; linked order / subscription / billing cycle; source · method / gateway · collected-by
Payment breakdownGross · Discount · GST · Wallet used · Net received · Refunded · Invoice; "Print / PDF" receipt button
Gateway responseGateway order id · gateway payment id · raw JSON payload (if stored)
Wallet activityLast few wallet transactions for this customer
Refund historyEach refund: reason, "· wallet" flag, amount, date
AttemptsRetry attempts: #, status, timestamp
ActionsRefund · Retry · Send receipt · Reconcile / Unreconcile
Timeline & NoteAppend-only event log; free-text note box (adds a dated line)
Figure — the expanded per-payment detail and actions panel.

Action buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
RefundOpens a small form (₹ amount pre-filled to the refundable balance, "to wallet" checkbox, reason). Only shown when the payment is Captured or Part-refunded and there is still a refundable balance.PATCH /api/admin/payments/{id} action refund → creates a PaymentRefund (ref RFND-XXXXXX), lowers the refundable balance, sets status to Refunded (full) or Part refund; if "to wallet" it credits the customer's wallet; notifies the customer; a fully-refunded order/billing is flipped to Refunded too.Super AdminAdminAccountant (refund special)
RetryRecords a fresh charge attempt. Only shown for Pending or Failed payments.action retry → adds a PaymentLedgerAttempt and an ATTEMPT event. For a Razorpay row it notes that a live re-charge needs gateway keys — settle it with Record Payment if the retry can't clear.Super AdminAdminAccountant (edit)
Send receiptE-mails the customer a receipt for this payment.action receipt → queues an EMAIL notification + RECEIPT event.Super AdminAdminAccountant (edit)
Reconcile / UnreconcileToggles the "matched to bank settlement" flag.action reconcile → flips reconciled, appends a RECONCILE event.Super AdminAdminAccountant (edit)
Add noteAppends an internal, date-stamped note.action note → appends to notes and logs a NOTE event.Super AdminAdminAccountant (edit)
Print / PDFOpens a clean, branded receipt in a new window ready to print or save as PDF.Rendered in the browser from the loaded detail — no server call.Any viewer

Refund form fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Amount ₹How much to refund1 … refundable balanceYesMust be > 0 and ≤ refundable (net − already refunded); capped at ₹1,00,000. A larger amount is rejected with the exact refundable shown.₹300
To walletCredit the DOODLY wallet instead of the original methodCheckbox (on by default)Opt.When ticked, the amount is credited to the customer's wallet as a refund credit and a WALLET event is logged.On
ReasonWhy the refund was issuedFree text, up to 300 charsOpt.Stored on the refund and shown in the refund history and the customer notification.Spilt bottle on delivery
⚠️
Refunds are one-directional

A refund can only be raised against a Captured or Part-refunded payment, and never for more than the remaining refundable balance. A Pending or Failed payment cannot be refunded — there is nothing to return yet. Each refund gets its own RFND- reference and a customer notification fires immediately, so double-check the amount and the "to wallet" toggle before you press Process.

Record a Payment (manual)

A three-step form to log a cash or off-gateway payment against a customer and, optionally, mark the linked order or billing cycle as paid.

Purpose Capture money that did not flow through the online gateway — cash on the doorstep, a bank transfer, a UPI paid to a staff phone — so the ledger and the customer's order/billing stay accurate.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — needs payments:create Path Admin → Commerce → Payments → Record Payment
Record Payment — 3-step form
1 · CustomerSearch by name / mobile / e-mail, pick a result; "change" to reset
2 · Link (optional)Order · Billing cycle · Subscription dropdowns (that customer's payable items) + "Mark the linked order / billing as paid" checkbox
3 · Amount & methodMethod · Amount ₹ · Wallet used ₹ · GST ₹ · Discount ₹ · Reference no. · Invoice no. · Notes
Footer"Net to collect: ₹X" live preview · Record payment button
Figure — the guided Record Payment form (customer → link → amount).

Form fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
CustomerWho paidSearch & selectYesSearch hits /payments/options?q= (name / e-mail / phone, customers only). The customer must exist or the save is rejected "Customer not found."Ananya Reddy
Order (link)Tie the payment to a pending orderThat customer's Pending / Failed ordersOpt.Loaded from /payments/options?userId=.subscription · ₹1,799
Billing (link)Tie it to a subscription billing cyclePending / Failed / Partial cyclesOpt.A cycle that already has a Captured payment is rejected "This billing cycle is already paid."SB-000110 c3 · ₹1,799
Subscription (link)Tie it to a subscriptionThat customer's subscriptionsOpt.Reference only.Daily 500ml · active
Mark as paidFlip the linked order/billing to PAIDCheckbox (on by default)Opt.When set, the linked order is set to PAID and the billing cycle to PAID with the amount recorded.On
MethodHow the money was paidCASH / UPI / CARD / NETBANKING / WALLETYesEnum. Gateway defaults to "cash" for CASH else "manual".CASH
Amount ₹Gross amount received1 … 1,00,000YesPositive integer paise; must be > 0.₹1,799
Wallet used ₹Part of it settled from the wallet0 or moreOpt.Net collected = Amount − Wallet used (never below 0).₹0
GST ₹Tax portion, for the receipt/report0 or moreOpt.Stored on the row and rolled into reports.₹0
Discount ₹Discount applied0 or moreOpt.Stored and rolled into reports.₹0
Reference no.Bank/UPI reference for auditText, up to 80 charsOpt.Saved as the unique transactionId; a duplicate reference is rejected "A payment with that reference already exists."UPI-8837XXQ
Invoice no.Attach an invoice numberText, up to 60 charsOpt.Stored on the row.INV-2026-0442
NotesAny free-text remarkText, up to 500 charsOpt.Stored on the row and visible in the detail panel.Collected at doorstep

A manual payment is always saved with source MANUAL, status Captured (SUCCESS), a fresh PAY- code and a SUCCESS event stamped with the recording staff member. The footer shows the live Net to collect as you type.

Payment Reports

Date-ranged revenue analytics — gross / net / GST / refunds / wallet / Auto-Pay, plus breakdowns by day, month, method, gateway and status, with a one-click CSV.

Purpose Give the accounts team the numbers they need to close a period: what was collected, how it broke down by channel, what was refunded, and a row-level export for reconciliation.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — payments:view Path Admin → Commerce → Payments → Reports
Payments — Reports · date preset · Refresh · Export CSV
Range pickerToday · Last 7 days · Last 30 days · This month · This year · Custom (From / To)
Summary KPIsGross revenue · Net collected · GST collected · Refunds (count + to-wallet) · Wallet used · Cashback / referral · Auto-Pay collected · Discounts
Breakdown tablesDaily collections · Monthly collections · By method · By gateway · By status
ExportCSV of every payment row in range
Figure — the Reports tab: range picker, KPI grid and breakdown tables.

Report contents

BlockWhat it showsBackend
Gross revenueSum of gross amount on Captured rows in rangerevenue.grossPaise
Net collectedNet (gross − wallet) on Captured rowsrevenue.netPaise
GST collectedTax portion on Captured rowsrevenue.gstPaise
RefundsTotal refunded amount, count, and how much went to walletrefunds.amountPaise / count / toWalletPaise
Wallet usedWallet balance applied to paymentswallet.usedPaise
Cashback / referralWallet credits granted as cashback and referral rewardswallet.cashbackPaise / referralPaise

DiscountsDiscount value on Captured rowsrevenue.discountPaise

By method / gateway / statusCount and collected split by channel and outcomebyMethod[] / byGateway[] / byStatus[]

Gateways

Turn payment gateways on or off, switch Razorpay between Test and Live, and see at a glance whether the API keys and webhook secret are configured.

Purpose Control which processors are accepting money and confirm the environment is wired correctly before real charges flow.
Access Super Admin Admin — view with payments:view, change with payments:edit (mode / keys are a Super-Admin-grade change) Path Admin → Commerce → Payments → Gateways
Payments — Gateways · one card per processor
Gateway cardLabel · Enabled/Disabled toggle · Mode (TEST/LIVE) with "switch" · Key ID · Refunds supported · Webhook configured
Razorpay readiness● API keys present/MISSING · ● Webhook secret present/MISSING · Webhook URL & the events to subscribe
Figure — a gateway card with its toggle and the Razorpay readiness strip.

Gateway card fields & actions

Field / actionWhat it doesBackendPermission
Enabled / DisabledToggles whether the gateway accepts paymentsPATCH /api/admin/payments/gateways → sets enabledSuper AdminAdmin
Mode (switch)Flips Razorpay between TEST and LIVESets mode TEST/LIVESuper AdminAdmin
Key IDThe public key id (safe to store/display)keyId — secret keys are never sent to the browserRead-only display
RefundsWhether the gateway supports refundssupportsRefundRead-only
Webhook configuredWhether the webhook has been marked set upwebhookConfiguredSuper AdminAdmin
API keys readinessLive check that RAZORPAY_KEY_ID and RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET exist in the server environmentliveKeysPresentRead-only
Webhook secret readinessLive check that RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET existswebhookSecretPresentRead-only
⚠️
Secrets live in the server, not this screen

You can toggle a gateway, switch Test/Live and confirm readiness here, but the actual Razorpay secret key and webhook secret are only ever set as server environment variables (RAZORPAY_KEY_ID, RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET, RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET) and never travel to the browser. Point Razorpay's webhook at /api/payments/webhook and subscribe to payment.captured, payment.failed, and subscription.charged / halted / cancelled.

Workflow — Record a manual (cash) payment
Open Record PaymentSearch & pick customerLink order / billing (optional)Choose method, enter amountTick "mark as paid"Record paymentRow appears Captured in the ledger
Figure — logging money that did not flow through the online gateway.
Workflow — Refund a customer
Find the paymentView detailRefundEnter ₹ ≤ refundable, choose "to wallet", add reasonProcessRefund logged · status Refunded/Part refund · customer notified · wallet credited (if chosen)
Figure — the refund path and everything it triggers.

Who can do what

CapabilityPermissionSuper AdminAdminAccountantOther roles
View ledger, dashboard, reports, gatewayspayments:viewYesYesYesNo
Record a manual paymentpayments:createYesYesYesNo
Retry / reconcile / note / receipt / bulk / gateway configpayments:editYesYesYesNo
Issue a refundpayments:refund (a "full"-level special)YesYesYesNo
ℹ️
The Payments module is finance-only

Only Super Admin, Admin and Accountant have the payments module in the permission matrix, and all three hold it at "full" — so all three can view, record, edit and refund. Support, operations, procurement, quality, inventory, marketing and delivery roles have no payments access at all and never see this screen. Server-side requirePermission is the real security boundary; the client menu filter is convenience only.

✅
Best Practice — reconcile against the bank, then refund from the ledger

Run Reports for the period, export the CSV and match it to the Razorpay settlement report using the Payment ID and Transaction ID columns. Tick the matched rows and hit Reconcile so the ledger records what's been banked. Only refund from the payment's own detail panel (never by editing the customer's wallet directly) — that way the refund is capped at the refundable balance, gets its own RFND- reference, notifies the customer and, when you choose "to wallet", credits them in one auditable step.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"Your role doesn't have permission to view payments (403)"Signed in as a role without the payments module (e.g. Operations, Support)Use a Super Admin, Admin or Accountant account — those are the only roles with payments access.
Ledger shows "Backend offline" and no rowsThe Next.js backend isn't running / unreachableStart the backend (next-app) so the storefront page can reach the live APIs.
"A payment with that reference already exists"The Reference no. you typed is already used by another paymentReferences are unique — leave it blank or enter the true, distinct bank/UPI reference.
"This billing cycle is already paid"You linked a billing cycle that already has a Captured paymentPick a different (unpaid) cycle, or leave the billing link empty.
Refund button doesn't appear on a paymentThe payment is Pending / Failed, or already fully refunded (nothing left to refund)Only Captured or Part-refunded payments with a positive refundable balance can be refunded.
"Refund exceeds the refundable balance"Amount entered is larger than net minus what's already been refundedEnter an amount up to the refundable figure shown next to the box.
Gateways tab warns "API keys MISSING"RAZORPAY_KEY_ID / RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET not set in the environmentAdd the keys as server environment variables and redeploy; the readiness dot turns green.
A retry on a Razorpay payment doesn't actually chargeLive gateway re-charges need valid keys; retry only records the attemptOnce the customer pays, settle it with Record Payment, or wait for the webhook to capture it.

Frequently asked questions

Q. A customer paid by UPI to our staff phone. How do I record it?

Open Record Payment, search and pick the customer, optionally link the order or billing cycle, set method UPI, enter the amount and the UPI reference, tick Mark as paid, and press Record payment. It appears in the ledger as a Captured MANUAL payment.

Q. What's the difference between "Amount" and "Net"?

Amount is the gross charged. Net is what actually came in through the gateway or cash, i.e. gross minus any wallet balance the customer applied. Reports total both so you can see wallet usage separately.

Q. If I refund "to wallet", does the customer get real money back?

No — "to wallet" credits the refund to the customer's DOODLY wallet for their next purchase. Leave it unticked to signal the money is going back to the original payment method; either way the refund is logged with its own reference and the customer is notified.

Q. Why do some payments show a "✓" on the Source column?

That means the payment has been reconciled — matched to a bank settlement — either individually from its detail panel or via the bulk Reconcile action.

Q. Can I edit or delete a payment after it's recorded?

No. The ledger is append-only for integrity — you can refund, add notes, reconcile, resend receipts and log retry attempts, and every one of those actions is written to the payment's timeline. There is no "delete", by design.

DOODLYAdmin · Commerce · Subscription Billing (AutoPay)
§Admin · Commerce

Subscription Billing (AutoPay)

One control room for every recurring-payment mandate — see who has hands-free renewals, which renewals are coming up, which have failed, and step in to retry, pause, resume or cancel any customer's AutoPay.

ℹ️
What "AutoPay" means at DOODLY

AutoPay is a Razorpay recurring e-mandate attached to a customer's milk subscription. Once the customer authorises it (their bank / UPI app approves a standing instruction), DOODLY charges the renewal amount automatically before the plan expires — so the morning delivery never stops. AutoPay is subscription-only and strictly opt-in: a mandate is only ever created when a customer ticks "Enable AutoPay" at checkout. This admin screen does not create mandates — it monitors and manages the ones that already exist.

Subscription Billing — AutoPay dashboard

Live KPIs plus a searchable, filterable table of every AutoPay mandate.

Purpose Monitor the health of recurring billing at a glance (active mandates, upcoming renewals, failures) and manage any individual mandate — retry a failed charge, suspend, resume or cancel — without leaving the page.
Access Super Admin · Admin · Accountant (view) — actions need the same roles (billing "edit") Path Admin → Commerce → Subscription Billing (/admin/billing.html)
Header — Subscription Billing · live-status banner (● Live — N mandates from the DOODLY database)
KPI stripSix tiles: Active mandates · Upcoming (7d) · Failed renewals (30d) · Paused · Cancelled · Total.
Filters barSearch box (customer / phone / email) · Status dropdown · Export CSV button.
AutoPay mandates tableCustomer · Plan · Amount · Next renewal · Attempts · Status badge · row actions.
Row actionsPer mandate, only the buttons valid for its status: Retry · Resume · Suspend · Cancel.
Figure — labelled layout of the Subscription Billing (AutoPay) screen
ℹ️
Live vs demo

When the backend is reachable the screen loads real mandates from GET /api/admin/autopay and shows a green banner: "● Live — N AutoPay mandate(s) from the DOODLY database" with the API base. If the backend is offline (and demo mode is allowed) it shows a red "⚠ Backend offline — showing demo AutoPay data" banner over a sample table. In production with no demo mode the list is driven purely by real mandates and simply shows an empty state when there are none. This manual documents the live behaviour.

The KPI strip — six live tiles

Each tile is computed by the backend (adminAutopayStats()) directly from the mandate and renewal tables — never hand-entered.

TileWhat it countsHow it is calculated (backend)Example
Active mandatesMandates currently charging automaticallyCount of AutoPay rows with status ACTIVE128
Upcoming (7d)Active renewals due in the next seven daysACTIVE mandates whose nextRenewalAt ≤ now + 7 days19
Failed renewals (30d)Renewal charges that failed in the last monthCount of RenewalHistory rows with status FAILED and chargedAt in the last 30 days4
PausedMandates temporarily stopped by the customer or an adminCount of AutoPay rows with status INACTIVE6
CancelledMandates that have been terminatedCount of AutoPay rows with status CANCELLED11
TotalEvery mandate on record, any statusSum of all statuses145
ℹ️
"Failed" vs "Suspended"

Failed renewals (30d) counts individual failed charges in the payment history — a mandate can fail once, be retried by Razorpay, and still recover. A mandate only moves to Suspended after Razorpay exhausts its automatic retries. Suspended and Retry-pending mandates are the ones that need a human — filter the table by Suspended or Retry to find them.

Filters & search controls

ControlPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Search boxFind a mandate by the customerFree text — matches customer name, phone or emailOpt.Sent as ?q= after a 300 ms pause; backend matches user.name/email (case-insensitive) or phone containsAnanya
Status dropdownNarrow the list to one mandate stateAll statuses · Active · Paused · Retry · Suspended · CancelledOpt.Sent as ?status= (ACTIVE / INACTIVE / RETRY / SUSPENDED / CANCELLED); "Paused" maps to INACTIVE. Reloads the table on changeSuspended
Export CSVDownload the currently shown rowsButtonOpt.Client-side export of the rows on screen (respects the active filter/search)—

The AutoPay mandates table

AspectBehaviour (from the real table config)
ColumnsCustomer (name + phone/email subline) · Plan · Amount (₹, from amountPaise) · Next renewal (short date) · Attempts (retry count) · Status (colour badge) · Actions.
SortingFixed server order: soonest renewal first (nextRenewalAt ascending), so the mandates about to charge sit at the top.
SearchThe header search box (customer / phone / email), debounced 300 ms, re-queries the backend.
FiltersStatus dropdown (see above). Search and status combine.
PaginationNo page controls — the backend returns up to 300 mandates in one load, ordered by next renewal. Use search/status to narrow larger sets.
ExportsExport CSV → file autopay-mandates-YYYY-MM-DD.csv with columns Customer, Phone, Email, Plan, Amount (₹), Next renewal, Attempts, Status, Mandate (gateway id).
Row actionsStatus-aware buttons in the last column — Retry, Resume, Suspend, Cancel (see the button table below).
Empty state"No AutoPay mandates match." when the filter/search returns nothing.

Status badges

The Status column colour-codes each mandate's backend state so problem mandates jump out.

Backend statusBadge shownColourMeaning
ACTIVEActiveGreenCharging automatically each cycle.
INACTIVEPausedGreyTemporarily stopped (customer or admin); can be resumed.
RETRYRetry pendingAmberA charge failed; an automatic retry is queued.
SUSPENDEDSuspendedRedRazorpay's retries were exhausted — needs attention.
CANCELLEDCancelledGreyMandate terminated; no further charges.

Row actions — what each button does

Only the buttons that make sense for a mandate's current status are shown. Every action posts to POST /api/admin/autopay with the mandate's gateway id, calls the corresponding Razorpay control, updates the mandate, writes an audit record, then reloads the table. While an action is in flight the row's buttons are disabled, and on success a toast confirms ("AutoPay retried ✓", etc.).

ButtonShown when status is…What it doesBackend processPermission
RetrySuspended or RetryRe-attempts the failed renewal charge now and reactivates the mandate.{action:"retry"} → resumes the Razorpay subscription, sets status ACTIVE and resets attempts to 0Super Admin+ Admin Accountant
ResumePaused (Inactive)Restarts automatic charging on a paused mandate.{action:"resume"} → Razorpay resume, status ACTIVE, attempts reset to 0Super Admin+ Admin Accountant
SuspendActiveTemporarily stops automatic charging (the plan itself stays alive).{action:"suspend"} → Razorpay pause, status INACTIVESuper Admin+ Admin Accountant
Cancel (red)Any status except CancelledAsks "Cancel this customer's AutoPay mandate?", then terminates it — the subscription stops renewing automatically.{action:"cancel"} → Razorpay cancel, status CANCELLED, and the subscription's autoRenew is turned offSuper Admin+ Admin Accountant
ℹ️
Why "Retry" and "Resume" behave the same

Under the hood both Retry and Resume tell Razorpay to resume the subscription and flip the mandate back to Active with the attempt counter cleared. "Retry" is simply the label used when a mandate is in a failed state (Suspended / Retry), and "Resume" when it was deliberately paused (Inactive) — the customer or gateway then re-attempts the outstanding cycle. "Suspend" is the mirror of "Resume": it pauses an Active mandate.

The action request (POST /api/admin/autopay)

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
actionWhat to do to the mandateretry · resume · suspend · cancelYesEnum-validated (Zod); anything else is rejected with a 400cancel
gatewaySubIdWhich mandate to act onRazorpay subscription idYesMust exist, or the API returns 404 "Mandate not found."; the request needs the billing "edit" permissionsub_Nm2c…
⚠️
Warning — permission is enforced on the server

The buttons you see are filtered by your role in the browser, but the real security check is on the backend: GET /api/admin/autopay requires billing → view and POST requires billing → edit. A role without billing access gets a 403 and the page shows "Your role can't view subscription billing." Do not rely on hidden buttons alone — the server is the boundary.

⚠️
Warning — you cannot change a customer's card/UPI from here

Razorpay mandates are bound to the exact card or UPI the customer authorised; they cannot be swapped silently. To move a customer to a new payment method, they must cancel AutoPay and re-enable it at their next checkout, where their bank re-authorises a fresh e-mandate. There is no "change method" action on the admin table.

Mandate lifecycle

How an AutoPay mandate is born, charges, fails and recovers.

Purpose Understand the states behind the badges so you know what a mandate will do next — and when a human needs to step in.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant (reference) Path Admin → Commerce → Subscription Billing
Workflow — AutoPay mandate lifecycle
Customer opts in at CheckoutMandate created — INACTIVE (pending bank auth)Bank / UPI authorises → webhook → ACTIVERenewal charged each cycle (RenewalHistory)Charge fails → RETRY (auto re-attempt)Retries exhausted → SUSPENDED (notify + never silent-cancel)Admin/customer Retry or Resume → ACTIVE
Figure — the states an AutoPay mandate moves through
StageWhat happensWhere it is driven
Opt-inCustomer ticks "Enable AutoPay (Recommended)" at checkout — OFF by default, never pre-checked.Checkout toggle → the mandate is actually created by /api/checkout at pay time
CreatedA Razorpay recurring subscription + an AutopaySubscription row are created, status INACTIVE, awaiting the bank's authorisation.enableAutopay()
ActivatedThe bank/UPI authorises the standing instruction; a Razorpay webhook flips the mandate to ACTIVE and the customer is notified ("AutoPay is on").activateMandate() via /api/payments/webhook
ChargedEach cycle the renewal amount is charged and written to the payment history (SUCCESS / FAILED).recordRenewal()
ReminderTwo days before a renewal, a daily cron reminds the customer (opt-ins respected) — at most one reminder per renewal.autopayRenewalReminders() via /api/cron/notifications
HaltedIf Razorpay exhausts its retries the mandate becomes SUSPENDED — the customer is notified and told deliveries are paused, not cancelled. DOODLY never silently cancels.onMandateHalted()
Recovered / endedA Retry/Resume reactivates the mandate; a Cancel terminates it and turns the subscription's auto-renew off.This admin screen, or the customer's own AutoPay panel
⛔
Caution — Cancel is a hard stop

Cancel tells Razorpay to cancel the recurring subscription and sets the mandate to CANCELLED and the subscription's autoRenew to off. There is no "un-cancel" — the customer must enable AutoPay again at a future checkout, which requires a brand-new bank authorisation. If the goal is only to stop charging for a while, use Suspend (reversible with Resume) instead of Cancel.

ℹ️
Every action is audited

Each admin action writes an audit record — admin.autopay.retry / admin.autopay.resume / admin.autopay.suspend / admin.autopay.cancel — tagged with your role and the mandate id, on top of the service-level audit (autopay.resume, autopay.pause, autopay.cancel). A Super Admin can always trace who touched a mandate and when in the Admin → System → Audit Logs trail.

✅
Best Practice

Work the failures first. Each morning, filter the table by Suspended and then Retry, and clear that queue: call or message the customer to check their card/UPI, then hit Retry once they confirm. Keep an eye on Failed renewals (30d) as a trend — a rising number usually means an expired-card wave, not a one-off. Reserve Cancel for genuine churn; for a customer who simply wants a break, use Suspend so their plan and mandate survive.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"Your role can't view subscription billing" / a 403 bannerSigned-in role lacks the billing permissionUse a Super Admin, Admin or Accountant account, or have a Super Admin grant billing access in Admin → System → Roles & Permissions.
Red "Backend offline — showing demo AutoPay data" bannerThe Next.js API isn't reachable from the storefrontConfirm the backend is running and the API base is correct; the numbers shown are sample data until it reconnects.
A row action button does nothing / "Backend offline — action not applied"No live API connection when the button was clickedReconnect the backend, reload the page, and retry the action.
Action fails with "Mandate not found."The mandate's gateway id no longer exists (already cancelled/removed)Reload the table to refresh the list; the row is likely stale.
Customer says their card was declined but the mandate still shows ActiveRazorpay is still inside its automatic retry windowCheck the status — it moves to Retry, then Suspended only after retries are exhausted; you can Retry manually once the customer fixes their payment method.
Can't find a specific customer's mandateThey may have no AutoPay, or the search term doesn't match their stored detailsSearch by phone or email; remember AutoPay only exists if the customer opted in at checkout.

Frequently asked questions

Q. Can I set up AutoPay for a customer from this screen?

A. No. A mandate can only be created when the customer opts in at checkout, because their bank must authorise the recurring e-mandate. This screen manages existing mandates — it has no "create" or "enable" action.

Q. What is the difference between Suspend and Cancel?

A. Suspend pauses automatic charging but keeps the mandate — you can Resume it later with no re-authorisation. Cancel terminates the mandate permanently and turns the subscription's auto-renew off; re-enabling requires a fresh authorisation at a future checkout.

Q. Does cancelling AutoPay cancel the customer's subscription?

A. No. Cancelling the mandate only stops automatic renewals — it sets the subscription's autoRenew to off. The subscription itself continues for its remaining term; the customer can renew manually or re-enable AutoPay at checkout.

Q. Why does a mandate show "Suspended" instead of just being cancelled after a failed payment?

A. By design DOODLY never silently cancels. When Razorpay's retries are exhausted the mandate is set to Suspended and the customer is notified that deliveries are paused (not cancelled) so they can fix their payment method and resume — protecting both the customer's service and DOODLY's recurring revenue.

Q. What does the Export CSV include?

A. The rows currently on screen (respecting your active search and status filter), with Customer, Phone, Email, Plan, Amount in rupees, Next renewal date, Attempts, Status and the gateway Mandate id — saved as autopay-mandates-YYYY-MM-DD.csv.

DOODLYAdmin · Commerce · Business Invoices
§Admin · Commerce

Business Invoices

A premium, print-ready statement of account for every B2B partner — supply analytics, financial summary, an outstanding-and-credit tracker and payment history — backed by an automated engine that issues numbered GST invoices per B2B order and emails a branded PDF to the buyer.

ℹ️
Two things live under one name

"Business Invoices" is made of two real pieces of code that work together:

1 — The screen you open at Admin → Commerce → Business Invoices (/admin/invoice-b2b.html). The page's invoiceB2B block (registered in blocks.js) mounts a Business Partner Statement built by invoice.js → DOODLY_INVOICE.mountB2B. On load, layout.js → wireB2BInvoiceBackend() pulls the real businesses and orders from the DOODLY database and re-renders the statement from them.

2 — The invoice engine in the Next.js backend (next-app/lib/b2b/invoices.ts, invoice-email.ts, invoice-pdf.ts behind /api/b2b/invoices). This issues the actual numbered BusinessInvoice records, auto-emails the branded PDF, tracks delivery, and records an append-only audit trail. Invoices are generated from the B2B Orders module ("Generate invoice"); this section documents both the statement you read and the engine that powers it.

Business Partner Statement

The full statement of account for one B2B partner — the screen this page opens on.

Purpose Give the office one printable, shareable document per business that summarises what was supplied, what was billed, what is outstanding, how much credit is left, and the recent payment history — so a partner's account can be reviewed or sent in seconds.
Access Super Admin Admin — the underlying B2B data endpoints are Admin/Super-Admin only (canUseB2B in lib/b2b/guard.ts). No other staff role can load it. Path Admin → Commerce → Business Invoices

The page header reads "Business Partner Statement — Premium B2B statement: supply analytics, financial summary, outstanding tracker, payment history and credit insights. Print, PDF, Excel, email or WhatsApp." Below it, a single business is shown as a full statement document. A Business picker at the top switches between partners; choosing another business rewrites the page's ?id= and re-draws the whole statement for that partner. Money is animated with a count-up on open (animations are switched off for print and under "reduce motion").

Business Partner Statement — action bar · Print · Download PDF · Excel · Email · WhatsApp · ⚙ (Super Admin)
Business pickerDropdown of every registered business (Name + code); switching regenerates the statement
Statement headerBrand + "STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT" · Statement no · Date · Billing period · Due date · Status badge
Alerts stripConditional banners: overdue, payment-due-soon, credit-limit-near, pending invoices
Business + BillingPartner identity (GSTIN, contact, address) and billing terms (credit period, due, terms, sales rep)
Supply summaryChips: total litres · total bottles · products · delivery days · avg daily (L)
Financial summaryEight stat tiles: previous outstanding, current invoice, payments received, credit notes, discounts, GST, total outstanding, net payable
Outstanding trackerOutstanding · due date · days overdue · credit limit · available credit · settled-% bar · amount due today
Payment historyTable: Date · Invoice · Amount · Paid · Pending · Status
Business analyticsAvg daily / monthly / quarterly / lifetime purchase · highest & lowest month · avg payment time · most ordered
FooterThank-you message · accounts phone · website · terms · authorised signatory
Figure — labelled layout of the Business Partner Statement
ℹ️
Live wiring & the "● Live" banner

When the backend is reachable, wireB2BInvoiceBackend() fetches /api/b2b/businesses and /api/b2b/orders, mirrors them into the store the statement reads, re-mounts the statement, and shows a green banner: "● Live — B2B statement from the DOODLY database (N business(es), M order(s))." If the backend is offline the banner reads "⚠ Backend offline…"; if your role is blocked it reads "⚠ Your role can't access B2B (403)." The statement's identity, the selected business's profile (order count, revenue, average daily, outstanding, preferred products) and the payment-history rows are drawn from that business's real registered profile and recorded B2B orders.

Header & meta fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Statement noThe statement's reference number.Format DOODLY/B2B/{year}/{#####}.YesSame number scheme the engine mints for real invoices via formatInvoiceNumber().DOODLY/B2B/2026/00042
DateThe date the statement was produced.Day-Month-Year.YesDisplay field on the statement head.30 Jun 2026
Billing periodThe window the statement covers.Date range.YesDisplay field.1–30 Jun 2026
Due dateWhen payment is expected.A date.YesAlso shown in the Billing block and the outstanding tracker.10 Jul 2026
StatusThe account's standing.Paid · Pending · Overdue (badge).YesDerived from outstanding balance and days overdue.Pending
Sales repThe DOODLY person who owns this relationship.A name.Opt.Shown in the Billing block.Vikram Rao

Business & Billing panels

The Business panel is the partner's identity, drawn straight from the registered business record: name, type · code, GSTIN, contact person · mobile, email and address. The Billing panel lists Credit period, Payment due, Terms (the business's payment term — Cash / Credit / Weekly / Monthly billing) and Sales rep.

Alerts strip (shown only when relevant)

AlertWhen it appearsColour
Overdue paymentThe account is past its due date — shows days past due and the outstanding amount.Red
Payment due soonThere is an outstanding balance that is not yet overdue — shows the amount due and due date.Amber
Credit limit nearly reachedAvailable credit has fallen to 15% or less of the limit — shows how little is left.Red
Pending invoicesAt least one row in payment history is still unpaid.Amber

Supply summary (chips)

Five count-up chips summarise what was delivered: Total litres, Total bottles, Products, Delivery days and Avg daily (L). These are derived from the selected business's supply profile (average daily volume × the period and its preferred-product list).

Financial summary (stat tiles)

TileWhat it meansTone
Previous outstandingBalance carried forward from the last cycle.Neutral
Current invoiceNew charges billed this cycle.Neutral
Payments receivedMoney collected during the cycle.Green
Credit notesValue of any credit notes applied.Neutral
DiscountsDiscounts given on the cycle.Neutral
GSTTax charged on the cycle.Neutral
Total outstandingPrevious + current − received − credit notes − adjustments.Amber
Net payableThe amount the partner ultimately owes.Forest
ℹ️
How the totals relate

The statement enforces the accounting identity Total outstanding = previous outstanding + current invoice − payments received − credit notes − adjustments, and Available credit = credit limit − outstanding (never below zero). These same relationships are unit-tested inside invoice.js (runTests()).

Outstanding & credit tracker

FieldPurposeNotes
OutstandingThe live balance owed on this account.Rupees.
Due dateWhen that balance is due.Date.
Days overdueHow many days past due (0 if on time).Turns red when non-zero.
Credit limitThe maximum credit granted to this partner.Set when the business is registered (credit limit field).
Available creditCredit limit − outstanding.Shown in green.
Settled % barShare of this cycle already paid.An animated progress bar with a "% of this cycle settled" label.
Amount due todayThe headline figure the partner should pay now.Large count-up number.
Balance after paymentWhat remains once the amount due is paid.Rupees.

Payment history — table spec

PropertyDetail
ColumnsDate · Invoice · Amount · Paid · Pending · Status.
RowsBuilt from the selected business's recent B2B orders (up to 6). Each row's Amount = order total, Paid = amount collected, Pending = the difference; Status is a badge (Paid / Partial / Pending).
Status badgeGreen "Paid" when paid ≥ amount; blue "Partial Payment" when part-paid; amber "Pending" when nothing collected.
MobileOn small screens each row collapses to a compact card (invoice + badge, then date · amount · paid · pending).
Sorting / search / paginationNone — this is a fixed statement table, not the interactive DataTable. To search or filter across many invoices use the invoice engine's list endpoint (Screen 3).

Business analytics

Eight read-only insight tiles round off the statement: Avg daily purchase, Monthly purchase, Quarterly purchase, Lifetime value, Highest month, Lowest month, Avg payment time and Most ordered (the partner's top product from its profile).

Action bar — buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
PrintOpens the browser print dialog with the statement laid out for paper (animations off).Calls window.print(); print CSS hides the toolbar and freezes counters.Super Admin Admin
Download PDFProduces a PDF of the statement.Uses the same print pipeline (window.print() → "Save as PDF"). For a server-rendered vector PDF of a numbered invoice, use the engine's PDF endpoint (Screen 3).Super Admin Admin
ExcelDownloads the statement as a spreadsheet.Builds an .xls file (business, GSTIN, period, the financial summary rows and the full payment-history table) and triggers a download; a toast confirms "Excel exported".Super Admin Admin
EmailOpens a pre-filled email to send the statement.Opens a mailto: with subject "Your DOODLY statement …", the statement number, the amount due and a link. Sending happens in your own mail client. Super Admin Admin only; requires your confirmation before you send.Super Admin Admin
WhatsAppShares the statement over WhatsApp.Opens a wa.me link with the statement title, number, amount and a link, in a new tab. You choose the recipient and press send.Super Admin Admin
⚙ Settings (gear)Opens Invoice Settings (Screen 2).Renders the config modal. The gear is only drawn for a Super Admin.Super Admin
⚠️
Empty state

If no business is registered (and demo data is off, as on the live site), the page shows a clean empty card: 🧾 "No business statement yet — Register a business and record B2B orders to generate a partner statement here." Register the partner in B2B Orders first; the statement fills in automatically once orders exist.

Invoice Settings

The Super-Admin control panel for how every DOODLY invoice and statement looks.

Purpose Set the branding and legal text that appears on every invoice and statement — brand name, tagline, number format, footer, terms, signature, watermark, and whether GST and the pay-QR are shown.
Access Super Admin only — the gear button and the modal are guarded by isSuper(). Path Admin → Commerce → Business Invoices → ⚙ (action bar)

Clicking the gear opens the Invoice settings dialog. Saving writes the settings, records a invoice.config entry in the audit trail, closes the dialog, shows an "Invoice settings saved" toast and re-draws the statement with the new branding. These settings apply to both the B2B statement and the B2C customer invoice.

Invoice settings — modal dialog
BrandingBrand name · Tagline
NumberingInvoice prefix · Number format
Legal textFooter message · Terms & conditions
MarksSignature line · Watermark text
TogglesShow GST · Show QR code
Save"Save settings" button + ✕ close
Figure — Invoice Settings dialog
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Brand nameThe name printed at the top of every document.Text.YesDefaults to the DOODLY brand name.DOODLY
TaglineThe line under the brand name on the B2C invoice head.Text.Opt.Statement head uses "Business Partner Statement" regardless.Fresh A2 buffalo milk, delivered daily
Invoice prefixPrefix used when building document numbers.Text.Opt.Stored in config.DOODLY
Number formatThe pattern for invoice numbers.A template with {YYYY} and a {#####} counter.Opt.Must contain a {#…} counter (checked by runTests()).DOODLY/INV/{YYYY}/{#####}
Footer messageThe thank-you line at the foot of the document.Multi-line text.Opt.Shown on both statement and invoice.Thank you for choosing DOODLY…
Terms & conditionsThe legal terms printed in the footer.Multi-line text.Opt.Shown on both documents.Payment due within the agreed credit period…
Signature lineThe "For <company>" authorised-signatory line.Text (blank hides it).Opt.Rendered above "Authorised signatory".For DOODLY Foods Pvt Ltd
Watermark textA diagonal watermark across the page.Text (blank = none).Opt.e.g. PAID / DRAFT.DRAFT
Show GSTWhether the GST line is printed.On / Off checkbox.Opt.Toggles the GST row on both documents.On
Show QR codeWhether the scan-to-pay/verify QR is printed.On / Off checkbox.Opt.Uses DOODLY_QR to draw the code.On
✅
Best Practice

Set the branding, terms and signature once, before the first real invoice goes out. Because these settings also drive the customer invoice and are baked into every emailed PDF, changing them mid-cycle means older documents and newer ones won't match. Use the Watermark ("DRAFT") while you are still testing, and clear it before real billing.

Business Invoice engine & automated email

The backend that issues numbered GST invoices per B2B order and emails the branded PDF.

Purpose Turn a delivered B2B order into a permanent, numbered BusinessInvoice record; auto-email the buyer a premium branded email with the PDF attached; track delivery, payments and status; and keep an append-only audit trail — all money-safe and idempotent.
Access Super Admin Admin for every endpoint (canUseB2B); resend email is Super Admin only. The emailed PDF/View links carry a signed token so the buyer can open their own invoice without logging in. Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Orders → Generate invoice · API /api/b2b/invoices
ℹ️
Where invoices are created

An invoice is issued from a B2B order — either automatically as part of the order flow, or by POST /api/b2b/invoices with an orderId. Each order can have exactly one invoice (a second attempt is rejected: "This order already has an invoice."). Amounts and line items always derive from the order; payments come from the order's BusinessPayment records.

Invoice records & API surface — list · detail · email tracker
Filtersstatus · business · product · text (q) · date from/to · amount from/to · overdue · sort
Invoice listNumber · Business · GSTIN · Order · Issued · Due · Status · Payment status · Total · Paid · GST · Items · Email status
Invoice detailOrder + business + line items + payments + audit events + email delivery block
Email trackerstatus (PENDING/SENT/FAILED/SKIPPED) · to · sent-at · message id · retry count · error
Uninvoiced pickerB2B orders with no invoice yet — the "which order to invoice" list
ReportsTotals: invoiced revenue · collected · outstanding · GST · overdue · by-business top 10
Figure — the invoice engine's data & API surface

Create-invoice form fields (POST /api/b2b/invoices)

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
orderIdThe B2B order to bill.An uninvoiced order id.YesMissing → 400 "Select an order to invoice". Cancelled order → "Cannot invoice a cancelled order". Already invoiced → 409.o_ck9…
dueDateWhen payment is due.A date.Opt.If blank, computed from the business's payment term: Weekly +7, Monthly +30, Credit +15, otherwise same day.2026-07-30
notesA note printed on the invoice.Text.Opt.Trimmed; blank saved as none.Delivered to loading dock B.
termsInvoice-specific terms.Text.Opt.Trimmed; blank saved as none.Net 30, no returns on perishables.

On success the invoice is minted with number DOODLY/B2B/{year}/{#####} (a per-year serial counter), a "created" audit event is written, and the automated email fires (see below).

Invoice list — table spec (GET /api/b2b/invoices)

PropertyDetail
ColumnsNumber · Business (code + name) · GSTIN · Order code · Issued at · Due date · Status · Payment status · Total · Paid · GST · Items summary (first 3 items) · Email status · Email sent-at.
Filtersstatus, businessId, productSlug, date from/to, amount amountFrom/amountTo, and overdue=1 (issued/partial invoices past due).
Searchq matches invoice number, order code, and the business code / name / GSTIN (case-insensitive).
Sortingsort = latest (default) · oldest · amount_desc · amount_asc · business · due.
Paginationlimit (1–100, default 20) and offset; the response returns total for paging.
ExportsPer-invoice: server-rendered PDF and the exact email HTML (see button table). The statement screen also exports Excel.

Invoice actions (PATCH /api/b2b/invoices/[id] & sub-routes) — buttons

Button / actionWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Record payment (pay)Books a payment against the invoice's order and re-syncs the invoice status.Calls recordPayment on the order, then sets the invoice to PAID (paid ≥ total) or PARTIAL; logs a "payment" event. Rejected on a void invoice.Super Admin Admin
Edit (update)Change the due date, notes or terms.Updates the record and logs an "updated" event. A voided invoice cannot be edited.Super Admin Admin
Void (void)Cancels the invoice.Sets status VOID, stamps voidedAt, logs a "void" event. A void invoice can't be edited or paid. Already-void → 409.Super Admin Admin
Download PDF (pdf)Streams the branded, server-rendered vector PDF.GET …/[id]/pdf?dl=1 generates it on demand (invoice-pdf.ts); logs a "pdf" event. Access via an authorised admin session or a valid signed token.Super Admin Admin · buyer via signed link
Preview email HTMLOpens/downloads the exact branded email that was (or would be) sent.GET …/[id]/email-html renders the template via renderInvoiceEmailById.Super Admin Admin
Export (export)Marks/records an export of the invoice.Logs an "export" audit event.Super Admin Admin
Resend email (resend-email)Force-resends the branded invoice email + PDF.Calls sendBusinessInvoiceEmail(force:true). Blocked for Admin — "Only a Super Admin can resend invoice emails." Sends on the partner's behalf, so treat as a deliberate action.Super Admin
Workflow — issue & deliver a Business Invoice
B2B order deliveredGenerate invoice (orderId)Number minted DOODLY/B2B/YYYY/#####PDF renderedBranded email + PDF sent (Resend)Email status trackedPayments recorded → PAID/PARTIAL
Figure — from delivered order to a settled, emailed invoice

Automated email — status & safeguards

When an invoice is created the engine automatically builds the PDF and sends a premium branded email to the business's address, then records the outcome on the invoice's Email status:

Email statusMeaningWhat happens
SENTThe email was delivered to the provider.Records message id + sent-at; increments retry count; logs an "email" event. Won't re-send unless a Super Admin forces a resend (idempotent).
PENDINGThe email provider isn't configured (e.g. local dev without a Resend key).Queued quietly, no failure noise; sends once the provider is set.
SKIPPEDThe business has no email on file.Marks skipped and alerts Super Admins in-app to add an address and resend.
FAILEDSending failed after retries.Records the error, alerts Super Admins, and can be retried from the invoice.
ℹ️
Graceful degradation

If the PDF fails to generate, the email is still sent with a secure download link instead of an attachment, and Super Admins are alerted. The View/Download links in the email are signed and valid for 60 days, so the buyer can open the invoice without an account. The whole send flow never throws into the order/invoice code — a failed email never blocks issuing the invoice.

Invoice reports (GET /api/b2b/invoices/reports)

A date-ranged roll-up across all live (non-void) invoices: total invoices, count by status, invoiced revenue, collected, outstanding, GST, overdue count and overdue value, plus a by-business breakdown (top 10 by revenue, each with count, revenue and outstanding).

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common issues and quick answers.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Page shows "No business statement yet"No business is registered, or demo data is off with none in the database.Register the partner and record at least one B2B order in B2B Orders; the statement then appears.
Banner reads "⚠ Backend offline…"The Next.js API isn't reachable from the storefront.Confirm the backend is running and the storefront points at the right API base; reload once it's up.
Banner reads "Your role can't access B2B (403)"You're signed in as a role other than Admin / Super Admin.B2B invoices are Admin/Super-Admin only — switch to an authorised account.
Invoice email shows SKIPPEDThe business has no email address on file.Add the business email in B2B Orders → business, then resend (Super Admin).
Invoice email shows PENDING foreverThe email provider (Resend) key isn't set in the environment.Add the provider key in the deployment env; queued invoices then send.
"This order already has an invoice."An invoice was already issued for that order (one-to-one).Open the existing invoice instead of creating a new one; void it first if you must reissue.
"Resend" is greyed out / rejected for youResend is Super-Admin only.Ask a Super Admin to resend, or the customer can use the secure link in the original email.

Is the money on the statement live?

The partner's identity, profile (order count, revenue, average daily volume, outstanding, preferred products) and the payment-history rows come from the selected business's real registered record and recorded B2B orders. The statement enforces the standard accounting identities (total outstanding, available credit) shown above. For a strictly per-invoice, database-authoritative figure, open the numbered invoice via the engine (Screen 3).

What's the difference between the statement's "Download PDF" and the engine's PDF?

The statement's Download PDF prints the on-screen statement through the browser. The engine's PDF (/api/b2b/invoices/[id]/pdf) is a server-rendered vector PDF of a single numbered invoice, built with pdf-lib and used as the email attachment — that's the document you send to a partner for their accounts.

Who receives the automated invoice email?

The business's email on file. The email carries the branded invoice, a totals summary, and signed View/Download links valid for 60 days. Delivery is tracked on the invoice; if it fails or is skipped, Super Admins get an in-app alert to fix and resend.

✅
Best Practice

Before month-end billing, open each partner's statement, confirm the credit limit and due date are right, and clear any red alerts. Then issue invoices from B2B Orders so every partner gets the automated branded email + PDF. Reconcile the /api/b2b/invoices/reports outstanding and overdue totals against your ledger at the close of each cycle.

DOODLYAdmin · Commerce · B2B Order Management
§Admin · Commerce

B2B Order Management

Register commercial customers (hotels, cafés, caterers), create bulk milk & dairy orders, move them through a fixed delivery workflow, record payments, raise invoices, and read per-business and portfolio reports — all in one place.

ℹ️
Where this module lives

Open it from Admin → Commerce → B2B Orders. The whole module is one page with five tabs across the top: Businesses, Register, Create Order, Orders and Reports. Every record you create gets a permanent, sequential ID that is never reused: a business is DOO-B2B-000001, an order is B2B-ORD-2026-000125, and an invoice is DOODLY/B2B/2026/00001.

⚠️
Who may use this module

The B2B module is restricted to Admin and Super Admin only. Managers, Accountants, Delivery Executives and Customers cannot open it. This is enforced on the server (canUseB2B in lib/b2b/guard.ts) — hiding the menu item is only a convenience. One action, Delete a business, is reserved for Super Admin alone.

Businesses

The directory of every commercial customer, with a full drill-down profile.

Purpose Find any business, review its trading history and outstanding money, and jump straight into a new order, an edit, or negotiated pricing.
Access Super Admin Admin Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Orders → Businesses
Header — B2B Order Management · tabs: Businesses · Register · Create Order · Orders · Reports
Search barOne box that matches on Business ID, name, mobile or GST number
Businesses tableID · Name · Type · Contact · Mobile · Terms · Status · View
Profile drawer (on View)Address, GST/PAN, discount & credit limit, 4 KPI cards, negotiated prices, order history
Row action strip+ New order · Disable/Enable · Edit · Pricing & Commercials → · Delete
Figure — the Businesses list with an expanded profile drawer.

Table — Businesses list

PropertyDetail
ColumnsBusiness ID · Name · Type · Contact (person) · Mobile · Terms (payment) · Status (Active / Inactive badge) · View
SearchSingle field — matches Business ID, name, mobile number and GST number (case-insensitive, partial match)
Filters / SortingNo column filters or sort controls; the list shows all non-deleted businesses. Soft-deleted businesses are hidden automatically.
Row actionView / Close expands or collapses the full profile drawer for that business
Empty state"No businesses. Use the Register tab to add one."

Profile drawer — buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
+ New orderOpens the Create Order tab with this business already selectedPre-loads the business, its payment terms, discount and negotiated pricesAdmin+
DisableMarks the business Inactive (kept for history, hidden from new work)setActive(id,false) → audit b2b.business.updateAdmin+
EnableRe-activates a disabled businesssetActive(id,true) → audit b2b.business.updateAdmin+
EditLoads the business into the Register form to change any detailOpens Register in edit mode; save runs updateBusinessAdmin+
Pricing & Commercials →Jumps to the B2B Pricing screen scoped to this businessStores the business id and opens /admin/b2b-pricing.htmlAdmin+
DeleteSoft-deletes the business (order history is retained)softDelete(id) sets deleted=true + active=false → audit b2b.business.delete; asks for confirmation firstSuper Admin
Reorder (per history row)Recreates a past order for tomorrow's datereorder(id) clones the items into a new Pending orderAdmin+
ℹ️
What the profile shows

The drawer lists the full address, email & alternate number, GST / PAN, the business discount and credit limit, and preferred delivery time. Four KPI cards summarise Total orders, Revenue, Outstanding and Avg daily quantity, followed by last delivery date and the top preferred products. A Negotiated prices block shows any custom per-product rates (with a "% off" badge) or a prompt to set them. The last eight orders appear as a mini history, each with its code, date, item count, status, total, payment badge, invoice number and a Reorder link.

Register business

The onboarding form for a new commercial customer (also used to edit an existing one).

Purpose Capture everything the business needs to trade: contact, delivery address with map pin, tax details, and payment & delivery commercials.
Access Super Admin Admin Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Orders → Register
Form — Register business · four grouped sections · Register / Save button
Business informationName, type, contact person, mobile, alternate number, email
AddressAddress, landmark, area, city, state, pincode + Latitude/Longitude from a Google Maps pin
GST & billing (optional)GST number, PAN, billing address
Payment & deliveryPayment terms, discount %, credit limit, preferred time, delivery notes
Figure — the four-section registration form.

Field reference

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Business nameLegal / trading nameText, 2–120 charsYesmin 2, max 120Grand Park Hotel
Business typeCategory of establishmentHotel, Restaurant, Café, Bakery, Sweet Shop, Tea Stall, Catering, Hostel, Hospital, Corporate Office, OtherYesFixed dropdown (enum)Hotel
Contact personWho to call for ordersText, min 2 charsYesmin 2Mr. Suresh
MobilePrimary phoneIndian 10-digit mobile (optional +91)YesRegex [6-9]\d{9}98480 22113
Alternate numberBackup phoneIndian mobileOpt.Same mobile regex when filled90000 55221
EmailFor invoices & alertsValid emailOpt.Email format when filledorders@grandpark.in
Business addressDelivery address line 1Text, min 4 charsYesmin 4Benz Circle, Vijayawada
LandmarkHelps the driver find itTextOpt.Up to 200 charsOpp. bus stand
AreaLocality / zoneTextOpt.Up to 200 charsBenz Circle
CityDelivery cityTextOpt.Defaults to VijayawadaVijayawada
StateDelivery stateTextOpt.Defaults to Andhra PradeshAndhra Pradesh
PincodePostal code for routing6-digit PIN (first digit 1–9)YesRegex [1-9]\d{5}520010
LatitudeExact map positionDecimal numberOpt.Dropped from a Google Maps pin16.5062
LongitudeExact map positionDecimal numberOpt.Dropped from a Google Maps pin80.6480
GST numberTax registrationText, up to 20 charsOpt.Stored as-is37ABCDE1234F1Z5
PANTax account numberText, up to 12 charsOpt.Stored as-isABCDE1234F
Billing addressIf different from deliveryText, up to 300 charsOpt.Stored as-isHead office, Hyderabad
Payment termsHow this business paysCash, Credit, Weekly Billing, Monthly Billing, Advance PaymentOpt.Defaults to Cash; drives payment-status logicMonthly Billing
Business discount %Standing discount on every orderPercent (0–100)Opt.Stored as basis points (5% → 500 bps; 0–10000)5
Credit limit (₹)Max unpaid exposure allowedWhole rupees, ≥ 0Opt.Warns at order time if the total would exceed it50000
Preferred delivery timeStanding delivery slotFree textOpt.Pre-fills new orders' time7:00 AM
Delivery notesStanding instructionsText, up to 500 charsOpt.Stored on the businessUse rear service gate

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Register businessCreates the business and shows its new DOO-B2B IDregisterBusiness → assigns next sequential code → audit b2b.business.createAdmin+
Save changes (edit mode)Applies edits to an existing businessupdateBusiness → audit b2b.business.updateAdmin+
Cancel (edit mode)Discards edits and returns to BusinessesNo writeAdmin+
View businesses →Appears after a successful register; opens the listNo writeAdmin+
⚠️
Two layers of validation

The on-screen form only blocks a save when name, mobile or address is empty. The production backend (BusinessSchema in lib/b2b/validation.ts) is stricter — it also rejects a bad mobile number, an invalid 6-digit pincode, a name under 2 characters, and a contact person under 2 characters. Always fill every starred field correctly so the record saves cleanly end-to-end.

💡
Tip — set discount and credit limit at registration

The business discount is applied automatically to every future order, and the credit limit triggers an on-screen warning if an order would push the business over its exposure. Setting both here saves you from repeating them on each order.

Create Order

Build a bulk order line by line, with live pricing, discount, GST and a running grand total.

Purpose Turn a business's request into a priced, saved order that enters the delivery workflow as Pending.
Access Super Admin Admin Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Orders → Create Order
Form — Create Order · select business → add products → delivery → totals → Submit
Business selectorSearch box + Find; matching businesses appear as pick buttons. Once picked, a header shows code, contact, terms and credit limit with a Change link.
Product rowsProduct · Qty · Unit · Unit price ₹ · business (base) price with slab/retail badge and GST% · line total · Remove · + Add product
Override reasonAppears on any row whose unit price differs from the business price; a reason is mandatory
Delivery & reviewDelivery date, preferred time, discount %, additional charges, remarks
Totals panelSubtotal, Discount, Additional charges, GST, Grand total + credit-limit warning
Figure — the Create Order builder with the live totals panel.

Field reference

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Business (search)Pick who the order is forBusiness ID / name / mobile / GST, then click a matchYesMust resolve to an existing businessDOO-B2B-000001
ProductWhat to supplyA2 Buffalo Milk, Buffalo Pot Curd, Malai Paneer, Palkova, Buffalo GheeYesFrom the live catalogueA2 Buffalo Milk
QtyHow muchNumber > 0Yesquantity positive; can retrigger a quantity slab price40
UnitMeasure for this productMilk: Litres/Bottles · Curd: KG/Litres/Tubs · Paneer: KG/Packs · Palkova: KG/Packs · Ghee: KG/Litres/TinsYesProduct-specific listLitres
Unit price ₹Charged price per unitNumber ≥ 0YesAuto-loads the business (negotiated/slab/retail) price; editable only if you have override permission66
Override reasonWhy the price differs from the business defaultFree textYes (only when overridden)Order is blocked until every overridden line has a reasonNegotiated for 250 L
Delivery dateWhen to deliverDateYesDefaults to tomorrow2026-07-13
Preferred delivery timeDelivery slotFree textYesDefaults to 7:00 AM (or the business's preferred time)5:30 AM
Discount %Order-level discountPercentOpt.Pre-filled from the business discount; stored as basis points5
Additional charges ₹Delivery / packaging add-onNumberOpt.Added after discount, before/with GST into the total150
RemarksFree note on the orderTextOpt.Stored on the orderFestival week — extra curd

How the total is calculated

LineHow it is worked out
SubtotalSum of every line's (unit price × quantity), rounded per line
DiscountSubtotal × discount % (held internally as basis points)
Additional chargesAdded as entered
GSTSum of each line's GST, using that product's live GST slab (0 / 5 / 12 / 18%)
Grand totalSubtotal − Discount + Additional charges + GST

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
FindSearches for a business to order forlookup(query) lists matchesAdmin+
+ Add productAdds another product rowPre-fills the business price + GST for the chosen productAdmin+
RemoveDeletes a product rowRecalculates totals liveAdmin+
ChangeSwitches to a different business (clears the draft)Resets the order formAdmin+
Submit orderSaves the order as PendingcreateOrder → next B2B-ORD code, records any price overrides, audit b2b.order.create; opens the Orders tabAdmin+
ℹ️
Price overrides are traceable

Each product row shows its business price ("Biz ₹…") with a slab or retail badge and the GST %. If you change the unit price away from that base, the row is flagged as overridden and a mandatory Override reason appears. On submit, every override is recorded against the order and the business in B2B Pricing — the business's standing default is never altered. If your role lacks override permission the unit-price box is disabled.

⚠️
Credit-limit warning

If the grand total would push the business over its credit limit, a red "⚠ Exceeds credit limit of ₹…" banner appears in the totals panel. It is a warning, not a hard block — use judgment and, if needed, take a payment or raise the limit before submitting.

Orders

The working list of every B2B order, with status changes, payments and invoicing in one drawer.

Purpose Track and move orders through the delivery workflow, record money against them, and raise invoices.
Access Super Admin Admin Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Orders → Orders
List — Orders · status filter chips · search · Manage drawer
Filter chipsAll + Pending · Confirmed · Preparing · Out for Delivery · Delivered · Completed · Cancelled
Search boxMatches order code or business name
Orders tableOrder · Business · Delivery (date + time) · Items · Total · Payment · Status · Manage
Manage drawerNext-status buttons, record payment, generate invoice, reorder, print, item list, outstanding, notes
Figure — the Orders list with the Manage drawer open on one row.

Table — Orders list

PropertyDetail
ColumnsOrder (code) · Business · Delivery (date + time) · Items (count) · Total · Payment (badge) · Status (badge) · Manage
FiltersStatus chips: All, Pending, Confirmed, Preparing, Out for Delivery, Delivered, Completed, Cancelled
SearchMatches order code and business name (case-insensitive)
SortingNewest first (by created date)
Row actionManage / Close opens the action drawer for that order
Empty state"No orders yet."

Manage drawer — buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Next-status buttonsMove the order one step forward (e.g. Confirmed →, Preparing →) or CancelsetStatus — only the allowed next step is offered; audit b2b.order.statusAdmin+
Record paymentAdds a payment for the amount typed in the boxrecordPayment — updates paid amount & payment status; audit b2b.order.payAdmin+
Generate invoiceRaises a numbered invoice (DOODLY/B2B/…)genInvoice — assigns the next invoice number once; audit b2b.invoiceAdmin+
ReorderClones this order for tomorrow as a new Pending orderreorder — copies items into a fresh orderAdmin+
PrintOpens the browser print dialog for the orderwindow.print()Admin+
ℹ️
What else the drawer shows

Below the buttons the drawer lists the full items (quantity, unit and product), the Outstanding amount, and any remarks. Once an invoice exists, a green "Invoice DOODLY/B2B/…" badge replaces the Generate button. The Record-payment controls disappear automatically once an order is fully Paid.

Workflow — B2B order status lifecycle
PendingConfirmedPreparingOut for DeliveryDeliveredCompleted
Figure — the forward path. From Pending through Out for Delivery, any step can also go to Cancelled. Delivered can only move to Completed; Completed and Cancelled are final.

Status rules

Current statusAllowed next steps
PendingConfirmed, Cancelled
ConfirmedPreparing, Cancelled
PreparingOut for Delivery, Cancelled
Out for DeliveryDelivered, Cancelled
DeliveredCompleted
Completed— (final)
Cancelled— (final)

Payment status — how it is decided

BadgeWhen it shows
PaidAmount paid is greater than or equal to the total
Partially PaidSome money is paid, but less than the total
CreditNothing paid yet and the terms are Credit / Weekly / Monthly billing
PendingNothing paid yet on Cash or Advance terms
✅
Best Practice — advance a status only when it is physically true

Move an order to Out for Delivery only when it is actually loaded, and to Delivered only when the customer has it. The workflow is forward-only past each step, so an accidental jump cannot be quietly walked back — the only escape from a mid-workflow status is Cancelled.

Reports

Portfolio-level revenue, collection and outstanding numbers with top businesses and products.

Purpose See how the whole B2B book is performing over a chosen date range and export it.
Access Super Admin Admin Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Orders → Reports
Report — Reports · date presets · Export CSV
Date presetsAll · Last 7d · Last 30d
KPI cardsOrders · Revenue · Collected · Outstanding
Top businesses / Top productsTwo ranked tables by revenue
Outstanding payments / Orders by statusMoney owed per order and a status breakdown
Figure — the four-KPI header over the four report tables.

What each block means

BlockWhat it shows
OrdersCount of non-cancelled orders in the range
RevenueTotal value of those orders
CollectedMoney actually received against them
OutstandingRevenue minus Collected — money still owed
