Top businessesBusiness · orders · revenue, ranked by revenue
Top productsProduct · quantity · revenue, ranked by revenue
Outstanding paymentsOrder · business · amount still owed, per unpaid order
Orders by statusCount of orders in each status

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
All / Last 7d / Last 30dSets the date range for every figure and tableRecomputes reports(from,to) liveAdmin+
Export CSVDownloads the KPIs, top businesses and top products as a CSV fileBuilds doodly-b2b-report.csv in the browserAdmin+
💡
Tip — the range filters by delivery date

The date presets bracket orders by their delivery date, and cancelled orders are excluded from every figure. Use "Last 30d" for a monthly review and export the CSV before a billing run so Accounts has the same numbers you see on screen.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Quick fixes for the most common B2B snags.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Submit order stays greyed outNo product rows added yetClick + Add product and set a quantity — the button enables once at least one line exists.
"Add a reason for each overridden price"You changed a unit price away from the business price without a reasonFill the red Override reason box on each flagged row, then submit.
"Cannot move to …" when changing statusThe step isn't allowed from the current statusFollow the workflow: only the next forward step (or Cancel) is valid. See the Status rules table.
Record-payment controls are missingThe order is already fully PaidNone needed — the box hides automatically once paid in full.
"⚠ Exceeds credit limit" bannerOrder total is above the business's credit limitTake a payment, lower the order, or raise the credit limit on the business (Edit) — it is a warning, not a block.
Business won't saveMissing name, mobile or address (or an invalid mobile/pincode on the backend)Complete every starred field with a valid 10-digit mobile and 6-digit pincode.
Delete button not visible on a businessYou are signed in as Admin, not Super AdminDeleting a business is Super-Admin only; ask a Super Admin, or just Disable it instead.

Q. Can I change a business's standing price from the order screen?

No. Editing a unit price on an order overrides it for that one order only, and the change is logged with your reason. To change the standing negotiated price, use Pricing & Commercials → (the B2B Pricing screen).

Q. What is the difference between Disable and Delete?

Disable keeps the business and all its order history but marks it Inactive so it is set aside from new work — any Admin can do it and it is reversible with Enable. Delete is a Super-Admin-only soft delete that hides the business from the list while still retaining its order history.

Q. Do I have to invoice before I can take payment?

No. Payments and invoices are independent — you can record a payment on any not-fully-paid order, and generate the invoice before or after. An order gets at most one invoice number, assigned the first time you click Generate invoice.

Q. Why do some order numbers reset each year?

Order and invoice numbers include the year and run a fresh sequence per year (e.g. B2B-ORD-2026-000001, DOODLY/B2B/2026/00001). Business IDs are a single lifetime sequence (DOO-B2B-000001) that never resets and never reuses a number.

DOODLYAdmin · Commerce · B2B Pricing
§Admin · Commerce

B2B Pricing

Give every commercial customer its own negotiated product prices, quantity slabs and effective dates — with a full change history, bulk tools, reports and an order-time override log that never touches the saved default.

ℹ️
What this screen is for

Retail (storefront) customers pay one published price. A hotel, café or reseller usually negotiates a different rate. B2B Pricing is where you record that negotiated rate per business, per product, so every B2B order automatically fills in the right price. If a business has no custom price for a product, the order simply uses the normal retail price. Prices are stored as whole rupees on screen; the DOODLY database keeps them as paise (₹1 = 100 paise) for exactness.

⚠️
Who can do what

Viewing pricing is open to Admin and Super Admin. Every change — editing a price, setting slabs, bulk updates, copy/import, scheduling, deactivating a business's pricing and editing settings — is restricted to the Super Admin. The server (/api/b2b/pricing) additionally blocks any role that is not Admin or Super Admin with a 403 Forbidden. Managers, Accountants, Delivery Executives and Customers cannot open this module.

Pricing Workspace & Header

The four live counters and the seven-tab shell that every B2B Pricing task lives inside.

Purpose Give a one-glance summary of how much custom pricing exists, and switch between the seven work areas.
Access Super Admin full · Admin view-only Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Pricing
B2B Pricing — Dynamic B2B Pricing · live-from-database banner · Run tests
KPI strip (4 counters)Businesses priced · Custom prices · Order overrides · Scheduled
Run testsSelf-test button; shows a green/red "X/Y tests" badge
Tab bar (7)Product Pricing · Bulk & Schedule · Copy/Import/Export · Price History · Order Overrides · Reports · Settings
Tab bodyThe selected tab's content renders here
Figure — the persistent header (KPIs + tabs) that frames all seven screens.

When the page loads it first pulls live pricing rules from the DOODLY database (GET /api/b2b/pricing?limit=500) and shows a green banner such as ● Live — B2B pricing from the DOODLY database (12 rule(s)). For each business + product it keeps the rule with the smallest minimum quantity and converts paise to rupees for display.

CounterWhat it countsWhere the number comes from
Businesses pricedHow many businesses have at least one custom price savedBusinesses in the pricing store with a numeric price on any product
Custom pricesTotal number of custom product prices across all businessesCount of products with a set price
Order overridesTotal order-time price overrides recordedThe Order Overrides log
ScheduledFuture price changes not yet appliedScheduled-changes list where applied = false
💡
Tip — the "Run tests" button is safe

"Run tests" runs a built-in self-check of the pricing engine (slab selection, effective-date handling, bulk maths, override logging and a 500-business speed test). It snapshots and restores your real data, so it never changes any live price. A green X/Y tests badge means the engine is healthy.

Product Pricing (main tab)

Set, reset, enable/disable and slab-price every product for one chosen business.

Purpose Maintain the negotiated price list for a single business, product by product.
Access Super Admin edit · Admin view Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Pricing → Product Pricing
Product Pricing — business picker · product search · Deactivate/Activate pricing · Customer preview
Business dropdownChoose which business's prices you are editing (code · name)
Search productFilter the table by product name or slug
Deactivate / Activate pricingTurns all custom pricing for the business on or off at once
Customer previewShows the catalogue exactly as this business sees it
Price tableProduct · Retail price (+GST%) · Business price (+% off) · Status · row actions
Edit / Slab drawerInline row editor and quantity-slab editor open here
Figure — the Product Pricing tab with picker row, action buttons and price table.

Each row shows the product's normal retail price (with its GST %), the business price in effect, a savings badge (% off in green, or +% in amber if the business price is higher than retail), and a status badge. When custom pricing is deactivated for the business a warning strip appears and every product falls back to retail.

Row action buttons (Super Admin only — Admins see "View only")

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
EditOpens the inline editor to type a new business price and notessetPrice() saves the price; blank clears it. Writes a Price History entry and an audit logSuper Admin
SaveConfirms the edited price + notessetPrice() + setNotes(); empty price calls resetToRetail()Super Admin
CancelDiscards the inline editCloses the editor — no saveSuper Admin
SlabsOpens the quantity-slab editor for that productToggles the slab drawer (see field table below)Super Admin
ResetClears the custom price and slabs → back to retail (shown only if a custom price exists)resetToRetail(); logs a "Reset to retail price" history entry + auditSuper Admin
Disable / EnableMarks the product unavailable / available to this businesstoggleProduct(); a disabled product resolves as "Disabled" and falls back to retail for pricingSuper Admin
Deactivate / Activate pricingTurns the whole business's custom pricing off/on in one clicksetBizActive(); while off, all products use retailSuper Admin
Customer previewShows a read-only "your negotiated catalogue" card (your price / unit, retail struck through, you-save badge)Renders catalogue() for enabled products — no data changeAdmin+

Inline edit — fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Business price ₹The negotiated per-unit price for this businessNumber ₹0 or moreYesMust be ≥ 0 and ≤ 1,000,000; blank clears the custom price back to retail. Placeholder shows the retail price78
NotesFree note explaining the rate (shown under the product name)Any textOpt.Saved via setNotes()Negotiated bulk rate

Quantity-slab editor — fields (per slab row)

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Min qtyLowest order quantity this slab applies toWhole numberYesDefaults to 0 if blank101
Max (∞)Highest quantity for the slab; leave blank for "and above"Whole number or blankOpt.Blank = no upper limit (infinity)250
Price ₹Per-unit price when the order quantity falls in this bandNumberYesRows with no price are dropped when saving (setSlabs())77

Slab editor buttons: + Add slab adds a new band, Remove deletes a row, Save slabs stores them and logs a history entry, and Close discards. Slabs only take effect if slab pricing is enabled in Settings.

Price table — columns & controls

AspectDetail
ColumnsProduct (+ notes + "N qty slab(s)" hint) · Retail price (+ GST %) · Business price (+ % off / +%) · Status · Actions
SearchLive filter on product name + slug (the "Search product" box)
FiltersBusiness dropdown selects whose price list is shown
Row actionsEdit · Slabs · Reset · Disable/Enable (Super Admin) — Admins see "View only"
Status badgesCustom (green) · Slab (blue) · Retail (grey) · Disabled (red) · Scheduled (amber) · Expired (amber)
Workflow — how a price is resolved for a B2B order line
Business pricing off? → RetailProduct disabled? → DisabledEffective date not started / expired? → RetailSlab matches qty (slabs on)? → Slab priceFlat business price set? → Business priceOtherwise → Retail
Figure — the fixed order of checks the engine (resolveCfg) runs to pick a price.
✅
Best Practice

Always type a clear reason when you change a price so the Price History stays useful during audits and contract reviews. Use slabs for volume deals (for example ₹78 up to 100 L, ₹77 for 101–250 L, ₹75 for 501 L and above) instead of editing the price by hand every time the order size changes.

Bulk & Schedule

Change many products at once, or set a price to take effect on a future date.

Purpose Apply a percentage change across products in one action, and queue price changes for a future start date.
Access Super Admin only Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Pricing → Bulk & Schedule
Bulk & Schedule — business picker
Bulk price updateProduct checkboxes · Action · Percentage · Reason · Apply
Schedule a future priceProduct · New price · Effective from · Reason · Schedule change
Upcoming changes listEach scheduled row with a Cancel link
Figure — two side-by-side panels: bulk update (left) and scheduling (right).

Bulk price update — fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Product checkboxesWhich products to changeOne or more productsOpt.Leave all unchecked to apply to every product☑ A2 Buffalo Milk
ActionThe kind of changeIncrease by % · Reduce by % · Promotional (% off retail)YesIncrease/Reduce work off the current business price; Promotional works off retailIncrease by %
PercentageHow much to change byNumberYesApplied and rounded to whole rupees; a result below 0 becomes 05
ReasonNote stored on every history entry the bulk run createsAny textOpt.Falls back to an auto reason like "Bulk increase 5%"Seasonal increase

Apply bulk update runs bulkUpdate(), updates the chosen products, records a history entry for each and one audit line, then jumps back to Product Pricing showing the new prices.

Schedule a future price — fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
ProductWhich product the future price is forProduct from the listYes—A2 Buffalo Milk
New price ₹The price to switch to on the start dateNumberYesMust be a valid number (schedule())75
Effective fromThe date the new price startsDate (defaults to today)YesA start date is required2026-08-01
ReasonNote kept with the scheduled changeAny textOpt.Defaults to "Scheduled price change"New contract rate

Schedule change queues the change; it appears in the "Upcoming changes" list with a Cancel link. Scheduled changes whose date has arrived are applied automatically each time the module opens (applyScheduled()), which then writes the price and a history entry as if you had set it by hand.

ℹ️
Note — "Promotional" resets from retail

Increase and Reduce adjust the current business price. Promotional is different: it computes the new price as a percentage off the retail price, ignoring whatever custom price was there. Use Promotional for festival or launch offers, and Reduce for shaving a few percent off an already-negotiated rate.

Copy / Import / Export

Reuse one business's price list on another, or move price lists in and out as CSV/Excel.

Purpose Speed up setting up new businesses by copying an existing price map, and exchange price lists with spreadsheets.
Access Super Admin for copy/import · Admin can export Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Pricing → Copy / Import / Export
Copy / Import / Export — target business picker
Copy pricing"Copy from" source dropdown → Copy pricing button
ExportExport CSV · Export Excel of the current price list
ImportPaste box (product, price, notes) → Import price list · Download template
Import resultShows count imported and any skipped rows
Figure — copy panel (left) and import/export panel (right).

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Copy pricing →Copies the full product price map (and slabs) from the source business into the targetcopyPricing(); existing prices on the target are overwritten; logs a history + audit entrySuper Admin
Export CSVDownloads the current business's price list as a .csv fileexportPriceList("csv") — Product, Retail, Business, Status, GST, NotesAdmin+
Export ExcelDownloads the same list as an Excel (.xls) fileexportPriceList("xls")Admin+
Import price listReads the pasted lines and sets each product's priceimportList(); matches by slug or exact product name; unknown products are skipped and listedSuper Admin
Download templateDownloads a starter CSV (product,price,notes) you can fill inGenerates doodly-price-template.csvSuper Admin

Import — fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Copy fromSource business whose prices you want to reuseAny other business (the target is excluded)YesSource and target must differ and the source must have custom pricingDB-000002 · Grand Café
Price list (paste box)One product per line to importproduct or slug, price, notesYesA header line beginning "product," is ignored; rows without a product and price are droppedA2 Buffalo Milk, 78, Bulk rate
⚠️
Warning — Copy overwrites

Copying a price list replaces the target business's existing prices for every product it copies. Check the target in the dropdown before you press Copy pricing →. Use Price History afterwards to confirm exactly what changed.

Price History

A dated log of every price change, who made it and why.

Purpose Provide an audit trail of all pricing changes for a business or across all businesses.
Access Admin+ view · Super Admin Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Pricing → Price History
Price History — business filter · search · Export CSV
Business filter"All businesses" or a specific business
SearchFilter by product, reason, user or business code
History tableProduct · Previous · New price · Changed by · Reason · Effective date
Export CSVDownloads the filtered history
Figure — the Price History log with filters and export.
AspectDetail
ColumnsProduct (+ business code) · Previous · New price · Changed by · Reason · Effective
SearchMatches product, reason, changed-by user and business code
FiltersBusiness dropdown (including "All businesses")
Rows shownMost recent first, up to 300 on screen (the store keeps up to 2,000)
ExportsExport CSV (Date, Business, Product, Previous, New, Changed by, Reason)
Row actionsNone — this is a read-only record

The DOODLY database keeps a matching per-rule history (BusinessPricingHistory) recording each action — created, updated, deleted, restored — with the old and new price, old and new GST, the reason and the role that made the change. This is the tamper-evident source of truth behind the on-screen log.

Order Overrides

Every one-off price change made while creating a B2B order — the saved default is never touched.

Purpose Track ad-hoc negotiations at order time so you can see how often, and by how much, staff deviate from the agreed price.
Access Admin+ view Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Pricing → Order Overrides
Order Overrides — business filter · Export CSV
Business filter"All businesses" or a specific one
Overrides tableOrder · Product · Qty · Business price · Charged · Δ · Reason · By
Export CSVDownloads the filtered overrides
Figure — the Order Overrides log with delta badges.
AspectDetail
ColumnsOrder (+ business code) · Product · Qty · Business price · Charged · Δ (difference) · Reason · By (+ date/time)
Delta badgeGreen "−₹X" when charged below the business price; amber "+₹X" when charged above
FiltersBusiness dropdown (including "All businesses")
Rows shownMost recent first, up to 300 (store keeps up to 2,000)
ExportsExport CSV (Date, Order, Business, Product, Qty, Business price, Charged, Reason, By)
Row actionsNone — read-only record
ℹ️
Note — where overrides come from

Overrides are created over in B2B Orders → Create order. When an authorized user changes a line price while building an order and gives a reason, that change is logged here (recordOverride) and the order is charged the new amount — but the business's saved price is left exactly as it was. Order-time overriding is allowed for the Super Admin, or anyone granted the pricing override permission or B2B edit permission.

Reports

Pricing analytics — average selling price, margin, discount given and sales by business.

Purpose See how negotiated pricing affects revenue, discount and margin across products and businesses.
Access Admin+ view Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Pricing → Reports
Reports — Pricing analytics · CSV · Excel · PDF
KPI cards (4)Orders · Revenue · Discount given · Est. margin
Average selling price & marginProduct · Qty · Avg price · Retail · Disc % · Margin %
Business-wise pricingBusiness · Custom/Total · Avg disc · Status
Sales by businessBusiness · Orders · Revenue
Recent price overridesOrder · Product · Biz→Final · Reason
Export barCSV · Excel · PDF (print)
Figure — four KPI cards over four analytics tables.
Report blockWhat it tells you
OrdersNumber of live (non-cancelled) B2B orders in range
RevenueTotal value of those orders
Discount givenRupees of discount vs retail, summed across products
Est. marginEstimated margin using the cost prices set in Settings
Average selling price & marginPer product: qty sold, average charged price, retail, discount % and margin %
Business-wise pricingPer business: how many products are custom-priced, average discount and whether pricing is Active/Off
Sales by businessPer business: order count and revenue
Recent price overridesThe latest order-time overrides (biz price → final)

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission

ExcelDownloads the same report as .xlsexportReports("xls")Admin+

Settings

Platform-wide pricing switches and the cost prices used for margin analysis.

Purpose Turn slab pricing and override approval on/off, record product costs, and (carefully) reset all pricing data.
Access Super Admin only Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Pricing → Settings
Pricing settings
Slab pricing switchEnable quantity-slab pricing platform-wide
Override approval switchRequire approval for order-time price overrides
Product cost pricesOne ₹ field per product, used for margin analysis
Save / ResetSave settings · Reset all pricing data (danger)
Figure — two toggles, the cost-price grid, and the save/reset controls.
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Enable quantity-slab pricing platform-wideMaster switch for slab pricing everywhereOn / OffOpt.When off, orders use the flat business price and ignore slabsOn
Require approval for order-time price overridesFlags that overrides should be approvedOn / OffOpt.Stored on the settings recordOff
Product cost ₹ (per product)The cost used to work out margin % in ReportsNumberOpt.Defaults to a sensible per-product cost if left blank52

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save settingsSaves both switches and all cost pricessaveSettings() + audit "settings updated"Super Admin
Reset all pricing dataDeletes ALL pricing, history, overrides, scheduled changes and settingsClears every pricing store key; asks for confirmation first; writes an audit lineSuper Admin
⛔
Caution — Reset all pricing data is irreversible

Reset all pricing data wipes every negotiated price, the whole change history, all order overrides and every scheduled change in this workspace. There is no undo. Only use it to clear demo/test data on a fresh setup, and export a CSV backup first.

Under the hood — the pricing rule in the database

What a saved price looks like on the server, and the validation it must pass.

Purpose Explain the real backend fields so you can read the database and API responses confidently.
Access Super Admin · Admin (API) API /api/b2b/pricing · /api/b2b/pricing/[id] · /api/b2b/pricing/reports · /api/b2b/pricing/products

Each negotiated price is one BusinessPricing row with an auto code like B2BP-000001. Money is stored as integer paise and GST as basis points (bps): 500 bps = 5%. The system derives the discount and the GST-inclusive price from the base and B2B prices.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
businessIdWhich business the rule belongs toExisting business idYesBusiness must exist and be activecuid…
productSlugWhich productProduct slugYes—milk
productNameDisplay name saved with the ruleTextYes—A2 Buffalo Milk
variantLabelOptional variant this rule targetsText up to 40 charsOpt.Blank = applies to the base product500 ml
unitThe selling unit the price is perA unit for the productYes—Litres
basePricePaiseThe retail/base price to discount fromWhole paise > 0YesMust be a positive whole number6600 (₹66)
b2bPricePaiseThe negotiated B2B priceWhole paise > 0YesMust be > 0 and ≤ base price (discount can't be negative)6400 (₹64)
gstBpsGST rate in basis points0 – 10000 (0%–100%)Opt.Defaults to 0; used for the GST-inclusive price500 (5%)
minQtyMinimum quantity this rule applies to (slab)Whole number ≥ 1Opt.Defaults to 1; part of the duplicate-prevention key101
effectiveFromWhen the price startsDateOpt.Defaults to now2026-08-01
effectiveUntilWhen the price stopsDate or blankOpt.Blank = open-ended2026-12-31
⚠️
Warning — no duplicate rules

The server refuses a second active rule for the same business + product + variant + minimum quantity and returns the existing rule's code (for example "A pricing rule already exists … B2BP-000004. Edit it instead."). To change that price, edit the existing rule rather than creating a new one.

Workflow — API lifecycle of a pricing rule
POST create (B2BP code)GET list / detail (+ history)PATCH update (logs old→new)PATCH enable / disablePATCH delete (soft) / restore
Figure — create → read → update → enable/disable → soft-delete/restore, each logged.
EndpointMethodDoesPermission
/api/b2b/pricingGETList rules with filters (businessId, productSlug, active, q, priceFrom/To, sort, limit, offset, includeDeleted)Admin / Super Admin
/api/b2b/pricingPOSTCreate a rule (auto B2BP code)Admin / Super Admin
/api/b2b/pricing/[id]GETOne rule plus its change historyAdmin / Super Admin
/api/b2b/pricing/[id]PATCHaction = update · delete · restore · enable · disableAdmin / Super Admin
/api/b2b/pricing/reportsGETCoverage, average discount, by-business and by-product breakdownsAdmin / Super Admin
/api/b2b/pricing/productsGETThe product lookup (slug, name, units, base price)Admin / Super Admin

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common issues and quick answers.

Purpose Resolve the most frequent B2B Pricing questions without escalating.
Access Admin+Path Admin → Commerce → B2B Pricing
SymptomLikely causeFix
All rows show "View only", no Edit/Slabs buttonsYou are signed in as Admin (or lower), not Super AdminPricing changes are Super-Admin only. Ask a Super Admin, or switch role if you hold that account.
The whole module shows "Access restricted"Your role lacks the b2bPricing view permissionAsk a Super Admin to grant view access, or use an Admin/Super Admin account.
A slab price isn't being applied at order timeSlab pricing is turned off in Settings, or the quantity falls outside every slab bandEnable "quantity-slab pricing" in Settings and check the min/max bands cover the order quantity.
A saved custom price is showing as retail on the orderThe product is disabled, the business's pricing is deactivated, or an effective-from date hasn't started / effective-until has passedRe-enable the product, Activate pricing for the business, or adjust the effective dates.
Server rejects a new price with a "already exists" messageA rule already covers that business + product + variant + quantityEdit the existing rule (its code is in the message) instead of creating a new one.
New price won't save — "B2B price cannot exceed the base price"You entered a B2B price higher than the base/retail priceEnter a B2B price at or below the base price; the discount can't be negative on the server.
Prices look wrong right after loadingThe live banner hasn't finished hydrating from the database yetWait for the green "● Live — B2B pricing from the DOODLY database" banner, then refresh the tab.

Does changing a business price affect orders already placed?

No. A price change only affects new orders from that point. Past orders keep the price they were charged, and any order-time override is logged separately in Order Overrides.

What happens if a business has no custom price for a product?

The order simply uses the normal retail price. You don't have to price every product — only the ones you've negotiated.

Why are there two prices for one product (₹78 and ₹77)?

Those are quantity slabs. Small orders pay the higher rate; larger orders automatically drop to the lower slab. The engine picks the slab that matches the order quantity, as long as slab pricing is enabled in Settings.

Is GST included in the price I type?

The price you type is the pre-GST unit price. The retail column shows the product's GST %, and the database also stores a GST-inclusive figure derived from the GST rate on the rule.

✅
Best Practice — set up a new B2B customer's pricing fast

Register the business first under B2B Orders → Register. Then, if a similar customer already exists, use Copy / Import / Export → Copy pricing to clone their price map, and tweak the few products that differ. Finish by opening Customer preview to confirm the customer sees exactly the rates you intended.

Module chapter

Admin · Catalogue

5 screen groups documented from the live application.

DOODLYAdmin · Catalogue · Products
§Admin · Catalogue

Products

The catalogue control room — the one place where you create products, price them, stock their variants, write their storefront copy, and flip a single status to launch or pull a product live, with no code change and no redeploy.

ℹ️
Where this screen lives and what it is

This is the staff-facing Products screen at route /admin/products.html (manifest key admin/products). It is built from three blocks: a page head (title "Products", sub-line "Manage the catalogue. Flip status to launch a product.", with two header buttons — Recommendations and Add product), a green notice banner about zero-code launch, and the product table (block type productAdmin). When the page opens it pulls the live catalogue from the DOODLY database with GET /api/admin/products?pageSize=200&sort=created and re-draws the table.

ℹ️
DOODLY sells one product today — but the catalogue is fully multi-product

The live storefront currently sells A2 Buffalo Milk; Curd, Paneer, Palkova and Ghee are staged as "Coming soon". This screen is not milk-only — it is a complete Product Information Management (PIM) surface that can hold any number of products, each with its own variants, pricing, nutrition, quality, images and SEO. The whole point of the module is that you launch each new dairy line with a status flip here rather than a developer deploy.

⚠️
Two save paths — know which button writes where

This screen has two different persistence routes, and it matters which control you use:

• The Add product button and the Recommendations manager write straight to the DOODLY database (real API calls — POST /api/admin/products and PATCH /api/admin/products/[id]). Those changes are permanent, audited and reach every user.

• The per-row Edit button opens the rich 12-tab Product CMS editor. In this build that editor saves your changes to the browser (local overrides via DOODLY_CMS) and overlays them onto the catalogue on the next page load; the very same 12 tabs map one-to-one to the backend's PATCH /api/admin/products/[id] actions (update, pricing, seo, nutrition, quality, status, feature, variant and image operations) that persist to the database in production. When in doubt, use Add product for a brand-new product and the header/Recommendations flows for status and merchandising.

ℹ️
Live vs. demo data — read the banner

Just above the table a status banner appears after load. Green "● Live — N product(s) from the DOODLY database (…)" means you are looking at the real catalogue. If the backend is unreachable you'll see "⚠ Backend offline … — couldn't load live catalogue."; if your role can't view products you'll see "⚠ Your role can't view products (403)."; any other error shows as "⚠ <message>". Confirm the green banner before trusting what the table shows.

Products — the catalogue table

Every product in the catalogue, its variant count, price range and live status, with an Edit and a storefront-View action per row.

Purpose Give staff a single, at-a-glance list of the whole catalogue — what products exist, how many size/plan variants each has, the price they sell at, and their current storefront status — and a jumping-off point to edit any product or to preview it exactly as a shopper sees it.
Access Super Admin (full) · Admin (full). The backend enforces the products : view permission on every request; no other role has the Products module, so Manager, Accountant, Delivery Executive, Customer and every operational role are denied with a 403. Path Admin → Catalogue → Products
Header — Products · "Manage the catalogue. Flip status to launch a product." · [Recommendations] [Add product]
Page headTitle "Products" and the sub-line, plus two buttons: Recommendations (ghost, award icon) opens the related-products manager; Add product (primary, + icon) opens the new-product form.
Zero-code launch noticeA green info banner: "Zero-code launch: set a product's status to AVAILABLE and it becomes orderable on the storefront instantly."
Live bannerA one-line status strip above the table — green "● Live — N product(s)…", or an amber/red warning if the backend is offline or the role lacks permission.
Product tableSix columns — Product, Variants, Price, Status, Edit, View — one row per product, newest-created first.
Status badgesThe Status column shows a colour-coded pill (Available / Coming Soon / Low Stock / Out of Stock / Discontinued / Hidden) computed by the shared status engine.
Row actionsEach row has an Edit button (opens the Product CMS slide-over) and a View eye-icon link that opens the product's public page /products/{id}.html in the storefront.
Figure — labelled layout of the Admin Products screen.

Table columns

The productAdmin table has a fixed six-column layout. Each cell is derived on the client from the catalogue rows loaded from the backend.

ColumnWhat it showsHow it is builtExample
ProductA thumbnail (the product image, or a 🥛 emoji fallback) next to the product name, with the category shown as a small grey sub-line beneath the name.image / emoji + name + category🥛 A2 Buffalo Milk · Milk
VariantsThe number of size/plan variants attached to the product; a dash when it has none.count of variants where productId matches, else "—"3
PriceThe price range across the product's variants — the lowest to the highest daily price — or a single dash when no priced variant exists.min–max of variant dailyPrice (₹), else "—"₹70–₹75
StatusA colour-coded status pill (see the status table below), computed from the product's status plus its stock, launch date and variant states.DOODLY_STATUS.badgeFor(product)Available (green)
EditAn "Edit" button that opens the 12-tab Product CMS slide-over for that product.data-edit="{id}" → opens editor[ ✎ Edit ]
ViewAn eye-icon link that opens the product's live storefront page in a normal browser navigation, so you can preview exactly what a shopper sees.<a href="/products/{id}.html">👁
ℹ️
The Price column is a variant range, not a single sticker price

Milk is sold per-day per-variant (e.g. a 500 ml daily plan and a 1000 ml daily plan). The Price cell therefore shows the span of those daily prices (lowest–highest), not one number. A product whose variants aren't priced yet — or a "coming soon" product with no active variants — shows "—". To set exact prices, open Edit → Pricing (product-level MRP/selling) and Edit → Variants (per-variant daily/fixed price).

Status values — what each pill means

The Status pill is produced by the shared status engine (DOODLY_STATUS), which resolves the product's stored status together with its stock, launch date and variant states into one of these badges. The orderable column is the practical bottom line: can a customer buy it right now?

BadgeColourStored statusOrderable?Meaning on the storefront
AvailableGreenAVAILABLEYesLive and buyable. Customers can add it to cart and check out.
Low StockAmberAVAILABLE + stock at/under thresholdYesStill buyable, but stock has fallen to the low-stock threshold — a nudge to restock.
Out of StockRedOUT_OF_STOCK (or stock ≤ 0)NoShown but not buyable; the storefront offers a "Notify me" action instead of Add to cart.
Coming SoonGoldCOMING_SOON (or a future launch date)NoTeased on the storefront with a "Notify me" capture; not yet purchasable. The default status for a newly created product.
DiscontinuedGreyDISCONTINUEDNoRetired product. This status cascades to its variants — none are orderable.
HiddenGreyHIDDENNoKept in the catalogue but not listed on the storefront at all.
ℹ️
Two more states you won't set from the pill

DRAFT — a work-in-progress product that isn't shown publicly; used while you build a product before launch. ARCHIVED — the state a product enters when it is soft-deleted (it is hidden and de-listed but never hard-deleted, so history and reports stay intact). Restoring an archived product returns it to DRAFT, not straight to live, so you re-check it before re-launch.

The "flip a status to launch" workflow

This is the headline capability the notice banner promises. Because the storefront reads product status live from the catalogue, changing a product's status is all it takes to launch it (or pull it) — no code change, no deploy.

Workflow — launch a product with a status flip
Create the product (starts COMING_SOON)Fill in pricing, variants, images, copySet status → AVAILABLEStorefront shows it as buyable instantlyCustomers can order it
Figure — from draft to live in one status change; setting AVAILABLE also forces the product Visible.
⚠️
Setting AVAILABLE also makes the product Visible

When a product's status is set to AVAILABLE, the backend automatically forces its Visible flag on, so a freshly launched product is guaranteed to appear on the storefront. The reverse is also true for bulk operations: deactivating hides the product and clears Visible. If a product is "Available" but still not showing to customers, check that it has at least one active, priced, in-stock variant.

How the list loads

When the Products route mounts, the front end calls GET /api/admin/products?pageSize=200&sort=created, maps each returned product into a catalogue row (id = slug, plus name, status, visibility, category, image, featured flag, stock and a selling/MRP-derived price), replaces the in-memory catalogue, re-renders the productAdmin table and shows the green Live banner with the product count and the API base URL. Every mutation on the backend appends a ProductEvent to that product's history, so who changed what (and when) is always recorded.

✅
Best Practice

Treat status as a deliberate release gate. Build a new product fully in DRAFT or COMING_SOON — set its pricing, add and price every variant, upload images, write the description and complete SEO — and only flip it to AVAILABLE once you've clicked the View eye-icon and confirmed the storefront page looks right. Launching is a one-click, instant, customer-facing action, so preview first.

Add product — new-product form

A short modal that creates a brand-new product straight in the DOODLY database.

Purpose Register a new product in the catalogue with the essentials — name, slug, launch status, a short description and optional headline pricing — so it exists as a record you can then flesh out (variants, images, nutrition, SEO) via the Edit editor.
Access Super Admin · Admin. Creating a product requires the products : create permission; the backend rejects anyone else with a 403. Path Admin → Catalogue → Products → Add product
Modal — Add product
Product name *Free-text name. Typing it auto-fills the Slug beneath.
Slug * & StatusSlug (URL id, auto-suggested from the name) beside a Status dropdown (Coming soon default).
Short description *One-line storefront blurb.
MRP ₹ & Selling ₹Optional headline pricing pair.
GST % & FeaturedOptional tax rate and a Yes/No featured flag.
Footer[Cancel] and [Add product]. An inline error line shows validation problems.
Figure — the Add product modal.

Fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Product nameThe display name shown everywhere in the catalogue and storefront.Text, 2–80 characters.YesClient requires ≥ 2 chars; backend enforces 2–80 and rejects a duplicate name (case-insensitive) with a conflict error.A2 Buffalo Milk
SlugThe stable URL id — becomes the product's storefront path /products/{slug}.html and its catalogue key.Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens only.YesAuto-generated from the name (spaces → hyphens, lower-cased) until you edit it. Backend regex ^[a-z0-9-]+$, 2–50 chars; a duplicate slug is rejected as a conflict.a2-buffalo-milk
StatusThe launch state the product starts in.Coming soon · Draft · Available · Out of stock · Hidden.Opt.Defaults to COMING_SOON. Choosing Available here launches the product immediately on save.Coming soon
Short descriptionThe one-line blurb shown on cards and the product page.Text, up to 300 characters.YesClient requires ≥ 1 char; backend enforces 1–300.Fresh A2 buffalo milk, delivered daily.
MRP ₹The maximum retail (strike-through) price.Number (rupees).Opt.Only sent if a Selling price is given; converted to paise. Falls back to the Selling price if left blank.80
Selling ₹The actual price the product sells at.Number (rupees).Opt.If > 0, a pricing record is created (MRP/selling/tax as paise). If blank, the product is created with no pricing yet — add it later under Edit → Pricing.70
GST %The tax rate applied to the product.Number (percent).Opt.Stored as basis points (e.g. 5% → 500 bps). Only sent alongside a Selling price.0
FeaturedWhether to flag the product as featured/merchandised.No / Yes.Opt.Defaults to No. Can also be toggled later from the Recommendations manager.No

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Add productValidates the form, creates the product, reloads the table with a live banner and shows a toast "Product <name> saved to the database".POST /api/admin/products with {slug, name, description, status, featured, pricing?}. On success the product is created (default visible = true, featured = false) and a CREATED ProductEvent is logged.Super Admin Admin
Cancel / × / EscCloses the modal without saving.None.—
Workflow — create a product
Click Add productType name → slug auto-fillsPick status & write the blurb(Optional) enter MRP / Selling / GSTAdd product → POST to DBTable reloads with the new rowOpen Edit to add variants, images, copy
Figure — the Add product flow. The modal captures the essentials; the full detail is completed in the Edit editor.
💡
Tip — create it as Coming soon, launch it later

Leaving Status on the default Coming soon lets you create the record now, then complete pricing, variants and images calmly under Edit. When everything is ready, flip the status to Available to go live. If you pick Available in this modal, it launches the moment you click Add product — even before you've added a single variant.

Edit product — the Product CMS slide-over

A tabbed editor that exposes every product attribute across 12 tabs, from basics and pricing to nutrition, quality, images and SEO.

Purpose Edit the full detail of an existing product — its identity, price, size/plan variants, subscription plans, nutrition, quality parameters, storefront copy, trust badges, image gallery, availability rules, SEO metadata and read-only performance analytics — in one schema-driven panel, so a merchandiser can manage a product end-to-end without touching code.
Access Super Admin · Admin. Editing requires the products : edit permission on the backend; the corresponding PATCH /api/admin/products/[id] actions reject other roles with a 403. Path Admin → Catalogue → Products → Edit
Slide-over — Product CMS · <product name>
Header"Product CMS" label, the product name, and a × close.
Tab strip12 tabs: Basic · Pricing · Variants · Subscriptions · Nutrition · Quality · Description · Badges · Images · Availability · SEO · Analytics.
Tab bodyThe fields for the active tab — forms, editable tables, badge/image lists or read-only KPI cards.
Footer[Reset all CMS edits] on the left; [Cancel] and [Save changes] on the right.
Figure — the Product CMS slide-over with its 12 tabs.
ℹ️
One Save writes the whole panel

You move freely between tabs; nothing saves until you click Save changes. Save then reads every field across all tabs at once — scalar fields (product / variant / plan), the rebuilt badge list, and the re-ordered image gallery with its featured pick — persists them, re-renders the table and shows the toast "Saved — live across the storefront". In this build those changes are stored as local catalogue overrides; the same fields map to the backend's per-tab PATCH actions in production.

Tab 1 — Basic

The product's core identity and listing controls.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Product nameThe display name.Text (≤ 80).YesMaps to the update action's name.A2 Buffalo Milk
SlugThe URL id / catalogue key.Lowercase, hyphenated.Opt.Identifies the storefront page; change with care as it moves the public URL.milk
StatusThe launch state.Active · Draft · Coming Soon · Out of Stock · Discontinued.Opt.Maps to the status action; setting Active (AVAILABLE) also forces Visible on.Active
Visible on siteWhether the product is listed on the storefront ("Listed in storefront").Checkbox.Opt.Independent of status — a product can be Available but hidden, or shown but not orderable.☑
CategoryThe product's category label.Text.Opt.Free-text here; the ERP layer can also bind a formal category id.Milk
Display orderSort position among products.Number.Opt.Lower shows first (sortOrder, 0–9999).0
From (label)The "from ₹…" style price/eyebrow label shown on cards.Text.Opt.Presentational; used on storefront product cards.From ₹70/day
Rating valueThe star rating shown for the product.Number (e.g. 0–5, one decimal).Opt.Display value; the live customer rating comes from verified reviews.4.8
Rating countHow many ratings back the value.Number.Opt.Display value.240

Tab 2 — Pricing

Product-level commercial fields. In production each maps to the backend's pricing action and is stored in paise / basis points; the rupee and percent inputs here are converted on save.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Backend fieldExample
MRP (₹)Maximum retail (strike-through) price.Number (rupees).Opt.mrpPaise80
Selling (₹)Actual selling price.Number (rupees).Opt.sellingPaise70
Cost (₹)Your cost price (for margin).Number (rupees).Opt.costPaise52
Offer (₹)A promotional price.Number (rupees).Opt.offerPaise65
Discount %Percentage discount.Number (0–100).Opt.discountBps (0–10000)10
Tax %GST rate for the product.Number (0–100).Opt.taxBps (0–10000)0
Glass deposit (₹)The refundable glass-bottle deposit.Number (rupees).Opt.depositPaise120
Delivery charge (₹)Per-order delivery fee.Number (rupees).Opt.deliveryPaise0
Free delivery over (₹)Basket threshold above which delivery is free.Number (rupees).Opt.Presentational threshold.500
⚠️
Product price and variant price are different things

The Pricing tab sets the product-level headline price and commercial fields. What a customer is actually charged for a daily milk plan comes from the variant price (Variants tab) resolved server-side at checkout, with the config catalogue as a safety net. The Price column on the list shows the variant range for that reason. Set both: product Pricing for display/margin, and each variant's daily/fixed price for the real charge.

Tab 3 — Variants

An editable table of the product's size/plan variants (e.g. 500 ml, 1000 ml, trial pack). Each row is a variant.

ColumnPurposeAccepted valuesBackend
Display nameThe shopper-facing variant name.Text.displayName (falls back to label)
SKUStock-keeping code — must be unique.Text (≤ 40).sku; a duplicate SKU is rejected as a conflict
PriceThe variant's price — a daily price for subscription variants ("/day") or a fixed price for a trial variant ("fixed").Number.dailyPaise or fixedPaise by variant type
StockUnits on hand for this variant.Number (≥ 0).stock; a change logs a STOCK event
WeightPack weight/size label.Text.weightG / weight label
ActiveWhether the variant is orderable.Checkbox.active
ℹ️
A variant in use can't be deleted — deactivate it

The backend refuses to hard-delete a variant that is referenced by any subscription ("This variant is used by subscriptions and cannot be deleted; deactivate it instead."). To retire such a variant, untick Active. New variants default to Active with a low-stock threshold of 20 units.

Tab 4 — Subscriptions (plans)

An editable table of the recurring plans that apply across the catalogue (the delivery-cadence + discount tiers customers subscribe to).

ColumnPurposeAccepted values
PlanPlan name.Text.
DaysThe plan length / cadence in days.Number.
DiscountThe plan discount, entered as a percent (stored as a fraction / basis points).Number (%).
BadgeAn optional tag shown on the plan (e.g. "Popular").Text.
Auto-renewWhether the plan renews automatically.Checkbox.
ActiveWhether the plan is offered.Checkbox.

Tab 5 — Nutrition

The nutrition panel shown on the product page. All fields are free-text so you can include units.

FieldExampleFieldExample
Fat6.5 gSugar4.8 g
SNF9.0%Minerals0.7 g
Protein3.4 gVitaminsA, D
Calcium120 mgEnergy90 kcal
Carbohydrates5 g

Persisted via the backend nutrition action (fat, snf, protein, calcium, energy, carbs, sugar are stored on the product's Nutritional Information record).

Tab 6 — Quality

The A2-milk quality parameters shown as trust signals on the product page.

FieldExampleFieldExample
Fat %6.5Batch no.PB-20260712-0007
SNF9.0Milk typeA2
Lactometer28Animal typeBuffalo
Collection temp4°CCollection date2026-07-12
Storage temp4°CExpiry2026-07-14

Persisted via the backend quality action (fatPct, snf, lactometer, storageTemp, milkType, animalType, expiry live on the product's Quality Parameters record).

Tab 7 — Description

All the storefront prose for the product.

FieldPurposeBackend field
Short descriptionThe one-line card/summary blurb.description
Long descriptionThe full product-page body copy.longDesc
Product storyThe brand/origin narrative.story
Usage instructionsHow to use the product.usage
Storage instructionsHow to store it.storage
IngredientsIngredient list.ingredients
Allergen infoAllergen declaration.allergens

Tab 8 — Badges

The trust badges shown on the storefront (e.g. "Farm fresh", "A2 certified", "Glass bottle"). Each badge row has its icon, an editable label and a Show checkbox. On save the whole badge list is rebuilt from the rows, so you can rename or toggle any badge on/off.

Tab 9 — Images

The product image gallery. Each image row shows a thumbnail and filename, a Featured star (radio — the featured image is the one used on cards and as the main image), and ↑ / ↓ buttons to reorder. On save the gallery order and the featured pick are stored. In production these map to the backend image actions (add-image, delete-image, reorder-images, set-featured-image).

ℹ️
Images are added by URL

The backend stores images by URL (binary file upload needs a storage provider, which is a future step). The first image added becomes the featured image automatically; deleting the featured image promotes the next one in order.

Tab 10 — Availability

FieldPurposeAccepted values
Low-stock threshold (units)The level at/under which the product shows "Low Stock".Number.
Launch date (YYYY-MM-DD)A future launch date — the product reads as "Coming Soon" until then.Date.
Restock date (YYYY-MM-DD)Expected restock date for an out-of-stock product.Date.
Available citiesCities where the product is offered.Comma-separated list.
Delivery slotsDelivery windows for the product.Comma-separated list.
End dateWhen availability ends.Date.
Inventory statusA free-text inventory state label.Text.

Tab 11 — SEO

FieldPurposeBackend field
Meta titleThe browser/search title for the product page.metaTitle
Meta descriptionThe search-snippet description.metaDescription
OG imageThe social-share preview image URL.ogImageUrl
Canonical URLThe canonical page URL.canonicalUrl
KeywordsSEO keywords.keywords (comma-separated list)

Tab 12 — Analytics (read-only)

A grid of KPI cards for the product — Total orders, Revenue, Views, Conversion, Stock remaining and Rating. This tab is read-only; the note states it is "populated from orders/payments in production", so it reflects real performance rather than anything you type.

Editor buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save changesReads every field across all 12 tabs, persists them, re-renders the product table and toasts "Saved — live across the storefront".In production maps to the per-tab PATCH /api/admin/products/[id] actions (update, pricing, seo, nutrition, quality, status, feature, add/update/delete-variant, add/delete/reorder/set-featured image); each mutation logs a ProductEvent.Super Admin Admin
Cancel / × / scrim / EscCloses the editor without saving.None.—
Reset all CMS editsClears all local catalogue overrides and reloads the page, returning to the database catalogue.Clears the DOODLY_CMS override store, then reloads.Super Admin Admin
⛔
Caution — "Reset all CMS edits" wipes every local override, not just this product

The Reset button clears the entire local CMS override store and reloads. Any unsaved local edits across all products in this browser are discarded. It does not undo changes already written to the database (Add product, Recommendations, or production PATCH saves). Use it to start clean, not to revert one field.

Recommendations — related-products manager

Controls the storefront "You may also like" rail and each product's merchandising flags, straight against the database.

Purpose Turn the cross-sell rail on or off site-wide, set each product's merchandising flags (Featured / Best seller / New / Recommended), change its status inline, and hand-pick the ordered list of related products shown under "You may also like" on each product page.
Access Super Admin · Admin. Opening the manager needs products : view; each change writes with products : edit. Other roles get "Only Admin can manage this (403)". Path Admin → Catalogue → Products → Recommendations
Modal — Related-products recommendations
Global toggleA single checkbox: "Show the 'You may also like' rail on product pages" — enables/disables the whole rail.
Product rowsOne block per product: its name and category.
Status dropdownPer product — Available / Coming soon / Out of stock / Draft, changed inline.
Flag chipsFour toggle chips per product: Featured · Best seller · New · Recommended.
Related products inputA comma-separated, ordered list of related product slugs.
FooterA "✓ Saved" flash line and a [Done] button. Every change saves immediately as you make it.
Figure — the Recommendations manager. Changes autosave per control.

Controls

ControlWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Show the rail (global toggle)Turns the "You may also like" rail on or off across all product pages.POST /api/admin/products/recommendations with {enabled}; audited.Super Admin Admin
Status dropdownChanges a product's launch status inline (Available / Coming soon / Out of stock / Draft).PATCH /api/admin/products/[id] action status.Super Admin Admin
Featured / Best seller / New / Recommended chipsToggles the product's merchandising flags (they light up when on).Action reco-flag with {flag, value} (Featured routes through the feature toggle).Super Admin Admin
Related products inputSets the ordered list of related product slugs shown in the rail for that product.Action set-related with {slugs} (up to 12, lower-cased, order preserved).Super Admin Admin
Done / × / scrim / EscCloses the manager. (Changes have already saved as you made them.)None.—
ℹ️
Related products are picked by slug, in order

Type the slugs of the products you want in the rail, comma-separated, in the order you want them to appear — e.g. curd, paneer, ghee. The list is capped at 12 and lower-cased on save. Get a slug from the product's row in the main table or its Basic tab. The rail only shows if the global toggle is on.

✅
Best Practice

Curate related products deliberately — pair complementary dairy lines (milk → curd, paneer, ghee) rather than listing everything. Keep the rail short (3–4 items) and lead with your highest-margin or newest line. Use the New flag on a just-launched product and the Best seller flag on your hero SKU so shoppers get honest, useful signals.

Behind the screen — the Products ERP/PIM API

Capabilities the backend exposes that this screen only partly surfaces.

The Products screen is the friendly front for a full Product Information Management backend. A Super Admin should know these API-level capabilities exist, because reports, bulk operations and power lookups run through them even though the static screen shows a simpler table:

CapabilityEndpointWhat it doesPermission
Rich listGET /api/admin/productsFilter by status, visibility, featured, stock state, category, variant type and date range; free-text search across name/slug/id/category/SKU; sort by created / name / updated / price / stock; paginate.products : view
KPIsGET /api/admin/products/statsTotals for active, inactive, draft, coming-soon, featured, out-of-stock and low-stock products, plus total stock value.products : view
Detail + historyGET /api/admin/products/[id]Full product detail with variants, pricing, images, SEO, nutrition, quality, derived inventory and the last 40 change events.products : view
Bulk actionsPOST /api/admin/products/bulkActivate, deactivate, soft-delete, re-categorise, bulk price or bulk stock (set or increment) up to 500 products at once.products : edit / delete
ReportsGET /api/admin/products/reportsCounts by status and category, low-stock and out-of-stock lists, stock value, and a top-20 sales performance table (units + revenue from paid orders).products : view
Soft delete / restoreDELETE /api/admin/products/[id]Archives a product (never hard-deletes); restore returns it to Draft.products : delete
Public catalogueGET /api/catalogueThe DB-authoritative price/availability feed the storefront overlays — this is how an admin price edit reaches shoppers with no deploy.Public
ℹ️
Every change is recorded

Each mutation appends a ProductEvent (CREATED, UPDATED, STATUS, PRICE, STOCK, VARIANT, IMAGE, SEO, FEATURE, DELETED, RESTORED) capturing the actor's role and IP, and header-level actions also write to the platform audit trail. Nothing in this module is silently changed.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common Products-screen questions a Super Admin will field.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Table empty with "⚠ Your role can't view products (403)."The signed-in role lacks products : view. Only Super Admin and Admin have the Products module.Sign in as Super Admin/Admin, or grant the role the Products module under Admin → System → Roles & Permissions.
Table empty with "⚠ Backend offline — couldn't load live catalogue."The backend API was unreachable at load.Confirm the backend is running and reload. The screen shows an honest empty state rather than stale demo rows.
Add product fails with "A product with that slug/name already exists."The slug or name duplicates an existing (or soft-deleted) product.Choose a distinct slug/name. Slugs must be unique; names are checked case-insensitively among non-deleted products.
Set a product to Available but it still isn't buyable on the storefront.The product has no active, priced, in-stock variant.Open Edit → Variants: add at least one Active variant with a price and stock. Status alone doesn't create sellable inventory.
Price column shows "—".The product has no variant with a daily price yet.Add/price variants under Edit → Variants (the list column ranges over variant daily prices).
Can't delete a variant ("used by subscriptions").A live subscription references that variant.Untick Active to retire it instead of deleting.
An edit in the Edit modal didn't reach the live storefront.The Edit CMS editor saves local overrides in this build; header Add product / Recommendations write to the DB directly.For DB-persisted changes to status/merchandising use the header/Recommendations flows (or the production PATCH actions); reserve the local editor for previewing copy/pricing.

What's the fastest way to launch a new product end-to-end?

Click Add product, enter name + slug + a short description, leave status on Coming soon, and save. Then open Edit to set Pricing, add and price Variants, upload Images, and write the Description/SEO. Click the View eye-icon to preview the storefront page. When it's right, set the status to Available to go live.

What's the difference between Status and Visible?

Status is the launch state (Available, Coming Soon, Out of Stock, etc.) and drives whether the product is orderable. Visible is simply whether it is listed on the storefront. Setting Status to Available automatically turns Visible on, but you can independently hide an Available product or show a not-yet-orderable one.

Do products ever get permanently deleted?

No. Deleting a product soft-deletes it — it becomes ARCHIVED, hidden and de-listed, but the record and its history are kept so orders, invoices and reports stay accurate. Restoring returns it to Draft for re-checking before re-launch.

Who can see and change the catalogue?

Only Super Admin and Admin. The Products module isn't granted to any operational role in the default permission matrix, so Support, Operations, Accountant, Delivery Executive, Customer and the rest cannot view or edit products.

How do admin price edits reach shoppers without a deploy?

The storefront reads DB-authoritative commercial fields from GET /api/catalogue and overlays them onto its static presentational data by matching ids. So when you change a variant's price or a product's availability here, the next storefront load reflects it — and the same DB price is what the customer is charged at checkout.

DOODLYAdmin · Catalogue · Categories
§Admin · Catalogue

Categories

The master list of product groups — Milk, Curd, Paneer, Ghee, Sweets, Bundles — that organises the whole catalogue; every product hangs off a category, the storefront uses them to filter and browse, and this screen is where a Super Admin creates and manages them, live in the DOODLY database.

ℹ️
What a category is, and why it matters

A category is a normalised grouping row in the Category table (id, slug, name, description, image, sort order, active flag). Each product points at one category through its categoryId, so categories are the backbone of catalogue navigation, product filtering and reporting. The screen you see at Admin → Catalogue → Categories is a live card board: when it loads it fetches the real categories from the database and redraws itself, so the tiles you read are the true, current catalogue — never demo data. A green "● Live" banner confirms it.

ℹ️
Categories vs. Products — which screen does what

Use Categories to create and name the groups (e.g. "Dairy products"). Use the neighbouring Products screen to create the actual sellable items and assign each one to a category. The product count you see on a category tile ("4 products") is simply how many products are currently linked to that category. Deleting a category is blocked while any product is still linked to it, so the two screens stay in sync.

Category Board

A live grid of tiles — one per category — showing its icon, name, product count and whether it is active or hidden, drawn fresh from the database on every load.

Purpose Give staff a one-glance map of the catalogue's structure: which groups exist, how many products sit in each, and which are live to customers versus hidden.
Access Super Admin Admin — the page is gated to anyone with categories:view Path Admin → Catalogue → Categories
Categories — Board · header action "Add category" · live banner · card grid (reads GET /api/categories)
Page headerTitle "Categories" · subtitle "Organise products into categories." · primary button + Add category
Live bannerGreen strip: "● Live — N categories from the DOODLY database (…backend URL…)"
Category grid (3 columns)One tile per category: icon · name · "N products · active/hidden"
Figure — the Categories board: header action, live banner and the auto-generated tile grid.

On load the screen calls GET /api/categories, receives the real rows (ordered by sort order, then name), and rebuilds the tile grid to match. Each tile is display-only (there are no per-tile edit or delete buttons on this screen); all creation happens through the Add category button in the header. If the backend can't be reached or your role lacks access, the banner turns red with the reason instead (see Troubleshooting).

What each tile shows

Tile elementShowsBackend fieldHow it is built
IconA small glyph chosen from the category's slugderived from slugmilk → drop; curd / paneer / kova / ghee / dairy → box; sweets / bundles → gift; anything else → a generic tag icon
NameThe category's display namenameShown verbatim as the tile heading
Product count & statuse.g. "4 products · active" or "1 product · hidden"_count.products, activeCount is pluralised ("1 product" vs "2 products"); the word after the dot is active when the category is live, hidden when it is not

Board data & behaviour

AspectBehaviour
Data sourceLive from GET /api/categories — the same endpoint the storefront reads; no local or demo data.
OrderingBy sort order ascending, then by name — so lower "Display order" numbers appear first.
What staff seeStaff with categories:view (Super Admin, Admin) see every category, including hidden ones. (The public storefront, calling the same endpoint without that permission, only receives active categories.)
Filters / search / sort / paginationNone on this board — it is a compact card grid, not a paginated data table. The catalogue is small enough to show every group at once.
Row / tile actionsTiles are static (view only). The only action on the screen is the header Add category button.
Live bannerGreen "● Live — N categor(y/ies) from the DOODLY database (backend URL)" once the fetch succeeds.

Header actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
+ Add categoryOpens the Add category dialog (Screen 2) to create a new categoryOn save → POST /api/categories; on success the board refetches and redrawsSuper AdminAdmin

Add Category

A short modal form — name, slug, display order, description and status — that creates a new category row in the database and instantly refreshes the board.

Purpose Create a new product group so that products can be assigned to it and, if active, so the storefront can browse and filter by it.
Access Super Admin Admin — needs categories:create Path Admin → Catalogue → Categories → Add category
Add category — modal dialog
Header"Add category" title · close (×)
Name fieldCategory name (required)
Slug & order rowSlug (required, auto-filled from the name) · Display order (number)
Description fieldOptional one-line description
Status fieldActive / Hidden dropdown
FooterCancel · Add category (save) · inline error line
Figure — the Add category dialog with its five fields and the save/cancel footer.

As you type the Category name, the Slug auto-fills with a URL-safe version (lowercased, spaces and symbols turned into hyphens). You can override the slug by editing it directly — once you touch it, it stops auto-following the name. Pressing Enter inside any field saves; Esc or clicking outside the dialog cancels.

Fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Category nameThe display name shown on tiles and to customersTextYesTrimmed; must be 2–60 characters. The dialog also blocks save if it is under 2 chars ("Name and slug are required.").Dairy products
SlugThe URL-safe key used in links and to look the category up; must be uniqueLowercase letters, numbers and hyphensYesAuto-generated from the name; trimmed & lowercased; must match ^[a-z0-9-]+$ ("Use lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens") and be 2–40 chars. A duplicate is rejected: "A category with that slug already exists."dairy-products
Display orderControls where the tile sits (lower shows first)Whole numberOpt.Integer 0–9999; defaults to 0 when left blank. Saved as sortOrder.6
DescriptionA short line describing the groupTextOpt.Trimmed; up to 280 characters. Left blank if empty.Curd, paneer, ghee and more…
StatusWhether the category is live to customersActive / HiddenOpt.Select; defaults to Active. "Active" saves active = true (visible to the storefront); "Hidden" saves active = false (staff-only).Active
ℹ️
Category image is set elsewhere

The database supports an optional image on a category (imageUrl), but this create dialog does not ask for one — new categories start without an image and pick up their icon from the slug. An image can be added later through the category-edit API. The slug is set once at creation and is not editable afterwards, so choose it carefully.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Add category (save)Validates the form and creates the categoryPOST /api/categories with { slug, name, description, sortOrder, active } → creates a Category row, writes a category.create audit entry, returns 201. On success a toast confirms "Category Name saved to the database" and the board refreshes.Super AdminAdmin
Cancel / × / EscCloses the dialog without savingNo server callAny viewer
Workflow — Create a category
Click "+ Add category"Type the name (slug auto-fills)Set display order & status(optional) add a descriptionClick "Add category"POST /api/categories → 201Toast + board redraws with the new tile
Figure — end-to-end path from button click to a new live tile.

Category Lifecycle — Edit, Hide & Delete

How a category changes over its life: renaming, re-ordering, hiding from the storefront, and the guarded rules around deleting one.

Purpose Document the full set of operations the categories API supports so staff understand what can be changed after creation and what the safety rails are.
Access Super Admin Admin — edit needs categories:edit, delete needs categories:delete Path Admin → Catalogue → Categories
ℹ️
Editing & deleting run through the API

Today the on-screen board offers view and Add category. Renaming, re-ordering, hiding and deleting a category are backed by the /api/categories/{id} endpoint and are performed by staff with the right permission (typically via the product/catalogue tooling). The most common day-to-day change — hiding a category from customers — is simply setting its status: create or update it with status Hidden, and the storefront stops listing it while staff still see it on the board marked "hidden".

Operations on an existing category

OperationWhat it changesBackend processPermission
Rename / re-describeUpdates the display name (2–60) or description (up to 280)PATCH /api/categories/{id} → updates the row, writes a category.update audit entrySuper AdminAdmin
Re-orderChanges the sortOrder (0–9999) so the tile moves earlier or laterPATCH /api/categories/{id} (audited)Super AdminAdmin
Hide / showFlips active — Hidden pulls it from the storefront, Active restores itPATCH /api/categories/{id} (audited)Super AdminAdmin
Set / change imageAttaches or replaces the category's imageUrlPATCH /api/categories/{id} (audited)Super AdminAdmin
DeletePermanently removes the categoryDELETE /api/categories/{id} → allowed only when no products are linked; otherwise rejected. Writes a category.delete audit entry.Super AdminAdmin
⚠️
The slug can't be changed after creation

Renaming, re-ordering, hiding and image changes are all allowed, but the slug is fixed for life — the update endpoint deliberately does not accept a new slug, because links and product associations depend on it. If you truly need a different slug, create a fresh category with the correct slug, move the products across on the Products screen, then delete the old (now-empty) one.

⛔
Delete is blocked while products are linked

You cannot delete a category that still has products in it — the API rejects it with "Reassign or remove its products before deleting this category." First open the Products screen and move every product to another category (or remove them), so the tile reads "0 products". Only then will the delete succeed. Deletion is permanent; there is no undo, so most teams simply Hide a category they no longer use rather than delete it.

Workflow — Retire a category safely
Open ProductsRe-assign every product to another categoryCategory tile now reads "0 products"Either Hide it (recommended) or Delete itDelete allowed only when empty
Figure — the guarded path to removing a category without orphaning products.

Who can do what

CapabilityPermissionSuper AdminAdminOther roles
Open the Categories board / see hidden categoriescategories:viewYesYesNo
Add a categorycategories:createYesYesNo
Rename / re-order / hide / set imagecategories:editYesYesNo
Delete a categorycategories:deleteYesYesNo
See active categories on the public storefront(public)YesYesEveryone — customers included
ℹ️
Categories is an Admin-and-above module

In the permission matrix only Super Admin and Admin hold the categories module (Super Admin has everything; Admin holds it at "full", i.e. view / create / edit / delete). Marketing, operations, support, procurement, quality, inventory, accountant and delivery roles have no categories access and never reach this screen. The one exception is the read side: the public list endpoint returns active categories to anyone, because the storefront needs them to build its catalogue navigation. Server-side permission checks are the real security boundary — the menu filter is convenience only.

✅
Best Practice — plan the slug and the order before you save

Because the slug is permanent, decide it deliberately: keep it short, lowercase and hyphenated (e.g. dairy-products, not Dairy_Products), and matching how you'd want it to read in a link. Set a sensible Display order in tens (10, 20, 30…) so you can slot new categories between existing ones later without renumbering everything. Create a category as Hidden first, add and check its products on the Products screen, then flip it to Active — that way customers only ever see a category once it actually has products in it.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Board shows "⚠ Backend offline …" and the demo tiles don't updateThe Next.js backend isn't running / can't be reachedStart the backend (next-app) so the storefront page can call the live categories API; reload the page.
"⚠ Your role can't view categories (403)"Signed in as a role without the categories module (e.g. Operations, Support)Use a Super Admin or Admin account — those are the only roles with categories access.
"A category with that slug already exists."The slug you're saving is already taken by another categoryChange the slug to a unique value (slugs must be unique across all categories).
"Name and slug are required."Name or slug is under 2 characters when you press saveGive the category a real name (the slug auto-fills); both must be at least 2 characters.
"Use lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens"The slug contains spaces, capitals or symbolsKeep the slug to a–z, 0–9 and hyphens only — e.g. dairy-products.
"Reassign or remove its products before deleting this category."Products are still linked to the category you tried to deleteOn the Products screen move every product to another category (tile should read "0 products"), then delete.
A new category doesn't appear on the storefrontIt was created as Hidden, or has no products yetSet its status to Active, and make sure at least one available product is assigned to it.
Categories show in the wrong orderTheir Display order values need adjustingLower the sort order of the ones you want first (the board sorts ascending, then by name).

Frequently asked questions

Q. What is the difference between "Hidden" and "Delete"?

Hidden keeps the category and all its product links intact but stops the storefront from listing it — staff still see it on the board marked "hidden", and you can flip it back to Active any time. Delete removes the category permanently and is only allowed once no products are linked. For anything you might reuse, hide it; only delete truly obsolete, empty groups.

Q. Can I change a category's slug later?

No. The slug is set when you create the category and cannot be edited afterwards, because product associations and links rely on it. If you need a different slug, create a new category, re-assign the products to it on the Products screen, and delete the old empty one.

Q. Where does the product count on a tile come from?

It's a live count of how many products are currently linked to that category (their categoryId points at it). Assigning or moving products on the Products screen changes the number the next time this board loads.

Q. Why can customers see categories if it's an admin module?

The management of categories is Admin-only, but the read of active categories is public so the storefront can build its catalogue menu and filters. Hidden categories are never sent to customers — only staff with categories:view receive them.

Q. Do I set the category image here?

Not in the Add category dialog — new categories start without an image and use an icon derived from their slug. A category image (imageUrl) can be attached later through the category-edit endpoint.

Q. Is every change to a category recorded?

Yes. Creating, updating and deleting a category each write an audit entry (category.create / category.update / category.delete) to the platform Audit Logs, so there is always a trail of who changed the catalogue structure and when.

DOODLYAdmin · Catalogue · Inventory
§Admin · Catalogue

Inventory

The single stock ledger for everything DOODLY holds on hand — filled milk bottles ready to sell and the raw materials and supplies that keep the dairy running — showing live on-hand counts, what is reserved for open orders, a colour-coded health status, and a one-click way to correct any count with an audited reason.

ℹ️
One screen, two stock sources

The Inventory view combines two kinds of stock into one table so staff never have to look in two places. (1) Filled-bottle products come from each product Variant — its stock (on hand) and reservedStock (held for open orders). (2) Raw materials and supplies — empty bottles, foil caps, sanitiser and so on — come from InventoryItem rows and their quantity. Each row is tagged internally as a variant or a material so the same "Adjust stock" tool works on both. The overview reads GET /api/admin/inventory/overview; a green banner ("● Live — N inventory item(s) from the DOODLY database") confirms the numbers are live, not the demo seed.

ℹ️
How the status colour is decided

Every row gets a single traffic-light Status computed from its numbers, so you can scan the whole page and instantly see what needs attention. The rule (in statusOf) is applied in order: Available (stock − reserved) is 0 or less → Out of stock (red); else stock is at or below its Reorder at level → Reorder (red); else stock is at or below twice the reorder level → Low (amber); otherwise → Healthy (green). For raw materials, reserved is always 0, so their status is judged on quantity versus reorder level alone.

Inventory Overview

Four live KPIs over a searchable, filterable table of every stock item — filled bottles and supplies together — with each item's SKU, on-hand count, reorder level and health status.

Purpose Give whoever runs stock a one-glance answer to "what do we have, what is running low, and what must be reordered right now?" — across finished product and raw supplies at once.
Access Super Admin Admin Inventory Manager Operations — anyone with inventory:view Path Admin → Catalogue → Inventory
Header — Inventory · "Stock levels and reorder alerts" · Adjust stock button
KPI row1000 ml ready · 500 ml ready · Low SKUs · Reorder now — refilled from the live overview on load
Filter chipsAll · Low · Reorder — narrow the table to items needing attention
DataTable toolbarSearch box · column sort · facet filters · saved views · favourite · export · pagination
Stock tableSKU · Item · Stock · Reorder at · Status — one row per variant and per supply item
Live bannerGreen "● Live — N inventory item(s) from the DOODLY database" (or an error strip)
Figure — the Inventory overview: KPI row, filter chips, and the unified stock table.

The four KPIs and where each number comes from

KPIPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
1000 ml readySellable 1-litre bottles on handCountRead-onlyAvailable (stock − reserved) of the variant whose name contains "1000 ml"; shows 0 if none.640
500 ml readySellable half-litre bottles on handCountRead-onlyAvailable of the variant whose name contains "500 ml".410
Low SKUsHow many items are running low or need reorderCountRead-onlystats.lowStock — count of items with status Low or Reorder.2
Reorder nowItems that must be reordered immediatelyCountRead-onlyCount of items whose status is Reorder or Out of stock.1
