The tiles ship with placeholder numbers (640 / 410 / 2 / 1) so the page is never blank, but they are overwritten from the live overview the instant it loads.

Stock table columns

ColumnShowsBackend field
SKUThe item's stock-keeping code (a variant's sku, or a supply item's sku); shows "—" if a variant has nonesku
ItemProduct name + variant label (e.g. "A2 Buffalo Milk 1000 ml") for bottles, or the supply item's namename (variant: product.name + label; material: name)
StockUnits physically on handstock (variant stock / material quantity, rounded)
Reorder atThe level at which the item is flagged for reorderreorderAt (variant lowStockThreshold / material reorderAt)
StatusColoured pill: Healthy / Low / Reorder / Out of stockstatus — computed by statusOf(stock, reserved, reorderAt)
ℹ️
More is tracked than the five visible columns

Behind each row the overview also carries Available (stock − reserved), Reserved (held for open orders, always 0 for supplies), the item Unit (e.g. "1000 ml", "pcs"), a stock value in paise (variant stock × its daily/fixed price; supplies are valued at 0), and the last updated time. The whole-inventory stats block also totals items, units, available, reserved, low-stock, out-of-stock and inventory value — the KPI row surfaces the four that matter most day to day.

Filters, search & table behaviour

AspectBehaviour
Filter chipsAll (default), Low and Reorder — one click narrows the table to items in that state so you can act on just what needs attention.
SearchFree-text box in the shared DataTable toolbar; matches across the visible cells (SKU, item name, status).
SortingClick any column header to sort — e.g. by Stock to find the lowest counts, or by Status to group the reds together.
Facet filters / saved views / favouriteThe unified DataTable engine adds facet filters, saveable views and a favourite toggle on top of the three chips.
PaginationThe table pages when the item list is long; a static render is shown first so data is never invisible while the engine loads.
ExportThe toolbar's export downloads the current table (SKU, Item, Stock, Reorder at, Status) as a spreadsheet for offline stock-taking.
Row actionsNone inline — all changes go through the Adjust stock button in the header (Screen 2).
ℹ️
If the live data can't load

Instead of the green live banner you may see a red strip: "Backend offline … — couldn't load live stock" if the API is unreachable, "Your role can't view inventory (403)" if you're signed in as a role without inventory:view, or a plain error message otherwise. The table then falls back to the built-in demo seed so the layout still renders.

Adjust Stock

A small pop-up form to correct any item's on-hand count — receive new stock, issue it out, record returns or damage, or set an exact figure — always with a written reason that is saved to the audit trail.

Purpose Keep the on-hand count honest. Whenever the physical shelf and the system disagree — a delivery arrived, bottles broke, a cycle count found a difference — you record the change here so the number is corrected and the reason is logged.
Access Super Admin Admin Inventory Manager — needs the inventory:adjust special (a "full"-level grant). Operations sees the page but is view-only. Path Admin → Catalogue → Inventory → Adjust stock
Adjust stock — modal dialog
ItemDropdown of every inventory item — shows name, SKU and current count, e.g. "Foil caps (CAP-FOIL) · 80 in"
ActionIncrease / receive · Decrease / issue · Returned stock · Damaged / write-off · Set exact count · Stock correction
QuantityNumeric amount to apply (for "Set exact count" it is the new total)
ReasonFree-text note — required, e.g. "stock received", "breakage", "cycle count"
FooterCancel · Apply adjustment; a toast confirms "Item: from → to"
Figure — the Adjust stock dialog: item, action, quantity and required reason.

Form fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
ItemWhich stock item to adjustSelect from the live item listYesOptions are built from the loaded inventory; each carries its kind (variant/material) and id. If nothing is picked, "Pick an item." is shown. If the list is still loading, the button toasts to try again in a moment.Foil caps (CAP-FOIL) · 80 in
ActionHow to change the countIncrease / receive · Decrease / issue · Returned stock · Damaged / write-off · Set exact count · Stock correctionYesMaps to modes increase, decrease, returned, damaged, set, correction — see the action table below.Increase / receive
QuantityThe amount to applyWhole number, 0 and up (max 1,000,000)YesRounded to a whole number. Must be positive; a 0 is only allowed with Set exact count (to zero the item). Server rejects anything that would drive the count below zero.500
ReasonWhy the count is being changedFree text, 1–300 charactersYesCannot be blank ("A reason is required."). Saved to the audit log and, for bottle variants, to the product event history.Cycle count — 12 caps damaged in store

The six actions and what each does to the count

Action (label)ModeEffect on on-hand countWhen to use it
Increase / receiveincreaseCurrent + quantityNew stock arrived / was produced.
Decrease / issuedecreaseCurrent − quantityStock issued out or consumed.
Returned stockreturnedCurrent + quantityPreviously-issued stock came back in.
Damaged / write-offdamagedCurrent − quantityBreakage, spoilage or loss to write off.
Set exact countsetCount becomes exactly the quantityA physical count found the true figure.
Stock correctioncorrectionCount becomes exactly the quantityFixing a data-entry mistake to a known number.

Button actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Adjust stock (header)Opens the Adjust stock dialogOpens the modal in the browser; loads nothing new (uses the already-loaded item list)Super AdminAdminInventory Manager
Apply adjustmentValidates the form, applies the change and refreshes the table & KPIsPOST /api/admin/inventory/adjust with { kind, id, mode, quantity, reason }. The server re-checks the item exists, applies the mode, refuses a negative result, saves the new count, writes an inventory.adjust.<mode> audit entry (and, for a bottle variant, a STOCK product event), and returns the before/after. A toast shows "Item: from → to".Super AdminAdminInventory Manager (adjust special)
Cancel / × / EscCloses the dialog without changing anythingNoneAny viewer
⚠️
Stock can never go negative

If a Decrease, Issue or Damage would take the count below zero, the server rejects it with the exact current figure, e.g. "Adjustment would make stock negative (current 80)." — the count is left untouched. To zero an item deliberately, use Set exact count with a quantity of 0. Every applied adjustment is permanent in the audit trail; there is no "undo" other than a compensating adjustment.

Workflow — correct an item's on-hand count
Click "Adjust stock"Pick the itemChoose an actionEnter the quantityType a reasonApply adjustmentCount updates · audit logged · KPIs & table refresh
Figure — the end-to-end stock-correction path.

Managing supply items (API)

The raw-material / supplies catalogue behind the table — create, edit and remove InventoryItem rows. These endpoints exist in the backend for setting up new supply lines.

Purpose Add a brand-new supply line (e.g. a new cap type or cleaning agent), rename a unit, adjust a reorder level, or retire an item that is no longer stocked.
Access Super Admin Admin Inventory Manager — create needs inventory:create, edit needs inventory:edit, delete needs inventory:delete Path Admin → Catalogue → Inventory (backed by /api/admin/inventory)
ℹ️
Filled-bottle stock is created with the product, not here

These create/edit/delete endpoints govern only the raw-material / supply side (InventoryItem). The filled-bottle rows in the table come from product Variant records and are created on the Products screen, not here — on the Inventory screen you correct their counts with Adjust stock.

Add a supply item — POST /api/admin/inventory

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
SKUUnique stock code for the itemUppercase letters, numbers, underscores; 2–40 charsYesAuto-uppercased; must match ^[A-Z0-9_]+$. A duplicate SKU is rejected "An item with that SKU already exists."CAP-FOIL
NameHuman-readable item name2–80 charactersYesTrimmed; required.Foil caps
UnitThe counting unit1–20 charactersYesTrimmed; free text (e.g. "pcs", "litre").pcs
QuantityOpening on-hand count0 or moreOpt.Defaults to 0 if omitted.500
Reorder atLevel that flags the item for reorder0 or moreOpt.Defaults to 0 if omitted; drives the status colour.500

Edit / remove a supply item

OperationWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Edit fields — PATCH /api/admin/inventory/{id}Change name, unit, quantity or reorder levelAny subset of name (2–80), unit (1–20), quantity (≥0), reorderAt (≥0). Changing the quantity also requires the inventory:adjust special (it is a stock change). Audited as inventory.update.Super AdminAdminInventory Manager
Delete — DELETE /api/admin/inventory/{id}Removes the supply item entirelyDeletes the InventoryItem if it exists (else "Item not found."). Audited as inventory.delete.Super AdminAdminInventory Manager (delete)
✅
Prefer "Adjust stock" over editing the quantity directly

You can change an item's quantity with a PATCH, but the Adjust stock tool is the better path: it forces a reason, records the exact before → after, refuses negatives, and writes a typed inventory.adjust.<mode> event — so the why is never lost. Use the edit path for the item's descriptive fields (name, unit) and its reorder level; use Adjust stock for the count.

Who can do what

CapabilityPermissionSuper AdminAdminInventory ManagerOperationsOther roles
View the inventory overview & KPIsinventory:viewYesYesYesYesNo
Adjust an item's stock countinventory:adjust (a "full"-level special)YesYesYesNoNo
Add a supply iteminventory:createYesYesYesNoNo
Edit a supply iteminventory:editYesYesYesNoNo
Delete a supply iteminventory:deleteYesYesYesNoNo
ℹ️
Inventory is a supply-side module

In the permission matrix the Inventory Manager role holds this module at "full" (so it can view, adjust, create, edit and delete), Super Admin and Admin have full access to everything, and Operations gets it at "view" only — it can watch stock levels but not change them. Support, procurement, quality, marketing, accountant and delivery roles have no inventory access and never see this screen. Every API call runs requirePermission(inventory, …) on the server, so hiding the menu item is convenience only — the server is the real boundary. Even if a view-only user reaches the Adjust stock button, applying the change is rejected with a 403.

✅
Best Practice — count, then reconcile with a reason every time

Run a physical cycle count on a rota, then reconcile the system to it through Adjust stock — use Set exact count when you know the true figure, or Damaged / write-off for known breakage — and always write a specific reason ("morning cycle count", "3 crates broken in transit"). Set each item's Reorder at to about half of your safe minimum so the amber Low band gives you lead time before it turns red. Watch the Reorder now KPI daily; when it is above zero, use the Reorder filter chip to see exactly which items to purchase.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"Your role can't view inventory (403)"Signed in as a role without inventory:view (e.g. Support, Accountant)Use a Super Admin, Admin, Inventory Manager or Operations account.
Table shows "Backend offline" and only demo rowsThe Next.js backend isn't running / unreachableStart the backend (next-app) so the overview API can be reached.
"Adjustment would make stock negative (current N)"A Decrease / Issue / Damage is larger than what's on handEnter a quantity no larger than the current count, or use Set exact count to correct to the real figure.
"A reason is required."The Reason box was left blankType a short, specific reason before applying — it's stored in the audit trail.
"Enter a positive quantity."Quantity is 0 (or negative) on an action other than Set exact countEnter a positive number, or switch the action to Set exact count if you really mean to zero the item.
"Inventory is still loading — try again in a moment."Clicked Adjust stock before the live item list finished loadingWait a moment for the green live banner, then reopen the dialog.
Apply adjustment fails with a permission errorRole has inventory:view but not the inventory:adjust special (e.g. Operations)Ask a Super Admin, Admin or Inventory Manager to make the change, or have your role upgraded.
"An item with that SKU already exists."Creating a supply item with a SKU already in useSKUs are unique — pick a different code.

Frequently asked questions

Q. What's the difference between "Stock" and "1000 ml ready"?

Stock in the table is everything physically on hand. The 1000 ml ready and 500 ml ready KPIs show the available figure — stock minus whatever is already reserved for open orders — so they tell you how many bottles you can still sell right now.

Q. Why did an item turn red even though it still has stock?

Because its Stock has fallen to or below its Reorder at level (that's the Reorder state), or its available count hit zero because everything is reserved (that's Out of stock). Red means "act now", not necessarily "empty".

Q. I received a delivery of caps. How do I add them?

Click Adjust stock, pick the caps item, choose Increase / receive, enter how many arrived, type a reason like "delivery received", and press Apply adjustment. The count goes up and the change is logged.

Q. Can I undo an adjustment I made by mistake?

There is no delete or undo — the ledger is append-only for integrity. Post a compensating adjustment instead (e.g. if you added 500 too many, do a Decrease / issue of 500, or use Set exact count to fix it to the true number) and note the correction in the reason.

Q. Does adjusting a bottle's stock affect what customers can order?

Yes — filled-bottle rows are the product Variant stock, so lowering a variant's count reduces what's sellable, and its status will flip to Low / Reorder / Out of stock accordingly. A bottle adjustment also writes a STOCK entry to that product's event history, unlike a supply-only item.

DOODLYAdmin · Catalogue · Bottle Inventory
§Admin · Catalogue

Bottle Inventory

Track DOODLY's reusable glass-bottle fleet across its full lifecycle — from sanitised stock, out on delivery, back for collection and cleaning, to damaged or lost — and see the customer deposits the business is holding against those bottles.

ℹ️
Why this screen exists

Every DOODLY bottle is a reusable asset the company owns, not packaging that is thrown away. A single bottle is issued to a customer, returned empty, washed, and sent out again many times. Bottle Inventory is the asset register for that fleet: it counts how many bottles sit in each stage, warns when too many are stuck with customers, and keeps an append-only ledger of every movement so the physical count and the books always agree.

Fleet Overview & Movement Ledger

The main Bottle Inventory page — six live fleet counters, a pending-return alert, and the movement history table.

Purpose Give a one-glance view of where the whole glass-bottle fleet is right now, flag bottles overdue for collection, and list every recorded fleet movement.
Access Super Admin Admin Inventory — needs the bottleInventory:view permission. Any other role receives a 403 and an "Your role can't view bottle inventory" banner. Path Admin → Catalogue → Bottle Inventory
Header — Bottle Inventory · "The glass bottle fleet and deposits." · [ Record movement ]
KPI strip (6 tiles)Total bottles owned · In sanitised stock · In circulation · Pending return · Lost / damaged (mo) · Deposits held
Amber notice barLive "X bottles awaiting collection · Y in circulation · fleet utilisation Z%" alert
Movement ledger tableTime · Capacity · Movement · Qty · Reason · By — newest first, with a live-data banner
Live-data bannerGreen "● Live — fleet of N bottles · M recent movement(s) from the DOODLY database" line above the table
Figure — labelled layout of the Bottle Inventory page

The KPI tiles and the ledger below are populated the moment the page opens by two backend calls — GET /api/admin/bottles/overview (the counters, notice and fleet matrix) and GET /api/admin/bottles/movements?pageSize=100 (the ledger). Until those return, the tiles show demo placeholder numbers; the green banner confirms when live database figures have replaced them.

The six fleet counters (KPI tiles)

TileWhat it countsHow it is calculated (backend)
Total bottles ownedEvery physical bottle the company still owns, across all capacities.Sum of the Available, In circulation, Awaiting collection, Cleaning and Damaged stages. Lost bottles are excluded — a lost bottle is written off, not owned.
In sanitised stockClean, filled/ready bottles sitting in the hub, available to send out.The AVAILABLE stage total.
In circulationBottles currently out with customers.The IN_CIRCULATION stage total.
Pending returnEmpties overdue for pickup — the number to chase.The AWAITING_COLLECTION stage total. (The customer-ledger equivalent is Issued − Returned − Lost.)
Lost / damaged (mo)Bottles broken or gone missing.LOST + DAMAGED stage totals added together.
Deposits heldMoney the company is holding as bottle deposits against customers.Deposits charged minus deposits refunded, from the customer bottle ledger; shown compact (e.g. ₹1.0L, ₹52k).
ℹ️
Two views of the same fleet

The backend keeps two coupled records. The bottle stock side counts physical bottles by capacity and stage (the six tiles above). The customer ledger side records per-customer Issued / Returned / Lost events and the deposit money. "Pending return" and "Deposits held" come from the customer ledger; the other four tiles come from the physical stock.

The amber notice bar

Directly under the tiles sits a warning banner. On a live page it is rewritten to read, for example: "418 bottles awaiting collection · 6,722 in circulation · fleet utilisation 85%." Fleet utilisation is the share of owned bottles that are out working — (In circulation + Awaiting collection) ÷ Total owned, as a percentage. A high figure means most of the fleet is in the field; a rising "awaiting collection" count is the cue to run a collection-reminder campaign.

The movement ledger table

Every fleet transition ever recorded appears here, newest first. It is an append-only ledger — rows are never edited or deleted, which is what lets the counts above be trusted.

ColumnShowsSource field
TimeWhen the movement was recorded, e.g. "26 Jun, 6:40 AM".createdAt
CapacityBottle size — 1000 ml, 500 ml or 300 ml.capacityMl
MovementThe transition, as "From stage → To stage" with a colour badge. A brand-new batch shows "New stock →".fromLabel → toLabel (coloured by toTone)
QtyHow many bottles moved.qty
ReasonWhy it moved; if a note was added it is appended after " · ".reason (+ note)
ByThe role of the person who recorded the movement (e.g. admin, inventory).actorRole
Table behaviourDetail
ColumnsTime · Capacity · Movement · Qty · Reason · By (6 columns, read-only).
FiltersThe page shows a single All filter chip. The backend additionally supports filtering by capacity and by stage (a movement matches if the stage is either its from or its to).
SortingFixed newest-first by time (createdAt descending) from the server; the client DataTable can re-sort the loaded rows by any column.
SearchBackend search matches the reason, note and role text (case-insensitive). The client DataTable also has its own quick search box over the loaded rows.
PaginationServer-paginated (default 20, maximum 100 per page). The page loads the 100 most recent movements; the DataTable paginates those on screen.
ExportsStandard DataTable export of the visible rows (the shared table toolbar).
Row actionsNone — the ledger is a historical record. To change stock, use Record movement (Screen 2), which adds a new row.
✅
Best Practice

Read the KPI tiles top-to-bottom before your morning dispatch. If In sanitised stock is running thin while Pending return and In circulation are high, the fleet is cash-flow-starved of clean bottles — prioritise a collection round or clean a backlog rather than buying new glass.

Record Bottle Movement

The pop-up form that moves bottles between lifecycle stages (or adds brand-new stock) and writes one ledger row.

Purpose Post a single, reasoned fleet transition — assign to customers, mark empties awaiting collection, send to cleaning, return to available, or write off damaged/lost bottles — while keeping every stage count non-negative.
Access Super Admin Admin Inventory — needs the bottleInventory:adjust permission, a full-level special. A view-only user can open the page but the save is rejected with 403. Path Admin → Catalogue → Bottle Inventory → Record movement

Clicking the blue Record movement button in the page header opens this dialog. (If the fleet is still loading, the button shows a "Fleet is still loading — try again in a moment" toast and does nothing, because it needs the stage list first.)

Modal — Record bottle movement · [ × close ]
Row 1Capacity (dropdown) · Quantity (number)
Row 2From stage (dropdown, incl. "New stock") · To stage (dropdown)
ReasonRequired free-text line
NoteOptional extra detail
Error lineRed inline validation message
Footer[ Cancel ] · [ Record movement ]
Figure — the Record bottle movement dialog

Form fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Capacity Which bottle size is moving. Dropdown: 1000 ml, 500 ml, 300 ml. Yes Server rejects anything other than 300, 500 or 1000 ml ("Capacity must be 300, 500 or 1000 ml."). 1000 ml
Quantity How many bottles to move. Whole number, 1 or more. Yes Must be a positive whole number (client: "Enter a positive quantity."). Server caps at 1,000,000 and re-checks > 0. Cannot exceed the count available in the From stage. 24
From stage Where the bottles are leaving. Choose "New stock" when adding bottles that weren't in the fleet yet. Dropdown: "— New stock (procurement) —" plus the six stages. Defaults to In circulation. Opt. If left as "New stock" the source count is untouched (a pure addition). If a stage is chosen it must have enough bottles, and must differ from To. In circulation
To stage Where the bottles are going. Dropdown: the six stages. Defaults to Cleaning. Yes Must be a known stage and must differ from From (client: "From and To stages must differ."). Cleaning
Reason Why the movement happened — the audit trail. Free text, up to 300 characters. Yes Cannot be blank (client: "A reason is required."; server: "A reason is required for every bottle movement."). Empties collected on route R3
Note Extra detail, shown after the reason in the ledger. Free text, up to 500 characters. Opt. Optional; trimmed and stored alongside the reason. 3 chipped, rest fine

The six lifecycle stages

StageMeaningBadge colourCounts toward "owned"?
AvailableClean, sanitised, ready to fill and dispatch.GreenYes
In circulationOut with a customer right now.BlueYes
Awaiting collectionEmpty at the customer, waiting to be picked up.AmberYes
CleaningBack at the hub, being washed and sterilised.BlueYes
DamagedChipped or broken; may be recovered or written off.RedYes
LostGone missing — written off the owned fleet.RedNo
💡
Tip — the natural bottle cycle

The recommended path a bottle follows is: Available → In circulation → Awaiting collection → Cleaning → Available, looping forever. Damaged and Lost are the exits. The dialog lets you post any transition (physical reality varies), but staying on the natural cycle keeps the counts easy to reconcile.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Record movement (header) Opens the Record bottle movement dialog. None until you save — just loads the fleet stage list into the dropdowns. Super Admin+ Admin Inventory
Record movement (dialog footer) Validates the form and posts the movement. Shows "Recording…" while it saves, then a success toast like "1000 ml · 24 → Cleaning (now 132)" and refreshes the whole page. POST /api/admin/bottles/movement in one database transaction: decrement the From stock (skipped for New stock), increment the To stock, append a bottle-movement ledger row, and write a central audit-log entry (bottle.move.<from>.<to>). Super Admin+ Admin Inventory (adjust)
Cancel / × Closes the dialog without saving. Pressing Esc or clicking the dark backdrop does the same. None. —
Workflow — recording a movement
Click "Record movement"Pick capacity & quantityChoose From → To stageType a reason (+ note)Save — transaction runsStock counts update & ledger row appendedAudit log written · page refreshes
Figure — end-to-end flow of a recorded bottle movement
⚠️
Warning — you cannot move more than you have

If you try to move more bottles out of a stage than it holds, the save is rejected with, for example, "Only 12 1000 ml bottle(s) in Available — cannot move 24." No stage can ever go negative. When that happens, count the physical bottles and correct the earlier movement by posting a fresh, opposite one — never assume the number is wrong.

⛔
Caution — movements are permanent

The ledger is append-only. There is no edit or delete. A movement you post cannot be undone from the screen — the only correction is to record a compensating movement in the other direction, with a reason that explains the fix. Marking bottles Lost removes them from "Total bottles owned"; recover them only by recording a Lost → Available movement.

✅
Best Practice

Write reasons a colleague could audit months later. "Empties collected on route R3, driver Ramesh" beats "collected". The reason and note are searchable in the ledger, so specific text pays off during stock reconciliation.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner: "Your role can't view bottle inventory (403)."Your role lacks bottleInventory:view (only Super Admin, Admin and Inventory have it by default).Ask a Super Admin to grant your role the Bottle Inventory permission, or have someone with access do the task.
Save fails: "Only N … bottle(s) in <stage> — cannot move X."The From stage doesn't hold enough bottles for the quantity entered.Lower the quantity, or first record the movement that should have put those bottles into that stage.
Save fails: "From and To stages must differ."Same stage chosen on both sides.Pick a different destination stage (or "New stock" as the source for a pure addition).
"Record movement" button does nothing / shows "Fleet is still loading".The overview call hasn't returned yet, so the stage dropdowns aren't ready.Wait a moment for the green live banner, then click again.
Banner: "Backend offline … couldn't load live fleet." and KPIs show demo numbers.The next-app backend isn't reachable from the browser.Confirm the backend is running and the API base URL is correct; reload once it's up.
KPI numbers look stale after a movement.—Saving already re-fetches automatically; if in doubt, reload the page to re-pull the overview.

Frequently asked questions

Q. What is the difference between "In sanitised stock" and "Available"?

They are the same thing. The KPI tile is labelled "In sanitised stock" for readability, but it counts the AVAILABLE stage — clean bottles ready to go out.

Q. Why doesn't "Total bottles owned" include lost bottles?

A lost bottle is written off — the company no longer has it, so counting it as "owned" would overstate the fleet. Lost bottles appear only in the "Lost / damaged" tile. If a lost bottle turns up, record a Lost → Available movement and it re-joins the owned count.

Q. Do I need a customer to record a movement?

No. This screen moves the fleet in bulk by capacity and stage — it is the warehouse/asset view. Per-customer issue/return and deposit tracking live in the customer bottle ledger (surfaced on the customer's account and in "Pending return" / "Deposits held").

Q. Who shows up in the "By" column?

The role of whoever recorded the movement (for example, admin or inventory). The system also stores the actor's ID and IP address behind the scenes for the audit trail, though only the role is shown in the table.

DOODLYAdmin · Catalogue · Delivery Settings
§Admin · Catalogue

Delivery Settings

One control panel that decides which dates a customer can pick to start milk delivery — the nightly order cut-off, the morning delivery window, how far ahead an order may be booked, which weekdays you run, and any holidays or maintenance days you close.

ℹ️
What this screen controls

Every rule you set here becomes the single source of truth for the start-date picker used everywhere on the storefront — the checkout, the "choose your first delivery day" calendar, and the subscription "change start date" panel. A brand-new employee can think of it as: "the settings that tell customers which mornings we can deliver." There is no code change and no redeploy — you edit the form, click Save settings, and the storefront honours the new rules immediately, platform-wide.

ℹ️
Where the data lives

All values are stored in one database row — the DeliveryConfig singleton (its id is literally "singleton", so there is exactly one shared config for the whole business). The row is created automatically with sensible defaults the first time the page is opened. The screen reads it with GET /api/admin/delivery/config and writes it with PATCH /api/admin/delivery/config. Every save is diffed old → new and written to the audit log.

Delivery Settings — configuration form

A single form with the ten scheduling rules that drive the storefront date picker.

Purpose Set and change the order cut-off time, delivery window, minimum/maximum advance days, the weekdays you deliver, the weekend switch, and the holiday & blackout date lists — then push them live everywhere in one click.
Access Super Admin · Admin · Operations (view & edit) — every other role is refused (403) Path Admin → Catalogue → Delivery Settings (/admin/delivery-settings.html)
Header — Delivery settings · green "Live" badge · live-status banner (● Live — delivery configuration from the DOODLY database · Saves apply platform-wide)
Intro line"These rules drive the start-date picker everywhere on the storefront. Changes apply immediately."
Form grid (two columns)Order cut-off time · Delivery window (start – end) · Minimum advance (days) · Maximum advance (days).
Available delivery daysSeven checkboxes — Sun Mon Tue Wed Thu Fri Sat.
Weekend delivery toggleSingle checkbox — "Weekend delivery (Sat & Sun)".
Date lists (two columns)Holidays textarea · Blackout dates (maintenance) textarea — comma-separated ISO dates.
Action rowSave settings · Reset to default · "✓ Saved — changes are live" confirmation.
Figure — labelled layout of the Delivery Settings screen
ℹ️
Live vs local

On open, the page fetches the config from the database and shows a green banner: "● Live — delivery configuration from the DOODLY database (<api-base>). Saves apply platform-wide." If the backend cannot be reached it shows a red banner "⚠ Backend offline at <api-base> — settings save locally only." and any edits stay in this browser only until you re-save with the backend up. This manual documents the live (database-backed) behaviour.

Every field on the form

These are the exact ten settings the screen edits. Field IDs are shown in code so support staff can match them to what they see on screen. All numeric limits below are enforced again on the server, so a bad value can never reach the database.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Order cut-off time
#ds-cutoff		 The nightly deadline. Orders placed before it qualify for next-morning delivery; after it, the earliest start shifts one more day. A time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour time picker) Yes Split into cutoffHour (0–23) and cutoffMinute (0–59); server clamps to those ranges. Hint reads "currently 8:00 PM". 20:00 (8:00 PM)
Delivery window — start
#ds-slotStart		 Left side of the morning window text shown to customers. Free text label Yes slotStart — trimmed, max 40 chars. 5:00 AM
Delivery window — end
#ds-slotEnd		 Right side of the window; also drives the "Before <end>" promise label on the storefront. Free text label Yes slotEnd — trimmed, max 40 chars. The storefront shows the slot as "Before <slotEnd>". 7:00 AM
Minimum advance (days)
#ds-min		 The earliest a customer may start — today plus this many days (then the cut-off is applied on top). Whole number, 1 or more Yes minAdvanceDays — coerced to at least 1 in the form; server clamps 1–60. Must be ≤ maximum advance (see caution below). 1
Maximum advance (days)
#ds-max		 The furthest ahead a start date can be booked — today plus this many days. Whole number, 1 or more Yes maxAdvanceDays — coerced to at least 1 (default 30); server clamps 1–365. Must be ≥ minimum advance. 30
Available delivery days
[data-day="0..6"]		 Which weekdays you run at all. Tick the days you deliver. Any of seven checkboxes — Sun(0) … Sat(6) Opt. availableDays — array of 0–6. If none are ticked the form falls back to all seven; server de-duplicates and sorts. Mon–Sat (1–6)
Weekend delivery
#ds-weekend		 A master switch for Saturday & Sunday. When off, both weekend days are dropped even if their day boxes are ticked. On / Off checkbox Opt. weekendDelivery (boolean). Off removes day 0 (Sun) and day 6 (Sat) from the effective delivery days. On
Holidays
#ds-holidays		 Fixed dates you will not deliver (public holidays). Blocked in the picker with reason "Holiday — no delivery". Comma- or space-separated ISO dates Opt. holidays — server keeps only YYYY-MM-DD matches, de-duplicated, max 200 entries. Hint: "Comma-separated ISO dates (YYYY-MM-DD)." 2026-08-15, 2026-10-02
Blackout dates (maintenance)
#ds-blackout		 One-off no-delivery days for maintenance or operations closure. Hint: "No delivery on these dates." Comma- or space-separated ISO dates Opt. blackoutDates — same ISO validation and 200-entry cap as holidays. 2026-07-20
⚠️
Minimum must never exceed maximum

If you set Minimum advance greater than Maximum advance, the server rejects the save with a 400 error: "Minimum advance days cannot exceed maximum advance days." Keep minimum ≤ maximum (for example min 1, max 30).

ℹ️
Holidays vs Blackout dates

Both lists block delivery on the dates you list. They exist as two boxes only to keep your records tidy — Holidays for planned public holidays and Blackout dates for maintenance / operational closures. On the storefront they simply show as unavailable days (reason "holiday" vs "blackout"). A date in either list is unbookable.

Buttons & actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save settings
#ds-save		 Reads every field, writes the settings so this browser's storefront picker updates instantly, and pushes the ten fields to the database. On success a toast reads "Delivery settings saved to the database — live platform-wide"; the inline "✓ Saved — changes are live" note flashes for ~2.6 seconds. PATCH /api/admin/delivery/config with the scheduling fields. Server validates & clamps each value, checks min ≤ max, writes the DeliveryConfig row, stamps updatedBy = your role, and records an audit entry (delivery.config.update) listing exactly which fields changed. Super Admin+ Admin Operations (needs deliverySettings:edit)
Reset to default
#ds-reset		 Clears the local override and re-renders the form with the built-in defaults (cut-off 8:00 PM, all seven days, weekend on, min 1 / max 30, empty holiday & blackout lists), then saves those defaults to the database the same way a Save does. Removes the local doodly-delivery override, then the same PATCH /api/admin/delivery/config path runs with the default values — audited like any other change. Super Admin+ Admin Operations
⚠️
If your role cannot edit

If someone without edit rights clicks Save, the change is kept in their own browser but the database rejects the write (403). They see the toast "Saved locally — your role can't edit settings (403)" and nothing changes for other users. Only Super Admin, Admin, and Operations can make settings stick platform-wide.

How a change goes live

Workflow — saving a delivery-settings change
Open page → GET config from database Form renders with current values Edit fields (cut-off, days, holidays…) Click Save settings Local storefront picker updates instantly PATCH config to database (validated + audited) Storefront everywhere honours the new rules
Figure — the load → edit → save → live path for Delivery Settings

How each setting shapes the customer's date picker

Understanding this helps you predict exactly what a customer will see after you save. The storefront scheduling engine applies your rules in this order when deciding whether a date can be picked:

SettingEffect on the pickerBlocked-date reason shown
Order cut-off timeBefore the cut-off, the earliest start is today + minimum advance. After the cut-off, tomorrow is closed and the earliest start moves one more day."Past today's 8 PM cut-off" / "Too soon"
Minimum advanceSets the earliest bookable date (today + this many days)."Too soon"
Maximum advanceSets the last bookable date (today + this many days). Anything beyond is greyed out."Too far ahead to book"
Available delivery days + Weekend deliveryA date on a day you don't run (or a weekend when the weekend switch is off) is not selectable."We don't deliver on this day"
HolidaysListed dates are blocked out."Holiday — no delivery"
Blackout datesListed dates are blocked out."Delivery unavailable on this date"
Delivery window (start/end)Shown on each available date as the promise label — "Before <end>".—
ℹ️
This screen edits scheduling only

The DeliveryConfig row also stores a few operational values — delivery charge, free-delivery threshold, auto-assignment on/off, per-executive and per-trip bottle capacity, and the late-delivery SLA time and grace. Those are not exposed on this form; they are managed from the Operations → Delivery Mgmt, Auto Assignment and Late Deliveries screens. The Delivery Settings page saves only the ten scheduling fields listed above and leaves the operational fields untouched.

✅
Best Practice

Make scheduling changes ahead of time, especially the cut-off and holiday lists, so customers who are mid-checkout aren't surprised. After every save, confirm you got the green "…live platform-wide" toast, then open the storefront date picker in another tab and check the affected date shows the state you expect (available, or blocked with the right reason). Add holidays in bulk at the start of each month rather than one at a time.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Toast says "Saved locally — your role can't edit settings (403)".The signed-in role lacks deliverySettings:edit.Have a Super Admin, Admin, or Operations user make the change.
Save is rejected with a "minimum … maximum" error.Minimum advance was set higher than maximum advance.Set Minimum advance ≤ Maximum advance (e.g. 1 and 30).
Red banner "⚠ Backend offline … settings save locally only."The backend/API is unreachable, so edits stay in this browser only.Restore connectivity, reload the page, and Save again to push to the database.
A date customers expect to see is missing from the picker.That day is unticked, weekend delivery is off, or the date is in the Holidays / Blackout list.Tick the weekday, turn the weekend switch on, or remove the date from the holiday/blackout box, then Save.
Other devices still show the old rules.They read the config from the database on load; the save may not have reached the database.Confirm the green "live platform-wide" toast fired, then refresh the storefront on the other device.
Holiday/blackout date I typed didn't take effect.It wasn't in YYYY-MM-DD form, so the server dropped it.Re-enter using ISO dates, e.g. 2026-08-15, comma-separated.

Frequently asked questions

Q: Does changing the cut-off time reschedule customers who already subscribed?

A: No. The cut-off and advance-day rules only decide the earliest start date the picker offers on new orders and start-date changes. Active subscriptions already have their dates and are not moved.

Q: What happens if I un-tick every delivery day?

A: The form never lets the list be empty — if no day is ticked it falls back to all seven days. An empty set would make every date unbookable, so it is prevented on purpose.

Q: The weekday boxes have Saturday and Sunday ticked, but weekend delivery is off. Which wins?

A: The weekend switch wins. "Weekend delivery" off removes Saturday and Sunday from the effective days even when their individual boxes are checked — treat it as a quick override.

Q: Is every change tracked?

A: Yes. Each Save writes an audit entry (delivery.config.update) that names the exact fields that changed and records who changed them (your role), so the history is fully accountable.

Module chapter

Admin · Operations

6 screen groups documented from the live application.

DOODLYAdmin · Operations · Delivery Management
§Admin · Operations

Delivery Management

The daily control room for milk deliveries — see who is delivering what, dispatch the day's route in one click, assign or reassign a delivery executive, move an order through its delivery stages, and log any problem in the field.

ℹ️
Note

This screen reads and writes the live DOODLY database. Every number on the page is calculated from real Delivery records — it is not a demo sheet. When the backend is reachable, a green banner appears below the toolbar reading "● Live — N delivery record(s) from the DOODLY database". If the backend is offline or your role lacks permission, a red banner explains why.

Delivery Management — command strip

Header, four live KPIs and the one-click dispatch button.

Purpose Give the operations team a one-glance status of the day and a single button that assigns every scheduled delivery to an executive.
Access Super Admin Admin Operations Manager — anyone with deliveries:view Path Admin → Operations → Delivery Mgmt
Header — Delivery Management · sub-title "Tomorrow's deliveries, zones, dispatch and executive performance" · button Generate deliveries
KPI · ScheduledDeliveries still in SCHEDULED status (not yet assigned)
KPI · ZonesCount of delivery zones on record
KPI · Milk requiredTotal bottles across today's deliveries, shown in "L"
KPI · DriversActive executives (distinct drivers with deliveries)
Live bannerGreen "● Live — N record(s)" or a red offline / 403 message
Generate deliveriesRuns auto-assignment for today's 06:00–08:00 slot
Figure — the top command strip: four KPIs on the left, the Generate-deliveries action on the right.

The four headline KPIs are placeholders in the page markup (312 / 4 / 236 L / 4) and are overwritten the instant the live data loads. They are computed by the backend endpoint GET /api/admin/deliveries/stats and mapped as follows.

KPIWhat it countsBackend field
ScheduledDeliveries in status SCHEDULED — booked but not yet handed to an executivekpis.scheduled
ZonesNumber of DeliveryZone records configuredkpis.zones
Milk requiredSum of bottle counts across the loaded deliveries, printed as "<n> L"kpis.milkLitres
DriversDistinct executives that have deliveries today (falls back to the active-driver count)kpis.activeExecutives
💡
Tip

If the "Scheduled" number is high late in the morning, deliveries are booked but nobody is carrying them. Press Generate deliveries to dispatch them, or open the Auto Assignment board for finer control.

Button — Generate deliveries

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Generate deliveries Auto-assigns every unassigned, scheduled delivery for today's morning slot to available executives; overflow goes to a pending queue. Shows a toast: "Auto-assignment: N assigned, M queued." then refreshes the whole screen. POST /api/assignments/auto with { date: today 00:00, slot: "06:00-08:00" }. Runs the capacity-aware engine (45 bottles per executive) under the active strategy (Equal distribution by default). Super Admin Admin Operations Manager
Workflow — Generate deliveries (auto-assignment)
Click GeneratePOST /assignments/auto (today · 06:00–08:00)Read the active strategyCollect SCHEDULED, unassigned deliveriesCollect AVAILABLE executives (≤45 bottles each)Plan trips + overflow queueWrite assignments · notify executivesToast + refresh KPIs, list & analytics
Figure — one click walks the whole day's scheduled deliveries through the assignment engine.
ℹ️
Note — fixed slot

From this screen the dispatch always targets today and the 06:00–08:00 morning slot (DOODLY's core delivery window). To dispatch a different date or slot, or to drag-and-drop individual stops, use the Auto Assignment screen.

⚠️
Warning — Manual strategy

If the assignment strategy is set to Manual, pressing Generate does nothing automatic — the response says "Manual mode — auto-assignment is switched off" and reports 0 assigned. Switch the strategy on the Auto Assignment screen first, or assign each delivery by hand from the list below.

Delivery performance analytics

Eight operational KPIs plus a per-executive scoreboard.

Purpose Show how the day is going — completion, delays, bottle collection, ratings — and rank each executive on throughput and speed.
Access Super Admin Admin Operations Manager Path Admin → Operations → Delivery Mgmt
Panel — Delivery performance
Analytics KPI bandEight tiles: Today's deliveries · Completed · Pending · Delayed · Avg delivery time · Bottle collection · Customer rating · Distance covered
Deliveries per executiveTable: Executive · Zone · Assigned · Completed · Avg time · Rating
Figure — the analytics block sits between the KPI strip and the deliveries list.
Analytics tileMeaningHow it's derived
Today's deliveriesTotal delivery rows in scopekpis.total
CompletedDeliveries marked DELIVEREDkpis.delivered
PendingStill SCHEDULEDkpis.pending
DelayedDated before today and still not closed (not delivered / failed / skipped)kpis.delayed
Avg delivery timeAverage minutes from the delivery's due time to when it was marked deliveredkpis.avgTimeMin (shows "—" when no data)
Bottle collectionShare of delivered stops where at least one empty bottle came backkpis.bottleCollectionPct
Customer ratingAverage rating across executiveskpis.avgRating
Distance coveredReserved — always shows "—" until route geodata is wiredNot yet computed

Deliveries per executive lists every executive who has at least one delivery assigned. Columns:

ColumnMeaning
ExecutiveDriver's name
ZoneFirst zone seen on that executive's routed deliveries ("—" if none)
AssignedDeliveries handed to them
CompletedOf those, how many are delivered
Avg timeTheir average completion time in minutes ("—" if none finished)
RatingTheir driver rating, out of 5★
✅
Best Practice

Read the KPIs top-to-bottom each morning: Delayed and Pending tell you what to fix now; Bottle collection and Customer rating tell you how yesterday's service actually landed. A steadily falling bottle-collection % usually means executives are skipping the empties step, not that customers aren't returning bottles.

Deliveries list

Every delivery record with filters, search and a Manage action per row.

Purpose The working list — find a delivery, see who has it and its status, and open it to assign, progress or flag it.
Access Super Admin Admin Operations Manager Path Admin → Operations → Delivery Mgmt
Table — Deliveries · search · status filter chips · export · pagination
ToolbarFree-text search, status facet chips, date range, saved views, export
ColumnsDelivery · Customer · Zone / area · Driver · Slot · Bottles · Status · Manage
Row actionManage link opens the delivery panel
PagerPage controls at the foot of the table
Figure — the deliveries data table, driven by the unified DataTable engine.

Table specification

AspectDetail
ColumnsDelivery (short ID #xxxxxx), Customer, Zone / area, Driver, Slot, Bottles (collected/total), Status (colour badge), Manage
FiltersStatus chips: All · Scheduled · Assigned · Out for delivery · Delivered · Failed
SearchFree-text across the row (customer, area, driver, ID) via the DataTable engine
SortingClick any column header to sort; backend returns newest date first, then route sequence
Date range & saved viewsDataTable engine also offers a date-range picker, saved views and row favourites
ExportsExport the current view from the toolbar
PaginationEnabled; the backend returns up to 150 records, newest first
Row actionManage → opens the Manage-delivery panel (Screen 4)

The Bottles column

Bottles reads as collected / total — for example 2/3 means the executive has recorded two empty bottles back out of three expected for that stop. It comes from the record's bottlesIn and bottleCount.

Status badge — the delivery lifecycle

The Status column shows a colour-coded badge. These are the ten real states a delivery can hold:

StatusBadge labelColourStage
SCHEDULEDScheduledGreyBooked, no executive yet
ASSIGNEDAssignedBlueGiven to an executive
ACCEPTEDAcceptedBlueExecutive accepted the trip
PACKEDPackedBlueBottles loaded
OUT_FOR_DELIVERYOut for deliveryAmberOn the road
ON_THE_WAYOn the wayAmberEn route to this stop
REACHEDNear customerAmberAt the doorstep
DELIVEREDDeliveredGreenHanded over — done
FAILEDFailedRedCould not deliver
SKIPPEDSkippedRedIntentionally skipped
Workflow — a delivery's normal life
ScheduledAssignedAcceptedPackedOut for deliveryOn the wayNear customerDelivered
Figure — the happy path; a stop can branch to Failed or Skipped from any active stage.
ℹ️
Where the rows come from

Each row is a Delivery record. The Customer is taken from the linked subscription (or one-off order); Zone / area is the delivery address's city and pincode; Driver is the assigned executive's name, or "—" when unassigned. The executive updates status and bottle counts from their mobile delivery app; this admin list is the mirror.

Manage delivery (panel)

Assign an executive, set the status, and log a field issue.

Purpose The single place to intervene on one delivery — reroute it to a different executive, force its status, or record a problem such as "customer not available".
Access Super Admin Admin Operations Manager — assigning a driver needs the full-level deliveries:assign right Path Admin → Operations → Delivery Mgmt → Manage
Modal — Delivery #xxxxxx · customer · area · slot · bottles
Assignment rowDelivery executive (dropdown) · Status (dropdown)
Save assignment & statusWrites the driver and/or status
Report an issueType · Priority · Comments
Log issueRecords a DeliveryIssue against this stop
Error line + CloseInline error text; Close dismisses the panel
Figure — the Manage-delivery panel; the top half reassigns/progresses, the bottom half reports a problem.

The panel header shows the short ID and a one-line summary: customer · area · slot · bottles in/total.

Fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Delivery executive Who carries this delivery Any active driver, or "— Unassigned —" Opt. List loaded from GET /api/admin/drivers; a chosen driver must exist server-side or the save is rejected ("That driver does not exist"). Setting a driver with no explicit status auto-sets the delivery to ASSIGNED. Ramesh Kumar
Status Force the delivery's stage Any of the 10 states (Scheduled → Skipped) Opt. Must be one of the fixed enum values; sent as status on the PATCH. Out for delivery
Issue · Type Category of the field problem Customer not available · Wrong address · Damaged bottle · Payment issue · Product damage · Delivery failed Yes Fixed enum (CUSTOMER_UNAVAILABLE, WRONG_ADDRESS, DAMAGED_BOTTLE, PAYMENT_ISSUE, PRODUCT_ISSUE, DELIVERY_FAILED). The delivery must already have an executive assigned, else the server refuses: "Assign a delivery executive before logging an issue." Customer not available
Issue · Priority How urgent the issue is Low · Medium · High Opt. Defaults to Medium if left as-is. High
Issue · Comments Free-text description of what happened Short text Opt. Up to 500 characters; trimmed and stored on the DeliveryIssue. Gate locked, guard off-duty

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save assignment & status Applies the chosen executive and/or status; refreshes the list and shows "Delivery #xxxxxx updated". PATCH /api/admin/deliveries/[id] with { driverId, status }. A plain status change needs deliveries:edit; changing the driver additionally needs the full-level deliveries:assign right and is audited as delivery.assign. Super Admin Admin Operations Manager
Log issue Files a delivery issue with the selected type, priority and comments; clears the comment box so you can add another; shows "Issue logged for #xxxxxx". POST /api/admin/deliveries/[id]/issue. Needs deliveries:edit; creates a DeliveryIssue and writes an audit entry (delivery.issue.<type>). Super Admin Admin Operations Manager
Close / × / Esc Dismisses the panel without saving. None Super Admin+
Workflow — reassign & progress a delivery
Open ManagePick executivePick status (optional)Save assignment & statusPATCH deliveries/[id]Audit: delivery.assign / updateList refreshes
Figure — the assign/progress path; the issue path is independent and can be used any time after an executive is assigned.
⚠️
Warning — assigning is a privileged action

Setting or changing the Delivery executive requires the full-level deliveries:assign special. If your role only has "manage" on deliveries you can still change the Status, but the save will return "Your role can't assign/update deliveries (403)" the moment it detects a driver change. Operations Managers, Admins and Super Admins hold the full level.

ℹ️
Note — issue needs an executive first

You cannot log an issue on an unassigned delivery. Assign an executive and save first; then the Log issue action will succeed. This keeps every issue attributed to a specific driver.

✅
Best Practice

Use Status overrides sparingly — normally the executive drives status from their mobile app. Reach for the manual override only to correct a mistake (e.g. mark a wrongly-failed stop back to Scheduled for a re-attempt), and always add an issue note so the reason is on the record.

Routes snapshot

A read-only list of the day's routes beneath the deliveries table.

Purpose A quick reference showing which routes exist, their zone, the executive on them and how many stops — without leaving Delivery Management.
Access Super Admin Admin Operations Manager Path Admin → Operations → Delivery Mgmt
Table — Routes (no toolbar · no pager)
ColumnsRoute · Zone · Driver · Stops · Distance · Status
Read-only hereThe Manage link is hidden on this screen
Figure — the compact routes table; full route editing lives on the Routes screen.
AspectDetail
ColumnsRoute (code/name), Zone, Driver, Stops, Distance, Status
SourceGET /api/admin/routes
Toolbar / PagerBoth off — this is a static snapshot
Row actionNone on this screen (Manage is only wired on the dedicated Routes page)
💡
Tip

To create, edit, optimise or delete a route, go to Admin → Operations → Routes. This snapshot is for reading only.

Troubleshooting

Common symptoms and how to clear them.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner "⚠ Backend offline — couldn't load live deliveries"The next-app backend isn't reachableStart / restore the backend service; the banner turns green once records load.
Red banner "Your role can't view deliveries (403)"Your role lacks deliveries:viewAsk a Super Admin to grant Operations access, or sign in with an operations/admin account.
Generate deliveries reports "0 assigned, 0 queued"Nothing scheduled for today's 06:00–08:00 slot, or every stop is already assigned/queuedCheck the Scheduled KPI; if it's non-zero, confirm the strategy isn't Manual and that executives are marked Available.
Generate returns "Manual mode — auto-assignment is switched off"Assignment strategy is set to ManualSwitch to Equal/Capacity on the Auto Assignment screen, or assign each delivery by hand.
Save fails with "Your role can't assign/update deliveries (403)"You changed the executive but only hold "manage" on deliveriesHave an Operations Manager / Admin (full level) make the assignment, or change only the status.
Log issue fails with "Assign a delivery executive before logging an issue"The delivery has no driverAssign an executive and Save first, then log the issue.
"Delivery not found — refresh the list"The list is stale (the record changed since it loaded)Reload the page or press Generate to re-pull; then reopen Manage.
Distance covered shows "—"Route geodata isn't computed yetExpected — this metric is reserved and not yet populated.

Frequently asked questions

Delivery Management.

Does "Generate deliveries" send anything to customers?

No. It assigns deliveries to executives and notifies the executives of their new trips. Customer-facing messages are handled elsewhere (order and delivery notifications).

What's the difference between this screen and Auto Assignment?

Delivery Management is the day's dashboard and record list with a one-click dispatch for today's morning slot. Auto Assignment is the full engine — pick any date/slot, change the strategy, watch live capacity, and drag stops between executives. Both write to the same records.

Why can I change a delivery's status but not its executive?

Changing status needs deliveries:edit (the "manage" level). Assigning or moving an executive is a higher-trust action that needs the full-level deliveries:assign special. Super Admin, Admin and Operations Manager have both.

How many deliveries does the list show?

Up to 150 records, newest date first then route sequence. Use search and the status filter chips to narrow down; the DataTable engine paginates the rest.

What does 45 mean in the dispatch?

Each executive can carry up to 45 glass bottles per trip. The engine packs trips up to that cap; anything that doesn't fit lands in the pending queue and is picked up when an executive returns to the dairy.

DOODLYAdmin · Operations · Auto Assignment
§Admin · Operations

Auto Assignment

Automatically distribute each morning's confirmed deliveries across your available delivery executives — capped at 45 bottles per trip — queue the overflow, and re-fill executives the moment they return to the dairy.

The Auto Assignment board is the operations control room for the "last mile". Every confirmed delivery for a slot is packed onto executives up to a strict 45-bottle carrying capacity per trip (counted by bottles, not by customers). Anything that does not fit drops into a Pending Assignment Queue; when an executive comes back to the dairy, the next batch is pulled from that queue automatically. The screen adds workload balancing, nearest-neighbour route optimisation, a full executive status workflow, and manual override by drag-and-drop, lock and unassign. A live activity feed records every move.

ℹ️
Note — the board is a planner that overlays a real engine

The interactive board (assign.js / DOODLY_ASSIGN) is a self-contained planner that keeps its own working copy of the executives, deliveries and queue in the browser (localStorage). When the DOODLY backend is reachable, the page overlays live data on top of it: the KPI cards, the Strategy selector and the ⚡ Auto-assign button all read and write the production database through /api/assignments/*. The green "● Live" banner at the top confirms this. The drag-drop board, Generate deliveries, Run tests and Reset act on the local planner only — they never touch live orders.

⚠️
Warning — the 45-bottle cap is an invariant

No executive is ever loaded past capacity, by any path — auto-assign, drag-drop, or return-trip pull. The engine guards this on every placement. A single order larger than the biggest capacity can never fit a trip and is parked in the queue with the reason oversize. Per-driver capacity can be raised or lowered on the Drivers screen (DeliveryCapacity); 45 is only the default.

Auto Assignment board — controls, KPIs, executives & queue

The single working screen: a control bar, a live KPI strip, one card per executive, and the pending queue + activity feed.

Purpose Run and supervise the morning assignment: launch auto-assign, watch capacity fill, resolve the queue, and step executives through their trip.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager — viewing the live dashboard needs the deliveries:view permission; running auto-assign, overrides and the strategy switch need deliveries:assign or deliveries:edit. A role without those gets a "403 — your role can't…" toast. Path Admin → Operations → Auto Assignment
Header — Auto Delivery Assignment · "Capacity-based auto-assignment (45 bottles/executive)…"
Live bannerGreen "● Live — auto-assignment engine on the DOODLY database" with order / executive / queued counts, or a red offline / 403 warning
Control barSlot · Search · ⚡ Auto-assign · Strategy · Generate deliveries ▾ · Run tests · Auto-refresh · Reset · test-result chip
KPI stripTotal orders · Total bottles · Assigned · Pending queue · Available execs · On route · Returned · Completed (+ live: Offline · Assigned orders · Unassigned orders · Completion %)
Executive cards (left)One capacity card per executive: status badge, 0–45 bottle bar, route summary, delivery chips, status buttons
Side panel (right, top)Pending Assignment Queue — queued chips + "Assign queue to available execs"
Side panel (right, bottom)Activity & notifications — the last 8 log entries
Figure — labelled layout of the Auto Assignment board.
💡
Tip — a clean board starts empty

On a production site with no demo data, the board opens empty — no synthetic executives or deliveries — and fills only from real assignments. An empty queue shows "Queue empty — everything assigned ✓". This is the correct, honest state; use ⚡ Auto-assign to populate it from the day's real orders.

The control bar

The controls sit in one row above the KPIs. Some drive the local planner; the highlighted ones are wired to the live engine.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Slot selectThe delivery time-window you are planning.5:30 AM · 7:00 AM · 11:30 AM · 5:00 PMYesChanging it re-filters the board and saves as the working slot. Defaults to 7:00 AM (the morning promise).7:00 AM
SearchFilter the chips on the board by text.Free textOpt.Matches delivery code, customer, area and the assigned executive's name; empty shows all. Client-side only.Benz Circle
Strategy select — liveThe distribution mode the real auto-assign engine uses (see Screen 5).Equal distribution (Startup) · Capacity based · Area based (future) · ManualYesSaved to POST /api/assignments/strategy; audited. Reverts on 403. Only appears when the backend is live.Equal distribution (Startup)
Auto-refresh checkboxRe-pull the board on a timer.On / OffOpt.Local planner refresh every 8 seconds. The live wiring switches this off on load so it does not fight the DB overlay.Off

Generate deliveries (planner only)

The Generate deliveries ▾ button opens a small menu offering 25 · 100 · 500 · 1000 synthetic deliveries. It appends that many confirmed test deliveries to the current slot in the local planner so you can rehearse a busy morning. It does not create real orders.

⚠️
Warning — Reset wipes the planner

Reset asks "Reset all executives, deliveries and the queue?" and, on confirm, clears the entire local planner back to its seed. This is a planner-only action — it does not delete anything from the database — but you will lose any manual drag-drop arrangement you had staged. Use it only to start a fresh rehearsal.

The KPI strip

Eight cards summarise the selected slot. When the backend is live, their numbers are overwritten from GET /api/assignments/dashboard and four extra live-only cards are appended.

CardWhat it showsLive source (dashboard totals)
Total ordersDeliveries in this slot.totals.orders
Total bottlesSum of bottles across those deliveries.totals.totalBottles
Assigned greenBottles already placed on executives.totals.assignedBottles
Pending queue amberBottles waiting for capacity.totals.pendingBottles
Available execsExecutives free to take work (Available + Returned).executiveCounts.available
On route blueExecutives out delivering (Assigned/Accepted/Out-for-delivery).executiveCounts.onRoute
ReturnedExecutives back at the dairy.executiveCounts.returned
Completed greenDeliveries marked delivered.totals.completedDeliveries
Offline execs live onlyExecutives off-shift (Offline/Break).executiveCounts.offline
Assigned orders green live onlyOrder-count view of what is placed.totals.assignedOrders
Unassigned orders amber live onlyOrders still without an executive.totals.unassignedOrders
Completion % blue live onlyAssigned ÷ total orders.totals.completionPct

Buttons on this screen

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
⚡ Auto-assignRuns assignment for the day and refreshes the KPIs from the database.POST /api/assignments/auto for today's slot, then GET /api/assignments/dashboard. Toast reports "N assigned, M queued (E exec)".Super Admin+ deliveries:assign / edit
Assign queue to available execsRe-runs assignment to drain the pending queue onto any executive with room.Same auto-assign pass (planner) / next engine run.Super Admin+
Generate deliveries ▾Appends 25 / 100 / 500 / 1000 synthetic deliveries to the current slot.Local planner only — logged as a generate activity.Super Admin+
Run testsExecutes the built-in 12-check engine harness and prints "X/12 tests passed · 1k in Nms".Pure in-browser test harness on synthetic state; never touches live data. Hover the chip for the per-test list.Super Admin+
ResetClears the local planner back to seed (with confirm).Planner localStorage only; no DB write.Super Admin+
✅
Best Practice — the morning run

Before the 5:30 AM dispatch: (1) confirm executives are marked Available, (2) pick the correct Slot, (3) press ⚡ Auto-assign, (4) clear any Pending queue either by freeing more executives or by dragging chips manually, then (5) step each executive to Accepted → Out For Delivery as they leave. Aim to reach 0 unassigned orders before the vans roll.

Executive trip card & status workflow

One card per executive showing live load, route and stops, with buttons that step the trip through its life-cycle.

Purpose Read an executive's fill at a glance and advance their trip: Available → Assigned → Accepted → Out For Delivery → Completed → Returned To Dairy.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager — status changes are delivery-management actions. Path Admin → Operations → Auto Assignment → (executive card)
Card — Executive name · zone · home area · status badge
Capacity bar"N / 45 bottles · P%" — turns high (≥80%) then full (≥100%)
Route summary"K stops · ~D km route" (nearest-neighbour), or "No active trip"
Stops (drop zone)One chip per delivery: bottle count · customer · area · lock · unassign; "Drop deliveries here" when empty
Status buttonsThe next allowed step(s) plus Break / Go available
Figure — anatomy of an executive trip card.

What each region tells you

RegionReadsDetail
HeaderExecutive name, zone and home area, and a colour status badge.Badge tone: green = Available / Completed / Returned; amber = Assigned; blue = Accepted / Out For Delivery; grey = Offline / Break.
Capacity barBottles carried out of the capacity, as a filled bar and "N / 45 bottles · P%".Fill tone flips to high at 80% and full at 100% so an over-loaded card is obvious. Capacity is the driver's DeliveryCapacity, default 45.
Route summaryStop count and an estimated route distance in km.Stops are ordered by nearest-neighbour from the executive's home point; distance is the summed leg length. "No active trip" when empty.
StopsThe delivery chips on this trip (a drop target for drag-drop).Each chip is draggable and carries its own lock and unassign controls (Screen 4).

Status buttons

The card only ever offers the next legal step for the current status, so the workflow cannot be skipped. Advancing the status cascades onto the deliveries on that trip.

ButtonWhat it doesEffect on deliveriesPermission
Accepted →Executive has accepted the assigned trip.Trip stays assigned; executive badge turns blue.Super Admin+
Out For Delivery →Executive has left the dairy.Every stop on the trip moves to Out For Delivery.Super Admin+
Completed →All stops delivered.Every stop moves to Delivered.Super Admin+
Returned To Dairy →Executive is back and free.Archives the trip to history, frees the executive to 0 bottles, then auto-pulls the next batch from the queue (Screen 3).Super Admin+
BreakTakes an Available executive off work.Executive can no longer receive assignments until brought back.Super Admin+
Go availableBrings an Offline / Break executive back on shift.Executive re-enters the assignable pool.Super Admin+
Workflow — executive status life-cycle
AvailableAssignedAcceptedOut For DeliveryCompletedReturned To Dairy↺ pull queue → Assigned / Available
Figure — an executive advances one step at a time; only Available and Returned To Dairy executives can receive new work.
ℹ️
Note — who can receive work

Auto-assign only loads executives whose status is Available or Returned To Dairy. Once an executive is Accepted, Out For Delivery, Offline or on Break they are skipped, and their in-flight deliveries are protected from re-assignment. The working set is captured once at the start of a pass, so an executive keeps filling toward 45 even after their badge flips to Assigned.

Pending Assignment Queue & return-trip continuation

Overflow that could not fit any trip, and the automatic re-fill when an executive returns to the dairy.

Purpose Make sure nothing is dropped: capacity overflow waits in the queue and is pulled onto the next free executive, nearest-zone first.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager Path Admin → Operations → Auto Assignment → Pending Assignment Queue
Panel — Pending Assignment Queue · badge "N bottles"
Queue drop zoneOne chip per queued delivery (bottle count · customer · area); "Queue empty — everything assigned ✓" when clear
Assign queue button"Assign queue to available execs" — appears only while the queue has items
Figure — the pending queue panel in the right-hand column.

Why a delivery is queued

The engine tags every queued delivery with a reason so you know how to clear it.

ReasonMeaningHow to clear it
capacity_fullEvery available executive reached 45 bottles.Bring more executives on shift, or wait for one to return.
no_executiveNo executive was available at all.Mark an executive Available and re-run auto-assign.
oversizeThe single delivery is larger than the biggest capacity — it can never fit one trip.Split the order, or raise that driver's capacity on the Drivers screen.
locked_capacityThe delivery is locked to an executive who has no room.Unlock it, or free capacity on that executive.
manual_unassignAn admin unassigned it back to the queue (priority 1).Re-run auto-assign or drag it onto an executive.

Return-trip continuation

When an executive is stepped to Returned To Dairy, the system closes their trip and immediately tries to start a fresh one from the queue. It pulls queued deliveries preferring the executive's own zone, then nearest, then queue priority and FIFO order, up to the full 45-bottle capacity. If nothing fits, the executive simply goes back to Available.

Workflow — return trip pulls the next batch
Executive returnsTrip archived to historyExecutive freed to 0 bottlesQueue sorted (zone → nearest → priority → FIFO)Fill to 45New trip assigned + executive notified
Figure — an empty queue leaves the returned executive Available; a full queue starts a new trip automatically.
ℹ️
Note — nothing is ever lost

Overflow is captured, not dropped. In the live engine, DeliveryAssignment.deliveryId and AssignmentQueue.deliveryId are unique, so a delivery can appear in exactly one place — assigned or queued — and two admins clicking at once can never double-assign it. When queued bottles cross 50, admins get a "queue growing" alert; if there are no executives at all, they get a "no executives" alert.

Manual override — drag-drop, lock, unassign, reassign

Hands-on control when the automatic plan needs a human decision.

Purpose Move a delivery between executives, push it back to the queue, or pin it so auto-assign leaves it alone.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager — overrides need deliveries:assign or deliveries:edit. Path Admin → Operations → Auto Assignment → (delivery chip)
Chip — bottles · customer · area · 🔓 lock · ✕ unassign (draggable)
Drag to another cardReassign the delivery to that executive (capacity-checked)
Drag to the queueUnassign and park in Pending Assignment Queue
🔒 / 🔓 LockPin the delivery to its executive so auto-assign won't move it
✕ UnassignFree the delivery back to unassigned / queue
Figure — a delivery chip and the four override gestures.

Override actions

Button / gestureWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Drag chip → executive cardMoves the delivery onto that executive.POST /api/assignments/override action reassign (or manual for a queued one). Refused with a toast if it would exceed 45 or the chip is locked.Super Admin+
Drag chip → queueRemoves it from its executive and parks it in the pending queue.unassign then enqueue (reason manual_unassign, priority 1).Super Admin+
🔒 / 🔓 LockToggles a lock so auto-assign and reassign leave the delivery on its current executive.override action lock ({ locked: true/false }). A locked chip refuses reassign unless force is passed, and refuses unassign outright.Super Admin+
✕ UnassignFrees the delivery from the executive.override action unassign; delivery returns to SCHEDULED and to the queue. Blocked if locked.Super Admin+
⚠️
Warning — capacity and locks are enforced server-side

A manual move that would push the target past capacity is rejected with a 409 and a message like "Over capacity: needs 6, only 3 left." A locked assignment cannot be reassigned (unless forced) or unassigned. Both the executive who loses a delivery and the one who gains it are notified, and every move is written to the assignment log with your role attached.

✅
Best Practice — lock the fragile ones

For VIP customers, cold-chain-critical orders or a stop an executive knows personally, lock the chip after placing it. That guarantees the next auto-assign run and any queue pull will route around it instead of shuffling it to another van.

Assignment strategy — the distribution mode switch

Choose how the engine spreads work: evenly (Startup Mode), by capacity, by area, or fully manual.

Purpose Change how auto-assign distributes deliveries without a code deploy — the setting is stored in the database and applies to every future run.
Access Super Admin Admin — reading it needs deliveries:view; changing it needs deliveries:assign/edit and is audited. Path Admin → Operations → Auto Assignment → Strategy

The four modes

StrategyHow work is spreadWhen to use it
Equal distribution (Startup) defaultBalances today's deliveries by order count across all available executives so the difference between any two is never more than one — areas and routes ignored, but the 45-bottle cap still holds.A small, early-stage fleet where fairness of workload matters more than route efficiency. This is the default.
Capacity basedPacks each executive as full as possible before opening the next (fuller trips, fewer vans on the road); locality bias is switched off.When you want to minimise the number of executives out delivering.
Area based (future)The enterprise locality/zone-affine, route-optimised planner: groups by neighbourhood, orders stops nearest-neighbour, keeps a zone on one van.A larger operation where drive time and geographic clustering drive cost. Kept fully functional for when the fleet scales.
ManualAuto-assign becomes a no-op — you assign every delivery by hand from the board.Special days (festivals, disruptions) when a human must place every stop.
Workflow — running auto-assign with a strategy
Pick StrategySaved to AppSetting (audited)Press ⚡ Auto-assignEngine reads strategyDeliveries distributed (or queued)Executives notified
Figure — the strategy is read fresh on every auto-assign run.
ℹ️
Note — Manual mode reports honestly

With Manual selected, pressing ⚡ Auto-assign does nothing and returns "Manual mode — auto-assignment is switched off. Assign deliveries from the board." Nothing is queued or moved. Switch back to Equal or Capacity to resume automatic distribution.

💡
Tip — it is a global, sticky setting

The strategy lives in one AppSetting row (assignment.strategy), so it survives page reloads and applies for everyone until changed. Changing it is recorded in the audit trail as assignment.strategy.update with the old → new value, so you can always see who switched modes and when.

Where the data lives

The live engine reads and writes these tables inside race-safe, serialisable transactions. Attribution (who ran what) rides on your role, recorded on every log row.

TableHolds
DeliveryThe order to deliver — its slot, bottle count, status and assigned driver.
DeliveryAssignmentOne row per placed delivery — driver, trip, slot, bottles, sequence, area/zone, lock flag. deliveryId is unique.
AssignmentQueueOverflow waiting for capacity — bottles, area, reason and priority. deliveryId is unique.
ExecutiveStatusEach executive's live availability, current trip and bottles carried.
TripHistoryArchived trips — driver, slot, date, bottles, stops and status.
AssignmentLogAppend-only record of every action (AUTO_ASSIGN, QUEUE, MANUAL_ASSIGN, REASSIGN, UNASSIGN, LOCK/UNLOCK, RETURN_TRIP, DEQUEUE) with the actor's role.
DeliveryCapacityPer-driver maximum bottles (default 45).
AppSettingThe single assignment.strategy key that stores the distribution mode.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Banner reads "⚠ Backend offline — showing the local planner simulation."The DOODLY API is unreachable from the page.Confirm the backend is running and reachable; the board keeps working as a planner meanwhile, but ⚡ Auto-assign will not touch real orders until it reconnects.
⚡ Auto-assign toast: "Your role can't run auto-assignment (403)."Your role lacks deliveries:assign / deliveries:edit.Sign in as Super Admin or Admin, or have your permissions granted on the Roles screen.
Everything lands in the pending queue.No executive is Available (all Offline / on Break / out on a trip).Mark executives Available (or Go available), then re-run auto-assign or press "Assign queue to available execs".
A delivery will not move to a van.Target is full, or the chip is locked.Free capacity on the target or drop onto a less-loaded card; unlock the chip (🔓) if it is pinned.
A big order stays queued with reason oversize.It needs more bottles than any single capacity.Split the order across trips, or raise that driver's DeliveryCapacity on the Drivers screen.
KPIs look stale.Live auto-refresh is off (it is disabled under the live overlay by design).Press ⚡ Auto-assign or reopen the page to re-pull the dashboard.

Frequently asked questions

Why is capacity counted in bottles instead of customers?

Because the physical constraint of a delivery trip is how many glass bottles fit on the vehicle, not how many stops there are. One customer might take six bottles and another one — the engine packs to a 45-bottle ceiling so a van is never over-loaded regardless of how those bottles are split across stops.

Do I have to press Auto-assign again when an executive comes back?

No. Stepping an executive to Returned To Dairy automatically archives their finished trip and pulls the next batch from the queue for them — nearest-zone first, up to capacity. You only run auto-assign again to distribute newly confirmed orders or to drain the queue onto other free executives.

What is the difference between the "Assigned" and "Assigned orders" cards?

Assigned counts bottles already loaded onto executives; Assigned orders (a live-only card) counts deliveries. The pair, together with Completion %, lets you judge both the volume on the road and how many stops are still unplaced.
