Is the drag-drop board changing real assignments?

The drag-drop arrangement is staged in the planner. Per-delivery override gestures (reassign / unassign / lock) are wired to /api/assignments/override and do change the live plan; the ⚡ Auto-assign button and the KPI/Strategy overlay are the other live paths. Generate deliveries, Run tests and Reset are always planner-only.

DOODLYAdmin · Operations · Late Delivery Monitoring
§Admin · Operations

Late Delivery Monitoring

Protects the 7:00 AM morning-milk promise — it auto-detects deliveries that arrive after the promised time, sends the customer one apology, scores each Delivery Executive, escalates repeat offenders, and reports the trend across routes, areas and customers.

ℹ️
Note — what this module measures

DOODLY promises every morning milk delivery before 7:00 AM. A delivery is "late" when its actual delivery time is after the configured promise time plus any grace minutes. Delivered exactly at 7:00 AM counts as on-time. The module never changes an order or a payment — it only monitors timing, notifies customers, and holds executives accountable.

💡
How the data reaches this screen

In production the monitor reads real Delivery records from the last 30 days (up to 500) and compares each completed delivery's time against the SLA promise from Admin → Catalogue → Delivery Settings. Live driver completions also flow in the moment an executive marks a stop delivered, so a late delivery is flagged and the apology fires automatically — no one has to press a button.

Screen 1 — Monitoring Dashboard

Today's on-time health at a glance, plus the executives, routes and areas that need attention.

Purpose Give the manager a one-look answer to "did we keep the 7 AM promise today, and who is dragging the numbers down?"
Access Super AdminAdmin view Path Admin → Operations → Late Deliveries → Dashboard
Late Delivery Monitoring — Dashboard · tabs: Dashboard · Late Deliveries · Executive Performance · Reports · Configuration · Run tests
KPI strip (5)Today's Deliveries · On-Time · Late · Avg Delay · On-Time Rate %
Top performing executivesBest 3 execs by score, with band + on-time% + late count
Executives requiring attentionFlagged / Critical / Needs-Improvement execs, worst first, with escalation banner
Routes with frequent delaysTop 5 routes this month by late count & late %
Areas with frequent delaysTop 5 areas this month by late count & late %
Figure 1 — Dashboard tab: a KPI strip over four insight panels.

The five KPI cards summarise today only:

KPI cardWhat it showsHow it is computed
Today's DeliveriesCount of deliveries scheduled/completed todayAll of today's rows in the 30-day window
On-TimeDeliveries delivered at or before the deadlineToday total − today late
LateDeliveries past the promise time (turns red when > 0)Today rows where actual time > promise + grace
Avg DelayAverage minutes late across today's late deliveriesSum of delay minutes ÷ number of late deliveries
On-Time RatePercentage on-time today (green ≥ 95%, else amber)On-time ÷ total × 100

The two executive panels rank by performance score. "Executives requiring attention" lists anyone who is flagged, Critical or Needs Improvement, worst score first, and shows a "⚠ Escalation threshold exceeded" banner when the monthly limits are breached. If everyone is inside their thresholds the panel reads "All executives within thresholds. 👍".

✅
Best Practice — start every morning here

Open the Dashboard just after the 7:00 AM window closes. If "Late" is above zero, jump straight to the Late Deliveries tab, add a reason to each row, and confirm the apology went out. A clean daily habit keeps the on-time rate visible to the whole team.

Screen 2 — Late Deliveries Log

The filterable list of every late delivery, with the apology status and a click-through detail card.

Purpose Investigate individual late deliveries, record why they happened, review or resolve them, and send or resend the customer apology.
Access Super AdminAdmin view; recording a reason / review / resolve / apology needs the edit permission Path Admin → Operations → Late Deliveries → Late Deliveries
Late Deliveries — filters + table · CSV · Excel · PDF
Search boxFree text over customer, executive, area, route, ID, reason
Filter rowPeriod · Executive · Area · Status · Notified
Count + Clear + Export"N late deliveries" · Clear filters · CSV / Excel / PDF
Data table (10 columns)Date · Customer · Executive · Area · Scheduled · Actual · Delay · Reason · Apology · Status
Pager25 rows per page · ‹ Prev / Next › · "Page X of Y"
Row click → detail cardOpens the late-delivery modal (see Screen 3)
Figure 2 — Late Deliveries tab: search, five filters, a 10-column table and a pager.

Table specification

PropertyDetail
ColumnsDate (with delivery ID beneath), Customer (with customer ID), Executive, Area (with route beneath), Scheduled, Actual, Delay, Reason, Apology, Status
SearchSingle box; matches across customer, executive, area, route, delivery ID and reason (case-insensitive)
FiltersPeriod (Today / This Week / This Month / 30 Days / All) · Executive · Area · Status (Late / Reviewed / Resolved) · Notified (All / Sent / Not sent)
SortingFixed — newest date first, then longest delay first
Pagination25 rows per page with Prev / Next controls
Row actionsClick any row to open the detail card; an empty result shows "No late deliveries 🎉"
ExportsCSV, Excel (.xls) and PDF (browser print). Export honours the current filters and is audited

How each cell reads

ColumnMeaningDisplay rule
DelayMinutes past the deadlineAmber badge, or red when the delay is 20 minutes or more
ApologyWhether the customer apology was sentGreen "Sent" badge, or grey "No"
StatusWhere the incident standsAmber "Late" (open) → blue "Reviewed" → green "Resolved"
ReasonRecorded causeReason text, or "—" until a manager records one
ℹ️
Note — the "Notified" filter

"Sent" lists deliveries where the apology has already gone out; "Not sent" surfaces late deliveries that still owe the customer an apology — a useful daily clean-up view.

The export files carry these columns in order: Delivery ID, Date, Customer, Customer ID, Executive, Route, Area, Scheduled, Actual, Delay (min), Reason, Notified, Reviewed, Status (Notified and Reviewed print as Yes / No).

Screen 3 — Late Delivery Detail Card

The action modal that opens from any row — record the reason, review/resolve, and send or resend the apology.

Purpose Work a single late delivery end-to-end: understand it, log the cause, notify the customer and close it out.
Access Super AdminAdmin — "Save reason" appears only with the edit permission Path Admin → Operations → Late Deliveries → (click a row)
Late delivery +N min · delivery ID · date · ✕
Facts panelCustomer (ID) · Executive · Route / Area · Scheduled deadline · Actual delivery · Delay duration · Customer notified · Manager reviewed · Status
Reason pickerDropdown of standard reasons
Apology previewThe exact message that will reach the customer, with placeholders filled in
Action footerSave reason · Mark reviewed / Resolve · Send apology / Resend apology · Close
Figure 3 — Detail card: facts, reason picker, apology preview and the action buttons.

Reason field

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
ReasonRecords why the delivery was late so trends can be analysedTraffic · Vehicle Breakdown · Heavy Rain · Route Change · Milk Collection Delay · Customer Unavailable · OtherOpt.Saved against the delivery; writes an audited "Reason Added" record (old → new value). On the server a reason can also accompany an escalation (max 500 characters)Heavy Rain

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save reasonStores the chosen reason on the deliveryRecords the reason; audit "delivery.reason_added" capturing the previous and new valueSuper AdminAdmin edit
Mark reviewedMarks that a manager has looked at the incident; status → ReviewedAudit "delivery.manager_review" (Open → Reviewed)Super AdminAdmin
ResolveCloses the incident; status → Resolved (replaces "Mark reviewed" once reviewed)Audit "delivery.manager_review" (Open → Resolved). Server: the resolve action logs "delivery.late.resolve"Super AdminAdmin
Send apologySends the customer the apology message shown in the previewMarks the delivery notified (once) and audits "delivery.apology_sent" with the channels used. Server: the notify action logs the customer notification to the audit trailSuper AdminAdmin
Resend apologyRe-sends the apology if the customer needs it again (shown once an apology exists)Forces a fresh send even though the delivery is already marked notifiedSuper AdminAdmin
Close / ✕Dismisses the card without changes (also closes on Esc or clicking the backdrop)—Any viewer
⚠️
Warning — one automatic apology per delivery

The system sends exactly one apology per late delivery automatically. Use Resend apology only when the customer genuinely needs another message — repeated apologies for the same delay read as clumsy, not caring.

Screen 4 — Executive Performance

A scorecard per Delivery Executive with an on-time score, a band and an escalation flag.

Purpose Rank executives fairly on punctuality so coaching and escalation target the right people.
Access Super AdminAdmin view Path Admin → Operations → Late Deliveries → Executive Performance
Executive Performance — scope chips: Today · This Week · This Month · 30 Days
Per-executive cardName · zone · score · band badge
Metric grid (8)Total · On-time · Late · On-time % · Avg delay · Longest · Complaints · Avg completion
Flag bannerShown when the monthly late count or on-time rate breaches the escalation thresholds
Figure 4 — Executive Performance: one scorecard per executive, sorted best-score first.
MetricMeaning
Score (0–100)On-time rate for the period, minus a small penalty for each complaint. Drives the band and the sort order
BandExcellent (green) / Good (blue) / Needs Improvement (amber) / Critical (red), decided by the configured thresholds
Total · On-time · LateDelivery counts for the selected period (On-time + Late = Total)
On-time %Share of deliveries that met the deadline
Avg delay / LongestAverage and worst delay in minutes among that executive's late deliveries
ComplaintsCustomer complaints attributed to that executive
Avg completionThe executive's typical delivery clock time
ℹ️
Note — the flag always uses the month

The scope chips change what the metric numbers cover (Today, Week, Month, 30 Days), but the red Flagged banner is always judged on the monthly late count and monthly on-time rate against the escalation thresholds — so a good day never hides a bad month.

Screen 5 — Reports

Five month-to-date breakdowns of the late-delivery trend, exportable to CSV, Excel and PDF.

Purpose See where lateness clusters — which days, executives, routes, areas and customers — to drive process fixes.
Access Super AdminAdmin view; export needs the export permission Path Admin → Operations → Late Deliveries → Reports
Late-delivery reports · this month — CSV · Excel · PDF
Daily late deliveriesLast 14 days: Date · Deliveries · Late · On-time %
Executive performanceExecutive · Late · On-time % · Score
Route performanceRoute · Late · Late % · Avg delay
Area performanceArea · Late · Late % · Avg delay
Customer impactMost-affected 8: Customer · Late · Apologies · Avg time
Figure 5 — Reports tab: five month-to-date tables with export buttons.
Report tableColumnsCoverage
Daily late deliveriesDate · Deliveries · Late · On-time %Rolling 14 days
Executive performanceExecutive · Late · On-time % · Score (band-coloured)This month
Route performanceRoute · Late · Late % · Avg delayThis month, worst first
Area performanceArea · Late · Late % · Avg delayThis month, worst first
Customer impactCustomer · Late · Apologies · Avg timeTop 8 most-affected customers
✅
Best Practice — fix the pattern, not the incident

If one route or area sits at the top of these tables week after week, the fix is usually structural — re-sequence the route, shift the milk-collection cut-off, or reassign the executive — not another apology. Export the Route and Area tables and take them to the operations review.

Screen 6 — Configuration Super Admin only

The promise time, grace, apology message, channels, scoring bands and escalation thresholds that drive the whole module.

Purpose Define what "late" means, what the apology says and where it goes, and the thresholds for scoring and escalation.
Access Super Admin only — the Configuration tab is hidden for every other role Path Admin → Operations → Late Deliveries → Configuration
Delivery promise & monitoring — Save configuration
Promise blockDelivery promise time · Grace period (minutes) · Auto-apology on/off
Apology templateEditable message with {{Customer Name}} and {{Actual Time}} placeholders
Notification channelsIn-App · WhatsApp · SMS · Email checkboxes
Scoring thresholdsExcellent ≥ · Good ≥ · Needs Improvement ≥ · Deduct per complaint
Escalation thresholdsMax late / month · Min on-time rate %
Figure 6 — Configuration tab (Super Admin only): five setting blocks and one Save button.

Configuration fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Delivery promise timeThe morning deadline every delivery must beatTime (HH:MM, 24-hour)YesDefaults to 07:00 if blank. In production this is the slaPromiseTime on Delivery Settings, shared with the SLA engine07:00
Grace period (minutes)Minutes of slack added to the promise before a delivery counts as lateWhole number ≥ 0Opt.Defaults to 0. Deadline = promise time + grace (slaGraceMin)0
Auto-apologyWhether apologies send automatically on a late deliveryEnabled / DisabledYesWhen Disabled, apologies must be sent manually from the detail cardEnabled
Apology message templateThe exact wording the customer receivesFree text with placeholdersYesSupports {{Customer Name}} and {{Actual Time}}, filled in per delivery. Previewed live in the detail card"Dear {{Customer Name}}, we're sorry today's milk reached you at {{Actual Time}}…"
Notification channelsWhere the apology is deliveredIn-App · WhatsApp · SMS · Email (any combination)Opt.Recorded on the apology audit entry. Real WhatsApp / SMS / Email sending depends on the provider keys configured for NotificationsIn-App + WhatsApp
Excellent ≥Score at or above which an executive is rated ExcellentWhole number (0–100)YesDefaults to 9595
Good ≥Score threshold for the Good bandWhole number (0–100)YesDefaults to 8585
Needs Improvement ≥Score threshold for Needs Improvement (below it is Critical)Whole number (0–100)YesDefaults to 7070
Deduct / complaintScore points removed for each customer complaintWhole number ≥ 0Opt.Defaults to 22
Max late / monthMonthly late-delivery count that triggers an escalationWhole number ≥ 1YesDefaults to 30 (falls back to 5 if left blank on save)30
Min on-time rate %Monthly on-time rate below which an executive is escalatedWhole number (0–100)YesDefaults to 9595

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save configurationPersists every setting on this tab and re-runs the monitor against the new rulesWrites the config, clears the cached delivery calculations, and audits "settings.update" (System Settings Modified)Super Admin
⛔
Caution — the promise time is a company-wide commitment

Changing the Delivery promise time or Grace period instantly re-scores every executive and re-labels history as more or less late. It shares the same SLA value used across Delivery Settings and the late-detection engine, so change it only with an operational decision behind it — not to make a bad week look better.

💡
Tip — keep the score bands in order

Set the thresholds so Excellent ≥ Good ≥ Needs Improvement. If they overlap or invert, executives can land in the wrong band. The shipped 95 / 85 / 70 ladder is a sensible default for a fresh setup.

Screen 7 — Customer "Your delivery quality" panel

The read-only summary a signed-in customer sees on their account page — built from their own deliveries.

Purpose Show each customer, honestly, how well DOODLY kept the 7 AM promise for them.
Access Customer (their own data only) Path Account → Dashboard → Your delivery quality
Your delivery quality — last 30 days
Stat cards (5)Deliveries · On-time · Late · Avg time · Apologies
Honest noteA friendly line — "Every delivery arrived before our 7:00 AM promise" or an apology acknowledgement
Figure 7 — The customer's own delivery-quality summary.
ℹ️
Note — real data only, never the demo set

For a genuinely signed-in customer this panel is computed from their own delivered records against the 7:00 AM promise. Before any deliveries exist it says the quality will appear once deliveries begin — it never borrows the monitoring sample data.

End-to-end workflow — from a late stop to a closed incident

Workflow — automatic late detection, apology and escalation
Executive marks stop delivered Actual time vs promise + grace Late? create delay record Send one apology (if auto-apology on) Log against the executive Audit the incident Over monthly threshold? escalate (once) Manager adds reason → review → resolve
Figure 8 — The detector runs on every completed delivery; managers finish the loop from the detail card.

Late detection is a simple, transparent rule: delay = actual delivery time − (promise time + grace). If the delay is greater than zero the delivery is late; delivered at or before the deadline is on-time. On the server, a delivery still un-delivered after the promise time shows as "Pending · late" so a stuck stop is visible before it is even completed; a FAILED delivery shows as "Failed" and a SKIPPED one as "Rescheduled".

Workflow — escalation guard
Monthly late count ≥ Max late / month OR monthly on-time % < Min on-time rate Already escalated this month? stop Raise escalation (once per executive per month) Flag on dashboard + alert Super Admin & Operations
Figure 9 — Escalation fires at most once per executive per month. On the server it also opens a HIGH-priority Delivery Issue.
ℹ️
Note — how the parts connect in production

The read screens are served by GET /api/admin/deliveries/late (needs deliveries:view), which reads real Delivery rows, applies the SLA from Delivery Settings, and returns the stats, the late list and the per-executive performance. The detail-card actions call POST /api/admin/deliveries/[id]/late-action (needs deliveries:edit) with notify, escalate or resolve. An escalate opens a HIGH-priority Delivery Issue for that driver; notify and resolve are written to the audit trail. No new database tables were added — escalations reuse Delivery Issues and notifications ride on the audit log.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Deliveries that were clearly on time show as "Late"Promise time or grace is set too tight, or the delivery clock is in a different time zoneCheck Configuration → Delivery promise time / Grace; delivered-at times are read in India Standard Time on the server
No apology went out for a late deliveryAuto-apology is disabled, or the apology was already sent onceTurn on Auto-apology in Configuration, or use Send apology in the detail card; the "Notified · Not sent" filter lists everyone still owed one
Sending a second apology does nothingOne automatic apology per delivery is the ruleUse Resend apology in the detail card to force another send
The Configuration tab is missingYou are not signed in as Super AdminConfiguration is Super-Admin-only; ask a Super Admin to change the settings
An executive is not flagged despite many late daysThe flag is judged monthly, and the monthly totals are still under both thresholdsReview Max late / month and Min on-time rate %; lower them if your service level demands it
The screen shows no deliveries at allNo real Delivery records fall in the last 30 days (a fresh or off-day environment)Monitoring only shows real completions; the empty state is expected until deliveries exist
Export button does nothing / says no permissionYour role lacks the export permissionAsk a Super Admin to grant export on the Late Deliveries module in Roles & Permissions

Frequently asked questions

Is a delivery at exactly 7:00 AM counted as late?

No. Delivered at or before the promise time (plus any grace) is on-time. Only a delivery strictly after the deadline is late.

Does changing the grace period rewrite the past?

Yes. The delay for every delivery is recalculated live against the current promise time and grace, so history is re-labelled the moment you save. Adjust these settings deliberately.

Who gets told when an executive is escalated?

The dashboard shows an escalation banner and an on-screen alert flags the executive to the Super Admin and Operations. In production the escalation also opens a HIGH-priority Delivery Issue for that driver, which the operations team works from the delivery tools.

How is the executive score calculated?

It starts from the on-time rate for the selected period and subtracts the configured points for each complaint, clamped between 0 and 100. That score then maps to the Excellent / Good / Needs Improvement / Critical bands.

Can the apology message be customised per customer?

The wording is one shared template, but it automatically fills in each customer's name and the actual delivery time via the {{Customer Name}} and {{Actual Time}} placeholders, so every message reads personally.

✅
Best Practice — close the loop, don't just apologise

An apology soothes today; a recorded reason plus a Resolve builds the trend that fixes tomorrow. Make it routine: for every late delivery, pick a reason, confirm the apology, and resolve the incident once it is understood. The Reports tab then turns that discipline into the insight that protects the 7 AM promise.

DOODLYAdmin · Operations · Serviceable Areas
§Admin · Operations

Serviceable Areas

The map of where DOODLY delivers — the list of pincodes you cover, the delivery charge, slot and ETA for each, the zone it belongs to, plus the customer waitlist for areas you have not reached yet. Add a pincode here and the storefront starts accepting orders from it the same instant, with no code change and no redeploy.

ℹ️
What this screen controls

This page is the single source of truth for delivery coverage. Whenever a customer types their pincode on the website — on the home-page checker, at checkout, or while adding an address — the storefront asks this list whether it can deliver there. A brand-new employee can think of it as: "the switchboard that turns delivery on or off for each pincode, and the sign-up sheet for people who want us in their area." Turn a pincode on and its customers can order; turn it off (or remove it) and checkout politely refuses and offers the waitlist instead.

ℹ️
Where the data lives

Every serviceable pincode is one row in the ServiceablePincode database table, grouped into DeliveryZone records (the same zones the routing and address systems use). The screen reads the list with GET /api/admin/delivery/pincodes and the zones with GET /api/admin/delivery/zones. Adding, editing, toggling and removing rows call POST, PATCH and DELETE /api/admin/delivery/pincodes — and each write is recorded in the audit log. The public storefront checks the very same table through GET /api/geo/serviceable?pincode=, which is why your changes go live instantly.

⚠️
The waitlist is stored in the browser, not the database

Unlike the pincode list, the waitlist capture (name, mobile, e-mail and locality of people in areas you don't yet cover) is saved in the visitor's own browser storage and mirrored into the admin panel. There is no server table for it. That is why the only thing you can do with it here is read it and Export CSV — always export before clearing browser data, or the entries are gone.

Serviceable Areas — coverage board

The live KPI strip, the editable pincode table, and the waitlist — all on one page.

Purpose See coverage at a glance, add or edit a serviceable pincode, enable or disable delivery for it, remove one you no longer serve, and review who is waiting for areas you have not launched.
Access Super Admin · Admin (add / edit / remove) · Operations (add / edit, but not remove) — every other role is refused (403) Path Admin → Operations → Serviceable Areas (/admin/serviceable-areas.html)
Header — Serviceable Areas · live-status banner (● Live — N serviceable pincode(s) across N zone(s) from the DOODLY database)
KPI strip (six cards)Total Areas · Active Areas · Serviceable Pincodes · Delivery Zones · Customers Covered · Orders Today.
Serviceable areas panelGreen "Live" badge · intro line · one editable table row per pincode.
Editable tableColumns: Pincode · Area · City · State · Zone · Charge · Slot · ETA · On · Delete (×).
Action row+ Add pincode · Save areas · Reset · "✓ Saved — live on storefront" note.
Waitlist panelCount badge · Export CSV · one group per pincode with the people waiting.
Figure — labelled layout of the Serviceable Areas screen
ℹ️
Live vs local

On open, the page loads the pincodes and zones from the database and shows a green banner: "● Live — N serviceable pincode(s) across N zone(s) from the DOODLY database (<api-base>)." If the backend cannot be reached it shows a red banner — "⚠ Backend offline … couldn't load live pincodes." — or, if your role is not allowed, "⚠ Your role can't view serviceable areas (403)." This manual documents the live, database-backed behaviour.

The six coverage KPIs

The strip above the table is calculated fresh from the database every time the page loads (numbers are formatted in the Indian style, e.g. 1,20,000). An "area" is a unique Area + City pair, so two pincodes in the same locality count as one area.

CardWhat it countsSource
Total AreasHow many distinct Area + City localities you have on file (enabled or not), excluding removed rows.stats.totalAreas
Active AreasLocalities that have at least one enabled pincode — i.e. places you are actually delivering to now.stats.activeAreas
Serviceable PincodesThe total number of live (non-removed) pincode rows in the table.stats.total
Delivery ZonesHow many distinct zones your pincodes are grouped into.stats.zones
Customers CoveredDistinct customers whose saved address pincode falls inside an enabled serviceable pincode.stats.customersCovered (from the Address table)
Orders TodayDeliveries scheduled for today across all areas — a quick pulse of activity.stats.ordersToday (from the Delivery table)

The Serviceable-areas table

Each row is one pincode and is inline-editable — you type straight into the cells and click Save areas to push every change to the database at once. The table is not paginated; it shows the whole coverage list, ordered by City then Pincode as returned by the backend.

AspectBehaviour on this screen
ColumnsPincode · Area · City · State · Zone (drop-down) · Charge (₹) · Slot · ETA · On (enable checkbox) · Delete (× button).
SortingFixed — the backend returns rows ordered by City (A→Z) then Pincode (ascending). There are no clickable column sorts on this table.
Search & filtersNo search box on the admin table itself. (The underlying GET /api/admin/delivery/pincodes endpoint does accept search, status = active/inactive/deleted and zoneId filters for API callers.)
PaginationNone — the full list is shown so you always see every covered pincode.
Row actionsEdit any cell inline · toggle the On checkbox to enable/disable delivery · click × to remove the row (takes effect on the next Save).
ExportsThe table has no export. Only the Waitlist panel exports (CSV) — see Screen 3.

Every editable field in a row

These are the exact cells you can edit, with the limits the server enforces (so a bad value can never reach the database). The same fields appear, more clearly labelled, in the Add-pincode modal on Screen 2.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Pincode
data-k="pincode"		 The 6-digit postal code delivery is switched on for. This is the unique key of the row. Exactly six digits Yes Must match /^\d{6}$/; must be unique — a duplicate is rejected with a 409 conflict ("That pincode is already serviceable."). 520013
Area
data-k="area"		 The locality / neighbourhood name shown to customers ("we deliver to <Area>, <City>"). Text Yes Trimmed, 1–80 characters; cannot be blank. Krishnalanka
City
data-k="city"		 The city the pincode sits in. Text Yes Trimmed, 1–80 characters; cannot be blank. New rows default to "Vijayawada". Vijayawada
State
data-k="state"		 The state, shown in the coverage details. Text Opt. Trimmed, max 80 characters. Defaults to "Andhra Pradesh" when left blank. Andhra Pradesh
Zone
data-k="zone"		 The delivery zone this pincode belongs to — used to group areas and link them to an executive and routes. One of the existing zones (drop-down) Opt. Stored as zoneId; may be left as "— No zone —". Zones come from the DeliveryZone table. Central Vijayawada
Charge
data-k="charge"		 The delivery fee charged for this pincode at checkout. Zero means free delivery. Whole rupees, 0 or more Opt. Whole number, clamped to 0–100000 on the server. Defaults to 0. 0
Slot
data-k="slot"		 The delivery window label shown to the customer for this pincode. Free-text label Opt. Trimmed, max 40 characters. Server default "Before 7 AM". 6:00–8:00 AM
ETA
data-k="eta"		 A short "expected by" promise shown alongside the slot. Free-text label Opt. Trimmed, max 40 characters; may be blank (stored as null). By 8:00 AM
On (enabled)
data-k="enabled"		 The master switch for the pincode. Ticked = delivery on and checkout accepts it; un-ticked = checkout refuses and offers the waitlist. On / Off checkbox Opt. Boolean; new rows are on by default. A disabled pincode still appears in your list but is not serviceable to customers. On
💡
Disable vs remove

To pause a pincode (say a zone is temporarily out of stock), just un-tick its On box and Save — the row stays in your list and you can switch it back on later. Use the × Delete only when you genuinely stop serving an area. Deletes are a soft-delete on the server (the row is hidden and disabled but kept for history), so nothing is truly lost.

Buttons & actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
+ Add pincode
#pc-add		 Opens the validated "Add serviceable pincode" modal (Screen 2). It does not add a blank row — the click is intercepted so every new pincode goes through the checked, one-shot form. Opens the modal; the actual save is a POST /api/admin/delivery/pincodes from the modal. Super Admin+ Admin Operations
Save areas
#pc-save		 Reads every row and syncs the whole table to the database in one pass — new pincodes are created, changed ones are updated, and any you removed are deleted. Shows a "✓ Saved — live on storefront" note and a "Serviceable areas synced to the database" toast. For each row: POST (new) or PATCH /api/admin/delivery/pincodes/<id> (existing); any pincode dropped from the list is sent to DELETE. The sync is idempotent and keyed by pincode. Super Admin+ Admin Operations (delete part needs Admin+)
Reset
#pc-reset		 Clears the page's local working copy of the list and re-draws the table from the built-in seed list. On the database-wired admin page this also triggers the same sync as Save, so use it carefully — see the caution below. To simply throw away unsaved edits, reload the page instead. Clears the local override, then the wired page runs the same one-pass sync — POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/delivery/pincodes — over the resulting list. Super Admin+ Admin Operations (the follow-on sync needs create/edit rights)
Delete (×)
.pc-del		 Removes that row from the table. The removal is applied to the database on the next Save areas. DELETE /api/admin/delivery/pincodes/<id> — a soft-delete (sets deletedAt and disables the row). Audited as delivery.pincode.delete. Super Admin+ Admin (Operations cannot delete)
⛔
Caution — "Reset" is not just an undo

On the database-wired page, Reset clears the local working copy so the table falls back to the built-in seed list, and then runs the very same sync as Save over that seed list. In practice this can overwrite your live coverage with the seed defaults and remove pincodes that are not in it (deletes are soft, but the storefront still closes for those areas at once). If you only want to discard edits you have not saved, reload the page instead — that pulls a fresh copy from the database without writing anything.

⚠️
Operations can add and edit, but not remove

The Operations role has "manage" rights on Serviceable Areas — it can view, add, edit and enable/disable pincodes. It does not have delete rights. A DELETE from that role is refused with a 403. Only Admin and Super Admin can remove a pincode.

Add serviceable pincode — modal

The validated, one-shot form for launching delivery in a new pincode.

Purpose Add a single pincode to your coverage with all the details filled in and checked before it is saved — so the storefront starts accepting orders from it the moment you click Add.
Access Super Admin · Admin · Operations Path Admin → Operations → Serviceable Areas → + Add pincode
Dialog — Add serviceable pincode · close (×)
Row 1Pincode * · Zone (drop-down).
Area *Full-width locality name.
Row 2City * (default Vijayawada) · State (default Andhra Pradesh).
Row 3Delivery charge (₹) · ETA.
Delivery slotFull-width slot label.
Serviceable checkbox"Serviceable (enabled on storefront)" — ticked by default.
FooterInline error line · Cancel · Add pincode.
Figure — labelled layout of the Add-pincode modal

Every field in the modal

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Pincode
#dap-pin		 The postal code to switch on. Exactly 6 digits (non-digits are stripped as you type) Yes Client checks the 6-digit format and that it is not already in your list, then the server re-checks format and uniqueness (409 if a duplicate). 520013
Zone
#dap-zone		 Which delivery zone to file the pincode under. "— No zone —" or an existing zone Opt. Sent as zoneId; options are loaded live from GET /api/admin/delivery/zones. Central Vijayawada
Area
#dap-area		 The locality name customers will see. Text Yes Required (1–80 chars). A blank Area shows "Area is required." and blocks the save. Krishnalanka
City
#dap-city		 The city. Text (pre-filled "Vijayawada") Yes Required (1–80 chars). A blank City shows "City is required." Vijayawada
State
#dap-state		 The state. Text (pre-filled "Andhra Pradesh") Opt. Max 80 chars; falls back to "Andhra Pradesh" if cleared. Andhra Pradesh
Delivery charge (₹)
#dap-charge		 The delivery fee at checkout for this pincode. Whole rupees (non-digits stripped), default 0 Opt. Coerced to a whole number ≥ 0 and clamped to 100000 on the server. 0
ETA
#dap-eta		 The "expected by" promise line. Free text (pre-filled "By 8:00 AM") Opt. Trimmed, max 40 chars; blank is stored as null. By 8:00 AM
Delivery slot
#dap-slot		 The delivery window label. Free text (pre-filled "6:00–8:00 AM") Opt. Trimmed, max 40 chars; server default "Before 7 AM" if blank. 6:00–8:00 AM
Serviceable (enabled)
#dap-enabled		 Whether the pincode is live on the storefront immediately. On / Off checkbox (ticked by default) Opt. Sent as enabled. Leave ticked to launch straight away; un-tick to add it but keep delivery off for now. On

Modal buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Add pincode
#dap-save		 Validates the fields, saves the new pincode, reloads the coverage board and shows a toast — e.g. "520013 · Krishnalanka added — live on the storefront". The button reads "Adding…" while it works. POST /api/admin/delivery/pincodes with all the fields. On success the board is refreshed; audited as delivery.pincode.create. Super Admin+ Admin Operations
Cancel / close (×) Closes the modal without saving. Pressing Esc or clicking the dim background does the same. None. Any viewer
ℹ️
Friendly error messages

The modal turns backend errors into plain English on the inline error line: a duplicate reads "<pincode> is already serviceable.", a permission problem reads "Your role can't add pincodes (403).", and a connection problem reads "Backend offline — can't add right now." The Enter key inside any field submits the form, so you can add a pincode without reaching for the mouse.

Waitlist — demand for areas you don't cover yet

Everyone who asked to be notified when DOODLY reaches their pincode.

Purpose See where customer demand is building so you know which pincode to launch next, and export the contacts so you can tell them the day you go live.
Access Super Admin · Admin · Operations Path Admin → Operations → Serviceable Areas → Waitlist panel
Panel — Waitlist · total-count badge · Export CSV
Pincode group headerPincode · known area (or "Unknown area") · "N waiting" badge.
Group rowsEach person: Name · Mobile · Email (or —) · date added.
Empty state"No waitlist requests yet." when nobody has signed up.
Figure — labelled layout of the Waitlist panel
ℹ️
How people get onto the waitlist

When a visitor checks a pincode on the storefront and it is not serviceable, the checker shows a short form — Full name, Mobile, Email, Pincode and locality — under the message "Join the waitlist and we'll tell you the moment we launch near you." Name and Mobile are required. On submit they see "You're on the waitlist — we'll be in touch the moment we launch." Those entries are what appear here, grouped by pincode and sorted so the areas with the most demand are easy to spot.

Waitlist actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Export CSV
#pc-csv		 Downloads the whole waitlist as doodly-waitlist.csv with the columns Name, Mobile, Email, Address, Pincode and Date — ready for a "we've launched near you!" campaign. Client-side download; no server call. Values are quoted so commas in an address don't break the file. Any viewer of this page
💡
Use the waitlist to choose your next launch

The "N waiting" badge on each pincode group is a ready-made demand signal. When one pincode has clearly the most people waiting, add it on Screen 2, then export the CSV and reach out to exactly those contacts — you launch where demand already exists.

How coverage flows end-to-end

From adding a pincode here to a customer being able to order.

Purpose Understand exactly what happens on the storefront the moment you change coverage.
Access Super Admin and other allowed rolesPath Admin → Operations → Serviceable Areas
Workflow — launching (and gating) a pincode
Add / enable a pincode here Saved to ServiceablePincode (database) Customer types their pincode on the storefront Storefront asks GET /api/geo/serviceable Serviceable → checkout allowed, shows area · slot · ETA · charge Not serviceable → checkout refuses, waitlist form shown Waitlist entry appears here → Export CSV → launch there next
Figure — the add → live → order (or waitlist) path
ℹ️
One list, checked everywhere

The same coverage list gates the home-page pincode checker, the checkout, and the address picker. Because they all read the live database through GET /api/geo/serviceable, there is never a stale copy: enable a pincode and orders open immediately; disable or remove it and checkout closes for that area at once, with the waitlist offered in its place.

Who can do what

RoleViewAddEdit / enable / disableRemove (delete)
Super AdminYesYesYesYes
AdminYesYesYesYes
OperationsYesYesYesNo (403)
All other rolesNo (403)NoNoNo
✅
Best Practice

Before adding a new pincode, decide its zone, delivery charge and slot up front so customers see consistent promises across an area — two pincodes in the same locality should share the same charge and window. When you retire an area, prefer disabling (un-tick On) over deleting so the row and its history stay available if you return. After every Save, confirm the green "Serviceable areas synced to the database" toast, then open the storefront checker in another tab and type the pincode to verify the customer-facing result matches what you set.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner "⚠ Your role can't view serviceable areas (403)."The signed-in role has no serviceableAreas:view right.Sign in as Super Admin, Admin or Operations to view the page.
Add pincode fails with "…is already serviceable."That pincode already exists in the list (it is the unique key).Find the existing row and edit it instead of adding a new one.
Delete (×) does nothing / errors with 403.The role is Operations, which cannot delete.Ask an Admin or Super Admin to remove the pincode.
Customer says checkout won't accept a pincode you added.Its On box is un-ticked, or Save wasn't clicked, or the change didn't reach the database.Tick On, click Save areas, confirm the sync toast, then re-check on the storefront.
Waitlist is empty on another computer.The waitlist lives in the browser that captured it, not the database.Export the CSV from the machine that has the entries; don't rely on it appearing everywhere.
Red banner "⚠ Backend offline … couldn't load live pincodes."The API is unreachable.Restore connectivity and reload the page.

Frequently asked questions

Q: Does disabling a pincode cancel the orders already placed from it?

A: No. The On switch only decides whether new checkouts are accepted from that pincode. Orders and subscriptions already placed are unaffected — disable it and existing deliveries carry on while new orders from the area are turned away.

Q: If I delete a pincode by mistake, is it gone forever?

A: Not physically. Delete is a soft-delete — the row is hidden and disabled but kept in the database (with a deletedAt stamp) for history, and the server supports restoring it. Re-adding the same pincode is also always possible.

Q: The charge and slot I set here — do they actually reach checkout?

A: Yes. The public serviceability check returns the pincode's area, city, state, delivery charge, slot and ETA, so the customer sees exactly the values you entered and is charged the delivery fee you set.

Q: What does a Zone add beyond grouping?

A: A zone ties a set of pincodes to a delivery executive and to routes, and it drives the "Delivery Zones" KPI. Leaving a pincode with no zone still makes it serviceable; assigning one keeps your coverage organised for routing and reporting.

DOODLYAdmin · Operations · Drivers
§Admin · Operations

Drivers

Onboard and manage your delivery executives — the people who carry the morning A2 buffalo milk from the dairy to each customer's door — with a live fleet dashboard, one-page profiles, zone and capacity settings, password resets and safe soft-deletes.

ℹ️
One person, several records — no duplicate data

A "driver" (the app calls them Delivery Executives) is not a single table. Each driver links a login User account (role DELIVERY_EXECUTIVE) to a Driver record, plus a Delivery Capacity (how many bottles they can carry), an Executive Status (are they available right now) and their Deliveries for the day. The Drivers screen stitches all of these together so you edit one profile and every part stays in step. Nothing here keeps a second copy of the delivery board.

Drivers board

Live list of every delivery executive, a seven-tile KPI strip and a per-driver Manage action.

Purpose See the whole delivery fleet at a glance — who is available, busy or offline, how far each has got today, their rating — and open any driver to edit, reset or retire them.
Access Super Admin Admin Operations — view, add and edit. Soft-delete / restore needs Super Admin or Admin. Path Admin → Operations → Drivers
Header — Delivery Executives · "Manage drivers and assignments." · Add driver button
KPI stripSeven live tiles: Total Drivers · Active · Available · Busy · Offline · Deliveries Today · Completed Today
Live status bannerGreen "● Live — N delivery executive(s) from the DOODLY database" or a red offline / 403 / error message
ToolbarSearch box, filter chips (All · On route · Completed · Idle), sort, saved views, favourites and Export
Drivers tableID · Driver · Zone · Progress · Rating · Status · Manage
Figure — labelled layout of the Drivers board

KPI strip

The tiles above the table are drawn live from GET /api/admin/drivers/stats. Every count excludes soft-deleted drivers.

TileWhat it showsHow it is calculated
Total DriversEvery live executiveCount of Driver records that are not soft-deleted
ActiveExecutives switched onCount where the Driver's Active flag is on
AvailableFree to take a run nowActive drivers whose live availability is Available (or Returned-to-dairy)
BusyOn a delivery runDrivers whose availability is Assigned, Accepted, Out-for-delivery or Completed
OfflineNot on shiftDrivers with no live status or an Offline status
Deliveries TodayStops assigned todayCount of today's deliveries that have a driver attached
Completed TodayStops delivered todayCount of today's deliveries marked DELIVERED
ℹ️
Also computed in the background

The same stats call additionally works out On leave, Suspended, Pending (today's stops not yet delivered/failed/skipped), average delivery time in minutes, an on-time percentage and the fleet's average star rating. The strip surfaces the seven tiles above; the extra figures are available in the data for reports and other tools.

Drivers table

AspectBehaviour in this screen
ColumnsID · Driver · Zone · Progress · Rating · Status · Manage
IDThe driver's Employee ID if one was set (for example DRV-02); otherwise a short fallback code built from the record's internal ID
DriverThe person's name with an initials avatar
ZoneThe assigned delivery zone's name, or "—" if none
ProgressToday's stops as completed / assigned — e.g. "12/20 stops"
RatingThe driver's star rating, e.g. "4.8★"
StatusColoured badge — see "Understanding the status badge" below
SearchThe toolbar search box matches across ID, name, phone, email and zone
FiltersFilter chips All · On route · Completed · Idle are declared for this table; the shared DataTable engine also derives facet filters from the visible columns (for example the Status badge)
SortingClick a column header to sort; the shared engine supports column sort and saved views
PaginationOn — long fleets are paged by the DataTable engine
ExportsThe toolbar Export writes the current table page to CSV
Row actionsManage — opens the driver drawer (Screen 3)
ℹ️
The table is powered by the shared DataTable engine

Drivers uses DOODLY's unified table with search, sort, facet filters, saved views, favourites, Export and pagination built in. If that engine has not finished loading, the page still renders a plain table so the data is never invisible.

Understanding the status badge

The Status column condenses two things — whether the account is switched on, and the driver's live availability — into one badge. The rules apply in this order:

BadgeColourShown when
InactiveGreyThe driver's Active flag is off (they are on file but not working)
SuspendedRedTheir login is locked (from the Suspended checkbox) — overrides availability
AvailableGreenActive and free — live status Available, or just returned to the dairy
BusyAmberOn a run — assigned, accepted or out for delivery
On leaveBlueThe driver has flagged a break in the driver app
OfflineGreyActive but not on shift / no live status reported
ℹ️
Availability comes from the driver, status from you

Available / Busy / On leave / Offline are reported by the driver's own app as they work. Inactive and Suspended are set by you from the Manage drawer. That is why a suspended driver always reads red no matter what their app says.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Add driver (header)Opens the "Add delivery executive" dialogOn save → POST /api/admin/driversSuper Admin Admin Operations
Manage (row)Opens the driver drawer to edit, reset the password or soft-deleteReads the loaded driver listSuper Admin Admin Operations
Export (toolbar)Downloads the current table page as a CSV fileClient-side — no server callAnyone who can view the page
✅
Best Practice

Give every executive a clear Employee ID (for example DRV-02) and assign a zone the day you onboard them. The ID becomes their identifier in the table, reports and audit trail, and the zone is what the auto-assignment engine uses to hand them the right stops each morning.

Add delivery executive

Create a driver's login and profile together in one step.

Purpose Onboard a new driver — this creates their delivery-executive login account and their Driver record in a single, safe transaction.
Access Super Admin Admin Operations Path Admin → Operations → Drivers → Add driver
Dialog — Add delivery executive · ✕ close
Full name *Text, e.g. "Anil Sharma"
Email *Login email, e.g. name@example.com
MobileOptional, e.g. +91…
Employee IDOptional, e.g. DRV-02
Vehicle no.Optional, e.g. AP16 CD 1234
Temporary password *Min 8 chars — driver resets on first login
FooterCancel · Add driver
Figure — the Add delivery executive dialog

Fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Full nameThe driver's name shown everywhereTextYesCannot be empty (client); server requires 1–80 charactersAnil Sharma
EmailTheir login username and contact emailA valid email addressYesMust be a valid email; stored lower-case. A duplicate returns "An account with this email already exists."anil@doodly.in
MobileContact number for the driverPhone number, may start with +Opt.Server accepts 7–15 digits (spaces / dashes allowed). A duplicate phone returns a conflict+91 98765 43210
Employee IDShort staff code used as the table IDText, up to 20 charactersOpt.Must be unique — a clash returns "That employee ID is already in use."DRV-02
Vehicle no.The bike / van registration they runText, up to 20 charactersOpt.Trimmed and stored on the Driver recordAP16 CD 1234
Temporary passwordA first-login password you hand overText, at least 8 charactersYesClient blocks under 8; server enforces the full password policy. Saved hashed, and the driver is forced to reset it on first loginWelcome@2026
ℹ️
What "Add driver" creates

On save, DOODLY creates the login User (role DELIVERY_EXECUTIVE, marked to reset the password on first login) and the linked Driver record (Active, with the employee ID and vehicle number) together in one transaction — so you never end up with a half-made account. The action is written to the audit trail as driver.create.

⚠️
Warning — the temporary password is shown only in this dialog

Type a password you can pass to the driver securely, then note it before you close. They must change it the first time they log in. If it is lost, use Reset password in the Manage drawer to issue a fresh one.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Add driverValidates the name, email and password, then creates the account and refreshes the boardPOST /api/admin/drivers → creates User + Driver, audits driver.create, returns the new driverSuper Admin Admin Operations
Cancel / ✕ / EscCloses the dialog without saving——
Workflow — onboarding a driver
Click Add driverEnter name, email, temp password (+ mobile / ID / vehicle)Add driverUser + Driver created & auditedBoard refreshes with the new rowDriver resets password on first login
Figure — from empty dialog to a working delivery-executive login
✅
Best Practice

Use the driver's real work email, add their mobile, and give them a memorable temporary password you can share over a call or in person. Then open Manage straight away to set their zone and daily bottle capacity so the assignment engine can start giving them stops.

Manage driver

The full control drawer for a single executive — edit details, set zone and capacity, reset the password, suspend or soft-delete.

Purpose Everything you need to run one driver's account: change their ID and vehicle, move them to another zone, adjust how many bottles they carry, lock their login, issue a fresh password, or retire them safely.
Access Edit, zone, capacity, suspend & reset password: Super Admin Admin Operations. Soft-delete: Super Admin Admin only. Path Admin → Operations → Drivers → Manage
Drawer — {driver name} · email · phone · N deliveries · rating★ · availability · ✕
Employee IDEditable text
Vehicle no.Editable text
Assigned zoneZone dropdown (— No zone —)
Daily capacity (bottles)Numeric, default 45
ActiveCheckbox — driver on/off
Suspended (locks login)Checkbox — blocks sign-in
FooterReset password · Soft-delete · Close · Save
Figure — the Manage driver drawer

The drawer header carries a read-only summary line — the driver's email, their phone (or "no mobile"), their total lifetime deliveries, their star rating and their current availability label — so you can identify them before making changes.

Editable fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Employee IDChange the staff codeText, up to 20 characters (blank = none)Opt.Must stay unique across drivers — a clash returns "That employee ID is already in use."DRV-02
Vehicle no.Update the vehicle registrationText, up to 20 charactersOpt.Trimmed; blank clears itAP16 CD 1234
Assigned zoneMove the driver to a delivery zonePick a zone / — No zone —Opt.Zone list loaded from GET /api/admin/delivery/zones; saving is audited as driver.assign_zoneVijayawada West
Daily capacity (bottles)How many bottles they can carry per runWhole number, defaults to 45Opt.Clamped to 1–10,000; written to the driver's Delivery Capacity (created if missing); audited as driver.capacity45
ActiveTurn the driver on or offCheckboxOpt.Unticking marks them Inactive (kept on file, excluded from Active counts)Ticked
Suspended (locks login)Block the driver from signing inCheckboxOpt.Ticking locks the login (badge turns red "Suspended"); unticking restores it. Audited as driver.suspendUnticked
ℹ️
Active vs Suspended — two different switches

Active is an operational flag: an inactive driver stops counting toward your fleet and won't be handed new stops, but their login still works. Suspended is a security lock: it blocks the person from signing in altogether (their badge goes red). Use Suspended when someone leaves or is under review; use Inactive for a temporary pause.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
SaveSaves the employee ID, vehicle, zone, capacity, Active and Suspended settingsPATCH /api/admin/drivers/{id} → audited as driver.update / assign_zone / capacity / suspendSuper Admin Admin Operations
Reset passwordIssues a fresh temporary password and shows it once in a toast; the driver must change it on next loginPOST /api/admin/drivers/{id}/reset-password → new random password, force-reset flag; audited as driver.reset_passwordSuper Admin Admin Operations
Soft-deleteRetires the driver (recoverable) and refreshes the boardPATCH /api/admin/drivers/{id} with deleted = true → sets a deleted timestamp; audited as driver.deleteSuper Admin Admin
Close / ✕ / EscCloses the drawer without saving——
⚠️
Warning — Reset password shows the new password only once

After you click Reset password, the new temporary password appears in a toast message (for example "Temp password: Doodly@4821 — driver resets on next login"). Copy it and pass it to the driver right away — it is not stored in readable form and cannot be shown again. The driver is forced to set their own password the next time they log in.

ℹ️
Soft-delete, not permanent delete

Soft-delete hides the driver from the live list and marks the record deleted; the account and its delivery history are kept and can be restored. This screen never hard-deletes a driver, and deleting also switches them inactive. Restore is available through the backend (see below).

Workflow — reassigning a driver's zone & capacity
Open ManagePick the new zoneSet daily bottle capacitySaveDelivery Capacity updated & auditedAssignment engine uses the new zone next run
Figure — moving a driver to a new zone
ℹ️
Bulk operations exist behind the scenes

The backend also supports acting on many drivers at once — activate, deactivate, assign a zone, soft-delete and restore (POST /api/admin/drivers/bulk; delete and restore need delete permission). The current Drivers screen manages one driver at a time; the bulk actions — including restore — are available to tooling built on the API.

✅
Best Practice

Set a realistic daily capacity for each driver (the default is 45 bottles). The auto-assignment engine caps how many stops a driver receives by this number, so an accurate figure keeps morning runs balanced and prevents anyone being overloaded or left idle.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common Drivers issues and quick answers.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red "Backend offline" banner and no driversThe API server is unreachableConfirm the backend is running and reachable at the shown address, then reload
Red "Your role can't view drivers (403)"Your role lacks drivers:viewSign in as Super Admin, Admin or Operations, or ask for the Operations role
"An account with this email already exists."That email is already used by a userUse a different email, or find the existing account instead of creating a new one
"That employee ID is already in use."Another driver has the same IDPick a different, unique employee ID
"Temporary password must be at least 8 characters."Password too short / weakEnter at least 8 characters that meet the password policy
Driver shows red "Suspended" and can't log inThe Suspended checkbox is tickedOpen Manage and untick Suspended, then Save
Driver never gets any stopsInactive, no zone, or capacity set to a tiny numberIn Manage, tick Active, set a zone, and give a sensible daily capacity
Soft-delete button seems missing / blockedYour role has manage (Operations) but not deleteDeleting a driver needs Super Admin or Admin

Do I create the driver's login separately?

No. Add driver creates both the login account and the Driver profile together in one step. You only supply a temporary password, which the driver changes on their first login.

What is the difference between the Status badge and Availability?

Availability (Available / Busy / On leave / Offline) is reported by the driver's own app as they work. The Status badge combines that with the two switches you control — Inactive (Active off) and Suspended (login locked) — which always take priority. A suspended driver reads red regardless of their app.

Can I recover a driver I soft-deleted?

Yes. Soft-delete only hides and flags the record; the account and its history remain. Restoring is done through the backend's restore action (part of the bulk operations), which reactivates the driver and clears the deleted flag.

Why is a driver's capacity 45 when I never set it?

45 bottles is the default per-executive capacity. It applies until you set a specific number in the Manage drawer, at which point your value (1–10,000) is saved to that driver's Delivery Capacity.

DOODLYAdmin · Operations · Routes
§Admin · Operations

Routes

Plan, name and optimise the delivery routes that carry DOODLY A2 buffalo milk from the dairy to each doorstep, zone by zone.

A route is a named driving plan for one delivery zone — a route name, an optional short code, an assigned delivery executive, and an ordered list of stops. In DOODLY there is no separate "stops" table: a stop is simply one of tomorrow's deliveries that has been tagged with this route (its routeId) and given a position in the running order (its sequence). Because of that, the Routes screen sits on top of the same live delivery data you see everywhere else in Operations — nothing here is invented or duplicated. The page shows a live seven-card KPI strip, the full list of routes, an overview map, and two pop-up dialogs — Plan a route and Manage route — that drive the whole life cycle: create, edit, assign a driver, re-sequence the stops with one click, activate/deactivate, and soft-delete. Everything reads and writes the production database through the /api/admin/routes* endpoints, so what you see is always the true state.

ℹ️
Note — a route is a plan, its stops are real deliveries

Creating a route does not create any deliveries. A route starts empty and fills up when the delivery-generation and assignment steps tag deliveries onto it. That is why a brand-new route shows 0 stops and a blank distance until it is used.

Routes — dashboard, list & overview map

Live KPI strip, the full route table with a per-row Manage link, and a map that draws the first geocoded route.

Purpose See every delivery route and its health at a glance, then open any one to edit or optimise it.
Access Super Admin Admin Operations Mgr — view for all three (permission routes:view). Delivery Executives get a read-only view of routes in their own portal, never on this admin screen. Path Admin → Operations → Routes
Header — Routes · "Delivery routes by zone." · + Plan route
KPI strip (7 cards)Total Routes · Active · In Use Today · Assigned Drivers · Zones · Scheduled Deliveries · Avg Distance
Live status bannerGreen "● Live — N route(s) from the DOODLY database" or a red offline / 403 warning
Route tableRoute · Zone · Driver · Stops · Distance · Status · Manage
Overview mapDraws the first route that has geocoded stops; otherwise a 🗺️ placeholder card
Figure — labelled layout of the Routes dashboard, list and overview map.

The KPI strip

Seven live cards sit above the table, computed by GET /api/admin/routes/stats from the real Route and Delivery tables. They refresh whenever the page loads or a change is saved.

CardWhat it countsHow it is derived
Total RoutesEvery live (non-deleted) route.Count of Route records where deletedAt is empty.
ActiveRoutes switched on and usable.Route active = true.
In Use TodayRoutes that have at least one delivery scheduled for today.Distinct routeId across today's Delivery rows.
Assigned DriversDistinct delivery executives currently tied to a route.Distinct non-empty driverId across routes.
ZonesDistinct delivery zones covered by routes.Distinct non-empty zoneId across routes.
Scheduled DeliveriesTotal stops across all routes.Sum of each route's delivery count.
Avg DistanceAverage route length, in km, over routes that have been optimised.Mean of distanceKm across routes where it is set.
💡
Tip

The stats endpoint also computes numbers the seven cards do not print — inactive route count, average duration (minutes), and an on-time % for today's completed deliveries. These are available to reporting without any backend change.

The live status banner

Directly under the strip, a coloured banner confirms the data is live: green reads "● Live — N route(s) from the DOODLY database" with the API base URL. If the backend is unreachable it turns red — "Backend offline … couldn't load live routes" — and if your role lacks access it shows "Your role can't view routes (403)". The screen deliberately shows the banner instead of stale numbers.

The route table

The list is rendered by the shared DataTable engine. On this screen the search/filter toolbar and pagination are intentionally switched off, so the whole route list (up to 300 routes) is shown in one table; you can still click any column header to sort. Columns are fixed for this dataset:

ColumnShowsSource field
RouteThe route code (e.g. RT-VJ-02); if no code is set, the first part of the route name before the "·" separator.code → falls back to name
ZoneThe delivery zone the route serves, or "—" when none is set.zone (from DeliveryZone)
DriverThe assigned delivery executive's name, or "—" when unassigned.driver (from the linked Driver/User)
StopsHow many deliveries are tagged onto this route.stops (count of deliveries)
DistanceThe optimised route length in km, e.g. "6.4 km"; if only a duration exists it shows "~N min"; "—" until the route is optimised.distanceKm → durationMin
StatusA colour-coded pill — green "Active" or grey "Inactive".active
ManageA Manage link that opens the Manage-route dialog for that row.row action
ElementBehaviour on this screen
SearchNo search box on this screen (toolbar is off). The backend list endpoint does accept a search= parameter matching code, name, driver and zone — it is simply not wired to a toolbar here.
FiltersNo filter chips on this screen. The API supports status (active/inactive/deleted), zoneId and driverId filters for other callers.
SortingEnabled — click any column header to sort ascending / descending.
PaginationOff — the full list renders at once (the backend caps the fetch at 300 routes, newest first).
ExportNot shown here (toolbar off).
Row actionManage per row — opens the Manage-route dialog.

The overview map

Below the table is a map region. On load it looks at the first few routes that have stops and draws the first one whose stop addresses are geocoded (numbered pins in delivery order, joined by a line). Until any route has geocoded stops, it stays a placeholder card that reads: "Open a route below to see its delivery map — stops appear once their addresses are geocoded."

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
+ Plan routeOpens the Plan-a-route dialog.On submit: POST /api/admin/routes.Super Admin Admin Operations Mgr
Manage (row)Opens the Manage-route dialog for that route.Loads full detail via GET /api/admin/routes/{id}; saves via PATCH /api/admin/routes/{id}.Super Admin Admin Operations Mgr

Plan a route

Create a new, empty delivery route and (optionally) assign its zone and driver up front.

Purpose Set up a named driving plan for a zone before deliveries are tagged onto it.
Access Super Admin Admin Operations Mgr — requires routes:create. Path Admin → Operations → Routes → + Plan route
Dialog — Plan a route
Route nameRequired text (e.g. "RT-VJ-02 · Governorpet")
Route codeOptional short code (side by side with the name)
Zone & DriverTwo optional dropdowns from live zones and drivers
NotesOptional free text
FooterCancel · Create route
Figure — the Plan-a-route form.
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Route nameThe main label for the route, shown in the header and dialogs.Free text.YesForm blocks fewer than 2 characters; backend trims and enforces 2–60 chars.RT-VJ-02 · Governorpet
Route codeShort human-friendly code shown in the Route column.Text, up to 30 chars.Opt.Trimmed; must be unique across routes — a duplicate returns 409 "That route code is already in use."RT-VJ-02
ZoneThe delivery zone this route covers.Pick a live zone, or "— No zone —".Opt.Stored as the route's zoneId; options load from GET /api/admin/delivery/zones.Governorpet
DriverThe delivery executive who will run this route.Pick a live driver, or "— Unassigned —".Opt.Stored as driverId; the backend rejects a driver that does not exist. Options load from GET /api/admin/drivers.Anil Sharma
NotesFree operational notes about the route.Text, up to 500 chars.Opt.Trimmed and stored on the route.Narrow lanes; park at the temple.
Workflow — creating a route
Fill the formCreate routeValidate name / unique code / driver existsRoute saved (active, dated today, 0 stops)Audit "route.create"List & KPIs refresh · toast confirms
Figure — what happens when you save a new route.
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Create routeValidates the form, then creates the route.POST /api/admin/routes with name (and any code, zone, driver, notes). Creates the Route (active = true, dated today); writes an audit entry; returns 201.Super Admin Admin Operations Mgr
Cancel / ✕Closes the dialog with no changes. Pressing Esc does the same; pressing Enter in a field submits.None.—
💡
Tip — name and code convention

The placeholder text suggests a pattern like "RT-VJ-02 · Governorpet" for the name and "RT-VJ-02" for the code. Keeping the code as the first part of the name means the Route column reads cleanly whether or not a code is filled in.

Manage route

Edit the route, re-assign zone/driver, view and optimise the stops, activate/deactivate, or soft-delete.

Purpose The control panel for one route — every routine change and the one-click stop optimiser live here.
Access Super Admin Admin Operations Mgr for edits & optimise (routes:edit); Soft-delete needs routes:delete — Super Admin Admin only. Path Admin → Operations → Routes → Manage
Dialog — {Route name}
Summary lineN stop(s) · distance km · ~duration min
Route name & Route codeEditable text (side by side)
Zone & DriverDropdowns, prefilled with the current values
ActiveCheckbox — on/off the working roster
Stops listOrdered read-only stops with a ⚠ on any address that has no map pin
Route mapNumbered stops + line, shown only when stops are geocoded
Footer actionsOptimize stops · Soft-delete · Close · Save
Figure — the Manage-route control panel.

The dialog opens from the already-loaded row, then fetches the route's full detail (GET /api/admin/routes/{id}) to fill the summary line, the stops list and the map. The summary line reads, for example, "12 stop(s) · 6.4 km · ~52 min" — distance and duration only appear once the route has been optimised.

Editable fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Route nameThe route's main label.Free text, 2–60 chars.YesCannot be saved empty; trimmed.RT-VJ-02 · Governorpet
Route codeThe short code in the Route column.Text, up to 30 chars (blank clears it).Opt.Must stay unique across routes (409 if it clashes with another route).RT-VJ-02
ZoneThe zone this route serves.Pick a live zone, or "— No zone —".Opt.Saved to zoneId.Governorpet
DriverThe delivery executive on this route.Pick a live driver, or "— Unassigned —".Opt.Saved to driverId; a driver-only change is audited as "route.assign_driver".Anil Sharma
ActiveWhether the route is on the working roster.Checkbox on / off.Opt.Sets Route active; an active-only change is audited as "route.status". Inactive routes show a grey "Inactive" pill.On
ℹ️
Note — where Notes lives

A route's Notes can be set when you Plan it. The Manage dialog focuses on name, code, zone, driver and the active flag; it does not expose the Notes field for editing.

The stops list

Under the fields is a read-only list of the route's stops in delivery order. Its header summarises progress — e.g. "Stops (8/12 done · 15 bottles)" — where the counts come from the route's performance figures (completed vs. total deliveries, and empty bottles collected). Each line shows the stop's sequence number, the customer's name and their address. A ⚠ marker appears on any stop whose address has no map pin, with the hint "No map pin on this address — add one so it shows on the route map." If the route has no deliveries yet, the list reads "No stops on this route yet."

Stop detailShowsSource
SequenceThe stop's position in the running order.Delivery sequence
CustomerName of the customer receiving the delivery.From the subscription or order's user
AddressLine 1, city and pincode of the drop.The delivery address
Pin warning ⚠Flags a stop whose address has no latitude/longitude yet.Address lat/lng missing

The route map

When the route's stops are geocoded, a map is drawn under the list with the stops as numbered pins in delivery order, joined by a line. If none of the stops have coordinates yet, the map stays hidden — geocode the customer addresses (from the address / serviceable-areas tools) to make it appear.

Actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
SaveWrites the name, code, zone, driver and active flag together.PATCH /api/admin/routes/{id}; audited. Refreshes the list, KPIs and map; shows a "{route} updated" toast.Super Admin Admin Operations Mgr
Optimize stopsRe-orders the stops into an efficient sequence and stamps the route's distance & duration.POST /api/admin/routes/{id}/optimize — nearest-neighbour ordering of geocoded stops; rewrites each delivery's sequence, computes distance (haversine) and estimated time; audited "route.optimize". Toast shows the new stops · km · min.Super Admin Admin Operations Mgr
Soft-deleteRemoves the route from the active list but keeps it so it can be restored.DELETE /api/admin/routes/{id} — stamps deletedAt; audited "route.delete". Toast: "{route} soft-deleted (restorable)".Super Admin Admin
Close / ✕Closes the dialog without saving (also Esc).None.—
Workflow — optimising a route
Open ManageOptimize stopsBackend orders geocoded stops (nearest-neighbour)Delivery sequences rewrittenDistance & duration stampedToast: "Optimized: N stops · X km · ~Y min"
Figure — the one-click stop optimiser.
⚠️
Warning — optimise needs geocoded stops

Optimisation orders stops by their map coordinates. A route with no stops is rejected ("This route has no stops to optimise."), and stops with no map pin cannot be positioned by distance. Geocode the addresses first (look for the ⚠ markers in the stops list) so the sequence and the distance figure are meaningful.

✅
Best Practice — deactivate for a pause, soft-delete only when a route is retired

To take a route out of use for a while — a zone on hold, a seasonal round — clear the Active toggle instead of deleting it. That keeps the route, its driver and its stops intact and is instantly reversible. Reserve Soft-delete for routes you have genuinely retired; even then the record is only date-stamped, so an admin can restore it later.

Permissions & fleet-wide actions

Who can do what, and the bulk operations the backend supports.

Purpose Reference for role-based access and batch route management.
CapabilityPermission keyRoles
View routes, KPIs and the maproutes:viewSuper Admin, Admin, Operations Mgr
Plan (create) a routeroutes:createSuper Admin, Admin, Operations Mgr
Edit / assign driver / optimise / activateroutes:editSuper Admin, Admin, Operations Mgr
Soft-delete / restoreroutes:deleteSuper Admin, Admin
ℹ️
Note — bulk endpoint

The platform exposes POST /api/admin/routes/bulk for batch moves — activate, deactivate, assignDriver, delete and restore across up to 1,000 routes at once (delete/restore require routes:delete). Every bulk run is audited. Day-to-day work on this screen is done per-route through the Manage dialog; the bulk endpoint is for scripted or future batch tools.

⛔
Caution — this is production routing

Client-side role hiding is only for tidiness — the real security boundary is enforced on every API call. Re-sequencing stops with Optimize rewrites the actual delivery order that drivers follow, and assigning a driver changes who runs the round. Treat these as live operational changes, not tests.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common snags and quick answers.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner "Backend offline — couldn't load live routes".The API server is unreachable.Confirm the backend is running and reachable, then reload. The page shows this rather than stale data on purpose.
"Your role can't view routes (403)".Signed-in role lacks routes:view.Use a Super Admin, Admin or Operations account, or have an admin grant the permission.
"That route code is already in use" when saving.Another route already holds the same code.Pick a unique code, or clear the code field to leave it blank.
New route shows 0 stops and a blank distance.No deliveries have been tagged to the route yet.Expected. Stops appear after delivery generation/assignment tags deliveries to this route; distance appears after you Optimize.
"This route has no stops to optimise."Optimise was run on a route with no deliveries.Wait until the route has stops, then optimise.
Overview / route map stays blank or shows the placeholder card.The route's stop addresses are not geocoded (⚠ markers in the stops list).Add map pins to the customer addresses (address / serviceable-areas tools); the map draws once at least one stop has coordinates.
"Route not found — refresh." when clicking Manage.The list is stale (the route was deleted elsewhere).Reload the page to pull the current list, then try again.

What exactly is a "stop"?

A stop is one of tomorrow's deliveries that has been assigned to this route and given a position in the running order. Routes do not have their own stop records — they borrow the live Delivery rows, so the stops list always matches the real deliveries.

Does creating a route schedule any deliveries?

No. A route starts empty. Deliveries are attached to it by the delivery-generation and assignment steps in Operations, after which they show up as its stops.

What does "Optimize stops" actually change?

It re-orders the stops into an efficient sequence (nearest-neighbour by map coordinates), rewrites each delivery's position, and stamps the route with a total distance in km and an estimated duration in minutes (roughly 20 km/h plus about 3 minutes per stop). It does not add, remove or reassign any deliveries.

Can I get a deleted route back?

Yes — soft-delete only date-stamps the record; it is not erased. An admin can restore it (the bulk endpoint offers a restore action), which brings the route and its settings back.

Why is there no search box or filter chips like on other lists?

This screen deliberately turns the DataTable toolbar and pagination off and shows the whole route list in one table (up to 300). You can still sort by clicking a column header. The backend does support search and filters — they are just not wired to a toolbar here.

Module chapter

Admin · Supply

3 screen groups documented from the live application.

DOODLYAdmin · Supply · Farmers
§Admin · Supply

Farmers

Register, monitor and pay the family-run buffalo farms that supply every bottle of DOODLY A2 milk.

The Farmers module is the master register of DOODLY's milk suppliers. Every row is a Farmer record — the farm's name, owner, village, collection route and, most importantly, its procurement rate in rupees per litre. The screen shows a live seven-card dashboard, a searchable and filterable list of farms, and two pop-up dialogs — Register farmer and Manage farmer — that together drive the whole supplier life-cycle: onboarding a farm, editing its details, viewing its collection and quality history, and settling its outstanding payments. Nothing on this page is invented on the client: it all reads and writes the production database through the /api/admin/farmers* endpoints, so what you see is the true state of the supply chain.

ℹ️
Note — one register, no duplicate tables

The Farmers module does not keep its own copy of collections, quality or payments. A farm's daily supply, average fat, quality result and money owed are all derived live from the shared Procurement and Quality Test records. That is why every farm here always agrees with the Procurement and Quality Testing screens — there is a single source of truth. The milk type is fixed platform-wide as A2 Buffalo.

Farmers — dashboard & list

Live KPI strip plus the full farmer table with search, filters, export and per-row Manage.

Purpose See supply health at a glance and open any farm to edit it, view its history or settle its dues.
Access Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr — view/edit for all three; permanent removal (soft-delete) is Super Admin & Admin only. Path Admin → Supply → Farmers
Header — Farmers · "Partner farms and supply." · + Add farmer
KPI strip (7 cards)Total Farmers · Active · New This Month · Milk Today · Avg Daily · Quality Pass · Pending Pay
Live status bannerGreen "● Live — N farmer(s) from the DOODLY database" or a red offline / 403 warning
ToolbarSearch box · filter chips (All · Verified · Review) · Export
Farmer tableID · Farm · Owner · Village · Supply · Fat · Status · Manage
PagerPage-through controls at the foot of the table
Figure — labelled layout of the Farmers dashboard and list.

The KPI strip

Seven live cards sit above the table, refreshed from GET /api/admin/farmers/stats every time the page loads or a change is saved. All figures are computed from the real Farmer, Procurement and Quality-Test tables — money values are stored in paise and printed as rounded rupees.

CardWhat it showsHow it is derived
Total FarmersEvery farm on the register.Count of Farmer records where deletedAt is empty.
ActiveFarms currently supplying.Farmers with active = true.
New This MonthFarms onboarded since the 1st.Farmers whose createdAt falls in the current calendar month.
Milk TodayLitres collected across all farms today.Sum of today's Procurement litres.
Avg DailyAverage litres per day over the last 30 days.30-day Procurement litres ÷ 30.
