Quality PassShare of quality tests that passed in the last 30 days.Passed tests ÷ all tests (defaults to 100% when none tested).
Pending PayTotal money still owed to farmers.Sum of Procurement amountPaise where paidAt is empty.
💡
Tip

The stats endpoint also returns two figures the cards do not print — Inactive farmer count and the 30-day procurement cost in paise. These feed reporting and can be surfaced later without any backend change.

The farmer table

The list is rendered by the shared DataTable engine, so it comes with search, column sorting, filter chips, export and pagination out of the box. Columns are fixed for this dataset:

ColumnShowsSource field
IDA short reference built from the last 5 characters of the record ID, e.g. #9f3ac.id (last 5)
FarmThe farm name, shown in bold.name
OwnerThe owner / contact person, or "—" if none was entered.owner
VillageThe farm's village.village
SupplyAverage daily litres over the last 30 days, e.g. "48 L/day".dailySupply (30-day avg)
FatAverage fat percentage across the farm's collections, e.g. "7.8%".avgFat
StatusColour-coded pill (see below).status
ManageA Manage link that opens the farmer dialog.row action
ElementBehaviour on this screen
SearchFree-text box; the backend matches on ID, farm name, owner, phone, village, route and district.
FiltersToolbar chips: All · Verified · Review. The API also accepts richer filters described in the note below.
SortingClick any column header to sort ascending / descending (the list arrives sorted by farm name).
PaginationEnabled — the list pages when it grows long.
ExportToolbar export downloads the current view as CSV.
Row actionManage per row (opens the Manage-farmer dialog).
ℹ️
Note — server-side filters

Beyond the visible chips, the list endpoint accepts precise query filters: status (active / inactive / deleted), route, district, payment (due / settled) and quality (pass / fail). These let reporting and saved views slice the register by route, money owed or last quality result even though the header chips are simpler.

Status pill reference

The Status column combines the farm's active flag and whether it has been removed.

PillColourMeaning
ActiveGreenOn the register and supplying (active = true, not deleted).
InactiveGreyOn the register but switched off — not currently supplying.
DeletedGreySoft-deleted; hidden from the default list but restorable.

Two more status flags appear inside the Manage dialog and drive the server-side filters: a quality flag (Pass green / Fail red / — grey, from the farm's most recent quality test) and a payment flag (Due amber when money is owed / Settled green).

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
+ Add farmerOpens the Register farmer dialog.On submit: POST /api/admin/farmers.Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr
Manage (row)Opens the Manage-farmer dialog for that farm.Reads the loaded row, then fetches full history via GET /api/admin/farmers/{id}.Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr
ExportDownloads the visible farmer rows as CSV.Client-side, from the loaded table.Any viewer.

Register farmer

Onboard a new supply farm and set its procurement rate.

Purpose Add a family farm to the supplier register so it can start supplying milk and being paid.
Access Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr — requires farmers:create. Path Admin → Supply → Farmers → + Add farmer
Dialog — Register farmer
Farm name & OwnerFarm name required, owner optional (side by side)
Mobile & Alt. contactMobile required, alternate optional (side by side)
Village & PincodeVillage required, pincode optional (side by side)
Mandal & DistrictBoth optional (side by side)
Collection route & Collection centerBoth optional (side by side)
Procurement rate (₹/litre)Required — the price paid per litre
FooterCancel · Register farmer
Figure — the Register farmer form.
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Farm nameThe farm's name, shown across the register.Free text, 2–80 chars.YesForm needs 2+ chars; backend trims and enforces min 2, max 80.Lakshmi Dairy Farm
OwnerThe owner / main contact person.Text, up to 80 chars.Opt.Trimmed; stored as-is (shows "—" in the list if blank).G. Lakshmaiah
MobilePrimary contact number for the farm.7–15 digits, may start with "+".YesPattern ^[+]?[0-9\s-]{7,15}$. Must be unique among non-deleted farms — a repeat returns a 409 conflict.+91 98480 12345
Alt. contactSecond phone number.Up to 20 chars.Opt.Stored as altPhone; trimmed.+91 90000 11111
VillageVillage the farm is in.Text, 2–60 chars.YesForm needs 2+ chars; backend enforces min 2, max 60.Kankipadu
PincodePostal code of the village.Up to 10 chars.Opt.Trimmed; stored as text.521151
MandalSub-district (mandal) the farm falls under.Up to 60 chars.Opt.Trimmed.Penamaluru
DistrictDistrict — also used as a list filter.Up to 60 chars.Opt.Trimmed; shows "—" if blank.Krishna
Collection routeThe pickup route this farm sits on — used for routing and the route filter.Up to 60 chars.Opt.Trimmed.North Route
Collection centerThe chilling / collection center the milk goes to.Up to 60 chars.Opt.Trimmed.Kankipadu BMC
Procurement rate (₹/litre)The price DOODLY pays this farm per litre — drives every procurement's payable amount.Positive decimal rupees.YesForm requires greater than 0; stored in paise (rupees × 100) as a non-negative whole number.62.00
ℹ️
Note — extra fields the API accepts

The create endpoint also accepts state and notes fields, and this dialog leaves them empty. You can fill in State and free-text Notes through the API or a later edit; they are not part of the quick-onboarding form to keep it short.

Workflow — onboarding a farm
Fill the formRegister farmerServer checks phone is uniqueCreate Farmer record (active, rate in paise)Audit "farmer.create"List & KPIs refresh · toast confirms
Figure — what happens when you register a farm.
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Register farmerValidates the form, then creates the farm.POST /api/admin/farmers — creates a Farmer (active = true, rate stored in paise); rejects a duplicate phone with 409; writes a farmer.create audit entry.Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr
Cancel / ✕Closes the dialog with no changes.None.—
⚠️
Warning — the rate is entered in rupees but stored in paise

Type the rate in ordinary rupees (for example 62.00 for ₹62 per litre). The system multiplies by 100 and stores it as paise. Do not type 6200 thinking in paise — that would set the price to ₹6,200 per litre and inflate every future payment. Every procurement's payable amount is rate × litres, so an incorrect rate silently over- or under-pays the farm.

Manage farmer

Edit the farm, read its collection & payment history, settle dues or remove it.

Purpose The life-cycle control panel for one farm — every routine change and the payment settlement live here.
Access Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr for edits & settlement (farmers:edit); Soft-delete needs farmers:delete — Super Admin Admin only. Path Admin → Supply → Farmers → Manage
Dialog — {Farm name}
Summary lineVillage, district · daily supply · A2 Buffalo · quality status · payment status
Farm name & OwnerEditable text (side by side)
Mobile & VillageEditable text (side by side)
Collection route & Rate (₹/litre)Editable text (side by side)
Active toggleCheckbox — on/off the register
History panelAvg fat · SNF · total litres · paid · due, then up to 8 recent collections
Footer actionsSettle payments · Soft-delete · Close · Save
Figure — the Manage-farmer control panel.

Editable fields

The dialog opens with the farm's details pre-filled and its history loading beneath. The fields you can change here are a focused subset of the full record:

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Farm nameThe farm's display name.Text, 2–80 chars.Opt.Cannot be saved blank; trimmed.Lakshmi Dairy Farm
OwnerOwner / contact person.Text, up to 80 chars (blank clears it).Opt.Sent as null when cleared.G. Lakshmaiah
MobilePrimary phone.7–15 digits, may start with "+".Opt.Same phone pattern as onboarding.+91 98480 12345
VillageVillage of the farm.Text, 2–60 chars.Opt.Trimmed.Kankipadu
Collection routePickup route the farm sits on.Text, up to 60 chars (blank clears it).Opt.A route change is logged as a farmer.route audit event.North Route
Rate (₹/litre)Price paid per litre.Decimal rupees.Opt.Stored in paise; a change is logged as a farmer.pricing audit event.64.00
ActiveWhether the farm is on the working register.Checkbox on / off.Opt.Sets Farmer active; a change is logged as a farmer.status event. Inactive farms show a grey pill.On

The history panel

As the dialog opens it calls GET /api/admin/farmers/{id} for the farm's full profile and prints a live summary followed by its most recent collections. This is read-only.

ElementShowsSource
Summary lineAverage fat %, average SNF %, total litres, total paid and (in bold) total due.Aggregated over the farm's procurements.
Recent collectionsUp to 8 most recent rows: date, litres, fat %, quality result (Pass / Fail / —) and the amount, with a ✓ when already paid.Latest Procurement records (up to 60 are fetched).
Empty state"No collections yet." for a farm that has never supplied.—
💡
Tip

The full profile behind this panel carries more per-collection detail than the eight rows print — including SNF %, lactometer reading, temperature, batch number and whether the batch was accepted. It is all available from the same endpoint for reporting or a future detail view.

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
SaveWrites the edited details and the Active toggle in one go.PATCH /api/admin/farmers/{id} with name, owner, phone, village, route, rate (paise) and active; audited.Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr
Settle paymentsMarks all of this farm's unpaid collections as paid and reports how many and how much.POST /api/admin/farmers/{id}/settle — stamps paidAt on every due Procurement; returns settled count & amount; audited as farmer.settle. Errors if nothing is due.Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr
Soft-deleteRemoves the farm from the active register but keeps the record so it can be restored.DELETE /api/admin/farmers/{id} — stamps deletedAt and switches it inactive; audited as farmer.delete.Super Admin Admin
Close / ✕Closes the dialog without saving.None.—
Workflow — pay a farmer
Milk collected (Procurement, unpaid)Payment flag shows "Due" (amber)Open ManageSettle paymentsAll due collections stamped paidFlag flips to "Settled" · Pending Pay drops
Figure — how outstanding milk payments are cleared from this screen.
⚠️
Warning — Settle is a money action

Settle payments marks every unpaid collection for the farm as paid at once — it does not let you pick individual batches, and there is no "unsettle" button in this dialog. Only settle after the farmer has genuinely been paid (bank transfer / cash), because the paid stamp is what the Pending Pay KPI and the payment filter rely on.

✅
Best Practice — deactivate for a pause, soft-delete only when a farm truly leaves

If a farm stops supplying for a season, or is under review, clear the Active toggle rather than deleting it — this keeps its full history and payment record intact and is instantly reversible. Reserve Soft-delete for farms that have genuinely left the network. Because soft-delete only stamps a date, an admin can still restore the record later, and its past collections and quality history remain in the shared tables.

Permissions & register-wide actions

Who can do what, and the bulk operations the backend supports.

Purpose Reference for role-based access and batch farmer management.
CapabilityPermission keyRoles
View farmers, KPIs, history, exportfarmers:viewSuper Admin, Admin, Procurement Mgr
Register a farmerfarmers:createSuper Admin, Admin, Procurement Mgr
Edit / route / rate / activate / settle paymentsfarmers:editSuper Admin, Admin, Procurement Mgr
Soft-delete / restorefarmers:deleteSuper Admin, Admin
ℹ️
Note — the Procurement Manager role

The Procurement role is granted "manage" on farmers, which covers view, create, edit and export — but not delete. So a procurement manager can onboard farms, edit rates and settle payments, yet only a Super Admin or Admin can soft-delete or restore a farm. Every change is written to the audit log with the acting role.

ℹ️
Note — bulk endpoint

The platform exposes POST /api/admin/farmers/bulk for register-wide moves — activate, deactivate, assign route, delete and restore across up to 2,000 farms at once (delete/restore require farmers:delete). Every bulk run is audited. Day-to-day work on this screen is done per-farm through the Manage dialog.

⛔
Caution

Client-side role hiding is only for tidiness — the real security boundary is enforced on every API call. Rates, settlements and deletions affect real money owed to real farmers, so treat every save on this screen as a production action, not a test.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common snags and quick answers.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner "Backend offline — couldn't load live farmers".The API server is unreachable.Confirm the backend is running and reachable, then reload. The page shows this instead of stale data on purpose.
"Your role can't view farmers (403)".Signed-in role lacks farmers:view.Use a Super Admin, Admin or Procurement account, or have an admin grant the permission.
"A farmer with this phone number already exists" on Register.Another non-deleted farm already uses that mobile.Search for the existing farm and edit it, or register with a different phone.
Payments won't settle — "No pending payments to settle."The farm has no unpaid collections.Nothing is owed; no action needed. Check the history panel's "due" figure to confirm.
A farm's Supply shows 0 L/day even though it delivers.No procurements were recorded for it in the last 30 days.Record collections on the Procurement screen; Supply is the 30-day average and fills in as data arrives.
Rate looks 100× too high after saving.The rupee value was typed as if it were paise.Open Manage and re-enter the rate in plain rupees (e.g. 62.00, not 6200).

Where do a farm's litres, fat and money owed come from?

All of them are derived live from the shared Procurement and Quality Test records — the Farmers module keeps no separate copy. Supply is the 30-day average daily litres, Fat is the average across the farm's collections, and money owed is the sum of unpaid procurement amounts.

What does "Settle payments" actually do?

It marks every unpaid collection for that farm as paid, in one step, and tells you how many collections and how much money were settled. Do it only after the farmer has really been paid outside the system.

Can a Procurement Manager delete a farm?

No. Procurement can view, register, edit and settle, but soft-delete and restore require farmers:delete, which only Super Admin and Admin hold.

Is a soft-deleted farm gone for good?

No — soft-delete only stamps a deletedAt date and hides the farm from the default list. Its collections and quality history stay in the shared tables, and an admin can restore it (the bulk endpoint offers a restore action).

Why is the milk type always "A2 Buffalo"?

DOODLY sources a single milk type, so it is fixed platform-wide as a constant rather than a per-farm field.

DOODLYAdmin · Supply · Procurement
§Admin · Supply

Procurement

Record every day's milk collection from the farms, check its quality, and settle what each farmer is paid.

The Procurement module is DOODLY's daily milk-intake book. Each row is one collection batch — the milk one farm handed over on one day, with its quantity in litres, its fat and SNF readings, the rate paid per litre, the money owed, and its quality result. The screen shows a live KPI dashboard, a table of recent batches, and two pop-up dialogs — New procurement (record a fresh collection) and Manage (accept or reject a batch, record its quality test, and mark it paid). Everything on the page reads and writes the production database through the /api/admin/procurement* endpoints, so what you see is the true state of today's supply.

ℹ️
Note — one shared supply record, no duplicate tables

A procurement batch is not a private table for this screen. Every batch is stored on the shared Procurement record, its price comes from the linked Farmer's rate per litre, and its quality is the linked Quality Test. That is why Procurement, Farmers and Quality Testing always agree — there is one source of truth. When you accept a collection, the milk is also added to the raw-milk stock item (MILK_RAW) in Inventory. The milk type is fixed platform-wide as A2 Buffalo.

Milk Procurement — dashboard & batch list

Two live KPI strips plus the table of recent collection batches with a per-row Manage link.

Purpose See today's intake, cost and quality at a glance, and open any batch to accept it, test it or pay for it.
Access Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr — full use; Quality — read-only view. Path Admin → Supply → Procurement
Header — Milk Procurement · "Daily collection, testing and payments." · + New procurement
Top KPI row (4 cards)Collected today · Payable · Avg fat · QC passed
Supply KPI strip (6 cards)Total Milk (30d) · Active Farmers · Procurement Cost · Avg Price · Batches · Pending Quality
Live status bannerGreen "● Live — N procurement record(s) from the DOODLY database" or a red offline / 403 warning
Batch tableDate · Farm · Litres · Fat · SNF · Rate · Amount · QC · Manage
Figure — labelled layout of the Procurement dashboard and batch list.

The top KPI row

Four cards sit directly under the header. They ship with placeholder seed numbers, but on load the page overwrites them with live figures from GET /api/admin/procurement/stats.

CardWhat it shows (live)How it is derived
Collected todayLitres collected across all farms today, e.g. "660 L".Sum of today's Procurement litres (todayLitres).
PayableTotal money still owed to farmers, e.g. "₹41,040".Sum of every Procurement amountPaise where paidAt is empty (pendingPaymentsPaise).
Avg fatAverage fat percentage over the last 30 days, e.g. "7.8%".30-day average of Procurement fatPct (avgFatPct).
QC passedShare of quality tests that passed in the last 30 days, e.g. "75%".Passed tests ÷ all tests over 30 days; defaults to 100% when nothing was tested (qualityPassRate).

The supply KPI strip

A second strip of six cards is inserted just above the table, from the same stats endpoint. All money is stored in paise and printed as rounded rupees.

CardWhat it showsHow it is derived
Total Milk (30d)Litres collected in the last 30 days.30-day sum of litres (totalLitres30).
Active FarmersDistinct farms that supplied in the last 30 days.Distinct farmerId across 30-day procurements (activeFarmers).
Procurement CostTotal money the milk cost over 30 days.30-day sum of amountPaise (procurementCostPaise).
Avg PriceAverage price paid per litre, e.g. "₹62/L".30-day cost ÷ 30-day litres (avgPricePaise).
BatchesTotal collection batches ever recorded.Count of all Procurement records (batches).
Pending QualityAccepted batches still waiting for a quality test.Count where qualityTest is empty and accepted = true (pendingQualityTests).
💡
Tip

The stats endpoint also returns numbers the cards do not print — today's batch count and today's cost in paise. They are available to reporting and can be surfaced later without any backend change.

The batch table

The table title reads "Milk procurement (today)". Rows come from GET /api/admin/procurement — the most recent 300 batches, newest first. Columns are fixed for this dataset:

ColumnShowsSource field
DateThe day the milk was collected, e.g. "2026-07-12".collectedAt (date part)
FarmThe supplying farm's name.farmer.name
LitresQuantity handed over, e.g. "45 L".litres
FatFat percentage of the batch, e.g. "7.2%".fatPct
SNFSolids-not-fat percentage, e.g. "9.1%".snfPct
RatePrice paid per litre, e.g. "₹62".derived — amountPaise ÷ litres
AmountTotal payable for the batch, in bold rupees.amountPaise
QCColour-coded quality pill (see below).quality
ManageA Manage link that opens the batch dialog.row action (_id)
ElementBehaviour on this screen
SearchThe toolbar search box is switched off for this table (toolbar:false) — there is no on-screen search on the Procurement page.
FiltersNo visible filter chips on this screen. The list endpoint still accepts server-side filters (see the note below).
SortingRows arrive sorted by collection date, newest first.
PaginationOff (pager:false) — the newest 300 batches render in one scroll.
Row actionManage per row (opens the batch dialog).
ℹ️
Note — server-side filters the API supports

Even though this screen shows no filter chips, the list endpoint accepts precise query filters for reporting and saved views: search (matches batch no, farm, centre or route), farmerId, quality (passed / failed / pending), payment (paid / due), center, route, and a from / to date range.

QC (quality) pill reference

The QC column reflects the batch's quality test and whether it was accepted.

PillColourMeaning
PassedGreenA quality test was recorded and it passed.
FailedRedA quality test was recorded and it failed.
PendingAmberNo test yet, but the batch was accepted — it is waiting for a quality test.
RejectedRedNo test and the batch was not accepted (turned away).

A separate payment flag — Paid (green, once paidAt is stamped) or Due (amber) — is shown inside the Manage dialog rather than in the table.

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
+ New procurementOpens the New procurement dialog.On submit: POST /api/admin/procurement.Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr
Manage (row)Opens the batch dialog for that collection.Reads the loaded row; actions inside call the accept, quality and pay endpoints.Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr
⚠️
Warning — New procurement needs the farmer list to be loaded

The dialog fills its Farmer dropdown from GET /api/admin/farmers, which loads a moment after the page. If you click + New procurement too quickly you will see the toast "Farmers still loading — try again in a moment." Wait a second and click again.

New procurement entry

Record a fresh milk collection from one farm.

Purpose Log the milk a farm handed over — its quantity, quality readings and, optionally, add it straight into raw-milk stock.
Access Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr — requires procurement:create. Path Admin → Supply → Procurement → + New procurement
Dialog — New procurement entry
FarmerDropdown of farms, each shown with its ₹/L rate — required
Quantity (litres) & Batch no.Litres required; batch optional (auto-generates)
Fat % & SNF %Both required (side by side)
Lactometer & Temperature (°C)Both optional (side by side)
Accept collectionCheckbox, ticked by default — adds the milk to raw-milk stock
Payable previewLive "Payable: ₹X (₹rate/L × litres)" line
FooterCancel · Save procurement
Figure — the New procurement form.
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
FarmerWhich farm supplied the milk — sets the price per litre.Pick one from the dropdown (each option shows "Farm · ₹rate/L").YesA farm must be selected; backend rejects an unknown farmer with "That farmer does not exist."Lakshmi Dairy Farm · ₹62/L
Quantity (litres)How much milk was handed over.Positive decimal number.YesForm needs a value greater than 0; backend enforces litres > 0.45
Batch no.A reference for the batch.Text, 2–40 chars — or leave blank.Opt.If blank, the server auto-generates a unique code like PB-20260712-4821. If you type one that already exists, it is rejected as a conflict.(auto-generate)
Fat %Fat content reading of the milk.Number, 0–100.YesForm requires fat and SNF to be entered; backend caps at 0–100.7.2
SNF %Solids-not-fat reading.Number, 0–100.YesAs above; backend caps at 0–100.9.1
LactometerLactometer reading (density check).Number.Opt.Sent only if a non-zero value is entered.29
Temperature (°C)Temperature of the milk at intake (cold-chain check).Number.Opt.Sent only if a non-zero value is entered.4
Accept collectionWhether to accept the milk into stock now.Checkbox — ticked by default.Opt.When ticked, the litres are added to the raw-milk inventory item (MILK_RAW) in the same database transaction. Unticked, the batch is stored but not added to stock (shows "Rejected" until tested).On
ℹ️
Note — the price is not typed here

There is no rate field on this form. The payable amount is worked out for you as the farm's rate per litre × the litres you enter, using the rate stored on the Farmer record. The live "Payable" line under the fields previews it as you type, and the exact figure is confirmed in the success toast (e.g. "Batch PB-20260712-4821 recorded · ₹2,790.00"). To change what a farm is paid per litre, edit the farm on the Farmers screen.

Workflow — recording a collection
Pick farmer & enter litres / fat / SNFSave procurementServer prices it (rate × litres)Auto-batch-no if blankCreate Procurement recordIf accepted → add litres to MILK_RAW stockAudit "procurement.create" · list & KPIs refresh
Figure — what happens when you save a new collection.
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save procurementValidates the form, then records the batch.POST /api/admin/procurement — computes amountPaise = rate × litres, auto-generates a batch number if none was given (rejecting a duplicate as a 409 conflict), creates the Procurement, and — if "Accept collection" is ticked — increments the MILK_RAW stock by the litres. Writes a procurement.create audit entry.Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr
Cancel / ✕ / EscCloses the dialog with no changes.None.—
💡
Tip — leave the batch number blank

Unless your operation numbers batches by hand, leave Batch no. empty and let the system mint a unique one in the form PB-YYYYMMDD-####. This avoids duplicate-batch conflicts and keeps codes consistent across every collection centre.

Manage — batch dialog

Accept or reject a batch, record its quality test, and mark it paid.

Purpose The control panel for one collection batch — its acceptance, its quality result and its payment all live here.
Access Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr — accept / reject / mark paid need procurement:edit. Record quality test writes to the Quality module and needs quality:create. Path Admin → Supply → Procurement → Manage
Dialog — {Batch no.}
Summary lineFarmer · collection centre · route · A2 Buffalo
Batch factsLitres · fat % · SNF % · ₹rate/L · amount · Quality · Payment status
Accepted batchCheckbox reflecting whether the milk was accepted
Record quality testOnly shown if the batch has no test yet: Fat · SNF · Lactometer · Temp · Result + "Record test"
Footer actionsMark paid · Close · Save
Figure — the Manage-batch control panel.

What the dialog shows

The header is the batch number. Beneath it a read-only summary prints the farmer, collection centre, route and milk type, then a facts line: litres, fat %, SNF %, rate per litre, total amount, the current Quality label and the current Payment label.

Editable / action controls

ControlPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Accepted batchWhether this collection is accepted.Checkbox on / off.Opt.Saved with the Save button via PATCH …/{id}; recorded as a procurement.accept or procurement.reject audit event.On
Fat % qualityFat reading for the quality test.Number (pre-filled from the batch).Opt.Part of the Record-quality-test panel; only shown when the batch has no test.7.2
SNF % qualitySNF reading for the test.Number (pre-filled).Opt.As above.9.1
Lactometer qualityDensity reading for the test.Number (defaults to 27 if blank).Opt.As above.29
Temp (°C) qualityTemperature for the cold-chain check.Number (defaults to 4 if blank).Opt.As above.4
Result qualityWhether the batch passes the test.Passed / Failed.Opt.Posted to the Quality module; sets the QC pill to Passed (green) or Failed (red).Passed
ℹ️
Note — the quality panel only appears once

The Record quality test section is shown only while the batch has no test. As soon as a result is saved the batch shows Passed or Failed, and re-opening Manage no longer offers the panel — a batch is tested once from here. Further quality work happens on the Quality Testing screen.

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
SaveSaves the Accepted batch checkbox.PATCH /api/admin/procurement/{id} with { accepted }; audited as procurement.accept or procurement.reject.Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr
Record testSaves the quality readings and pass/fail result for the batch.POST /api/admin/quality with the batch id, fat, SNF, lactometer, temperature and passed flag.Super Admin Admin Quality — needs quality:create.
Mark paidSettles this batch's payment.POST /api/admin/procurement/bulk with { action:"markPaid", ids:[id] } — stamps paidAt; audited as procurement.bulk.markPaid. The Payment flag flips to "Paid" and the Payable KPI drops.Super Admin Admin Procurement Mgr
Close / ✕ / EscCloses the dialog without saving.None.—
Workflow — batch life-cycle
New procurement recorded (accepted → Pending)Record quality testPassed / Failed pill setMark paidPayment flag "Paid" · Payable KPI drops
Figure — a batch from collection through testing to payment.
⚠️
Warning — "Mark paid" is a money action

Mark paid stamps this batch as settled. On this screen it is used one batch at a time, and there is no "un-pay" button in the dialog. Only mark a batch paid after the farmer has genuinely been paid, because that stamp is what the Payable KPI and the payment filter rely on.

ℹ️
Note — a Procurement Manager cannot record the quality test

Recording the test posts to the Quality module, which requires quality:create. The Procurement Manager role has only view on quality, so it will get a "Your role can't record quality (403)" message. Recording the pass/fail is done by a Super Admin, Admin or the Quality role — here or on the Quality Testing screen. Accepting the batch and marking it paid, however, are fully within the Procurement Manager's rights.

Permissions & batch-wide actions

Who can do what, and the bulk operations the backend supports.

Purpose Reference for role-based access and batch settlement.
CapabilityPermission keyRoles
View batches, KPIsprocurement:viewSuper Admin, Admin, Procurement Mgr, Quality
Record a new collectionprocurement:createSuper Admin, Admin, Procurement Mgr
Accept / reject / mark paid / mark dueprocurement:editSuper Admin, Admin, Procurement Mgr
Record a quality test (Quality module)quality:createSuper Admin, Admin, Quality
ℹ️
Note — bulk settlement endpoint

The platform exposes POST /api/admin/procurement/bulk for batch-wide moves — accept, reject, markPaid and markDue across up to 2,000 batches at once (all need procurement:edit). The Manage dialog uses this endpoint for a single batch when you click "Mark paid"; the same endpoint powers reporting-side bulk settlement. Every run is audited as procurement.bulk.<action>.

⛔
Caution — real stock, real money

Client-side role hiding is only for tidiness — the real security boundary is enforced on every API call. Accepting a collection changes physical raw-milk stock, and marking a batch paid changes real money owed to a real farmer. Treat every save on this screen as a production action, not a test.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common snags and quick answers.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner "Backend offline — couldn't load live procurement".The API server is unreachable.Confirm the backend is running and reachable, then reload. The page shows this instead of stale data on purpose.
"Your role can't view procurement (403)".Signed-in role lacks procurement:view.Use a Super Admin, Admin, Procurement or Quality account, or have an admin grant the permission.
Clicking "+ New procurement" shows "Farmers still loading".The farmer dropdown data has not arrived yet.Wait a second for the farmer list to load, then click again.
"That batch number already exists" when saving.A hand-typed batch no. clashes with an existing one.Clear the Batch no. field to auto-generate a unique code, or type a different number.
"Your role can't record quality (403)" inside Manage.Procurement Manager has only view on the Quality module.Have a Super Admin, Admin or Quality user record the test, or use the Quality Testing screen.
Payable KPI didn't drop after "Mark paid".The list re-fetch happens right after the action; a slow network delays it.Give it a moment — the dialog re-loads the batches and KPIs after settling. Reload the page if it persists.
A batch shows "Rejected" instead of "Pending"."Accept collection" was left unticked when it was recorded.Open Manage, tick Accepted batch and Save — or record a quality test to set Passed/Failed.

Where does the payable amount come from?

From the farm, not this form. When you save a collection the server multiplies the farm's rate per litre (stored on the Farmer record) by the litres you entered. To change a farm's rate, edit it on the Farmers screen; every future batch then prices at the new rate.

What does "Accept collection" actually do?

Ticked (the default), it accepts the milk and adds the litres to the raw-milk stock item (MILK_RAW) in Inventory, in the same transaction that creates the batch. Unticked, the batch is still recorded but not added to stock, and it shows as "Rejected" until it is accepted or tested.

Can I record a quality test from the Procurement screen?

Yes, from the Manage dialog — but only while the batch has no test yet, and only if your role can create quality tests (Super Admin, Admin or Quality). A Procurement Manager, who has view-only on quality, must use the Quality Testing screen or ask a quality user.

Why is there no search box or pager on the table?

This table is configured with the toolbar and pager switched off, so it shows the newest 300 batches in one continuous view. The list endpoint still supports search and date/quality/payment filters for reporting and saved views.

Why is the milk type always "A2 Buffalo"?

DOODLY sources a single milk type, so it is fixed platform-wide as a constant rather than a per-batch field.

DOODLYAdmin · Supply · Quality Testing
§Admin · Supply

Quality Testing

Test every raw-milk batch for fat, SNF, temperature and adulteration before it is trusted — pass or fail it, approve or reject it, and keep raw-milk inventory perfectly in step with those decisions.

The Quality Testing screen is the lab desk of the Supply team. Each day, farmers' milk arrives as procurement batches; each batch has to be screened before it can become part of the milk DOODLY bottles and sells. This screen is where a quality officer records those lab readings — fat percentage, SNF (Solids-Not-Fat) percentage, lactometer/density and temperature — and where the system decides whether the batch passes or fails against the configurable acceptance rules. It also gives supervisors a batch-approval workflow that adds accepted milk to, or pulls rejected milk out of, the raw-milk inventory count.

ℹ️
Note — one shared record, three screens

A "quality record" is not a separate table. It is a procurement batch (the same Procurement row you see under Admin → Supply → Procurement and in that farmer's history under Admin → Supply → Farmers) with an optional lab test (QualityTest) attached. Recording a test here writes to that shared batch; approving or rejecting it flips the same accepted flag the Procurement desk uses. There is no duplicate data to reconcile.

⚠️
Warning — quality decisions move real inventory

Failing a test, or rejecting a batch, does more than change a label. If the batch had already been counted into raw-milk stock, the system automatically removes those litres from the MILK_RAW inventory item. Approving a batch adds its litres back. Treat every pass/fail and approve/reject as a stock movement, not just a note.

Quality Testing — batch queue & dashboard

Live KPI bands plus the list of batches waiting for, or holding, a quality result.

Purpose See every batch's test status at a glance, watch daily pass/fail and compliance figures, and open any batch to test, approve or reject it.
Access Super Admin Admin Quality Control Procurement (view only) Path Admin → Supply → Quality Testing
URL /admin/quality.htmlData source GET /api/admin/quality · /api/admin/quality/stats (RBAC quality:view)
Header — Quality Testing · "Batch test results and cold-chain logs." · [Record test] [Quality rules]
Headline KPI row (4)Batches tested · Flagged · Avg temp · Avg SNF — placeholder numbers overwritten with live figures on load.
Second stat strip (6)Tested Today · Passed · Failed · Pending · Avg Fat · Compliance — inserted above the table from /quality/stats.
Live status bannerGreen "● Live — N quality record(s) from the DOODLY database"; red if the backend is offline or your role is denied (403).
Batch tableBatch · Farm · Fat · SNF · Temp · Result · Manage — newest first, no toolbar, no pager.
Row "Manage" linkOpens the batch drawer: readings, grade, farmer quality history, inventory impact, Approve / Reject.
Header buttons"Record test" opens the test-entry modal; "Quality rules" opens the acceptance-threshold editor.
Figure — labelled layout of the Quality Testing screen

The two KPI bands

Two rows of live numbers sit above the batch table. The top row (four large tiles) is the headline strip that ships with the page and is overwritten live via bkKpis. The second strip (six smaller tiles, id ql-stats) is inserted just above the table by the page script. Every value is pulled from GET /api/admin/quality/stats; the placeholder numbers baked into the page ("100% batches tested", "1 flagged", "4.0°C avg temp", "9.1 avg SNF") are replaced the moment live data arrives.

TileBandWhat it showsHow it is calculated (backend field)
Batches testedTop rowCount of batches currently accepted (in inventory).approvedBatches — Procurement rows where accepted = true.
FlaggedTop rowCount of batches not accepted (rejected / excluded).rejectedBatches — Procurement rows where accepted = false.
Avg tempTop rowAverage recorded temperature (°C) over the last 30 days of tests.avgTempC — average temperatureC across 30-day tests.
Avg SNFTop rowAverage SNF % over the last 30 days of tests.avgSnfPct — average snfPct across 30-day tests.
Tested TodayStripHow many tests were recorded since midnight today.testedToday — QualityTest rows with today's testedAt.
PassedStripOf today's tests, how many passed.passedToday — today's tests where passed = true.
FailedStripOf today's tests, how many failed.failedToday — today's tested minus passed.
PendingStripAccepted batches still waiting for a test.pendingTests — accepted batches with no linked QualityTest.
Avg FatStripAverage fat % over the last 30 days of tests.avgFatPct — average fatPct across 30-day tests.
ComplianceStripShare of the last 30 days' tests that passed (100% if none yet).complianceRate — passed ÷ total × 100 over 30 days.

The batch table

Below the KPIs is the queue of batches, newest first. The backend returns up to the 300 most recent batches ordered by collection time. This table is deliberately compact — the manifest sets toolbar:false and pager:false, so there is no in-page search box, filter chips, export button or pagination bar on this screen; you read the batches and act on each through its Manage link.

AspectDetail (from the live quality dataset)
ColumnsBatch · Farm · Fat · SNF · Temp · Result · Manage
BatchBatch number (e.g. "B-2026-0627"), shown as a monospaced ID.
FarmSupplying farmer's name (falls back to "—" if unlinked).
FatFat % — the test reading if a test exists, otherwise the collection reading.
SNFSolids-Not-Fat % — test reading, else collection reading.
TempRecorded temperature in °C (cold-chain reading); "—" if none.
ResultColour badge — see the Status table below.
ManageLink that opens the batch drawer (approve / reject / view history).
Default sortNewest collection first (collectedAt descending); the query caps at 300 rows.
SearchNot shown on-screen (toolbar off), but the API supports ?search= across batch no, farmer, centre and the internal ids.
FiltersNot shown on-screen; the API supports ?status=passed|failed|pending, ?farmerId=, ?center=, ?from=, ?to=.
Pagination / exportNone on this screen (pager:false, no toolbar).
Row actionManage → opens the Quality record drawer (Screen 3).

What the Result badge means

The badge is derived from the batch — whether it has a lab test yet, whether that test passed, and whether the batch is accepted. There are four possible states:

BadgeColourMeaningUnderlying condition
PassedGreenA test was recorded and it met the standard.Test exists and passed = true.
FailedRedA test was recorded and it fell short.Test exists and passed = false.
PendingAmberAccepted into the day's collection but not yet tested.No test and accepted = true.
RejectedGreyNot accepted, and never tested.No test and accepted = false.

Grade (A / B / C)

Alongside the pass/fail status, every batch is given a letter grade calculated on the fly from the current acceptance rules (Screen 4). You see this grade inside the Manage drawer.

GradeWhen it is awarded
CFat is below Minimum Fat % or SNF is below Minimum SNF % — i.e. it fails the standard.
AFat is at least Minimum Fat % + 1.5 and SNF is at least Minimum SNF % + 0.5 — comfortably above standard.
BMeets the minimum but not the "A" margin — everything in between.
💡
Tip — read the banner first

The green "● Live" banner tells you the numbers came straight from the DOODLY database and how many records loaded. A red banner means you are looking at stale or empty data — either the backend is offline or your role lacks quality:view. Never make a pass/fail call off a red banner.

Record quality test

Enter lab readings for an untested batch and mark it Passed or Failed.

Purpose Attach a lab test to a batch that has none yet — fat, SNF, density and temperature — and record the pass/fail decision, which the rules engine pre-fills for you.
Access Super Admin Admin Quality Control Path Admin → Supply → Quality Testing → Record test
Opens from the header Record test button.Writes to POST /api/admin/quality (RBAC quality:create)
Modal — Record quality test · [Cancel] [Record test]
Batch selectorDrop-down of untested batches only — each shows batch no · farmer · litres.
Reading fieldsFat % · SNF % · Density/lactometer · Temperature °C — pre-filled from the collection readings.
Result selectPassed / Failed — auto-set from the rules as you type, still overridable.
Reject reasonFree text, only relevant on a Failed result (e.g. "low SNF, added water").
Rule hint line"Rule: fat ≥ X%, SNF ≥ Y% → meets / below standard".
Error lineShows validation and permission (403) or conflict (already tested) messages.
Figure — the Record quality test modal
ℹ️
Note — only untested batches appear

The Batch drop-down is built from batches that do not already have a test. If every collected batch has been tested, clicking Record test simply shows a message ("No untested batches — all collections have quality results") and the modal does not open. To change an existing result you cannot re-test; the batch is instead accepted or rejected from its Manage drawer (Screen 3).

Fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
BatchWhich collection batch this test belongs to.One of the untested batches in the list.YesMaps to procurementId. Must exist (else 400 "That batch does not exist.") and have no prior test (else 409 "This batch has already been tested.").B-2026-0627 · Lakshmi Dairy · 45 L
Fat %Butterfat content measured in the lab.Number 0–100 (decimal keypad).YesfatPct, zod min(0).max(100). Pre-filled from the collection reading; drives auto pass/fail.7.8
SNF %Solids-Not-Fat content.Number 0–100 (decimal keypad).YessnfPct, zod min(0).max(100). Pre-filled; drives auto pass/fail.9.1
Density / lactometerLactometer reading used to screen for dilution/adulteration.Number (decimal keypad).Yeslactometer, zod number. Defaults to 27 if left blank on submit.28
Temperature (°C)Cold-chain temperature at testing.Number (decimal keypad).YestemperatureC, zod number. Defaults to 4 if left blank on submit.4.0
ResultWhether the batch passes the standard.Passed / Failed.Yespassed (boolean). Auto-set from the rules as you type fat/SNF, but you can override before saving.Passed
Reject reason (if failed)Why the batch failed — for the record and the farmer's history.Short free text, up to 160 characters.Opt.rejectReason, zod max(160). Ignored on a Passed result; on a Failed result with no reason the backend stores "Did not meet quality standards".Low SNF, added water

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Record testValidates that fat and SNF are present, then saves the test and closes on success with a toast.POST /api/admin/quality — creates the QualityTest. A Failed result also sets the batch accepted = false, and if it had been accepted, decrements the MILK_RAW inventory by the batch litres. Audited as quality.pass or quality.fail.Super Admin+ quality:create
Cancel / × / EscCloses the modal without saving.None.—
💡
Tip — let the rules decide, then verify

As you type the fat and SNF, the Result field flips itself to Passed or Failed and a hint line spells out the exact threshold it used ("Rule: fat ≥ 6.5%, SNF ≥ 8.5% → meets standard"). This keeps decisions consistent across officers. Only override it if the lab sheet clearly disagrees — and if so, record why in the reject reason.

Workflow — recording a test
Pick untested batchReadings auto-fillEnter fat / SNF / density / tempResult auto-set by rules(if failed) add reasonRecord testBatch shows Passed / Failed · inventory synced on fail
Figure — from batch to recorded result

Quality record — Manage drawer

Full readings, grade, farmer history and inventory impact, with Approve / Reject.

Purpose Inspect one batch in depth and make the accept/exclude decision that controls whether its milk counts toward raw-milk stock.
Access Super Admin Admin Quality Control Path Admin → Supply → Quality Testing → (row) Manage
Reads GET /api/admin/quality/{id} (quality:view)Writes POST /api/admin/quality/{id} (quality:edit)
Drawer — {Batch number} · [Reject batch] … [Close] [Approve batch]
Batch header lineFarmer · centre · collected date · tested date (if tested).
Readings lineLitres · fat % · SNF % · density · temperature °C.
Grade & statusLetter grade (A/B/C) · status label · reject reason if any.
Inventory impact"In raw-milk inventory" or "Excluded from inventory".
Farmer quality historyThat farmer's last 10 tested batches — date · batch · fat % · Pass/Fail.
Action buttonsReject batch · Approve batch (plus Close).
Figure — the Quality record Manage drawer

What the drawer shows

RegionContentSource
HeaderBatch number as the title.batchNo
Context lineFarmer name · collection centre · "collected {date}" · "tested {date}" (only if a test exists).farmer, center, collectionDate, testDate
ReadingsLitres · fat % · SNF % · density · temperature (°C).litres, fatPct, snfPct, density, temperatureC
Grade & statusGrade letter and the status label; the reject reason is appended when present.grade, status, rejectReason
Inventory impact"In raw-milk inventory" when accepted, "Excluded from inventory" when not.inventoryImpact
Farmer quality historyUp to the farmer's 10 most recent tested batches — date, batch number, fat %, and Pass/Fail — so you can spot a farmer trending down.history[] from the detail API

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Approve batchMarks the batch accepted and closes with a toast ("approved (in inventory)").POST /api/admin/quality/{id} with {action:"approve"} → sets accepted = true and, in one transaction, increments MILK_RAW stock by the batch litres (no change if it was already accepted). Audited quality.approve.Super Admin+ quality:edit
Reject batchMarks the batch not accepted and closes with a toast ("rejected (excluded)").POST /api/admin/quality/{id} with {action:"reject"} → sets accepted = false and decrements MILK_RAW stock by the batch litres (no change if it was already rejected). Audited quality.reject.Super Admin+ quality:edit
Close / × / EscCloses the drawer without changing anything.None.—
ℹ️
Note — approve/reject is idempotent on inventory

The stock movement only fires when the accepted state actually changes. Approving an already-approved batch, or rejecting an already-rejected one, is a safe no-op (inventory delta 0). You cannot double-count or double-subtract by clicking twice.

Workflow — batch approval and raw-milk stock
Open ManageReview readings + grade + farmer historyApprove → accepted, +litres to MILK_RAWReject → excluded, −litres from MILK_RAWAudit log records the decision
Figure — how the accept/reject decision keeps inventory in step

Quality acceptance rules

The configurable thresholds that auto-grade every batch and set pass/fail.

Purpose Define the fat, SNF, temperature and density limits DOODLY accepts milk against. These apply to new tests immediately and drive the automatic grade and Result.
Access Super Admin Admin Quality Control Path Admin → Supply → Quality Testing → Quality rules
Reads GET /api/admin/quality/rules (quality:view)Writes PATCH /api/admin/quality/rules (quality:edit)
Modal — Quality acceptance rules · [Cancel] [Save rules]
Minimums rowMinimum Fat % · Minimum SNF %.
Temperature rowTemp min (°C) · Temp max (°C).
Density rowDensity min · Density max.
Note"Thresholds used to auto-grade and pass/fail batches. Applies to new tests immediately."
Figure — the Quality acceptance rules editor

These six values live on a single settings record (the QualityConfig singleton). The Minimum Fat % and Minimum SNF % are the two thresholds that actually decide pass/fail and the A/B/C grade; the temperature and density bounds are the cold-chain and adulteration limits the lab checks against.

Fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Minimum Fat %Lowest fat % a batch may have and still pass.Number 0–20.YesminFatPct, clamped 0–20. Drives auto pass/fail and grade.6.5
Minimum SNF %Lowest SNF % a batch may have and still pass.Number 0–20.YesminSnfPct, clamped 0–20. Drives auto pass/fail and grade.8.5
Temp min (°C)Coldest acceptable temperature.Number −5 to 30.YestempMinC, clamped −5–30.2
Temp max (°C)Warmest acceptable temperature (cold-chain ceiling).Number −5 to 40.YestempMaxC, clamped −5–40.6
Density minLowest acceptable lactometer/density.Number 0–50.YesdensityMin, clamped 0–50.26
Density maxHighest acceptable lactometer/density.Number 0–50.YesdensityMax, clamped 0–50.32

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save rulesSaves the six thresholds and closes with a toast ("Quality rules saved — live for new tests").PATCH /api/admin/quality/rules — each value is clamped to its allowed range; the change set (each field's previous → new value) is written to the audit log as quality.rules.update, stamped with who changed it.Super Admin+ quality:edit
Cancel / × / EscCloses without saving.None.—
⚠️
Warning — rules change the grade of the whole queue, live

Because the A/B/C grade and the pass/fail hint are computed from these thresholds each time the list loads, raising the Minimum Fat % or Minimum SNF % can instantly re-grade batches you are about to test — and can turn an "A" into a "B" or a borderline batch into a fail. Change them deliberately, ideally between shifts, not mid-testing.

✅
Best Practice

Set the acceptance rules once with your dairy technologist to match your FSSAI and internal standards, then leave them alone. Record every test through the modal so the Result is decided by the rules, not by opinion — that consistency is what makes the Compliance KPI and the farmer quality history trustworthy. Reserve manual overrides for genuine lab-sheet discrepancies, and always add a reject reason when you fail a batch so the farmer's history explains itself.

ℹ️
Note — bulk approve / reject exists behind the scenes

The backend also exposes POST /api/admin/quality/bulk, which approves or rejects many batches at once (up to 1000 ids), syncing raw-milk inventory per batch and writing one audit entry (quality.bulk.approve / quality.bulk.reject). It also needs quality:edit. The current Quality Testing screen has its toolbar turned off, so there is no on-screen "select many → approve" control; this route is available for scripted or future bulk operations rather than a button you click today.

Who can do what

Quality Testing is governed by the quality permission. Levels map to actions as follows: view = read only; manage = view + create + edit; full = everything. In the default role matrix:

RoleQuality levelView queue & statsRecord testsApprove / reject / edit rules
Super Admin* (all)YesYesYes
AdminfullYesYesYes
Quality ControlfullYesYesYes
ProcurementviewYesNo (403)No (403)
All other rolesnoneNoNoNo

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner "Couldn't load live quality" / empty table.Backend offline, or your role lacks quality:view (403).Confirm the backend is running; if it is a 403, ask a Super Admin to grant your role the quality module (at least view).
"Record test" shows "No untested batches" and won't open.Every collected batch already has a test.Nothing to test. To change a result, open the batch's Manage drawer and Approve / Reject instead.
Saving a test fails with "This batch has already been tested."A test was recorded for that batch (a 409 conflict).Close the modal, refresh, and use the Manage drawer to approve/reject; a batch can only be tested once.
Saving a test fails with "That batch does not exist."The batch was removed or the list is stale (a 400).Refresh the screen to reload the current batch list, then retry.
"Your role can't record quality (403)."You have quality:view only (e.g. the Procurement role).Recording needs quality:create. Have a Quality Control, Admin or Super Admin user record it.
Failing / rejecting a batch changed the raw-milk stock number.Expected — a fail or reject on an accepted batch removes its litres from MILK_RAW.No action needed; this is the inventory staying honest. Approve it back if the reject was a mistake.
Grades shifted across the whole list unexpectedly.The acceptance rules were changed; grades recompute live.Open Quality rules to review the thresholds; check the audit log entry quality.rules.update to see who changed what.

Frequently asked questions

Can I edit a test after I have saved it?

No — a batch can hold only one QualityTest, and trying to test it again returns a conflict. What you can still change is whether the batch is accepted: open its Manage drawer and use Approve or Reject. That is the lever that controls inventory, and it can be flipped as many times as needed.

What is the difference between "Failed" and "Rejected"?

Failed means a lab test was recorded and the milk did not meet the standard (a test exists, passed = false). Rejected means the batch was simply never accepted and has no test at all. A fail automatically un-accepts the batch, so a failed batch is also excluded from inventory — but the badge shows "Failed" because there is a test behind it.

Why does the Result flip by itself when I type the readings?

The modal reads the live acceptance rules and compares your fat and SNF against the Minimum Fat % and Minimum SNF %. If both meet the minimum it pre-selects Passed; otherwise Failed. It is a guide to keep officers consistent — you can still override the selection before saving.

Does approving a batch here change anything on the Procurement screen?

Yes. Approve/Reject flips the very same accepted flag the Procurement desk sees, and the batch's QC badge updates on both screens. There is one shared record, so the two teams never disagree about a batch's status.

Where do the temperature and density limits actually get enforced?

They are stored on the acceptance rules and shown to the tester, and temperature feeds the "Avg temp" KPI. The automatic pass/fail decision, however, is driven specifically by the fat and SNF minimums; temperature and density are the reference bounds your lab checks against and records, with the final call made by the tester and the Approve/Reject decision.

Module chapter

Admin · Finance

3 screen groups documented from the live application.

DOODLYAdmin · Finance · Daily Expenses
§Admin · Finance

Daily Expenses

The books-of-account for everything DOODLY spends — milk procurement, farmer payments, fuel, salaries, bottles, rent, bills and more — recorded with a supplier and bill number, sent through an approve → pay workflow, backed by attachments and a full audit trail, and rolled up into dashboards and exportable reports.

ℹ️
One module, two implementations, identical rules

The Daily Expenses item under Admin → Finance is served by the storefront admin page (/admin/expenses.html), which mounts the DOODLY_EXPENSES module into #expensesMount. It presents five working tabs — Dashboard, Expenses, Add Expense, Categories, Reports — that record and manage every business expense. The same feature set is mirrored by a production-grade Next.js API (/api/expenses) whose engine, validation, statuses, categories and workflow match the screen exactly. This manual documents the complete capability set. Money is entered and shown in ₹; the backend stores every amount as integer paise (₹1 = 100 paise). Every action — create, edit, approve, reject, pay, cancel, delete — appends an immutable line to the expense's audit trail.

ℹ️
Finance-only, with separation of duties

Only three roles can open this module at all: Accountant, Admin and Super Admin. An Accountant may view, create, edit, record payments and run reports — but may not approve, reject, cancel, delete or manage categories. Those are reserved for Admin and Super Admin. This is a deliberate separation of duties: the accountant who records an expense is not the person who approves it. Server-side role checks (canUseExpenses, canApproveExpenses, canManageExpenseSettings) are the real security boundary; the on-screen hiding of buttons is convenience only.

ℹ️
How an expense is numbered

Every expense gets a human-readable, never-reused code of the form EXP-YYYYMMDD-NNNN — the expense date, then a four-digit per-day sequence. The first expense dated 1 Jul 2026 is EXP-20260701-0001, the next that day EXP-20260701-0002, and so on. The backend allocates the sequence atomically from a Counter so two people saving at the same moment never collide on a number.

Expenses Dashboard

Six live spend KPIs and four trend/breakdown charts — the finance pulse of the business at a glance.

Purpose Tell staff in one glance how much has been spent today, this week and this month, how many expenses are waiting for approval, how much has been paid, and how much is still owed — plus where the money is going by day, month, category and payment mode.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — anyone who can open the module Path Admin → Finance → Daily Expenses → Dashboard
Daily Expenses — Dashboard · KPI cards + four chart panels
Tab barDashboard · Expenses · Add Expense · Categories · Reports
KPI cards (6)Today's Expenses · This Week · This Month · Pending Approvals · Paid · Outstanding
Daily expense trend14-day bar chart of daily spend
Monthly trend6-month bar chart of monthly spend
Category-wise spendingTop categories this month, as ranked bars
Payment modeThis month's spend split by mode, as ranked bars
Figure — the Dashboard tab: six KPI cards over four analytics panels.

Every KPI and where the number comes from

KPI cardPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Today's ExpensesTotal spent today₹ amountRead-onlySum of the total of every non-rejected, non-cancelled expense dated today (cards.todayPaise).₹21,600
This WeekSpend over the last 7 days₹ amountRead-onlySum of totals for the trailing 7-day window (cards.weekPaise).₹1,04,300
This MonthSpend so far this calendar month₹ amountRead-onlySum of totals from the 1st to today (cards.monthPaise).₹4,86,200
Pending ApprovalsHow many expenses await sign-offCount (card highlights when > 0)Read-onlyCount of non-deleted expenses with status Pending Approval (cards.pendingApprovals).3
PaidMoney actually paid out₹ amountRead-onlySum of the paid amount across all live expenses (cards.paidPaise).₹3,90,000
OutstandingApproved spend not yet fully paid₹ amount (card highlights when > 0)Read-onlySum of total − paid for expenses that are Approved or Partially Paid (cards.outstandingPaise).₹96,200

The four analytics panels

PanelWhat it showsBackend
Daily expense trend (14 days)A bar per day for the last 14 days; hovering a bar shows the day and its total. "No spending in this period." when the window is empty.dailyTrend[] — 14 date buckets of summed totals (excludes rejected/cancelled)
Monthly trend (6 months)A bar per month for the last 6 months, labelled by short month name.monthlyTrend[] — 6 month buckets of summed totals
Category-wise spending (this month)The top categories this month as ranked horizontal bars with the ₹ value, largest first (top 8).categoryBreakdown[] — grouped by category, this month, ordered by total desc
Payment mode (this month)This month's spend split by payment mode, as ranked bars.paymentModeBreakdown[] — grouped by payment mode, this month
ℹ️
Rejected and cancelled spend never counts

Every dashboard number is computed over "live" spend only — expenses whose status is Rejected or Cancelled are excluded from all totals and charts, and soft-deleted expenses disappear entirely. This keeps the KPIs honest: a rejected bill never inflates "This Month".

Expenses (list, filters & detail)

The searchable, filterable table of every expense, with an expanding in-line detail panel that carries the approve / pay / edit / print / delete controls.

Purpose Find any expense fast, narrow the list to a date range / category / mode / status / amount band, and open a row to see its full breakdown, act on its workflow and read its audit trail.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — view/edit/pay for all three; approve/reject/cancel/delete need Admin+ Path Admin → Finance → Daily Expenses → Expenses
Daily Expenses — Expenses · preset chips · filter row · totals bar · table · detail
Preset chipsAll · Today · Yesterday · Last 7d · Last 30d · This month (default) · Last month
Filter rowAll categories · All modes · All statuses · Min ₹ · Max ₹ · Search box

TableExpense ID · Date · Name · Category · Vendor · Amount · Mode · Status · View

Table columns

ColumnShowsBackend field
Expense IDThe EXP-YYYYMMDD-NNNN code (bold)code

NameThe expense title; a "📎N" badge appears when it has N attachmentstitle, _count.attachments

ViewOpens / closes ("Close") the in-line detail panel for that row—

Filters, search & sorting

ControlPurposeAccepted valuesBackend param
Preset chipsJump the date rangeAll · Today · Yesterday · Last 7d · Last 30d · This month (default) · Last monthpreset → resolves to from/to
CategoryShow one category"All categories" or any live categorycategoryId
ModeShow one payment modeAll modes / Cash / UPI / Bank Transfer / Credit Card / Debit Card / Cheque / Wallet / OtherpaymentMode
StatusShow one statusAll / Pending Approval / Approved / Paid / Partially Paid / Rejected / Cancelledstatus
Min ₹ / Max ₹Amount band on the totalRupees (converted to paise)min / max
SearchFree-text matchText — matches ID, name, vendor, category and descriptionq
AspectBehaviour
SearchCase-insensitive, live as you type, across Expense ID / name / vendor / category / description.
FiltersDate preset, category, mode, status and amount band all combine (AND).
SortingFixed newest-first — sorted by date then Expense ID, descending. There is no user-selectable sort.
Totals barAbove the table: the count of matching rows and their combined Total, both react to the filters.
PaginationThe list shows the filtered set in one scroll; the backend caps a query at 300 rows (adjustable via limit).
Row action"View" expands the detail & workflow panel (below); clicking again closes it.
Empty state"No expenses match these filters." when nothing matches.

The detail & workflow panel

Expense EXP-20260701-0003 · two-column detail
Left — breakdownPurpose · Invoice/Bill · Amount (base) · GST (incl./added) · Total · Paid / Outstanding · Requested / Approved / Paid by · Created by & when · Notes · Documents list
Right — WorkflowApprove → / Reject · Mark fully paid · Cancel · Edit · Print · Delete (shown by status & role)
Right — Record paymentAmount ₹ · Mode · Add — for Approved / Partially Paid, with the outstanding shown
Right — Audit trailEvery action, who did it and when, newest first
Figure — the expanded per-expense detail, workflow buttons, payment box and audit trail.

Workflow & action buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Approve →Signs off a Pending Approval expense so it can be paid. Stamps the approver and time.PATCH /api/expenses/{id} action approve → status Approved, sets approvedAt/approvedBy, logs an "approved" audit line. Only shown while Pending Approval.Super AdminAdmin
RejectTurns down a pending expense; prompts for a reason that is stored on the audit line.action reject → status Rejected (terminal), logs "Rejected: {reason}". Only shown while Pending Approval.Super AdminAdmin
Mark fully paidSettles the whole outstanding balance in one click.action markPaid → records a payment for the outstanding amount using the expense's own mode; status becomes Paid, stamps paidAt. Shown for Approved / Partially Paid.Super AdminAdminAccountant
CancelVoids an expense that should not proceed; asks for confirmation.action cancel → status Cancelled (terminal), logs "cancelled". Shown while Pending Approval / Approved / Partially Paid.Super AdminAdmin
EditReopens the expense in the Add/Edit form (Screen 3) to correct its details.action update → validates and patches; recomputes the total when amount/GST change; logs "updated". Hidden for Paid / Cancelled / Rejected — those can no longer be edited.Super AdminAdminAccountant
PrintOpens a clean "DOODLY — Expense Voucher" in a new window, ready to print or save as PDF.Rendered in the browser from the loaded expense — no server call. Available to any viewer.Any viewer
DeleteSoft-deletes the expense — it is hidden from lists and totals but kept in records; asks for confirmation.action delete → sets deletedAt, logs "deleted". The row never truly leaves the database.Super AdminAdmin

Record payment (partial payments)

Field / actionPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Amount ₹How much of the outstanding to pay now> 0, up to the outstanding balanceYesCapped at the outstanding; a positive amount is required. Adds an ExpensePayment line.₹2,000
ModeHow this instalment was paidCash / UPI / Bank Transfer / Credit Card / Debit Card / Cheque / Wallet / Other (defaults to the expense's mode)YesSaved with the payment line.Cash
AddRecords the instalmentButton—Increases paidPaise; if paid ≥ total the status flips to Paid (and stamps paidAt), otherwise to Partially Paid. Requires the expense to be Approved or Partially Paid first.—
⚠️
Approve before you pay

A payment can only be recorded against an Approved or Partially Paid expense — the server rejects "Expense must be approved before recording a payment." on anything still Pending Approval. The workflow is intentional: request → approve → pay. Likewise a Paid, Rejected or Cancelled expense is terminal and can no longer be edited.

Add / Edit Expense

A four-section form — basic details, payment details (with a live total), approval workflow and supporting documents — to record a new expense or correct an existing one.

Purpose Capture a spend with its category, supplier, bill number, amount and GST; attach the invoice/receipt; and set who requested, approved and paid it. New expenses always start at Pending Approval.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — the "Add Expense" tab only appears for roles that can create Path Admin → Finance → Daily Expenses → Add Expense
Add Expense — 4-section form
1 · Basic detailsExpense date · Name/title · Category · Vendor/payee · Invoice/bill no. · Description/purpose
2 · Payment detailsPayment mode · Amount ₹ · GST included? toggle · GST amount ₹ · live Total box
3 · Approval workflowRequested by · Approved by · Paid by (staff pickers)
4 · Supporting documentsDrag & drop / Browse — invoices, receipts, bills, screenshots, PDFs, images; file list with Remove
Notes + footerInternal remarks textarea · "Create expense" / "Save changes" · Cancel
Figure — the Add/Edit form with its four sections and the live total box.

Form fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Expense dateThe date the spend belongs toDate (defaults to today)YesDrives the EXP-YYYYMMDD-NNNN code. Backend requires a date.2026-07-01
Expense name / titleShort description of the spendTextYesFrontend requires a title; backend requires 2–160 characters.Morning milk collection — Kanuru
CategoryWhich spend bucket it belongs toAny live category (21 defaults, editable)YesMust be a real category; the backend rejects an unknown or deleted category.Milk Procurement
Vendor / payeeWho was paidText, up to 160 charsOpt.Stored as vendor; used by the vendor-wise report.Ravi Dairy Farm
Invoice / bill numberThe supplier's bill referenceText, up to 80 charsOpt.Stored as invoiceNo for audit.SG/2026/418
Description / purposeLonger note on what it was forText, up to 1000 charsOpt.Stored as description; searchable.Weekly bottle top-up for the plant
Payment modeHow it was / will be paidCash / UPI / Bank Transfer / Credit Card / Debit Card / Cheque / Wallet / OtherYesEnum; defaults to Cash.UPI
Amount (₹)The base amount (before any added GST)Number > 0 (2 decimals)YesFrontend requires > 0; backend stores positive integer paise.18400.00
GST included?Whether GST is already inside the amountCheckbox — "Amount already includes GST" vs "GST added on top"Opt.When on, Total = Amount (GST is informational); when off, Total = Amount + GST.off
GST amount (₹)The tax portionNumber ≥ 0 (2 decimals)Opt.Added on top when "GST included?" is off; shown as "(incl.)" when on.4320.00
Requested byWho raised the expenseStaff picker (defaults to the current user)Opt.Options are active non-customer, non-delivery staff. Stored as requestedBy.Rohan Mehta
Approved byWho approved itStaff pickerOpt.Stored as approvedBy; the Approve action stamps the actual approver anyway.Aarav Sharma
Paid byWho paid itStaff pickerOpt.Stored as paidBy; also stamped when a payment is recorded.Rohan Mehta
Supporting documentsAttach the invoice/receipt/billDrag & drop or Browse — PDFs / images / filesOpt.Each file is stored with a kind (invoice / receipt / bill / screenshot / pdf / image / other), name and size; up to 20 per expense.invoice.pdf · 214 KB
NotesInternal remarkText, up to 1000 charsOpt.Stored as notes; visible in the detail panel.Reimburse from petty cash

As you type the Amount and GST (and flip the GST toggle), a live Total box under the payment section recomputes and shows Amount, GST (incl./added) and the grand Total. Saving a new expense creates it at Pending Approval with a fresh EXP- code and paid = 0, and logs a "created" audit line; saving an edit patches the record, recomputes the total when amount/GST changed, and logs "updated".

Workflow — record a new expense end-to-end
Add ExpenseFill basic + payment detailsAttach invoiceSet requested / approved / paid byCreate expense (Pending Approval)Admin approvesRecord payment(s)Paid
Figure — request → approve → pay, the full expense lifecycle.

Categories

Manage the list of spend categories used everywhere in the module — add, rename and (when unused) delete.

Purpose Keep the category list clean and relevant to how DOODLY actually spends, so the Add form, filters and category-wise reports stay meaningful.
Access Super Admin Admin — Accountants see the list read-only Path Admin → Finance → Daily Expenses → Categories
Daily Expenses — Categories · add row + list
Add row"New category name" input + Add button
Category listOne row per category, each with Rename and Delete
Read-only viewFor Accountants: a sentence listing the current categories, no controls
Figure — the Categories tab (Admin view) with add, rename and delete.

The 21 default categories

The module seeds these once (they are then fully editable): Milk Procurement · Farmer Payment · Transportation · Fuel · Vehicle Maintenance · Staff Salary · Delivery Expenses · Office Rent · Electricity Bill · Water Bill · Internet & Mobile · Packaging Materials · Glass Bottle Purchase · Bottle Replacement · Cleaning & Sanitation · Equipment Maintenance · Marketing & Advertising · Printing & Stationery · Software & Subscriptions · Bank Charges · Miscellaneous.

Category actions

ActionWhat it doesBackend processPermission
AddAdds a new category to the list.POST /api/expenses/categories — name 2–80 chars; a duplicate name is refused ("Category already exists"). A URL-safe slug and a sort order are set automatically.Super AdminAdmin
RenamePrompts for a new name and updates the category.PATCH /api/expenses/categories/{id} — updates the name and regenerates its slug.Super AdminAdmin
DeleteRemoves a category — but only when no live expense uses it.DELETE …/{id} — blocked while any non-deleted expense references it ("Category is in use — reassign those expenses first."). On the backend a delete is a soft delete (marked deleted + inactive), so history is preserved.Super AdminAdmin
⚠️
You can't delete a category that's in use

If a category still has expenses filed under it, deletion is refused. Reassign those expenses to another category first (edit each one and change its category), or simply leave the category in place — an unused category does no harm.

Reports

Date-ranged expense analytics — entries, total, paid, outstanding and GST, broken down by category, vendor and payment mode, plus an outstanding list — with CSV, Excel and PDF export.

Purpose Give the accounts team the numbers to close a period and reconcile: what was spent, how it split by category / vendor / mode, what's still owed, and how much GST rode along — exportable for the books.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant Path Admin → Finance → Daily Expenses → Reports
Daily Expenses — Reports · range chips · export buttons · KPI cards · breakdown tables
Range chipsToday · Weekly · This month (default) · Last month · Last 30d
ExportExport CSV · Export Excel · Export PDF
Summary KPIsEntries · Total · Paid · Outstanding · GST
Breakdown tablesCategory-wise · Vendor-wise · Payment mode · Outstanding payments
GST footer"N GST-bearing expense(s), GST ₹X on ₹Y total"
Figure — the Reports tab: range picker, exports, KPI cards and breakdown tables.

Summary KPIs (over the chosen range, excluding rejected/cancelled)

KPIWhat it showsBackend
EntriesNumber of expenses in rangetotals.count
TotalSum of their totalstotals.totalPaise
PaidHow much has been paid across themtotals.paidPaise
OutstandingTotal − Paidtotals.outstandingPaise
GSTGST portion across themtotals.gstPaise

Breakdown tables

TableColumnsBackend
Category-wiseCategory · Count · TotalbyCategory[], ordered by total desc
Vendor-wiseVendor · Count · TotalbyVendor[] (top 20)
Payment modeMode · Count · TotalbyPaymentMode[]
Outstanding paymentsExpense · Vendor · Outstandingoutstanding[] — Approved / Partially Paid with a balance
GST report (footer)Count of GST-bearing expenses, GST value and the total they sit ongst.count / gstPaise / totalPaise

Exports

ButtonWhat it doesFormat / columns
Export CSVDownloads the in-range expenses as a spreadsheet file.CSV: Expense ID · Date · Name · Category · Vendor · Mode · Amount · GST · Total · Paid · Outstanding · Status
Export ExcelSame columns as an .xls workbook.Excel (HTML-table workbook), same 12 columns
Export PDFOpens a branded "DOODLY — Expense Report" in a new window, ready to print or save as PDF.Printable HTML table, same 12 columns
ℹ️
Reports exclude rejected and cancelled expenses

Like the dashboard, reports count only real spend — Rejected and Cancelled expenses are filtered out of every KPI, breakdown and export, so a rejected bill never distorts a period's numbers.

The status model

Every expense sits in exactly one of six statuses. The allowed moves are strict — the server refuses any transition that isn't in this table.

StatusMeaningBadgeCan move to
Pending ApprovalJust created, awaiting sign-offAmberApproved · Rejected · Cancelled
ApprovedSigned off, ready to payGreenPaid · Partially Paid · Cancelled
Partially PaidSome but not all of it paidAmberPaid · Cancelled
PaidFully settledGreen— (terminal)
RejectedTurned down at approval (with a reason)Red— (terminal)
CancelledVoided before completionGrey— (terminal)
Workflow — expense status lifecycle
Pending ApprovalApprovedPartially PaidPaid
Figure — the happy path; Reject (from Pending) and Cancel (from Pending/Approved/Partially Paid) branch off to terminal states.

Who can do what

CapabilitySuper AdminAdminAccountantOther roles
Open the module (dashboard, list, reports)YesYesYesNo
Create & edit expensesYesYesYesNo
Record payments (partial / full)YesYesYesNo
Approve / RejectYesYesNoNo
CancelYesYesNoNo
Manage categoriesYesYesNoNo
Delete (soft)YesYesNoNo
ℹ️
Separation of duties is enforced server-side

The split is not just a UI nicety — the API checks canUseExpenses to open the module, then canApproveExpenses (Admin / Super Admin only) before approve, reject, cancel or delete, and canManageExpenseSettings before category changes. An Accountant who tries an approver-only action gets "Only an Admin or Super Admin can perform this action." Support, operations, procurement, quality, delivery and marketing roles have no expense access at all.

✅
Best Practice — file the bill with the expense, and let approval gate payment

When you record an expense, attach the actual invoice or receipt in the Supporting documents section and fill in the Vendor and Invoice/bill number — those three make the vendor-wise report and any later audit trivial. Let the workflow do its job: create as Pending Approval, have a different person (an Admin) Approve, and only then Record payment. Use Partially Paid honestly when a supplier is paid in instalments; the outstanding figure on the dashboard and reports depends on it. When something is wrong, prefer Reject (with a reason) or Cancel over Delete — a soft-delete hides the row but a rejected/cancelled expense keeps a visible, reasoned trail.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"Add Expense" tab is missingSigned in as a role without create rights (or outside Accountant / Admin / Super Admin)Use an Accountant, Admin or Super Admin account — the tab only shows for roles that can create.
No Approve / Reject / Cancel / Delete buttons on a rowYou're an Accountant — those are approver-only actionsAsk an Admin or Super Admin to approve, reject, cancel or delete; Accountants create, edit and pay.
"Expense must be approved before recording a payment."You tried to pay something still Pending ApprovalApprove the expense first (Admin), then record the payment.
Edit button doesn't appearThe expense is Paid, Rejected or Cancelled — a terminal stateTerminal expenses can't be edited by design; if it's genuinely wrong, cancel/record a correcting entry.
"Category is in use — reassign those expenses first."You tried to delete a category that still has expensesEdit those expenses to a different category (or leave the category as-is), then delete.
"Category already exists"The new category name duplicates an existing onePick a distinct name, or reuse the existing category.
Total doesn't match the amount I typed"GST added on top" is on, so GST is added to the base amountToggle "GST included?" if the amount already contains GST — the live total box shows the effect immediately.
Print / Export PDF window doesn't openThe browser blocked the pop-upAllow pop-ups for the admin site, then click Print / Export PDF again.

Frequently asked questions

Q. What's the difference between "Amount" and "Total"?
