Amount is the base you type. Total is what the expense actually costs: when "GST included?" is off, Total = Amount + GST; when it's on, the GST is already inside so Total = Amount (the GST figure is shown for information). The list, dashboard and reports all use the Total.

Q. Can I pay a supplier in instalments?

Yes. On an Approved expense, use the Record payment box to add each instalment with its amount and mode. The status becomes Partially Paid and the Outstanding figure drops with each payment; once the payments reach the total it flips to Paid automatically. Or use Mark fully paid to settle the whole balance at once.

Q. I filed an expense against the wrong category — how do I fix it?

Open the expense, click Edit, change the Category and save — as long as it isn't Paid/Rejected/Cancelled. The change is logged as an "updated" audit line. If you also want to remove the old category, you can only delete it once no expense uses it.

Q. What happens when I delete an expense?

It's a soft delete — the expense is hidden from every list, total and report, but it is never physically removed; the backend just stamps deletedAt and keeps the record (and its audit trail) for integrity. There is no hard delete in this module.

Q. Who shows up in the "Requested / Approved / Paid by" pickers?

Active staff only — the backend lists non-customer, non-delivery-executive users. Customers and delivery executives never appear, because they don't handle expenses.

Q. Are the dashboard and report numbers "real time"?

Yes — they recompute from the current expense data every time you open the tab, always excluding rejected, cancelled and soft-deleted rows. There's no cache to refresh.

DOODLYAdmin · Finance · Wallet Management
§Admin · Finance

Wallet Management

See every customer's DOODLY Wallet balance, post manual credits, debits and bottle-deposit refunds, reverse any entry, run wallet reports, and set the Trial-Pack cashback and Add-Money rules — all backed by the live database.

ℹ️
What a wallet is

Every customer has one DOODLY Wallet. Its balance is a single running total (stored in paise on the customer's User record) and every change writes an immutable WalletTxn row with the balance after the change and a unique reference. Money reaches a wallet from Trial-Pack cashback, referral rewards, promo credits, bottle-deposit refunds, customer "Add Money" recharges, and manual admin adjustments. It leaves the wallet only when a customer applies it at checkout, or an admin debits or reverses it. Because the balance is a real ledger, nothing here can be edited or deleted — you can only post a new opposite entry.

Wallet Management workspace

The page you land on: a live KPI strip, three tabs, and a live-data banner.

Purpose Give Finance a single command centre for all customer wallet money — balances, adjustments, reports and rules.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — the backend gates viewing on payments.view or billing.view; any other role gets a 403 and the banner reads "Finance, Admin & Super Admin only". Path Admin → Finance → Wallet Management
Header — Wallet Management · live wallet + trial-cashback control centre
Live KPI strip (wal-stats)Eight database-derived cards above the tabs: Total Wallet Balance · Active Wallets · Credits Today · Debits Today · Trial Cashback · Referral Rewards · Refund Credits · Wallet Used
Tab barThree tabs: Wallets · Reports · Settings
Tab bodyRenders the active tab (the customer list, the reports cards, or the two settings panels)
Live bannerGreen "● Live — N wallet(s) · M transaction(s) from the DOODLY database" or an amber/red warning if the backend is offline or your role is blocked
Figure — the Wallet Management page frame. The KPI strip and banner come straight from the backend; the tabs render on top of a locally-cached mirror of that data.

Live KPI strip — eight cards (from /api/wallet/admin?view=reports)

KPI cardWhat it showsBackend field
Total Wallet BalanceSum of every customer's wallet balance still on the bookstotalBalancePaise (sum of User.walletPaise)
Active WalletsCount of customers whose balance is greater than zeroactiveWallets
Credits TodayTotal credited to all wallets since midnightcreditsTodayPaise
Debits TodayTotal debited from all wallets since midnightdebitsTodayPaise
Trial CashbackLifetime Trial-Pack cashback issuedtotalCashbackIssuedPaise
Referral RewardsLifetime referral reward creditsreferralRewardsIssuedPaise
Refund CreditsLifetime refund credits (incl. bottle deposits)refundCreditsPaise
Wallet UsedLifetime wallet spend applied to orderswalletUsedPaise
ℹ️
Live vs. cached

The KPI strip and banner are computed fresh in the database on every page load. The Wallets and Reports tabs render from a local mirror of the same data (the page fetches the wallet list, up to 2,000 transactions, and the cashback config, then caches them) so the tabs are instant. Every money action you take is sent to the backend first, and the whole page reloads from the database the moment it succeeds — so what you see always matches the ledger.

Wallets tab

The searchable list of every customer with a balance or any wallet history, with per-row money actions.

Purpose Find a customer's wallet and act on it — credit, debit, refund a deposit, freeze, or open its full history.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — viewing needs payments.view/billing.view; posting money needs payments.refund, payments.edit or billing.edit. Path Admin → Finance → Wallet Management → Wallets
Header — Wallets · search + Export CSV
Search + export rowFree-text search box and an "Export CSV" button
Wallet tableColumns: Customer · Balance · Cashback · Txns · Trial · (actions)
Row action linksCredit · Debit · Refund deposit · Freeze/Unfreeze · View
Detail panel (on View)Slides in below the table with the customer's full transaction ledger
Figure — the Wallets tab. Each row is one customer's wallet; the action links post to the live backend.

Table specification

AspectDetail (from the real render)
ColumnsCustomer (name in bold + mobile below; a red "frozen" badge if frozen) · Balance (current wallet balance) · Cashback (lifetime Trial-Pack cashback on this wallet) · Txns (number of transactions) · Trial (status badge: green "redeemed", amber "eligible", or grey "—") · an unlabelled actions column.
SearchOne box, placeholder "Search customer, mobile, order or reference…". It matches the customer name, mobile, id, and any transaction description or reference. The box keeps focus and cursor position while you type. (On the server, the same query also matches name, phone and email.)
SortingFixed — the list arrives ordered by balance, highest first.
FiltersNone beyond the search box on this tab.
PaginationNone on-screen; the server returns up to 300 wallets and the whole matching set is shown.
Row actionsCredit, Debit, Refund deposit, Freeze/Unfreeze, View (detailed in the button table below).
Empty state"No wallets found." when the search matches nothing.
Export"Export CSV" downloads doodly-wallet-report.csv — one row per transaction across all loaded wallets, with columns: Customer, Mobile, Date, Description, Type, Credit/Debit, Amount, Balance After, Reference.

Row actions & buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
CreditPrompts for an amount (₹) then a reason, and adds money to the wallet.POST /api/wallet/admin {action:"credit"} → adminCredit posts a CREDIT/adjustment WalletTxn and increments the balance. Amount must be positive.Super AdminAdminAccountant
DebitPrompts for an amount (₹) then a reason (required), and removes money from the wallet.{action:"debit"} → adminDebit posts a DEBIT/adjustment txn. Blocks with 409 "Insufficient wallet balance" if the wallet can't cover it.Super AdminAdminAccountant
Refund depositPrompts for a deposit amount (₹), then bottles returned (optional qty), then a note (optional). Refunds a glass-bottle deposit into the wallet.{action:"bottleRefund"} → refundBottleDeposit writes a BottleLedger "DEPOSIT_REFUNDED" row and a CREDIT/refund txn atomically, and notifies the customer. Cannot exceed the deposit still held (deposits charged on paid orders minus deposits already refunded).Super AdminAdminAccountant
Freeze / UnfreezeMarks the wallet frozen (shows a red badge) so recharges and usage are blocked, or lifts it. Asks for confirmation first.Applied to the on-screen wallet only (a local flag) — it is not posted to the backend by this screen. Use it for a quick visual hold; it does not survive a hard refresh from the database.Super AdminAdminAccountant
ViewToggles the customer's full transaction ledger open (or shut) below the table.Reads the already-loaded transactions; opens the detail panel (Screen 3).Super AdminAdminAccountant
Export CSVDownloads a full transaction report of the loaded wallets as a CSV file.Generated in the browser from the loaded data; no server call.Super AdminAdminAccountant

Fields you enter for each action

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Credit amount (₹)How much to addNumber greater than 0YesMultiplied by 100 to paise; server rejects amounts ≤ 0200
Credit reasonWhy the credit is posted (stored on the txn)Free textOpt.Defaults to "Manual credit" if left blankGoodwill for late delivery
Debit amount (₹)How much to removeNumber greater than 0YesRejected if it exceeds the balance (409)50
Debit reasonWhy the debit is postedFree textYesMarked "(required)" in the prompt; defaults to "Manual debit" if skippedDuplicate credit clawback
Refund deposit amount (₹)Deposit to return to walletNumber greater than 0YesCannot exceed the deposit still held on file150
Bottles returned (qty)How many bottles came backWhole number ≥ 0Opt.Recorded on the BottleLedger row and the txn description3
Refund noteExtra context on the refundFree text, up to 200 charsOpt.Defaults to "Bottle deposit refund"Returned at doorstep, 2 Aug
⛔
Caution — money moves the moment you confirm

Credit, Debit and Refund deposit all post a real ledger entry against a live customer wallet the instant you finish the prompts. There is no "undo" button — the only way to cancel a mistake is to open the wallet's history and Reverse the entry, which posts a matching opposite entry (both stay visible forever). Read the amount back before confirming.

Workflow — posting a manual credit
Find the customer (search)Click CreditEnter amount (₹)Enter reasonServer posts CREDIT txnBalance updates, page reloads live
Figure — every manual money action follows this shape; Debit and Refund deposit add their own validation (balance / deposit-held checks).

Customer wallet detail (transaction ledger)

The full history for one wallet, with a Reverse control on each live entry.

Purpose Audit exactly what happened to a wallet and reverse any single entry if it was posted in error.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant Path Admin → Finance → Wallet Management → Wallets → View
Header — Customer name · balance · Close
Panel headCustomer name and current balance, plus a "Close" link
Ledger tableColumns: Date · Description · Type · Amount · Balance after · Ref · (action)
Reverse controlA "Reverse" link on every entry that has not already been reversed; reversed rows show faded with a "reversed" tag
Figure — the per-customer ledger. Nothing here is editable — you can only reverse an entry, which posts a new opposite entry.

Ledger columns

ColumnWhat it shows
DateWhen the entry was posted.
DescriptionThe free-text description on the transaction (e.g. "Trial Pack cashback", "Applied on order #DZ284913", your manual reason).
TypeThe human label for the transaction kind: Trial cashback · Referral reward · Promo credit · Wallet top-up · Used on order · Refund · Adjustment · Reversal.
AmountA green "+₹" badge for credits or a grey "−₹" badge for debits.
Balance afterThe wallet balance immediately after this entry (stored on the row, never recomputed).
RefThe unique transaction reference (e.g. WTX-4F9K2A).
(action)"Reverse" for a live entry, or a grey "reversed" tag once it has been reversed.

Reverse — button behaviour

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
ReverseAsks "Reverse this transaction? An opposite entry will be posted." then reverses the entry.{action:"reverse", txnId} → reverseTxn posts the opposite entry (kind "reversal") for the same amount. If the original was a Trial-Pack cashback, its cashback record is voided so the customer could become eligible again. Blocked with 409 "Transaction already reversed" if it was reversed before.Super AdminAdminAccountant
CloseCollapses the detail panel.Local — no server call.Super AdminAdminAccountant
⚠️
Reversal never deletes

Reversing does not remove the original entry. Both the original and the new "Reversal of WTX-…" entry stay in the ledger for good, and the balance nets out. This is by design — the wallet is an audit-grade ledger, so every correction is itself recorded.

Reports tab

Programme-level wallet analytics with quick date presets.

Purpose Track how much wallet money you are issuing, how much customers are spending, and how well Trial-Pack cashback converts.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant Path Admin → Finance → Wallet Management → Reports
Header — Reports · date presets
Preset chipsAll time · Today · Last 7d · Last 30d
Report cardsSix metric cards for the chosen range
FootnoteExplains how the conversion rate is calculated
Figure — the Reports tab. Cards recompute when you switch the date preset.

Report cards

CardWhat it measures
Total cashback issuedAll wallet credits posted in the range.
Trial cashback redeemedCount of Trial-Pack cashbacks and their total value.
Wallet usageAmount customers applied to orders (debits of type "usage").
Outstanding balanceTotal wallet money still held across all customers.
Cashback conversionTrial-Pack buyers who redeemed cashback, as a percentage of all Trial-Pack buyers.
WalletsHow many wallets exist.
ℹ️
Date presets

The four chips set the "from" date: All time (no filter), Today (since midnight), Last 7d and Last 30d. Amount cards respect the range; the "Outstanding balance" and "Wallets" cards are point-in-time totals. The footnote states: "Conversion rate = trial-pack buyers who redeemed cashback ÷ total trial-pack buyers."

Settings tab

Two panels: the Trial-Pack cashback rules, and the customer "Add Money" recharge rules.

Purpose Turn the Trial-Pack cashback promotion on or off and set its amount, window and eligible plans; and control whether and how customers can top up their own wallets.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — saving cashback settings needs a manage permission (payments.edit/billing.edit). Path Admin → Finance → Wallet Management → Settings
Header — Settings · two configuration panels
Trial cashback configurationEnabled toggle · Cashback amount · Expiry days · Promotion start/end · Eligible plans · Save settings
Wallet recharge (Add Money)Allow toggle · Minimum · Maximum · Preset amounts · Save recharge settings
Figure — the Settings tab, showing both configuration panels stacked.

Panel 1 — Trial cashback configuration

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Trial cashback enabledMaster switch for the whole promotionOn / Off checkboxYesSaved to CashbackConfig.enabled; when off, no cashback is creditedOn
Cashback amount (₹)How much a customer gets backNumberYesStored as paise in amountPaise; default ₹200200
Expiry (days, blank = never)How long the credit stays validNumber, or blankOpt.Saved to expiryDays; blank = never expires (expiry enforcement is future-ready)90
Promotion start (blank = now)First day the promo is liveDate, or blankOpt.Applied on the storefront to open the promo window; enforced client-side (not part of the backed config record)2026-08-01
Promotion end (blank = never)Last day the promo is liveDate, or blankOpt.Closes the promo window on the storefront; enforced client-side2026-08-31
Eligible plansWhich subscription plans trigger the cashbackAny of: Single Pour · 7-Day Fresh Start · 30-Day Morning Ritual · 90-Day Nourish PlanYesSaved to eligiblePlanSlugs (single/p7/p30/p90); default is 30-Day + 90-Day30-Day + 90-Day
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save settingsSaves the cashback rules; shows "✓ Saved — live instantly".{action:"config"} → setCashbackConfig upserts the single CashbackConfig record (enabled, amountPaise, eligiblePlanSlugs, expiryDays). Applies to the whole storefront immediately.Super AdminAdminAccountant
✅
Best Practice — the cashback is one-time by design

You never need to guard against paying a customer twice. The Trial-Pack cashback is protected in the database by a unique key on the customer id (TrialCashback.userId), so a customer can only ever be credited once — even under simultaneous payments. The panel's own note says so: "Benefit is one-time per customer (enforced by the backend)."

Panel 2 — Wallet recharge (Add Money)

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Allow customers to add moneyMaster switch for self-service wallet rechargeOn / Off checkboxYesWhen off, the customer "Add Money" flow shows "recharge is currently unavailable"On
Minimum recharge (₹)Smallest top-up a customer may makeNumberYesThe customer flow refuses amounts below this; default ₹100100
Maximum recharge (₹)Largest top-up per transactionNumberYesThe customer flow refuses amounts above this; default ₹100,000100000
Preset amounts (comma-separated ₹)The quick-pick chips shown to customersComma-separated numbersOpt.Non-positive values are dropped; falls back to 100, 250, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000 if empty100, 250, 500, 1000
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save recharge settingsSaves the Add-Money rules; shows "✓ Saved — live instantly".Stored for the storefront recharge flow so the customer "Add Money" dialog reads the new toggle, limits and presets. The recharge itself is credited only after a signature-verified Razorpay payment (the backend refuses unverified credits in production).Super AdminAdminAccountant
ℹ️
How a customer recharge actually credits the wallet

When a customer taps Add Money they pick an amount, then pay on Razorpay's secure screen. Only after the payment is signature-verified does the backend post the top-up — and it is idempotent on the gateway payment id, so a retried or duplicated callback never double-credits. Frozen wallets and disabled recharge are refused up front.

Reference — transaction kinds

Every credit and debit you will see, and where it comes from.

Purpose Read any ledger row at a glance and know which system created it.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant Path Admin → Finance → Wallet Management
KindDirectionCreated by
cashback (Trial cashback)CreditSystem, once per customer, after an eligible plan is paid.
referral (Referral reward)CreditSystem, once per referred customer, when the friend subscribes (default ₹100).
promo (Promo credit)CreditPromotions.
topup (Wallet top-up)CreditCustomer "Add Money" recharge (Razorpay-verified).
refund (Refund)CreditBottle-deposit refund (this screen) or other refunds.
adjustment (Adjustment)Credit or DebitYour manual Credit / Debit on this screen.
usage (Used on order)DebitCustomer applying wallet balance at checkout.
reversal (Reversal)Credit or DebitA Reverse action — the opposite of the entry it undoes.
✅
Best Practice — always write a real reason

Debit already forces a reason; do the same for Credit and Refund. The reason is stored on the ledger row and is the only explanation an auditor (or your future self) will have. Prefer specifics — "Goodwill: spoilt delivery order #DZ284913" beats "Manual credit".

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Banner reads "Your role can't access Wallet Management … (403)".Your role lacks payments.view/billing.view.Ask a Super Admin to grant a Finance/payments view permission, or use an Admin, Accountant or Super Admin account.
A Debit is refused with "Insufficient balance".The wallet balance is smaller than the debit amount.Debit only up to the available balance, or split it. The backend never lets a wallet go negative from this screen.
Refund deposit fails with "Refund exceeds the deposit on file".You tried to refund more than the customer still has on deposit (paid-order deposits minus deposits already refunded).Check the deposit held and refund at or below it.
"Transaction already reversed" when reversing.The entry was reversed once already; it can't be reversed twice.Look for the existing "Reversal of WTX-…" row — the correction is already in place.
Banner reads "Backend offline … couldn't load live wallets".The backend (next-app) isn't reachable.Start / restore the backend; the page auto-loads live data once it responds.
A wallet you froze is unfrozen again after refresh.Freeze/Unfreeze on this screen is a local visual hold, not a backend state.For a durable block, handle it through the customer/account controls rather than relying on the freeze toggle here.

Frequently asked questions

Can I edit or delete a wrong transaction?

No. The wallet is an append-only ledger. To correct a mistake, open the wallet's history and Reverse the entry — a matching opposite entry is posted and both remain visible.

If I reverse a Trial-Pack cashback, can the customer earn it again?

Yes. Reversing a cashback voids the one-time cashback record, so the customer becomes eligible again the next time they pay for an eligible plan.

Why is a customer missing from the Wallets list?

The list only includes customers who have a balance above zero or at least one wallet transaction. A brand-new customer who has never earned, spent or been credited won't appear until their first wallet activity.

Does turning off "Trial cashback enabled" claw back credits already given?

No. It only stops future cashbacks from being issued. Balances already credited stay with customers until spent; to remove one you must Debit or Reverse it deliberately.

What's the difference between the KPI strip and the Reports tab?

The KPI strip is computed live in the database on every load (whole-programme totals and today's activity). The Reports tab lets you pick a date range and focuses on cashback issuance, usage, outstanding balance and Trial-Pack conversion.

DOODLYAdmin · Finance · GST Management
§Admin · Finance

GST Management

One central, Super-Admin-controlled tax engine — create tax rates, assign them to products, let orders and invoices calculate GST automatically, and keep every historical invoice accurate even after a rate changes.

ℹ️
What this module does, in plain words

Instead of writing tax percentages into the code, DOODLY treats every GST rate as a configuration you edit on screen. You make a rate once (say "Dairy 5%"), attach it to the products it applies to, and from then on every order, subscription renewal, B2B order and invoice looks up that rate and does the sum for you. The clever part: whenever money actually changes hands, the system freezes a copy of the rate used on that transaction. So if you raise "Dairy 5%" to 9% next year, last year's invoices still correctly read 5%. Nothing historical ever silently changes.

⚠️
Who can change what

Every admin who can reach this screen can read the rates, product assignments, reports and audit trail. But only the Super Admin can create, edit, enable/disable, set-default, assign, delete, or change settings. For everyone else the action buttons disappear and the page shows a Read-only badge — "Read-only · Super Admin manages GST". On the backend this is enforced by the gst:config permission, which only the Super Admin holds.

GST Management — the five tabs

A single workspace split into GST Rates, Product GST, Reports, Audit and Settings.

Purpose Give the Super Admin one place to define tax, apply it to the catalogue, watch what has been collected, and review who changed what.
Access Super Admin (full control) · Admin Accountant and other admin roles with reports access (view only) Path Admin → System → GST Management (/admin/gst.html)
Header — GST Management · tab bar · Run tests button (far right) + test result badge
Tab: GST RatesThe master list of every tax rate — create, edit, enable/disable, set default, delete.
Tab: Product GSTAssign each catalogue product a rate (or mark it tax-exempt); shows the effective % and a ₹100 worked example.
Tab: ReportsFiltered totals of GST collected, drawn from the frozen transaction snapshots; export to CSV / Excel / PDF.
Tab: AuditEvery GST change with who, when and the previous → new value.
Tab: SettingsDefault rate, place of supply, invoice display options and future-ready toggles.
Run testsA built-in self-check (top-right) that runs the engine's own test harness and shows "N/N tests passed".
Figure — labelled layout of the GST Management workspace
💡
Tip — the "Run tests" button

At the top-right of every tab is a Run tests button. Clicking it runs the engine's own built-in checks (validation rules, the 5%-of-₹1000 = ₹50 sum, product resolution, and — most importantly — the "historical invoice stays unchanged after a rate edit" guarantee) and shows a green N/N tests passed badge. Hover the badge to see each check by name. It is a safe, read-only self-diagnostic: it snapshots your data, runs on a copy, and restores everything afterwards. Use it if you ever doubt the tax maths.

Tab 1 — GST Rates

The master register of tax rates. Everything else points back to a row here.

Purpose Define and maintain the list of GST rates the business uses (for DOODLY: 0% for milk, 5% for dairy, 12% for ghee & butter, 18% standard) — each with a name, percentage, tax type, effective dates and status.
Access Super Admin creates/edits; others see View only Path Admin → System → GST Management → GST Rates
Toolbar — Search · Status dropdown · Create GST rate (Super Admin) / Read-only badge
Search boxType to match a rate's name, tax type, percentage or ID.
Status filterAll statuses · Active · Inactive.
Rates tableGST Name · GST % · Tax Type · Status · Effective · Created By · Actions.
Row actionsEdit · Disable/Enable · Set default · Delete (Super Admin only).
Pager10 rows per page, with "N rates" count and page ‹ x / y › controls.
Figure — the GST Rates tab

The rates table — columns explained

ColumnWhat it showsNotes
GST NameThe rate's label in bold, with its description underneath. A green Default badge appears on the fallback rate.Sortable. This is the name that also appears in the Product GST and Settings dropdowns.
GST %The percentage, shown as a highlighted pill (e.g. 5%).Sortable (numeric).
Tax TypeOne of GST · CGST + SGST · IGST · CESS · Exempt."CGST + SGST" splits the calculated amount into two equal halves on invoices.
StatusGreen Active or grey Inactive badge.Sortable. Only active rates can be assigned to products or set as default.
EffectiveThe "effective from" date, and "→ until" if an end date is set.Documentary — records when the rate applies.
Created ByThe name of the person (or "System" for the seeded rates) who created the rate.Read-only.
ActionsEdit · Disable/Enable · Set default · Delete — or View only for non-Super-Admins.See the button table below.

Table behaviour

CapabilityHow it works here
SearchLive search box; matches across name, tax type, percentage and ID. Filters the list as you type.
FiltersStatus dropdown: All statuses / Active / Inactive.
SortingClick the GST Name, GST % or Status headers to sort; an ▲/▼ arrow shows the direction. Click again to reverse.
Pagination10 rates per page; footer shows the total count and ‹ page / pages › navigation.
ExportsNone on this tab — exports live on the Reports tab.
Row actionsEdit, Disable/Enable, Set default, Delete (Super Admin). Non-Super-Admins see "View only".

Row action buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Create GST rateOpens the create form (see below) to add a brand-new rate.create() — validates, assigns the next ID (GST-005, GST-006…), stamps creator + timestamps, logs gst.create.Super Admin
Edit (pencil)Opens the same form pre-filled to change any field of an existing rate.update() — re-validates, records the before → after and optional reason, logs gst.edit.Super Admin
Disable / EnableToggles a rate between Active and Inactive without deleting it.setStatus() — re-validates before re-activating; logs gst.status (e.g. "Dairy 5% → inactive").Super Admin
Set defaultMakes this the fallback rate for any product that has no rate assigned. Only shown on non-default rows.setDefault() — only an active rate qualifies; clears the flag on all others, updates Settings, logs gst.default.Super Admin
Delete (trash)Permanently removes an unused rate after a confirmation prompt.remove() — blocked if the rate is assigned to any product or linked to any transaction; logs gst.delete.Super Admin
⛔
Caution — you cannot delete a rate that is in use

Deletion is deliberately protected. If a rate is assigned to one or more products, or appears on any recorded transaction, the delete is refused with a message like "Cannot delete — this rate is linked to transactions. Deactivate it instead." This safeguards your historical invoices. When a rate is retired, use Disable instead of Delete — it stops the rate being used going forward while preserving every past record. Only a rate that was never assigned and never used can be deleted, and even then a confirmation prompt ("This can't be undone.") appears first.

Create / Edit GST rate — the form

Both "Create GST rate" and the row Edit button open the same pop-up dialog; the only difference is that Edit pre-fills the fields and adds a "Reason for change" box.

Dialog — Create / Edit GST rate
GST nameText — e.g. "Dairy 5%".
GST % · Tax typeNumber 0–100, plus the tax-type dropdown.
Effective from · untilTwo date pickers (until is optional).
Status · DescriptionActive/Inactive dropdown and a free-text description.
Reason for changeEdit only — captured into the audit trail.
Save"Create rate" / "Save changes"; inline error line if validation fails.
Figure — the Create / Edit GST rate dialog
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
GST nameThe human label for the rate.Free text.YesMust not be blank. Cannot duplicate the name of another active rate (case-insensitive).Dairy 5%
GST %The tax percentage applied to the taxable value.Number, step 0.01.YesMust be a number, not negative, not over 100. Stored exactly; the sum is round(subtotal × %)/100.5
Tax typeThe kind of tax, controlling how it is presented.GST · CGST + SGST · IGST · CESS · Exempt.YesDefaults to "GST". "CGST + SGST" splits the amount into two equal halves for the invoice.GST
Effective fromThe date the rate starts to apply.Date.Opt.Defaults to today if left blank.2026-04-01
Effective untilOptional end date for a temporary rate.Date.Opt.If set, must be on or after "Effective from".2027-03-31
StatusWhether the rate is usable now.Active · Inactive.YesOnly Active rates can be assigned or set as default. New rates default to Active.Active
DescriptionA note explaining what the rate covers.Free text (multi-line).Opt.Shown under the name in the rates table.Curd, paneer, palkova and similar dairy.
Reason for changeWhy you edited an existing rate (Edit only).Free text.Opt.Appended to the audit entry — e.g. "reason: Budget 2026 revision".Budget 2026 revision
⚠️
Validation you will meet

If the form can't be saved, a red line appears above the Save button with the exact reason:

  • "GST name is required." — the name box is empty.
  • "GST percentage must be a number." — a non-number was typed.
  • "GST cannot be negative." / "GST cannot exceed 100%." — the percentage is out of the 0–100 range.
  • "An active GST rate with this name already exists." — you'd create a duplicate active name.
  • "'Effective until' must be after 'effective from'." — the end date is earlier than the start.

Tab 2 — Product GST

Attach a rate to each product; unassigned products fall back to the default.

Purpose Decide, per product, which GST rate applies (or mark it tax-exempt). Orders, subscriptions, B2B and invoices then pick this up automatically — no per-order tax entry.
Access Super Admin assigns; others see the resolved rate as plain text Path Admin → System → GST Management → Product GST
Product GST — one row per catalogue product · fallback note ("Unassigned products fall back to Milk GST (Nil)")
ProductThe product name with its slug underneath.
GST RateA dropdown of every active rate, plus "Tax-exempt (0%)".
Effective %The percentage that will actually be charged (or "Exempt").
Sample (per ₹100)A worked example: ₹100 → GST → total.
Figure — the Product GST assignment table
ColumnWhat it showsNotes
ProductProduct name (bold) and its internal slug (e.g. ghee).The product list comes from the live catalogue.
GST RateSuper Admin: a dropdown listing every active rate plus Tax-exempt (0%). Others: the current rate as text.Changing the dropdown saves instantly (assign()) and logs gst.assign.
Effective %The resolved percentage — e.g. 12%, or Exempt.See the resolution rule below.
Sample (per ₹100)A live worked example, e.g. "₹100 → GST ₹12 → ₹112".Helps you sanity-check the choice at a glance.
ℹ️
How a product's rate is decided (the resolution rule)

When any part of DOODLY needs a product's tax, it resolves the rate in this order:

  1. If the product is marked Tax-exempt → 0%, labelled "Tax-exempt".
  2. Else if it is assigned to a rate that is currently Active → that rate is used.
  3. Otherwise (unassigned, or assigned to a rate that has been disabled) → the Default rate is used.

This is why disabling a rate is safe: any product still pointing at it quietly falls back to the default rather than breaking.

Workflow — how a rate reaches a customer's invoice
Super Admin creates a rateAssigns it to a productCustomer orders that productEngine resolves & calculates GSTAmount + rate snapshotted onto the transactionAppears on the invoice & in Reports
Figure — from rate definition to invoice line

Tab 3 — Reports

GST collected, broken down by product, rate, date and invoice — with export.

Purpose Show how much GST has been collected over a chosen period, sliced several ways, so you can reconcile and file returns. All figures come from the frozen transaction snapshots, never from re-calculating current rates.
Access Super Admin plus admin roles with reports access Path Admin → System → GST Management → Reports
Reports — filter bar · KPI cards · breakdown panels · Export ▾
Filter barFrom · To · Product · GST % · Clear · Export (CSV / Excel / PDF).
KPI cardsGST Collected · Taxable Value · Transactions · Tax Liability.
GST by ProductTaxable, GST and transaction count per product.
GST by RateThe same totals grouped by the tax percentage.
GST by DateDay-by-day taxable and GST.
GST by InvoiceTotals per invoice reference.
Figure — the Reports tab

Filters

FilterPurposeValues
FromStart of the reporting window.Date picker.
ToEnd of the reporting window.Date picker.
ProductLimit to a single product.All · or any catalogue product.
GST %Limit to one tax rate.All · or any active percentage (0, 5, 12, 18…).
ClearReset every filter to show all transactions again.Button.

KPI cards

CardMeaningHow it is computed
GST CollectedTotal tax on the filtered transactions.Sum of each snapshot's GST amount.
Taxable ValueTotal pre-tax value those transactions were based on.Sum of each snapshot's subtotal.
TransactionsHow many transactions match the filters.Row count.
Tax LiabilityWhat you owe — equal to GST Collected here.Same as GST Collected.

Breakdown panels

Four grouped tables sit below the cards, each with the columns key · Taxable · GST · Txns: GST by Product, GST by Rate, GST by Date and GST by Invoice. They respect the same filters as the cards.

Export

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Export ▾ → CSVDownloads the filtered rows as a comma-separated file.Builds a CSV with columns Invoice · Date · Product · Customer · Taxable · GST % · GST Amount; logs gst.export.Super Admin+
Export ▾ → ExcelDownloads the same rows as an .xls workbook.Same columns, HTML-table Excel format; logs gst.export.Super Admin+
Export ▾ → PDFOpens a print-ready GST report in a new tab and triggers Print → Save as PDF.Renders a titled report with the collected total, count and timestamp; needs pop-ups allowed.Super Admin+
ℹ️
Why the report can show zero on a fresh production site

Reports are built from real transaction snapshots only. Sample GST transactions are never seeded on the live site — they exist only for local exploration. So a brand-new production environment correctly shows empty reports until real orders and invoices start recording GST. This is expected, not a bug. In the DB-backed backend the same figures are aggregated from the live PaymentRecord, SubscriptionBilling and Invoice ledgers (each carrying a gstPaise snapshot).

Tab 4 — Audit

A permanent record of every GST change — who, when, and previous → new.

Purpose Give finance and compliance a defensible trail: no tax rate can be quietly altered. Every mutation is written to the shared audit log automatically.
Access Super Admin plus admin roles that can reach the page (read-only) Path Admin → System → GST Management → Audit
Audit — filtered to GST changes only, newest first (up to 80 entries)
Changed ByWho made the change.
ActionThe action code, e.g. gst.create, gst.edit.
DetailThe target and, for edits, the previous → new value.
WhenLocal date & time of the change.
Figure — the GST Audit tab
Action codeLogged when…Example detail
gst.createA new rate is created.Dairy 5% · 5% (GST-002)
gst.editA rate is edited.Dairy 5%: [5% · active · Dairy 5%] → [9% · active · Dairy 5%] · reason: Budget 2026 revision
gst.statusA rate is enabled or disabled.Ghee & Butter 12% → inactive
gst.defaultThe default rate is changed.default → Standard 18%
gst.assignA product is assigned a rate (or exempted).paneer → Tax-exempt
gst.deleteAn unused rate is deleted.Temp Unused
gst.exportA report is exported.CSV · 40 rows
✅
Best Practice — always fill in "Reason for change"

When you edit a rate, take the extra second to type a reason (e.g. "GST Council notification 12/2026"). It is optional, but it is stored verbatim in the audit trail and turns a bare number change into a self-explaining compliance record your accountant and any future auditor will thank you for.

Tab 5 — Settings

Global tax defaults, place of supply, invoice display and future-ready toggles.

Purpose Set the behaviours that apply across all products — the fallback rate, where supply is deemed to occur, how tax is shown on invoices, and switches for advanced tax models not yet enabled.
Access Super Admin only edits; others see the same panel disabled with a read-only note Path Admin → System → GST Management → Settings
Settings — defaults & invoice options, plus a "Future-ready (roadmap)" panel
Default GST rateThe fallback for unassigned products.
Place of supplyThe state where supply is deemed to occur.
Show CGST/SGST splitWhether invoices break tax into two halves.
Prices are tax-inclusiveWhether displayed prices already include GST.
Future-ready togglesInter-state IGST · CESS · Location-based tax.
Figure — the Settings tab
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Default GST rateThe rate applied to any product with no assignment.Dropdown of active rates.YesSaving updates the default and re-flags the chosen rate; logs gst.default.Milk GST (Nil) (0%)
Place of supplyThe state used for tax purposes on invoices.Free text.Opt.Stored as-is; drives future inter-state logic.Andhra Pradesh
Show CGST/SGST splitWhether invoices show tax as two equal halves.Checkbox (on/off).Opt.Presentation only; pairs with the "CGST + SGST" tax type.On
Prices are tax-inclusiveWhether the prices customers see already contain GST.Checkbox (on/off).Opt.Off by default (tax added on top).Off
Inter-state IGSTRoadmap — enables IGST for inter-state billing.Checkbox.Opt.Reserved for multi-jurisdiction billing; the engine already models the IGST tax type.Off
CESSRoadmap — enables an additional CESS component.Checkbox.Opt.Reserved for future use.Off
Location-based taxRoadmap — varies tax by delivery location.Checkbox.Opt.Reserved for future use.Off
ℹ️
Settings save as you change them

There is no separate "Save" button on this tab — each control saves the moment you change it (and shows a "GST settings saved" confirmation). Changing the Default GST rate here has the same effect as clicking "Set default" on the Rates tab. The three roadmap toggles are placeholders for tax models that switch on when multi-jurisdiction billing is enabled; they are safe to leave off for a single-state operation like DOODLY today.

✅
Best Practice — trust the snapshot, edit rates freely

The single most important thing to understand about this module: editing a rate never rewrites history. When a sale happens, the exact GST percentage, amount and the rate's ID are frozen onto that transaction. So the correct way to handle a rate change (e.g. the GST Council moves ghee from 12% to 5%) is simply to Edit the existing rate to the new percentage — past invoices keep their old figure, and every future sale uses the new one automatically. You never need to create a duplicate rate or worry about "closing off" the old one. The built-in test "Historical txn keeps original GST % after rate change" exists precisely to guarantee this.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
All the action buttons are gone; the page says "Read-only · Super Admin manages GST".You are signed in as a role other than Super Admin.GST changes are Super-Admin-only by design. Ask the Super Admin to make the change, or switch to the Super Admin role.
Delete is refused: "Cannot delete — this rate is linked to transactions / assigned to N product(s)."The rate is in use, so deleting it would orphan history.Use Disable instead of Delete. Re-assign the affected products to another rate first if you want it fully out of circulation.
"An active GST rate with this name already exists."Another active rate already uses that name.Rename the new rate, or disable the old one before reusing the name.
A product shows the wrong Effective % after you disabled its rate.Disabling a rate makes assigned products fall back to the Default.Assign the product to a different active rate on the Product GST tab, or re-enable the original.
Reports are empty on a live site.No real GST transactions have been recorded yet (sample data is never seeded in production).Expected — figures appear as orders and invoices start recording GST. Widen the From/To dates and click Clear to be sure no filter is hiding rows.
The PDF export doesn't open.The browser blocked the new tab.Allow pop-ups for the admin site, then click Export → PDF again (a "Allow pop-ups for PDF" message appears when blocked).
You can't set a rate as the default.Only an active rate can be the default.Enable the rate first, then use "Set default" (or the Settings dropdown).

Frequently asked questions

If I change a rate, will old invoices change too?

No. Every transaction freezes the GST percentage and amount at the moment of sale, so past invoices are unaffected. Only future sales use the new rate.

How do I stop charging tax on a product?

On the Product GST tab, set that product's rate to Tax-exempt (0%). Its effective rate becomes "Exempt" and no GST is added on future orders.

What happens to a product I never assigned a rate to?

It uses the Default rate (shown at the top of the Product GST tab and set on the Rates or Settings tab). For DOODLY the seeded default is "Milk GST (Nil)" at 0%.

Why can Admins and Accountants see this page but not change anything?

Viewing helps finance reconcile and answer customer queries, but tax configuration carries compliance risk, so the ability to change rates is deliberately restricted to the Super Admin via the gst:config permission.

What's the difference between "GST" and "CGST + SGST" tax types?

Both charge the same total percentage. "CGST + SGST" simply splits that amount into two equal halves on the invoice (Central and State GST) — used for intra-state supply. The split display is also governed by the "Show CGST/SGST split" setting.

Where do the four starting rates come from?

DOODLY seeds four sensible dairy rates on first use: Milk GST (Nil) 0%, Dairy 5% (curd, paneer, palkova), Ghee & Butter 12%, and Standard 18%. You can edit, disable or add to these at any time.

Module chapter

Admin · Growth

8 screen groups documented from the live application.

DOODLYAdmin · Growth · Reports
§Admin · Growth

Reports

One live business-intelligence screen that computes every headline number, trend chart and drill-down table straight from the DOODLY database — sales, customers, subscriptions, finance, operations, procurement and marketing — for any date range, ready to export to CSV, Excel, PDF or print.

ℹ️
Note — this is a read-only analytics page

Reports never changes an order, payment, subscription or any other record. It reads the authoritative source tables (orders, subscriptions, payments, wallet, procurement, quality tests, deliveries, referrals, trial cashback, products and expenses) and re-computes the figures on demand. There are no separate "analytics" tables to keep in sync — every number you see is the true, current state of the business for the range you picked.

💡
How the data reaches this screen

Opening Admin → Growth → Reports calls the backend endpoint GET /api/admin/reports?preset=…. The server resolves the preset to a date range, aggregates the source tables live, and returns the KPIs, chart series and category tables in one payload. A green "● Live — analytics computed from the DOODLY database" banner confirms the source and the exact range. Every filter change re-fetches; nothing is cached client-side.

Screen 1 — Reports Board (live analytics)

The single-page dashboard: date-range toolbar, 17 KPI cards, seven trend/breakdown charts, seven report tiles and a sales-by-day table.

Purpose Give leadership a one-look, always-current answer to "how is the whole business doing right now?" and let them slice it by any period and export it for a meeting.
Access Super AdminAdmin plus any role granted reports:view — Accountant, Operations, Procurement, Inventory, Quality and Marketing. Support, Delivery Executive and Customer cannot open it. Path Admin → Growth → Reports
Reports — Live business intelligence · date presets · Export CSV · Excel · Export PDF · Print
Range toolbarNine preset chips (Today → All time) + four export buttons
KPI strip (17 cards)Revenue, orders, customers, subscriptions, finance headline numbers
Report tiles (7)Sales · Customers · Subscriptions · Financial · Operations · Procurement · Marketing — each clickable to drill down
Charts (4 bar trends)Revenue trend · Orders trend · Customer growth (6 mo) · Subscription growth (6 mo)
Breakdowns (2)Orders by status · Subscriptions by status
Sales-by-day tableDate · Orders · Revenue, with the active range badge
Live bannerGreen "● Live" line naming the database and the resolved range
Figure 1 — The Reports board: a range toolbar over the KPI strip, report tiles, charts and the daily sales table.

The date-range toolbar

A single row of preset chips on the left and four export buttons on the right. Clicking a chip highlights it, re-fetches the whole board for that range, and logs a "filtered" audit event. The board opens on Last 30d by default.

Preset chipRange it selectsTrend granularity
TodayMidnight today → nowDaily bars
YesterdayThe previous full calendar dayDaily bars
Last 7dThe last seven days including todayDaily bars
Last 30d defaultThe last thirty days including todayDaily bars
This month1st of this month → end of this monthDaily bars
Last monthThe whole previous calendar monthDaily bars
QuarterThe current calendar quarterMonthly bars
Financial yrIndian financial year — 1 April to 31 MarchMonthly bars
All timeNo date bound — the entire historyMonthly bars
ℹ️
Note — daily vs. monthly charts switch automatically

The trend charts adapt to the range you pick. If the span is 45 days or shorter the bars are drawn one-per-day; anything longer (Quarter, Financial yr, All time) is bucketed into the last six months of monthly bars. The customer-growth and subscription-growth charts are always the last six months, regardless of the selected range.

KPI strip — the 17 headline cards

Across the top sits a strip of seventeen number cards. Money figures are shown in whole rupees (the database stores paise and the screen divides by 100). Every card reflects the values described below.

KPI cardWhat it showsHow it is computed (source)
Total RevenueLifetime revenue from paid ordersSum of totalPaise across all orders with status PAID
Today's RevenueRevenue booked since midnight todayPAID orders created today
This MonthRevenue this calendar month to datePAID orders since the 1st of the month
Total OrdersAll orders in every statusOrder count grouped by status, summed
Completed OrdersOrders that reached PAIDCount of orders with status PAID
Active CustomersDistinct customers who paid in the last 30 daysDistinct userId on PAID orders in the last 30 days
New CustomersCustomers who signed up inside the selected rangeCUSTOMER users created between from → to
Active SubscriptionsCurrently running subscriptionsSubscriptions with status ACTIVE
Renewal RateShare of ended subscriptions that completed vs. cancelledcompleted ÷ (completed + cancelled) × 100
Retention RateThe inverse of churn100 − churn rate
Avg Order ValueMean value of a paid orderTotal revenue ÷ paid-order count
Wallet UsageWallet money spent by customersSum of wallet DEBIT transactions of kind "usage"
Referral GrowthNumber of credited referral rewardsCount of ReferralReward rows with status CREDITED
Procurement CostTotal amount payable to farms for milkSum of Procurement amountPaise (all batches)
GST CollectedTax collected on paid ordersSum of order taxPaise across PAID orders
Gross ProfitRevenue after milk costTotal revenue − procurement cost (COGS proxy)
Net ProfitRevenue after milk cost and operating expensesTotal revenue − procurement cost − operating expenses
⚠️
Warning — several KPIs are lifetime, not range-scoped

Read the labels carefully. Total Revenue, Total Orders, Completed Orders, Active Subscriptions, Renewal/Retention Rate, Avg Order Value, Wallet Usage, Referral Growth, Procurement Cost, GST Collected, Gross Profit and Net Profit are computed over the whole business history, not the chip you selected. Only New Customers and the trend charts / sales table follow the chosen range. This is by design — the KPI strip is a "state of the business" summary; use the charts, the daily table and the drill-downs for range-specific analysis.

ℹ️
Note — "Gross Profit" and "Net Profit" are proxies

Gross profit currently treats milk procurement cost as the only cost of goods sold, and net profit additionally subtracts approved operating expenses (expenses that are not REJECTED or CANCELLED and not soft-deleted). These are honest, useful directional figures but not a full P&L; treat them as management indicators, not audited accounts.

Report tiles — seven clickable categories

Below the KPI strip sits a three-column grid of seven tiles. Each tile shows a headline summary and a "View report →" link; clicking any tile opens its detailed drill-down card (Screen 2).

TileSummary shown on the tile
Sales reportRevenue and the number of paid orders
Customer reportActive · repeat · retention %
Subscription reportActive · churn % · renewal %
Financial reportNet profit · GST collected
Operations reportOn-time % · bottle-return %
Procurement reportLitres · cost · quality pass %
Marketing reportReferral count · trial conversions

Charts and status breakdowns

A two-column grid holds six visual panels. Four are bar-trend charts (each bar is captioned and shows its value on hover); two are horizontal breakdown bars.

PanelWhat it plotsNotes
Revenue trendRevenue per day (or per month) across the rangeMoney bars; hover shows the rupee value
Orders trendNumber of paid orders per day/monthCount bars
Customer growth (6 mo)New customers per month, last six monthsAlways six months
Subscription growth (6 mo)New subscriptions per month, last six monthsAlways six months
Orders by statusOrder count per status (e.g. PAID, PENDING, …)Horizontal bars, largest first
Subscriptions by statusSubscription count per statusHorizontal bars, largest first

Sales-by-day table

At the foot of the board is a plain three-column table titled "Sales — by day" (or "by month" for long ranges), with a badge showing the resolved from → to dates.

PropertyDetail
ColumnsDate · Orders · Revenue
RowsOne per day (ranges ≤ 45 days) or one per month (longer ranges)
SortingFixed — chronological, oldest to newest
Search / filterNone on this table; use the range chips to change what it covers
Empty state"No sales in this range." spanning the table

Buttons on the board

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Preset chip (Today … All time)Re-runs the whole board for that rangeGET /api/admin/reports?preset=… ; POST logs a reports.filtered audit eventSuper Admin+ reports:view
Export CSVDownloads a .csv with KPIs, Daily Sales and Product Sales sectionsBuilt client-side from the loaded data; POST logs reports.exported [csv]Super Admin+ reports:view
ExcelSame content downloaded as a .xls (Excel-openable) fileClient-side; POST logs reports.exported [xls]Super Admin+ reports:view
Export PDFOpens a formatted print window (KPIs + Daily Sales + Product Sales) ready to "Save as PDF"Client-side print window; POST logs reports.exported [pdf]Super Admin+ reports:view
PrintOpens the same formatted window and triggers the browser print dialogClient-side; POST logs reports.printedSuper Admin+ reports:view
Report tile (any of 7)Opens the category drill-down card (Screen 2)No fetch — renders from loaded data; POST logs reports.generatedSuper Admin+ reports:view
⚠️
Warning — allow pop-ups for PDF / Print

Export PDF and Print open a new browser window. If your browser blocks pop-ups the screen shows "Allow pop-ups to pdf/print" and nothing downloads. Allow pop-ups for the admin site once and both work every time. CSV and Excel download directly and are never blocked.

✅
Best Practice — pick the range before you export

The exports and the daily table always reflect the currently selected chip, so choose your period first (for example "Financial yr" for a year-end pack), let the board reload, then click Export. For a board-meeting summary, "This month" or "Quarter" gives clean monthly bars; for finance reconciliation, "Financial yr" aligns with the April–March books.

Screen 2 — Category drill-down card

The detail modal opened by clicking any report tile — full tables for that category plus a per-report CSV export.

Purpose Go from the headline tile into the underlying detail — product-by-product sales, customer cohorts, subscription health, the financial breakdown, driver/farmer performance and marketing outcomes.
Access Super AdminAdmin plus any reports:view role Path Admin → Growth → Reports → (click a tile)
Drill-down — <Report name> · from → to · × close
HeaderReport title and the resolved date range; × to close
Detail tablesOne or more tables specific to the category (see below)
FooterExport CSV (this report) · Close
Figure 2 — The drill-down card overlays the board; press Esc, click the backdrop, × or Close to dismiss it.

The card that opens depends on which tile you clicked. Each shows the range in its title and carries its own Export CSV button in the footer.

Tile → cardTables inside the card
Sales reportBy product (Product · Category · Qty · Revenue, top 20) · By category (Category · Qty · Revenue) · By order type (Type · Orders · Revenue)
Customer reportMetrics (Total · Active 30d · Inactive · Repeat buyers · New in range · Retention rate) · Customer growth (6 mo) by month
Subscription reportCount by status · Health (Active · Cancelled · Completed · Renewal rate · Churn rate)
Financial reportRevenue (PAID) · Procurement cost · Operating expenses · Gross profit · Net profit · GST collected · Refunds (count + amount) · Wallet credited · Wallet used · Cashback issued · Referral rewards
Operations reportTotal deliveries · Delivered · Failed/late · Pending · On-time rate · Bottles out · Bottles in · Bottle return rate · Driver performance (Driver · Delivered · Bottles in, top 10)
Procurement reportTotal litres · Total cost · Batches · Range litres · Quality pass rate · Tests passed · Tests failed · Farmer performance (Farmer · Batches · Litres · Payable, top 10)
Marketing reportReferral rewards issued · Referral amount · Trial-pack conversions · Trial cashback paid · note that campaign & coupon performance are future-ready

Drill-down buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Export CSV (in card)Downloads the standard KPI + Daily Sales + Product Sales CSV, tagged with this report's nameClient-side; POST logs reports.exported [csv] for the named reportSuper Admin+ reports:view
Close / × / Esc / backdropDismisses the card and returns to the boardNone—
ℹ️
Note — driver and farmer names, top 10 only

Operations and Procurement drill-downs list the top 10 performers — drivers ranked by deliveries completed, farmers ranked by litres supplied. A driver or farmer with no name resolves to "—". Bottle-return and quality-pass percentages are rounded to one decimal.

Workflow — from open to exported report
Open ReportsBoard fetches live analyticsPick a date presetRead KPIs & chartsClick a tile to drill downExport CSV / Excel / PDF / PrintAction audit-logged
Figure 3 — Every open, filter, drill-down and export is recorded in the audit trail.
ℹ️
Note — everything is audit-logged

Filtering, generating a drill-down, exporting and printing each send a POST to /api/admin/reports that writes a row to the audit trail (actions reports.filtered, reports.generated, reports.exported, reports.printed). The log captures the report name, the chosen filter/preset and, for exports, the format. You can review who pulled which report and when under Admin → System → Audit Logs.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Board stays on "Loading live analytics…" then shows "⚠ Backend offline"The next-app backend is not running / not reachableStart or restart the backend; the banner names the exact base URL it tried
"⚠ Your role can't view Reports (403)"Your role lacks reports:viewAsk a Super Admin to grant the reports permission, or sign in with a role that has it
Export PDF or Print does nothingThe browser blocked the pop-up windowAllow pop-ups for the admin site, then click again (CSV/Excel are unaffected)
KPI numbers don't change when I switch presetsExpected — most KPI cards are lifetime totals, not range-scopedUse the charts, the daily/monthly table and the drill-downs for range-specific figures
Trend chart flips from daily bars to monthly barsThe chosen range is longer than 45 daysPick a shorter preset (Last 30d or shorter) to see per-day bars again
A panel reads "No data in this range."There are genuinely no matching records for that periodWiden the range, or confirm the underlying activity happened (orders, subscriptions, procurement, etc.)

Frequently asked questions

Q. Do these numbers match the Revenue and Finance screens exactly?

They are computed from the same source tables, so revenue, GST and order counts line up. Reports is the cross-functional summary; the dedicated Admin → Growth → Revenue screen adds a full transaction ledger and revenue-recognition breakdowns for accountants.

Q. Can I choose a custom start and end date?

The screen offers the nine preset chips. The backend endpoint itself accepts explicit from and to parameters, but the standard board UI drives it through the presets — pick the closest preset for on-screen analysis, and export if you need to reshape the period offline.

Q. Why is "Net Profit" different from our accountant's figure?

Net profit here is a management proxy: revenue minus milk procurement cost minus approved operating expenses. It does not include payroll, depreciation, taxes beyond GST collected, or other book adjustments. Use it to watch the trend, not to close the books.

Q. Are cancelled or pending orders counted in revenue?

No. Revenue, GST, average order value and the sales tables count only orders with status PAID. Pending and other statuses still appear in the "Orders by status" breakdown and the "Total Orders" count, but they contribute no revenue.

DOODLYAdmin · Growth · Revenue
§Admin · Growth

Revenue

One live revenue-analytics and revenue-recognition screen: eighteen headline KPI cards, a revenue-trend chart, three money breakdowns (by source, by payment method, by segment) and a full, searchable transaction ledger — every figure recognised on demand from the authoritative DOODLY database, ready to export to CSV, Excel or PDF.

ℹ️
Note — Revenue is read-only, and it has no tables of its own

The Revenue screen never edits an order, a payment or a wallet. It recognises revenue by reading the real source tables — orders and their items, payments and payment records, the wallet ledger, B2B business orders, invoices and payment refunds — and computes every total on the fly. A "revenue record" is simply a projection of an Order; there is no duplicate "revenue" table to keep in sync, so every number is the true, current state of the business.

💡
How the data reaches this screen

Opening Admin → Growth → Revenue calls GET /api/admin/revenue?view=dashboard&preset=… for the KPIs and charts, and GET /api/admin/revenue?view=records&… for the ledger. Clicking a row calls GET /api/admin/revenue?view=detail&id=…. A green "● Live — revenue recognised from the DOODLY database" banner confirms the source URL and the resolved date range. Every filter change re-fetches; nothing is cached in the browser.

Screen 1 — Revenue Board (live analytics)

The top of the page: a date-range toolbar, eighteen KPI cards, a revenue-trend bar chart and three horizontal money breakdowns.

Purpose Give finance and leadership a one-look, always-current picture of recognised revenue — how much, from where, by which payment method and from which customer segment — for any period.
Access Super AdminAdminAccountant — the roles granted revenue:view. Support, Operations, Procurement, Inventory, Quality, Marketing, Delivery Executive and Customer cannot open it (they get a 403). Path Admin → Growth → Revenue
Revenue — Live revenue analytics & recognition · date presets · Export CSV · Excel · Export PDF · ↻ Refresh
Range toolbarNine preset chips (Today → All time) + Export CSV · Excel · Export PDF · ↻ Refresh
KPI strip (18 cards)Today / week / month / year / total revenue, revenue by source, wallet, pending, refunds, gross, GST, net, AOV, MRR, ARR
Revenue trend chartBar chart of revenue per day (or per month); hover shows the rupee value
Revenue by sourceHorizontal bars: Milk Subscription · One-Time · Add-on · Trial Pack · B2B
Payment method distributionHorizontal bars: UPI · Card · Net Banking · Wallet · Cash
Revenue by segmentHorizontal bars: B2C — Consumers vs B2B — Businesses
Revenue records ledgerSearchable, filterable, paginated transaction table (Screen 2)
Live bannerGreen "● Live" line naming the database URL and the resolved range
Figure 1 — The Revenue board: a range toolbar over the KPI strip, one trend chart, three money breakdowns and the transaction ledger.

The date-range toolbar

A single row of preset chips on the left and four action buttons on the right. Clicking a chip highlights it, re-loads the whole board (dashboard + ledger) for that range, and logs a "filtered" audit event. The board opens on Last 30d by default.

Preset chipRange it selectsTrend granularity
TodayMidnight today → nowDaily bars
YesterdayThe previous full calendar dayDaily bars
Last 7dThe last seven days including todayDaily bars
Last 30d defaultThe last thirty days including todayDaily bars
This month1st of this month → end of this monthDaily bars
Last monthThe whole previous calendar monthDaily bars
QuarterThe current calendar quarterMonthly bars
Financial yrIndian financial year — 1 April to 31 MarchMonthly bars
All timeNo date bound on the ledger — the entire historySee note below
ℹ️
Note — daily vs. monthly bars, and how "All time" behaves

The revenue-trend chart switches automatically: if the resolved span is 45 days or shorter the bars are drawn one per day; anything longer (Quarter, Financial yr) is bucketed into the last six months of monthly bars. Because All time sends no explicit start/end to the dashboard, the trend chart falls back to a last-30-day daily window even though the ledger below shows the full history. The KPI cards are lifetime regardless (see the warning under the KPI strip).

KPI strip — the 18 headline cards

Across the top sits a strip of eighteen number cards. Every money figure is shown in whole rupees — the database stores paise and the screen divides by 100 and formats with Indian digit grouping (for example ₹1,24,500).

KPI cardWhat it showsHow it is computed (source)
Today's RevenueRevenue booked since midnight todaySum of totalPaise on PAID orders created today
This WeekRevenue over the last seven daysPAID orders created since the start of the day six days ago
This MonthRevenue this calendar month to datePAID orders since the 1st of the month
This YearRevenue this calendar year to datePAID orders since 1 January
Total RevenueLifetime revenue from paid ordersSum of totalPaise across all PAID orders
Subscription RevLifetime revenue from milk subscriptionsPAID orders of type SUBSCRIPTION
One-Time RevLifetime revenue from one-time ordersPAID orders of type ONE_TIME
Trial Pack RevLifetime revenue from trial packsPAID orders of type SAMPLE
B2B RevenueMoney actually collected from business ordersSum of paidPaise across all BusinessOrders
Wallet RevenueWallet money customers spent on ordersSum of wallet DEBIT transactions of kind "usage"
Pending PaymentsValue of orders still awaiting paymentSum of totalPaise on orders with status PENDING
RefundsTotal money refundedSum of amountPaise across all PaymentRefund rows
Gross RevenueOrder value before discount, tax and deliverySum of subtotalPaise across all PAID orders
GST CollectedTax charged on paid ordersSum of order taxPaise across PAID orders
Net RevenueCollected revenue after refundsTotal revenue − total refunds
Avg Order ValueMean value of a paid orderTotal revenue ÷ count of PAID orders
MRRMonthly recurring revenue (proxy)This calendar month's SUBSCRIPTION revenue
ARR (est.)Annual recurring revenue estimateMRR × 12
⚠️
Warning — the KPI cards are lifetime totals, not range-scoped

With the exception of the four period cards (Today / This Week / This Month / This Year), every KPI card is computed over the whole business history and does not change when you click a preset chip. The preset chip re-scopes only the revenue-trend chart and the ledger table below. This is by design — the strip is a "state of the business" summary. Use the trend chart, the ledger filters and the exports for period-specific analysis.

ℹ️
Note — "B2B Revenue" is money collected, not money billed

The B2B Revenue card shows the total paid amount across business orders (paidPaise). What has been invoiced but not yet paid is tracked separately as outstanding B2B — you will find the billed-vs-collected picture under Admin → B2B → Invoices and Admin → Finance. MRR is a proxy (this month's subscription revenue) and ARR is simply that figure multiplied by twelve; treat both as directional indicators, not booked contract values.

Charts and money breakdowns

A two-column grid holds four panels: one vertical bar-trend chart and three horizontal money-breakdown panels. Every breakdown row is drawn as a labelled bar with the rupee value on the right, sorted largest first.

PanelWhat it plotsScope & notes
Revenue trendRevenue per day (or per month) across the resolved rangeRange-scoped. Money bars; hover a bar to read its exact ₹ value
Revenue by sourceMilk Subscription · One-Time Order · Add-on Product · Trial Pack, plus a B2B Orders row when B2B has been collectedLifetime. One bar per order type; B2B appended from collected business orders
Payment method distributionRecognised revenue split by UPI · Card · Net Banking · Wallet · CashLifetime. Grouped from PaymentRecord by method (net amount)
Revenue by segmentB2C — Consumers vs B2B — BusinessesLifetime. Segments with zero revenue are hidden
ℹ️
Note — a panel can read "No data in this range."

If there is genuinely nothing to plot, the panel shows a small "No data in this range." message instead of empty bars. For the three breakdowns this simply means no lifetime revenue exists in that dimension yet; for the trend chart it means no PAID orders fell inside the resolved window.

Buttons on the board

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Preset chip (Today … All time)Re-loads the board (dashboard + ledger) for that rangeGET /api/admin/revenue?view=dashboard&preset=… ; then reloads records; POST logs revenue.filteredSuper Admin+ revenue:view
Export CSVDownloads a .csv with a title line, the 18 KPIs and the currently-filtered ledgerBuilt client-side; fetches the ledger (server caps at 200 rows); POST logs revenue.exported [csv]Super Admin+ revenue:view
ExcelThe same content downloaded as an Excel-openable .xls fileClient-side; POST logs revenue.exported [xls]Super Admin+ revenue:view
Export PDFOpens a formatted print window (KPIs + Revenue Records) ready to "Save as PDF"Client-side print window; POST logs revenue.exported [pdf]Super Admin+ revenue:view
↻ RefreshRe-fetches the live dashboard and shows a "Analytics refreshed" toastGET dashboard again; POST logs revenue.refreshedSuper Admin+ revenue:view
⚠️
Warning — allow pop-ups for Export PDF

Export PDF opens a new browser window. If your browser blocks pop-ups the screen shows "Allow pop-ups to export PDF" and nothing prints. Allow pop-ups for the admin site once and it works every time. CSV and Excel download directly and are never blocked.

✅
Best Practice — pick the range and filters before you export

The CSV/Excel/PDF exports include the ledger with the currently applied preset, source, status, sort and search. Choose your period and narrow the ledger first — for example "Financial yr" plus source "Milk Subscription" — let the board reload, then click Export. Because the server returns at most 200 ledger rows per export, tighten the filters for a large period rather than exporting "All time" in one shot.

Screen 2 — Revenue records ledger

The searchable, filterable, paginated transaction table at the foot of the board — one row per order, with a live record count.

Purpose Let an accountant audit revenue transaction by transaction — find a specific customer's order, filter to pending or refunded revenue, sort by value, and open any row for the full recognition breakdown.
Access Super AdminAdminAccountant — revenue:view Path Admin → Growth → Revenue → Revenue records
Revenue records — badge: N records · search · source · status · sort
Filter rowSearch box + All sources · All statuses · Sort selects
Ledger table11 columns: Revenue ID → Date; each row is clickable
Status badgeColour-coded Paid / Pending / Refunded / Failed
Pager"Page X of Y" with ← Prev / Next → (25 rows per page)
Figure 2 — The ledger sits below the charts; clicking any row opens the revenue record detail (Screen 3).

Filters, search and sort

A single filter row controls the ledger. The search box de-bounces (waits ~300 ms after you stop typing) and any change resets you to page 1 and re-fetches only the ledger. The date range comes from the preset chip at the very top of the board — the ledger always respects it.

ControlOptionsWhat it does (backend)
Search boxFree text — placeholder "Search REV ID, customer, invoice…"Matches the order ID, the customer name or phone, or the invoice number (contains)
Source selectAll sources · Milk Subscription · One-Time Order · Add-on Product · Trial PackFilters orders by type (SUBSCRIPTION / ONE_TIME / EXTRA / SAMPLE)
Status selectAll statuses · Paid · Pending · Refunded · FailedFilters orders by status (PAID / PENDING / REFUNDED / FAILED)
Sort selectLatest · Highest ₹ · Lowest ₹ · Customer A–ZOrders by createdAt desc / totalPaise desc / totalPaise asc / customer name asc
💡
Tip — the "Revenue ID" is a display code derived from the order

Each row's ID reads REV-XXXXXXXX, formed from the last eight characters of the underlying order ID (upper-cased). Search matches the customer name, phone, or invoice number most reliably. When you open a record its detail modal shows the same REV- code, so you can cross-reference it against an invoice or a payment.

Ledger table columns

ColumnShowsSource field
Revenue IDREV-XXXXXXXX display code (bold)"REV-" + last 8 of order.id
CustomerThe customer's name (or "—")order.user.name
SourceMilk Subscription / One-Time Order / Add-on Product / Trial Packorder.type (labelled)
ProductUp to three item names, then "+N" for the rest (or "—")order.items.productName
GrossOrder value before discount / tax / deliveryorder.subtotalPaise
GSTTax charged on the orderorder.taxPaise
WalletWallet money applied to this orderSum of wallet DEBIT "usage" for the order
NetThe order total (bold)order.totalPaise
MethodUPI / Card / Net Banking / Wallet / Cash (or "—")order.payment.method (labelled)
StatusColour-coded badge — see beloworder.status
DateOrder date (dd Mon yy)order.createdAt

Status badge colours

StatusBadge toneMeaning
PAIDGreenPayment captured — this order is recognised revenue
PENDINGAmberAwaiting payment — value shows under "Pending Payments", not revenue
REFUNDEDGreyRefunded — subtracted from Net Revenue via the refund total
FAILED (or other)RedPayment failed or cancelled

Ledger behaviour at a glance

PropertyDetail
Record count badgeShows "N record(s)" — the total matching the current filters, next to the panel title
Pagination25 rows per page; "Page X of Y" with ← Prev / Next → (disabled at the ends)
SortingVia the Sort select only — Latest (default), Highest ₹, Lowest ₹, Customer A–Z
SearchOrder ID, customer name, customer phone, invoice number (de-bounced)
Row actionClick any row to open the revenue record detail modal (Screen 3)
Empty state"No revenue records match these filters." across the table
Error state"Your role can't view revenue records (403)." or "Couldn't load records."
ℹ️
Note — the ledger lists all order statuses, not just PAID

Unlike the revenue KPIs (which count only PAID orders), the ledger is a full order projection and will list PENDING, REFUNDED and FAILED rows too, so you can audit them. Their value is still classified correctly in the KPI strip — pending value under "Pending Payments", refunds under "Refunds" — even though the row appears here.

Screen 3 — Revenue record detail

The modal opened by clicking a ledger row — the full revenue-recognition breakdown for one order.

Purpose Show, for a single order, exactly how its revenue was built up — customer, gross-to-net breakdown, line items, payment method, wallet usage, refunds and the order's audit history — so an accountant can reconcile the record end to end.
Access Super AdminAdminAccountant — revenue:view Path Admin → Growth → Revenue → (click a ledger row)
Revenue record — REV-XXXXXXXX · status badge · × close
HeaderREV code + status badge; source · date · invoice number
CustomerName · Phone · Wallet balance
Revenue breakdownGross · Discount · GST · Delivery · Deposit · Net revenue
Line itemsProduct (· variant) · Qty · Amount
PaymentMethod · Wallet used
Wallet usage / RefundsWallet transactions and any refunds (→ wallet flagged)
Audit historyThe order's recent events with timestamps
FooterClose
Figure 3 — The detail modal overlays the board; press Esc, click the backdrop, × or Close to dismiss it.

Opening the modal calls GET /api/admin/revenue?view=detail&id=<orderId> and logs a revenue.generated audit event. It shows the following sections, all read-only.

SectionFields shownSource
HeaderREV code, status badge, source label, order date, and "Invoice <number>" when an invoice existsorder + invoice
CustomerName · Phone · Wallet balanceorder.user (name, phone, walletPaise)
Revenue breakdownGross · Discount (shown as "− ₹…") · GST · Delivery · Deposit · Net revenueorder subtotal / discount / tax / delivery / deposit / total
Line itemsProduct name (with variant label if any) · Qty · line Amountorder.items
PaymentMethod · Wallet usedPaymentRecord (falls back to order.payment)
Wallet usageEach wallet transaction: kind · amount · reference (or "No wallet usage.")walletTxn for the order
RefundsEach refund: amount · reference, flagged "→ wallet" when refunded to wallet (or "No refunds.")PaymentRecord.refunds
Audit historyThe order's recent events — type + timestamp (or "No history.")order.events (latest 30)

Buttons in the detail modal

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Close / × / Esc / backdropDismisses the modal and returns to the boardNone—
ℹ️
Note — the detail is view-only

This modal has no edit, refund or resend controls. Refunds are issued from Admin → Finance → Payments and the invoice is managed under Billing / Invoices; Revenue only reflects what those screens have already done. If a refund is missing here, check that it was captured against the payment record for this order.

Workflow — from open to reconciled record
Open RevenueBoard loads live KPIs & chartsPick a date presetFilter / search the ledgerClick a rowRead the recognition breakdownExport CSV / Excel / PDFAction audit-logged
Figure 4 — Every open, filter, drill-down and export is recorded in the audit trail.
ℹ️
Note — everything is audit-logged

Filtering, refreshing, opening a record and exporting each send a POST to /api/admin/revenue that writes to the audit trail — actions revenue.filtered, revenue.refreshed, revenue.generated and revenue.exported (with the format captured). The endpoint also recognises a revenue.recalculated event, which is the only one that needs revenue:edit (Super Admin / Admin) rather than revenue:view. Review who pulled which figures and when under Admin → System → Audit Logs.

✅
Best Practice — reconcile Revenue against Payments and Reports monthly

Revenue, Payments and Reports all read the same source tables, so their headline figures line up. Once a month, cross-check Net Revenue here against the Admin → Finance → Payments collected total and the Admin → Growth → Reports financial drill-down. If they diverge, look for orders stuck in PENDING (in the ledger) or refunds captured against a payment but not reflected in an order status.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Board stays on "Loading live revenue analytics…" then shows "⚠ Backend offline"The next-app backend is not running / not reachableStart or restart the backend; the banner names the exact base URL it tried
"⚠ Your role can't view Revenue — Finance / Accountant / Admin only (403)"Your role lacks revenue:viewSign in as Super Admin, Admin or Accountant, or ask a Super Admin to grant the revenue permission
KPI cards don't change when I switch presetsExpected — all cards except Today / Week / Month / Year are lifetime totalsUse the revenue-trend chart, the ledger filters and the exports for period figures
"All time" trend chart looks like only the last monthAll time sends no explicit range, so the trend falls back to a 30-day daily windowThe ledger below still shows the full history; pick "Financial yr" for a longer trend
Trend chart flips from daily bars to monthly barsThe resolved range is longer than 45 days (Quarter, Financial yr)Pick a shorter preset (Last 30d or shorter) to see per-day bars again
Searching "REV-…" finds nothingSearch matches the raw order ID, name, phone or invoice — not the display REV- codeSearch by customer name, phone or invoice number instead
Export PDF does nothingThe browser blocked the pop-up windowAllow pop-ups for the admin site, then click again (CSV/Excel are unaffected)
