A CSV export seems to miss rows for a big periodThe server caps a ledger export at 200 rowsNarrow the range/source/status filters and export in slices
Ledger shows a PENDING or FAILED row inside "Revenue"Expected — the ledger is a full order projection, not just PAIDUse the Status filter to show only Paid, or read the KPI strip for recognised revenue

Frequently asked questions

Q. What exactly counts as "revenue" on this screen?

Recognised revenue is the value of orders with status PAID (their totalPaise), plus collected B2B business orders. Pending orders are shown separately as "Pending Payments", and refunds are subtracted to give "Net Revenue". A pending or failed order can appear in the ledger for auditing, but it contributes no recognised revenue.

Q. Why is B2B Revenue lower than what we invoiced this month?

The B2B Revenue card shows money actually collected (paidPaise), not billed. The difference — billed but unpaid — is B2B outstanding, visible on the B2B Invoices and Finance screens. Revenue deliberately recognises cash collected, not credit extended.

Q. Are MRR and ARR real contract figures?

No. MRR is a proxy — the current calendar month's subscription revenue — and ARR is MRR × 12. They are useful trend indicators but are not derived from active-subscription contract values, so do not use them as booked recurring revenue.

Q. Do the Revenue numbers match the Reports and Payments screens?

Yes — all three read the same authoritative tables. Reports is the cross-functional summary, Payments is the gateway/settlement view, and Revenue adds the transaction ledger and the gross-to-net recognition breakdown that finance needs. Small wording differences aside, revenue, GST and order counts reconcile.

Q. Can I choose a custom start and end date?

The board is driven by the nine preset chips. The underlying endpoint also accepts explicit from and to parameters, but the standard UI uses presets — pick the closest one for on-screen analysis, then export if you need to reshape the exact period offline.

DOODLYGrowth · Search Insights
§Growth

Search Insights

See exactly what customers and staff type into the DOODLY global search — how often, what they find, what they don't, on which device — and curate the trending searches that greet every empty search box.

Every DOODLY page carries a single global search box (open it with Ctrl-K / ⌘K, the header magnifier, the dashboard search bar, or the mobile menu). Whenever a customer runs a search, gets no results, or clicks a result, that action is quietly logged to the database. Search Insights is the reporting cockpit for that stream: a live analytics dashboard, a searchable event ledger, and a manager for the "trending" chips shown when the box is empty. It reads from the SearchEvent and TrendingSearch tables through the /api/search/admin endpoint — no spreadsheets, no manual counting.

ℹ️
Note — two tabs, two permission levels

The screen has two tabs: Analytics (read-only reporting, open to any manager who can view Reports or the CMS) and Trending (editing the search chips, restricted to CMS editors — Super Admin, Admin and Marketing). The page opens on Analytics.

Analytics tab

Live KPIs, volume trend, device & scope breakdowns, and top-keyword tables

Purpose Show, for a chosen date range, how much the search is used, whether people find what they want, and which terms and results dominate.
Access Super AdminAdminMarketingAccountantOperationsProcurementInventoryQuality — anyone who can view Reports or the CMS. Path Admin → Growth → Search Insights → Analytics
Search Insights — Analytics · [Analytics | Trending] tabs · live database banner
Date-range toolbarPreset chips: Today · Last 7d · Last 30d · This month · Financial yr · All time. Buttons: Export CSV · Export PDF · ↻ Refresh
KPI strip (12 cards)Total Searches, Searches Today, Unique Users, Success Rate, No-Result Searches, Avg Search Time, Product Searches, Category Searches, Trending Terms, Conversion Rate, Exit Rate, Result Clicks
Search volume trendBar chart of queries over time — daily for ranges ≤45 days, otherwise the last 6 months
Searches by deviceHorizontal bars — mobile / tablet / desktop
Searches by scopeHorizontal bars — public / customer / admin
Top search keywordsTable: Keyword · Searches (top 12)
Zero-result searchesTable: Missed keyword · Times (top 12)
Most clicked resultsTable: Result · Clicks (top 12)
Search event ledgerFilterable, paginated table of individual events (see next screen)
Figure 1 — Analytics tab layout. A green "● Live" banner at the top confirms the data source and the active date range.

The date-range toolbar

The preset chips set the reporting window; the dashboard reloads instantly when you pick one. The default on open is Last 30d. Choosing a range also re-scopes the volume-trend chart's granularity and the "range" figures.

PresetWhat it coversTrend chart granularity
TodayMidnight today → nowDaily
Last 7dThe last 7 calendar daysDaily
Last 30d (default)The last 30 calendar daysDaily
This month1st of this month → month endDaily
Financial yr1 Apr → 31 Mar of the current Indian FYMonthly (last 6 months)
All timeEvery event ever recordedMonthly (last 6 months)
💡
Tip — when the chart switches to months

Ranges of 45 days or less draw one bar per day; anything longer draws one bar per month for the last six months. So "Financial yr" and "All time" show a monthly shape, while the shorter presets show day-by-day detail.

The 12 KPI cards

Every figure is computed server-side from the SearchEvent table for the selected range (Total, Success, No-Result and the rates are all-time; Searches Today and the range figures respect the window).

KPI cardWhat it meansHow it is calculated (backend)
Total SearchesAll queries ever runCount of events where kind = query
Searches TodayQueries since midnightQuery events with createdAt ≥ today 00:00
Unique UsersDistinct searchersDistinct userId, falling back to distinct sessionId when not signed in
Success RateShare of searches that returned something(Total queries − no-result queries) ÷ total, as a %
No-Result SearchesSearches that found nothingCount of events where kind = noresult
Avg Search TimeAverage response time in millisecondsAverage of the client-measured durationMs on query events
Product SearchesQueries that map to a productTop terms matched against live product slugs/names
Category SearchesQueries that map to a categoryTop terms matched against live category slugs/names
Trending TermsActive chips shown in the empty boxCount of TrendingSearch rows with active = true
Conversion RateHow often a search leads to a clickTotal result clicks ÷ total queries, as a %
Exit RateHow often a search dead-endsNo-result queries ÷ total queries, as a %
Result ClicksResults opened from searchCount of events where kind = click

Charts and keyword tables

Below the KPIs sit five visual panels. The Search volume trend is a bar chart of query counts over the range. Searches by device and Searches by scope are proportional bar breakdowns. Three tables — Top search keywords, Zero-result searches and Most clicked results — each list up to 12 rows ranked by frequency. Empty panels read "No data yet." rather than showing a blank box.

✅
Best Practice — mine the Zero-result table weekly

The Zero-result searches table is the single most valuable panel here: it is a free, honest list of demand you are not meeting. If "lassi" or "butter" keep appearing with no results, that is a signal to add a product, a synonym, or a help article. Turn recurring misses into action items every week.

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Preset chip (Today … All time)Re-runs the whole dashboard for that windowGET /api/search/admin?view=dashboard&preset=…; also writes a search.filtered audit entrySuper Admin+ view
Export CSVDownloads a multi-section CSV (KPIs, top keywords, zero-result, most clicked) named doodly-search-insights-<from>_<to>.csvBuilt in the browser from the loaded data; logs a search.exported audit entrySuper Admin+ view
Export PDFOpens a print-ready report in a new tab and triggers the print dialog (save as PDF). Needs pop-ups allowed.Client-rendered print page; logs search.exportedSuper Admin+ view
↻ RefreshReloads the dashboard and shows an "Analytics refreshed" toastRe-calls the dashboard endpoint; logs search.refreshedSuper Admin+ view
ℹ️
Note — exports are audited, not silent

Every filter change, refresh and export writes an entry to the platform Audit Log (actions search.filtered, search.refreshed, search.exported). Super Admins can see who pulled which report and when from Admin → System → Audit Logs.

Search event ledger

Row-by-row record of every individual search, click and miss

Purpose Drill from the aggregate KPIs down to the raw events — to investigate a spike, trace one customer's search journey, or audit a specific keyword.
Access Super AdminAdmin+ (same view permission as Analytics) Path Admin → Growth → Search Insights → Analytics → Search event ledger

The ledger lives at the bottom of the Analytics tab. It reads the SearchEvent table directly with its own filters and pagination, so it can slice far deeper than the summary tables. A badge next to the title shows the total number of matching events.

Search event ledger — total count badge
Filter rowKeyword/target/session search box + three dropdowns: Event, Scope, Device
Events tableKeyword · Event · Target · Scope · Device · Results · Time · When
Pager"Page X of Y" with ← Prev / Next → (25 rows per page)
Figure 2 — the event ledger: filters, table and pager.

The event colour-codes each row by kind: no-result events show a red badge, click events a green badge, and ordinary query events a grey badge.

PropertyDetail (from the live table config)
ColumnsKeyword · Event (query/click/noresult badge) · Target (clicked item, or "—") · Scope · Device · Results (result count, or "—") · Time (durationMs in ms, or "—") · When (date & time, en-IN)
Search boxMatches against the keyword, target, user id and session id in one field (debounced ~300 ms)
FiltersEvent = All / Query / Click / No result · Scope = All / Public / Customer / Admin · Device = All / Mobile / Tablet / Desktop
SortingNewest first by default (server supports oldest-first)
Pagination25 events per page; ← Prev / Next → buttons; disabled at the ends
Row actionsNone — the ledger is read-only (events are immutable facts)
Empty state"No search events match these filters."
ℹ️
Note — what "Scope" tells you

Scope records who was searching: public = a visitor who is not signed in, customer = a signed-in customer, admin = a staff member searching inside the admin panel. It is derived server-side from the session, so it cannot be spoofed by the browser.

Trending tab

Curate the search chips shown when the box is empty

Purpose Decide which suggested searches greet every customer the moment they open the search box, steering them toward hero products and promotions.
Access Super AdminAdminMarketing — CMS editors only (managing trending needs the CMS edit right). Path Admin → Growth → Search Insights → Trending

When a customer opens search and hasn't typed anything yet, DOODLY shows a row of tappable "trending" chips. Tapping one runs that search instantly. This tab is where you add, hide and delete those chips. Terms are stored in the TrendingSearch table and are seeded automatically the first time (A2 Buffalo Milk, Trial Pack, 30-Day Morning Ritual, Delivery Tracking, Wallet, Bottle Return).

Search Insights — Trending
Explainer line"These appear in the search overlay when the box is empty… Toggle to hide without deleting."
Trending tableTerm · Status (Active/Hidden badge) · Actions (Hide/Show · Delete)
Add row"Add a trending search…" text field + Add button
Figure 3 — the Trending manager: a status table plus an add-a-term row.

Add a trending search — field

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Add a trending searchThe term customers see as a chip and search when tappedFree textYesMinimum 2 characters (checked in the browser and again server-side); appended at the end of the order. Duplicate terms are rejected (the term is unique in the database).Buffalo Ghee

Row & form actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
AddCreates a new chip and shows a "<term> added" toastPOST /api/search/admin {action:"add"} → new TrendingSearch row with the next sort order; audited as search.trending.addSuper AdminAdminMarketing
Hide / ShowToggles a term's visibility without deleting it (badge flips Active ↔ Hidden)POST … {action:"toggle", active} → flips TrendingSearch.active; audited as search.trending.toggleSuper AdminAdminMarketing
DeletePermanently removes the term (after a confirm prompt); shows a "Deleted" toastPOST … {action:"remove", id} → deletes the row; audited as search.trending.removeSuper AdminAdminMarketing
⚠️
Warning — Hide vs. Delete

Only the customer-facing search overlay pulls active terms (the live storefront shows up to 8 active chips, ordered by sort order). Use Hide for a term you might bring back — a seasonal promo, for instance — so its history and position are kept. Use Delete only for terms you never want again.

⛔
Caution — Delete is irreversible

Delete removes the TrendingSearch row for good. There is no undo and no soft-delete; the only way back is to add the term again as a fresh entry (which lands at the end of the order). The confirm prompt is your last checkpoint.

How the data gets here

From a keystroke in the search box to a row on this dashboard

Purpose Understand what each metric is actually counting so you read the numbers correctly.
Access Super Admin — background reference Path n/a — automatic

Nobody enters this data by hand. The global search palette records three kinds of event automatically and posts them to /api/search/log, which writes a row to the SearchEvent table. Search Insights then reads and aggregates those rows.

Workflow — a search event's journey
Customer types in the search box (Ctrl-K)Palette records kind = query / noresult / click + device, session, result count, response timePOST /api/search/log (fire-and-forget)Row saved to SearchEvent tableSearch Insights aggregates → KPIs, charts, tables, ledger
Figure 4 — every metric on this page is built from these logged events.
Event kindFired when…Feeds these KPIs
queryA search returns at least one resultTotal Searches, Searches Today, Success Rate, volume trend, top keywords
noresultA search returns zero resultsNo-Result Searches, Exit Rate, Zero-result table (and it lowers Success Rate)
clickA customer opens a result from searchResult Clicks, Conversion Rate, Most-clicked table
Field on each eventMeaning
termThe normalised search keyword (lower-cased, trimmed; events shorter than 2 characters are dropped)
targetThe title of the clicked result (click events only)
scopepublic / customer / admin — derived from the session, server-side
resultCountHow many results the query showed (0 for a no-result)
devicemobile / tablet / desktop — inferred from the window width
sessionId · userIdGroup one search journey; power the Unique Users count
durationMsClient-measured response time, averaged into Avg Search Time
ℹ️
Note — logging never blocks the customer

Analytics logging is fire-and-forget: if the logging call fails, the customer's search still works normally, and the browser also keeps a small offline cache (doodly-search in localStorage) so counts aren't lost while the backend is briefly unreachable. This means the numbers are highly reliable but not guaranteed to the last event.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common questions and quick fixes

Purpose Resolve the issues staff most often hit on this screen.
Access Super Admin reference Path Admin → Growth → Search Insights
SymptomLikely causeFix
Banner reads "Backend offline — start next-app"The application server isn't running / unreachableStart or restart the backend; the dashboard reloads once the API responds
"Your role can't view Search Insights (403)"The signed-in role lacks both Reports-view and CMS-viewSign in as a role with Reports or CMS access (Super Admin, Admin, Marketing, Accountant, Operations, etc.)
Trending edits give "Only CMS editors can manage trending (403)"Managing trending needs the CMS edit rightHave a Super Admin, Admin or Marketing user make the change
All KPIs show 0 / tables say "No data yet"No search events in the selected range yetWiden the range to "All time"; if still empty, confirm the storefront search box is live and being used
Export PDF does nothingThe browser blocked the pop-up windowAllow pop-ups for the admin site, then click Export PDF again
A trending term won't addUnder 2 characters, or a duplicate of an existing termUse a longer, unique term; hide the existing one instead of re-adding

Do these numbers include staff searches?

Yes. Staff searches inside the admin panel are logged with scope = admin. To see only customer behaviour, use the ledger's Scope filter and pick Customer (or Public for signed-out visitors).

Why is Success Rate all-time even when I pick "Last 7d"?

The volume-trend chart and the "range" figures follow the preset, but the headline KPIs like Total Searches, Success Rate and No-Result Searches are computed across all events for stability. Use the ledger with a date filter when you need a strictly range-bound view of individual events.

If I delete a trending term, does it remove those searches from analytics?

No. Trending terms and search events are separate. Deleting a chip only stops it being suggested; every past search anyone ran on that term stays in the analytics and the ledger.

Can I reorder trending chips?

New terms are added to the end of the list (next sort order), and the storefront shows up to 8 active terms in that order. There is no drag-to-reorder control on this screen; to move a term to the front, delete it and re-add it, or hide the ones you want to drop below it.

✅
Best Practice — align trending with what's live

Keep the trending chips in step with your current catalogue and promotions, and cross-check them against the Top search keywords table. Promoting a term customers already search for (or a new product you're launching) turns the empty search box into a zero-cost merchandising slot.

DOODLYAdmin · Growth · Coupons
§Admin · Growth

Coupons

Create and manage discount codes — percentage or flat-value — that customers type at checkout to reduce their order total, with live usage, redemption limits and revenue tracking.

ℹ️
Where this lives

Open it from Admin → Growth → Coupons (URL /admin/coupons.html). The whole page is driven live from the DOODLY database through the endpoint /api/admin/coupons. When the page loads you will see a green banner reading “● Live — N coupon(s) from the DOODLY database”. If the banner is red, the backend is offline or your role is not allowed to view coupons — see Troubleshooting.

Coupon List & Dashboard

The landing screen — headline numbers on top, a searchable table of every coupon below.

Purpose See how every discount code is performing at a glance, then drill into any one to edit it or read its full redemption history.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing can view and manage; Accountant and other reporting roles can view only. Path Admin → Growth → Coupons
Header — Coupons · “Discount codes and usage.” · [＋ Create coupon]
KPI strip (8 cards)Total Coupons · Active · Scheduled · Expired · Redeemed · Discount Given (₹) · Revenue via Coupons (₹) · Most Used
Live status bannerGreen “● Live — N coupon(s)…” on success; red warning if offline or blocked
ToolbarSearch box · filter chips (All / Active / Scheduled / Paused) · sort · export · pagination
Coupon tableColumns: Code · Description · Usage · Status · Manage
Figure — the Coupons list screen: KPI dashboard on top, filterable data table below.

The 8 dashboard cards (KPI strip)

These are calculated live from the Coupon and CouponRedemption tables (endpoint /api/admin/coupons?view=dashboard). They sit above the table.

CardWhat it countsNotes
Total CouponsAll coupons that are not soft-deleted.The full catalogue, active or not.
ActiveCoupons live right now.Marked Active — see the status rules below.
ScheduledCoupons whose start date is still in the future.Turn Active automatically on their start date.
ExpiredCoupons whose expiry date has passed.No longer accepted at checkout.
RedeemedTotal number of redemptions across all coupons ever.Counts every recorded use.
Discount GivenTotal rupee value of discount handed out.Sum of every redemption's discount.
Revenue via CouponsTotal order value of orders that used a coupon.Order totals on which a code was applied.
Most UsedThe single most-redeemed coupon code.Shows “—” when nothing has been redeemed yet.
ℹ️
Two more figures behind the scenes

The dashboard endpoint also returns an average discount per redemption, a conversion rate (discount as a percentage of revenue), and coupons used today. These feed the analytics layer even though the eight cards above are what appear on screen.

The coupon table

Every coupon is a row. The table is rendered by the shared DOODLY DataTable engine, so it supports search, sorting, filtering, export and pagination.

ElementDetail (from the real table config)
ColumnsCode (the code the customer types, e.g. FRESH10) · Description (the coupon's Name, plus its Campaign after a “·” if set) · Usage (times used, shown as used / limit when a total limit exists, otherwise just the count) · Status (colour badge) · Manage (opens the edit dialog).
Filter chipsAll · Active · Scheduled · Paused — quick facet filters above the table.
SearchFree-text box. The backend matches on code, name, campaign and description (case-insensitive).
SortingNewest first by default. The backend also supports sort by usage (most-redeemed), expiry (soonest), name (A→Z) and last-updated.
PaginationUp to 500 coupons are loaded into the page; the DataTable pages through them.
ExportThe toolbar's Export button downloads the visible table as CSV.
Row actionManage opens the Create/Edit Coupon dialog (next screen) pre-filled with that coupon.

Status badge — what each colour means

A coupon's status is worked out automatically from its flags and dates (it is never typed in). The order of precedence is:

StatusShown whenAccepted at checkout?
DeletedThe coupon has been soft-deleted.No.
InactiveThe Active switch is off. (The list's “Paused” filter chip maps to this state.)No.
ScheduledThe start date is in the future.No — not yet.
ExpiredThe expiry date has passed.No — anymore.
ActiveSwitched on, started, and not expired.Yes.
⚠️
“Paused” vs “Inactive”

The filter chip reads Paused, but a coupon you switch off shows the badge Inactive. They mean the same thing — a live coupon that has been turned off with the Active checkbox. Filtering by “Paused” finds your switched-off codes.

Create / Edit Coupon

The dialog where you build a new code or change an existing one.

Purpose Define exactly what the discount is, who can use it, on what, and for how long — then save it live.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing only. Other roles that can view coupons get a “403 — only Marketing / Admin can manage coupons” error if they try to save. Path Admin → Growth → Coupons → Create coupon (or the Manage link on any row)

Click ＋ Create coupon (top-right) for a blank form, or Manage on a row to edit that coupon. The dialog title reads “Create coupon” or “Edit <CODE>”.

Dialog — Create coupon / Edit CODE · ✕ close
Row 1Code * (with 🎲 random-code button) · Name
Row 2Campaign · Discount type (Percentage % / Flat ₹)
Row 3Value * (% or ₹, hint changes) · Max discount (₹)
Row 4Min order (₹) · Usage limit (total)
Row 5Per-customer limit · Expiry date
Row 6Applicable products (slugs) · Applicable plans (slugs)
Checkboxes☐ First order only ☑ Active
Footer(edit only) Duplicate · Delete/Restore | Cancel · Create coupon / Save changes
Figure — the Create/Edit Coupon dialog with its two-column field layout.

Field reference

Every field below is real. Values you type in rupees are stored internally in paise (1 ₹ = 100 paise) and in basis points for percentages, but you only ever work in ₹ and %.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
CodeWhat the customer types at checkout.Letters, numbers, hyphen and underscore only.Yes3–40 characters; automatically stored in UPPERCASE; must be unique (a duplicate code is rejected). The 🎲 button fills a random “DOODLY####” code.FRESH10
NameShort human label shown in the Description column.Free text, up to 120 chars.Opt.Trimmed; blank is stored as empty.10% off first subscription
CampaignGroups coupons under a marketing campaign for reporting.Free text, up to 120 chars.Opt.Appears after the name in the list and drives the “by campaign” report.Diwali 2026
Discount typeHow the discount is calculated.Percentage % · Flat ₹YesDefaults to Percentage. Switching it changes the Value hint.Percentage %
ValueThe size of the discount.A number — a percent when type is %, a rupee amount when type is Flat.YesMust be greater than zero. A percentage cannot exceed 100%.10 (= 10%)
Max discount (₹)Caps a percentage discount at a ceiling.Rupee amount.Opt.Only meaningful for percentage coupons; ignored for flat.150
Min order (₹)The smallest order the coupon works on.Rupee amount.Opt.Defaults to 0 (no minimum). Orders below this are rejected at checkout.499
Usage limit (total)How many times the coupon can be used in total, across all customers.Whole number.Opt.Must be a positive whole number. Blank = unlimited (∞). Once reached, the code stops working.1000
Per-customer limitHow many times one customer may use it.Whole number.Opt.Positive whole number. Blank = no per-customer cap.1
Expiry dateThe last day the coupon is valid.Date picker.Opt.After this date the status becomes Expired. Blank = never expires.2026-11-15
Applicable products (slugs)Restricts the coupon to specific products.Comma-separated product slugs.Opt.Blank = applies to all products. At checkout the cart must contain at least one listed product.milk,curd
Applicable plans (slugs)Restricts the coupon to specific subscription plans.Comma-separated plan slugs.Opt.Blank = applies to all plans. Otherwise the chosen plan must match.p30,p90
First order onlyLimits the coupon to a customer's very first paid order.Checkbox.Opt.When ticked, customers who already have a paid order are blocked.☑
ActiveMaster on/off switch.Checkbox.Opt.On by default. Unticking it makes the coupon Inactive immediately.☑
💡
Tip — flat vs percentage

Pick Flat ₹ when you want a fixed rupee cut (e.g. ₹50 off). Pick Percentage % for a proportional discount, and set Max discount so a big cart does not give away too much. A discount can never exceed the order total, so a flat ₹100 on a ₹80 cart only takes off ₹80.

Buttons in the dialog

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
🎲 (random code)Fills the Code box with a fresh “DOODLY####” code.Client-side only — no save.Marketing+
Create couponSaves a brand-new coupon.POST /api/admin/coupons action: create. Rejects a duplicate code; writes an audit entry.Marketing Admin Super Admin
Save changesUpdates the coupon you are editing.POST action: update. Audited.Marketing Admin Super Admin
Duplicate edit onlyMakes a copy of the coupon to tweak.POST action: duplicate — new code “<CODE>-COPY”, name “(copy)”, and it is created switched off so it can't go live by accident.Marketing+
Delete edit onlyRemoves the coupon from the live list.POST action: delete — a soft delete: it is hidden and switched off, not erased. Asks you to confirm first.Marketing+
Restore edit onlyBrings a deleted coupon back. (This button replaces Delete when the coupon is already deleted.)POST action: restore — clears the deleted flag.Marketing+
Cancel / ✕Closes the dialog without saving. (Pressing Esc or clicking outside also closes it.)None.—
⛔
Caution — deleting a live code

Delete is a soft-delete, so the redemption history is kept and you can Restore later — but the moment you delete, the code stops working at checkout. If a campaign is still running, switch Active off instead, or set an Expiry date, rather than deleting.

Workflow — coupon lifecycle
Create (draft) Active Customer applies at checkout Validated (limits & eligibility) Redeemed on paid order Expired / Deactivated / Deleted
Figure — a coupon's journey from creation to redemption and retirement.

How a coupon is checked at checkout

When a customer types a code, the storefront calls the validation service, which runs these checks in order and stops at the first failure — giving the customer a clear reason. This is why a coupon that looks Active can still be refused for a specific cart.

#CheckMessage the customer sees when it fails
1Not deleted“This coupon no longer exists.”
2Active switch is on“This coupon is not active.”
3Start date reached“This coupon is not yet active.”
4Not past expiry“This coupon has expired.”
5Order meets minimum value“Your order does not meet the minimum value for this coupon.”
6First-order rule (if set)“This coupon is valid on your first order only.”
7Customer eligibility (if restricted)“This coupon is not available for your account.”
8Total usage limit not reached“This coupon has reached its usage limit.”
9Per-customer limit not reached“You have already used this coupon the maximum number of times.”
10Product / plan applies to the cart“This coupon does not apply to the items in your cart.”
11Discount is greater than zero“This coupon gives no discount on this order.”
ℹ️
Redemption is recorded once, at payment

Validation happens as the customer types the code, but the usage count only goes up when the order is actually paid. The redemption is recorded atomically and is idempotent per order — so a retried payment never double-counts a coupon.

✅
Best Practice

Always set a Usage limit and an Expiry date on promotional codes, and add a Per-customer limit of 1 for welcome or first-order offers. This caps your maximum discount exposure and stops a single code from being shared and over-used. For a percentage coupon, also set a Max discount ceiling so a large basket never gives away more than you intend. Use the Campaign field consistently so the reports can total each promotion cleanly.

Who can do what

RoleView list & dashboardCreate / edit / delete
Super AdminYesYes
AdminYesYes
MarketingYes (this is the Marketing role's home page)Yes
AccountantYes (view only)No
Manager / support / operations with reporting accessYes (view only, where their role grants Reports or Customers view)No
Delivery Executive / CustomerNoNo
ℹ️
Note

Every create, update, activate, deactivate, delete, restore and duplicate is written to the central Audit Log with the actor's role and the coupon code — so there is always a record of who changed which promotion and when.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner “Your role can't view Coupons (403)”.Your account's role does not include coupon, reports or customer viewing.Ask a Super Admin to grant your role coupon-view (Marketing gets it by default).
Red banner “Backend offline… no coupons to display”.The DOODLY backend is unreachable.Confirm the backend is running and reachable; reload the page once it is back.
“Only Marketing / Admin can manage coupons (403)” when saving.You can view but not manage coupons.Have a Marketing, Admin or Super Admin user make the change.
“Coupon code <CODE> already exists.”A coupon (even a deleted one) already uses that code.Pick a different code — codes are unique. Use the 🎲 button for a fresh one, or Restore the existing coupon instead.
“Enter a coupon code (min 3 characters).” / “Enter a discount value greater than zero.”Code is too short/empty, or Value is 0 or blank.Give a code of 3+ characters (letters, numbers, - and _) and a Value above zero.
Customer says a valid-looking code is refused at checkout.An eligibility rule is failing — minimum order, first-order-only, usage or per-customer limit, or a product/plan restriction.Open the coupon and check those fields against the customer's cart; the checkout message names the exact reason.
Coupon shows Inactive though you expected it live.The Active checkbox is off (this is the “Paused” state).Edit the coupon and tick Active, then Save.

Frequently asked questions

Q. What is the difference between a Coupon and an Offer?

A Coupon is a code the customer must type at checkout (managed here). An Offer is a campaign-driven promotion managed on the separate Admin → Growth → Offers screen. Use Coupons for shareable discount codes.

Q. Can I change a coupon after customers have started using it?

Yes. Editing and saving updates the rules for all future redemptions; past redemptions are untouched and stay in the coupon's history and totals.

Q. How do I run the same coupon for a new campaign without retyping everything?

Open the coupon and click Duplicate. You get an identical copy with a “-COPY” code, created switched off. Rename its code, adjust the dates, then tick Active.

Q. What happens to redemption history if I delete a coupon?

Nothing is lost. Delete is a soft-delete — the code stops working but its redemptions, discount given and revenue stay recorded, and you can Restore the coupon later.

Q. Why does the Usage column sometimes show “∞”?

When you leave the total Usage limit blank, the coupon can be redeemed an unlimited number of times, so the list shows only the count with no ceiling.

DOODLYAdmin · Growth · Offers
§Admin · Growth

Offers

A campaign-driven promotion engine — create typed, prioritised, scheduled discounts that either apply automatically at checkout (codeless) or by code, then watch them earn revenue live through ten KPI cards and a single searchable, filterable table.

ℹ️
What this screen is, and how it is built

The Offers item under Admin → Growth opens the storefront admin page /admin/offers.html. That page renders a single block, offersBoard, which mounts into #offersMount and is driven entirely by the live DOODLY database through the API /api/admin/offers. Everything you see — the KPI cards, the filters, the table, the New offer and Manage forms — is real data; when the backend is reachable a green “● Live” banner confirms it and names the database. All money is typed and shown in ₹, but the backend stores every amount as integer paise (₹1 = 100 paise), so a 10% offer is stored as discountBps = 1000 and a ₹50 offer as flatPaise = 5000.

ℹ️
Offers vs Coupons — same maths, more power

Offers deliberately reuse the Coupons discount engine for all the arithmetic and eligibility rules (percent/flat, caps, minimum order, first-order, product/category/plan targeting, usage limits). What Offers add on top is a type (Percentage, BOGO, Bundle, Festival, Cashback and more), a numeric priority, a campaign name for roll-up reporting, and a full Draft → Active ⇄ Paused → Archived lifecycle. The other key difference: an offer’s code is optional. Leave it blank and the offer is codeless — it is resolved and applied automatically at checkout with no code to type.

ℹ️
Who can view and who can manage

Two permission levels guard this module. Viewing (the board, KPIs, table and analytics) is open to any role that can view offers, reports or customers — that is Super Admin, Admin, Marketing, and read-only viewers such as Accountant, Operations, Procurement, Quality, Inventory and Support (through their reports/customers access). Managing (create, edit, activate, pause, resume, archive, delete, restore, duplicate, bulk) requires the offers edit permission, which by default only Super Admin, Admin and Marketing hold. A blocked mutation returns HTTP 403 and the screen shows “Only Marketing / Admin can manage offers (403)”. The server-side checks canViewOffers / canManageOffers are the real boundary — hidden buttons are convenience only.

ℹ️
Status is derived — you set a state, the system decides the badge

You control an offer’s state (Draft / Active / Paused / Archived), but the coloured status badge on the table is computed from that state together with the scheduling window and the deletion flag. The rule, in order: deleted → Deleted; state ARCHIVED → Archived; state DRAFT → Draft; state PAUSED → Paused; a start date still in the future → Scheduled; an end date already passed → Expired; otherwise → Active. So an offer set to Active with a start date next week correctly shows as Scheduled until that date, then flips to Active on its own, and to Expired after its end date — no manual toggling required.

Offers Board

Ten live KPI cards over a searchable, filterable, priority-ordered table of every offer — the growth pulse of every promotion at a glance.

Purpose Show at a glance how many offers exist and in what state, how many redemptions and how much revenue and discount they have generated, which campaigns are live and which offer is winning — then let you drill into any one offer to manage it.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing (+ read-only report/customer viewers) — view. Managing requires the offers edit permission. Path Admin → Growth → Offers
Offers — Board · header “New offer” button · 10 KPI cards · toolbar · table
Page headerTitle “Offers” + intro line + New offer primary button (top-right)
KPI row (10)Total Offers · Active · Scheduled · Draft · Paused · Redemptions · Revenue · Discount Given · Active Campaigns · Top Offer
ToolbarSearch box · “All statuses” dropdown · “All types” dropdown
Offers tablePanel headed “Offers” with a live count badge; columns Offer · Type · Discount · Priority · Usage · Status · Manage
Live bannerGreen “● Live — N offer(s) · M active · K campaign(s)” from the database, or an amber/red offline / 403 notice
Figure — the Offers board: KPI cards, a three-control toolbar, and the offers table.

Every KPI card and where the number comes from

KPI cardPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Total OffersEvery live (non-deleted) offerCountRead-onlyCount of all offers where deletedAt is null (kpis.totalOffers).18
ActiveOffers live right nowCountRead-onlyOffers whose derived status is Active (state ACTIVE, started, not expired) — kpis.activeOffers.6
ScheduledSet to go live laterCountRead-onlyActive offers whose start date is still in the future (kpis.scheduledOffers).2
DraftNot yet launchedCountRead-onlyOffers in state DRAFT (kpis.draftOffers).4
PausedTemporarily switched offCountRead-onlyOffers in state PAUSED (kpis.pausedOffers).1
RedemptionsTotal times offers were usedCountRead-onlyCount of all OfferRedemption rows (kpis.offerRedemptions).312
RevenueOrder value on offer-bearing orders₹ amountRead-onlySum of orderTotalPaise across all redemptions (kpis.revenueGeneratedPaise), shown in ₹.₹4,86,200
Discount GivenMoney handed back to customers₹ amountRead-onlySum of discountPaise across all redemptions (kpis.totalDiscountPaise), in ₹.₹58,900
Active CampaignsDistinct live campaignsCountRead-onlyNumber of distinct campaign names among currently-Active offers (kpis.activeCampaigns).3
Top OfferThe best-earning offerOffer name (or “—”)Read-onlyThe offer whose redemptions summed to the highest revenue (kpis.topPerformingOffer).Monsoon 10%
💡
Tip — more analytics are computed than are shown

The dashboard endpoint also returns a conversion rate (discount ÷ revenue as a %), offers used today, and the average discount per redemption. Per-offer detail (a full redemption log with customer, order, discount and order total, plus unique-customer count) and campaign/offer report roll-ups are available from the same API (?view=detail and ?view=reports, with date presets Today, Last 7, Last 30, This Month, Financial Year, All). Generating a report writes an audit line via the log action.

The toolbar — search and two filters

ControlPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Search boxFree-text findAny textOpt.Debounced ~300 ms; matches offer name, code, campaign or description, case-insensitive (q=).monsoon
Status dropdownFilter by lifecycle/derived statusAll statuses · Active · Scheduled · Draft · Paused · Expired · ArchivedOpt.Filters the list to the chosen derived status (status=).Active
Type dropdownFilter by offer typeAll types + the 13 offer typesOpt.Filters by offerType exactly (offerType=).Festival

The offers table

ColumnShowsNotes
OfferOffer name in bold, with the campaign name beneath it (if set)The campaign appears as a small muted sub-line.
TypeFriendly type label (e.g. “Percentage”, “Buy X Get Y”, “Festival”)Mapped from the stored offerType enum.
DiscountThe discount value — “10%” or “₹50”Adds a muted “max ₹…” note when a maximum-discount cap is set.
PriorityThe numeric priority — higher wins at checkoutUsed to pick between competing codeless offers.
UsageTimes used, as usageCount / limitShows just the count when no total usage limit is set.
StatusColoured badge — Active (green), Scheduled (blue), Draft/Paused (amber), Expired/Archived (grey), Deleted (red)Derived, not stored (see the callout above).
ManageA Manage link that opens the Edit-offer formOnly actionable for roles with manage permission.
Table behaviourHow it works
SearchLive debounced search over name / code / campaign / description.
FiltersStatus (All/Active/Scheduled/Draft/Paused/Expired/Archived) and Type (all 13 types).
SortingDefault is newest first (by created date). The API also supports sort by priority, redemptions, expiry or recently-updated.
PaginationThe board requests up to 500 rows at once (pageSize=500); the API paginates and reports total / page / pages.
Row actionManage → opens the Edit-offer modal for that offer.
Count badgeThe panel header shows the total number of matching offers.
Empty state“No offers match these filters.” when nothing matches.
✅
Best Practice — always name a campaign

Filling the Campaign field on related offers (e.g. “Monsoon 2026”) costs nothing but unlocks the Active Campaigns KPI and the by-campaign report roll-up, so you can measure a whole promotion, not just one offer, and compare campaigns side by side.

New / Edit Offer

The single modal form that creates an offer or edits an existing one — type, discount, targeting, limits, schedule and state, plus Duplicate and Delete.

Purpose Capture everything about one promotion in one dialog: what kind of offer it is, how much it takes off, who and what it applies to, how often it can be used, when it runs, and whether it is live — then save it to the database.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing — the offers edit permission is required; other roles get a 403. Path Admin → Growth → Offers → New offer / Manage
Offers — New / Edit offer · modal dialog · 8 rows of paired fields · footer actions
HeaderTitle “New offer” or “Edit offer” + close (×)
Row 1Offer name* · Offer type
Row 2Campaign · Code (blank = codeless auto-apply)
Row 3Discount type (%/₹) · Value*
Row 4Max discount (₹) · Min order (₹)
Row 5Priority · Usage limit (total)
Row 6Applicable products (slugs) · Applicable plans (slugs)
Row 7Eligibility · Status
Row 8Start date · End date
FooterDuplicate · Delete/Restore (edit only) · Cancel · Create offer / Save changes
Figure — the offer editor: eight rows of paired fields with lifecycle actions in the footer.

Every field on the form

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Offer nameHuman title of the promotionTextYesAt least 2 characters (blocked client-side); server enforces 2–140 chars (name).Monsoon 10% Off
Offer typeClassifies the promotion for reporting/badgesOne of 13 types (see reference table)Opt.Enum offerType; defaults to Percentage. Descriptive — the money maths is driven by Discount type, not this field.Festival
CampaignGroups related offers for roll-up analyticsTextOpt.Up to 120 chars; stored as campaignName; blank is stored as none.Monsoon 2026
CodeOptional coupon code; blank = auto-applyLetters, digits, - and _Opt.Up to 40 chars, matched to ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]*$, force-uppercased, and must be unique (a duplicate returns “Offer code … already exists”, HTTP 409). Leave blank for a codeless offer.MONSOON10
Discount typeWhether the value is a percent or a flat rupee amountPercentage % · Flat ₹YesDefaults to Percentage. Switches the Value hint between “(enter %)” and “(enter ₹)”. Stored as discountType.Percentage %
ValueThe size of the discountNumber > 0YesMust be greater than zero (blocked client-side). For %, stored as discountBps = value × 100, capped at 100%. For ₹, stored as flatPaise = value × 100.10
Max discount (₹)Ceiling on a percentage discount₹ numberOpt.Stored as maxDiscountPaise; a % discount is never allowed to exceed it. Leave blank for no cap.₹100
Min order (₹)Smallest order the offer applies to₹ numberOpt.Stored as minOrderPaise; defaults to 0. Orders below it are declined with “does not meet the minimum”.₹499
PriorityWhich codeless offer wins when several qualifyWhole numberOpt.Integer, defaults to 0; higher wins. At checkout, eligible offers sort by priority, then by discount amount (priority).10
Usage limit (total)Cap on total redemptions across all customersPositive whole numberOpt.Stored as maxRedemptions; once reached the offer is declined with “reached its usage limit”. Blank = unlimited.500
Applicable products (slugs)Restrict the offer to specific productsComma-separated slugsOpt.Split on commas, trimmed, blanks dropped → applicableProductSlugs. If set, the cart must contain at least one; else “does not apply to the items in your cart”.milk,curd
Applicable plans (slugs)Restrict the offer to subscription plansComma-separated slugsOpt.Same parsing → applicablePlanSlugs. If set, the selected plan must match; else “does not apply to the selected plan”.p30,p90
EligibilityWhich customers can use the offerAll customers · First order onlyOpt.Stored as eligibility (ALL / FIRST_ORDER). First-order offers are declined for anyone who already has a paid order.First order only
StatusThe lifecycle state of the offerDraft · Active · Paused · ArchivedOpt.Stored as state; defaults to Draft on new offers. Setting Active makes it live (subject to schedule). Only ACTIVE offers auto-apply at checkout.Active
Start dateWhen the offer should beginDateOpt.Stored as startsAt; before it, the badge reads Scheduled and checkout declines with “not yet active”. Blank = starts immediately.2026-07-15
End dateWhen the offer should stopDateOpt.Stored as expiresAt; on/after it, the badge reads Expired and checkout declines with “has expired”. Blank = never expires.2026-08-31
ℹ️
The API stores a little more than the form shows

The offer record and its validation engine also support a description, a banner text/image for storefront display, category targeting (applicableCategorySlugs), a per-customer usage limit (perCustomerLimit), and a customer-specific eligibility list (eligibleUserIds when eligibility is SPECIFIC). These fields exist on the backend and are honoured at checkout but are not exposed on this admin form, which focuses on the common promotion controls above.

Buttons on the form and table

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
New offer (header)Opens the blank offer form (state defaults to Draft)None until you save.Super Admin+ Admin Marketing
Manage (table row)Opens the Edit-offer form pre-filled from that offerNone until you save.Super Admin+ Admin Marketing
Create offerValidates then saves a new offerPOST action create → parses the schema, checks code uniqueness, writes the Offer, appends an audit line, toasts “Offer created”, reloads the board.Super Admin+ Admin Marketing
Save changesSaves edits to an existing offerPOST action update → partial-schema update (incl. changing the Status/state), re-checks code uniqueness, audits, toasts “Offer updated”, reloads.Super Admin+ Admin Marketing
Duplicate (edit only)Clones the offer as a fresh DraftPOST action duplicate → copies all settings, names it “… (copy)”, appends “-COPY” to the code (de-duplicated), resets redemptions to 0 and state to DRAFT.Super Admin+ Admin Marketing
Delete (edit only)Soft-deletes the offer after a confirm promptPOST action delete → sets deletedAt (the offer is hidden, not erased). Confirms “Soft-delete offer …?” first.Super Admin+ Admin Marketing
Restore (edit only)Brings a deleted offer backPOST action restore → clears deletedAt and sets state back to Draft (shown in place of Delete once an offer is deleted).Super Admin+ Admin Marketing
Cancel / × / EscCloses the form without savingNone.Anyone with the form open
💡
Tip — activate, pause, resume and archive from one dropdown

This form manages the whole lifecycle through the single Status dropdown: pick Active to launch, Paused to switch off temporarily, Archived to retire, Draft to pull back into editing — then Save changes. (The backend also exposes dedicated activate / deactivate / pause / resume / archive actions and a bulk action over many offers at once for API and future multi-select use.)

Offer types reference

The 13 selectable types and what each is for.

Purpose Explain each value in the Offer type dropdown. The type classifies the promotion for filtering and reporting; the actual discount is always driven by the Discount type (% or ₹) and Value.
Stored valueLabel on screenTypical use
PERCENTPercentageA straight “X% off” promotion (the default).
FLATFlatA fixed “₹X off” promotion.
BOGOBuy X Get YBuy-one-get-one style deals.
BUNDLEBundleDiscount on a combined set of products.
SUBSCRIPTIONSubscriptionDeals aimed at subscription plans (pair with plan slugs).
FIRST_ORDERFirst OrderNew-customer welcome deal (pair with First-order eligibility).
SEASONALSeasonalSeason-long promotions (e.g. Monsoon, Summer).
FESTIVALFestivalFestival-specific campaigns (e.g. Diwali, Pongal).
LIMITED_TIMELimited-TimeShort, deadline-driven pushes (pair with a tight start/end).
CUSTOMER_SPECIFICCustomerTargeted at specific customers (backend eligibility list).
PRODUCT_SPECIFICProductTied to particular products (pair with product slugs).
CATEGORY_SPECIFICCategoryTied to a product category (backend category slugs).
CASHBACKCashbackCashback-style promotions.

Lifecycle & checkout workflows

How an offer moves from draft to retired, and how it is chosen at checkout.

Purpose Show the two processes this module drives: the admin lifecycle of an offer, and the automatic resolution of the best offer for a customer’s cart.
Workflow — offer lifecycle
Create (Draft)Set ActiveScheduled → Active (on start date)Pause ⇄ ResumeExpired (on end date)ArchiveDelete → Restore
Figure — a new offer starts as Draft; going Active makes it live (or Scheduled until its start date, then Expired after its end date); it can be Paused/Resumed, Archived, or soft-Deleted and later Restored.
Workflow — auto-apply at checkout (codeless offers)
Cart total + items sent to /api/offers/validateGather Active, in-window offersValidate each (min order · eligibility · targeting · limits)Sort by priority, then discountBest offer appliedRedemption recorded (idempotent per order)
Figure — at checkout the storefront asks the offers API for the best eligible offer; codeless offers apply automatically, a code validates one specific offer, and redemption is recorded once per order.
⚠️
Warning — only Active, in-window offers reach customers

An offer set to Draft, Paused or Archived, one whose start date has not arrived, one past its end date, or one that has hit its usage limit will never apply at checkout — even if its code is typed. If a customer says a promotion “isn’t working”, check the Status badge and the schedule first.

✅
Best Practice — build in Draft, schedule the launch, cap the risk

Create the offer as a Draft, review the numbers, then set it Active with a future Start date so it goes live on its own. Always set a Max discount on percentage offers and a Usage limit on aggressive ones so a single promotion can never give away more than you planned. Give competing codeless offers distinct priorities so the intended one wins.

⛔
Caution — a live code cannot be reused elsewhere

Offer codes are globally unique. If you need the same wording on a new offer, either duplicate the existing one (the copy gets a “-COPY” code you can rename) or archive the old offer first. Deleting is a soft delete — the offer and its redemption history are kept and can be Restored — but the code stays reserved until then.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common issues and answers.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Amber/red banner instead of the “● Live” one; no dataThe Next.js backend is offline, or your role can’t view offersStart the backend; if it reads “403”, ask an Admin to grant offers/reports/customers view access.
“Only Marketing / Admin can manage offers (403).”Your role has view but not edit on the offers moduleHave a Super Admin, Admin or Marketing user make the change, or request the permission.
“Offer code … already exists.”Another offer already uses that code (codes are unique)Choose a different code, leave it blank for a codeless offer, or archive/rename the other offer.
Saving is blocked with “Enter an offer name.”Name is empty or under 2 charactersType a name of at least 2 characters.
Saving is blocked with “Enter a discount value greater than zero.”The Value field is 0 or emptyEnter a positive number; for %, remember it is capped at 100%.
Offer shows Scheduled, customers can’t use itIts Start date is still in the futureExpected — it will flip to Active on that date, or clear the Start date to launch now.
Active offer never applies at checkoutBelow min order, wrong products/plan, first-order-only, or usage limit hitCheck Min order, product/plan slugs, Eligibility and Usage limit against the cart.

Frequently asked questions

Q: What’s the difference between an Offer and a Coupon?

They share the same discount engine and rules. Offers add a type, a priority, a campaign name and a lifecycle, and — crucially — can be codeless so they apply automatically at checkout. Use Coupons for a plain typed code; use Offers when you want auto-apply, campaign reporting, or several promotions competing by priority.

Q: If two offers both qualify for a cart, which one wins?

The one with the higher Priority. If priorities tie, the one giving the larger discount wins. Set distinct priorities to control the outcome.

Q: How do I run a limited festival promotion safely?

Create it as a Draft with type Festival and a campaign name, set the Discount type/Value, add a Max discount cap and a Usage limit, set the Start and End dates, then set Status to Active and save. It goes live on the start date and expires on its own.

Q: I deleted an offer by mistake — is the data gone?

No. Delete is a soft delete: the offer and its redemption history remain in the database. Open it (list deleted offers) and click Restore to bring it back as a Draft.

Q: Does editing an offer change orders that already used it?

No. Past redemptions are recorded with the discount that was given at the time; editing only affects future checkouts.

DOODLYAdmin · Growth · Referrals
§Admin · Growth

Referral Management

The control room for the refer-a-friend programme — every code, every friendship it created, the one-time ₹100 rewards, and the fraud alerts that keep it honest. Approve, reject or reverse a reward; only the Super Admin (and finance roles) can touch the money.

ℹ️
Note — how a referral becomes a reward

A customer shares their code → a friend signs up with it → the friend buys a qualifying subscription (30-day or longer) → the relationship becomes Eligible → the ₹100 is credited to the referrer's wallet as Reward Credited. The reward is intentionally delayed until the qualifying purchase, which is what stops fraud.

Workflow — referral to reward
Share codeFriend signs upQualifying planEligibleFraud checks₹100 → wallet
Figure — a reward is only credited after the friend's qualifying purchase clears the fraud checks.

Referrals — Overview

The programme's health at a glance, plus recent referral history.

Purpose See how the programme is performing and spot anything that needs action.
Access Super Admin (full) · finance roles view Path Admin → Growth → Referrals Data GET /api/admin/referrals?section=stats
Referral Management — tabs: Overview · Referrals · Top Referrers · Fraud Alerts · Settings
KPI stripTotal · Successful · Pending · Rejected · Rewards credited · Total paid · Fraud alerts
Referral historyFriend · Contact · Plan · Status · Reward · Date
Fraud badgered count on the Fraud Alerts tab when alerts exist
Figure — the five-tab Referral Management workspace; Overview shown.

The KPI strip

KPIWhat it counts
Total referralsEvery referral relationship ever created.
SuccessfulRelationships that reached Reward Credited.
PendingRegistered/eligible but not yet credited — awaiting a qualifying purchase or review.
RejectedRejected or cancelled relationships.
Rewards creditedCount of active (non-reversed) ₹100 rewards paid to wallets.
Total paidSum of active reward amounts — the programme's spend.
Fraud alertsOpen alerts flagged by the automatic checks.

Referrals — ledger

Every referral relationship, searchable and filterable, with per-row actions.

Purpose Find a specific referral and act on it — approve, reject or reverse the reward.
Access Super Admin manages · finance view Data GET /api/admin/referrals?section=records&q&status&planSlug&sort&page&from&to

The table

ElementDetail
ColumnsFriend (referred), Contact (mobile/email), Plan, Status, Reward, Date.
SearchBy referrer, friend, mobile or email (q).
FiltersStatus and plan (status, planSlug), and a date range (from/to).
Sort & pagessort and page — the ledger is paginated.
Row actionsApprove/process · Reject (with reason) · Reverse (with reason).

Status values

StatusMeaning
InvitedCode shared; the friend hasn't signed up yet.
RegisteredThe friend created an account with the code.
Trial PurchasedThe friend bought a trial — not yet a qualifying plan.
Eligible PurchaseThe friend bought a qualifying subscription — the reward is due.
Reward Credited₹100 credited to the referrer's wallet. ✔
Rejected / CancelledBlocked by an admin or a fraud check.
ExpiredThe window to qualify passed without a purchase.

Buttons & actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Approve / ProcessCredits the ₹100 reward for an eligible relationship.POST {action:"process"} → credits the wallet, marks Reward Credited, audits.Super Admin
RejectBlocks a relationship (with a reason).POST {action:"reject", refereeId, reason} → sets Rejected, audits.Super Admin
ReverseClaws back a reward already credited (with a reason).POST {action:"reverse", refereeId, reason} → debits the wallet credit, marks reversed, audits.Super Admin
Regenerate codeIssues a fresh referral code for a customer.POST {action:"regenerate"}.Super Admin
Bulk processApproves many eligible rewards at once.POST {action:"bulkProcess"}.Super Admin
ExportDownloads the current (filtered) ledger.Client-side export of the loaded records.view+
⛔
Caution — Reverse moves real money

Reverse pulls the ₹100 back out of the customer's wallet. Only do it for a confirmed fraudulent or mistaken reward, and always type a reason — it is written to the audit log. If the customer has already spent the balance, coordinate with Finance before reversing.

⚠️
Permission

Approve, Reject and Reverse require manage rights (Super Admin / finance). Other roles that can open this screen see it read-only — the action buttons return 403 Forbidden.

Top Referrers

Your best advocates, ranked.

Purpose See who refers the most so you can thank or reward them.
Top Referrers
Rank & namereferrer
Referralstotal invited / converted
Rewards earnedtotal ₹ credited
Figure — leaderboard of the highest-performing referrers.
💡
Tip

Your top referrers are your cheapest growth channel. A small personal thank-you or bonus to the leaderboard often produces more referrals than a broad discount.

Fraud Alerts

Automatic checks that block gamed rewards.

Purpose Review and clear the relationships the system flagged as suspicious before any money moves.

What the system checks automatically

CheckWhat it catches
Self-referralSomeone using their own code (same email/phone).
Duplicate accountThe same person creating a second account to farm rewards.
Circular referralTwo people referring each other in a loop.
Duplicate rewardAn attempt to credit the same relationship twice.
✅
Best Practice

Clear the Fraud Alerts tab (the red badge) before running a Bulk process. Bulk approval credits everything eligible — you want the flagged ones resolved first.

Settings

Turn the programme on/off and set the reward.

Purpose Configure the reward amount, the qualifying plan, and whether referrals are accepted right now.
Data POST /api/admin/referrals {action:"config", …}
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesBackend behaviour
Programme enabledAccept new referrals or pause the whole programme.On / OffWhen Off, new codes are rejected and the storefront hides referral entry.
Reward amountThe one-time wallet credit per successful referral.₹ (default 100)Applied to future rewards; past rewards keep their original amount.
Qualifying planThe minimum plan the friend must buy.e.g. 30-day+Relationships below this never reach Eligible.
⚠️
Warning

Turning the programme Off immediately stops new referrals across the storefront. Existing eligible rewards are unaffected, but customers can no longer enter a code — announce a pause before you flip it.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Referrer says they didn't get ₹100Friend hasn't bought a qualifying plan yet, or the relationship is Pending/flagged.Find them in the ledger; if Eligible and clean, use Approve. If Pending, the friend still needs to buy the qualifying plan.
Reward looks fraudulentSelf/duplicate/circular referral.Check the Fraud Alerts tab; Reject before crediting, or Reverse if already paid.
Action button gives "Forbidden"Your role has view-only rights.Approvals/reversals need Super Admin (or finance) — ask the Super Admin.
Referral link opens a 404Wrong share URL format.Links must be /signup.html?ref=CODE.

FAQ

Can one customer earn the reward many times?

Yes — once per successful friend. Each referral is a separate relationship; the reward is one-time per friend, not per referrer.

What happens to the reward if I reject after crediting?

Use Reverse (not Reject) once a reward is credited — Reverse is the action that pulls the ₹100 back from the wallet and marks the reward reversed. Both are audited.

Does the customer see any of this?

Customers only see their own side on Account → Referrals — their code, share link, friends referred and rewards earned. This admin screen is internal.

DOODLYAdmin · Growth · Pure Rewards
§Admin · Growth

Pure Rewards (Loyalty)

Configure every earning rate, redemption ratio, tier threshold, expiry window and bonus campaign of the DOODLY Pure Rewards programme, watch programme-wide KPIs, and manually grant or deduct a member's points — with every change written to the audit trail.

ℹ️
One page, three working areas

The whole module lives at one screen (/admin/loyalty.html, block type loyaltyAdmin, mount #loyaltyAdminMount). It loads two calls in parallel — GET /api/admin/loyalty/config and GET /api/admin/loyalty/members — then paints a KPI strip, a Programme-settings form, and a Members table. Each is documented below as its own screen.

✅
The settings row is the single source of truth

Every rule lives in one database row (LoyaltyConfig, id singleton). Nothing about earning, redeeming, tiers or expiry is hard-coded — what you save here is exactly what customers experience on their Rewards page and what every automatic earn hook (order paid, subscription, referral, bottle return, birthday, puzzle, review…) reads at run time.

Rewards dashboard — KPI strip

Live, programme-wide health of Pure Rewards at a glance.

Purpose Show, at the top of the page, six read-only totals computed across the whole points ledger so you can see the size and cost of the programme before you change any rule.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing — anyone with loyalty:view Path Admin → Growth → Pure Rewards
Header — DOODLY Pure Rewards · "Configure earning rates, tiers, redemption, expiry and campaigns — and manage members' points. Every change is audited."
MembersCustomers with any lifetime points earned
Points outstandingSum of every member's live balance
Lifetime earnedAll EARN points ever awarded
RedeemedAll points converted to wallet
Redeemed value₹ credited to wallets from redemptions
ExpiredPoints that lapsed unredeemed
Figure — the six KPI cards, drawn from loyaltyReports() in lib/loyalty/service.ts
KPI cardWhat it countsHow it is calculated (backend)Format
MembersEnrolled customersUser.count where role = CUSTOMER AND loyaltyLifetimeEarned > 0Whole number
Points outstandingUnredeemed live balance across everyoneSum of User.loyaltyPointsPoints
Lifetime earnedEvery point ever grantedSum of points on all EARN ledger rowsPoints
RedeemedPoints turned into wallet creditSum of points on all REDEEM rowsPoints
Redeemed valueThe rupee cost of those redemptionsredeemed ÷ redeemPointsPerRupee × ₹1 (paise → ₹)₹
ExpiredPoints that lapsed after their expiry dateSum of points on all EXPIRE rowsPoints
ℹ️
These cards are read-only

There are no buttons on the KPI strip. It refreshes whenever the page (re)loads — for example after you save settings or apply a points adjustment, both of which reload the module.

Programme settings

The master on/off switch and every tunable rule of the programme.

Purpose Turn the whole programme on or off and set every earning rate, the redemption ratio, the minimum redemption, the expiry window, expiry reminders, an optional bonus campaign, and the tier ladder. Saving writes back through PATCH /api/admin/loyalty/config.
Access Read: Super Admin Admin Marketing (loyalty:view). Save: same roles with loyalty:edit — a view-only role sees the form but the switch and Save button are disabled. Path Admin → Growth → Pure Rewards → Programme settings
Panel — Programme settings · Programme ON / OFF toggle (top-right)
Earning14 point-value fields (spend, registration, profile, subscribe, referral, bottle, renewal, streak, birthday, anniversary, puzzle play/win, review)
Redemption & expiryPoints per ₹1, minimum redemption, expiry days, reminder days
Bonus campaignEarning multiplier + campaign end date
TiersOne editable card per tier: name, min points, benefits (one per line)
Save settingsPrimary button + inline "Saved ✓" / error message
Figure — the settings form: master switch, four fieldsets and the Save action
⚠️
The master switch pauses the entire programme

The Programme ON/OFF toggle writes enabled. When OFF, every automatic earn hook returns early ("disabled"), redemption is refused, and the customer Rewards page shows "Rewards are taking a short break — your points are safe." Existing balances are never touched; the programme simply stops accruing and paying out until you switch it back on.

Earning rates

Each field is a whole number of points (integer, minimum 0). Blank or non-numeric input is stored as 0, and each value is clamped to a safe maximum on the server. Values below are the shipped defaults.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Points per ₹100 spentPoints earned on the paid value of every orderInteger 0–100,000YespointsPerHundred; order earn = floor(paidₚ/10000 × this) on total minus refundable deposit minus coupon discount10
RegistrationOne-time welcome bonus at account creationInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnRegistration50
Complete profileOne-time, when name + email + phone are filledInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnProfile25
Subscribe 30-dayBonus for taking a 30-day (or longer, under 90) planInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnSubscribe30; campaign multiplier applies300
Subscribe 90-dayBonus for a 90-day (or longer) planInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnSubscribe90; campaign multiplier applies1,000
Successful referralCredited to the referrer once the friend's qualifying subscription is paidInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnReferral500
Per bottle returnedPoints for each glass bottle returned in good conditionInteger 0–100,000YesearnBottleReturn × bottles15
Subscription renewalPoints on each renewal cycleInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnRenewal; campaign multiplier applies150
12-delivery streakBonus after 12 consecutive deliveries without a pauseInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnStreak12200
BirthdayAnnual birthday rewardInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnBirthday250
AnniversaryReward on each DOODLY anniversaryInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnAnniversary1,000
Puzzle participationPoints for playing the monthly puzzleInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnPuzzlePlay50
Puzzle winWinner's bonus for the monthly puzzleInteger 0–10,000,000YesearnPuzzleWin2,500
Verified reviewPoints for a verified review, once per delivered orderInteger 0–1,000,000YesearnReview30
💡
Set a rate to 0 to switch that earn off

Any earn hook that resolves to 0 points is skipped ("zero") — no ledger row is written. So you don't need code to disable, say, birthday points; just set the field to 0.

Redemption & expiry

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Points per ₹1 redeemedThe conversion ratio from points to wallet creditInteger 1–100,000YesredeemPointsPerRupee; forced to at least 1 to avoid divide-by-zero at redeem time10
Minimum redemptionFewest points a customer may redeem at onceInteger 0–10,000,000YesminRedeemPoints100
Expiry (days)How long a batch of points stays valid from the day it is earnedInteger 1–3,650YesexpiryDays; each EARN "lot" is stamped with its own expiresAt365
Expiry reminders days beforeHow many days before expiry to remind the customerComma-separated integers, each 1–365, up to 6 valuesOpt.remindDays; parsed from text, de-duplicated and sorted; drives the reminder cron (in-app + email + WhatsApp)30, 7
⛔
Changing the ratio does not restate points already earned

The redemption ratio is read at the moment of redemption. If you raise "Points per ₹1" from 10 to 20, existing balances instantly become worth half as much in wallet credit. Announce ratio changes; they affect every member's live balance value even though the point counts don't change.

Bonus campaign

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Earning multiplier 1 = offMultiplies spend, subscription and renewal earns during a campaignDecimal 1–10 (step 0.01)Opt.campaignMultiplier; only applies to earns marked applyCampaign (order, subscribe, renewal), and only while a future end date is set1.05 (+5%)
Campaign endsThe date the multiplier stops applyingDate (or blank to clear)Opt.campaignEndsAt; stored as ISO. The campaign is live only when multiplier > 1 and this date is in the future2026-08-31
ℹ️
A campaign needs both fields

A multiplier above 1 does nothing on its own — the end date must be a future date for the boost to count. When live, a "Bonus campaign live — earn X% extra points" banner appears on the customer Rewards page. When the date passes, earning quietly returns to the base rates.

Tiers

The tier a customer sits in is derived from their lifetime points earned — redeeming or expiring points never demotes them. Each tier card has three inputs. You must keep at least one tier (1–10 allowed); the server sorts them by minimum points ascending and ignores any card whose name is blank.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Tier nameThe label shown to members (e.g. on the hero card and ladder)Text, up to 60 charactersYesBlank-named cards are dropped on saveGold Member
Min pointsLifetime-earned points needed to reach the tierInteger 0–100,000,000YesTiers are re-sorted by this ascending3,000
BenefitsPerk lines listed under the tierOne benefit per line, up to 12 lines × 200 charsOpt.Split on newlines, trimmed, empties removed5% Extra Reward Points during campaigns
Free Surprise Dairy Gift
ℹ️
Default ladder

The shipped ladder is Fresh Member (0), Silver Member (1,000), Gold Member (3,000) and Platinum Member (6,000), each with its own benefit list. These load automatically the first time the config row is created and can be renamed, re-thresholded or extended here.

Actions

Button / controlWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Programme ON/OFF toggleEnables or pauses the whole programme (label updates to ON/OFF)Sets enabled; saved with the rest of the form on Saveloyalty:edit
Save settingsCollects every field (switch, all numbers, reminder days, campaign, tiers) and stores themPATCH /api/admin/loyalty/config — Zod-validated, clamped, then audited as loyalty.config.update recording exactly which fields changed (from → to). Button shows "Saving…" then "Saved ✓"; the module reloadsloyalty:edit
✅
Change-tracked, prev→new auditing

Save doesn't just log "settings changed" — it compares the old and new values of every tracked field (all earn rates, redemption, expiry, reminders, tiers, campaign, on/off) and records only the fields that actually changed. You can always see who moved the referral bonus from 500 to 750 and when, in Admin → System → Audit Logs.

Members

Every enrolled customer, tier-tagged, with live and lifetime balances.

Purpose Look up any member's tier and point balances and open the adjust action. The list is loaded once with the page and re-queried live as you type in the search box.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing (loyalty:view); the Adjust column appears only with loyalty:edit Path Admin → Growth → Pure Rewards → Members
Panel — Members · search box "Search name, phone, email…" (top-right)
MemberName, with phone or email underneath
TierColoured tier badge (🥉🥈🥇💎)
AvailableLive redeemable balance
Lifetime earnedTotal ever earned (drives tier)
RedeemedTotal ever redeemed
AdjustOpens the points-adjustment modal (edit only)
Figure — the Members table, from listMembers() in lib/loyalty/service.ts
Table aspectBehaviour (from the real code)
ColumnsMember (name + phone/email), Tier (badge), Available, Lifetime earned, Redeemed, and an Adjust action column (shown only with edit rights).
Who appearsCustomers only (role = CUSTOMER) who have loyaltyLifetimeEarned > 0 or a current balance loyaltyPoints > 0. Customers who have never earned a point are not listed.
SearchA single search box matches name, phone or email (case-insensitive). Typing is debounced ~300 ms and re-queries the server via GET /api/admin/loyalty/members?q=…; only the table rows repaint.
SortingFixed server-side order: highest lifetime earned first. There are no clickable column sorts.
PaginationNone. The list is capped at 300 members; narrow with search rather than paging.
ExportsNone from this table.
Row actionAdjust — opens the points-adjustment modal for that member (see next screen). Present only with loyalty:edit.
Empty state"No members with points yet." when nobody qualifies (or a search returns nothing).
ℹ️
Available vs. Lifetime earned

Available is what the member can redeem now (loyaltyPoints). Lifetime earned only ever goes up and decides the tier; redeeming or expiry lowers the Available balance but never the lifetime figure, so a customer keeps their tier even after spending their points.

Adjust points (modal)

Manually grant or deduct a member's points, with a mandatory reason.

Purpose Hand a goodwill bonus, restore points that expired by mistake, or correct an error — a positive number grants points, a negative number deducts them. The reason is recorded in the audit.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing — requires loyalty:edit Path Admin → Growth → Pure Rewards → Members → Adjust
Modal — Adjust points — {member name}
PointsNumber field · "positive grants, negative deducts"
ReasonText field · "recorded in the audit"
MessageInline validation / error line
Cancel / ApplyGhost + primary buttons
Figure — the adjustment dialog opened from a member's Adjust button
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
PointsHow many points to add or removeNon-zero integer; positive grants, negative deductsYesClient rejects zero/blank; server enforces non-zero. Negative larger than the balance is refused ("Customer doesn't have enough points to deduct.")100  or  -50
ReasonWhy the adjustment was made — kept for the auditText, 1–200 charactersYesRequired both client- and server-side; stored on the ledger row's description and in the audit targetGoodwill / restore expired points
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
ApplySubmits the grant/deduction; shows "Applying…", then closes and reloads the modulePOST /api/admin/loyalty/adjust with {userId, points, reason}. Positive → new EARN lot (kind admin_adjust, with its own expiry). Negative → deducts oldest-first (FIFO) from the balance, no wallet credit. Audited as loyalty.adjustloyalty:edit
CancelCloses the modal without changing anythingNone — also closes on a click of the dark overlayloyalty:view
Workflow — manual points adjustment
Find member (search)Click AdjustEnter points ± & reasonApplyServer writes ledger + auditModule reloads, KPIs update
Figure — end-to-end flow of a goodwill grant or correction
⛔
A deduction is not a redemption — it does not credit the wallet

Deducting points simply removes them from the balance (oldest lots first) and records an ADJUST ledger row. No money moves. Use a deduction only to correct wrongly-granted points; never as a way to "pay" a customer. To convert points to money, the customer redeems on their Rewards page.

✅
Always write a specific reason

The reason is the audit trail. Write "Restored 200 pts lost to expiry bug, ticket #4821" rather than "fix". Every adjustment is logged with your role, the member, the signed amount and this reason — a vague reason makes a later review impossible.

How points move (reference)

The lifecycle your settings drive — for context, not a screen.

Purpose Understand what your configuration actually does behind the scenes, so you can predict the effect of a change before you save it.
Access Super Admin reference Path n/a — background
Workflow — points lifecycle
Customer acts (order paid, subscribe, refer, return bottle, birthday, puzzle, review…)Earn hook reads config & awards a lot (idempotent, with expiry)Lifetime + available balances rise; tier may upgrade (member notified)Customer redeems ≥ minimum → wallet credit (FIFO consume)Reminder cron warns 30/7 days before expiryExpiry cron lapses past-due points
Figure — earn → redeem → remind → expire, all governed by the settings on this page
ℹ️
Earning is idempotent and event-anchored

Each earn carries a deterministic reference (for example earn:order:<id>, earn:reg:<uid>, earn:birthday:<uid>:<year>). That reference is unique, so a webhook retry or a double event awards the points at most once. This is why order points are safe to fire from both the payment callback and the webhook.

ℹ️
Redemption is the only place money is created

Redeeming credits the customer's DOODLY Wallet through a normal wallet transaction (kind loyalty) inside one atomic, serializable transaction. Points balances and wallet balances therefore never drift out of sync, and wallet history shows the redemption like any other credit.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"You don't have permission to view the rewards programme."Signed-in role lacks loyalty:viewSign in as Super Admin, Admin or Marketing, or grant the role loyalty access in Admin → System → Roles & Permissions.
Switch and Save button are greyed out; "You have view-only access to these settings."Role has loyalty:view but not loyalty:editExpected — a view-only role can read everything but not change it. Use an edit-capable role to save.
"Backend offline — the loyalty admin needs the DOODLY API."The Next.js API isn't reachable from the storefrontConfirm the backend is running and the API base URL is correct; reload the page.
Customers aren't earning any pointsProgramme is OFF, or that earn rate is set to 0Turn the master switch ON and check the specific earning field is above 0, then Save.
A campaign multiplier has no effectMultiplier is left at 1, or the end date is missing / in the pastSet the multiplier above 1 and a future "Campaign ends" date; the boost only applies to order, subscribe and renewal earns.
