If a role without CMS permission opens this screen, the editor UI still appears, but the backend load fails with a red “Your role can't edit Brand Story (403)” banner and any Save is refused by the server with the toast “Only Marketing / Admin can edit (403).” In that state the person is only changing their own browser copy — the live page and other users are unaffected. Real edits require Super Admin, Admin or Marketing.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner “Your role can't edit Brand Story (403)”.Your account does not have CMS (content) edit permission.Ask a Super Admin to grant your role CMS access (Marketing has it by default), or have a Marketing/Admin/Super Admin user make the change.
Toast “Only Marketing / Admin can edit (403).” when you click Save.Same as above — you can see the form but the server refuses to persist your change.Have an authorised user save it. Your local edits are not on the live page.
Red banner “Backend offline — editing locally only.”The DOODLY backend is unreachable.Confirm the backend is running; reload the page once it is back. Do not rely on edits made while offline — they are browser-only.
Toast “Saved locally — backend error.”The save reached the browser but the database call failed for a non-permission reason.Check your connection and try again; if it persists, report the error code to a developer.
The “Products heading” I saved keeps reverting to the default.By design — that field is not stored in the database (it is not on the backend whitelist).Use it only as a temporary preview; to change it permanently, a developer must edit the source.
I saved, but /doodly.html still shows the old text.The public page is cached and served from a copy.Wait a few minutes and refresh. Use Preview the live page to re-check.
The QR preview looks empty or doesn't update.The QR helper hasn't loaded, or the destination field is blank.Reload the page; if a field is blank the code falls back to the default /doodly.html destination.
I want the original wording back.You overrode a field and want the built-in copy.Clear that field and Save, or use Reset to defaults to clear everything at once — you cannot break the page this way.

Frequently asked questions

Q. Where exactly do my changes appear?

On the public page /doodly.html (“UNFOLD PURE.”). The four hero fields change the very top of that page; the QR destination changes the code in its “Scan & Connect” section and the file you download. Use Preview the live page → to see it.

Q. Do I need to publish or deploy after saving?

No. Saving writes straight to the live page and the DOODLY database — there is no separate publish step and no code change. Allow a few minutes for caching.

Q. What happens if I leave a field blank?

The page uses its built-in default for that field (shown to you as the grey placeholder). Blank never means “empty on the page”.

Q. Can I edit the whole story — the panels about the farm, trust, and products?

Not from this screen. Only the hero copy, the products heading (locally) and the QR destination are editable here. The long narrative panels are part of the product and are changed in code by a developer.

Q. What is the difference between the SVG and PNG downloads?

Both encode the same QR destination. SVG is a vector — it stays razor-sharp at any size, ideal for printing on packaging and large signage. PNG is a high-resolution image — handy for slides, documents and social posts.

Q. If I change the QR destination, do old printed codes still work?

Only if you point the destination at a URL you keep serving. Codes already printed are fixed images pointing at whatever the destination was when they were made. Keeping the destination at your stable /doodly.html lets you change the page's content later without reprinting.

DOODLYAdmin · Content · Help Centre
§Admin · Content

Help Centre

Manage the customer knowledge base — FAQs, categories, video guides, the platform-wide contextual tooltips, and help analytics — from one workspace, with every change publishing instantly to the storefront and no code change.

ℹ️
What this module controls

The Help Centre workspace is a full content-management system (CMS) for everything a customer sees on the public /help.html page: the searchable FAQ knowledge base, the category tabs, the "Video guides" grid, and the "Still need help?" contact block. It also edits the small ⓘ contextual-help tooltips that appear next to fields, buttons and metrics across the whole platform. Finally, it reports how customers use help — most-viewed FAQs, most-searched terms, unanswered searches and product-tour completion.

✅
How it saves

Edits are mirrored to your browser first (so the screen stays instant) and then persisted to the DOODLY Postgres database through /api/help/admin. A green "● Live" banner confirms the save reached the database and shows the current FAQ, category and video counts. The storefront Help Centre reads the same data live via /api/help/public, so a published change is visible to customers immediately.

Help Centre workspace

The six-tab console that manages the entire knowledge base.

Purpose Give one place to author FAQs, organise categories, attach video guides, edit contextual tooltips, control display settings and read help analytics.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager (Marketing) — editing needs the CMS · edit permission Path Admin → Content → Help Center
Help Center — Content CMS · tabs · "● Live" backend banner
Tab barFAQs · Categories · Videos · Tooltips · Settings · Analytics
Live bannerGreen "● Live — N FAQ(s) across M categories · V video guide(s)" or amber offline / 403 warning
Tab bodyThe editor for whichever tab is active (list rows, forms, toggles or analytics tables)
Row actionsPublish/Unpublish, Move up/down, Edit, Delete, inline URL fields
Figure — the Help Centre console: a six-tab workspace above a live database-status banner.
TabWhat it managesBackend it writes to
FAQsQuestions & answers, grouped by category; publish state and order./api/help/admin → HelpArticle
CategoriesThe topic groups (Getting Started, Delivery, Wallet, B2B…) and their order.HelpCategory
VideosVideo-guide titles and their playable URLs.HelpVideo
TooltipsThe platform-wide ⓘ contextual-help copy and per-module on/off switches.Browser store (doodly-help-tips, doodly-help-tip-modules)
SettingsBackground illustrations toggle, read-only contact details, reset-to-defaults.Browser store (doodly-help)
AnalyticsViews, searches, unanswered searches and product-tour completion.HelpArticle views · HelpSearch · tour Counters
⚠️
Two things persist to the browser only

The Tooltips and Settings tabs store their overrides in the admin's own browser (local storage), not Postgres. They still take effect immediately for everyone who loads the page from that build, but they are not shared across devices the way FAQs, categories and videos are. Edit them from the machine you administer with.

FAQs tab

Author, publish, reorder and delete the questions in each category.

Purpose Maintain the searchable question-and-answer knowledge base customers browse on the Help Centre and that the AI assistant and site search draw from.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager (CMS · edit) Path Admin → Content → Help Center → FAQs
FAQs — category chips · rows · "+ Add question"
Category chipsOne chip per category with its live question count; the active chip is highlighted
FAQ rowQuestion (bold) + Answer preview, a Published/Unpublished badge, and the row action buttons
Row actionsPublish/Unpublish · Move up · Move down · Edit · Delete
Add questionReveals an inline Question + Answer form with Save / Cancel
Figure — the FAQs editor: pick a category chip, then manage that category's questions row by row.

Add / Edit question form

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
QuestionThe question exactly as a customer would ask it.Free text (single line)YesClient blocks empty ("Enter a question and answer"). Backend requires ≥ 3 characters.How do I pause my subscription?
AnswerThe plain-English answer shown when the row is expanded.Free text (3-line box)YesClient blocks empty. Backend requires ≥ 3 characters. Stored and shown as plain text (HTML is escaped).Open My Subscription and tap Pause. Your remaining days are preserved.
ℹ️
Category is the chip you're on

A new question is added to whichever category chip is currently selected, so choose the right chip before pressing + Add question. Newly created questions are Published by default. The backend also generates a URL-friendly id from the question text and gives it the next sort position in the category.

Row action buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Publish / UnpublishToggles whether the question appears on the public Help Centre. Unpublished rows stay in the admin list with a grey badge.publish action sets HelpArticle.published. Only published FAQs are returned by /api/help/public.Manager+
Move up ▲Raises the question one place within its category.reorder writes new sortOrder values in a single transaction.Manager+
Move down ▼Lowers the question one place within its category.reorder (as above).Manager+
Edit ✎Opens the inline Question / Answer form pre-filled with the current text.update action patches the article.Manager+
Delete 🗑Removes the question after a confirm prompt ("Delete this question?").delete action removes the HelpArticle row.Manager+
SaveValidates and stores the Question + Answer, then closes the form.create (new) or update (edit).Manager+
CancelCloses the form without saving.None — client only.—
⛔
Delete is permanent

Deleting an FAQ hard-deletes the row from the database — there is no trash or undo. If you only want to hide a question temporarily, use Unpublish instead; it keeps the content and lets you restore it with one click.

Workflow — publishing an FAQ
Pick category chip+ Add questionType Question + AnswerSave (Published by default)Reorder if neededLive on /help.html
Figure — from draft to live customer-facing answer in one screen.

Categories tab

Create, rename, reorder and remove the topic groups that organise FAQs.

Purpose Control the top-level topics (Getting Started, Products, Delivery, Subscription, Wallet, Payments, Bottle Returns, B2B Orders, Account…) into which every FAQ is filed and by which customers browse.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager — Delete is Super Admin only Path Admin → Content → Help Center → Categories
Categories — category rows · "New category name" + Add
Category rowIcon + title, and "N question(s) · id: <slug>" underneath
Row actionsMove up · Move down · Rename · Delete (Super Admin)
Add row"New category name" text box + "＋ Add category" button
Figure — categories listed in display order, with an add box at the bottom.

Add category form

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
New category nameThe display title of the topic group.Free textYesEmpty names are ignored. A URL-friendly id is auto-generated from the name (lower-case, dashes). A default "info" icon is applied and the category starts empty.Festival Orders → id festival-orders

Row action buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Move up ▲ / down ▼Changes the order categories appear in, both here and on the storefront.catReorder updates each HelpCategory.sortOrder.Manager+
Rename ✎Prompts for a new title ("Rename category") and updates it.catRename patches the title.Manager+
Delete 🗑Removes the category and its questions after a confirm prompt. Shown only to Super Admin.catDelete soft-deletes the category (sets deletedAt).Super Admin
＋ Add categoryCreates the new category from the name box.catCreate with title + default icon.Manager+
⚠️
Deleting a category takes its questions with it

The confirm prompt reads "Delete this category and its questions?" for a reason — the FAQs filed under it disappear from the public knowledge base. Move any questions you want to keep into another category (via Edit on the FAQs tab) before deleting. On the storefront, an empty category is simply not shown.

💡
Canonical categories get the right icon automatically

The storefront maps well-known category slugs (getting-started, products, delivery, subscription, wallet, payments, bottle-returns, b2b, account) to a matching dairy icon even though the database stores a generic "info" icon. Naming a new category to match one of these slugs makes it inherit the correct icon.

Videos tab

Attach playable URLs to the "Video guides" grid on the Help Centre.

Purpose Manage the short how-to video guides ("How to place an order", "How subscriptions work"…) shown on the storefront Help Centre. A guide with no URL displays as a "Coming soon" placeholder.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager (CMS · edit) Path Admin → Content → Help Center → Videos
Videos — guide rows · "New video title" + Add
Guide rowPlay icon + title, and either the URL or "No video yet — placeholder shown to customers"
Inline URL box"Paste video URL…" — saves on change
DeleteRemoves the guide
Add row"New video title" box + "＋ Add video" button
Figure — each row is a video guide; paste a URL to turn a placeholder into a playable card.
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
New video titleThe label of a brand-new guide card.Free textYesEmpty titles are ignored. Created with a blank URL, so it starts as "Coming soon".How Auto Pay works
Video URLThe link the card plays / opens when clicked.URL (e.g. YouTube link)Opt.Saves automatically when you leave the box ("Video updated"). Blank keeps the card as a "Coming soon" placeholder.https://youtu.be/…

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
＋ Add videoCreates a new guide from the title box (URL blank).vidCreate → HelpVideo, next sort position.Manager+
(URL field)Attaches or changes the playable link.vidUpdate patches the URL.Manager+
Delete 🗑Removes the guide card.vidDelete soft-deletes the row.Manager+
ℹ️
The whole grid is labelled "Coming soon"

On the storefront the "Video guides" section carries a "Coming soon" tag until real URLs are added. Cards with a URL become clickable and open the video in a new tab; cards without one stay as inert placeholders.

Tooltips tab

Edit the platform-wide ⓘ contextual-help copy and switch it on/off per module.

Purpose Author the small hover / focus / long-press help bubbles that explain fields, status badges, buttons, table columns and metrics across every DOODLY screen — and control which modules show them.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager (CMS · edit) Path Admin → Content → Help Center → Tooltips
Tooltips — per-module switches · filter · reset · grouped editor
Enable per moduleA checkbox for every module — turn contextual help on or off for that screen
Filter box"Filter tooltips…" narrows the list by key or text
Reset all tooltip textRestores every tooltip to its built-in default copy
Grouped editorRows grouped into Features & terms · Status badges · Action buttons · Table columns · Metrics & charts
Figure — module switches and a searchable, grouped copy editor for the whole tooltip catalogue.

Per-tooltip editor row

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Key (read-only)The catalogue id the tooltip is matched by, shown as a code chip; an "edited" badge appears once you override it.——System value, not editable. Examples: wallet, status:pending, act:approve, col:amount, metric:revenue.autopay
TitleThe bold heading of the help bubble.Free textOpt.Saved to the override store on change ("Tooltip updated") and audited. Blank leaves the default.Auto Pay
DescriptionThe explanatory sentence in the bubble.Free text (2-line box)Opt.Saved on change; audited.Securely renews your plan so deliveries never pause.
ExampleAn optional "e.g. …" line under the description.Free textOpt.Saved on change; shown only if filled.₹100

Controls

ControlWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Module checkboxEnables or disables all contextual help on that module's screens.Writes doodly-help-tip-modules; every change is written to the audit trail (help.tips).Manager+
Filter tooltips…Live-filters the editor rows by key or copy.Client only.—
Reset all tooltip textClears every text override (module on/off settings are kept) after a confirm prompt.Empties doodly-help-tips; falls back to built-in defaults.Manager+
ℹ️
Where these tooltips appear

DOODLY resolves the right help bubble for almost any element automatically — a status badge, a table heading, a button label, a KPI card or a known term — without needing markup on the page. Editing the copy here changes the bubble everywhere that element appears. The system is described as multi-language ready, and overrides are stored separately from defaults so a reset never loses the originals.

✅
Best Practice

Keep tooltip copy short, factual and jargon-free — it is read at a glance while someone is mid-task. Use the Example line for concrete formats (a code like DOODLY12345, an amount like ₹100) rather than stuffing them into the description. Disable tooltips on a module only when they clutter a densely labelled screen, not as a way to hide missing copy.

Settings tab

Display options, the read-only contact block, and reset-to-defaults.

Purpose Toggle the decorative background on the Help Centre, review the contact details customers are shown, and reset the whole knowledge base to its shipped defaults.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager (CMS · edit) Path Admin → Content → Help Center → Settings
Settings — illustrations toggle · contact panel · reset
Illustrations toggle"Show dairy-themed background illustrations on the Help Center"
Contact support panelPhone · WhatsApp · Email · Hours — read-only, pulled from brand settings
Reset knowledge baseRestores FAQs, categories and videos to defaults
Figure — display options above a read-only contact panel and the reset control.
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Show background illustrationsTurns the animated dairy scene (sun, hills, buffalo, delivery van, milk drops) on the public Help Centre on or off.On / Off checkboxOpt.Saves to the browser store ("Saved"). On by default.On
Phone / WhatsApp / Email / HoursThe contact channels shown in the Help Centre's "Still need help?" block.Read-only—Displayed from brand / CMS settings; edited there, not here. The Help Centre reads them live.Mon–Sat, 8 AM – 8 PM

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Reset knowledge base to defaultsRestores all FAQs, categories and videos to the shipped defaults after a confirm prompt ("Reset all FAQs, categories and videos to defaults?").Overwrites the local knowledge-base store with the built-in defaults.Manager+
⛔
Reset discards your edits

"Reset knowledge base to defaults" replaces your current FAQs, categories and videos with the factory content. Anything you authored is lost. Use it only to recover a broken knowledge base or start fresh — never as a quick way to clear one bad entry (use Delete or Unpublish for that).

💡
To change the phone or email

The contact panel is intentionally read-only here so support details stay consistent everywhere. Update them once in Brand / CMS settings and every surface — the Help Centre, the floating help launcher, order emails — picks up the new values automatically.

Analytics tab

See what customers look for, what they can't find, and how the product tour performs.

Purpose Turn help usage into a content backlog — the most-viewed FAQs tell you what matters, the unanswered searches tell you what to write next, and tour completion shows how well onboarding lands.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager Path Admin → Content → Help Center → Analytics
Analytics — KPI cards · four insight tables
KPI cardsTour starts · Tour completions · Completion rate % · Searches
Most viewed FAQsQuestion | Opens
Most searched keywordsTerm | Searches
Unanswered searchesTerm | Times (searches that returned no result)
Most clicked help cardsCard | Clicks (the quick-help shortcuts)
Figure — four KPI cards over four ranked insight tables.

KPI cards

MetricMeaningSource
Tour startsHow many times the 60-second product tour was started.Tour counters
Tour completionsHow many times the tour was finished.Tour counters
Completion rateCompletions ÷ starts, as a percentage.Derived
SearchesThe number of distinct search terms customers have used.HelpSearch

Insight tables

TableColumnsWhat it tells youRanking
Most viewed FAQsQuestion · OpensWhich answers customers open most — keep these accurate and near the top of their category.Top ~8–10 by opens
Most searched keywordsTerm · SearchesWhat customers look for — a signal for new FAQs or better wording.Top by search count
Unanswered searchesTerm · TimesSearches that returned no result — your direct backlog of missing content.Top by count
Most clicked help cardsCard · ClicksWhich quick-help shortcuts customers tap most on the Help Centre.Top by clicks
ℹ️
Where the numbers come from

Opening an FAQ, running a search (and whether it found anything) and starting or finishing the product tour are all logged automatically as customers use the site. The backend keeps per-article view counts, groups search terms, isolates zero-result searches, and tallies tour events, so the tables reflect real behaviour rather than guesses.

✅
Best Practice — run a monthly content loop

Once a month, open Unanswered searches and write an FAQ for each recurring term, filed in the closest category and published. Then check Most searched keywords and make sure the top terms lead to a clearly-worded answer. This closes the gap between what customers ask and what the knowledge base answers.

Publishing & permissions

Who can edit, how a change reaches customers, and the live-status banner.

Purpose Explain the CMS permission gate and the path a saved change travels from this console to the public Help Centre.
Access gated by CMS · edit Path Admin → Content → Help Center
RoleCMS permissionCan do here
Super AdminFullEverything, including deleting categories.
AdminFullCreate / edit / publish / reorder / delete FAQs, categories, videos; edit tooltips and settings.
Manager (Marketing)ManageEdit FAQs, categories, videos, tooltips and settings. Category delete is Super Admin only.
Accountant · Delivery Executive · CustomerNoneNo access — the backend returns 403 and the console shows an amber "Your role can't manage Help" banner.
Workflow — a change from console to customer
Edit in a tabMirrors to browser (instant UI)Saves to Postgres via /api/help/admin"● Live" banner confirmsStorefront reads /api/help/publicCustomer sees it
Figure — the two-step save (browser mirror, then database) and how the storefront picks it up.
⚠️
Watch the banner colour

A green "● Live" banner means your FAQ / category / video edits reached the database. An amber banner means the backend is offline (edits are local only and will not reach customers) or your role was refused (403). If you see amber, do not assume a change published — resolve the connection or permission first.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common issues and quick answers.

Purpose Resolve the most frequent Help Centre admin problems fast.
Access Super Admin AdminPath Admin → Content → Help Center
SymptomLikely causeFix
Banner is amber "Backend offline — Help editing locally only".The admin API is unreachable from the browser.Confirm the backend is up and reachable, then reload. Edits made while offline are local only and must be re-saved once live.
Banner reads "Your role can't manage Help (403)".The signed-in role lacks the CMS · edit permission.Use an Admin, Super Admin or Marketing account, or have Roles & Permissions grant CMS edit.
An FAQ I added isn't on the storefront.It is saved but Unpublished, or filed in an empty/hidden category.Press Publish so its badge turns green. Empty categories are hidden on the storefront; make sure at least one published FAQ sits under it.
Save does nothing / "Enter a question and answer".Question or Answer is blank, or under 3 characters.Fill both fields with at least a few characters before pressing Save.
No Delete button on a category.Category delete is restricted to Super Admin.Ask a Super Admin to delete it, or empty and leave it hidden instead.
A video card still says "Coming soon".The guide has no URL.Paste a valid video URL into the row's "Paste video URL…" box; it saves on blur.
Tooltip edits didn't follow me to another computer.Tooltip and Settings overrides are stored in the browser, not the database.Make those edits from the machine you administer with, or repeat them where needed.

Do FAQ edits reach customers immediately?

Yes. Once the green "● Live" banner confirms the save, the storefront Help Centre reads the same published data, so browsing customers see the change on their next load — no deploy or code change.

What is the difference between Unpublish and Delete?

Unpublish hides a question from customers but keeps it in the admin list so you can bring it back with one click. Delete permanently removes it from the database with no undo. Prefer Unpublish for anything you might reuse.

Where do the tooltips I edit here actually show up?

Everywhere on the platform. The contextual-help engine automatically matches badges, buttons, table headings, KPI cards and known terms to a tooltip key, so editing the copy on the Tooltips tab changes that bubble on every screen it appears — customer, admin and portal pages alike.

Why can't I change the support phone number on the Settings tab?

Contact details are shown read-only so they stay identical across the whole product. Edit them once in Brand / CMS settings and the Help Centre, the floating help launcher and emails all update together.

What powers the "Unanswered searches" list?

Every customer search is logged with how many results it returned. Searches that returned zero results are grouped and ranked here, giving you a ready-made backlog of FAQs to write.

DOODLYAdmin · Content · Notifications (campaigns)
§Admin · Content

Notifications (campaigns)

Compose and blast WhatsApp, SMS, push and email campaigns to a live customer audience — every campaign is recorded, personalised and delivered straight from the DOODLY database.

ℹ️
What this screen is (and isn't)

This is a one-time marketing broadcast tool: pick an audience, pick a channel, type a message, and send. It is not the transactional notification system (order confirmed, out-for-delivery, delivered) — those fire automatically from order events and are not composed here. Both share the same delivery engine and the same customer consent rules, so a campaign only reaches people who have opted in to marketing on that channel.

Campaign overview

The KPI strip and live-database banner at the top of the Notifications page.

Purpose Give the sender an at-a-glance read on how many campaigns exist, how many have gone out, how many are still drafts, and how many people have actually been reached.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing — anyone with notifications:view Path Admin → Content → Notifications
Header — Notifications · button: New campaign
KPI · Total Campaignsevery non-deleted campaign in history
KPI · Sentcampaigns with status "Sent"
KPI · Draftscampaigns still in "Draft"
KPI · Recipients Reachedsum of delivered counts across all sent campaigns
KPI · Sent Todaycampaigns whose send time is today
Live bannergreen "● Live" status line with the database URL
Figure — the five live KPI cards and the database status banner that load first.

The board calls GET /api/admin/notifications?view=dashboard on open and paints five KPI cards from the real database, then a status banner beneath the table.

KPI cardWhat it countsBackend source
Total CampaignsAll campaigns that have not been deleted.notificationsDashboard() — count of NotificationCampaign where deletedAt is null.
SentCampaigns whose status is Sent.count where status = SENT.
DraftsCampaigns saved but not yet launched.count where status = DRAFT.
Recipients ReachedTotal notifications actually delivered across every sent campaign.sum of deliveredCount over sent campaigns.
Sent TodayCampaigns launched since midnight today.count where sentAt ≥ start-of-today.
ℹ️
The live banner text

When the dashboard loads you will see a green line such as "● Live — 12 campaign(s) · 3,480 notification(s) delivered from the DOODLY database (…). External channels also need provider credentials." If the backend is unreachable it turns red: "⚠ Backend offline … — start next-app." A red "⚠ Your role can't view Notifications (403)" means your role lacks notifications:view.

Compose campaign

The form that builds and immediately sends a broadcast.

Purpose Choose an audience and channel, write the title and message, see the live recipient count, and send — one click creates the campaign record and launches delivery.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing — sending needs notifications:edit Path Admin → Content → Notifications → Compose campaign
Panel — Compose campaign
Campaign nameoptional label · "Weekend offer blast"
Audiencedropdown + live "N recipient(s)" counter under it
ChannelWhatsApp / SMS / Push / Email
Titleoptional heading shown in-app
Messagerequired · multi-line · supports {{variables}}
Send campaignprimary button · error line above it
Figure — the compose panel with the live audience counter and the Send button.

Fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Campaign name (#nc-name) An internal label so you can recognise the campaign later in history. Free text. Opt. Trimmed, max 120 chars (CampaignSchema.name). If left blank the server names it automatically as "<Channel> · <Audience>" (e.g. WhatsApp · All customers). Weekend offer blast
Audience (#nc-audience) Who receives the campaign. A live counter beneath the dropdown shows exactly how many people match right now. All customers · Active subscribers · Paused · Trial users Yes Server resolves the audience to real user IDs and returns the count via ?view=audience. Only one of the four fixed segments (z.enum(AUDIENCES)). Active subscribers
Channel (#nc-channel) Which delivery rail the message goes out on. WhatsApp · SMS · Push · Email Yes Stored as WHATSAPP / SMS / PUSH / EMAIL (z.enum(CHANNELS)). Push is in-app only; the other three need provider credentials to leave the app. Email
Title (#nc-title) The heading shown in-app and used as the email subject / message heading. Free text. Opt. Trimmed, max 160 chars. If blank, a default "<Channel> update" heading is used at send time. Fresh milk, on us
Message (#nc-msg) The body of the campaign. Supports personalisation variables (see below). Multi-line text. Yes Client blocks an empty message ("Enter a message."). Server requires 1–4000 chars (message: z.string().min(1).max(4000)). Hi {{first_name}}, enjoy 10% off this weekend!
💡
Personalisation variables

The message and title are personalised per recipient. These placeholders are supported: {{first_name}}, {{name}}, and {{referral_code}}. Any variable that has no value (or an unrecognised one) is replaced with a blank string, so the send never breaks. {{first_name}} falls back to "there" when the customer has no name on file.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
New campaign (page header) Scrolls the compose panel into view and puts the cursor in the Campaign name field. A shortcut, not a save. Front-end only — DOODLY_ADMIN.newCampaign() focuses #nc-name. Super Admin+
Send campaign (#nc-send) Creates the campaign record and immediately launches it to the chosen audience. Button shows "Sending…" while in flight; on success a toast reads "Campaign sent to N recipient(s)". POST /api/admin/notifications with action:"createAndSend" → createAndSend() = create the NotificationCampaign, then sendCampaign() resolves the audience, enqueues per-recipient rows and dispatches them. Super Admin Admin Marketing
⚠️
Send is immediate — there is no "save as draft" in this UI

The compose form always does create-and-send in one step. Once you click Send campaign the message goes out to everyone in the audience who has opted in. Check the live recipient count and re-read the message before sending — there is no undo, and deleting the campaign afterwards only removes it from history, it does not recall messages already delivered.

ℹ️
Consent gating — who actually gets the message

The audience count is who matches the segment; the delivered count is who also consented. At send time each recipient is filtered by their CustomerPreference: they must have the global marketing opt-in on and the per-channel opt-in for the chosen channel. Defaults when a customer has never set preferences: Email on, WhatsApp on, Push on, SMS off. So an SMS campaign will typically reach fewer people than the segment size unless customers have explicitly enabled SMS.

Workflow — Send campaign
Pick audience + channel Write title + message Click Send campaign Campaign record created (DRAFT → SENDING) Audience resolved to user IDs Filter by marketing consent Per-recipient notification rows queued Dispatch (Push instant · Email/SMS/WhatsApp via provider) Status → Sent / Sending / Failed · delivered count stamped
Figure — end-to-end path of a single Send campaign click.
ℹ️
How delivery finishes for large audiences

Push is in-app only, so those rows are marked delivered the instant they are created. Email, SMS and WhatsApp rows are queued and dispatched inline within a short time budget (~9 seconds) so a big audience never times out the request. Anything left over stays queued and is finished by the daily drain job (or a manual retry). That is why a fresh campaign can briefly show status Sending before it settles to Sent.

Recent campaigns

The history table of every campaign, with delivery counts and a delete action.

Purpose Review what has been sent, to which audience, on which channel, whether it succeeded, and how many people it reached; remove old entries from the log.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing — deleting needs notifications:edit Path Admin → Content → Notifications → Recent campaigns
Panel — Recent campaigns · total-count badge
Campaigncampaign name (bold)
Audiencesegment it targeted
ChannelWhatsApp / SMS / Push / Email
Statuscolour badge: Sent / Sending / Failed / Draft
Delivereddelivered / recipient count
Sentsend date & time, or —
Row actionDelete link
Figure — the seven columns of the campaign history table.

The table loads via GET /api/admin/notifications?view=list&pageSize=200 (newest first). The panel header shows a badge with the total number of campaigns. When there are none you see "No campaigns yet — compose one above."

Table specification

AspectBehaviour
ColumnsCampaign (name) · Audience · Channel · Status · Delivered (delivered / recipients) · Sent (timestamp) · Delete.
Status badgeSent = green · Sending = blue · Failed = red · Draft = amber (ncStatusTone).
Delivered columnShows deliveredCount / recipientCount — how many were actually delivered out of how many the segment matched.
Sent columnSend date/time formatted as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM; a dash (—) for campaigns never sent.
SortingFixed — newest campaign first (orderBy createdAt desc). No column sorting in this UI.
Search / filtersNot exposed on this screen. (The backend list endpoint supports q, status and channel filters, but the board loads an unfiltered page.)
PaginationLoads up to 200 campaigns in one page (pageSize=200); server caps a page at 500.
ExportsNo dedicated export button; the backend records an audit event if an export/log action is fired.
Row actionsDelete — soft-removes the campaign from history after a confirm prompt.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Delete (row link) Asks "Delete this campaign from the history?" then removes the row and re-loads the board. Toast: "Campaign deleted". POST /api/admin/notifications with action:"delete" → softDeleteCampaign() stamps deletedAt (a soft delete — the record is hidden, not erased, and can be restored server-side). Super Admin Admin Marketing
⛔
Delete removes the record, not the messages

Deleting a campaign only takes it out of the history list. Messages already delivered to customers (in-app, email, SMS or WhatsApp) are not recalled — a broadcast cannot be un-sent. Treat delete as "tidy up the log", never as "cancel the campaign".

Audience segments (reference)

Exactly how each of the four audiences is resolved on the server.

Purpose Understand precisely who is in each segment so the recipient count is never a surprise.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing Path Admin → Content → Notifications → Compose → Audience
AudienceWho it includesBackend rule (resolveAudience)
All customersEvery customer account that is active.Users with role CUSTOMER and no deletedAt.
Active subscribersCustomers with a live subscription.Distinct users with a Subscription in status ACTIVE (non-deleted user).
PausedCustomers whose subscription is paused or on vacation hold.Distinct users with a subscription in status PAUSED or VACATION.
Trial usersCustomers linked to a Trial-Pack cashback benefit.Users with a related trialCashback record (non-deleted).
ℹ️
Count vs delivered, again

The counter next to the dropdown is the raw segment size. The Delivered figure on the sent campaign will be lower whenever some of those people have not opted in to marketing on that channel (especially SMS, which is off by default).

Channels & provider credentials

Which rails can leave the app, and what each one needs configured.

Purpose Explain why a campaign may deliver in-app but not reach the external channel until credentials are set.
Access Super Admin — credentials live in server environment variables (Vercel), not in this screen
ChannelLeaves the app?Provider(s) required
PushNo — in-app inbox only.None. Push rows are delivered instantly with no external provider.
EmailYes.Resend (RESEND_API_KEY, optional EMAIL_FROM).
SMSYes.MSG91 (DLT template) or Twilio (TWILIO_* + TWILIO_SMS_FROM).
WhatsAppYes.Superfone (preferred), MSG91, or Twilio (TWILIO_WHATSAPP_FROM).
⚠️
Fallback-safe by design

If a channel has no provider key, its sends are marked skipped (never an error) and the campaign quietly degrades to in-app delivery. That is why the live banner reminds you: "External channels also need provider credentials." If an SMS or WhatsApp campaign shows a low delivered count, first confirm the provider is configured — not just that customers opted in.

Permissions & audit trail

Who can see, send and delete campaigns — and what gets logged.

Purpose Make the access rules and the audit footprint explicit.
Access Super Admin Admin Marketing
CapabilityPermission checkedRoles that have it
View KPIs, list & recipient countsnotifications:viewSuper Admin, Admin, Marketing.
Compose & send a campaignnotifications:editSuper Admin, Admin, Marketing.
Delete a campaign from historynotifications:editSuper Admin, Admin, Marketing.
ℹ️
Everything sensitive is audited

Every send and delete writes an AuditLog entry (notification.createAndSend, notification.delete, etc.) recording the acting role and the target campaign. A blocked action returns 403 and the UI shows "Only Marketing / Admin can send campaigns (403)."

✅
Best Practice

Send yourself (or a test account that opted into all channels) a copy first by narrowing to a small segment, and always fill the optional Campaign name — a clear name like "Diwali 15% off · Active subscribers" makes the history table and audit log readable months later. Keep messages short, personalise with {{first_name}}, and never blast SMS as a first channel since it is off by default and DLT-template-restricted.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common issues and quick answers.

Purpose Resolve the most frequent problems without leaving the manual.
SymptomLikely causeFix
Delivered count far below the audience count.Many recipients have not opted in to marketing on that channel (SMS is off by default), or the channel's provider is not configured.Check the channel's provider credentials; try Email or WhatsApp (both default on); accept that consent will always trim the reach.
Campaign stuck on "Sending".A large audience — external sends are dispatched in batches within a time budget; the remainder is queued.Wait for the daily drain to finish the queue, then re-open the board; the status settles to Sent.
Status shows "Failed".Every attempted external send failed (e.g. provider rejected the message or all recipients were undeliverable).Verify provider keys and the sender/template setup; the campaign can be retried server-side once the provider is fixed.
"Enter a message." error under the form.The Message field is empty.Type a body — the message is the only required field.
Red banner "Your role can't view Notifications (403)".Your role lacks notifications:view.Ask a Super Admin to grant the Marketing/Admin role or the notifications permission.
Banner reads "Backend offline".The next-app backend is not running / unreachable.Start or restore the backend service; reload the page.

Can I schedule a campaign for later?

No. This screen only does create-and-send immediately. There is no scheduler or "save as draft" in the compose form — send it when you are ready.

Can I edit a campaign after sending?

No. A sent campaign is a delivery record; you can delete it from history but you cannot change or resend the same record. Compose a new campaign instead.

Do transactional messages (order confirmed, out for delivery) come from here?

No. Those fire automatically from order and delivery events through the same delivery engine and consent rules, but they are not composed on this screen and do not appear in the campaign history.

Why did a customer not receive my Push campaign externally?

Push is in-app only — it appears in the customer's DOODLY notification inbox, it is never sent to a phone's OS push service. Use Email, SMS or WhatsApp for messages that must reach people outside the app.

Module chapter

Admin · System

6 screen groups documented from the live application.

DOODLYAdmin · System · Support Tickets
§Admin · System

Support Tickets

A full customer-service ticket desk — every complaint or query becomes a numbered ticket you can prioritise, assign to a teammate, reply to, keep internal notes on, track against an SLA due-date and drive through its whole lifecycle from Open to Closed, all recorded live in the DOODLY database.

ℹ️
One page, three working surfaces

The whole desk lives at one screen (/admin/support.html, route key admin/support, block type supportBoard, mount #supportMount). It is wired by assets/js/layout.js → wireSupportBackend() and talks to one endpoint, /api/admin/support (service: next-app/lib/support/service.ts). On load it paints a KPI strip, a filter toolbar + tickets table, and — when you click Manage on any row or the header's New ticket button — a slide-over ticket drawer / new-ticket form. Each of those is documented below as its own screen.

ℹ️
Where tickets come from

Tickets are created two ways, both landing in the same desk: (1) staff raise them here with New ticket; (2) a signed-in customer raises one from their account (POST /api/account/support), which sets the ticket's customerId, name, email and phone from their authenticated profile — never trusted from the browser. The customer only ever sees a safe subset of their own ticket (subject, category, status, priority, dates) — never the assignee, SLA, internal notes, resolution text or tags.

Ticket desk — KPI strip

Eight live counters that read the pulse of the queue before you touch a single ticket.

Purpose Give the person on duty an instant read of the workload and health of support — how much is open, how much is overdue, how fast the team is responding, and how much is getting resolved — computed across every non-deleted ticket in the database.
Access Super Admin Admin Manager — anyone whose role has support:view (or customers:view, which also grants read access to the desk) Path Admin → System → Support Tickets
Header — Support Tickets · "Customer issues and resolutions — full ticket desk with SLA, assignment, internal notes, customer replies and lifecycle." · New ticket
OpenTickets in status Open
In ProgressTickets being actively worked
WaitingWaiting on the customer to reply
High PriorityUnresolved High + Urgent tickets
OverdueUnresolved tickets past their SLA due-time
Resolved TodayTickets resolved since midnight
Avg ResponseMean hours to first staff reply
Resolution Rate% of all tickets resolved or closed
Figure — the eight KPI cards, drawn from GET /api/admin/support?view=dashboard
KPI cardWhat it countsHow it is calculated (backend)Format
OpenTickets sitting in the Open status, not yet picked upCount of tickets where status = OPEN (deleted excluded)Whole number
In ProgressTickets a teammate is actively workingCount where status = IN_PROGRESSWhole number
WaitingTickets parked because the ball is in the customer's courtCount where status = WAITING_CUSTOMERWhole number
High PriorityUrgent work still open — the fire listOf the "open-ish" tickets (not Resolved / Closed), how many are HIGH or URGENTWhole number
OverdueOpen tickets that have blown their SLA deadlineOf the open-ish tickets, how many have a slaDueAt earlier than nowWhole number
Resolved TodayThe team's output so far todayCount where resolvedAt ≥ today 00:00Whole number
Avg ResponseHow quickly the team gives customers a first human replyMean of (firstResponseAt − createdAt) over the last 500 responded tickets, in hours, one decimale.g. "3.4h"
Resolution RateShare of all tickets that reached a done state(RESOLVED + CLOSED) ÷ total × 100, roundede.g. "82%"
ℹ️
The green live banner

Under the strip a status line reads, for example, "● Live — 128 ticket(s) · 14 unresolved · 3 overdue from the DOODLY database". Unresolved is every ticket that is not Resolved or Closed; overdue matches the Overdue KPI. If the backend is unreachable you get a red banner ("⚠ Backend offline…"); if your role lacks access you get "⚠ Your role can't view Support (403)."

💡
The cards are read-only and self-refresh

There are no buttons on the KPI strip. It re-computes whenever the page loads and again after any ticket action (assign, reply, status change, close, delete) via refreshSupportKpis(), so the numbers you see always reflect the queue you're looking at.

Tickets table — search & filter

The full queue in one list, searchable and filterable, one row per ticket.

Purpose Find and open the ticket you need. Narrow the queue by status or priority, or type to search across the ticket number, subject, customer and assignee, then click Manage to work it.
Access Read: Super Admin Admin Manager (support:view / customers:view). Acting on a ticket needs support:edit. Path Admin → System → Support Tickets
Toolbar — Search box · Status filter · Priority filter  |  Panel: Tickets · count badge
Search"Search ticket #, subject, customer…" — debounced 300 ms
Status filterAll statuses / Open / Assigned / In Progress / Waiting for Customer / Resolved / Closed
Priority filterAll priorities / Low / Medium / High / Urgent
Tickets tableTicket · Subject · Category · Priority · Status · Assignee · SLA · Manage
Count badgeTotal rows matching the current filters
Figure — the toolbar (search + two dropdowns) above the tickets panel

Table columns

ColumnWhat it showsBackend source
TicketThe ticket number in bold, e.g. TK-2042.number (auto-generated, sequential from TK-2042)
SubjectThe one-line subject, with the customer's name in small grey text underneath when known.subject, customerName
CategoryThe issue type (Delivery, Billing, Quality, Bottle, Subscription, Other) or "—" if none.category
PriorityA coloured badge — Urgent/High red, Medium amber, Low grey.priorityLabel
StatusA coloured badge — Open/Waiting amber, Assigned/In Progress blue, Resolved green, Closed grey.statusLabel
AssigneeWho owns the ticket, or "—" if unassigned.assigneeName
SLAThe due date (YYYY-MM-DD). If the ticket is open and past due it shows Overdue in bold red. "—" when no due-date.slaDueAt, overdue
ManageA link that opens the ticket drawer (Screen 4).Opens GET …?view=detail&id=…

List behaviour

AspectBehaviour in this screen
SearchServer-side, case-insensitive, matches any of: ticket number, subject, customer name, customer email, category, assignee name. Fires 300 ms after you stop typing.
FiltersStatus and Priority dropdowns. Selecting one reloads the table instantly. They combine with each other and with the search.
SortingFixed to newest-first (createdAt descending) on this board. The API additionally supports sort=priority, updated and sla, but no sort control is exposed on the desk.
PaginationThe board loads up to 500 tickets in one page (pageSize=500) — no pager. The API itself supports paging (page / pageSize, capped at 500).
Deleted rowsSoft-deleted tickets are hidden by default. They return only when a request passes includeDeleted=1 (not exposed on this board).
Row actionsA single Manage link per row → opens the ticket drawer. All work happens there.
Empty state"No tickets match these filters." when nothing matches.
ℹ️
Bulk & merge exist in the API, not on this board

The endpoint supports bulk status change / delete / restore (action:"bulk", up to 1000 IDs) and duplicate-merge (action:"merge", closes one ticket into another). The desk UI does not surface checkboxes or a merge button yet — these are backend-ready capabilities, not buttons you'll see today.

New ticket — raise a ticket by hand

A modal for logging a customer issue that came in by phone, WhatsApp or email.

Purpose Create a ticket for a customer who reached you off-platform. Capture the subject, category, priority and the customer's contact details, optionally tie it to an order or subscription, and (optionally) record their first message.
Access Super Admin Admin — creating needs support:edit. A view-only role gets "Only Support / Admin can create tickets (403)." Path Admin → System → Support Tickets → New ticket
Modal — New ticket · × close
Subject *Free-text summary of the issue
Category · PriorityTwo dropdowns, side by side
Customer name · phoneContact row 1
Customer email · Order/Sub refContact row 2
DescriptionWhat the customer reported (multi-line)
Cancel · Create ticketFooter actions
Figure — the New-ticket modal layout

Fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
SubjectShort summary shown as the ticket titleFree textYesAt least 3 characters (checked in the browser and again on the server, max 200)Milk not delivered this morning
CategoryGroups the ticket by issue type— · Delivery · Billing · Quality · Bottle · Subscription · OtherOpt.Stored as free text (max 40); blank saved as nullDelivery
PrioritySets urgency and drives the SLA due-dateLow · Medium · High · UrgentOpt.Defaults to Medium; controls the SLA clock (see below)High
Customer nameWho raised the issueFree textOpt.Trimmed, max 120; shown under the subject in the listAnanya Reddy
Customer phoneCallback numberPhone textOpt.Trimmed, max 30+91 98xxxxxx10
Customer emailEmail contactEmail textOpt.Trimmed, max 160ananya@example.com
Order / Subscription refLinks the ticket to an order or a subscriptionORD-… or SUB-…Opt.If the value starts with ORD it is saved as orderId; if it starts with SUB it is saved as subscriptionId; anything else is ignoredORD-10233
DescriptionWhat the customer reportedMulti-line textOpt.Max 20,000 chars. If filled it is also stored as the ticket's first message (a customer "reply"), so it shows in the timelineNo delivery today and no call…

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Create ticketValidates the subject, then saves the ticket and closes the modal; the desk reloads with the new row on top.POST /api/admin/support {action:"create", data} → assigns the next TK- number, status Open, computes the SLA due-date from priority, writes a "Ticket … created" system entry (and a first customer message if a description was given). Audited as support.create.Super Admin Admin (support:edit)
Cancel / × / EscCloses the modal without saving.None—
ℹ️
How the SLA due-date is set

When the ticket is created, DOODLY stamps a first-resolution target (slaDueAt) based on priority: Urgent = 4 hours, High = 8 hours, Medium = 24 hours, Low = 48 hours from now. That date drives the SLA column and the Overdue KPI. Changing priority later does not re-stamp the original due-date.

💡
Ticket numbers

Numbers are sequential and human-friendly — TK-2042, TK-2043, … generated from the current ticket count with a uniqueness check, so they never collide.

Ticket drawer — work a single ticket

The full conversation and every control for one ticket, in one slide-over.

Purpose Read the whole history, change status / priority / assignee, reply to the customer or leave an internal note, and close, reopen or delete the ticket — all in one place. Opens from the Manage link on any row.
Access Viewing needs support:view; every action inside needs support:edit — a view-only role sees the drawer but gets "Only Support / Admin can manage tickets (403)." when acting. Path Admin → System → Support Tickets → Manage
Drawer — TK-2042 · Subject · × close
Badge rowStatus · Priority · "SLA overdue" (if late) · Category
Contact / refs👤 name · phone · email   🔗 Order / Subscription
Status · Priority · AssigneeThree dropdowns that act on change
TimelineEvery message: replies, internal notes, system events
Add a messageText box feeding Reply or Note
Action barInternal note · Customer reply · Close/Reopen · Delete
Figure — the ticket drawer: header badges, the three control dropdowns, the message timeline and the action bar

Controls & fields

ControlPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
StatusMove the ticket along its lifecycleOpen · Assigned · In Progress · Waiting for Customer · Resolved · ClosedOn changeaction:"status". Choosing Resolved stamps resolvedAt; Closed stamps closedAt; Open clears both. A system line records the change.In Progress
PriorityRe-rank the urgencyLow · Medium · High · UrgentOn changeaction:"priority". Logged as a system entry. (Does not re-stamp the SLA due-date.)Urgent
AssigneeHand the ticket to a teammateUnassigned + every staff user (non-customer, non-delivery roles)On changeaction:"assign". If the ticket was Open and you pick someone, it auto-moves to Assigned. Logged as a system entry.Priya (Support)
Add a messageThe text you'll send as a reply or keep as a noteMulti-line textYes (to reply/note)1–20,000 chars. Empty → toast "Write a message first."We've re-scheduled your delivery for tomorrow 6 AM.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Add customer replyPosts the text box as a message the customer can see (green in the timeline).action:"reply". The first reply stamps firstResponseAt (this feeds the Avg Response KPI). A reply on a Waiting for Customer ticket moves it back to In Progress.Super Admin Admin
Add internal notePosts the text box as a staff-only note (amber, badged "internal note"). Never shown to the customer.action:"note". Does not change status or the response clock.Super Admin Admin
Close ticketPrompts for an optional resolution note, then closes the ticket. (Shown when the ticket is not already Resolved/Closed.)action:"close" → status Closed, stamps closedAt + resolvedAt, saves the resolution note, writes a system line.Super Admin Admin
ReopenBrings a Resolved/Closed ticket back to life. (Shown only when the ticket is Resolved or Closed.)action:"reopen" → status Open, clears closedAt and resolvedAt, writes a system line.Super Admin Admin
DeleteAsks "Soft-delete ticket TK-…?" then removes it from the desk and closes the drawer.action:"delete" → sets deletedAt (soft delete — the row is hidden, not erased, and can be restored via the API).Super Admin Admin
× / Esc / backdropCloses the drawer.None—

The timeline

Entry typeLooks likeMeaning
Customer replyGreen left-border card with the author's name and time.A message visible to the customer (staff reply, or the customer's own words).
Internal noteAmber card badged "internal note".Staff-only working note; never leaves the desk.
System eventPlain grey one-liner.Auto-logged lifecycle events: created, assigned, priority changed, status changed, closed, reopened, merged.
⚠️
The three dropdowns act the moment you change them

Status, Priority and Assignee save on change — there is no separate "Save" button for them. Each one fires its own API call and re-renders the drawer with a toast. Be deliberate: picking a value is the action.

✅
Reply vs. note — get this right

Add customer reply is customer-facing and starts/affects the SLA response clock; Add internal note is staff-only and invisible to the customer. Never put an internal comment ("customer is being difficult", pricing you can offer, teammate handover details) in a reply. When in doubt, use a note.

Workflow — a ticket's lifecycle
OpenAssignedIn ProgressWaiting for CustomerResolvedClosed
Figure — the six statuses. Assigning an Open ticket auto-advances it to Assigned; a customer reply on a Waiting ticket pulls it back to In Progress; a Closed/Resolved ticket can be Reopened to Open.
Workflow — handling an incoming issue
Ticket arrives (customer or New ticket)Set priorityAssign to a teammateReply to the customerResolve / Close with a note
Figure — the everyday path from a new issue to a closed, documented resolution
✅
Best practice — triage by SLA and priority every shift

Start each shift on the Overdue and High Priority KPIs, then filter the table to Urgent and High. Assign every unowned ticket so nothing sits in Open, give the customer a first reply quickly (it stamps firstResponseAt and improves your Avg Response), and always close with a short resolution note so the next person can see how it was solved. Use Waiting for Customer honestly — it keeps genuinely-blocked tickets out of your "needs action" view.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Red banner "⚠ Backend offline… — start next-app."The DOODLY backend (next-app) isn't running / reachable.Start the backend, then reload the page. The desk needs the live API to load tickets.
"⚠ Your role can't view Support (403)."Your role lacks support:view and customers:view.Ask a Super Admin to grant the Support module on Admin → System → Permissions.
Actions toast "Only Support / Admin can manage tickets (403)."You can view but don't have support:edit.Request the edit level for the Support module; view-only roles can read but not change tickets.
Create fails: "Enter a subject (at least 3 characters)."Subject is blank or too short.Type a subject of 3+ characters and try again.
Order/Subscription ref didn't attachThe value didn't start with ORD or SUB.Enter the reference beginning with ORD- or SUB-; other text is ignored.
Ticket vanished from the listIt was soft-deleted, resolved/closed and filtered out, or doesn't match the current filters.Clear the status/priority filters and search. Deleted tickets are hidden on this board (restore is available via the API).
SLA still shows Overdue after changing priorityThe due-date is stamped once at creation and isn't re-computed on priority change.This is expected. Treat the due-date as the original commitment; resolve or close to clear it from the Overdue count.

Frequently asked questions

Does the customer see my internal notes?

No. Internal notes (amber, badged "internal note") are staff-only. Only customer replies are meant for the customer, and the customer's own account view is further limited to the subject, category, status, priority and dates — never notes, assignee, SLA or resolution text.

Where does "Resolved Today" come from, and does closing count?

It counts tickets whose resolvedAt is on or after midnight today. Because Close ticket stamps both closedAt and resolvedAt, closing a ticket today also counts it as resolved today.

What decides whether a ticket is "Overdue"?

An open ticket (anything not Resolved or Closed) whose slaDueAt is earlier than the current time. The due-date is set at creation from the priority: Urgent 4h, High 8h, Medium 24h, Low 48h.

Can I delete a ticket permanently?

Not from this desk. Delete is a soft delete — it sets deletedAt and hides the ticket, but the record and its history are preserved and can be restored through the API. Every delete is also written to the audit trail.

Who can be an assignee?

Any staff user — the assignee dropdown lists all users whose role is not customer or delivery executive, plus an Unassigned option. Assigning an Open ticket automatically moves it to Assigned.

ℹ️
Everything here is audited

Every mutation — create, assign, priority, status, reply, note, close, reopen, merge, delete, restore, bulk — writes an audit record (support.<action>) with the actor and target. You can review the full trail on Admin → System → Audit Logs.

DOODLYAdmin · System · Chat Support
§Admin · System

Chat Support

Manage every conversation the Doodly Assistant has with customers — read live, resolved and escalated chats, watch satisfaction and volume, take over a chat as a human agent, keep a library of canned replies, and tune the assistant's answers without touching code.

ℹ️
What this module is, in plain words

Every DOODLY storefront and customer page carries a floating chat button — the Doodly Assistant. It answers questions about subscriptions, deliveries, products, referrals, wallet, bulk orders, invoices and payments from a built-in knowledge base, and can hand a customer over to a real person on WhatsApp, phone or callback. Chat Support is the admin side of that assistant: it is where you, as Super Admin, see all those conversations gathered from the DOODLY database, jump into any one, mark it resolved or assign it to yourself, curate the ready-made replies your team uses, and add or override the assistant's answers. It is one workspace split into four tabs — Dashboard, Conversations, Canned Responses and Knowledge Base.

⚠️
Who can open and manage chats

The page itself is gated by the chatSupport permission. If your role cannot view it, the whole workspace is replaced by an "Access restricted" panel ("You don't have permission to view chat support."). On the server the rule is broader than one permission: viewing chats is allowed to anyone with chatSupport:view, support:view, or customers:view; managing chats (assign, resolve, send, close, escalate, delete…) needs chatSupport:edit or support:edit. In practice that means the Super Admin and Admin get full control, the Support role can view and handle chats, and read-only viewers get the list without the action buttons. Every change is written to the shared Audit Log as chat.<action>.

Chat Support — the four tabs & the live feed

A single workspace: Dashboard, Conversations, Canned Responses, Knowledge Base — kept in sync with the database.

Purpose Give the Super Admin one place to monitor assistant activity, step into any conversation, standardise replies, and shape the assistant's answers — all backed by the live DOODLY database, never demo data on production.
Access Super Admin (full) · Admin (full) · Support (view + handle) · other roles with customers:view can read Path Admin → System → Chat Support (/admin/chat-support.html)
Header — AI Chat Support · live status banner · tab bar (Dashboard · Conversations · Canned Responses · Knowledge Base)
Live status bannerA green line — "● Live — N conversation(s) from the DOODLY database" — or a red warning if the backend is offline or your role is blocked (403).
Tab: DashboardSix KPI cards (active / resolved / escalated / satisfaction / response time / total) plus the "Frequently asked topics" panel.
Tab: ConversationsA table of every chat; click a row to open its full transcript and take action.
Tab: Canned ResponsesAn editable library of ready-made replies your team can reuse.
Tab: Knowledge BaseThe list of topics the assistant answers, plus a box to add an override answer for any topic.
Figure — labelled layout of the Chat Support workspace
💡
Tip — the page stays live on its own

When you open Chat Support, the page fetches the latest chats from /api/admin/chat and then auto-refreshes the Dashboard every 20 seconds so your counts stay current without a manual reload. The polling is considerate: it pauses while you have a conversation transcript open or a pop-up showing, and it only refreshes while you are on the Dashboard tab, so it never yanks you out of a chat you are reading. If the banner turns red — "⚠ Backend offline… start next-app" — the counts you see are the last good copy held on the page.

ℹ️
Where the conversations come from

Each chat here is a real ChatSession in the DOODLY database, created when a customer talks to the Doodly Assistant on the storefront. A background sync mirrors those sessions (and their messages) into the admin view, translating the server's status names into the three you see on screen — a database Waiting chat shows as Active, and a Closed chat shows as Resolved. On a fresh production site the list is deliberately empty until customers actually start chatting — sample conversations are never seeded on the live domain.

Tab 1 — Dashboard

At-a-glance health of the assistant: volume, outcomes, satisfaction and the most-asked topics.

Purpose Show, in one screen, how many chats are live, how many were resolved or escalated, how happy customers are, and what they ask about most — so you can spot a spike or a problem area early.
Access Super Admin Admin Support — read Path Admin → System → Chat Support → Dashboard
Dashboard — six KPI cards on top · "Frequently asked topics" panel below
Active chatsHow many conversations are open right now.
ResolvedHow many have been closed/resolved.
EscalatedHow many were handed to a human.
Customer satisfactionAverage CSAT rating, shown as "X / 5".
Avg response timeThe assistant's reply speed (shown as 1.0s).
Total chatsEvery conversation on record.
Frequently asked topicsTop six topics by how often they came up, each with a count.
Figure — the Dashboard tab

The six KPI cards

CardWhat it meansHow it is worked out
Active chatsConversations currently open (including database "Waiting" chats, which display as Active).Count of chats whose status is active.
ResolvedConversations that have been closed off.Count of chats whose status is resolved (a database "Closed" chat also counts here).
EscalatedConversations handed over to a human agent.Count of chats flagged escalated.
Customer satisfactionThe average of the star ratings customers gave.Mean of every chat's CSAT (1–5), shown to one decimal as "X / 5"; shows "—" when no ratings exist yet.
Avg response timeHow quickly the assistant replies.Shown as 1.0s — the assistant answers instantly from its knowledge base; this is a headline figure, not a per-chat measurement.
Total chatsEvery conversation on record.The full count of non-deleted chats.

Frequently asked topics

Below the cards, a panel lists the top six topics customers raised, in order, each with a count on the right (for example "Subscription plans — 12"). The topics come from tallying every conversation's detected intents — the same intent ids the assistant matches against (see the Knowledge Base tab). When there is no data yet it reads "No data yet."

ℹ️
The API tracks more than the six cards show

The dashboard cards above are the headline figures. The underlying /api/admin/chat?view=dashboard endpoint computes several more that power reporting: waiting, AI-handled vs human-handled counts, the number of CSAT responses, and resolved today, plus the top eight topics. These are available to any tooling built on the API even though the on-screen dashboard focuses on the six most useful cards.

Tab 2 — Conversations (list)

Every chat in one table; click any row to read it in full.

Purpose Give you a scannable register of every customer conversation with its owner, length, handler, start time, status and satisfaction, and a one-click way into the full transcript.
Access Super Admin Admin Support — read; actions inside a chat need manage rights Path Admin → System → Chat Support → Conversations
Conversations — a single table, newest activity first
Chat IDThe conversation reference, e.g. CHAT-9F2A1.
UserThe customer's name, or "Guest" if not logged in.
TurnsHow many messages the conversation contains.
Handled byAI or Human.
StartedWhen the chat began.
StatusA colour badge: Active (amber) · Resolved (green) · Escalated (red).
CSATThe star rating (e.g. 5★) or "—" if none.
Figure — the Conversations list

The conversations table — columns

ColumnWhat it showsNotes
Chat IDThe unique conversation number in bold (format CHAT-XXXXX).Generated by the system; the same number appears on the transcript header.
UserThe customer's name, or Guest for a not-logged-in visitor.The assistant never exposes one customer's data to another — a guest chat only carries what the guest typed.
TurnsThe number of messages exchanged.Counts customer, assistant, agent and system lines.
Handled byAI (assistant only) or Human (a staff member replied or it was assigned).Flips to Human automatically the moment an agent sends a reply or takes the chat.
StartedThe time the conversation began.Shown as local time.
StatusA badge — Active (amber), Resolved (green) or Escalated (red).Database "Waiting" shows as Active and "Closed" as Resolved.
CSATThe customer's satisfaction rating as stars (e.g. 5★), or — if unrated.Read-only here.

Table behaviour

CapabilityHow it works here
OrderingThe list is fetched newest-activity-first (by last message time) from the database.
Search / FiltersNo filter controls on this screen — the table lists the whole synced set. (The server list endpoint does support query, status, handler, assignee and date filters for tooling; see the backend reference.)
PaginationUp to 200 conversations are loaded into the view at once; there are no page controls on screen.
ExportsNo export button here. (The API accepts a log action to record an export event in the audit trail when tooling exports.)
Row actionClick any row to open its transcript (see the next screen). There is no separate row menu.
ℹ️
Empty list

When there are no conversations, the table shows a single centred line — "No conversations." — which is the correct, honest state for a new production site until customers begin chatting.

Conversation transcript & actions

Read the whole chat, mark it resolved, or take it over as a human agent.

Purpose Let you read a conversation end to end and act on it — close it out, or assign it to yourself so it becomes a human-handled chat.
Access read for viewers; the action buttons run only for roles with manage rights (Super Admin Admin Support) Path Admin → System → Chat Support → Conversations → (open a chat)
Transcript — ← Back to conversations · CHAT-ID · customer name · status badge
Back link"← Back to conversations" returns to the list.
HeaderThe chat ID, the customer's name, and the status badge.
TranscriptEvery message in order, labelled by who sent it — the customer, the Assistant, or System.
Mark resolvedCloses the conversation (hidden once it is already resolved).
Assign to meTakes ownership; afterwards shows an "Assigned: name" badge instead.
Figure — a conversation opened in full

How the transcript reads

Messages appear top to bottom in time order. Each line is tagged with its author: the customer's name for their own messages, Assistant for the bot's replies, and System for automatic notes (for example "Assigned to …", "Chat marked Resolved", or "Chat escalated to a human agent"). Each message shows its time. When you send a reply as an agent through the backend, it is labelled with your name.

Action buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
← Back to conversationsReturns to the list without changing anything.None — client navigation only.Any viewer
Mark resolvedCloses the conversation and clears its escalated flag. The button disappears once the chat is resolved.POST /api/admin/chat with action:"close" → sets status to RESOLVED, stamps closedAt, writes a "Chat marked Resolved" system note, and logs chat.close.Super Admin+ Support
Assign to meAssigns the chat to you and switches it to human-handled. Replaced afterwards by a blue Assigned: name badge.POST /api/admin/chat with action:"assign" (your user id + name) → sets the assignee, flips Handled by to Human, writes an "Assigned to …" system note, and logs chat.assign.Super Admin+ Support
ℹ️
These two buttons are database-backed and instant

When you click Mark resolved or Assign to me on a chat that exists in the database, the action is sent straight to the server, a small toast confirms it ("Marked resolved" / "Assigned to you"), and the list re-syncs so the status and handler update everywhere. If the server rejects it because your role can't manage chats, you'll see "Only Support / Admin can manage chats (403)." A rare chat that exists only locally (not yet in the database) is handled on the page instead, so the buttons always do something sensible.

Tab 3 — Canned Responses

A small library of ready-made replies your team can reuse in chats.

Purpose Keep the phrases your team says most often — refund timelines, delivery windows, pause instructions — in one editable list so replies stay consistent and fast.
Access Super Admin Admin Support — edit Path Admin → System → Chat Support → Canned Responses
Canned responses — one editable box per reply · + Add response · Save
Response boxesA multi-line text box for each saved reply.
RemoveA link beside each box to delete that reply.
+ Add responseAdds a fresh empty box at the bottom.
SaveStores the whole list; empty boxes are dropped.
Figure — the Canned Responses editor

Field

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Response textOne ready-made reply.Free text (multi-line).Opt.Trimmed on Save; blank boxes are discarded. The saved list is kept on the device for reuse.Your refund has been initiated and will reflect in 3–5 business days.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
+ Add responseAppends a new empty response box.Client-side only until you Save.Super Admin+ Support
RemoveDeletes that response box from the list.Client-side; the removal is committed on Save.Super Admin+ Support
SaveStores every non-empty box as the canned-response library and shows "Canned responses saved".Trims and filters out blanks, then persists the list for reuse.Super Admin+ Support
ℹ️
The starter set

The tab ships with four sensible defaults you can edit or delete: a friendly opener ("Thanks for reaching out! How can I help?"), the refund timeline, the delivery window ("Deliveries arrive every morning before 7:00 AM."), and the pause/Vacation Mode note. Edit them to match your team's exact wording.

Tab 4 — Knowledge Base

The topics the assistant answers, and a way to override any answer without code.

Purpose Show every topic the Doodly Assistant can answer, and let you supply a replacement answer for any of them — so you can correct or localise a reply instantly, no developer needed.
Access Super Admin Admin Support — edit Path Admin → System → Chat Support → Knowledge Base
Knowledge Base — add-override form on top · active overrides · full topic table
Topic idWhich topic to override, e.g. sub.plans.
Override answerThe replacement text the assistant should say.
Save overrideApplies the override immediately.
Active overridesThe overrides in force, each with a Remove link.
Topics tableTopic id · Name · the default answer.
Figure — the Knowledge Base tab

An explainer line at the top reminds you: "The assistant answers from these topics before any AI model. Add an override answer for any topic (no code change)." The assistant always tries its built-in knowledge base first; only if nothing matches (and a server AI proxy has been configured) does it fall back to an AI model, and otherwise it offers to connect the customer to a human. Your overrides sit on top of the built-in answers.

Add-override form

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Topic idThe internal id of the topic you want to override.A topic code from the table below.YesBoth fields must be filled or you get "Enter a topic id and answer". Use an id exactly as shown in the topics table.sub.plans
Override answerThe exact text the assistant will say for that topic instead of the default.Free text (multi-line).YesSaved as the override; from then on the assistant uses this wherever it would have used the built-in answer for that topic.Our plans start at ₹70/day — Single, 7-day, 30-day and 90-day. Longer plans save more.

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save overrideStores the override for the entered topic and refreshes the list; shows "Override saved".Requires both fields; the override is kept and layered over the assistant's answer for that topic.Super Admin+ Support
Remove (on an active override)Deletes that override so the topic reverts to its built-in default.Drops the override for that topic id and re-renders.Super Admin+ Support

The topics table

ColumnWhat it showsNotes
TopicThe topic id as code, e.g. sub.plans, delivery.bottles, pay.failed.Copy this exact id into the "Topic id" box to override it.
NameA friendly label — e.g. "Subscription plans", "Bottle returns", "Failed payment".Documentary; the internal id is what actually addresses the topic.
Default answerThe first part of the built-in answer the assistant gives today.Shown truncated so you can recognise the topic at a glance.
💡
Tip — a few of the topics you can tune

The assistant already answers dozens of topics out of the box, including greetings, all five products (A2 milk, paneer, ghee, curd, palkova), subscription plans / pause / resume / renewal / schedule, order & delivery status, bottle returns, referral, wallet, payment methods and failed payments, invoices & GST, the company story, farmers, quality, refunds, privacy, and human handover. Pick the id from the table and drop in your own wording to fine-tune any of them.

ℹ️
Overrides change what customers see

An override is applied on top of the assistant's answer the moment you save it — the next customer who asks about that topic sees your wording, on the live storefront widget. Keep overrides accurate and on-brand; if in doubt, remove the override to fall back to the tested default.

Workflow — the life of a support chat
Customer opens the Doodly AssistantAssistant answers from the knowledge baseSession saved to the databaseCustomer asks for a human → chat escalatesSuper Admin opens it here & "Assign to me"Chat becomes Human-handledIssue solved → "Mark resolved"Customer rates it (CSAT)
Figure — from first message to a resolved, rated conversation

Backend & data model — for reference

What sits behind the screen: the API, the record fields, and the full action set.

Purpose Document the real endpoint, the stored fields and every server action, so power users and integrators understand exactly what the module can do beyond the four on-screen tabs.
Access Super Admin — reference Endpoint /api/admin/chat

What a conversation stores (ChatSession)

FieldMeaningValues / notes
numberThe human-readable chat reference.Format CHAT-XXXXX; unique.
customerName / customerEmail / customerIdWho the chat is with.Name defaults to "Guest" when not logged in; email/id optional.
channelWhere the chat came from.Defaults to web.
statusLifecycle state.ACTIVE · WAITING · RESOLVED · ESCALATED · CLOSED.
handledByWho is answering.AI or Human; flips to Human on an agent reply, assign or transfer.
assigneeId / assigneeNameThe staff member who owns the chat.Set by Assign / Transfer; cleared when unassigned.
csatThe customer's satisfaction rating.1–5, or empty.
escalatedWhether it was handed to a human.Set when a chat is escalated.
topicsA tally of the intents the assistant matched.Powers the "Frequently asked topics" panel.
lastMessageAt / closedAt / createdAt / deletedAtTimestamps.Sort key is lastMessageAt; deletes are soft (deletedAt), so nothing is truly erased.

What a message stores (ChatMessage)
