GST rates (default + per-product)Central tax rates and product tax assignment.Admin → System → GST Management
Referral & rewards rulesReferral reward amounts and eligibility.Admin → Growth → Referrals
Roles & permissionsPer-role and per-module access control.Admin → System → Permissions

Buttons & actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Save settingsCollects every field on the form and writes the changes. Button shows “Saving…” while it works, then a toast “Settings saved to the DOODLY database” and the form reloads with the stored values.POST /api/admin/app-settings with a patch of key→value. General/notify/security keys upsert into AppSetting; company name + GSTIN upsert into BillingConfig; the change is written to the audit trail as settings.update.Super Admin
Managed-elsewhere linksNavigate to the dedicated page that owns that setting.Plain in-app navigation; no write.Super Admin Admin
⚠️
Warning

There is no per-field Save and no Cancel/Reset. Save settings submits the whole form at once — every field currently on screen is included in the patch. Check every panel before you save, and if you change your mind before saving, simply reload the page to discard edits.

Workflow — editing a platform setting
Open SettingsBanner confirms Live + your edit rightsChange fields across General / Notifications / SecurityClick Save settingsKeys upsert to AppSetting; company/GSTIN to BillingConfigAudit logged as settings.updateToast + form reloads with saved values
Figure — the end-to-end save flow, server-authoritative and audited.
✅
Best Practice

Treat the notification toggles as intent, not delivery. Turn a channel On here and confirm the provider credentials are configured before you rely on it — otherwise customers will silently receive nothing. When rolling out a new channel, enable it, send one test campaign from Admin → Content → Notifications, verify delivery, then announce it.

⛔
Caution

Lowering Password min length or raising Max login attempts weakens account security for every user. Only relax these deliberately, and prefer stricter values. Keep Legal company name and GSTIN exactly matching your registration — they feed straight into invoices, and a wrong value is reflected on tax documents.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Banner is red: “Backend offline”.The next-app API isn't running / unreachable at the shown base URL.Start the backend (next-app). The page reloads settings automatically once it responds.
Form is read-only with an amber “Read-only” badge and no Save button.You are signed in as Admin (or another role), not Super Admin — only Super Admin can edit.Have a Super Admin make the change, or get your role elevated.
Save fails with “Only Super Admin can change settings (403).”The save was attempted without Super Admin edit rights (server rejected it).Sign in as Super Admin; the API enforces this regardless of what the screen shows.
“Your role can't view Settings (403).”Your role has no settings:view permission at all.Only Super Admin and Admin can open this screen — request access.
Legal company name / GSTIN saved but a general field reverted.You left a general field blank — the server keeps the previous value for company name and stores it as-is for other keys.Re-enter the value and Save again; blank company-name input is intentionally ignored to avoid wiping it.

FAQ

Why can Admins see Settings but not change anything?

By design. The permission matrix grants Admin settings:view only; the edit action (settings:edit) is reserved for Super Admin. Admins get full visibility for reference, but platform-level configuration is a single-owner responsibility.

I switched on WhatsApp but customers get no messages — why?

The toggles are master enable-flags only. Actual sending needs the provider (Twilio/Resend/etc.) credentials configured. Enable the channel here, then confirm the provider keys are set before relying on it.

Where do currency, GST rate and delivery times really come from?

Currency, time zone and date format live in Settings. The default GST rate and product tax live on GST Management; delivery cut-off/slots live on Delivery Settings. The bottom “Managed on their own pages” panel links straight to each of them.

Is every change tracked?

Yes. Each save writes an audit entry with action settings.update and the changed keys, viewable under Admin → System → Audit Logs.

Module chapter

Admin · HR

6 screen groups documented from the live application.

DOODLYAdmin · HR · HR Dashboard
§Admin · HR

HR Dashboard

A single, read-only snapshot of the whole workforce — headcount, who is present or absent today, who is on leave, how many payslips still need to be run, how much salary-advance money is still owed, and how staff are split across departments — pulled live from the same records that power Employees, Attendance, Leave, Salary Advances and Payroll.

ℹ️
What this screen is (and is not)

The HR Dashboard item under Admin → Human Resources is served by the storefront admin page /admin/hr-dashboard.html, which mounts the DOODLY_HR module's mountDashboard() renderer into #hrDashboardMount. It is a reporting screen only — there are no forms, no filters, no buttons and nothing to save. Every number is calculated fresh by the backend each time the page loads, from one call to GET /api/admin/employees?view=dashboard (server function hrDashboard()). To change any of the underlying figures you go to the relevant HR section — the dashboard just summarises them. Money (the "Advances outstanding" figure) is shown in ₹; the backend stores it as integer paise (₹1 = 100 paise).

ℹ️
Who can open it

The API gate is requirePermission(req, "employees", "view") — access is granted by the Employees module permission, not a separate one. In the default role matrix that means Super Admin and Admin (full access to everything HR) and Accountant (view-level access to Employees, HR Dashboard, Attendance and Leave, plus deeper rights on Payroll and Advances). No other staff role — Operations, Procurement, Inventory, Quality, Marketing, Support, Delivery Executive — can open HR at all, and Customers never can. Server-side permission checks are the real boundary; hiding the menu item is only convenience.

HR Dashboard

Eight workforce KPI cards over a department-wise headcount table — the people pulse of the business at a glance.

Purpose Give HR and management one place to see, at a glance, the size of the team, today's attendance picture, who is on leave, how much payroll and advance work is outstanding, how many people have exited, and how the team is distributed across departments — without opening five separate screens.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — anyone with Employees · view Path Admin → Human Resources → HR Dashboard
Human Resources — HR Dashboard · 8 KPI cards + department headcount panel
KPI cards — row 1Total employees · Present today · Absent today · On leave
KPI cards — row 2Payroll pending · Advances outstanding · Resigned / exited · Active
Department-wise headcountPanel with a two-column table: Department · Employees (highest count first)
Footer hint"Attendance, leave, salary, advances and payroll are managed under Human Resources → their sections."
Figure — the HR Dashboard: eight colour-coded KPI cards laid out in a responsive grid, above the department-wise headcount panel.

Every KPI card and where the number comes from

Cards render in a fixed order left-to-right, top-to-bottom. Each card shows a large number (or a ₹ amount) with a small label under it. A colour tone is applied to draw the eye: green = healthy, amber = needs attention, red = loss of headcount. All counts ignore soft-deleted (removed) employees — every query filters deletedAt: null.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Total employees
neutral
 Whole active workforce on the books, regardless of status. Whole number ≥ 0 Read Count of EmployeeProfile where deletedAt = null. Includes on-leave, resigned and terminated staff (anyone not removed). Returned as kpis.total. 42
Present today
green
 How many people are marked in today's attendance as working. Whole number ≥ 0 Read Count of Attendance rows for today (00:00 local) whose status is PRESENT, HALF_DAY or WFH. Returned as kpis.presentToday. 31
Absent today
amber
 Rough count of active staff not accounted for as present or on leave today. Whole number ≥ 0 Read Derived, not a direct query. Calculated as max(0, Active − Present today − On leave). It is not a count of attendance rows marked ABSENT — see the Warning below. Returned as kpis.absentToday. 6
On leave
amber
 Staff whose employment status is currently "On Leave". Whole number ≥ 0 Read Count of EmployeeProfile where status = ON_LEAVE and deletedAt = null. This is the status flag on the employee record, set from the Employee detail ("Mark on leave"), not a count of approved Leave requests. Returned as kpis.onLeave. 5
Payroll pending
neutral
 Payslips created but not yet finalised — payroll work still to do. Whole number ≥ 0 Read Count of Payslip rows where status = DRAFT. Finalised and Paid payslips are excluded. Returned as kpis.payslipsPending. 12
Advances outstanding
neutral · ₹
 Total salary-advance money still owed back by staff. ₹ amount ≥ ₹0 Read Sum of remainingPaise across all SalaryAdvance rows whose status is APPROVED (i.e. disbursed and being recovered; COMPLETED, PENDING and REJECTED advances are excluded). Returned as paise in kpis.advancesOutstandingPaise and shown in ₹. ₹18,500
Resigned / exited
red
 People who have left — resignations and terminations combined. Whole number ≥ 0 Read Count of EmployeeProfile where status ∈ {RESIGNED, TERMINATED} and deletedAt = null. Returned as kpis.resigned. 3
Active
green
 Currently employed and working staff. Whole number ≥ 0 Read Count of EmployeeProfile where status = ACTIVE and deletedAt = null. This is the denominator behind the "Absent today" derivation. Returned as kpis.active. 34
⚠️
"Absent today" is an estimate, not a headcount of absences

Read this card carefully. It is computed arithmetically as Active − Present today − On leave (floored at zero). It does not read attendance rows marked ABSENT. So early in the day, before attendance has been marked, this number will be large simply because few people are marked present yet — it will shrink as staff are marked in. It also treats employees whose status flag is ON_LEAVE as "on leave", but not those merely marked PAID_LEAVE/SICK_LEAVE in today's register. For the true, per-person absence picture use Admin → Human Resources → Attendance, which counts real ABSENT marks for the chosen date.

Department-wise headcount

Below the KPI cards sits one panel titled Department-wise headcount. It lists how many people belong to each department, so you can see where the team is concentrated. When there are no employees yet it shows the plain message "No employees yet." A short footer line under the panel reminds you that the detailed work lives elsewhere: "Attendance, leave, salary, advances and payroll are managed under Human Resources → their sections."

AspectBehaviour on this screen
ColumnsDepartment (name) and Employees (the count, shown in bold).
RowsOne row per department that currently has at least one non-removed employee. Departments with no one are omitted. Source: groupBy(department) over EmployeeProfile where deletedAt = null, returned as byDepartment.
SortingFixed — highest headcount first (descending by count), decided by the backend. Not clickable / not user-sortable.
Filters / SearchNone. This screen has no filter bar and no search box.
PaginationNone — every department is shown in one list.
Row actionsNone. Rows are display-only; there is nothing to click.
ExportNone on this screen. (CSV export exists on the Attendance and Leave screens, not here.)
ℹ️
The known departments

Each employee is filed under one of ten standard departments defined in the module: Delivery, Procurement, Production, Quality, Inventory, Customer Support, Finance, Marketing, Operations, Administration. The headcount table only lists departments that actually have staff, so a brand-new business may show just two or three rows.

ℹ️
The API also returns an employment-type breakdown

Behind the scenes the same hrDashboard() response includes a byType array (headcount split by Full Time / Part Time / Contract / Internship). This screen does not draw it — it renders only the eight KPI cards and the department table — but the figure is available to other tools and reports.

Loading and error states

While the figures are being fetched the page shows a skeleton placeholder (a shimmering panel). If the request fails — no network, session expired, or no permission — the whole area is replaced by an amber warning box reading "Couldn't load the HR dashboard." (or the specific server message). There is no auto-refresh: the numbers are captured at page load, so reload the page to get the latest figures.

Workflow — how the dashboard builds itself
Open HR DashboardShow skeletonGET ?view=dashboardhrDashboard() runs 9 live counts in parallelRender 8 KPI cardsRender department table (desc)
Figure — one page-load runs nine database aggregates at once, then paints the cards and the headcount table.
✅
Best Practice — read it in the right order, at the right time

Check the HR Dashboard after the morning attendance is marked, not before — otherwise "Present today" is artificially low and "Absent today" artificially high. Treat the two amber cards (Absent today, On leave) and the red card (Resigned / exited) as your daily watch-list, and the two work-queue cards (Payroll pending, Advances outstanding) as your month-end to-do list: a non-zero "Payroll pending" means draft payslips still need finalising, and a large "Advances outstanding" is money you are still recovering. When a number looks wrong, don't fix it here — open the matching HR section, correct the source record, then reload the dashboard.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"Absent today" is very high in the morningAttendance hasn't been marked yet, so few people count as "Present today"; the card is Active − Present − On leave.Mark attendance in HR → Attendance for today, then reload. The number falls as staff are marked present.
Whole area shows "Couldn't load the HR dashboard."Session expired, offline, or the account lacks Employees · view.Sign in again / check the connection. If it persists, confirm the role has Employees view rights (Super Admin, Admin or Accountant).
"On leave" doesn't match today's leave registerThis card counts employees whose status flag is ON_LEAVE, not approved leave requests for the date.Set the person's status via the Employee detail ("Mark on leave" / "Mark active"), or use HR → Leave for the day-by-day picture.
A department is missing from the headcount tableNo non-removed employee is currently filed under it.Add or reassign an employee to that department in HR → Employees; the row appears once someone is in it.
Numbers look stale after I changed a recordThe dashboard is a one-time snapshot taken at page load — it does not live-refresh.Reload the page to re-run the queries.
"Advances outstanding" seems too lowOnly APPROVED advances still being recovered are summed; PENDING and COMPLETED ones are excluded by design.Approve pending advances in HR → Salary Advances so they enter the outstanding total.

Frequently asked questions

Can I edit anything from the HR Dashboard?

No. It is entirely read-only — there are no forms, buttons, filters or exports. It only reports. To change a figure, open the underlying section (Employees, Attendance, Leave, Salary Advances or Payroll), make the change there, then reload the dashboard.

Does "Total employees" include people who have left?

Yes. "Total" counts everyone still on the books (not soft-deleted), including On-leave, Resigned and Terminated staff. Use the "Active" card for the number of currently-working people, and "Resigned / exited" for those who have left.

Why doesn't "Present + Absent + On leave" always add up to "Active"?

Because "Present today" counts attendance rows (Present, Half-day or WFH) which can include employees whose profile status isn't strictly Active, while "Absent today" is floored at zero and derived arithmetically. The three cards are indicators, not a strict reconciliation. For an exact daily tally, use the Attendance register.

Is the ₹ figure in rupees or paise?

The card shows rupees. Internally the API returns advancesOutstandingPaise in paise and the screen divides by 100 for display, so ₹18,500 is stored as 1,850,000 paise.

DOODLYAdmin · HR · Employees
§Admin · HR

Employees

The Employee Master — the single place to add staff, record their employment and personal details, keep their identity and bank documents safely, and manage their working status across their whole time at DOODLY.

The Employees page is the master record for every person who works at DOODLY — delivery executives, procurement and production staff, support agents, accountants, managers and admins. In DOODLY an employee is not a separate thing from a login account: each employee record is joined one-to-one to a staff user account. When you add an employee here, DOODLY quietly creates their sign-in account too (with the login role you pick), and when you mark them Resigned, Terminated or remove them, that same account is switched off. This means you manage the whole person — who they are, what they do, and whether they can sign in — from one screen.

Every employee gets an auto-generated employee code in the form EMP-0001, EMP-0002 and so on (four digits, counted up and checked so two people never share a code). This code, not the name, is the stable handle used across Attendance, Leave, Salary Advances and Payroll.

The page is built from three parts that we document in turn: a live statistics strip at the top, the employee directory table with its search and filters, and the Add / Edit employee form that opens as a pop-up. We then cover the employee detail view (opened by the View button on each row) and the photo & documents area inside the form.

ℹ️
Where the data lives

This page is fully backend-driven — it reads and writes real records through /api/admin/employees (Postgres). There is no demo data. An employee record is an EmployeeProfile row joined 1:1 to a User account: HR fields (department, identity, bank, documents) live on the profile; the name, email, phone, login role and account status live on the user. Every create, edit, status change, delete and document upload is written to the Audit Log.

⚠️
Who can open this page

Employees is an HR module. Super Admin and Admin have full access (view, create, edit, delete). Accountant has view-only access and sees sensitive identity numbers masked. All other roles (Operations, Procurement, Inventory, Quality, Marketing, Support, Delivery Executive, Customer) have no access to this page at all. Permission is enforced on the server for every request — the buttons you see are only the UX half of the same rule.

Employee statistics strip

Four live counters summarising your whole workforce at a glance.

Purpose Give a one-look picture of the team — total headcount, how many are actively working, how many are on leave, and how many departments you span — before you drill into the table.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant (view). Hidden entirely for roles without the Employees → View permission. Path Admin → Human Resources → Employees (top of page)
Header — Employee Master · "Manage staff records, employment details and identity documents."
TotalCount of all non-deleted employees
ActiveEmployees with status = Active (green)
On leaveEmployees with status = On Leave (amber)
DepartmentsHow many distinct departments have staff
Figure — the four KPI tiles that sit above the employee table
TileWhat it countsColourWhere it comes from
TotalEvery employee that has not been soft-deleted.NeutralCount of EmployeeProfile rows where deletedAt is empty.
ActiveEmployees whose status is Active — currently working.Greenstatus = ACTIVE.
On leaveEmployees whose status is On Leave — temporarily away but still on the roll.Amberstatus = ON_LEAVE.
DepartmentsNumber of different departments that have at least one employee.NeutralDistinct departments among non-deleted employees.
💡
Tip

The strip refreshes automatically after every action — add, edit, mark on leave, mark active, or remove. You never need to reload the page to see the numbers update. The counters reflect your current filter is not applied to them: they always show the whole workforce, so they stay a true company-wide summary even while you filter the table below.

Employee directory

The searchable, filterable list of every employee, one row each, with a View button.

Purpose The working list of the whole team. Search or filter to find a person, then open their full record. The "Add employee" button starts a brand-new record.
Access Super Admin Admin (full) · Accountant (view-only — no "Add employee" button). The Add employee button appears only if your role has Employees → Create. Path Admin → Human Resources → Employees
Toolbar — Search box · Department ▾ · Status ▾ · + Add employee
CodeEmployee code, bold (e.g. EMP-0007)
EmployeeFull name + designation underneath
DepartmentWhich team they belong to
PhoneMobile number (or —)
TypeFull Time / Part Time / Contract / Internship
StatusActive (green) · On Leave (amber) · Resigned/Terminated (red)
JoinedDate of joining
ViewOpens the full employee record
Figure — one row per employee; a footer reads "N employees"

Table specification

AspectBehaviour in this table
ColumnsCode, Employee (name + designation), Department, Phone, Type, Status, Joined, and a View button.
Rows shownAll employees that are not soft-deleted, matching your current search and filters.
SearchOne box at the top ("Search name, code, phone, designation…"). It searches server-side across employee code, designation, department, name, phone and email (case-insensitive). Typing is debounced by ~350 ms so it waits until you pause before searching.
FiltersTwo dropdowns: Department (All departments, or one of the 10 departments) and Status (All status, Active, On Leave, Resigned, Terminated). Changing either reloads the list immediately.
SortingAlways by employee code, ascending (EMP-0001 first). There are no clickable column sorts on this screen.
PaginationLoads the first page of up to 50 employees (the backend supports up to 200 per page). The footer shows the total count.
Row actionA single View button per row opens the full detail pop-up, where all the edit / status / delete actions live.
ExportsNone on this screen — employee identity and bank data are sensitive. Attendance, Leave and Payroll have their own CSV exports.
Empty stateWith no employees yet, the table shows "No employees yet — add your first with 'Add employee'."

Status badges

BadgeColourMeaningCan they still sign in?
ActiveGreenNormal, currently-working employee.Yes
On LeaveAmberTemporarily away (long leave, sabbatical) but still employed.Yes
ResignedRedHas left of their own accord. Their sign-in account is disabled automatically.No
TerminatedRedEmployment ended by the company. Their sign-in account is disabled automatically.No
ℹ️
Status is tied to the login account

Setting an employee to Resigned or Terminated automatically disables their staff sign-in account, so they can no longer log in. Setting them back to Active re-enables the account. On Leave keeps the account enabled — leave is about attendance, not access.

Add / Edit employee form

The pop-up that creates a new employee or updates an existing one — grouped into four sections.

Purpose Capture everything about a person: their basics and personal details, their employment and login role, their identity & bank details, and (once saved) their photo and documents. On create, DOODLY also builds their staff sign-in account.
Access Super Admin Admin — creating needs Employees → Create; editing needs Employees → Edit. Accountant (view-only) cannot open this form. Path Admin → Human Resources → Employees → Add employee (or the View button → Edit on any row)
Modal — Add employee / Edit employee — EMP-0007 · ✕ close
Basic informationName, mobile, email, alt mobile, DOB, gender, blood group, emergency contact
Employment detailsDepartment, designation, type, joining date, login role, reporting manager, location, status
Identity & bankAadhaar, PAN, driving licence, bank a/c, IFSC, bank name, UPI
Photo & documentsProfile photo + attached files (edit mode only)
Cancel / SaveSticky footer buttons
Figure — the four-section form; when editing, it opens pre-filled with the employee code in the title

The form checks five required fields before it will save: Full name, Mobile number, Department, Designation and Date of joining. If any is blank, an inline message names the missing field and jumps you to it. Everything else is optional.

Section A — Basic information

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Full nameThe person's display name across DOODLY; saved to their user account.Text, up to 80 characters.YesCannot be blank. Stored on User.name.Ravi Teja
Mobile numberPrimary contact and a unique identity for the account.Phone number, up to 20 characters (7–15 digits, may start with +).YesValidated by pattern; must be unique — a duplicate phone is rejected ("A user with this phone already exists."). Stored on User.phone.+91 98765 43210
EmailOptional login identity and contact address.A valid email, up to 120 characters.Opt.Lower-cased on save; must be unique — a duplicate email is rejected ("A user with this email already exists."). Stored on User.email.ravi.delivery@doodly.in
Alternate mobileA backup contact number.Phone number, up to 20 characters.Opt.Stored on the profile as altPhone.+91 90000 11111
Date of birthThe person's birthday (for records / HR).Date picker.Opt.Stored as dob.1996-04-18
GenderRecords the person's gender.— · Male · Female · Other.Opt.Free choice from the dropdown.Male
Blood groupUseful for emergencies.Short text, up to 5 characters.Opt.Placeholder shows "O+".O+
Emergency contact nameWho to call if something happens to the employee.Text, up to 80 characters.Opt.Stored as emergencyName.Lakshmi Teja
Emergency contact numberPhone number for the emergency contact.Phone number, up to 20 characters.Opt.Stored as emergencyPhone.+91 98888 22222

Section B — Employment details

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
DepartmentWhich team the person belongs to.One of: Delivery, Procurement, Production, Quality, Inventory, Customer Support, Finance, Marketing, Operations, Administration.YesRequired; stored as department. Feeds the department filter and headcount.Delivery
DesignationThe person's job title.Free text, up to 80 characters.YesRequired; stored as designation. Shown under the name everywhere.Senior Delivery Executive
Employment typeThe nature of the engagement.Full Time · Part Time · Contract · Internship.Opt.Defaults to Full Time. Stored as employmentType.Full Time
Date of joiningWhen they started (or will start).Date picker.YesRequired; stored as dateOfJoining. Shown in the "Joined" column.2026-02-01
Login role (access)What the person can see and do when they sign in — this is the access decision.Delivery Executive · Support · Operations · Procurement · Inventory · Quality · Marketing · Accountant · Admin · Super Admin.Opt.Defaults to Support. Sets the backing User.role. An unrecognised value falls back to Support on the server.Delivery Executive
Reporting managerWho this person reports to.— None — or any active employee (shown as "CODE · Name").Opt.List comes from active employees; a person cannot be their own manager. Stored as reportingManagerId.EMP-0002 · Priya Nair
Work locationWhere they are based.Free text, up to 120 characters.Opt.Placeholder shows "Vijayawada". Stored as workLocation.Vijayawada
StatusTheir current employment status.Active · On Leave · Resigned · Terminated.Opt.Defaults to Active. Setting Resigned/Terminated disables the sign-in account; Active re-enables it.Active
⚠️
"Login role" grants real power

The Login role decides what the person can do once they sign in — pick the lowest role that lets them do their job. A Delivery Executive should not be given Admin. Choosing Admin or Super Admin here creates a high-privilege account, so use those sparingly and only for people who genuinely need them.

Section C — Identity & bank

These are the sensitive fields used for payroll and compliance. They are masked for anyone who only has view access (they see "•••• 1234" instead of the full number), and shown in full only to roles that can edit employees (Admin / Super Admin).

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Aadhaar numberGovernment identity number.Up to 20 characters.Opt.Stored as aadhaar. Masked when the viewer lacks edit rights.1234 5678 9012
PAN numberTax identity number.Up to 12 characters.Opt.Stored as pan. Masked for view-only roles.ABCDE1234F
Driving licenceNeeded for delivery staff who ride/drive.Up to 30 characters.Opt.Stored as drivingLicence. Masked for view-only roles.AP0920210012345
Bank account numberWhere salary is paid.Up to 30 characters.Opt.Stored as bankAccount. Masked for view-only roles.50100123456789
IFSC codeIdentifies the bank branch for transfers.Up to 20 characters.Opt.Stored as ifsc. Shown in full (not treated as secret).HDFC0001234
Bank nameThe employee's bank.Up to 60 characters.Opt.Stored as bankName. Shown in full.HDFC Bank
UPI ID (optional)Alternative payout handle.Up to 60 characters.Opt.Stored as upiId. Shown in full.ravi@okhdfcbank
⛔
Caution — do not overwrite masked numbers

When you edit an employee and a number shows as masked ("•••• 1234"), the form deliberately clears that box so you do not accidentally save the mask over the real value. Leaving it blank keeps the stored number safe. Only type into these boxes if you actually want to replace the number. If you have view-only access you cannot type a real value in anyway.

Form buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Add employee (create mode)Validates the five required fields, creates the employee and their staff sign-in account, and closes the form.POST /api/admin/employees — generates the next EMP-#### code, creates the backing User with the chosen login role (status Active), then the EmployeeProfile. Returns HTTP 201. Logged as employee.create.Super Admin Admin (Employees → Create)
Save changes (edit mode)Saves every changed field back to the record.PATCH /api/admin/employees/{id} with the fields. Splits updates across the user (name/email/phone/role) and the profile; records exactly which fields changed. Logged as employee.update.Super Admin Admin (Employees → Edit)
Cancel / ✕Closes the form without saving. Clicking the dark backdrop also closes it.No backend call.—
Upload photoAdds/replaces the profile photo (edit mode only — see next screen).POST /api/admin/employees/{id}/photo.Super Admin Admin (Employees → Edit)
Choose file + UploadAttaches a labelled document (edit mode only — see next screen).POST /api/admin/employees/{id}/documents.Super Admin Admin (Employees → Edit)
Workflow — onboarding a new employee
Click Add employeeFill name, mobile, department, designation, joining datePick employment type + login roleAdd personal + emergency detailsAdd employee (account + code created)Reopen the recordUpload photo + documents
Figure — end-to-end flow; identity/bank and files can be added on the first save or later
ℹ️
Why photos & documents wait until after the first save

Files are stored against the employee's ID, which only exists once the record is created. So when you first add someone, the Photo & documents area says "Save the employee first, then reopen this record to add a photo and documents." Save, click View, then Edit, and the upload controls appear.

Employee detail view

The read-only record that the View button opens, with all the action buttons.

Purpose See a full, tidy summary of one employee — their employment, personal, and identity/bank details — and act on them (edit, salary, mark on leave / active, remove) without leaving the page.
Access Super Admin Admin (all actions) · Accountant (view, with masked numbers; sees the Salary structure button). Action buttons appear only for the permissions you hold. Path Admin → Human Resources → Employees → View (any row)
Modal — Ravi Teja · EMP-0007 · Senior Delivery Executive — Delivery · [Active]
HeaderName · code · designation — department · status badge
Employment listPhone, Email, Type, Joined, Reporting manager, Work location, Login role
Personal listDate of birth, Gender, Blood group, Emergency contact
Identity & bank listAadhaar, PAN, Driving licence, Bank a/c, IFSC, Bank, UPI (masked for view-only)
Action barEdit · Salary structure · Mark on leave · Mark active · Remove
Figure — the detail pop-up; only fields that have a value are shown in each list

What each section shows

SectionFields displayed (only if filled)
HeaderFull name, employee code, designation — department, and the coloured status badge.
EmploymentPhone, Email, Employment type, Date of joining, Reporting manager (name), Work location, Login role.
PersonalDate of birth, Gender, Blood group, Emergency (name · number).
Identity & bankAadhaar, PAN, Driving licence, Bank account, IFSC, Bank, UPI. A "(masked)" note appears next to the heading when your role only has view access.

Action buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
EditCloses the detail view and reopens the full Add/Edit form, pre-filled, so you can change any field.Opens the form → PATCH /api/admin/employees/{id} on save.Super Admin Admin (Employees → Edit)
Salary structureOpens this employee's salary structure (basic pay, allowances, deductions) — the payroll side of the record.Opens the salary-structure editor for the employee.Needs Payroll → Edit — held by Super Admin Admin Accountant
Mark on leaveSets the employee's status to On Leave in one click (their login stays enabled).PATCH /api/admin/employees/{id} with action:"status", status:"ON_LEAVE". Logged as employee.status.Super Admin Admin (Employees → Edit)
Mark activeSets the status back to Active and re-enables the sign-in account.PATCH /api/admin/employees/{id} with action:"status", status:"ACTIVE" → also flips the user back to Active. Logged as employee.status.Super Admin Admin (Employees → Edit)
RemoveAfter a confirm prompt ("Remove {name}? Their staff account is disabled."), soft-deletes the employee.DELETE /api/admin/employees/{id} → stamps deletedAt, sets status Terminated, and disables the sign-in account. The row drops out of the list. Logged as employee.delete.Super Admin Admin (Employees → Delete)
⚠️
Remove is a soft-delete — but there is no Restore button here

"Remove" does not erase anything: it hides the record, marks it Terminated, and stamps a deletion time so history (attendance, payslips, audit) stays intact. However, the Employees page does not offer an in-page "Restore". If you removed the wrong person, the record still exists in the database — recovering it needs a Super Admin / engineering step, not a button on this screen. So confirm carefully before removing.

Photo & documents

Private, secure storage for the profile photo and identity proofs, inside the edit form.

Purpose Keep the person's photo and their supporting documents (Aadhaar card, PAN card, bank proof, licence) attached to their record — stored privately and shown only to HR / Admin.
Access Super Admin Admin (Employees → Edit). Only appears when editing an already-saved employee and when file storage is configured. Path Admin → Human Resources → Employees → View → Edit → Photo & documents
Section — Photo & documents
Avatar + Upload photoRound photo (or initials) with an "Upload photo" button
Document listEach doc: label, file name, size, View link, Remove
Add documentLabel box + "Choose file" + "Upload" button
Limits note"PNG, JPG, WEBP or PDF · up to 5 MB · stored privately"
Figure — the upload area shown only in edit mode of a saved record

Upload rules (enforced by the server)

ItemAllowed typesMax sizeNotes
Profile photoPNG, JPG, WEBP (images only — a PDF is rejected for the photo).5 MBReplaces the previous photo (the old file is deleted). Shown as a round avatar; if none, the person's initials show instead.
DocumentPNG, JPG, WEBP or PDF.5 MBNeeds a short label (e.g. "Aadhaar card", up to 60 characters). Stored in a private bucket; opened via a temporary signed "View" link, visible to HR/Admin only.

Buttons in this area

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Upload photoPick an image; it uploads immediately and the avatar updates.POST /api/admin/employees/{id}/photo (multipart). Logged as employee.photo.set.Super Admin Admin (Edit)
Choose file → UploadPick a file, type a label, then Upload to attach it to the list.POST /api/admin/employees/{id}/documents (multipart, with label). Logged as employee.document.add.Super Admin Admin (Edit)
View (on a doc)Opens the file in a new tab via a short-lived secure link.Signed URL minted on read; no permanent public link exists.Super Admin Admin (Edit)
Remove (on a doc)After a confirm ("Remove this document?"), deletes the file and its list entry.DELETE /api/admin/employees/{id}/documents?path=…. Logged as employee.document.remove.Super Admin Admin (Edit)
ℹ️
If uploads are switched off

When file storage (Supabase Storage) has not been configured on the environment, the section shows "Photo & document uploads (Aadhaar/PAN/bank proof) turn on once Supabase Storage is configured. All other fields save now." — the rest of the employee record still saves normally; only the file uploads wait.

How status changes affect the sign-in account

Because an employee and their login account are one and the same, changing an employee's status can enable or disable their access. This table is the full set of rules.

You set the employee to…Employee status becomesTheir sign-in accountHow
Active (Mark active, or on create)ActiveEnabled — can sign inBacking user set to Active.
On Leave (Mark on leave)On LeaveLeft enabled — can still sign inOnly the profile status changes.
Resigned (via the form)ResignedDisabled — cannot sign inBacking user set to Disabled automatically.
Terminated (via the form)TerminatedDisabled — cannot sign inBacking user set to Disabled automatically.
Remove (soft-delete)Terminated + hiddenDisabled — cannot sign inRecord stamped deletedAt; user disabled.

Who can do what on this page

Access to the Employees module is decided by your role. The server enforces these rules on every request; the buttons only reflect them.

RoleView list & detailsSee full identity/bankAdd / EditRemove
Super AdminYesYesYesYes
AdminYesYesYesYes
AccountantYesNo — masked (•••• 1234)NoNo
All other roles (Operations, Procurement, Inventory, Quality, Marketing, Support, Delivery Executive, Customer)No access to the module———
ℹ️
Masking, precisely

Only the four most sensitive numbers are masked for view-only roles: Aadhaar, PAN, Driving licence and Bank account (shown as "•••• " plus the last four digits). IFSC, Bank name and UPI are shown in full to any viewer. Uploaded document files are only openable by roles that can edit employees.

✅
Best Practice

Add every real team member here rather than only in User Management — this way their department, joining date, documents and payroll all hang off one record and their EMP-#### code flows into Attendance, Leave, Advances and Payroll. Give each person the lowest login role that fits their job. Set the reporting manager so the org chart and leave approvals make sense. Keep Aadhaar/PAN/bank details accurate for payroll, and upload the matching proof documents. When someone goes on extended absence use Mark on leave (keeps their login); when they leave the company set Resigned/Terminated or Remove to cut access immediately — and double-check before removing, since there is no one-click restore on this page.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"A user with this phone already exists." / "…email already exists."The mobile number or email is already used by another account (customer or staff).Search for the existing person and edit them instead of creating a duplicate, or use a different number/email that is genuinely unique.
"Please fill in the …" appears and the form won't save.One of the five required fields (name, mobile, department, designation, joining date) is blank.Fill the named field — the form jumps you straight to it — then save again.
The Photo & documents area says "Save the employee first…".You are still creating the record — there is no ID to attach files to yet.Save with Add employee, then reopen via View → Edit; the upload controls will now appear.
"Photo & document uploads turn on once Supabase Storage is configured."File storage isn't set up on this environment.All other fields still save. Ask an admin/engineer to add the Supabase Storage keys to enable uploads.
Upload rejected: "File is too large (max 5 MB)." or "Only PNG, JPG, WEBP or PDF…".The file is over 5 MB or in a type that isn't allowed (photos must be images, not PDF).Compress or re-scan the file under 5 MB and use PNG/JPG/WEBP (or PDF for documents).
Aadhaar/PAN/bank show as "•••• 1234" and the edit box is empty.You have view-only access, or the form blanked the masked value on purpose so you don't overwrite it.This is intended. Leave the box empty to keep the stored number. To reveal or change full numbers you need Edit rights (Admin / Super Admin).
A former employee can still be found by search.Their status is Resigned/Terminated but they were not removed.Their login is already disabled. Use the Status filter, or Remove them to drop them from the default list — history is preserved.

Frequently asked questions

Does adding an employee also create their login account?

Yes. Each employee is joined one-to-one to a staff user account. When you click Add employee, DOODLY creates the backing account with the Login role you chose and sets it Active. That is why the phone and email must be unique across all accounts.

How is the employee code decided?

Automatically. DOODLY takes the current employee count, forms the next EMP-#### (four digits, zero-padded), and checks it isn't already taken before assigning it. You never type it — it appears once the record is saved, e.g. EMP-0007.

Why can the Accountant see employees but not the full Aadhaar or bank number?

The Accountant role has view-only access so it can reference the roster for payroll, but the four most sensitive numbers (Aadhaar, PAN, driving licence, bank account) are masked to the last four digits. Full numbers and the ability to edit are limited to Admin and Super Admin.

What's the difference between "Mark on leave" and "Resigned/Terminated"?

Mark on leave is for a temporary absence — the person stays employed and their login keeps working. Resigned/Terminated means they have left; DOODLY disables their sign-in account automatically. Use On Leave for people coming back, and Resigned/Terminated for people who aren't.

If I "Remove" someone by mistake, is their data gone?

No — Remove is a soft-delete. The record is hidden, marked Terminated and time-stamped, and their login is disabled, but nothing (attendance, payslips, audit) is erased. There is no one-click restore on this page, so recovering the record needs a Super Admin / backend step — which is exactly why the button asks you to confirm first.

Where do I set someone's salary or mark their attendance?

The employee's Salary structure is reachable from the detail view (needs Payroll rights). Day-to-day attendance, leave, salary advances and payroll runs each have their own screens under Admin → Human Resources, all keyed to the same employee code.

DOODLYAdmin · HR · Attendance
§Admin · HR

Attendance

Mark, correct and review the daily attendance of every DOODLY employee — one status per person per day, with instant present/absent counts, bulk marking, and a per-employee monthly calendar that feeds payroll.

ℹ️
Where this sits

Attendance is one screen in the Human Resources group (Admin → Human Resources → Attendance). It reads the Employee Master (only active staff appear), and what you record here flows straight into the monthly Payroll (payable days and overtime) and is written back automatically when a Leave request is approved. Every record is one row keyed to a single employee and a single date, so re-marking the same day simply overwrites the previous status — it never creates duplicates.

Daily Attendance Register

The main screen — every active employee for one date, with their status and one-click marking.

Purpose See at a glance who is Present, Absent, on Leave or still Unmarked for a chosen day, and mark or correct each person inline.
Access Super Admin Admin full · Accountant view-only (cannot mark) Path Admin → Human Resources → Attendance
Header — Attendance · Daily register, corrections, bulk marking and monthly calendar
Filter barDate picker (defaults to today) · Department dropdown (All + 10 departments) · Search employee box · "Bulk mark…" button (edit only) · "Export CSV" button
KPI stripFive live counts for the chosen date: Present (green) · Absent (red) · On leave (amber) · Off/Holiday · Unmarked
Register tableOne row per active employee: Code · Employee (name + designation) · Dept · Status (inline "— mark —" dropdown) · OT · Calendar link
Inline status dropdownPer row — pick a status to mark that person for the chosen date instantly (no separate save)
Figure — labelled layout of the Daily Attendance Register

Filter & toolbar fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
DateWhich day's register you are viewing and marking.Any calendar dateYesDefaults to today. Sent as date to GET /api/admin/attendance?view=register; the whole screen reloads on change.2026-07-12
DepartmentNarrow the register to one department.All departments · Delivery · Procurement · Production · Quality · Inventory · Customer Support · Finance · Marketing · Operations · AdministrationOpt.Sent as department; server filters the Employee Master by exact department.Delivery
Search employeeFind a person by code or name.Free textOpt.Debounced 350 ms, sent as q; matches employee code or name (case-insensitive, partial).DL-014 / Anita

Register table specification

AspectDetail (from the live table)
ColumnsCode (employee code, bold) · Employee (name with designation beneath) · Dept · Status (inline dropdown for editors, or a coloured badge / "—" for view-only roles) · OT (overtime, shown as "Xh OT" when non-zero) · a Calendar link.
Rows shownEvery active employee (status ACTIVE or ON_LEAVE, not deleted). Ordered by employee code ascending. Up to 500 employees per load.
FiltersDate · Department · free-text search (see field table above).
SortingFixed — employee code ascending. No column-header sorting.
SearchYes — the search box (matches code or name).
PaginationNone — the filtered list renders in full (capped at 500).
ExportsExport CSV — downloads doodly-attendance-<date>.csv with columns Code, Employee, Department, Status (or "Unmarked"), OT (min), for exactly the rows currently displayed.
Row actionsInline Status dropdown (mark/correct); Calendar link (opens that person's monthly calendar).

Toolbar buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Bulk mark…Opens a dialog to apply one status to every employee currently listed.POST /api/admin/attendance with action:"bulk".Super AdminAdmin
Export CSVDownloads the current register as a CSV file.Client-side download of the loaded rows — no server call.Super AdminAdminAccountant
Calendar (row link)Opens the selected employee's monthly attendance calendar.GET …?view=calendar&employeeId=&month=.Super AdminAdminAccountant
💡
Tip — the "Bulk mark…" button only appears for editors

Accountants (and any role granted only view on Attendance) see the register and can export it, but the inline status dropdown is replaced by a read-only badge and the "Bulk mark…" button is hidden. Marking and correcting require the edit permission, which Admin and Super Admin hold by default.

Mark / Correct One Day

The inline dropdown that records or overwrites a single person's status for the chosen date.

Purpose Set the exact status for one employee on one day. Re-marking the same day silently corrects the earlier entry and is logged as a correction.
Access Super Admin Admin (edit permission) Path Admin → HR → Attendance → Status dropdown on a row
Row — DL-014 · Anita · Delivery
Status dropdown"— mark —" then the 8 statuses: Present · Absent · Half Day · Paid Leave · Sick Leave · Weekly Off · Holiday · WFH
Instant saveChoosing a value posts immediately and shows "Marked ✓"; the register reloads with fresh counts
OT columnFilled automatically only when check-in/check-out times exist for that day
Figure — inline marking on a register row

The eight attendance statuses

StatusMeaningCalendar codeCounts toward
PresentFull working day worked.PPresent (green); payable
AbsentDid not report; unpaid.AAbsent (red); not payable
Half DayHalf a working day.½Present-like; counts as 0.5 payable day
Paid LeaveApproved paid leave.PLOn leave (amber); payable
Sick LeaveApproved sick leave.SLOn leave (amber); payable
Weekly OffScheduled weekly rest day.WOOff/Holiday
HolidayCompany / public holiday.HOff/Holiday
WFHWorked from home.WPresent (green); payable

Fields written by the mark endpoint

The register's inline dropdown sends only status (the employee and date come from the row and the top date filter). The mark endpoint accepts the fuller record below — used for corrections, imports and future GPS/biometric/QR sources.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
employeeIdWhose attendance this is.Employee idYesMust exist in the Employee Master, else "Employee not found." Taken from the row automatically.ckyz…014
dateThe day being marked.Date string (min 4 chars)YesNormalised to midnight; combined with employeeId as the unique key (upsert).2026-07-12
statusThe attendance status.One of the 8 statuses aboveYesServer rejects any value outside the enum.PRESENT
checkInTime work started (for worked-hours & OT).Time / timestampOpt.Used with checkOut to compute worked minutes.2026-07-12T07:00
checkOutTime work ended.Time / timestampOpt.Worked mins = checkOut − checkIn; overtime = worked − 8h, floored at 0. Invalid or checkOut ≤ checkIn ⇒ 0.2026-07-12T16:30
noteFree-text reason / remark.TextOpt.Trimmed; max 200 characters.Late — traffic
sourceHow the record was captured.MANUAL · GPS · BIOMETRIC · QROpt.Max 20 chars; defaults to MANUAL. GPS/BIOMETRIC/QR are reserved for future capture methods.MANUAL
Workflow — mark or correct one day
Pick dateFind employeeChoose statusServer upserts [employee + date]Worked/OT computedAudit: mark or correct"Marked ✓" · counts refresh
Figure — end-to-end marking flow
ℹ️
Marking twice = a correction, not a duplicate

Attendance is unique per employee per day. If a day already has a status and you choose a new one, the system overwrites it and records the change in the Audit Log as attendance.correct (showing the old and new status, e.g. "DL-014 2026-07-12 → ABSENT (was PRESENT)"). A first-time mark is logged as attendance.mark. Nothing is ever silently lost.

Bulk Mark Attendance

Apply one status to every employee currently listed for the chosen date.

Purpose Mark a whole department (or the whole company) in one action — ideal for a public holiday, a company-wide Weekly Off, or a full-attendance day.
Access Super Admin Admin (edit permission) Path Admin → HR → Attendance → Bulk mark…
Dialog — Bulk mark attendance
Scope line"Apply a status to all N listed employees for <date>." — N and the date come from the current filter
Status dropdownThe same 8 statuses (Present, Absent, Half Day, Paid/Sick Leave, Weekly Off, Holiday, WFH)
ButtonsCancel · "Apply to N"
Figure — bulk-mark dialog

Fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
DateThe day to mark for everyone.Inherited from the register's date filterYesNot editable in the dialog — set it on the register first.2026-08-15
EmployeesWho gets marked.All rows currently listedYesThe visible register (respecting your department / search filter). Server accepts 1–1000 ids per call.All Delivery staff
StatusThe status applied to everyone.One of the 8 statusesYesEnum-validated; each employee is upserted for that date.HOLIDAY

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Apply to NMarks the chosen status for all listed employees on the date, then reports how many were marked.POST …{action:"bulk"}; upserts each employee/date, skips any bad id, audits attendance.bulk, returns the count. Toast: "Marked N ✓".Super AdminAdmin
CancelCloses the dialog, changes nothing.——
⚠️
Bulk mark respects your current filter

"Apply to N" targets exactly the employees on screen. If you filtered to Delivery, only Delivery staff are marked. To mark the whole company, clear the department filter and the search box first so all active employees are listed. Bulk marking also overwrites any status already set for that day — use it before you fine-tune individuals, not after.

Monthly Calendar (per employee)

A month-at-a-glance grid of one person's daily statuses, with a summary.

Purpose Review or audit an individual's whole month before payroll — spot patterns, count leave, verify overtime.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant (view) Path Admin → HR → Attendance → Calendar (row link)
Dialog — Monthly calendar · Anita · DL-014 · 2026-07
HeaderEmployee name · code · the month (YYYY-MM, taken from the register's date)
Day gridOne cell per day of the month; marked days show the short code (P, A, ½, PL, SL, WO, H, W) and are colour-tinted by status
Summary KPIsPresent · Absent · Leave (paid + sick) · Off (weekly off + holiday) · OT (hours)
Figure — per-employee monthly calendar

Calendar summary — what each tile counts

TileCounts
PresentDays marked Present.
AbsentDays marked Absent.
LeavePaid Leave + Sick Leave days combined.
OffWeekly Off + Holiday days combined.
OT (h)Total overtime for the month, in hours (summed from each day's computed overtime).
ℹ️
The month follows the register date

The calendar opens on the month of whatever date is selected in the register (for example, choosing 12 July shows all of July). To view a different month, change the register's date filter first, then reopen the calendar. The calendar is read-only — to change a day, close it and mark that day on the register.

Who can do what

Attendance permissions, enforced on the server for every call.

Purpose Define exactly which roles can view versus mark attendance.
Access Super Admin configures roles Path Admin → System → Roles & Permissions
RoleView register & calendarMark / correctBulk markExport CSV
Super AdminYesYesYesYes
AdminYesYesYesYes
AccountantYesNo (view-only)NoYes
Manager / Operations / othersNo (no Attendance module access by default)NoNoNo
ℹ️
The rule under the hood

Reading uses the view action; marking, correcting and bulk marking all use the edit action. A role granted only view (like Accountant) is blocked from every write, both in the interface and at the API. These defaults can be changed by a Super Admin in Roles & Permissions; the server matrix — not the browser — is the real security boundary.

✅
Best practice — mark daily, reconcile before payroll

Mark attendance every day (or use Bulk mark for the common case, then correct the exceptions), because Payroll counts payable days straight from these records: Present, WFH, Paid Leave and Sick Leave count in full, Half Day counts as 0.5, and overtime carries through in hours. Before running month-end payroll, open each employee's Monthly Calendar and clear any Unmarked days — an unmarked day is neither present nor absent and can distort the payslip. Remember that approving a Leave request automatically writes the matching status onto those days, so you usually only need to mark days leave doesn't cover.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
An employee is missing from the register.They are not active (inactive/exited or soft-deleted), or a department/search filter excludes them.Clear the filters. If still missing, check the Employee Master — only ACTIVE and ON_LEAVE staff appear here.
The status dropdown is greyed out / shows only a badge.Your role has view-only access to Attendance (e.g. Accountant).Ask a Super Admin to grant edit on the Attendance module, or have an Admin mark it.
"Employee not found." when marking.The employee record was removed or its id changed after the page loaded.Reload the page so the register refreshes, then mark again.
OT column stays blank even though the person worked late.No check-in / check-out times are recorded — the register's inline mark only sets a status.OT is only computed when both check-in and check-out exist (via a correction or a future GPS/biometric source). A plain status mark shows no OT.
Bulk mark only marked part of the team.A department or search filter was active, so only those rows were listed.Clear the department dropdown and search box, confirm the "Apply to N" count matches your headcount, then bulk mark.
Calendar shows the wrong month.The calendar follows the register's date filter.Change the date at the top of the register to any day in the month you want, then reopen the calendar.

Frequently asked questions

Can I mark attendance for a past date?

Yes. Set the register's Date to any past day and mark as usual — the record is keyed to that date. Re-marking a day that already has a status is logged as a correction, so the change is fully traceable in the Audit Log.

What is the difference between "Present" and "WFH"?

Both count as a present, payable working day and both show green. WFH simply records that the person worked from home rather than on site; it is a separate status so reports can distinguish the two.

How does Half Day affect pay?

Half Day counts as 0.5 of a payable day in the payroll calculation, whereas Present, WFH, Paid Leave and Sick Leave each count as a full payable day and Absent counts as zero.

Do I need to mark attendance for approved leave?

No. When a Leave request is fully approved, the system automatically stamps the correct status (Paid Leave, Sick Leave, or Loss-of-Pay as Absent) on every day of the leave. You only mark days that leave does not cover.

Is there an attendance report across a date range?

Yes, at the data level: the attendance service can produce a per-employee summary (present, half-day, absent, paid/sick leave, weekly off, holiday, overtime and payable days) over any From–To range, and this is exactly what Payroll consumes for the month. In this screen you export the single-day register as CSV or review a full month per employee via the Calendar.

DOODLYAdmin · HR · Leave
§Admin · HR

Leave

Raise, review and approve staff leave in one queue — with per-employee yearly balances, a two-step (manager then HR) approval path, and automatic marking of the approved days on the attendance register.

ℹ️
What this screen is for

Leave Management is the single place where all staff leave lives. A request can start two ways: HR raises it on an employee's behalf from this screen (New request), or an employee raises it themselves from Account → My HR → Leave (Apply for leave). Either way the request lands in the same queue here. From here you approve or reject it. On final approval DOODLY does three things automatically: it records the approval, deducts the days from that employee's yearly balance for the leave type, and marks each leave day on the Admin → HR → Attendance register. Everything runs against the live database through /api/admin/leave — there is no demo data.

⚠️
Who can do what

Viewing the queue is open to Super Admin, Admin and Accountant (Accountant is view-only). Creating a request and approving / rejecting require the leave module's edit right, which by default only Super Admin and Admin hold. The server backs this up: GET needs leave:view and POST needs leave:edit, otherwise it answers 403 Forbidden. When an approver who is Admin, Super Admin or Accountant approves, the server treats it as the final HR approval; a plain manager (granted leave:edit by a custom role/override) only performs the first step, moving the request to Manager approved for HR to finish.

Leave Queue

The filterable list of every leave request, with inline approve / reject and CSV export.

Purpose See all leave requests in one place, filter and search them, act on the ones awaiting a decision, and export the list.
Access Super Admin full · Admin full · Accountant view-only Path Admin → Human Resources → Leave
Leave Management — Apply, approve and track staff leave and balances
Status filterDropdown: All status · Pending · Manager approved · Approved · Rejected · Cancelled
Search box"Search code / employee…" — matches leave code, employee code or name (350 ms debounce)
New requestGreen + button (only if you have edit rights) — opens the New leave request form
Export CSVDownloads the currently listed rows as doodly-leave.csv
Pending banner"N requests awaiting approval." shows above the table when any are pending
Requests tableCode · Employee · Type · Dates · Status · Approve/Reject actions
Figure — labelled layout of the Leave Queue screen

The list is fetched with GET /api/admin/leave?view=list, newest first. The status dropdown and search box each re-query the server, so the table always reflects the live queue. Below is the exact make-up of the table.

Table specification — Requests table

AspectDetail (from the live code)
ColumnsCode (e.g. LV-0001) · Employee (name, with employee code · department underneath) · Type (Casual / Sick / Earned / Maternity / Emergency / Loss of Pay) · Dates (start → end, with the day count underneath, e.g. "3 days") · Status (colour badge) · a final actions column.
FiltersStatus dropdown (All / Pending / Manager approved / Approved / Rejected / Cancelled). The backend also accepts type, from and to date filters, though this screen only surfaces the status filter and search.
SearchSingle box, case-insensitive, matches leave code, employee code, or employee name. Typing waits 350 ms before querying.
SortingFixed — most recently created request first (createdAt descending). No column sorting.
PaginationServer paginates (default 50 per page, hard cap 200). This screen requests the default page.
Row actionsApprove and Reject buttons appear only when the row is Pending or Manager approved and you hold edit rights. For decided rows the reason text (if any) shows instead.
ExportExport CSV writes doodly-leave.csv with columns: Leave, Employee, Emp Code, Department, Type, From, To, Days, Status.

Status badges

StatusColourMeaning
PendingAmberJust raised; awaiting the first approval.
Manager approvedBlueA manager cleared step 1; awaiting final HR approval.
ApprovedGreenFully approved — balance deducted and attendance marked.
RejectedRedDeclined; a rejection reason may be recorded.
CancelledRedWithdrawn (status exists in the system for cancelled requests).

Buttons on this screen

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
New requestOpens the New leave request form (Screen 2).None until you submit; then POST /api/admin/leave with action:"create".Super Admin+Admin
Export CSVDownloads the rows currently on screen as a spreadsheet.Client-side only — builds the file from data already loaded.Any viewer
ApproveAdvances the request one step (manager approve) or finalises it (HR approve). On final approval, balance and attendance update.POST /api/admin/leave with action:"approve".Super Admin+Admin
RejectPrompts for a reason, then declines the request and notifies the employee.POST /api/admin/leave with action:"reject" and the reason.Super Admin+Admin
💡
Tip — approve without hunting

Set the status filter to Pending (or Manager approved) to isolate exactly the requests that need a decision. The blue "N requests awaiting approval" banner at the top of the table counts Pending + Manager approved together, so it is your quick daily to-do figure.

New Leave Request

The form HR uses to raise leave on an employee's behalf, with a live balance preview.

Purpose Record a leave request for any employee — for example when someone phones in — and see their remaining balance before you submit.
Access Super Admin · Admin (needs leave:edit) Path Admin → HR → Leave → New request
Modal — New leave request
Employee *Dropdown of all staff (loads up to 500), shown as "empCode · Name"
Leave type * / BalanceType dropdown next to a read-only balance chip that updates live
Start date * / End date *Two date pickers
ReasonOptional free text, up to 500 characters
Cancel / Submit requestFooter buttons
Inline error lineRed text if validation or the server rejects the request
Figure — the New leave request form

Field table

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
EmployeeWhose leave this is.Any active employee (dropdown).YesMust be chosen; server checks the employee exists (else "Employee not found.").EMP-0007 · Ravi Kumar
Leave typeCategory of leave, which decides the balance bucket and how the day is marked on attendance.Casual · Sick · Earned · Maternity · Emergency · Loss of Pay.YesServer enum-validates the type. Defaults to Casual.Sick
BalanceRead-only helper showing days left for the chosen employee + type this year.Auto-filled, e.g. "9 of 12 days left".AutoFetched live via GET ?view=balances&employeeId=…; shows "—" if none.9 of 12 days left
Start dateFirst day of leave.Calendar date.YesRequired; must not be after the end date.2026-07-14
End dateLast day of leave (inclusive).Calendar date.YesRequired; server rejects "End date can't be before the start date." Day count = whole days inclusive (minimum 1).2026-07-16
ReasonWhy the leave is needed.Free text.Opt.Trimmed; maximum 500 characters.Family function
ℹ️
How the day count works

DOODLY counts calendar days, inclusive of both ends — a Monday-to-Wednesday request is 3 days. It does not currently skip weekends or holidays automatically, so for staff who work six days a week the count is straightforward; for others, check the range you enter. The count you see is what gets deducted from the balance and marked on attendance.

On Submit request the form calls POST /api/admin/leave with action:"create". The server assigns the next code (LV-0001, LV-0002, …), stores the request as Pending, and — if the employee has a reporting manager on file — sends that manager a notification, "Leave request to review". The request now appears at the top of the queue. Nothing is deducted from any balance yet; that happens only on final approval.

Approve / Reject a Request

The decision step — a one- or two-stage approval that updates balances and attendance.

Purpose Clear a request from the queue: approve it (advancing or finalising it) or reject it with a reason.
Access Super Admin · Admin approve as HR (final); a manager with leave:edit does step 1 only Path Admin → HR → Leave → (row) Approve / Reject
Workflow — leave request lifecycle
Raised (Pending) Manager approves → Manager approved HR approves → Approved Balance deducted Attendance auto-marked Employee notified
Figure — from request to approval; rejection at any open stage ends the flow with a notified employee

Two-step approval, explained. When the approver is Admin, Super Admin or Accountant, the server treats the approval as the final HR approval — one click finishes it. Only when a plain manager (someone granted leave:edit through a custom role or per-user override, who is not Admin/Super Admin/Accountant) approves does the request move to Manager approved and wait for HR to finish it. In a default DOODLY setup, where only Admin and Super Admin can act, an approval finalises immediately. The on-screen toast tells you which happened: "Leave approved ✓" for a final approval, or "Sent for HR approval" for a first step.

Actions

ActionPreconditionWhat the server doesPermission
Approve (final / HR)Status is Pending or Manager approved; approver is Admin / Super Admin / Accountant.Sets status Approved, stamps the HR approver and time, deducts the days from the employee's yearly balance for that type (creating the balance row on first use), and marks each leave day on Attendance. Then notifies the employee "Leave approved ✅" and writes an audit entry.Super Admin+Admin
Approve (manager, step 1)Status is Pending; approver is a manager with edit rights but not HR-level.Sets status Manager approved, stamps the manager and time. No balance or attendance change yet. Audit entry written.manager w/ leave:edit
RejectStatus is Pending or Manager approved.Prompts you for a reason, sets status Rejected, records the reason and rejecting HR, notifies the employee "Leave rejected" (with the reason), and writes an audit entry. No balance/attendance change.Super Admin+Admin

What "final approval" changes — the automatic effects

Leave typeDefault yearly allotmentMarked on attendance as
Casual12 daysPaid Leave
Sick12 daysSick Leave
Earned15 daysPaid Leave
Maternity180 daysPaid Leave
Emergency6 daysPaid Leave
Loss of Pay0 (no balance kept)Absent
⚠️
Loss of Pay is different

A Loss of Pay leave keeps no balance (there is nothing to deduct) and each day is marked as Absent on attendance — which is what feeds unpaid-day handling in Payroll. Every other type is marked as Paid or Sick Leave and reduces its balance. Choose the type carefully: it is the single field that decides whether the day is paid.

⛔
Caution — an approved or rejected request can't be re-decided here

Once a request is Approved or Rejected, the server refuses another decision on it ("This request is already decided.") and the Approve / Reject buttons disappear from that row. Approval also writes the attendance days immediately. If you approved by mistake, you must correct the attendance entries on the Admin → HR → Attendance register and adjust the balance separately — there is no one-click "un-approve".

Leave Balances

The per-employee, per-year, per-type balance the form previews and approvals draw down.

Purpose Know, for any employee, how many days of each leave type remain this year before you approve more.
Access Super Admin · Admin · Accountant (view) Path Admin → HR → Leave → New request → Balance chip · also Account → My HR → Leave for staff
Balance preview — inside the New request form / My HR cards
Casualremaining · used of allotted
Sickremaining · used of allotted
Earnedremaining · used of allotted
Maternityremaining · used of allotted
Emergencyremaining · used of allotted
Figure — the five balance buckets returned by the balances view (Loss of Pay is excluded)

Balances are read with GET /api/admin/leave?view=balances&employeeId=… (optionally with a year). The server returns, for the year, one row per type — allotted, used and remaining (never below zero). If an employee has no balance row yet, DOODLY shows the default allotment for that type (Casual 12, Sick 12, Earned 15, Maternity 180, Emergency 6). The row is actually created in the database the first time a leave of that type is finally approved. The balances view covers the five paid types only — Loss of Pay is deliberately excluded because it has no balance.

ℹ️
Where staff see their own balance

Employees see the same numbers on their Account → My HR → Leave tab, alongside their own request history and an Apply for leave button (type, from/to dates with a live day count, and an optional reason). Those self-service requests submit to /api/account/hr with action:"apply-leave" and appear in this admin queue as Pending, exactly like ones you raise here.

✅
Best Practice

Work the queue daily from the Pending filter, and always check the balance chip before approving — if someone is out of Casual balance, either approve knowingly or switch the type to Loss of Pay so the day is correctly unpaid. Give every rejection a clear reason: the employee receives it in their notification, and it is the only explanation stored. Because a final approval writes attendance and deducts balance in one irreversible step, confirm the dates and type are right before you click Approve.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
No New request button, and no Approve / Reject on rows.Your role has only leave:view (e.g. Accountant), not leave:edit.An Admin or Super Admin must act, or grant you leave:edit under Admin → System → Permissions.
Approving shows "Sent for HR approval" instead of finishing.You approved as a manager (edit rights but not HR-level), so it is only step 1.An Admin, Super Admin or Accountant then approves the now Manager approved request to finalise it.
"End date can't be before the start date."The end date is earlier than the start date.Fix the dates; the end date must be the same day or later.
Balance chip shows "—".No balance row exists yet and/or the balances call hasn't returned.Normal for a fresh employee — the default allotment applies; the row is created on first approval. Re-select the employee/type to refetch.
"This request is already decided."The request was already approved or rejected (possibly by someone else).Refresh the queue; correct any mistake via Attendance and the balance, not by re-deciding here.
Manager didn't get a review notification.The employee has no reporting manager set on their profile.Set a reporting manager on the Admin → HR → Employees record; HR still sees the request in this queue regardless.
"Couldn't load leave." banner.Backend unreachable or you lack leave:view.Check you are signed in with an HR-capable role and that the API is up; retry.

Frequently asked questions

Does approving leave automatically update attendance?

Yes. On final approval DOODLY marks every day of the range on the attendance register — as Sick Leave for Sick, Absent for Loss of Pay, and Paid Leave for all other types — with a note referencing the leave code. You do not mark those days by hand.

How are the number of days calculated?

It counts calendar days inclusive of both the start and end dates, with a minimum of one day. Weekends and holidays are not skipped automatically, so a request spanning a weekend counts those days too.

Can an employee raise their own leave?

Yes — from Account → My HR → Leave using Apply for leave. It lands in this same queue as a Pending request for you to approve or reject.

What happens to the balance when I reject or cancel a request?

Nothing is deducted. Balances only change on final approval. A rejected request never touches the balance or attendance; it just records the reason and notifies the employee.

Why can't I change an allotment on this screen?

This module uses fixed default allotments (Casual 12, Sick 12, Earned 15, Maternity 180, Emergency 6; Loss of Pay has none). A balance row can carry a different allotted value once created, but there is no allotment editor on the Leave screen itself.

DOODLYAdmin · HR · Salary Advances
§Admin · Human Resources

Salary Advances

Request, approve and recover staff salary advances — a small loan against future pay that DOODLY automatically claws back, installment by installment, from the employee's next payslips.

ℹ️
What a "salary advance" is at DOODLY

A salary advance is money paid to a team member ahead of payday — for an emergency, a festival, a medical bill. HR raises the advance, a decision-maker approves it, and payroll then recovers it in equal installments (up to 24 salary cycles) by deducting from each month's net pay until the full amount is repaid. This screen is the control room for that whole lifecycle: raise, approve/reject, and watch the outstanding balance shrink. It shares one backend with Salary Structure and Payroll — every rupee is stored in paise (₹1 = 100 paise) so nothing is ever lost to rounding.

Salary Advances register

Live KPIs plus a searchable, filterable list of every advance and its recovery status.

Purpose See every advance on record, how much is still outstanding, which requests are waiting for a decision, and act on any one — raise a new advance, approve or reject a pending request — from a single page.
Access Super Admin · Admin · Accountant (view). Raising, approving and rejecting need the same three roles (payroll → edit). Path Admin → Human Resources → Salary Advances (/admin/advances.html)
Header — Salary Advances · "Request, approve and recover staff salary advances."
ToolbarStatus dropdown · Search box (code / employee) · New advance button · Export CSV button.
KPI stripTwo live tiles: Pending (amber) and Outstanding (₹ still to be recovered).
Advances tableCode · Employee (name + code · department) · Amount · Recovery (installments × per-installment) · Remaining · Status badge · Actions.
Row actionsFor a Pending row only: Approve · Reject. Decided rows show their reason instead.
New advance modalEmployee · Amount (₹) · Recover over (installments) · Reason → Submit advance.
Figure — labelled layout of the Salary Advances screen

The KPI strip — two live tiles

Both tiles are computed by the backend (listAdvances()) directly from the advance table — never typed in. They update on every filter, search or action.

TileWhat it countsHow it is calculated (backend)Example
Pending (amber)Requests still waiting for an approve/reject decisionCount of SalaryAdvance rows with status PENDING (ignores the current filter — it is the true global backlog)3
OutstandingTotal rupees still to be recovered across all live advancesSum of remainingPaise for every APPROVED advance, shown in ₹ (rejected/completed advances contribute nothing)₹42,500

Filters & search controls

ControlPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Status dropdownNarrow the list to one advance stateAll status · Pending · Approved · Rejected · CompletedOpt.Sent as ?status= (PENDING / APPROVED / REJECTED / COMPLETED); reloads the table on changePending
Search boxFind an advance by its code or the employeeFree text — "Search code / employee…"Opt.Sent as ?q= after a 350 ms pause; backend matches the advance code, the employee's employeeCode, or the employee's name (all case-insensitive "contains")ADV-0007 / Ramesh
Export CSVDownload the rows currently loadedButtonOpt.Client-side export of the rows on screen (respects the active status + search)—

The advances table

AspectBehaviour (from the real renderer & backend)
ColumnsCode (e.g. ADV-0007) · Employee (name, with an employeeCode · department subline) · Amount (₹, from amountPaise) · Recovery (shown as installments × ₹per-installment, e.g. "6 × ₹2,500") · Remaining (₹ still owed) · Status (colour badge) · Actions.
SortingFixed server order: newest first (createdAt descending), so the latest requests sit at the top.
SearchThe toolbar search box (code / employee code / name), debounced 350 ms, re-queries the backend.
FiltersStatus dropdown (see above). Status and search combine.
PaginationNo page controls on screen — the backend returns up to 50 advances per load (server cap 200), newest first. Use status + search to narrow larger sets.
ExportsExport CSV → file doodly-advances.csv with columns Advance, Employee, Emp Code, Department, Amount, Installments, Recovered, Remaining, Status (amounts in ₹).
Row actionsOnly on a Pending row (and only for editors): Approve / Reject. A decided row shows its stored reason in grey instead of buttons.
Empty state"No advances." when the filter/search returns nothing.

Status badges

The Status column colour-codes each advance's stage so requests that need action stand out.

Backend statusBadgeColourMeaning
PENDINGPendingAmberRaised, waiting for an approve/reject decision. This is the only state with row buttons.
APPROVEDApprovedBlueGranted. Payroll now recovers it installment-by-installment; the "Remaining" balance shrinks each paid payslip.
REJECTEDRejectedRedDeclined. No money is paid and no recovery happens; the rejection reason is stored on the row.
