COMPLETEDCompletedGreenFully recovered — Remaining has reached ₹0. Set automatically by payroll on the last recovery.

New salary advance

Raise an advance for a team member — it starts as a Pending request.

Purpose Record that a staff member has asked for money ahead of payday, choose how many salary cycles to recover it over, and submit it for approval.
Access Super Admin+ Admin Accountant (payroll → edit) — the New advance button is hidden for view-only roles. Path Admin → Human Resources → Salary Advances → New advance
Modal — New salary advance
Employee *Dropdown of all staff (code · name), loaded live from the employee master.
Amount (₹) *Whole-rupee number, minimum ₹1.
Recover overInstallments — number 1 to 24, default 1.
ReasonOptional free text, up to 500 characters.
FooterCancel · Submit advance (disabled while saving).
Figure — the New salary advance form

Every field on the form

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
EmployeeWho the advance is forOne staff member from the dropdownYesOptions come from GET /api/admin/employees?pageSize=500 (each shown as employeeCode · name); the id is validated server-side — an unknown employee returns 404 "Employee not found."EMP-0012 · Ramesh Kumar
Amount (₹)How much money is advancedWhole rupees, min ₹1YesConverted to paise before sending. Client blocks ≤ 0 ("Employee and a valid amount are required."). Server (Zod) requires an integer amountPaise between 1 and 100,000,000 (₹1 – ₹10,00,000); zero or below is rejected "Amount must be greater than zero."15000
Recover over (installments)How many salary cycles to spread the recovery acrossNumber 1–24, default 1Opt.Clamped to 1…24 server-side. The per-installment amount is computed as ceil(amount ÷ installments) and stored on the advance; "1" means recover the whole amount from the next payslip.6
ReasonWhy the advance is being givenFree text, ≤ 500 charactersOpt.Trimmed; stored on the advance and shown to approvers. Max length 500 (Zod).Medical emergency — hospital bill
ℹ️
How the installment is worked out

You enter the total amount and the number of installments; DOODLY does the maths. Per-installment = ceil(amount ÷ installments), so a ₹15,000 advance over 6 cycles becomes 6 × ₹2,500. The final installment is automatically trimmed so the employee never repays more than they borrowed — recovery stops the moment Remaining hits ₹0. Recovery is always taken from salary (the recovery method defaults to SALARY; the form does not offer a lump-sum option).

⚠️
Submitting does not pay any money out

Submit advance only creates a Pending record and writes an audit entry (advance.create). No cash moves and no recovery begins until someone approves it. The advance is auto-numbered ADV-0001, ADV-0002… and its Remaining balance is set to the full amount.

Approve or reject a request

The decision that turns a Pending advance into a live loan — or declines it.

Purpose Decide each pending advance. Approving starts salary recovery and notifies the employee; rejecting records a reason and notifies the employee that the request was declined.
Access Super Admin+ Admin Accountant (payroll → edit). Path Admin → Human Resources → Salary Advances → row actions on a Pending advance

The two row buttons

They appear only on a Pending row, and only for editors. Each posts to POST /api/admin/salary with {action:"decide"}, updates the advance, notifies the employee, writes an audit record, then reloads the list.

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Approve (primary)Grants the advance. Recovery now begins on the employee's next paid payslip.{action:"decide", decision:"approve"} → status APPROVED, stamps approvedById + approvalDate, notifies the employee ("Salary advance approved ✅ — recovered over N cycle(s)"), audit advance.approve. Toast: "Advance approved ✓".Super Admin+ Admin Accountant
Reject (red)Declines the request. Prompts "Reason for rejection:" first — you may leave it blank, but Cancel on the prompt aborts.{action:"decide", decision:"reject", reason} → status REJECTED, stores rejectReason, stamps decider + date, notifies the employee ("Salary advance rejected" + your reason), audit advance.reject. Toast: "Advance rejected".Super Admin+ Admin Accountant
⚠️
A decision can only be made once

The server checks the advance is still Pending before acting. If it was already approved or rejected (for example by a colleague a moment earlier), the request is refused with "This advance is already decided." Reload the page to see the current state. There is no "undo" and no "edit" on a decided advance — approve or reject is final.

Workflow — advance lifecycle
HR raises advance → PENDINGApprover clicks Approve → APPROVED (employee notified)Payroll drafts a payslip → plans this cycle's installment as a deductionPayslip marked PAID → installment recovered, Remaining dropsRemaining reaches ₹0 → COMPLETED
Figure — from request to fully recovered (rejection ends the flow at PENDING → REJECTED)

How advances are recovered (via Payroll)

Advances are never collected here — Payroll deducts them automatically from each payslip.

Purpose Understand where the "Recovered" and "Remaining" figures come from, so you can explain to staff exactly how their repayment works.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant (reference) Path Admin → Human Resources → Payroll (recovery is applied there)

The Salary Advances screen never takes money back directly. Recovery is a side-effect of Payroll:

StageWhat happensWhere it is driven
Planned (draft payslip)When a payslip is generated, DOODLY looks up every Approved advance for that employee with a balance and plans this cycle's deduction — the sum of min(per-installment, remaining) across their advances. It appears on the payslip as "Advance recovery".Payroll generate → advanceRecoverPaise on the draft
Recovered (payslip marked Paid)Only when the payslip status becomes Paid is money actually taken back. DOODLY records an AdvanceRecovery ledger row, decreases Remaining and increases Recovered on the advance. Multiple advances are paid off oldest-first.Payroll "Mark paid" → recovery applied inside one transaction
CompletedWhen an advance's Remaining reaches ₹0 its status flips to Completed automatically — no further deductions are ever planned for it.Set during the same Mark-paid step
ℹ️
Recovery is idempotent and oldest-first

Recovery is applied once per payslip — re-marking or reloading never double-deducts, because DOODLY checks for existing recovery ledger rows before applying. When an employee has more than one live advance, the current cycle's recovery is applied to their oldest advance first (by creation date) until this cycle's planned amount is used up.

💡
Tip — to actually reduce a balance, pay the payslip

Approving an advance sets up the plan, but the Remaining figure on this screen only moves when the matching payslip is marked Paid in Admin → Human Resources → Payroll. If a balance "isn't going down", check whether that month's payslip has actually been paid.

What the employee sees (My HR → Advances)

A read-only view of their own advances — they cannot raise one themselves.

Purpose Let a team member track their own advances and outstanding balance, so HR doesn't have to answer "how much do I still owe?" by hand.
Access Delivery Executive and any staff member (their own record only) via /api/account/hr Path Account → My HR → Advances

On the employee's My HR page the Overview card shows an Advance outstanding figure with the number of active advances, and the Advances tab lists their own advances read-only: Code · Amount · Recovery (installments × per-installment) · Remaining · Status. Employees have no request button — when they have none, the panel reads "No salary advances. Advances are requested through your manager or HR." All raising and approving stays with Super Admin, Admin and Accountant on the admin screen above.

✅
Best Practice

Clear the Pending queue daily — filter by status "Pending" and decide each request while the reason is fresh, because the employee is waiting and nothing recovers until you approve. Always type a reason when you reject: it is stored on the advance and sent to the employee, so a blank rejection just creates a follow-up question. Set the installments to something the employee's take-home can absorb — a large advance recovered in one cycle can wipe out a payslip; spreading it over several months keeps their salary livable while still clearing the balance. Reconcile the Outstanding KPI against your books at month-end; it is the single source of truth for money the company is still owed.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
No New advance button, or approve/reject buttons missingSigned-in role lacks payroll → edit (view-only)Use a Super Admin, Admin or Accountant account, or have a Super Admin grant payroll edit in Admin → System → Roles & Permissions.
"Couldn't load advances." warning where the table should beThe Next.js backend (/api/admin/salary) isn't reachableConfirm the backend is running and the API base is correct, then reload.
"Employee and a valid amount are required." on submitNo employee chosen, or amount is blank / zeroPick an employee and enter a whole-rupee amount of ₹1 or more.
"This advance is already decided."The advance was approved/rejected already (often by a colleague)Reload the page — the row is stale; a decided advance can't be re-decided.
Approved advance's Remaining isn't going downThe employee's payslips haven't been marked Paid yetRecovery only happens at Mark-paid in Payroll; generate and pay the payslip for that month.
Employee says they can't find where to request an advanceEmployees can't self-raise — it's an HR/admin actionRaise it for them from this screen; they'll then see it under My HR → Advances.

Frequently asked questions

Q. Who can raise and approve advances?

A. Super Admin, Admin and Accountant. Viewing needs the payroll permission at "view"; raising, approving and rejecting need it at "edit". The client hides the buttons for other roles, but the real check is on the server — a role without payroll access is refused with a 403.

Q. Does approving an advance immediately deduct money?

A. No. Approving only starts the recovery plan and notifies the employee. The actual deduction happens later, cycle by cycle, when each of the employee's payslips is marked Paid in Payroll.

Q. Can I edit or cancel an advance after it's approved?

A. There is no edit or cancel action. An advance moves PENDING → APPROVED → COMPLETED (as it is recovered) or PENDING → REJECTED, and a decision can't be reversed. If a mistake was made, raise a correcting entry and note it in the reason field.

Q. What happens if an employee has several advances at once?

A. Each payslip plans a combined "Advance recovery" and, at Mark-paid, pays them off oldest first until this cycle's planned amount is used up. Each advance tracks its own Recovered and Remaining independently and completes on its own.

Q. What's in the CSV export?

A. The rows currently loaded (respecting your status filter and search), with Advance code, Employee, Emp Code, Department, Amount, Installments, Recovered, Remaining and Status — amounts in rupees — saved as doodly-advances.csv.

Q. What is the maximum advance and how many installments are allowed?

A. Up to ₹10,00,000 per advance, recovered over 1 to 24 salary cycles. Entering "1" installment recovers the whole amount from the next payslip.

DOODLYAdmin · HR · Payroll
§Admin · HR

Payroll

Run the monthly salary for the whole team in one click: DOODLY reads each person's attendance and salary structure, works out earnings, deductions and net pay, produces a printable salary slip per employee, recovers any salary advance from the pay, and prepares a bank-transfer report — moving each payslip through Draft → Finalized → Paid.

ℹ️
What this screen is (and where it lives)

The Payroll item under Admin → Human Resources is served by the storefront admin page /admin/payroll.html, whose manifest block { type:"hrPayroll" } mounts a single empty #hrPayrollMount container. The DOODLY_HR module's mountPayroll() renderer (in assets/js/employees.js) fills it and talks to one backend endpoint, /api/admin/payroll (server code in next-app/lib/payroll/service.ts). Every rupee shown on this page is stored by the backend as an integer number of paise (₹1 = 100 paise) and only divided by 100 for display, so the money is always exact — there is no floating-point rounding of your salaries.

ℹ️
Who can open it, and who can run payroll

The endpoint is gated per action: reading the list, a slip, or the bank report needs requirePermission(req, "payroll", "view"); generating, finalizing or marking a payslip paid needs requirePermission(req, "payroll", "edit"). In the default role matrix that means Super Admin (all access) and Admin (full) can both view and run payroll, and the Accountant role also has payroll: full — so an Accountant can generate, finalize and pay too. No other staff role (Operations, Procurement, Inventory, Quality, Marketing, Support, Delivery Executive) has any Payroll rights, and Customers never do. If a user only has view rights, the front-end hides the Generate payroll button and the per-row Finalize / Mark paid buttons — but the server-side permission check is the real boundary.

⚠️
Payroll depends on two other HR screens

A payslip can only be produced for an employee who has (1) an active salary structure — set on the Employee detail / Salary structure, defining Basic, HRA, Conveyance, Special allowance, other earnings, Professional Tax and other deductions — and (2) their attendance marked for that month in HR → Attendance. Employees with no active salary structure are silently skipped when you generate. Advance recovery pulls from approved requests in HR → Salary Advances. So the correct month-end order is: mark attendance → confirm salary structures → generate payroll → review → finalize → pay.

Payroll Register

The month's payslips in one table, with four KPI totals, filters, and the Generate / Bank report / Export controls.

Purpose Give the person running payroll one screen to generate the month's payslips, see the totals (how many slips, gross, deductions, net payable), review each person's pay, and push each slip through Draft → Finalized → Paid.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — view to read; edit to generate / finalize / pay Path Admin → Human Resources → Payroll
Human Resources — Payroll · filter bar + 4 KPIs + payslip table
Filter barMonth picker · Status dropdown (All / Draft / Finalized / Paid) · Search employee · Generate payroll · Bank report · Export CSV
KPI cardsPayslips (count) · Gross · Deductions (amber) · Net payable (green)
Payslip tableSlip · Employee (name + code · dept) · Gross · Deductions · Net · Status · row actions
Row actionsSlip (view) · Finalize (drafts) · Mark paid (finalized) — action buttons depend on status and edit rights
Figure — the Payroll Register: a filter/action bar above four money KPIs, above the month's payslip table.

The filter and action bar

Across the top sits a row of controls. The Month field is a native month picker that defaults to the current month; changing it reloads the table for that month. The Status dropdown filters to a single stage (All status / Draft / Finalized / Paid). The Search employee box filters by employee name, employee code or payslip code and is debounced — it waits about a third of a second after you stop typing before searching. Then come the three action buttons described in the button table below.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Month Which salary month to view and act on. Month picker (YYYY-MM) Yes Defaults to the current month. Sent as ?month=; the backend validates the YYYY-MM shape and rejects a malformed value with "Invalid month (YYYY-MM)." 2026-07
Status Narrow the list to one payroll stage. All status · Draft · Finalized · Paid Opt. Sent as ?status=DRAFT|FINALIZED|PAID; blank means all. Filters the Payslip.status column server-side. Finalized
Search employee Find one person's payslip quickly. Free text Opt. Debounced ~350 ms, sent as ?q=. Case-insensitive match on payslip code, employee code, or employee name. Ramesh / EMP-0007 / PS-2026-07

The four KPI totals

Directly under the filter bar are four cards summarising whatever is currently in view (they reflect the active month, status and search filters). They come from the backend's stats aggregate, computed across every matching payslip — not just the rows on screen.

KPIMeaningBackend
PayslipsHow many payslips match the current filters.stats.count — total row count for the query.
GrossSum of all earnings before deductions.Aggregate SUM(grossPaise), shown in ₹.
Deductions (amber)Total taken off — advance recovery + PT + other deductions.Aggregate SUM(deductionsPaise), shown in ₹.
Net payable (green)What the business will actually pay out.Aggregate SUM(netPaise), shown in ₹.

The payslip table

The main table lists one row per payslip. The Employee cell shows the name on the first line and the employee code and department on a second, greyed line. Amounts are in ₹. The Status cell is a coloured badge, and the last cell holds the action buttons that apply to that row's status.

AspectBehaviour on this screen
ColumnsSlip (payslip code, e.g. PS-2026-07-0003) · Employee (name + code · department) · Gross · Deductions · Net (bold) · Status (badge) · actions.
FiltersMonth, Status and Search employee (the filter bar). No column-header filters.
SortingFixed by the backend — newest month first, then payslip code ascending. Not user-sortable by clicking headers.
SearchThe single "Search employee" box (code / employee code / name).
PaginationThe screen loads the first page only (server default page size 100, hard cap 500); the admin page does not render pager buttons, so use Search or Status to narrow very large months.
ExportExport CSV button — downloads the rows currently loaded as doodly-payroll-<month>.csv (see button table).
Row actionsSlip (always), Finalize (only on Draft rows, edit rights), Mark paid (only on Finalized rows, edit rights).
Empty state"No payslips for <Month Year> — click "Generate payroll"." shown when the month has no slips yet.
Loading / errorA shimmering skeleton panel while loading; an amber warning box ("Couldn't load payroll." or the server message) on failure.

Buttons on this screen

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Generate payroll Creates or recalculates draft payslips for every active employee for the chosen month. Asks for confirmation first ("Generate payroll for all active employees for <Month>? Existing drafts are recalculated."). On success it toasts how many were generated and how many were skipped. POST { action:"generate", month } → generatePayroll(). See Screen 2. Writes an audit entry payroll.generate. Super AdminAdminAccountant — edit
Bank report Opens the Bank transfer report pop-up for the current month (Screen 4). GET ?view=bank&month= → bankReport(). Read-only. view (all three roles)
Export CSV Downloads the loaded payslip rows as a spreadsheet. Client-side — writes doodly-payroll-<month>.csv with columns Slip, Employee, Emp Code, Department, Gross, Deductions, Net, Status. Amounts are in rupees (paise ÷ 100). view
Slip (row) Opens the salary slip pop-up for that employee (Screen 3), with a Print / PDF option. GET ?view=detail&id= → payslipDetail(). Read-only. view
Finalize (row · Draft only) Locks a draft payslip so it can no longer be recalculated by a re-generate. Toasts "Finalized ✓". POST { action:"finalize", id } → setPayslipStatus(id, "FINALIZED"). Only a Draft can be finalized (else 409 conflict). Audit payroll.finalize. Super AdminAdminAccountant — edit
Mark paid (row · Finalized only) Records the salary as paid. Asks to confirm ("Mark this salary as paid? This records the advance recovery and notifies the employee."). On success: "Marked paid ✓ — employee notified". POST { action:"pay", id } → setPayslipStatus(id, "PAID"). Applies advance recovery, stamps paidAt, notifies the employee, audit payroll.paid. See Screen 5. Super AdminAdminAccountant — edit
⚠️
Export CSV covers only the loaded rows

The Export CSV button exports the payslips that are currently loaded into the table (the first page — up to 100 rows — after your Month / Status / Search filters). It is a client-side download of what is on screen, not a fresh server-side pull of the entire company. For a full month with more than 100 payslips, narrow by department search or status before exporting, or export in slices.

Generate Payroll

The one-click monthly run that builds a draft payslip per employee from attendance + salary structure.

Purpose Turn a month of attendance and each person's salary structure into draft payslips, automatically — proration for absences, overtime, planned advance recovery and fixed deductions all worked out for you.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — edit Path Admin → Human Resources → Payroll → Generate payroll

Clicking Generate payroll from the register (after a confirm dialog) sends action:"generate" with the selected month. The backend loops every eligible employee, computes a payslip, and saves it as a Draft. It is idempotent: running it again recalculates existing drafts in place but never touches a payslip that is already Finalized or Paid, and never creates duplicates.

ℹ️
Who gets a payslip when you generate

Generate-all builds slips for every employee whose profile is not removed (deletedAt = null) and whose status is ACTIVE or ON_LEAVE. Anyone without an active salary structure is skipped (counted in the "skipped" toast). Anyone whose payslip for that month is already Finalized or Paid is left untouched. The toast reads, for example, "Generated 34 payslip(s), 2 skipped (no salary structure)".

The request fields

From the admin button, only month is sent (all active employees, no ad-hoc bonus). The underlying API also accepts three optional fields, used when payroll is run for a single employee (for example from tooling or a future per-person action).

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
month The salary month to run. YYYY-MM Yes Must match ^\d{4}-\d{2}$. Rejected as "Invalid month (YYYY-MM)." otherwise. 2026-07
employeeId Run for one employee instead of all. Employee id Opt. When present, only that employee is processed; when absent, all eligible employees run. Bonus/incentive below apply only in the single-employee case. ckv…e21
bonusPaise A one-off bonus added to earnings. Whole paise ≥ 0 Opt. Integer ≥ 0. Added to gross for that person. Ignored on a generate-all run (bonus is forced to 0). 500000 (₹5,000)
incentivePaise A one-off incentive added to earnings. Whole paise ≥ 0 Opt. Integer ≥ 0. Added to gross for that person. Ignored on a generate-all run. 250000 (₹2,500)

How each payslip is calculated

For every employee the engine pulls the month's attendance and the active salary structure, then works top-to-bottom. This is the exact logic that fills the salary slip; understanding it makes the numbers self-explanatory.

LineHow it is worked out
Working daysThe number of calendar days in the month (e.g. 31 for July).
Loss-of-pay (unpaid) daysAbsent days + (Half-days × 0.5). Paid leave, sick leave, weekly-off and holiday do not reduce pay.
Proration factorPaid days ÷ Working days, where Paid days = Working days − LOP days. Every fixed earning below is multiplied by this factor.
Basic, HRA, Conveyance, Special, Other earningsEach taken from the salary structure and prorated by the factor above.
Overtime pay(Overtime minutes ÷ 60) × hourly Basic × 1.5, where hourly Basic = monthly Basic ÷ (Working days × 8). Overtime minutes come from the attendance records.
Bonus / IncentiveAdded if supplied (single-employee run only); otherwise ₹0.
GrossSum of all the earnings above.
Advance recoveryFor each of the employee's approved advances still owing, the smaller of its installment amount and its remaining balance is planned for recovery this cycle. (Actually deducted from the advance ledger only when the slip is marked Paid.)
Professional Tax + Other deductionsFixed amounts from the salary structure.
Total deductionsAdvance recovery + Professional Tax + Other deductions.
Net paymax(0, Gross − Total deductions) — never negative.
💡
Tip — re-generate safely, as many times as you like

If you fix an attendance mark or update a salary structure after generating, just click Generate payroll again for the same month. Every Draft is recomputed from the corrected data, so the drafts always reflect the latest records. Only once you Finalize a slip is it frozen against re-generation — that is the point of finalizing.

Workflow — one generate run
Pick monthClick Generate payrollConfirmLoop active employeesRead attendance + salary structureCompute gross → deductions → netSave as Draft (or recompute existing draft)Toast: N generated, M skipped
Figure — generate builds or refreshes a draft payslip per eligible employee in a single pass.

Salary Slip

The per-employee payslip pop-up — earnings, deductions, net pay, and a Print / PDF button.

Purpose Show the full breakdown of one employee's pay for a month, exactly as it will be paid, in a clean layout that can be printed or saved as PDF and handed to the employee.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — view Path Admin → Human Resources → Payroll → (row) Slip
Pop-up — Salary slip · header, two earnings/deductions columns, net, print
HeaderEmployee name · code · designation — department · status badge · month
Attendance linePayable days X of Y · overtime hours
Earnings columnBasic, HRA, Conveyance, Special, Overtime, Bonus, Incentive, Other → Gross
Deductions columnAdvance recovery, Professional tax, Other → Total
Net pay stripNet pay = Gross − Deductions
Footer buttonsClose · Print / PDF
Figure — the salary slip pop-up: header and attendance line above side-by-side Earnings and Deductions, with the net-pay strip and the Print / PDF action.

What the slip shows

The header names the employee (name, code, designation — department), the status badge and the month. A grey line beneath states Payable days X of Y and the overtime hours. Then two columns list every non-zero line — a line is only printed if it has a value, so a slip with no HRA simply omits the HRA row. If there are no deductions at all, a single "— ₹0" row is shown.

SectionLines (each shown only when non-zero)Source field
EarningsBasic · HRA · Conveyance · Special allowance · Overtime · Bonus · Incentive · Other → Gross (total)basicPaise, hraPaise, conveyancePaise, specialPaise, overtimePaise, bonusPaise, incentivePaise, otherEarnPaise → grossPaise
DeductionsAdvance recovery · Professional tax · Other deductions → TotaladvanceRecoverPaise, ptPaise, otherDeductPaise → deductionsPaise
Net payThe single figure the employee receives.netPaise
Employee details (detail API)Code, name, designation, department, date of joining, bank name, IFSC, and a masked account number (only the last 4 digits, e.g. "•••• 4821").payslipDetail() employee block
ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Print / PDFOpens a clean, branded "DOODLY — Salary Slip" print window and triggers the browser print dialog (choose "Save as PDF" to keep a copy). Earnings and deductions are laid out side by side, ending in a Net Pay box and the note "Computer-generated payslip — no signature required."Client-side window.open + print(). Needs pop-ups allowed (otherwise it toasts "Allow pop-ups to print the slip").view
CloseDismisses the pop-up. No data change.—view
ℹ️
Employees see their own slips too

The same payslips appear to each staff member on their self-service My HR → Salary slips tab (account surface), where they can view and print any slip once payroll is finalized. Employees never see anyone else's pay — that surface is scoped to the signed-in person only.

Bank Transfer Report

A pop-up listing net payable per employee with bank details, for handing to the bank / uploading for payment.

Purpose Produce the single list a payments team needs to actually move the money: each employee's bank, account number, IFSC and net amount, plus the grand total, for the chosen month.
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — view Path Admin → Human Resources → Payroll → Bank report
Pop-up — Bank transfer report — <Month Year>
Note"Finalized + paid payslips only."
TableCode · Name · Bank · Account · IFSC · Net
FooterTotal (sum of all Net amounts)
Figure — the bank transfer report: one row per payable employee with full bank details, totalled at the foot.

What it contains

The report opens for the month currently selected on the register. It includes only payslips whose status is Finalized or Paid — drafts are excluded, because you should not pay out an amount you have not finalized. Rows are ordered by payslip code.

ColumnMeaning
CodeThe employee code.
NameThe employee's name.
BankBank name from the employee's profile ("—" if not on file).
AccountThe full bank account number ("—" if not on file).
IFSCThe branch IFSC code ("—" if not on file).
NetThe net amount to transfer, in ₹.
Total (footer)Sum of every Net amount in the report.
⛔
Caution — this report shows full account numbers

Unlike the salary slip (which masks the account to its last four digits), the Bank transfer report deliberately shows the complete account number because it is meant to drive a real bank transfer. Treat this pop-up as sensitive financial data: do not screen-share it, do not paste it into chat or email, and only open it when you are ready to process payments. Access is already limited to Super Admin, Admin and Accountant.

Finalize & Pay (payslip lifecycle)

Moving each payslip through Draft → Finalized → Paid, and what happens when you pay.

Purpose Control when a payslip stops being editable (Finalize) and when the money is actually released, advances are recovered, and the employee is notified (Mark paid).
Access Super Admin Admin Accountant — edit Path Admin → Human Resources → Payroll → (row) Finalize / Mark paid

The three statuses

StatusBadgeWhat it meansWhat you can do
DraftamberFreshly generated. Editable — a re-generate recalculates it.Review the slip; Finalize it.
FinalizedblueLocked figures. No longer recalculated by generate; appears on the Bank report.Mark paid.
PaidgreenSalary released. paidAt is stamped, advances recovered, employee notified.View / print the slip only — terminal state.

What "Mark paid" actually does

Marking a Finalized payslip as Paid runs inside a single database transaction so the money and the advance ledger always stay in step:

StepEffect
Recover advancesIf this slip has planned advance recovery and hasn't been recovered before, the engine walks the employee's approved advances oldest-first and, for each, deducts the smaller of its installment, its remaining balance, and what's still to recover. Each deduction creates an AdvanceRecovery record, reduces the advance's remaining balance, and marks the advance Completed when it hits zero. Recovery is applied once per slip (idempotent).
Stamp paidThe payslip status becomes PAID and paidAt is set to now.
Notify the employeeSends a "Salary credited 💰" notification with the month, the net amount and the payslip code (non-blocking — payment still succeeds if the message fails).
AuditWrites a payroll.paid audit entry (employee code, slip code and net amount). Finalize writes payroll.finalize; generate writes payroll.generate.
⚠️
Finalize before you pay — and check the order of operations

The register only offers Mark paid on a Finalized slip, so you must Finalize first. Finalizing is what freezes the figures against a later re-generate, so if you spot a mistake on a Draft, fix the source (attendance / salary structure / advance) and re-generate before finalizing. Marking Paid a second time is harmless (it returns without re-recovering advances), but there is no "un-pay" button — treat Mark paid as the point of no return.

⛔
Caution — paying recovers advances for real

Advance recovery is only planned at generate time; it is actually applied to the salary-advance ledger the moment you Mark paid. That reduces the employee's outstanding advance balance and can complete the advance. There is no reversal in the UI — if you paid in error, you must correct the advance and pay records manually. Confirm the net amount and the "Advance recovery" line on the slip before clicking Mark paid.

Workflow — payslip lifecycle
Generate → DraftReview slipFinalize → Finalized(appears on Bank report)Mark paidRecover advances + stamp paidAtNotify employeePaid
Figure — the full path a payslip travels, from a recalculable draft to a paid, notified salary.
✅
Best Practice — the month-end payroll checklist

Run payroll in this order every month: (1) close and correct Attendance for the month so proration and overtime are right; (2) confirm every active employee has an up-to-date salary structure and that pending advances are approved; (3) click Generate payroll and read the "N generated, M skipped" toast — investigate any "skipped (no salary structure)"; (4) open a few Slips and sanity-check gross, deductions and net; (5) re-generate if you had to fix anything; (6) Finalize the slips; (7) open the Bank report, transfer the money through your bank, then (8) Mark paid so advances are recovered and staff are notified. Keep the CSV export as your internal record of each run.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
An employee is missing from the generated listThey have no active salary structure, or their status isn't ACTIVE/ON_LEAVE, or the profile is removed — so they were skipped.Set a salary structure on the Employee detail, confirm their status, then re-generate. Watch the "skipped" count in the toast.
Net pay looks too low for someoneLoss-of-pay days (absent / half-day) prorated their earnings, or an advance recovery / PT / other deduction applied.Open their Slip — check the "Payable days X of Y" line and the Deductions column. Fix attendance and re-generate the (still Draft) slip if needed.
"Generate payroll" button isn't visibleThe signed-in role has payroll: view but not edit.Only Super Admin, Admin and Accountant have edit rights. Ask one of them to run payroll, or have the role adjusted.
Can't Finalize — "Only a draft payslip can be finalized."The slip is already Finalized or Paid.No action needed; it's past the draft stage. Use Mark paid (if Finalized) or just view it (if Paid).
I re-generated but a slip didn't changeThat slip is already Finalized or Paid, which generate never overwrites.Only Drafts are recalculated. There is no un-finalize; correct downstream if the figures are wrong.
Employee didn't get the "Salary credited" messageNotification delivery failed — it is non-blocking, so payment still went through.The payment is fine (status is Paid). Check the notifications/provider setup; the slip is available in the employee's My HR regardless.
Bank report is empty for the monthNo payslips are Finalized or Paid yet — the report excludes Drafts.Finalize the drafts first, then reopen the Bank report.
Print / PDF does nothingThe browser blocked the pop-up window.Allow pop-ups for the admin site, then click Print / PDF again (it toasts "Allow pop-ups to print the slip").
CSV is missing some payslipsExport writes only the loaded rows (first page, after filters).Narrow by Status / Search or export in slices for a large month.

Frequently asked questions

If I run "Generate payroll" twice, do employees get paid twice?

No. Generate is idempotent — it recalculates each existing Draft in place and never creates duplicates, and it never touches a Finalized or Paid slip. Money only moves when you Mark paid, and marking an already-Paid slip does nothing.

How are absences and overtime reflected in pay?

Fixed earnings (Basic, HRA, etc.) are prorated by "paid days ÷ working days", where unpaid days are absences plus half of any half-days (paid/sick leave, weekly-off and holidays are not unpaid). Overtime is paid separately at 1.5× the hourly Basic rate for the recorded overtime minutes.

When exactly is a salary advance recovered?

The recovery amount is planned (shown as "Advance recovery" on the slip) when payroll is generated, but it is only applied to the advance ledger when you Mark the slip Paid — reducing the outstanding balance and completing the advance if it reaches zero. It is applied once per slip.

Can an Accountant run payroll, or only Admins?

The Accountant role has payroll: full in the default matrix, so an Accountant can generate, finalize and mark paid — the same as Admin and Super Admin. No other staff role can open Payroll at all.

What is the payslip code format?

Each slip is coded PS-<month>-<sequence>, e.g. PS-2026-07-0004 — the month plus a four-digit running number within that month.

Are the ₹ amounts stored as rupees or paise?

The screen shows rupees, but the database stores every amount as integer paise (₹1 = 100 paise), which is why the totals are always exact. The CSV export writes rupees (paise ÷ 100).

Module chapter

Delivery Executive

6 screen groups documented from the live application.

DOODLYDelivery Executive · My Route
§Delivery Executive

Delivery Executive — My Route

The mobile-first field app a delivery executive opens each morning to see today's assigned stops, navigate to every door, mark each delivery through its stages, collect empty bottles, and report any problem — with the customer and the ops team kept in sync the whole time.

ℹ️
What this module is

"My Route" is a separate, self-contained logistics app that lives under its own URL space (/delivery/*) with its own login. It is not part of the office Admin console — it is the phone screen the executive uses on the road. A Super Admin should understand it end-to-end because everything the executive taps here writes straight into the same Delivery, BottleLedger, DeliveryIssue and audit records the office team manages under Admin → Operations → Delivery Mgmt. This section documents exactly what the executive sees and does so you can train drivers and diagnose field issues accurately.

⚠️
Strict per-executive scoping

An executive only ever sees their own route. The backend (GET /api/delivery/my-route) filters deliveries by the driver record linked to the signed-in user, and every stop update is re-checked — updating a stop that is not on your route returns 403 "That stop isn't on your route." The role must be delivery_executive (a super_admin may open it for testing). On the live site, anyone who is not a signed-in executive is redirected to /delivery/login.html.

My Route (the daily home screen)

Today's summary, the interactive route map, and one card per delivery stop.

Purpose Give the on-the-road executive a single screen that shows how many stops are left, the route on a map, and every stop's address, contact, payment, workflow stage and bottle count — with one-tap navigate, call and status buttons.
Access Delivery Executive (own route only) · Super Admin (testing) Path Delivery Executive → Today → My Route
Header — Good morning, {FirstName} 👋 · route name/code · date · N stops · availability pill · Live badge
Hero greetingFirst name from the driver record, the route's name/code, the date (with "(latest assigned day)" when it fell back), and the total stop count
Availability pillTap toggle — "🟢 Available for assignment" or "⚪ Not available — start shift" — plus a green "Live" / "On shift" badge
KPI strip (6 tiles)Assigned · Completed · Pending · Remaining stops · Bottles to collect · Distance left (km)
Today's route cardInteractive route map with numbered stops, plus Start route / Open navigation / Refresh route buttons
Stop listOne card per delivery — name, plan, payment, address, instructions, workflow steps, bottles, comms and actions
Empty state"No deliveries assigned for today. New stops will appear here once your route is assigned."
Figure 1 — Labelled layout of the My Route home screen (hero, KPI strip, route map, stop cards).

The hero header

At the top the app greets the executive by first name — "Good morning, {FirstName} 👋" — taken from the signed-in driver's own name. The sub-line reads the route name or code, then the date, then the stop count, for example "Central Morning · RT-07 · 2026-07-12 · 6 stops". When today has no assignments the app automatically shows the most recent day that does and appends "(latest assigned day)" so the screen is never blank for an active executive. If no route is assigned at all the sub-line reads "No route assigned yet".

The availability (shift) pill

On the right of the hero, when the executive is a real signed-in driver, a tappable pill shows the current shift state, read from GET /api/driver/availability. Tapping it flips the shift via POST /api/driver/availability.

Pill stateMeaningTap doesBackend
🟢 Available for assignment (green)On shift — the assignment engine may hand you new stopsEnds the shiftSets ExecutiveStatus.availability = OFFLINE
⚪ Not available — start shift (grey)Off shift — you will not receive auto-assignmentsStarts the shiftSets ExecutiveStatus.availability = AVAILABLE
⚠️
You cannot go off-shift mid-trip

If the executive is in the middle of a trip (backend availability is ASSIGNED, ACCEPTED or OUT_FOR_DELIVERY), the shift toggle is blocked with 409 "You have an active trip — complete your deliveries (or mark returned to dairy) before changing availability." Only executives whose status is AVAILABLE are eligible for new auto-assignments, so an executive who forgets to start their shift will simply receive no work. Every shift change is written to the AssignmentLog as a STATUS_CHANGE entry for the audit trail.

ℹ️
The pill only appears when the live status loads

If the availability call fails or the account has no executive profile, the pill is simply hidden — only the plain "Live" / "On shift" badge shows. No pill does not mean "off shift"; it means the shift status could not be read.

The KPI strip — six live tiles

Directly under the hero sit six count tiles, recomputed on every render from the current stops and their saved state.

KPI tileWhat it countsHow it is calculated
AssignedTotal stops on today's routeCount of all stops
CompletedStops already marked deliveredStops whose status = delivered
PendingStops not yet deliveredTotal − Completed
Remaining stopsSame running count of work leftEquals Pending
Bottles to collectEmpty bottles still owed across all stopsSum of (expected − collected), floored at 0 per stop
Distance left kmRoute distance for the stops not yet deliveredRoute-map distance over undelivered stops, to 1 decimal

The "Today's route" card

Below the KPIs is a map card titled "Today's route" (pin icon). It renders an interactive route map (via the shared DOODLY_MAPS engine) with the stops laid out as numbered markers and a route line; tapping a marker scrolls the matching stop card into view. Three buttons sit under the map:

ButtonWhat it doesBackend / effectPermission
Start route (primary)Marks every stop still on "Assigned" as "On the way" in one tap and begins live GPS reportingFlips those stops to ON_THE_WAY (see stop actions); starts a location ping every ~30 s; the first ON_THE_WAY flip notifies the customer their milk is en routeDelivery Executive
Open navigation (ghost)Opens real Google Maps driving directions to the next undelivered stopExternal link — google.com/maps/dir with travelmode=driving to the next stop's coordinates; opens in a new tabDelivery Executive
Refresh route (ghost)Re-renders the screen from the current data and shows a "Route refreshed" toastLocal re-render (does not re-fetch the server route)Delivery Executive

Live GPS reporting

Once the route is started (or if the page is reloaded while a trip is already in progress), the app quietly reports the executive's position to POST /api/delivery/location immediately and then every 30 seconds. This updates Driver.lat / lng / lastSeenAt, which is what powers the moving driver dot on both the admin route view and the customer's live tracking map. Reporting stops automatically once every stop on the route is delivered. If the phone denies location permission, the app silently skips reporting — nothing else breaks.

ℹ️
Why the customer sees the truck move

The executive does not "share location" as a separate action. Simply starting the route turns on GPS reporting, and the coordinates flow to the customer's tracking screen and the admin map. Ask executives to keep location permission granted so customers can watch their milk approach.

The stop card — every field explained

Each delivery on the route renders as its own card. Cards are ordered by the route sequence. The fields come straight from the delivery, its subscription/order, and the customer's saved address, so the executive can find the exact door without phoning ahead.

Card elementWhat it showsSource field / mappingExample
Sequence badgeThe stop's position on the routeseq (delivery sequence)3
NameThe person to meet at the doorAddress contactName → else the account holder's namePriya Sharma
Plan · quantityWhat is being delivered and how many bottlesplan name (plus the item label) · qty30-Day Morning Ritual · 2 bottles
Payment badgeWhether cash is due at the door"Paid" (green) or "COD ₹{amount}" (amber) when the linked order is PENDING and paid by CASHCOD ₹70
Address (main line)The building-level address, boldedHouse no. + building name + floor → else the joined address line12-4, Sai Residency, 2nd floor
Address (locality line)Street, area and pincode under the main linestreet, area, pincodeMG Road, Benz Circle, 520010
LandmarkAn orange highlighted "find me" cueAddress landmark (shown only when present)Landmark: opposite the temple
Extra locatorsFiner door detailsblock, wing, gateNumber, doorColor joined with dotsBlock C · East Wing · Gate 2 · Blue door
Account lineThe account holder, when different from the door contactcustomerName (only if it differs from the contact name)Account: Rahul Sharma
InstructionsThe customer's delivery note, with an alert iconAddress deliveryNote (defaults to "Deliver before 7 AM" when blank in live mode)Leave with security
Workflow stepsThe four-stage progress bar, lit up to the current stageAssigned → On the way → Reached → Delivered(On the way lit)
Bottles lineEmpty-bottle tally with − / + steppersexpected bottlesExpected · collected bottlesCollected · pending (expected − collected)expected 2 · collected 1 · pending 1
ℹ️
How the backend maps delivery status to the four stages

The card's progress bar is driven by the delivery's real status, simplified to four executive-facing stages: SCHEDULED / ASSIGNED / ACCEPTED / PACKED → Assigned; OUT_FOR_DELIVERY / ON_THE_WAY → On the way; REACHED → Reached; DELIVERED / SKIPPED → Delivered; a FAILED delivery falls back to Assigned so it can be re-attempted.

The communication row

Each card carries three one-tap contact buttons that use the door contact's phone number (the address contact phone, or the account holder's phone):

ButtonWhat it doesOpens
Call (phone icon)Places a phone call to the customertel: the stop's mobile number
WhatsApp (chat icon)Opens a WhatsApp chat with the customerwa.me/ the number (digits only), new tab
SMS (message icon)Opens the phone's text-message composersms: the stop's mobile number

The action row

The bottom of each card holds the workflow controls. The primary button is context-aware: it always shows the next stage's name, and once the stop reaches "Reached" it becomes "Mark delivered", which opens the confirmation screen (Screen 2).

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Navigate (ghost)Reveals a row of travel-mode links for this stopToggles the hidden nav-modes row (see below)Delivery Executive
On the way (primary, when Assigned)Advances the stop to "On the way"POST /api/delivery/stop/{id} action=status, status=onway → sets ON_THE_WAY; first flip fires notifyOutForDelivery to the customerDelivery Executive
Reached (primary, when On the way)Advances the stop to "Reached"POST /api/delivery/stop/{id} action=status, status=reached → sets REACHEDDelivery Executive
Mark delivered (primary, when Reached)Opens the Confirm delivery screen (proof, bottles, notes)See Screen 2Delivery Executive
Delivered (static chip, when done)Read-only "Delivered ✓" once completeNo action — the card is locked as done—
Report issue (ghost)Opens the issue-reporting screenSee Screen 3Delivery Executive
− / + (bottle steppers)Adjust empty bottles collected at this stopClamped between 0 and the expected count; saved locally until the delivery is confirmedDelivery Executive

The navigation modes row

Tapping Navigate reveals four travel-mode links, each opening real Google Maps directions to that stop's coordinates with the correct travel mode:

Mode linkGoogle Maps travel mode
Walkingwalking
Bikebicycling
Scootertwo-wheeler
Cardriving
💡
Tip — "Scooter" uses two-wheeler routing

The Scooter link opens Google Maps in two-wheeler mode, which prefers two-wheeler-friendly roads and gives more realistic ETAs than car routing. Encourage executives to pick the mode that matches their actual vehicle for accurate timing.

✅
Best practice — start the shift, then start the route

Train executives to do two taps at the depot: first tap the availability pill to go 🟢 Available (so the assignment engine can still hand them last-minute stops), then tap Start route once loaded. Starting the route both moves every stop to "On the way" and turns on the live GPS trail that customers rely on. Skipping "Start route" leaves customers with no tracking dot.

Confirm delivery (proof of delivery)

The pop-up that captures a photo, empty bottles and notes, then closes the stop.

Purpose Turn a "Reached" stop into a completed delivery — optionally attach a proof photo, record how many empty bottles were collected, add a note, and write it all to the delivery record and the customer's bottle ledger.
Access Delivery Executive (own stop only) Path My Route → (stop card) → Mark delivered
Modal — Confirm delivery · customer name & address · close ✕
Proof of deliveryCamera / photo picker — "Upload proof of delivery (optional)"; shows a thumbnail once attached
Empty bottles collected− / + stepper with the current count "of {expected}"
Delivery notesOptional free-text note about the drop
Mark deliveredPrimary button that finalises the stop and closes the modal
Figure 2 — The Confirm delivery pop-up (proof photo, bottle count, notes, confirm).
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Proof of delivery photoVisual proof the milk was droppedAn image (opens the rear camera on a phone)Opt.Previewed locally; the photo is a field aid — the completion API records status, bottles and notesdoorstep.jpg
Empty bottles collectedHow many empties were taken backWhole number, 0 up to the stop's expected countYesClamped 0…expected; sent as bottles (0–50) to the deliver action2
Delivery notesAny note about the dropFree textOpt.Trimmed, max 400 chars; saved to Delivery.customerRemarkLeft with security guard

What "Mark delivered" does

Confirming sends POST /api/delivery/stop/{id} with action = deliver, the bottle count and any note. In a single database transaction the backend:

StepEffect
1. Close the stopSets Delivery.status = DELIVERED, stamps deliveredAt, records bottlesIn and customerRemark
2. Update the bottle ledgerIf bottles > 0, writes a BottleLedger RETURNED entry against the customer ("Collected by delivery executive")
3. AuditWrites a delivery.completed audit entry (with the bottle count) for the enterprise audit trail
4. Notify the customerFires notifyDelivered so the customer gets their delivery confirmation (with the bottle count)
5. Late-delivery checkThe portal runs the late-delivery monitor against the 7:00 AM promise; if late, it auto-sends an apology and shows the executive "⚠ Late by {N} min — apology sent"
ℹ️
Delivering twice is safe

The completion call is idempotent — if a stop is already DELIVERED, re-confirming simply returns the delivered status without creating a second bottle-ledger entry or a duplicate notification. A double tap on a flaky connection will not double-count bottles.

✅
Best practice — count bottles before you tap deliver

The bottle count entered here is what lands in the customer's bottle ledger and drives any deposit refund. Ask executives to physically count the empties, set the stepper, then confirm — correcting a bottle count after the stop is closed needs an office-side adjustment.

Report an issue

The pop-up for flagging a problem at a stop to the ops team.

Purpose Let the executive raise a structured problem report against a stop — pick a type and priority, optionally add a photo and comments — that becomes a tracked DeliveryIssue for the office to action.
Access Delivery Executive (own stop only) Path My Route → (stop card) → Report issue
Modal — Report an issue · customer name · stop number · close ✕
Issue typeSix selectable chips; the first is pre-selected
PriorityLow / Medium / High — Medium pre-selected
PhotoOptional camera / photo attachment
CommentsOptional free-text detail
Submit reportSends the issue to ops and closes the modal
Figure 3 — The Report an issue pop-up (type chips, priority, photo, comments).
FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Issue typeWhat went wrongCustomer unavailable · Wrong address · Damaged bottle · Payment issue · Product issue · Delivery failedYesMapped to the DeliveryIssue.type enum (e.g. CUSTOMER_UNAVAILABLE); unknown text falls back to DELIVERY_FAILEDCustomer unavailable
PriorityHow urgent it isLow · Medium · HighYesStored as DeliveryIssue.priority (LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH); defaults to MEDIUMHigh
PhotoVisual evidenceAn image (rear camera on a phone)Opt.Attached locally as a field aiddamaged.jpg
CommentsExtra detail for the ops teamFree textOpt.Trimmed, max 500 chars; saved to DeliveryIssue.commentsBottle cracked in transit

What "Submit report" does

Submitting sends POST /api/delivery/stop/{id} with action = issue. The backend creates a DeliveryIssue row tied to this delivery and this driver, with the mapped type, priority and comments, then writes a delivery.issue audit entry. The executive sees an "Issue reported to ops" toast. Reporting an issue does not mark the stop delivered — the stop stays open so it can be re-attempted or resolved.

Workflow — one stop, start to finish
AssignedOn the way (customer notified)ReachedMark delivered → proof + bottles + notesDelivered (ledger + audit + customer notified)
Figure 4 — The per-stop delivery workflow; "Report issue" can branch off at any point without closing the stop.

Reference — data, persistence & the empty state

How the route is fetched, how progress is saved, and what a driver with no work sees.

Purpose Give support and admin staff the technical facts behind the field app so they can answer "why does the driver see X?".
Access Super Admin Admin (reference) Path Delivery Executive → Today → My Route

Where the route comes from

On load, the portal calls GET /api/delivery/my-route, which returns the driver header (name, employee ID, vehicle number, rating), the route (name and code), the date, an isFallbackDate flag, and the array of stops. It defaults to today; if today has no assignments it automatically returns the most recent day that does (hence the "(latest assigned day)" note in the hero). Each stop carries its contact, structured address, plan, quantity, expected/collected bottles, payment and the mapped status.

How progress is saved

Stop status and bottle counts are held in the phone's local storage (doodly-del-state) so the app is responsive even on a weak signal. In live mode the server is the source of truth — on every load the local state is re-seeded from the server's stop statuses and bottle counts, and each meaningful tap (status change, deliver, issue) is pushed to the backend. If a push fails, the app keeps the local note and shows a "Couldn't sync — will keep your local note" toast rather than losing the work.

The empty state

An executive with no assignments for the day (and none on any prior day) sees the hero sub-line "No route assigned yet" and, in the list area, the message "No deliveries assigned for today. New stops will appear here once your route is assigned." This is the correct, honest state — the field app never shows sample or demo stops on the live site.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Opening the portal bounces to the login pageNot signed in as an executive (or the session token expired)Sign in at /delivery/login.html with the executive's Employee ID/mobile
"No delivery-executive profile is linked to this account"The user has no Driver recordIn Admin → Drivers, create/link a Driver record for that user
Stop update returns "That stop isn't on your route" (403)The delivery is assigned to a different driverReassign the delivery to this executive under Delivery Mgmt / Assignment
The availability pill won't turn off ("active trip", 409)The executive is mid-trip (ASSIGNED / ACCEPTED / OUT_FOR_DELIVERY)Finish the deliveries or mark returned to dairy, then toggle the shift
The availability pill is missing entirelyThe availability status could not be readConfirm the executive has a Driver + ExecutiveStatus record and network access; refresh
The customer's live tracking dot never movesRoute not started, or phone location permission deniedTap "Start route" and grant location permission; reporting pings every ~30 s
The hero says "(latest assigned day)"No deliveries assigned for today, so the app fell back to the last active dayAssign today's route; the note disappears once today has stops

Frequently asked questions

Can an executive see another driver's stops?

No. Both the route fetch and every stop update are scoped to the driver record linked to the signed-in user. A stop that is not on their route is rejected with a 403.

Does "Start route" notify customers?

Yes, indirectly. "Start route" moves every "Assigned" stop to "On the way", and the first move to on-the-way fires the "out for delivery" notification to that customer. It also begins the live GPS trail.

Where do the empty bottles collected go?

Into the customer's BottleLedger as a RETURNED entry (only when the count is above zero), written in the same transaction that marks the delivery complete. That ledger is what any bottle-deposit refund reads from.

Is the proof-of-delivery photo stored on the server?

In the current build the photo is a field-side confirmation aid shown as a thumbnail; the completion that is written to the backend records the delivered status, the bottle count and the note. Treat the photo as a courtesy capture, not a stored evidence file.

DOODLYDelivery Executive · Deliveries & Delivery Detail
§Delivery Executive

Deliveries & Delivery Detail

The delivery executive's working list of today's stops and the per-stop screen where each delivery is marked, bottles are counted, proof is captured and problems are reported — the last mile of every DOODLY order.

ℹ️
Where this lives in the product

DOODLY ships two views of the same work. The Deliveries list and Delivery Detail screens documented here are the executive-facing pages (route keys driver/deliveries and driver/delivery). The functional field app the executive actually taps through on their phone is the Delivery Executive Portal (DOODLY_DELIVERY), which renders the very same stops as interactive cards and writes every action to the live backend. Both are described below so a new employee understands the screen they see and the record it creates.

Deliveries — today's stops

The ordered list of every stop on the executive's assigned route, with live status.

Purpose Give the executive one scannable list of who to deliver to, in what sequence, with the item, the payment type and whether each stop is still Pending or already Delivered.
Access Super Admin Delivery Executive — an executive only ever sees their own route Path Driver → Today → Deliveries
Header — Deliveries · "Mark each stop as you go."
ToolbarSearch box, Filters button, Export ▾ (CSV / Excel / PDF)
Quick filtersAll · Pending · Delivered chips
Stops tableColumns: # · Customer · Address · Item · Payment · Status
Status badgeDelivered (green) · Next stop (amber) · Pending (grey)
PaginationPage size 25, removable filter chips, saved view persists
Figure 1 — labelled layout of the Deliveries list screen

The list is powered by the shared DOODLY DataTable engine, so it is never a static table: it supports instant search, multi-column sort, auto-detected facet filters, a date range, saved views, favorites, CSV / Excel / PDF export and pagination. The active view (search text, filters, sort, page size) is remembered per user, so it survives a reload or navigating away and back.

Table 1 — Column reference (dataset driverStops)

ColumnWhat it showsSource / notes
#Stop sequence number on the routesequence on the Delivery — the order the executive should drive the stops
CustomerThe person to meet at the doorAddress contact name if set, else the account holder's name
AddressDelivery address lineHouse / building / area / pincode assembled from the saved Address
ItemProduct and pack size for this stope.g. "1000 ml", "500 ml" — the subscription / order line item
PaymentHow the stop is paidPrepaid / Paid or COD ₹<amount> when it is a cash order
StatusWhere the stop is in the flowDelivered · Next stop · Pending (colour-coded badge)

Table 2 — List controls

ControlWhat it doesDetail
SearchFilters stops as you typeMatches customer, address, item, payment; placeholder "Search today's stops…"
Filters — All / Pending / DeliveredQuick status filterConfigured filter set for this screen; "All" clears the status filter
Facet filtersAuto-built from the dataPayment and Status become one-click facets when 2–14 distinct values exist
SortOrder by any columnClick a header; multi-column sort supported
Export ▾Download the current viewCSV (.csv), Excel (.xls) or PDF; respects the export permission where enforced
PaginationPages long routes25 rows per page by default; removable filter chips above the table
Row → open stopOpens the Delivery Detail screenTapping a stop drills into its detail / stop card (Screen 2)
💡
Tip

Use the Pending filter the moment you start driving — it hides everything you've already delivered so the top row is always your next stop.

Delivery Detail — confirm a stop

The single-stop screen: confirm the delivery and record the empties collected.

Purpose Let the executive finish one stop — verify the order and payment, confirm the milk was handed over, count returned bottles, add a remark and attach proof.
Access Super Admin Delivery Executive — only for a stop on this executive's own route Path Driver → Deliveries → (open a stop)
Header — Stop 3 · Priya N. · "Flat 304, My Home Avatar, Narsingi"
Panel · Confirm deliveryItem (500 ml × 1), Payment (₹70 COD), delivery-OTP entry, "Confirm delivered" button
Panel · Bottles & remarksEmpties to collect, "Collected empty bottle" checkbox, Remarks box, "Capture proof photo" button
Figure 2 — labelled layout of the Delivery Detail screen (two panels)

The detail screen puts the two decisions the executive must make side by side: did the milk get delivered (left panel) and what came back / anything to note (right panel).

Table 3 — Delivery Detail fields

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Item (read-only)Confirms what to hand overPack size × quantityReadFrom the subscription / order line item500 ml × 1
Payment (read-only)Whether to collect cashPaid / COD ₹amountReadCOD shown only for cash orders still pending₹70 COD
Delivery OTPVerifies handover to the right person4-digit codeOpt.Design element on this screen; the live app finalizes via the confirm sheet (see below)4 2 9 1
Collected empty bottleMarks that empties were picked upCheckboxOpt.Feeds the bottle count written to the ledger on delivery☑
RemarksFree note about the dropShort textOpt.Saved as customerRemark; max 400 characters via the API"left with guard"

Table 4 — Delivery Detail buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Confirm deliveredMarks this stop DeliveredPOST /api/delivery/stop/<id> action:"deliver" → sets DELIVERED + deliveredAt, writes bottlesIn, posts the customer's bottle ledger and sends the "delivered" notificationDelivery Executive+
Capture proof photoAttaches a proof-of-delivery imageOpens the camera (capture); the photo rides along with the confirm-delivery stepDelivery Executive+
ℹ️
Note — how the live app confirms a delivery

In the production executive app, tapping Mark delivered opens a confirm sheet with three inputs: an optional proof-of-delivery photo (camera), an empty-bottles collected counter (0 … expected), and an optional delivery-notes box. Submitting posts action:"deliver" to the stop API. This is the real path that writes DELIVERED, so proof and the empties count are captured there.

Stop card — the live executive app

The interactive card the executive works through on the road, backed by the live route API.

Purpose The functional field screen: contact the customer, navigate, progress the status through the four stages, count bottles and report an issue — every tap syncing to the DOODLY backend.
Access Super Admin Delivery Executive — hard-scoped to the signed-in executive's route Path Delivery → Today → My Route
Header — Good morning, {name} · route · date · stop count · Availability pill · Live badge
Summary KPIsAssigned · Completed · Pending · Remaining stops · Bottles to collect · Distance left (km)
Route map + buttonsStart route · Open navigation · Refresh route
Stop card — headerSequence, name, plan + bottle count, Paid / COD badge
Stop card — addressHouse/building/floor, street/area/pincode, landmark, block/wing/gate/door colour, delivery instruction
Stage stepperAssigned → On the way → Reached → Delivered
Bottles rowExpected · collected · pending, with − / + counter
Contact rowCall · WhatsApp · SMS
Action rowNavigate (Walking/Bike/Scooter/Car) · Next stage / Mark delivered · Report issue
Figure 3 — labelled layout of one interactive stop card in the live portal

The card fetches the executive's real route from GET /api/delivery/my-route. That route is scoped hard on the server: the role must be delivery executive (or a super admin testing), and only deliveries assigned to their driver record are returned. It defaults to today and, if today has no assignments, falls back to the most recent day that does, so the app always shows real work.

Table 5 — Stop card actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Start routeFlips every "Assigned" stop to "On the way" and starts live GPS reportingPer stop: action:"status" status:"onway"; begins pinging /api/delivery/location every ~30sDelivery Executive+
Open navigationOpens turn-by-turn to the next undelivered stopBuilds a Google Maps navigation URL from the stop's lat/lngDelivery Executive+
Refresh routeRe-renders the list from current stateNo write; re-reads local + live stateDelivery Executive+
Call / WhatsApp / SMSContacts the door contactNative tel: / wa.me / sms: links — no server callDelivery Executive+
Navigate (per stop)Reveals Walking / Bike / Scooter / Car map linksGoogle Maps URL per travel mode from the stop's coordinatesDelivery Executive+
− / + bottlesCounts empties picked upClamped 0 … expected; saved locally, written on deliveryDelivery Executive+
On the way → ReachedAdvances the delivery stageaction:"status"; first "On the way" flip triggers the customer "out for delivery" notificationDelivery Executive+
Mark deliveredCompletes the stop via the confirm sheetaction:"deliver" → DELIVERED, deliveredAt, bottlesIn, remark, bottle ledger, "delivered" notification, auditDelivery Executive+
Report issueRaises a delivery problem to opsaction:"issue" → creates a DeliveryIssue with type + priority + comments, auditedDelivery Executive+
Workflow — one stop, start to finish
AssignedStart route → On the wayReachedConfirm sheet: photo · empties · notesMark deliveredLedger + notify + audit
Figure 4 — the four-stage delivery workflow and what completing it writes

Table 6 — Stop-update request (POST /api/delivery/stop/<id>)

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
actionWhich update this isstatus · deliver · issueYesRejected if not one of the threedeliver
statusAdvance the stage (action=status)onway · reachedCond.Maps to ON_THE_WAY / REACHEDonway
bottlesEmpties collected (action=deliver)Integer 0–50Opt.>0 writes a RETURNED bottle-ledger entry for the customer2
notesDelivery remark (action=deliver)Text ≤ 400 charsOpt.Saved as customerRemarkLeft with guard
issueTypeKind of problem (action=issue)Customer unavailable · Wrong address · Damaged bottle · Payment issue · Product issue · Delivery failedCond.Mapped to the DeliveryIssue enum; unknown → DELIVERY_FAILEDWrong address
priorityIssue urgency (action=issue)Low · Medium · HighOpt.Defaults to MediumHigh
commentsIssue detail (action=issue)Text ≤ 500 charsOpt.Stored on the DeliveryIssueGate locked, no answer
ℹ️
Live location & availability

While a route is running, the app reports GPS to POST /api/delivery/location every ~30 seconds (updating the driver's last-seen position for the admin route view and the customer's tracking map) and stops once every stop is Delivered. The availability pill toggles the executive's shift via /api/driver/availability — Available ↔ Offline. Only Available executives receive new auto-assignments, and an executive mid-trip cannot go offline until they finish or return to the dairy.

✅
Best Practice

Progress the stage honestly in real time — tap On the way when you leave for the stop and Reached when you arrive. The first "On the way" flip is what tells the customer their milk is coming, and the timestamps feed late-delivery monitoring against the 7:00 AM promise. Skipping straight to "Delivered" hides the trip from the people tracking it.

⚠️
Warning — count the empties before you confirm

The bottle count you enter on the confirm sheet is written to the customer's bottle ledger as RETURNED and can affect their refundable deposit. Get it right before tapping Mark delivered — a delivered stop is completed and re-submitting does not change the recorded count.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"No deliveries assigned for today"No route has been assigned to this executive yet, or today has no stopsWait for ops to assign a route; the app auto-falls back to the most recent assigned day if today is empty
Stops load but belong to someone else / a 403 on an actionThe stop isn't on this executive's routeOnly your own route is returned; open your own list — a stop off your route is rejected server-side
Cannot end the shift / availability won't turn offAn active trip is in progress (Assigned / Accepted / Out for delivery)Finish the deliveries or mark returned to dairy, then toggle Availability
"Delivered" did not change the customer's bottle balanceThe empties counter was left at 0, or the stop was already DeliveredSet the count on the confirm sheet before submitting; a stop can only post its ledger the first time it is delivered
Customer isn't getting the tracking / "on the way" alertThe stage was jumped straight to Delivered, so the first "On the way" flip never firedAdvance stages in order; the out-for-delivery notification sends on the first move to On the way
Navigation opens the wrong or no locationThe address has no saved coordinatesUse the on-screen address / call the customer; ask ops to geocode the address for future runs

Frequently asked questions

Can I see yesterday's or another executive's deliveries here?

You only ever see your own route. Today is the default; if today has nothing assigned, the app shows your most recent assigned day (marked as such) so the screen is never empty. Completed past days live under the separate History screen.

What happens to the empty bottles I collect?

The count you enter when confirming a delivery is written to that customer's bottle ledger as a RETURNED entry, which keeps their bottle balance and refundable deposit accurate. Bottles handed over are recorded as ISSUED.

Do I have to enter the delivery OTP to complete a stop?

The OTP box is a verification aid on the detail screen. In the live app, a stop is completed through the confirm sheet (optional proof photo, empties count, notes), which is what actually posts the delivery to the backend.

What does "Report issue" do?

It raises a delivery problem to operations — you pick a type (customer unavailable, wrong address, damaged bottle, payment issue, product issue, delivery failed), a priority and optional comments. It creates an auditable DeliveryIssue record so ops can follow up; the stop stays open.

⛔
Caution — Delivered is a committed record

Marking a stop Delivered stamps the time, writes the bottle ledger, notifies the customer and audits the action. It cannot be quietly undone from the field app — if you tapped it by mistake, tell ops so a supervisor can correct it in the admin Delivery Management screen.

DOODLYDelivery Executive · Route & Maps
§Delivery Executive

Route & Maps

The delivery executive's road-map for the day — the ordered list of stops to visit, a map of the route, and one-tap turn-by-turn navigation to each customer's exact doorstep.

ℹ️
How this feature is built — two layers

"Route & Maps" exists in two places that share the same purpose. First, the Today's Route page inside the driver portal (/driver/route.html) is a lightweight sheet that lists today's stops and shows a map area. Second — and this is what an executive actually drives with in the field — the live executive portal at /delivery/ mounts the real map engine (DOODLY_MAPS) and the full route workflow (DOODLY_DELIVERY): an interactive route map, distance and ETA, and real Google Maps navigation links. This section documents both, and is honest about which parts are a placeholder and which are live.

⚠️
Not the admin Routes screen

Do not confuse this with Routes under Admin → Operations, where a manager plans and optimises route templates. This section is the executive-facing view: it only ever shows the signed-in executive their own assigned stops for the day.

Today's Route

The driver-portal page that lists the day's stops in delivery order with a map area.

Purpose Give the executive a single screen showing every stop they must deliver today, in the exact order to drive them, with a map at the top and a status for each stop.
Access Delivery Executive Super Admin — the live route data is scoped to the signed-in executive's own Driver record; a manager viewing the portal only sees demo rows. Path Delivery Executive → Today → Today's Route  ·  /driver/route.html
Header — Today's Route · sub "RT-JH-01 · 42 stops · tap a stop to navigate" · button Open in Maps
Route map areaFull-width card with a 🗺️ map. On the demo sheet this reads "Route map placeholder — turn-by-turn in production"; in the live portal it becomes the real interactive route map
Today's stops tableOne row per stop, in delivery order: # · Customer · Address · Item · Payment · Status
No toolbarThis table is rendered with the toolbar switched off — no search box, filters, date range or export
No pagerPaging is switched off — every stop for the day is shown at once so the executive can scroll the whole run
Figure — Today's Route: a page header with the Open in Maps action, a route-map card, then the day's stops as a single scrollable table.

The page is assembled from three blocks: a page header (title, sub-title and one action button), a map card, and the driverStops table. The header's sub-line shows the route code and the number of stops (for example "RT-JH-01 · 42 stops").

Button — page header

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Open in Maps primary Meant to open the whole route in a maps app. On the driver-portal sheet this button carries no link, so tapping it shows a small confirmation toast rather than opening a map. The real map-open and navigation happen from the live executive portal (Screen 2), where each stop produces a genuine Google Maps directions link. No network call from this sheet. In the live portal, navigation links are built by DOODLY_MAPS.navUrl(lat, lng, mode), which returns a https://www.google.com/maps/dir/?api=1&destination=… URL. Delivery Executive Super Admin
ℹ️
Note — placeholder becomes a live map

The grey card with the 🗺️ emoji and the words "Route map placeholder — turn-by-turn in production" is exactly that: a placeholder on the lightweight driver sheet. The identical route, drawn as a real map with a numbered line through every stop, appears the moment the executive signs into the field portal at /delivery/ — see Screen 2.

Today's stops — table columns

ColumnWhat it showsExample
#The stop's sequence number — the order to deliver in, shown in bold3
CustomerThe customer's name for that stopPriya N.
AddressThe delivery address, in muted textFlat 304, My Home Avatar, Narsingi
ItemWhat to hand over at the door — the milk quantity/variant500 ml
PaymentWhether the stop is already paid or is cash-on-delivery, with the amount to collectPrepaid · ₹70 COD
StatusA coloured pill for the stop's progressDelivered · Next stop · Pending

The status pill is colour-coded so the executive can read the run at a glance:

PillColourMeaning
DeliveredGreenThis stop is complete
Next stopAmberThe stop the executive is heading to now
PendingGreyNot yet reached

Table behaviour

CapabilityOn this screen
Columns#, Customer, Address, Item, Payment, Status (six fixed columns)
SearchOff — the toolbar is disabled
FiltersOff on this page (the sibling Deliveries page adds All / Pending / Delivered filters)
SortingNone — rows stay in delivery-sequence order
PaginationOff — the full day is shown as one list
ExportNot offered on this screen
Row actionsNone on the row itself — the executive taps a stop / uses navigation in the live portal
💡
Tip — where the numbers come from

When a real executive is signed in, the list is the output of GET /api/delivery/my-route (Screen 2). If nobody is signed in — for example a manager browsing the portal to learn it — the sheet shows a small set of sample rows so the layout is never blank.

Live route map & navigation

The field portal — an interactive route map, distance/ETA and real turn-by-turn links.

Purpose Show the executive their whole run as a map, tell them how far and how long is left, and open real Google Maps navigation to the next doorstep in one tap.
Access Delivery Executive Super Admin — GET /api/delivery/my-route rejects any other role with "Executive portal only"; each executive sees only deliveries assigned to their own Driver record. Path Delivery Executive → My Route  ·  /delivery/
Header — Good morning, {name} 👋 · route name/code · date · N stops · shift toggle · Live badge
KPI stripAssigned · Completed · Pending · Remaining stops · Bottles to collect · Distance left (km)
Route mapInteractive map: current location, a line through every remaining stop, numbered pins (done stops dimmed)
Map meta bar"📍 N stops · {distance} km · ~{eta} min"
Route buttonsStart route · Open navigation · Refresh route
Stop cardsPer stop: address, instructions, status steps, bottle counter, Call/WhatsApp/SMS, Navigate
Figure — the live executive portal: a greeting hero, six live KPIs, the interactive route map with its distance/ETA bar, three route buttons, then a card per stop.

When a signed-in executive opens the portal, it first shows "Loading your route…", calls the backend, and then draws the map and the stop list from real data. The map is provided by DOODLY_MAPS.routeMap(), which threads a line from the executive's current position through every stop that is not yet delivered and drops a numbered pin at each one; delivered stops are shown dimmed.

Buttons — route card

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
