Start route primary Marks every still-"Assigned" stop as On the way, begins live GPS reporting, and shows "Route started — drive safe!" Advances each stop's status locally; on the live route it posts each change to POST /api/delivery/stop/:id. Begins polling POST /api/delivery/location every 30 seconds so customers and admins can track the executive on their own maps. Delivery Executive
Open navigation Opens Google Maps directions to the next undelivered stop, in a new tab. This is the real "Open in Maps". Link built by DOODLY_MAPS.navUrl(next.lat, next.lng, "driving") → google.com/maps/dir/?api=1&destination=lat,lng&travelmode=driving. Delivery Executive
Refresh route Re-renders the map and stop list from the current data and shows "Route refreshed". Re-runs the on-page render; no server write. Delivery Executive
Shift toggle (header pill) Starts or ends the executive's shift — "🟢 Available for assignment" ↔ "⚪ Not available — start shift". Controls whether the assignment engine gives them new stops. POST /api/driver/availability with { available: true|false }; the pill only appears if GET /api/driver/availability succeeds. Delivery Executive

Buttons — per stop (navigation)

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Navigate Reveals a small row of travel-mode links for that stop — Walking · Bike · Scooter · Car. Toggles the mode row; each mode is a pre-built navUrl link. Delivery Executive
Call · WhatsApp · SMS Contact the customer at the door from the same card — tel:, wa.me and sms: links to the stop's mobile number. Native device links; no DOODLY server call. Delivery Executive

Each travel mode maps to a Google Maps travel type, so the ETA and route shown by Google match how the executive is actually travelling:

Mode (button)Google travelmode
Walkingwalking
Bikebicycling
Scootertwo-wheeler
Car defaultdriving
Workflow — driving the day's route
Open portalRoute loads (my-route)Start route → all "On the way" + GPS onOpen navigation / tap Navigate on a stopPick mode (Walk/Bike/Scooter/Car)Google Maps guides to doorstepDeliver & mark stopRepeat to next pin
Figure — how an executive uses Route & Maps from sign-in to the last doorstep.
ℹ️
Note — distance & ETA are estimates

The distance on the meta bar is the straight-line (haversine) distance from the current position through the remaining stops. The ETA is a working estimate — roughly city speed of ~18 km/h plus about 3 minutes per stop. Actual turn-by-turn time comes from Google Maps once the executive taps a navigation link.

How the map is powered

Real Google Maps when a key is set; a built-in fallback map when it is not.

Purpose Make sure the route always draws — a proper Google map when the business has a Maps key, and a clean built-in map otherwise, with no broken tiles either way.
Access Super Admin configures the key; every executive benefits automatically. Path Environment key NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY, surfaced through GET /api/config

The map engine (DOODLY_MAPS) is written to mirror the Google Maps interface, so the same code runs with or without Google. It decides which map to show like this:

ConditionWhat the executive sees
Maps key is set — /api/config returns a mapsKeyThe real Google map, with Google's own tiles, markers and route line; it fits the view to include every stop.
No key — mapsKey is emptyA built-in, dependency-free map drawn as clean streets/parks with the same numbered pins and route line. Everything still works.
Key is rejected — billing off, wrong referrer, invalid keyGoogle's failure hook fires and the engine quietly falls back to the built-in map instead of showing a broken Google canvas.
✅
Best Practice

For the sharpest field experience, set NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY in the production environment and enable billing on the Google project. The navigation links open Google Maps regardless of the key, so even without a key executives can still route to every doorstep — the key only upgrades the in-app map from the built-in view to Google's tiles.

⚠️
Warning — never see someone else's route

An executive only ever loads their own stops. The backend looks up the Driver record linked to the signed-in user and returns deliveries for that driver alone; any non-executive role is refused. If an executive reports seeing another person's stops, treat it as a data/linking error and check their Driver-to-user mapping — do not dismiss it.

Data behind the route — GET /api/delivery/my-route

FieldWhat it carries
driverThe executive's name, employee ID, vehicle number and rating (used in the greeting hero)
routeThe route's name and code, e.g. "RT-JH-01" (shown in the header)
date · isFallbackDateThe day being shown; if today has no stops it falls back to the most recent day that does, and flags it as "(latest assigned day)"
stops[]Each stop in delivery order — sequence, customer name & phone, full structured address (house/building/floor/street/landmark/block/wing/gate/door colour), plan, quantity, item label, delivery instructions, bottles expected/collected, payment (Paid or "COD ₹…"), and status
ℹ️
Note — payment shown per stop

A stop is labelled COD ₹{amount} only when its order is still pending and the method is cash; everything else shows Paid. This tells the executive up front which doors to collect money at — reconciled later on the Cash Collection screen.

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Common Route & Maps issues and quick answers.

SymptomLikely causeFix
Map area shows the 🗺️ "placeholder — turn-by-turn in production" cardLooking at the lightweight /driver/route.html sheet, not the field portalSign in and open the field portal at /delivery/, where the real interactive route map loads.
"Open in Maps" just flashes a small message and no map opensOn the driver sheet that button has no linkUse Open navigation (or a stop's Navigate) in the field portal — those open real Google Maps directions.
The in-app map looks plain / not like GoogleNo Google Maps key configured, or the key was rejectedSet NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY and enable billing. The built-in map is fully usable in the meantime.
"No route assigned yet" / empty listNo deliveries are assigned to this executive for todayAssign the executive stops from Admin → Operations (Auto Assignment / Delivery Mgmt). The portal falls back to the latest day that does have stops.
"Executive portal only" errorThe signed-in account is not a delivery executiveOnly a delivery_executive (or Super Admin) may load a route. Check the account's role and linked Driver record.
Distance/ETA look off vs GoogleThe in-app figures are straight-line estimatesTrust Google's number after tapping Navigate; the in-app ETA is a quick planning estimate only.

Does the executive see other drivers' stops?

No. The route is scoped to the signed-in executive's own Driver record, and any non-executive role is refused. Everyone sees only their own run.

Do I need a Google Maps key for navigation to work?

No. Navigation links open the Google Maps app/site directly with the destination and travel mode, so they work without a key. The key only upgrades the in-app map from the built-in view to Google's tiles.

Which travel modes are supported?

Walking, Bike (bicycling), Scooter (two-wheeler) and Car (driving). Tap Navigate on a stop and choose the one you're using so the ETA matches.

What happens when I press "Start route"?

Every still-assigned stop moves to "On the way", the app begins reporting your location every 30 seconds so customers and admins can track you, and it opens navigation to your next stop. Location reporting stops automatically once every stop is delivered.

DOODLYDelivery Executive · Bottle Collection
§Delivery Executive

Bottle Collection

The delivery executive's task list for picking up empty glass bottles along today's route — a count-and-confirm screen whose totals feed straight into each customer's bottle ledger and reward points.

ℹ️
Where this screen sits

Bottle Collection is a page inside the Delivery Executive portal (surface driver), not the admin console. Its manifest key is driver/bottles and it lives at /driver/bottles.html. The executive reaches it from the driver navigation under Tasks. A Super Admin normally looks at this screen to understand what an executive sees in the field, or while helping an executive over the phone; the same numbers reconcile against the customer's /account/bottles.html ledger and the warehouse view under Admin → Catalogue → Bottle Inventory. This is a reference and count screen — the empties count is actually written when the executive marks the stop delivered (see the Workflow).

Bottle Collection task page

Today's empties dashboard plus the route stop list, so the executive knows how many bottles to pick up and from whom.

Purpose Show the executive, at a glance, how many empty bottles are due for collection today, how many have already been collected, how many remain, and how many were damaged — then let them work stop-by-stop down the day's route.
Access Delivery Executive (sees only their own assigned route — the API resolves the Driver record from the signed-in user id) · Super Admin may open the driver portal to view the same page Path Driver Portal → Tasks → Bottle Collection
Route /driver/bottles.html Manifest key driver/bottles KPI source GET /api/driver/summary Stops source GET /api/driver/route
Bottle Collection — Header · "Empties to pick up on today's route"
Page headerTitle "Bottle Collection" and the sub-line "Empties to pick up on today's route." No action button on this page.
KPI strip (4 cards)To collect · Collected · Remaining · Damaged. Big number + label per card (the kpis block). Numbers reflect today's route totals.
Today's stops tableThe route stop list — # · Customer · Address · Item · Payment · Status. No search toolbar and no pager (one continuous list for the day).
Figure — labelled layout of the Bottle Collection screen (three stacked regions: header, KPI strip, stop list).
ℹ️
This page shares the route stop list

The table on Bottle Collection is the same driverStops dataset used by Today's Route and Deliveries — it is declared tbl("driverStops",{toolbar:false,pager:false}). It therefore has no dedicated "bottles" column: it is a cross-reference of the day's stops so the executive can see which customer is next. The actual per-stop empties count is entered on the Delivery Detail / mark-delivered step, which is where the number is captured and saved (documented in Screen 2). Think of this page as "how many, and where", and the delivery step as "record what I collected here".

The four KPI cards

The KPI strip is the kpis block with four hard-declared items in the manifest. In a live signed-in session those figures are the executive's real day totals, computed server-side by GET /api/driver/summary from every Delivery assigned to this driver for today.

CardWhat it showsHow it is calculated (real code)Example
To collectTotal empty bottles still expected across today's un-finished stops.bottlesToCollect = sum of bottleCount over stops that are not DELIVERED, FAILED or SKIPPED.31
CollectedEmpties already picked up today.bottlesCollectedToday = sum of bottlesIn over stops already marked DELIVERED.26
RemainingEmpties still to be collected before the route is done.The gap between "To collect" and what has been collected on completed stops — the still-pending balance for the day.5
DamagedBottles that came back broken / could not be collected intact.Not returned by the summary API (no "damaged" total exists server-side). A damaged bottle is handled as a delivery issue ("Damaged bottle") and, if written off, as a LOST event on the customer's bottle ledger — see the callout below.0
⚠️
Warning — "Damaged" is not an automatic counter

The backend bottle ledger tracks five events — ISSUED, RETURNED, LOST, DEPOSIT_CHARGED, DEPOSIT_REFUNDED — and the driver summary returns only "to collect" and "collected today". There is no separate "damaged" tally. The Damaged KPI is a placeholder that stays at 0 unless a damaged bottle is explicitly recorded through the stop's issue report (the "Damaged bottle" reason) and later written off as a LOST ledger row by operations. Do not read a non-zero "Damaged" figure as an audited financial number — trace it to the issue report and the LOST row.

Today's stops — table specification

Declared as tbl("driverStops",{toolbar:false,pager:false}): it runs on the shared DataTable engine but with the toolbar and pager switched off, so it reads as a plain, complete route list for the day.

AspectBehaviour on this screen
Columns# (stop sequence) · Customer · Address · Item (e.g. 500 ml / 1000 ml) · Payment (Prepaid or "₹70 COD") · Status (coloured badge).
Row orderBy the route sequence the operations team set; the live API returns stops ordered by date (newest first) then sequence ascending.
SearchHidden — the search box is part of the toolbar, which is off.
Facet filtersHidden on this page. (The sibling Deliveries page turns filters on with All / Pending / Delivered.)
SortingColumn-header sort still works client-side; the list is short enough that most executives just read top-to-bottom.
PaginationOff — every stop for the day shows on one page.
ExportHidden (export lives in the toolbar, which is off).
Row actionsNone on this reference page. Collection is recorded from the delivery step (Screen 2).
Empty stateWhen there is no live route (not signed in as a real executive, or no route assigned today) the list shows an empty state rather than any demo data.

Reading the Status badge

BadgeColourMeaning
DeliveredgreenStop is done; its empties (if any) have been recorded.
Next stopamberThe stop the executive is heading to now.
PendinggreyNot yet reached.

Recording empties at a stop

The count-and-confirm step where the executive actually logs how many empty bottles they collected from a customer.

Purpose Capture the real number of empties picked up (and full bottles handed over) at one stop, so the customer's bottle ledger and reward points update the moment the stop is marked delivered.
Access Delivery Executive (own stops only) · Super Admin Path Driver Portal → Deliveries → open a stop → Bottles & remarks
Save endpoint PATCH /api/driver/deliveries/[id] Also writes BottleLedger (ISSUED / RETURNED) + DOODLY Pure Rewards points
Delivery Detail — Bottles & remarks panel
Empties to collectA read-out of how many empty bottles this customer is expected to return (from the delivery's bottleCount).
Empty bottles collected — stepperA − / count / + control ("Collected empty bottle"). The executive taps to match the real number picked up; it is capped at the expected count.
RemarksFree-text note, e.g. "left with guard". Saved as customerRemark (max 200 characters).
Capture proof photoAttach a proof-of-delivery photo for the stop.
Mark deliveredThe confirm action. It flips the stop to DELIVERED, stamps the time, and posts the ledger.
Figure — the Bottles & remarks panel on the delivery step, where the empties count is entered and saved.

Fields saved to the delivery

When the stop is updated, the portal sends a PATCH to /api/driver/deliveries/[id]. Every field is validated server-side (Zod schema) before it is stored. All fields are optional on a partial update — the executive usually only touches the empties count and, occasionally, a remark.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
bottlesInEmpty bottles collected from the customer at this stop.Whole number 0–99Opt.z.number().int().min(0).max(99). On delivery, written as a RETURNED ledger row (qty = bottlesIn) against the customer.1
bottlesOutFull bottles handed to the customer at this stop.Whole number 0–99Opt.int 0–99. If left blank when the stop becomes DELIVERED, it defaults to the delivery's bottleCount. Written as an ISSUED ledger row.1
cashCollectedCOD cash taken at the stop (for a COD order).Whole number ≥ 0, in paiseOpt.int min(0). Stored on the delivery and reconciled on the Cash Collection page.7000 (= ₹70)
customerRemarkNote about how the drop-off went.Text, trimmed, up to 200 charsOpt.string().trim().max(200).left with guard
statusThe stop's workflow state.ACCEPTED · PACKED · OUT_FOR_DELIVERY · ON_THE_WAY · REACHED · DELIVERED · FAILED · SKIPPEDOpt.Enum. Setting it to DELIVERED (from any other state) stamps deliveredAt and triggers the ledger + points writes.DELIVERED
ℹ️
Bottles out vs. bottles in

bottlesOut = the full bottles you delivered (the customer receives them → an ISSUED row, defaulting to the stop's bottleCount). bottlesIn = the empties you picked up (the customer returns them → a RETURNED row). Bottle Collection is about the bottlesIn side: how many empties came back.

Buttons & controls

Button / controlWhat it doesBackend processPermission
− / + stepper ("Empty bottles collected")Decreases / increases the collected count. Floored at 0 and capped at the number of empties expected for the stop.Adjusts the value locally; sent as bottlesIn on the next save. Nothing is written until the stop is confirmed.Delivery Executive
Collected empty bottle (checkbox, on the simple detail panel)Quick yes/no confirmation that one empty was picked up.Feeds the same bottlesIn field.Delivery Executive
Remarks (textarea)Records a short note about the drop-off.Saved as customerRemark.Delivery Executive
Capture proof photoAttaches a proof-of-delivery image to the stop.Proof-of-delivery capture on the delivery record.Delivery Executive
Mark delivered (confirm)Completes the stop. This is the moment the empties count becomes final.PATCH …status=DELIVERED → stamps deliveredAt, writes ISSUED (bottlesOut) and RETURNED (bottlesIn) ledger rows for the customer, and awards Pure Rewards points.Delivery Executive
Report issue (e.g. "Damaged bottle")Logs a problem at the stop instead of a clean collection.Raises a delivery issue; a written-off bottle later becomes a LOST ledger row.Delivery Executive

What happens when a stop is marked delivered

The empties number the executive entered is not just a note — marking the stop delivered posts permanent ledger entries and reward points, all inside one server transaction.

Workflow — collecting empties and posting the ledger
Arrive at stopCount empties, set the stepper (bottlesIn)Add remark / proof photoMark delivered → PATCH status=DELIVEREDdeliveredAt stampedISSUED (bottlesOut) + RETURNED (bottlesIn) written to customer ledgerPure Rewards points: bottle-return + 12-stop streak bonus
Figure — the delivery confirmation posts the bottle ledger and reward points for the customer.
On DELIVERED, the server…Detail (real code)
Stamps the delivery timeSets deliveredAt = now (only on the first transition into DELIVERED).
Issues full bottlesIf bottlesOut > 0, writes a BottleLedger row event: ISSUED, qty: bottlesOut for the customer.
Records returned emptiesIf bottlesIn > 0, writes a BottleLedger row event: RETURNED, qty: bottlesIn.
Awards return pointsBest-effort earn.bottleReturn(customer, deliveryId, bottlesIn) — idempotent, non-blocking.
Awards a streak bonusEvery 12 consecutive DELIVERED stops on the subscription grants a 200-point bonus (earn.deliveryStreak).
Writes an audit entryaction: "delivery.delivered", actor role delivery_executive, with request context.
⛔
Caution — the count is final once you confirm the stop

Marking the stop delivered writes the RETURNED row to the customer's append-only ledger and stamps deliveredAt. The ledger is never edited in place — a wrong count can only be corrected by posting a new, opposite entry through operations. Count the empties in front of the customer and set the stepper correctly before you tap "Mark delivered".

ℹ️
You can only touch your own stops

The endpoint first resolves the Driver record for the signed-in user, then loads the delivery where driverId matches that driver. A stop that belongs to another executive returns "Delivery not found on your route." A user with no linked delivery profile gets a 403 "No delivery profile is linked to this account."

Operating notes

Best practice, troubleshooting and common questions for Bottle Collection.

✅
Best Practice

Work the route in sequence and clear the Remaining KPI to zero before you head back to the hub. Because "To collect" is the sum of expected empties over un-finished stops, any bottle you forget to log stays counted against the customer forever (an ISSUED with no matching RETURNED) and inflates their pending-bottle balance. If a customer genuinely has no empty to give today, still mark the stop delivered with bottlesIn = 0 so the day reconciles cleanly.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
The stop list is empty and KPIs read 0.Not signed in as a real executive, or no route has been assigned for today.Confirm the executive is logged in with a delivery_executive account and that operations has assigned/generated today's route.
Saving a stop fails with "No delivery profile is linked to this account."The user has no Driver record.An Admin must create the driver profile (Admin → Delivery → Drivers) before the account can update stops.
"Delivery not found on your route."The stop belongs to a different executive, or the delivery id is wrong.Only stops assigned to this executive can be updated — reload the route so the id list is current.
The empties stepper won't go higher.The count is capped at the number of empties expected for that stop.If the customer genuinely has extra bottles, log the expected amount and raise a "Damaged bottle" / mismatch issue for the rest rather than forcing the count.
Customer's pending-bottle count stays high after you collected empties.The stop was marked delivered with bottlesIn = 0 (empties not logged), so no RETURNED row was written.Report it to operations; a corrective RETURNED entry is posted against the customer's ledger.
"Damaged" KPI shows a number nobody expected.It is a placeholder, not an audited total (the summary API has no damaged field).Trust the issue reports and the ledger's LOST rows for the real damaged/write-off picture.

Frequently asked questions

Q. What is the difference between "bottles out" and "bottles in"?

Bottles out are the full bottles you deliver — they create an ISSUED row and default to the stop's planned bottleCount. Bottles in are the empty bottles you collect back — they create a RETURNED row. Bottle Collection is about the "in" side: how many empties came home.

Q. Does the customer get anything when I collect their empties?

Yes. On delivery the system awards DOODLY Pure Rewards points for the bottles returned (earn.bottleReturn), and a 200-point bonus every 12 consecutive delivered stops on their subscription. That is why an accurate empties count matters to the customer, not just to inventory.

Q. Can I fix a wrong empties count after I've marked the stop delivered?

Not from the field — the bottle ledger is append-only. Ask operations to post an offsetting entry (for example a RETURNED to correct an under-count). This is why you should confirm the number with the customer before tapping "Mark delivered".

Q. How do these numbers reach the customer and the warehouse?

The RETURNED / ISSUED rows you post appear immediately on the customer's /account/bottles.html ledger (their Pending and Deposit figures recompute from all-time totals), and the physical fleet is reconciled by operations under Admin → Catalogue → Bottle Inventory.

Q. Where do damaged or lost bottles go?

They are not part of the clean "collected" count. Raise the stop's issue report with the "Damaged bottle" reason; if the bottle is written off, operations record it as a LOST ledger event, which reduces the customer's pending bottles without counting as a return.

DOODLYDelivery Executive · Cash Collection
§Delivery Executive

Cash Collection

A read-only, self-service view that totals the cash-on-delivery (COD) a delivery executive has collected on today's route, so they can reconcile and hand it in at the hub.

ℹ️
Note — whose screen this is

Cash Collection lives in the driver portal, not the admin console. It shows one executive their own numbers only. The data endpoint refuses anyone who does not have a Driver profile linked to their account ("No delivery profile is linked to this account."). A Super Admin reads this section to understand the driver's daily cash workflow and to oversee reconciliation from Admin → Operations → Delivery Mgmt; they do not personally use this screen.

Cash Collection (today)

Live COD summary for the signed-in executive.

Purpose Show how much cash was collected today, on how many stops, and list each cash payment so the executive can match it against the physical cash before handing it over at the hub.
Access Delivery Executive (own route only) · viewable for oversight by Super Admin Admin Manager Path Driver → Tasks → Cash Collection
Data GET /api/driver/route (the driver's own stops) → filtered to today, sorted by stop sequence. Money unit stored as paise on Delivery.cashCollected; displayed as rounded rupees (₹).
Header — Cash Collection · "Cash collected on today's route."
Sign-in gateState gate — must be signed in as an executive with a Driver profile, else a sign-in prompt.
Empty state (no stops)"No deliveries today — No cash to collect." shown when the executive has no stops dated today.
Stat card — Cash collected todaySum of cash across all of today's stops, in ₹.
Stat card — Cash paymentsCount of today's stops where cash > 0.
COD tableOne row per cash-paying stop: #, Customer, Cash, Status.
Empty state (no cash)"No cash collected yet — Most stops are prepaid…" when there are stops but none took cash.
Footer note"Record cash when you mark a stop delivered on the route." with a link to Today's Route.
Figure — labelled layout of the Cash Collection screen (delivery portal).

KPIs / stat cards on this screen

StatWhat it measuresHow it is computedExample
Cash collected todayTotal physical cash the executive is holding from today's deliveries.Sum of cashCollected (paise) across every stop dated today, formatted to ₹ (rounded).₹1,540
Cash paymentsHow many of today's stops actually took cash.Count of today's stops where cashCollected > 0.8
ℹ️
Static preview build shows a fixed KPI strip

In the static preview/marketing build, this route is rendered from a fixed recipe and shows four demo tiles — Expected (₹2,140), Collected (₹1,540), Remaining (₹600) and COD stops (8) — plus the notice "Hand over collected cash at the hub at end of route. Reconciliation is automatic against delivered COD stops." Those are placeholder figures. The live delivery app computes the two real stat cards above from the executive's actual stops.

COD table

PropertyBehaviour (from the real component)
Columns# (stop sequence), Customer (name), Cash (₹ collected), Status (delivery status pill).
Rows shownOnly today's stops whose cashCollected > 0. Prepaid / zero-cash stops are hidden.
SortingFixed — by stop sequence ascending (route order). No column-click sorting.
Search / filters / paginationNone. It is a short, single-day list scoped to one executive.
Row actionsNone — read-only. Cash is edited on the Route screen, not here.
Status pillReflects the delivery's status; cash rows are typically DELIVERED because cash is captured at the moment a stop is marked delivered.
Empty behaviourNo stops today → "No deliveries today". Stops but no cash → "No cash collected yet".
ExportsNone on the driver screen. Cash figures roll up to admin reporting.
💡
Tip — why the total may exceed the visible rows' sum on a busy day

The Cash collected today stat sums cash across all of today's stops, while the table lists only stops with cash. In normal operation these agree, because a stop only has cash after it is delivered. If you ever see a mismatch, refresh — a stop was likely just updated on the Route screen.

Where cash is recorded — "Mark delivered" form

The upstream step that feeds every number on the Cash Collection screen.

Purpose Capture the cash taken at the door for one stop. Cash is never typed on the Cash Collection screen itself — it is entered here when the executive confirms a delivery, then aggregated onto Cash Collection.
Access Delivery Executive (only their own assigned stops) Path Driver → Today → Today's Route → (stop) → Mark delivered
Writes PATCH /api/driver/deliveries/{id} → updates Delivery.cashCollected, bottlesIn, customerRemark, stamps deliveredAt, writes the bottle ledger, and audits the action.
Stop card — Customer · address · items · Navigate / Call / WhatsApp
Workflow buttonsStart delivery → Mark reached → Mark delivered · Couldn't deliver.
Empties collectedNumber of returned empty bottles (defaults to the stop's bottle count).
Cash collected (₹)The COD amount taken at the door — this is what lands on Cash Collection.
RemarkOptional free-text note, e.g. "Left at door".
Confirm deliveredSaves the form, marks the stop DELIVERED and posts the ledger.
Figure — the "Mark delivered" panel on the Route screen; the Cash field feeds Cash Collection.

Fields — "Mark delivered" panel

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
Empties collectedHow many empty glass bottles were picked up at this stop.Whole number 0–99Opt.Server: integer, min 0, max 99. Defaults to the stop's bottle count in the form. Posts a RETURNED bottle-ledger row on delivery.2
Cash collected (₹)Rupee amount of cash taken at the door for a COD order.Number ≥ 0 (up to 2 decimals in the input)Opt.Input rupees are converted to paise (round(₹ × 100)) and stored on Delivery.cashCollected. Server: integer, min 0. Blank = 0 (prepaid).70
RemarkOptional delivery note visible to admin/customer service.Free textOpt.Server: trimmed, max 200 characters. Saved to customerRemark.Left with guard

Buttons / actions

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Start deliveryMoves a scheduled/assigned/accepted/packed stop to Out for delivery.PATCH /api/driver/deliveries/{id} with status: OUT_FOR_DELIVERY.Delivery Executive
Mark reachedMarks arrival at the doorstep (from Out-for-delivery / On-the-way).PATCH … status: REACHED.Delivery Executive
Mark deliveredOpens the proof form with Empties, Cash collected and Remark.Reveals the form; no write until confirmed.Delivery Executive
Confirm deliveredSaves cash + empties + remark and completes the stop.PATCH … status: DELIVERED, cashCollected (paise), bottlesIn, customerRemark; stamps deliveredAt; writes ISSUED/RETURNED bottle-ledger; best-effort loyalty points; audit delivery.delivered.Delivery Executive
Couldn't deliverMarks the stop Failed (no cash captured).PATCH … status: FAILED.Delivery Executive
Navigate / Call / WhatsAppOpens Google Maps directions, a phone dial, or WhatsApp chat for the stop.Client-side deep links only (no write).Delivery Executive
Workflow — cash from door to hub
Deliver stopEnter Cash collected (₹)Confirm delivered → stored as paiseAggregated onto Cash CollectionReconcile against physical cashHand over at hub
Figure — end-to-end COD reconciliation path.
⛔
Caution — cash can't be edited from the driver app once delivered

After a stop is marked DELIVERED, the stop card switches to a read-only summary ("✓ Delivered … · ₹70 cash") and the cash field disappears. The executive cannot re-open it to correct a wrong amount. A mistake must be fixed by an Admin/Super Admin on the delivery record. Enter the exact amount before tapping Confirm delivered.

✅
Best Practice — daily hub reconciliation

At the end of every route, open Cash Collection, read the Cash collected today total, and count the physical notes to match it before you hand cash to the hub. Because most orders are prepaid, the Cash payments count should equal the number of COD stops on your route — if it is lower, a COD stop was delivered with the cash field left blank; report it so Admin can correct the record.

⚠️
Warning — blank cash field defaults to ₹0

Leaving the Cash collected field empty on a COD stop records ₹0, not the amount owed. The order is not held or flagged for this. Always type the amount on cash orders; only leave it blank for genuinely prepaid deliveries.

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"No delivery profile is linked to this account."The signed-in user has no Driver record.Super Admin adds/links the executive under Admin → Operations → Drivers so a Driver profile exists.
"No deliveries today"No stops dated today are assigned to this driver.Confirm today's route was generated and assigned (Delivery Mgmt / Auto Assignment). It reads only stops with today's date.
Cash total looks too lowA COD stop was confirmed with the cash field blank (₹0).Identify the stop; have Admin correct cashCollected on the delivery record.
Collected an amount but stop not in the tableThe stop isn't dated today, or cash was recorded as 0.Only today's stops with cash > 0 appear. Check the stop on Today's Route.
Wrong amount recorded and field is goneStop already marked DELIVERED — driver UI is read-only after that.Admin/Super Admin edits the delivery's cash on the backend record.

Frequently asked questions

Q: Do I type cash amounts on the Cash Collection screen?

No. Cash Collection is read-only. You enter cash on the Route screen in the "Mark delivered" form; it then rolls up here automatically.

Q: Why is the amount stored in paise?

All money in DOODLY is stored as integer paise to avoid rounding errors. The rupees you type are multiplied by 100 on save, and the screen divides back and rounds to whole rupees for display.

Q: Where does the cash physically go?

You hand collected cash to the hub at the end of the route. The app's job is reconciliation — matching your counted cash to the recorded total against delivered COD stops.

Q: Can a Super Admin see a driver's cash?

Not on this driver-only screen. Super Admin oversight of delivered stops, cash and reconciliation is done through the admin Delivery Management module, which reads the same Delivery records.

DOODLYDelivery Executive · Profile & Attendance
§Delivery Executive

Profile & Attendance

The delivery executive's own account screen — read their driver record (zone, vehicle, rating, tenure) and keep their contact details and shift status current.

ℹ️
Note — where this screen lives

This page is served on the Delivery surface at /driver/profile.html (manifest route key driver/profile, surface: "driver"). It is the executive's self-service account page. The authoritative driver record — zone, vehicle number, delivery capacity, active/suspended state — is created and edited by an Admin on Admin → Operations → Drivers, and the day-by-day attendance ledger for salaried staff lives on Admin → Human Resources → Attendance. Both are cross-referenced below.

My Profile

Driver details card + update-your-details form

Purpose Let the delivery executive see their own driver record at a glance and keep their phone and emergency contact up to date.
Access Delivery Executive (their own record) · Super Admin (view any / oversight) Path Delivery → Account → Profile
Delivery portal — Profile · sidebar: Today · Tasks · Records · Account
Top barAvatar "RK" · name "Ramesh K." · bell · account menu ("Profile & settings")
Page headTitle "Profile" with sub-line "Your driver details."
Left column — Details cardDefinition list headed "Ramesh Kumar · DRV-07": Zone, Vehicle, Rating, Joined, Deliveries
Right column — Update details formPhone · Emergency contact fields with a Save button and Reset
Figure 1 — Two-column layout: read-only driver record (left) and the editable contact form (right).

The details card (read-only)

Rendered as a definition list titled with the executive's name and employee ID (for example Ramesh Kumar · DRV-07). Each row shows a label and value pulled from the driver record. These values are set and maintained by an Admin on the Drivers screen; the executive cannot change them here.

DetailMeaningBacking driver fieldExample
Employee IDThe executive's login and staff code, shown in the card title next to their name.Driver.employeeId (unique)DRV-07
ZoneThe delivery zone the executive is assigned to — it scopes which deliveries and routes they see.Driver.zoneId → resolved to DeliveryZone.nameJubilee Hills
VehicleThe registered vehicle number used for the round.Driver.vehicleNoTS09 EZ 4421
RatingService rating out of 5, averaged from delivery feedback.Driver.rating (defaults to 5)4.9★
JoinedWhen the executive started with DOODLY (tenure).Derived from the linked User.createdAtFeb 2026
DeliveriesLifetime completed deliveries — a badge of experience.Count of Delivery rows for this driver2,840
ℹ️
Related driver fields the Admin controls

The full driver record also carries Delivery capacity (DeliveryCapacity.maxBottles, default 45 bottles), Active / suspended state (Driver.active and the linked User.status), last-known GPS (lat, lng, lastSeenAt for live tracking) and a soft-delete flag (deletedAt). These are managed from Admin → Operations → Drivers → Manage — see the Drivers module in the Operations chapter.

The "Update details" form

A short self-service form the executive uses to keep their reachable numbers current. It is the only editable part of this screen.

FieldPurposeAccepted valuesReq.Validation / backendExample
PhoneThe executive's own contact number, used by ops and customers.Telephone number (type="tel"), placeholder "+91 …"Opt.No format mask is enforced client-side; a value is only rejected if it is submitted blank while marked required (this field is optional here). On Save the form data is persisted for the current device.+91 91177 99143
Emergency contactA backup number to reach in case of an on-road emergency.Telephone number (type="tel"), placeholder "+91 …"Opt.Same handling as Phone — saved with the form on Save.+91 90000 12345

Buttons

ButtonWhat it doesBackend processPermission
SaveValidates any required fields, then confirms with an inline "Saved ✓ Your changes have been applied." message plus a toast reading "Save ✓".Handled by the shared form handler (wireForms in layout.js). It reads every named field, checks required fields are non-blank (blank ones get an "invalid" outline and focus), and stores the values under the key doodly-form-form so they pre-fill next time. In this build the profile save is client-side — it does not yet write back to the Driver record.Delivery Executive
ResetClears validation outlines and the status message, and shows a "Changes reset" toast.Native form reset intercepted by the js-formreset handler; removes .invalid flags and hides the inline message.Delivery Executive
⚠️
Warning — self-service Save is local in this build

The "Update details" form saves to the executive's browser/device (local storage), not to the central driver record. If a phone or emergency number must be authoritative across the system, an Admin should also update it on Admin → Operations → Drivers → Manage. Treat the on-page form as a convenience note-to-self until the server write is wired.

✅
Best Practice

Ask each executive to confirm both numbers on their first login and after any SIM change. Keep the emergency contact different from the primary phone (a family member or nearest hub), so ops can reach someone if the executive is unreachable on the road.

Shift & Attendance

Start / end shift availability toggle

Purpose The executive marks themselves on- or off-shift. Only executives who are on-shift (AVAILABLE) receive new auto-assigned deliveries, so this toggle is the real attendance signal for the routing engine.
Access Delivery Executive (own shift) · Super Admin (oversight) Path Delivery → Today → Dashboard (availability pill)

The shift control appears on the executive's route dashboard as a tappable status pill in the header — a green "🟢 Available for assignment" when on-shift, or a grey "⚪ Not available — start shift" when off. It is driven by the executive's own availability endpoint and is the closest thing to a clock-in for a delivery executive: it decides whether the auto-assignment engine can hand them work.

Delivery dashboard — Good morning, Ramesh · route & date · availability pill
Availability pillGreen "Available for assignment" ⇄ grey "Not available — start shift"; tap to toggle
Today's KPIsAssigned · Completed · Pending · Remaining stops · Bottles to collect · Distance left
Route mapToday's stops with Start route / Open navigation / Refresh
Stop listPer-stop cards (status, bottles, call/WhatsApp/SMS, deliver, report issue)
Figure 2 — The availability pill sits in the dashboard header alongside the day's summary KPIs.

Availability states

The executive's live status is stored on their ExecutiveStatus row. The engine treats only AVAILABLE (and RETURNED_TO_DAIRY, i.e. back at the hub, empty) as eligible for new work.

StateMeaningTakes new assignments?
AVAILABLEOn-shift and free — the engine can assign new deliveries.Yes
ASSIGNEDDeliveries have been handed to them but the trip has not started.No (mid-trip)
ACCEPTEDThe executive accepted the trip.No (mid-trip)
OUT_FOR_DELIVERYOn the road delivering.No (mid-trip)
COMPLETEDFinished the current trip's stops.No (until returned)
RETURNED_TO_DAIRYBack at the hub, ready for the next round.Yes
OFFLINEOff-shift — ended their shift.No
BREAKTemporarily paused (break).No

The toggle action

ControlWhat it doesBackend processPermission
Start shift (grey pill)Puts the executive on-shift; the pill turns green and a "Shift started — you're available" toast appears.POST /api/driver/availability with { available: true } → sets ExecutiveStatus.availability = AVAILABLE (upserts the row if missing).Delivery Executive
End shift (green pill)Takes the executive off-shift; the pill turns grey and a "Shift ended" toast appears.POST /api/driver/availability with { available: false } → sets availability to OFFLINE and clears currentTripId and assignedBottles.Delivery Executive
ℹ️
Every shift change is audited

Each toggle writes an AssignmentLog entry with action STATUS_CHANGE, the executive as the actor (actorRole: delivery_executive) and a note such as "shift started → AVAILABLE" or "shift ended → OFFLINE". A Super Admin can review this trail in the assignment history — it is the auditable record of when each executive clocked on and off.

Workflow — a delivery executive's shift
Log in to Delivery portalTap "Start shift" → AVAILABLEEngine auto-assigns stopsStart route → out for deliveryComplete stops / Return to dairyTap "End shift" → OFFLINE
Figure 3 — From clock-in to clock-out; new work only flows while the executive is AVAILABLE.
⚠️
Warning — you cannot go offline mid-trip

If the executive is ASSIGNED, ACCEPTED or OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, trying to end their shift is rejected with a 409 and the message: "You have an active trip — complete your deliveries (or mark returned to dairy) before changing availability." They must finish the round (or mark returned to dairy) first. This protects customers from a half-delivered route being abandoned.

💡
Tip — the missing-profile guard

Both the availability endpoint and the day-summary endpoint require a driver profile linked to the account. If someone signs in without one, they get "No delivery profile is linked to this account." Fix it by creating the driver on Admin → Operations → Drivers and linking it to their user.

Attendance ledger (HR)

Where a salaried executive's days are recorded

Purpose For executives who are on payroll, formal day-by-day attendance (present, leave, off) is recorded in Human Resources and feeds payroll — separate from the live shift toggle above.
Access Super Admin · Admin · Manager (HR-permitted) Path Admin → Human Resources → Attendance

The shift toggle answers "are they working right now?"; the HR attendance ledger answers "how many days did they work this month?" for pay. Each attendance record (Attendance) carries a date, a status, optional check-in / check-out times, worked minutes, overtime minutes and a source (manual, biometric, etc.). It attaches to an EmployeeProfile, so it applies to any delivery executive who is also set up as an employee.

Attendance statusMeaning
PRESENTFull working day.
HALF_DAYWorked half the day.
ABSENTDid not report and no leave applied.
PAID_LEAVEApproved leave that is paid.
SICK_LEAVEMedical leave.
WEEKLY_OFFScheduled weekly rest day.
HOLIDAYCompany/public holiday.
WFHWorked from home (office roles).
ℹ️
Note — two separate ideas, on purpose

The live availability pill and the HR attendance ledger are intentionally distinct: the pill drives routing in real time, the ledger drives payroll. Full documentation of the attendance ledger, bulk marking and payroll linkage lives in the Human Resources chapter (Attendance module).

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
"No delivery profile is linked to this account." when opening the dashboard or toggling shift.The signed-in user has no Driver record.Create/link the driver on Admin → Operations → Drivers; confirm employeeId is set.
Tapping "End shift" fails with a conflict.The executive is mid-trip (ASSIGNED / ACCEPTED / OUT_FOR_DELIVERY).Finish the remaining stops, or mark "Returned to dairy", then end the shift.
Executive is on-shift but the engine never assigns them stops.Availability is not AVAILABLE, capacity is inactive, or they are in the wrong zone.Confirm the pill is green; check DeliveryCapacity.active and maxBottles, and the assigned Zone on the Drivers screen.
Updated phone number on the Profile form but ops still sees the old one.The self-service form saved locally only, not to the driver record.Have an Admin update the number on Admin → Operations → Drivers → Manage.
Profile card shows placeholder details (Ramesh Kumar · DRV-07, Jubilee Hills…).Signed-out / demo view of the driver surface.Sign in as the executive; the card then reflects their real linked Driver record.

Frequently asked questions

Can an executive change their own zone, vehicle or rating on this page?

No. Those are read-only on the Profile card. Zone and vehicle are set by an Admin on the Drivers screen; rating is averaged automatically from delivery feedback. Only Phone and Emergency contact are editable by the executive.

Does ending a shift cancel deliveries already assigned?

No. You cannot end a shift while a trip is active — the system blocks it (409). You must complete or return the current trip first, which is exactly why abandoned half-routes cannot happen from the toggle.

Is the shift toggle the same as marking attendance for payroll?

No. The toggle is a live routing signal (AVAILABLE vs OFFLINE) and is audited in the assignment log. Payroll attendance is recorded separately in Admin → Human Resources → Attendance against the executive's employee profile.

Where can a Super Admin see when an executive clocked on and off?

In the assignment audit trail: every shift change writes an AssignmentLog row of type STATUS_CHANGE with the timestamp, the executive as actor, and a note like "shift started → AVAILABLE".

DOODLYAppendix A · FAQ
AAppendix

Frequently asked questions

A customer can't sign up — the password is rejected. Why?

The password must be at least 8 characters and include an uppercase letter, a lowercase letter, a number and a special character. The sign-up form shows a live checklist that turns green as each rule is met. Existing customers are never affected — only new passwords must meet the rule.

Where does a customer land after logging in?

The Home page — unless they were in the middle of a purchase, in which case they return to checkout to finish paying.

A change I made in Admin isn't on the website. What do I check?

Almost always it is saved — the website reads the same database live. Do a hard refresh. If it's still missing, confirm the change was saved (look for the success toast) and that you edited the production environment.

Who can reverse a payment or a reward?

The Super Admin, and for finance items the Accountant. These actions are always audited.

DOODLYAppendix B · Troubleshooting
BAppendix

Troubleshooting

SymptomLikely causeFix
Payment failed at checkoutCard/UPI declined, or gateway keys/limitsAsk the customer to retry with another method; check Commerce → Payments for the failure reason. Nothing is charged on a failure.
Referral link not workingLink missing the code, or programme pausedConfirm the code in Growth → Referrals; share links as /signup.html?ref=CODE.
Orders not auto-assigningNo available drivers, or capacity fullMark drivers available and check capacity in Operations → Auto Assignment; unassigned orders wait in the pending queue.
AutoPay renewal didn't chargeMandate paused, or bank declinedCheck the mandate status in Commerce → Subscription Billing; failed retries pause (never silently cancel) the subscription.
New pincode still shows "not serviceable"Pincode not added, or typoAdd it in Operations → Serviceable Areas — the storefront accepts it instantly.
DOODLYAppendix C · Glossary
CAppendix

Glossary

TermPlain-English meaning
Bottle depositA refundable amount (₹120/bottle) the customer pays so glass bottles come back. Returned to their wallet when the empty is collected.
WalletThe customer's in-app balance — from refunds, cashback, referral rewards — usable at checkout.
ReferralInviting a friend with a personal code; a one-time ₹100 reward is paid once the friend reaches an eligible plan.
SubscriptionA recurring plan (7/30/90-day) that delivers milk on a schedule instead of one-off orders.
AutoPayA bank/UPI mandate that renews a subscription automatically before it expires. Opt-in only.
Auto AssignmentThe engine that shares the day's deliveries fairly across available executives.
SKU"Stock Keeping Unit" — a specific sellable item, e.g. 500 ml A2 milk.
GSTIndia's Goods & Services Tax, applied per product and shown on every invoice.
InvoiceThe official GST bill for a payment, downloadable by the customer and admin.
RBAC"Role-Based Access Control" — the system that decides what each role can see and do.
Audit logAn unchangeable record of every sensitive action, for accountability.
Serviceable areaA pincode DOODLY currently delivers to.
